004d64769c1d5367.action_steps:
Setup:  Three PEs (PE1, PE2, PE3) sharing ES-1 (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00) with ethernet-segment configured under agg-eth; create vlan-aware-bundle EVPN instance covering VLANs 100-105; configure Highest-Preference DF algorithm on all PEs; apply consistent local policy override on PE1 and PE2 (e.g., static DF preference or route-map forcing preference value) for VLAN 100.
Action: Verify DF election converges with consistent override; modify local policy on PE2 only to a different preference value creating inconsistency across PEs; inject tagged BUM traffic (VLAN 100, 100 pps) from CE on ES-1; capture BUM replication to core and remote PEs.
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00 detail (DF/non-DF state per VLAN on each PE), show evpn instance <name> (election state), IXIA capture on core-facing ports of all 3 PEs for duplicate BUM frames.
004d64769c1d5367.expectation:
Pass:    With consistent local policy override, exactly one PE is DF for VLAN 100, BUM replicated once to core, non-DF blocks BUM on access, no duplicate frames at remote PE.
Fail-on: With inconsistent policy (different override values on PE1 vs PE2), multiple PEs claim DF for same VLAN OR duplicate BUM frames reach remote PE OR DF flaps between PEs OR non-DF forwards BUM to core.

0064f503112b8164.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP configured under routing/bgp/<asn>/vrf/<vrf>, neighbor relationship established with EVPN address-family enabled (af-l2vpn evpn). Session in Established state receiving L2VPN EVPN prefixes. Baseline prefix count recorded via `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`.
Action: Configure maximum-prefix limit of 100 under neighbor af-l2vpn evpn mode. Commit. Trigger neighbor to advertise 101 L2VPN EVPN MAC/IP Advertisement routes (type 2). Observe session teardown. Reset session, reconfigure with warning-only option, trigger 101 prefixes. Verify session persists. Reset session, reconfigure with restart 5 (minutes), trigger 101 prefixes. Verify session tears down then auto-restarts after 5 minutes.
Verify: `show running-config` displays maximum-prefix configuration under neighbor af-l2vpn evpn. `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows prefix count reaching 100 then session state change. `show bgp neighbor <ip>` or tcpdump captures NOTIFICATION code 6 sub-code 1. Syslog entries confirm warning messages for warning-only mode. Session uptime in `show bgp neighbor <ip>` confirms 5-minute auto-restart for restart mode.
0064f503112b8164.equipment: DUT + IXIA (BGP route generator capable of 100+ L2VPN EVPN routes) or DUT + neighbor PE with route injection capability
0064f503112b8164.expectation:
Pass:    Default behavior: session tears down at 101st prefix, NOTIFICATION message code 6 sub-code 1 (Maximum Number of Prefixes Reached) sent. With warning-only: session remains Established at 101+ prefixes, syslog warning logged, no NOTIFICATION. With restart 5: session tears down at 101st prefix with NOTIFICATION, auto-restarts after 5 minutes (±10 sec tolerance).
Fail-on: Session does not tear down when limit exceeded (without warning-only), wrong NOTIFICATION code/sub-code sent, warning-only causes session teardown instead of syslog-only, restart fails to auto-restart or timing exceeds 5:30, configuration is rejected or does not persist.

006f2d826daf176b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance, IRB interface 192.168.1.1/24, gateway MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01, `advertise-mac enable`. Configure BGP EVPN `af-l2vpn evpn` to neighbor PE. Configure neighbor PE with EVPN instance for same subnet, IXIA connected as host port.
Action: IXIA transmits frames sourcing from MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01. Neighbor PE learns MAC, advertises EVPN Type-2 (MAC/IP) route without Default Gateway extended community. DUT advertises IRB gateway MAC with Default Gateway extended community. Capture BGP update exchange.
Verify: On neighbor PE: `show bgp evpn route type mac-ip 00:00:5e:00:01:01` shows initial local advertisement, then withdrawal after receiving DUT's route. `show system alarms` logs MAC conflict alarm. On DUT: `show bgp evpn route type mac-ip 00:00:5e:00:01:01` shows route with Default Gateway EC only, no MAC Mobility EC attached.
006f2d826daf176b.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor PE withdraws its MAC route (without Default Gateway EC) within BGP convergence time after receiving DUT's route (with Default Gateway EC). Alarm raised on neighbor PE for default gateway MAC conflict per §10.1.1. DUT's route carries Default Gateway EC but no MAC Mobility EC. Gateway reachable from other hosts.
Fail-on: Both routes coexist beyond 3× BGP hold-timer, neighbor PE fails to withdraw, no alarm raised, MAC Mobility EC is present on route with Default Gateway EC, or gateway MAC unreachable from legitimate hosts.

00d04b7dc7c0c3af.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VLAN-Aware Bundle EVPN service (service-type vlan-aware-bundle) with VLANs 100, 200, 300 on DUT and neighbor PE. Configure matching Ethernet Segment on agg-eth interfaces. IXIA sends continuous broadcast to multihomed CE. Record baseline DF state (`show evpn ethernet-segment <esi>` on both PEs).
Action: Power-cycle DUT. After full recovery, power-cycle DUT again.
Verify: After each power-cycle, verify `show evpn ethernet-segment <esi>` on both PEs shows same DF-per-VLAN assignments as baseline (proving deterministic DF election using VLAN 100 in modulo per RFC7432bis §8.5). Verify IXIA broadcast traffic resumes after each cycle.
00d04b7dc7c0c3af.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
00d04b7dc7c0c3af.expectation:
Pass:    DF assignments are identical after each power-cycle (deterministic DF election using numerically lowest VLAN 100 in modulo); broadcast traffic resumes each time
Fail-on: DF assignments differ across power-cycles (non-deterministic or wrong VLAN tag in modulo), or DF state stuck, or broadcast traffic fails to resume

00f1fea021ec2b1f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure vlan-based EVPN instance with local access port, establish BGP EVPN to neighbor PE. Send traffic from local port (learn local MACs) and from remote PE (learn remote MACs). Verify `show evpn mac address-table` shows both local-learned and remote-learned dynamic entries before the test.
Action: Execute `clear evpn mac address-table`.
Verify: `show evpn mac address-table` confirms local-learned dynamic MACs are cleared. Remote-learned MACs persist. Resume traffic on local port; local MACs re-learn within 10 seconds. `show evpn` and `show bgp evpn summary` confirm the instance and BGP peering remain operational.
00f1fea021ec2b1f.expectation:
Pass:    Local dynamic MAC entries cleared immediately; remote MACs unchanged; local MACs re-learn correctly; no control-plane disruption.
Fail-on: Local MACs not cleared, remote MACs incorrectly cleared, local MACs fail to re-learn, or EVPN/BGP state disrupted.

012c7a97be4e6a7d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance with iBGP sessions to two neighbor PEs (or IXIA emulating two PEs). Both peers advertise identical RT-2 route for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 in EVI 100, but PE1 sends local-preference 100, PE2 sends local-preference 200.
Action: Advertise RT-2 MAC route from both PEs with differing BGP path attributes (local-pref 100 vs 200, or AS-path length 1 vs 2, or MED 50 vs 100). Send L2 data frames destined to MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from IXIA access port.
Verify: Execute `show bgp evpn route-type 2 [mac 00:11:22:33:44:55]` and `show bgp evpn route-type 2 detail` — confirm DUT received both routes, and the route with superior attributes per RFC4271 §9.1 (higher local-pref or shorter AS-path) is marked best ('>' or 'best' flag). Capture forwarded frames — verify DUT forwards traffic toward the best-path PE (PE2 if local-pref 200 wins).
012c7a97be4e6a7d.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE (IXIA must emulate two BGP EVPN peers advertising overlapping routes with controllable path attributes)
012c7a97be4e6a7d.expectation:
Pass:    DUT applies RFC4271 BGP best-path algorithm to EVPN RT-2 routes: selects route with local-pref 200 (or shorter AS-path, or lower MED per tie-break order) as best; show output marks it with best-path indicator; data frames forwarded to best-path next-hop PE.
Fail-on: DUT selects inferior route as best path, ignores BGP path attributes (local-pref/AS-path/MED), load-balances when only one best path exists, or forwards traffic to non-best-path PE. Missing best-path marker in CLI output.

013a7c2006308f6d.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with no EVPN instances configured; LAG interface agg-lag 1 created and admin up; Ethernet interface x-eth 0/0/1 admin up.
Action: Enter configuration / l2-services mode; create EVPN instance with command "evpn evpn-port-test service-type port-based"; configure auto-discovery with import-rt 65000:100 and export-rt 65000:100; attach agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 to evpn-port-test; commit.
Verify: Execute "show running-config l2-services" and confirm evpn-port-test appears with service-type port-based; execute "show evpn instance evpn-port-test" and confirm both agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 are listed as bound interfaces with operational state.
013a7c2006308f6d.expectation:
Pass:    show running-config displays "evpn evpn-port-test service-type port-based" with both interfaces bound; show evpn instance displays operational state active/up for evpn-port-test with both agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 members.
Fail-on: Configuration commit fails; service-type displays as vlan-based, vlan-bundle, or vlan-aware-bundle instead of port-based; either agg-lag 1 or x-eth 0/0/1 not listed in bound interfaces; show evpn instance reports error or down state.

014b087446401268.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with multi-homed ES (agg-eth with ethernet-segment, load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving default). Establish EVPN session with remote PE. Send unknown unicast (unlearned destination MAC) in BUM traffic via MPLS from remote PE with P2MP label or ingress-replication downstream label.
Action: Trigger BUM flooding by injecting unknown unicast frames. Collect tech-support bundle using `show tech-support evpn` and `show tech-support l2-services`.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: `show evpn instance <EVI>` (BUM replication mode, P-tunnel attribute), `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` (DF status, split-horizon label), `show l2-services forwarding-database <BD>` (MAC entries), `show mpls forwarding-table` (P2MP LSP or ingress-replication labels), syslog entries for DF election, running-config snippet showing `load-balancing-mode all-active` and `service-carving default`.
014b087446401268.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
014b087446401268.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: EVPN instance with P-tunnel attribute (P2MP or ingress-replication), DF status = true for the ES, split-horizon label in `show evpn ethernet-segment`, MPLS forwarding entry matching the downstream/P2MP label, and MAC table showing absence of unknown destination (justifying flood). Syslog includes DF election completion.
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn instance` output, missing DF election status, missing split-horizon label, missing MPLS forwarding-table entries for BUM labels, or missing running-config for ES (ethernet-segment, load-balancing-mode, service-carving).

0177f01b121d473c.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology with multi-homed CE; configure `ethernet-segment` on `agg-eth 0` at both PEs with `identifier 0 <type0-value>` and `load-balancing-mode all-active`; PE1 is designated forwarder for the EVPN instance; configure ingress replication for BUM traffic; IXIA connected to remote PE (sending traffic) and to CE (receiving traffic).
Action: IXIA sends unknown unicast traffic (destination MAC not in PE1's MAC table) at line rate via remote PE with MPLS label indicating BUM forwarding; traffic volume: 10 Gbps sustained for 300 seconds; packet size sweep: 64, 512, 1500 bytes.
Verify: Capture PE1 control-plane CPU utilization, forwarding latency (IXIA timestamp analysis), and packet loss; use `show evpn ethernet-segment` to confirm PE1 DF role; confirm no MAC learning or lookup events in PE1 logs during the test window.
0177f01b121d473c.expectation:
Pass:    Throughput ≥ 99.9% of line rate (9.99 Gbps), forwarding latency ≤ 50 µs (mean) and ≤ 200 µs (99th percentile), packet loss ≤ 0.001%, control-plane CPU ≤ 15% (confirming no MAC lookup overhead per RFC7432bis ch.13.2.1 default behavior).
Fail-on: Packet loss > 0.001%, latency spikes > 200 µs indicating MAC table lookups, throughput < 9.99 Gbps, or control-plane CPU > 15%.

0185f3dba789c6b2.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with aggregate ethernet interface configured (agg-eth 0); peer device connected to member ports running LACP; no prior `lacp-system-mac` configuration (default: LACP System ID uses the interface's LAG MAC address).
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0` mode; issue `lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55`; commit. Capture LACP PDUs on a member interface. Issue `no lacp-system-mac`; commit; verify LACP re-negotiates.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` includes `lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55`; captured LACP PDU Actor System field = 00:11:22:33:44:55; after `no` form, config line removed and LACP PDU Actor System reverts to default LAG MAC address.
0185f3dba789c6b2.equipment: DUT + peer device with LACP capability (IXIA traffic generator or neighbor PE)
0185f3dba789c6b2.expectation:
Pass:    Custom MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55) persists in running-config; LACP Actor System field in PDUs matches configured value; `no lacp-system-mac` removes config and restores default LAG MAC in both running-config and protocol behavior.
Fail-on: Commit rejection; custom MAC not reflected in LACP PDUs; `no` form fails to remove config line; LAG down after configuration change; or default LAG MAC not restored in LACP Actor System field.

01f0ada275077db1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE with 2+ EVIs (EVI 100, 200), each with distinct RD (Type 1 format: <IP>:<nn> per Section 7.9); enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor; verify `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default) to trigger IMET route origination; configure router-id (e.g., loopback0) as Originating Router IP source.
Action: Confirm IMET (Type-3) routes advertised via show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3; collect tech-support bundle including BGP EVPN routes, l2-services config, routing config, syslog.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 output (RD field, Originating Router IP field, IP Address Length field per EVI); running-config (RD per EVI, router-id, af-l2vpn evpn); syslog (EVPN/BGP events).
01f0ada275077db1.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains IMET routes for each EVI with valid RD format per Section 7.9; Originating Router IP field is identical across all EVIs (matches router-id/loopback0); IP Address Length = 32 (IPv4) or 128 (IPv6); all show commands, configs, and logs present.
Fail-on: Missing show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 output; IMET routes absent when unknown-mac-flooding enabled; Originating Router IP differs between EVIs; RD missing/malformed; IP Address Length incorrect/absent; incomplete running-config or syslog in bundle.

01f45f5aa9017410.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVI 100, bridge-domain MTU 1500, control-word disabled (`configuration / l2-services / evpn; control-word disable`). Configure neighbor PE with same EVI 100, RT, but MTU 9000 and control-word enabled. Establish BGP EVPN AF (`configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor; af-l2vpn evpn`).
Action: Neighbor PE advertises EVPN type 2 (MAC/IP) routes with L2-Attr extended community containing MTU=9000 (non-zero) and C-flag=1. DUT receives and processes the BGP update.
Verify: On DUT, run `show evpn instance 100 detail` (neighbor PE absent from destination list), `show l2-services evpn routes received` (route flagged as incompatible due to MTU or control-word mismatch), `show evpn mac all` (remote MAC addresses NOT installed). Check syslog for L2 attribute mismatch notification.
01f45f5aa9017410.expectation:
Pass:    DUT receives routes but does NOT add neighbor PE to EVPN destination list for EVI 100; `show evpn instance` confirms no remote PE forwarding entry; syslog logs MTU mismatch (1500 ≠ 9000) and/or control-word mismatch; remote MAC addresses not in local MAC table.
Fail-on: DUT installs routes despite L2 attribute mismatch; neighbor PE appears in `show evpn instance` destination list; traffic forwards to mismatched PE; no operator notification (syslog/trap) of mismatch.

022d4b4411da4d57.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: DUT, neighbor PE-1 (advertises Ethernet A-D per EVI route with EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community + P-flag=1), neighbor PE-2 (advertises same route without Layer 2 Attributes extended community + P-flag=1); both PEs multi-homed to remote ES (ESI 01:22:22:22:22:22:22:22:22:22); IXIA generates 10 Gbps L2 traffic to 10k MAC addresses behind remote ES.
Action: Withdraw PE-1's route via `configuration / routing / bgp / neighbor <PE-1> / shutdown`; measure time until DUT reconverges to PE-2's route and traffic loss; restore PE-1 after 60 seconds; repeat cycle 10 times to measure average convergence performance.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 01:22:22:22:22:22:22:22:22:22 detail` confirms DUT selects PE-1 (Layer 2 Attributes present) per §7.13.2(1.1) when both routes active; `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms aliasing state; IXIA reports per-cycle convergence time and packet loss.
022d4b4411da4d57.expectation:
Pass:    DUT always selects PE-1's route when both active (Layer 2 Attributes preference per §7.13.2 ordering); average convergence time ≤ 1 second (PE-1 withdraw → PE-2 installed); packet loss ≤ 0.1% per convergence event; all 10 cycles meet thresholds.
Fail-on: DUT selects PE-2 while PE-1 active (violates §7.13.2 criterion ordering); any convergence event > 1 second; packet loss > 0.1% in any cycle; or traffic blackhole during transition.

0245128b44bb081c.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology; configure `host mac-address-duplication-detection enable move-interval <M> move-count <N> freeze-time <F>` on both PEs; attach IXIA ports behind each PE emulating hosts in same VLAN/EVI; initially use distinct MAC addresses.
Action: Change both IXIA hosts to the same MAC address and generate bi-directional traffic to trigger N MAC move events within M seconds; after detection threshold is hit, run `tech-support` and save bundle off-box.
Verify: Inspect bundle for `show evpn duplicate-mac` output listing the frozen MAC, syslog alert to operator, `show bgp l2vpn evpn` showing no new MAC/IP Advertisement routes for the duplicate MAC, and running-config showing the configured move-interval/move-count/freeze-time values.
0245128b44bb081c.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
0245128b44bb081c.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains evidence of the duplicate MAC in frozen state, operator alert (syslog/alarm), BGP advertisement cessation for that MAC, and the configured M/N threshold values.
Fail-on: Bundle missing `show evpn duplicate-mac` output, no operator alert logged, or BGP MAC/IP routes still being advertised/processed for the frozen MAC.

0290f6db0548d1d5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment on PE1 (DUT) and PE2 with `load-balancing-mode all-active`, assign ESI identifier (type 0 or 1), enable P2MP MPLS LSPs for BUM forwarding, attach IXIA to the ES.
Action: Trigger PE1 (non-DF) to advertise Ethernet A-D per ES route (RT-1) with upstream-assigned ESI label; send BUM traffic from remote PE3 encapsulated with PE1's advertised ESI label.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 1` confirms RT-1 with ESI label from PE1; `show mpls forwarding-table label <esi-label>` on PE2 shows label programmed in PE1's context space; IXIA captures confirm BUM packets received with ESI label are NOT forwarded onto the Ethernet segment (split-horizon enforced); `show mpls forwarding-table` on PE3 shows label programmed.
0290f6db0548d1d5.equipment: DUT (PE1) + neighbor PE (PE2, co-attached to same ESI) + remote PE (PE3, imports route but not on ESI) + IXIA (attached to ES for BUM traffic source/capture)
0290f6db0548d1d5.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 programs PE1's ESI label in PE1's context label space; BUM traffic received by PE2 with this label is NOT forwarded to the ES (0 packets egress on ES interface per `show interface agg-eth <id> statistics`); remote PE3 programs the label.
Fail-on: PE2 forwards BUM traffic with ESI label back onto the ES (split-horizon violated); label not programmed in MPLS forwarding table on PE2 or PE3; RT-1 route missing ESI label attribute.

02d2f40aac76ccc6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE with EVPN instance EVI 100, RD 1.1.1.1:100; establish BGP sessions to three neighbor PEs (PE1=2.2.2.2, PE2=3.3.3.3, PE3=4.4.4.4) with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled; configure `import-rt 100:100` under VPLS auto-discovery; enable `advertise-mac enable` under l2-services/evpn.
Action: PE1 advertises Type-2 route (MAC 00:00:00:00:00:01/IP 192.168.1.10) with Default Gateway extended community (0x000d); PE2 advertises same MAC/IP with Default Gateway community and local next-hop (same ES/site); PE3 advertises same MAC/IP without Default Gateway community. Observe best path selection via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-advertisement mac 00:00:00:00:00:01 detail`. Withdraw PE2 route, verify re-selection to PE1; withdraw PE1, verify fallback to PE3.
Verify: DUT best path initially points to PE2 (next-hop 3.3.3.3, path marked with '>' in BGP table). After PE2 withdrawal, best path switches to PE1 (2.2.2.2). After PE1 withdrawal, best path switches to PE3 (4.4.4.4). Confirm selection order via `show evpn mac table` and BGP best-path reason codes.
02d2f40aac76ccc6.equipment: DUT + 3 neighbor PEs (or IXIA emulating 3 BGP EVPN peers with Type-2 route injection and Default Gateway community control)
02d2f40aac76ccc6.expectation:
Pass:    Best path selection strictly follows §7.13.1 order: (1) routes with Default Gateway community preferred over routes without; (2) among Default Gateway routes, local next-hop (PE2) selected over remote (PE1); (3) route without Default Gateway (PE3) used only when no Default Gateway routes available. Each withdrawal triggers immediate re-selection per criteria sequence.
Fail-on: DUT selects PE3 (no Default Gateway) while PE1 or PE2 available; DUT selects PE1 (remote Default Gateway) over PE2 (local Default Gateway); selection order does not match RFC7432bis §7.13.1 sequence; no re-selection on withdrawal.

02f452987c102c04.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure loopback 0 with IPv4 address 10.0.0.1/32; enter `configuration / l2-services` mode.
Action: Create EVPN instance using `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle`; do NOT manually configure RD.
Verify: Execute `show evpn evpn-1` and `show running-config l2-services` to inspect the auto-generated RD; parse RD encoding to confirm Type field = 1, Administrator subfield = 10.0.0.1 (loopback 0 IP), and Assigned Number subfield is a non-zero system-generated unique value.
02f452987c102c04.expectation:
Pass:    RD is auto-generated in RFC4364 Type 1 format `1:<loopback0-IP>:<instance-number>` (e.g., `1:10.0.0.1:1`); Type field = 1, Administrator = loopback 0 IPv4 (10.0.0.1), Assigned Number is unique and non-zero.
Fail-on: RD not present, RD Type != 1, Administrator subfield != loopback 0 IP, Assigned Number = 0 or duplicates another EVPN instance, or system requires manual RD configuration.

030a199e55ee7259.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with identical ESI type-0 (identifier 0 01.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88) on agg-eth interfaces facing CE; set load-balancing-mode all-active on both; enable EVPN instance with MAC/IP route advertisement; IXIA generates 1 Gbps continuous bidirectional L2/L3 traffic from CE-attached hosts to remote EVPN destinations.
Action: Trigger EVPN control-plane process restart on DUT (platform-specific CLI, e.g., 'debug process restart evpn'); measure ES recovery time from down to operational and capture IXIA packet loss duration.
Verify: 'show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88' transitions to operational status; 'show bgp evpn route-type 4 esi 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88' confirms Type-4 ES route re-advertised; 'show bgp evpn route-type 1' shows Type-1 EAD routes present; 'show evpn esi' confirms all-active forwarding resumed; IXIA traffic analyzer reports packet loss duration.
030a199e55ee7259.expectation:
Pass:    ES recovers to operational within es-waiting-time (default 3s) + 2s BGP convergence; Type-1 and Type-4 routes re-advertised within 5s total; all-active load-balancing automatically restored; IXIA-measured traffic loss ≤ 1s; no manual intervention required.
Fail-on: ES stuck in down state >10s after process restart; Type-1 or Type-4 routes missing after 10s; IXIA traffic loss >1s; all-active mode fails to restore (falls back to single-homed or single-active); feature requires manual 'no ethernet-segment' / 'ethernet-segment' config toggle to recover.

032eb0111313c645.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure All-Active ES on two PEs: DUT `configuration/interface/agg-eth/ethernet-segment` with `identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09` and `load-balancing-mode all-active`; mirror config on neighbor PE with same ES identifier. Configure matching EVI instance on both PEs.
Action: Flap the BGP session between DUT and neighbor PE using `clear bgp neighbor <peer-ip>`. Capture BGP UPDATE messages during reconvergence for Ethernet A-D per EVI route (type 1). Parse L2-Attr extended community (type 0x8004) in each PE's advertisement, focusing on P-bit (bit 0 of flags octet) during session re-establishment and DF re-election.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route type 1 detail` on both PEs to confirm L2-Attr presence. Wireshark capture on BGP peering to decode P-bit encoding in L2-Attr extended community across 3 snapshots: pre-flap, mid-convergence, post-convergence.
032eb0111313c645.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + packet capture on BGP peering link. IXIA emulates LACP partner CE multi-homed to both PEs to trigger ES formation and Ethernet A-D route generation.
032eb0111313c645.expectation:
Pass:    Throughout BGP flap and reconvergence, both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI routes with L2-Attr extended community where P bit = 1 (per RFC7432bis §8.6 MUST for All-Active mode). DF election completes within `es-waiting-time` default (3 sec); no P-bit toggling observed.
Fail-on: Any PE in the All-Active redundancy group advertises L2-Attr with P bit = 0, or L2-Attr extended community is omitted from type 1 routes, or P-bit value flaps during BGP convergence, or DF election stalls beyond es-waiting-time.

037b6c3e90e42632.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES on DUT (PE1) and neighbor PE (PE2) with identical ESI (e.g., 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11) via `ethernet-segment` under agg-eth interface; create EVPN instance with `advertise-mac enable`; establish BGP EVPN session using `af-l2vpn evpn` in neighbor config; assign matching import-rt/export-rt.
Action: Bring up ES; run `show bgp evpn routes type 4` to capture Type 4 (Ethernet A-D per ES) route; decode ESI Label Extended Community to extract LT4 label value; send L2 broadcast frame from IXIA CE attached to non-DF PE; IXIA captures MPLS-encapsulated BUM forwarded PE↔PE on NNI.
Verify: Type 4 route on both PEs contains ESI Label Extended Community per RFC7432bis ch.7.1 with allocated LT4; captured BUM frame shows MPLS label stack: [VPN label (LT3)][ESI label (LT4)][Ethernet payload]; LT4 value in packet matches `show evpn ethernet-segment` allocated label.
037b6c3e90e42632.expectation:
Pass:    Type 4 route advertises ESI Label Extended Community with non-zero LT4 label; non-DF→DF BUM frame has label stack [LT3][LT4] in order; LT4 value consistent between control plane (Type 4) and data plane (IXIA capture).
Fail-on: Type 4 route missing ESI Label Extended Community; BUM forwarding omits LT4 label or places it before LT3; LT4 label value in data plane ≠ advertised label; non-DF PE strips LT4 when forwarding to DF.

03816f3f729a4c6f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE in multi-homing: same EVPN instance (evpn-100), same ESI (00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99), enable `auto-discovery`, set matching `import-rt` / `export-rt`, enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbors.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN peering; execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi <value>` on both PEs to capture ESI Label in Extended Community; use IXIA to inject Type-1 route for same ESI with different ESI Label value; then withdraw or correct the mismatched route.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi <value>` confirms all Type-1 routes carry identical ESI Label; `show alarms` or `show system notifications` captures mismatch alarm appearance and subsequent clearing; `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms mismatched route saved but not used in forwarding.
03816f3f729a4c6f.expectation:
Pass:    All Type-1 routes for the same ES advertise identical ESI Label value in Extended Community (per RFC7432bis §8.2.1); injecting a Type-1 route with different ESI Label triggers "Mismatched Configuration" alarm; alarm clears when mismatch resolved (route withdrawn or corrected); DUT saves mismatched route but excludes it from split-horizon forwarding.
Fail-on: ESI Label values differ across legitimate Type-1 advertisements from DUT and neighbor PE, or no alarm raised on mismatch injection, or alarm persists after mismatch resolution, or mismatched route used in active forwarding path.

03b499946121cf2c.action_steps:
Setup: Configure 3-PE EVPN topology: PE1 and PE2 multihomed to CE-A via Ethernet Segment (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99), PE3 remote with CE-B attached. Enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on all BGP peers, configure matching `import-rt` and `export-rt`, verify `advertise-mac enable` (default).
Action: (1) Capture Ethernet A-D Per EVPN Instance (Type 1) BGP UPDATE from PE1/PE2 to PE3; decode to check L2-Attr Extended Community presence. (2) Verify PE3 behavior: if at least one Type 1 lacks L2-Attr, apply RFC7432 ch.14.1.1; if all contain L2-Attr, apply RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 (deduce redundancy from ESI Label extended community). (3) From CE-A send IPv4 (10.0.0.1 → 10.0.0.2) and IPv6 (2001:db8::1 → 2001:db8::2) frames to CE-B. (4) Capture Type 2 BGP UPDATEs on PE1→PE3; verify NLRI per RFC7432bis §7.2: RD(8B) + ESI(10B) + Eth-Tag(4B) + MAC-len(48) + MAC(6B) + IP-len(32/128) + IP(4B/16B) + Label1(3B). (5) Verify PE3 MAC table has both addresses with labels. (6) Send return traffic; verify MPLS-encapsulated forwarding.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 detail` (L2-Attr check), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 detail` (Type 2 structure), `show evpn mac address-table` (learned MACs + labels), `show evpn ethernet-segment` (redundancy mode), packet captures on BGP and data sessions.
03b499946121cf2c.expectation:
Pass: L2-Attr Extended Community correctly detected in Type 1 routes; PE3 applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 when absent, RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 when present. Type 2 routes for IPv4 and IPv6 match §7.2 encoding (MAC-len=48, IP-len=32/128, all mandatory fields present). PE3 MAC table populated with both entries and MPLS labels. Bidirectional traffic forwards using learned labels.
Fail-on: L2-Attr misdetected or ignored, incorrect RFC logic applied, malformed Type 2 (wrong field lengths or missing RD/ESI/Eth-Tag/IP/Label), MAC not learned for either address family, or forwarding failure in either direction.

03c7dab489216d7b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS instance `vpls-100` with `auto-discovery` and `export-rt 65000:100` under `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-100 / auto-discovery`; establish BGP L2VPN EVPN session with a neighbor PE; verify neighbor PE's `show bgp l2vpn evpn` displays routes from DUT with extended community RT 65000:100.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-100 / auto-discovery`, issue `no export-rt 65000:100`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-100 auto-discovery` no longer displays `export-rt 65000:100`; on neighbor PE, `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms routes from DUT either lack RT 65000:100 (replaced by auto-derived RT if configured) or are withdrawn if no alternative export-rt remains.
03c7dab489216d7b.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP L2VPN EVPN peer)
03c7dab489216d7b.expectation:
Pass:    `export-rt 65000:100` absent from DUT running-config; neighbor PE BGP RIB shows DUT EVPN routes without extended community RT 65000:100 or routes withdrawn
Fail-on: `export-rt 65000:100` persists in running-config after commit, or neighbor PE BGP RIB still shows routes with RT 65000:100, or BGP session resets during config change

042e634454135d11.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure syslog server destination (e.g. `logging host <ip> port 514`); configure EVPN instance with `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300`; attach syslog collector.
Action: Inject duplicate MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from IXIA (>5 moves within 180s to trigger duplicate-MAC alarm ON state); wait for freeze-time expiry (300s) to transition alarm OFF.
Verify: Parse syslog messages: alarm ON event shows severity=warning (facility.level 4); alarm OFF event shows severity=informational (facility.level 6); messages include EVI ID, MAC address, and state transition.
042e634454135d11.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + syslog collector
042e634454135d11.expectation:
Pass:    Syslog message for duplicate-MAC alarm OFF=>ON transition sent with severity=warning; alarm ON=>OFF transition sent with severity=informational; both messages contain EVPN instance identifier and offending MAC.
Fail-on: Missing syslog message for either state transition; incorrect severity (not warning for ON, not informational for OFF); message sent before actual alarm state change; message lacks EVI or MAC context.

04d3bfacdd27ef76.action_steps:
Setup:  Create EVPN service `l2-services / evpn EVPN-100 service-type vlan-based`; attach interface `interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1` under EVPN-100; IXIA sends L2 traffic to DUT on GigabitEthernet0/0/1 to populate MAC table; verify MACs appear in `show evpn mac-table`.
Action: Under EVPN-100 config mode, issue `no interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN-100` excludes the interface; `show evpn EVPN-100 detail` does not list it as attachment circuit; `show evpn mac-table` shows no MACs learned on GigabitEthernet0/0/1; `show forwarding-table` contains no entries for that interface.
04d3bfacdd27ef76.expectation:
Pass:    Interface removed from EVPN service; running-config, operational state, MAC table, and forwarding table all clean (no references to GigabitEthernet0/0/1).
Fail-on: Interface persists in `show running-config`, listed in `show evpn detail`, or MAC/forwarding entries remain associated with the interface after removal.

04ff28c22da6ce14.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE EVPN topology (EVI 100) with VXLAN tunnels; confirm `advertise-mac enable` (default) active on all PEs via `show running-config l2-services evpn`; attach Host1 to PE1 x-eth 0/0/1, Host2 to PE2 x-eth 0/0/2, Host3 to PE3 x-eth 0/0/3; generate bidirectional traffic to learn all MAC addresses (verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn mac` lists all three MACs with correct next-hops).
Action: Send 100 known-unicast frames from Host1 to Host2 (Host2's MAC already present in PE1's forwarding table via Type 2 MAC/IP advertisement route); simultaneously capture packets on PE1→PE2 VXLAN tunnel, PE1→PE3 VXLAN tunnel, and PE2's egress access port x-eth 0/0/2.
Verify: Inspect `show bgp l2vpn evpn mac` on PE1 confirms Host2's MAC with PE2 next-hop; decode captured VXLAN frames on PE1→PE2 tunnel (verify VNI = 100, inner Ethernet DA = Host2 MAC, SA = Host1 MAC); confirm zero unicast frames on PE1→PE3 tunnel (no flooding per RFC7432bis ch.13); verify Host2 receives all 100 frames (IXIA ingress vs. egress counter match).
04ff28c22da6ce14.expectation:
Pass:    All 100 unicast frames forwarded PE1→PE2 only (zero on PE1→PE3 tunnel); PE1 MAC table lookup succeeds (Host2 MAC resolves to PE2 next-hop); VXLAN encapsulation valid (VNI 100, inner DA/SA intact); Host2 IXIA port shows 100 received frames matching 100 sent from Host1.
Fail-on: Any unicast frame appears on PE1→PE3 tunnel (unicast treated as BUM/flooded), MAC table lookup miss at PE1, incorrect VNI in VXLAN header, modified inner Ethernet headers (DA/SA/payload), or frame count mismatch at Host2 egress (indicates loss).

051912cd7ff97f1f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two EVPN instances: `configure`, `l2-services`, `evpn bd-test-1 service-type vlan-based`, `exit`, `evpn bd-test-2 service-type vlan-bundle`, `commit`.
Action: Run `show evpn broadcast-domains`.
Verify: Output displays both `bd-test-1` (vlan-based) and `bd-test-2` (vlan-bundle) with their respective service-type values and operational state.
051912cd7ff97f1f.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
051912cd7ff97f1f.expectation:
Pass:    Command lists all configured EVPN broadcast domains with name, service-type, and state columns; both test instances appear.
Fail-on: Command fails, omits a configured broadcast domain, or displays incorrect service-type.

060d1cc3748821b8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with matching ES identifier (type 0: 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09) on agg-eth interfaces to shared CE; set 'service-carving highest-random-weight', 'es-waiting-time 3' (default); test both 'load-balancing-mode single-active' (default, verify DF election) and 'load-balancing-mode all-active'.
Action: Start IXIA bidirectional traffic (1 Gbps, 64-byte frames); shutdown the DF/active PE's agg-eth interface in each mode; monitor BGP Type 4 ES route withdrawal (RFC7432bis §14 mass-withdrawal mechanism).
Verify: Execute 'show evpn ethernet-segment' (DF role change in single-active, both active in all-active), 'show evpn routes type 4' (mass-withdrawal route present), 'show l2-services forwarding-db' (next-hops updated to surviving PE); measure IXIA packet loss duration.
060d1cc3748821b8.expectation:
Pass:    Both modes: traffic converges within es-waiting-time (3 sec default) + BGP propagation (≤ 6 sec total); Type 4 ES route sent. Single-active: standby PE becomes DF, no duplicate forwarding. All-active: traffic redistributes to surviving PE.
Fail-on: Convergence > 10 sec in either mode; stale FIB entries to failed PE persist after 10 sec; traffic blackhole beyond convergence window; single-active: duplicate forwarding or stuck DF election; all-active: traffic not redistributed.

06524575e7d9c813.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure three ethernet segments on the DUT: Type 0 (manual ESI on `x-eth 0/0/1`), Type 1 (LACP-derived ESI on `agg-eth 0` connected to LACP partner), and Type 4 (single-home ESI on `x-eth 0/0/2`). Configure EVIs and bring all segments operational. Establish multi-homing with neighbor PE for Type 0 and Type 1 segments.
Action: Run `tech-support` command to generate diagnostic bundle. Save the bundle off-box. Extract and inspect contents for ESI-related configuration, operational state, and LACP correlation data.
Verify: Confirm bundle contains: (1) `show running-config` with all three ESI type configurations, (2) `show evpn ethernet-segment` listing all three segments with type and value, (3) `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` showing type discriminator and interface binding, (4) LACP state output for Type 1 showing LAG system-id and key correlation to ESI, (5) syslog entries and FIB state for all segments.
06524575e7d9c813.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + LACP partner + IXIA
06524575e7d9c813.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle captures complete state for all three ESI types: Type 0 shows manual 10-byte ESI value, Type 1 shows LACP-derived ESI with LAG correlation data, Type 4 shows router-id-based ESI with local discriminator.
Fail-on: Any ESI type missing from bundle, or critical data absent (Type 1 missing LACP correlation, ESI type field not displayed, interface-to-ESI binding missing, or FIB entries omitted).

066dcfe688a86a2b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance (e.g., `evpn EVPN1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle`) using Type 1 RD (format <loopback-IP>:<2-octet-number>, e.g., 192.0.2.1:100). Configure ESI on multi-homed interface (e.g., Type 1 LACP: 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99). Enable `auto-discovery`; configure `import-rt` / `export-rt`. Configure neighbor PE identically except different RD (e.g., 192.0.2.2:100). Establish iBGP session between DUT and neighbor PE (or via route reflector); enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor.
Action: Bring up multi-homed interface on both PEs. Allow BGP EVPN route exchange (≥30 seconds).
Verify: On DUT, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` and locate ES route for configured ESI. Inspect: (1) RD format is Type 1 (IP:2octet, e.g., 192.0.2.1:100, where IP = DUT loopback); (2) ESI field = 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99; (3) Originating Router's IP Address = DUT loopback (192.0.2.1); (4) IP Address Length field = 32 bits (IPv4).
066dcfe688a86a2b.expectation:
Pass:    ES route present; RD is Type 1 format (<loopback-IP>:<2-octet-number>); ESI matches configured ESI; Originating Router's IP = DUT loopback; IP Address Length = 32 (IPv4) or 128 (IPv6).
Fail-on: ES route missing, RD is Type 0 (2-octet-admin:2-octet) or Type 2 (4-octet-ASN:2-octet), ESI mismatch, Originating Router's IP ≠ DUT loopback or = 0.0.0.0, or IP Address Length incorrect.

069b43d2b735795c.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE each configure EVPN instance (`evpn EVPN1 service-type vlan-based`) with multi-homed Ethernet Segment (ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00); enable `auto-discovery`, configure matching `import-rt` / `export-rt`; establish iBGP session with `af-l2vpn evpn` activated.
Action: Both PEs advertise Type 1 route with unique RD, shared ESI, ET ID = 0xFFFFFFFF (MAX-ET), MPLS Label = 0, ESI Label Extended Community = 5000; capture BGP UPDATEs (AFI 25 / SAFI 70); inject Type 1 route from 3rd-party PE with mismatched ESI Label (e.g., 6000) for same ESI; observe DUT alarm and route quarantine behavior per RFC7432bis §8.2.1; align label back to 5000 and verify alarm clears.
Verify: Decode BGP UPDATE MP_REACH_NLRI fields (RD, ESI, ET ID, ESI Label Extended Community); run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` on DUT; confirm routes with ESI Label = 5000 installed and used for split-horizon filtering; verify mismatched-label route saved but excluded from forwarding plane; check alarm raised on mismatch and auto-clears when label value aligns.
069b43d2b735795c.expectation:
Pass:    DUT and 3rd-party PE exchange Type 1 routes with consistent ESI Label (5000) for ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00; routes installed in EVPN table; split-horizon active (BUM traffic from local ES not looped back); when ESI Label mismatch injected, DUT saves route, excludes it from forwarding, raises config-mismatch alarm; alarm clears and route activates when label aligns.
Fail-on: DUT rejects valid Type 1 route from 3rd-party PE, installs mismatched ESI Label route without alarm or quarantine, incorrectly decodes ET ID ≠ 0xFFFFFFFF or Single-Active flag, forwards BUM back to source ES (split-horizon broken), or alarm persists after label alignment.

06d52ab75e0c4c9f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP with a neighbor, enable af-l2vpn evpn under the neighbor context, configure an EVPN instance with service-type vlan-based, and establish the BGP session to exchange EVPN routes.
Action: Issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <neighbor-ip> advertised-routes` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <neighbor-ip> received-routes` to display EVPN NLRI sent to and received from the peer.
Verify: Output displays EVPN routes with Route Type, RD, NLRI fields (ESI, MAC, IP as applicable), and label information; cross-check advertised routes against local EVPN configuration and received routes against peer's advertisements.
06d52ab75e0c4c9f.expectation:
Pass:    Both advertised-routes and received-routes render EVPN-specific NLRI fields (Route Type, RD, ESI/MAC/IP, labels) in the documented tabular format without parser errors.
Fail-on: Parser error, empty output when EVPN routes are configured and exchanged, or missing EVPN route-type and NLRI fields.
06d52ab75e0c4c9f.req_id: CLI:show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors advertised/received routes
06d52ab75e0c4c9f.sub_category: show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors advertised/received routes

06e3162195de89cf.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two-PE topology: PE1 (DUT) and PE2 (neighbor) both in EVPN instance "EVP-100", RD 100:1, `export-rt 100:1`. Multi-home CE to both PEs via ESI Type-0 (00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99), All-Active redundancy mode configured. Enable `auto-discovery` under EVPN on both PEs. Establish iBGP session PE1↔PE2 with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled.
Action: Activate EVPN instance on PE1 to trigger Ethernet A-D per-ES route (Route Type 1) advertisement to PE2. Use IXIA (inline or span port) to capture BGP UPDATE message from PE1→PE2 (filter: tcp port 179). Decode Extended Communities path attribute in the UPDATE carrying Route Type 1 NLRI for ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99.
Verify: Locate ESI Label Extended Community (8-octet structure) in Path Attribute. Decode octets: [0]=Type=0x06, [1]=Sub-Type=0x01, [2]=Flags with bits 6-7 (RED)=0b00 for All-Active mode, bits 0-5=0 (MBZ), [3-4]=Reserved=0x0000, [5-7]=ESI Label (3 octets, high-order 20 bits contain MPLS label ≤0xFFFFF or zero per RFC7432bis §7.5).
06e3162195de89cf.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Label Extended Community present in Route Type 1 UPDATE with Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01, Flags octet RED=0b00 (All-Active) with MBZ bits=0, Reserved octets=0x0000, ESI Label 3-octet field carrying valid 20-bit MPLS label (≤0xFFFFF) or zero.
Fail-on: Community absent from Route Type 1, or encoding error: Type≠0x06, Sub-Type≠0x01, RED bits≠0b00 when All-Active configured, MBZ bits≠0, Reserved octets≠0, or ESI Label high-order 20 bits exceed 0xFFFFF.

06f01f9fc2aa1e4e.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with agg-eth 0 configured; EVPN peering with neighbor PE active; enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` mode.
Action: Issue `identifier 0 AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:11:22:33` to set type 0 manual ESI; commit; verify in running-config and EVPN routes. Issue `no identifier`; commit; verify type 4 default restored.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` for identifier line; show EVPN Ethernet Segments on DUT; capture EVPN Ethernet Segment routes on neighbor PE.
06f01f9fc2aa1e4e.expectation:
Pass:    Type 0 ESI (AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:11:22:33) accepted, persists in running-config, advertised to PE; `no identifier` restores type 4 (router-ID-derived) default in config and advertisements.
Fail-on: Commit error; ESI missing from running-config; ESI wrong/absent in EVPN routes; `no identifier` fails or doesn't restore type 4 default; config/advertisement mismatch.

06fa89fa66e1b35e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn EVI-100 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` with Ethernet Segment on `interface agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment` (multi-homed to neighbor PE); attach VLAN 100 as BD. Enable `auto-discovery` under the EVPN instance. Establish BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE. Configure IXIA to capture BGP UPDATE messages and inject test traffic.
Action: Bring up the agg-eth interface. Capture BGP EVPN UPDATE from DUT containing route-type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI). Parse NLRI to extract: RD, ESI (10-byte), Ethernet Tag ID (expect 100 for vlan-aware-bundle), MPLS Label LT2 (per <EVI, ES, ET> tuple), L2-Attributes Extended Community (Primary/Backup flag), export RTs, Next Hop. Inject traffic from IXIA destined to the multi-homed ES on the remote PE *before* remote PE receives route-type 4 (Ethernet A-D per ES), then repeat after route-type 4 is received.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 evi EVI-100` confirms one route per <EVI, ES, Ethernet-Tag=100>; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows ESI and label allocation. Verify captured BGP UPDATE: Ethernet Tag ID = 100 (not 0), MPLS Label LT2 allocated, Next Hop = DUT loopback IP. Verify remote PE does NOT forward traffic to this ES until it also receives the associated route-type 4 (RFC7432bis §8 MUST NOT constraint): IXIA traffic counters show 0 Rx frames at ES destination before route-type 4, >0 Rx frames after route-type 4.
06fa89fa66e1b35e.expectation:
Pass:    BGP UPDATE contains route-type 1 with all mandatory fields (RD, 10-byte ESI, Ethernet Tag ID=100 for vlan-aware-bundle, MPLS Label LT2 per <EVI, ES, ET>, L2-Attr Extended Community with Primary or Backup flag set, export RTs, Next Hop=DUT loopback). Remote PE forwards 0 frames to the ES before receiving route-type 4, then successfully forwards frames after receiving both route-type 1 and route-type 4 (RFC7432bis §8.4 compliance).
Fail-on: Missing route-type 1 advertisement; Ethernet Tag ID = 0 instead of 100 for vlan-aware-bundle; missing or unallocated MPLS Label LT2; missing L2-Attr Extended Community; both Primary and Backup flags unset; Next Hop ≠ DUT loopback; remote PE forwards traffic to ES using only route-type 1 before route-type 4 is received (violates RFC7432bis §8 MUST NOT forwarding constraint)

07019a347afcd30e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (PE1=DUT, PE2=neighbor) multi-homed to the same CE; configure `ethernet-segment` on PE1 and PE2 agg-eth interfaces with identical ESI (type 0, 10-byte value); configure EVPN instance with `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor; verify PE1 is non-DF and PE2 is DF for the ES (DF election via `show evpn ethernet-segment`).
Action: Enable EVPN (`advertise-mac enable`); capture BGP UPDATE on PE1↔PE2 session filtered for EVPN Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes; inject broadcast frame from CE into PE1 (non-DF); capture MPLS-encapsulated BUM frame on PE1→PE2 core link.
Verify: Parse captured Type 1 route (NLRI = RD + ESI + Eth-Tag) for ESI Label Extended Community (RFC7432bis §7.1); extract LT4 label value; decode MPLS label stack in captured BUM packet; confirm label order is [transport label][LT3 (EVPN instance label)][LT4 (ESI label from Type 1 route)][Ethernet payload].
07019a347afcd30e.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 advertises Type 1 route with ESI Label Extended Community carrying non-zero 20-bit LT4 label (range 16–1048575); BUM packet from non-DF PE1 to DF PE2 has 3-label stack with LT4 matching the advertised ESI label, positioned after LT3.
Fail-on: Type 1 route missing ESI Label Extended Community, OR LT4 = 0 or out of range, OR BUM packet label stack omits LT4, OR LT4 appears before LT3, OR LT4 value differs from advertised ESI label.

071b4070a377679d.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (DUT + neighbor PE) multi-homed to CE via LAG; configure same ESI (identifier 1 type0-value xx.xx...) on both under interface agg-eth / ethernet-segment; set service-carving highest-random-weight on both; configure load-balancing-mode all-active; IXIA generates bidirectional traffic (1000 pps) through multi-homed CE.
Action: Verify DUT is DF for at least one service (show evpn ethernet-segment); power-cycle DUT via PDU harness while traffic flows; after full boot, verify service-carving and ESI configuration intact; capture BGP EVPN Ethernet Segment Route (type 4) advertisements from DUT.
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment on neighbor PE confirms new DF during DUT outage; after DUT recovers, show bgp evpn routes type 4 on DUT includes DF Election Extended Community with DF Alg = highest-random-weight (0x02 per RFC8584); show running-config interface agg-eth ethernet-segment confirms service-carving highest-random-weight persisted; measure traffic loss duration via IXIA.
071b4070a377679d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
071b4070a377679d.expectation:
Pass:    DF re-election completes on neighbor PE within es-waiting-time (3 s default); after DUT reboot, DF Election Extended Community (DF Alg field) advertised correctly as highest-random-weight; service-carving configuration intact; traffic loss ≤ (es-waiting-time + BGP convergence) ~5 s total.
Fail-on: service-carving reverts to default after power-cycle; DF Election Extended Community missing or DF Alg field incorrect in recovered type-4 route; no DF elected (traffic blackhole >10 s); split-brain (both PEs forwarding in single-active mode); configuration lost.

07c0e8346516c659.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment on DUT interface (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, all-active mode) without explicit DF Election Bitmap config to test default; configure BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE (af-l2vpn evpn enabled); bring interface operationally up.
Action: Monitor interface state change to trigger Ethernet Segment Route (Type 4) advertisement; capture BGP UPDATE messages on PE↔PE session using tcpdump or IXIA BGP capture; filter for EVPN NLRI Route Type 4 matching ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99.
Verify: Decode captured Type 4 route per RFC8584 §2.2; extract DF Election Extended Community (Type 0x06, Sub-Type 0x02); confirm Bitmap field (2-octet value at offset +3 in the extended community) equals 32767 (0x7FFF). Optionally reconfigure with explicit bitmap values (0, 100, 65535) and re-verify to validate full range 0–65535.
07c0e8346516c659.expectation:
Pass:    Type 4 route advertised contains DF Election Extended Community with Bitmap = 32767 when unconfigured (default per MUST); explicitly configured Bitmap values 0–65535 encode correctly in transmitted route.
Fail-on: Default Bitmap ≠ 32767, or Bitmap field absent, or configured values outside 0–65535 accepted, or values >65535 truncated/wrapped instead of rejected.

07c43dd9783f1660.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: PE1 and PE2 multi-home to CE1 via agg-eth with same ES `identifier 0 xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx`, both configured `load-balancing-mode all-active`; remote PE3 in same EVI.
Action: Advertise MAC from CE1; at PE3 capture BGP Ethernet A-D per ES routes (type 4) from PE1 and PE2; decode ESI Label extended community octets 6-7 (flags) to verify bit 0 (Multihoming redundancy mode) = 0; send traffic from PE3 to the MAC and capture MPLS label stack.
Verify: PE3 BGP table shows both PE1 and PE2 Ethernet A-D per ES routes with ESI Label EC redundancy-mode = 0; PE3 L2FIB for the MAC lists both PE1 and PE2 as next-hops; egress MPLS label stack matches the label from the MAC/IP Advertisement route (type 2) received from PE1/PE2.
07c43dd9783f1660.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE (minimum 3 PEs: two for All-Active multi-homing, one remote to verify ESI Label EC and next-hop construction)
07c43dd9783f1660.expectation:
Pass:    All Ethernet A-D per ES routes from PE1 and PE2 carry ESI Label extended community with redundancy-mode field = 0; PE3 forwarding installs both PE1 and PE2 next-hops for the MAC; traffic uses label from MAC/IP Advertisement route.
Fail-on: Any Ethernet A-D per ES route has redundancy-mode ≠ 0, or PE3 only installs single next-hop (not deducing All-Active), or egress label stack does not match MAC/IP Advertisement route label.

07cf595ace732471.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with two EVPN access interfaces: `agg-eth 0` without `ethernet-segment` configuration (ESI=0, reserved value) and `agg-eth 1` with `ethernet-segment` + `identifier 1` (non-reserved ESI). Establish EVPN peering with neighbor PE; neighbor PE advertises MAC/IP Advertisement routes for MACs behind both segments plus Ethernet A-D per ES routes for `agg-eth 1`. IXIA generates continuous traffic (≥ 1 Mbps) to MACs on both segments; verify baseline forwarding.
Action: Kill BGP process to trigger control-plane failure: identify via `show platform process | include bgp`, then reset process. During recovery, flap `agg-eth 1` to simulate PE-to-CE failure (§17.3). Capture route re-learning sequence using `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and `show evpn mac`. Monitor IXIA loss histogram throughout recovery period.
Verify: BGP process restarts ≤ 5 s. MAC learned via `agg-eth 0` (ESI=0) re-appears in `show evpn mac` immediately upon receiving MAC/IP Advertisement route (no dependency on Ethernet A-D routes). MAC learned via `agg-eth 1` (non-reserved ESI) only re-appears after BOTH MAC/IP Advertisement AND Ethernet A-D per ES routes are received (§9.2.2 MUST). `show ethernet-segment` confirms `agg-eth 1` operational after `es-waiting-time` (3 s default). IXIA traffic recovers; total loss ≤ 5 s. `show alarms` clears within 30 s.
07cf595ace732471.expectation:
Pass:    ESI=0 MAC installs from MAC/IP Advertisement alone (no Ethernet A-D wait); non-reserved ESI MAC waits for both route types per §9.2.2; BGP recovers ≤ 5 s; traffic loss ≤ 5 s; no persistent alarms.
Fail-on: ESI=0 MAC installation blocked waiting for Ethernet A-D routes; non-reserved ESI MAC installs before receiving Ethernet A-D routes; BGP restart > 5 s; traffic loss > 5 s; alarms persist beyond 30 s.

083b56dd3a2e7dfc.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) as multihomed PEs for the same Ethernet Segment (e.g., ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11); both advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes (BGP EVPN route type 1) and MAC/IP routes (type 2) for EVI 100. CE dual-homed to both PEs; verify `show bgp evpn ethernet-segment` on DUT lists the ES as multihomed and `show route advertising-protocol bgp <peer-ip>` includes type-1 routes.
Action: Shut down DUT interface toward CE (`configuration / interface / <iface> / admin-state disable`); capture BGP updates on 3rd-party PE. Alternatively, administratively remove EVI from ES: `configuration / l2-services / evpn / evi 100 / no ethernet-segment`.
Verify: Within convergence window, verify DUT sends BGP UPDATE withdrawing Ethernet A-D per ES routes for the failed ES and MAC/IP routes for impacted MACs. On 3rd-party PE, confirm `show bgp evpn route-type 1` no longer lists DUT next-hop for that ESI, and traffic destined to CE uses only the surviving PE.
083b56dd3a2e7dfc.expectation:
Pass:    DUT withdraws all Ethernet A-D per ES routes (type 1) for the failed ES within 3 seconds of link-down event; 3rd-party PE removes DUT from MPLS next-hop set and traffic forwarding shifts entirely to surviving PE with zero loss after convergence. MAC/IP withdrawals (type 2) are optionally staggered 0–MAC-ageout-timer per RFC7432bis §17.3.
Fail-on: Ethernet A-D per ES routes remain advertised >5s after link down, or 3rd-party PE continues forwarding traffic to DUT causing black-hole, or 3rd-party PE rejects withdrawal UPDATE (malformed encoding or missing mandatory path attributes).

088eced29f2e19cf.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 on the DUT; enter interface configuration mode.
Action: Configure the LACP Administrative Key using the lacp-key command with a non-default value (e.g., 100); commit. Then restore the default configuration using `no lacp-key`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` confirms the configured key value (100) after the first commit, then shows the key reverted to the default (the interface number, 0) after the no command.
088eced29f2e19cf.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
088eced29f2e19cf.expectation:
Pass:    The lacp-key command successfully sets the administrative key value, persists in running-config after commit, and `no lacp-key` restores the default (agg-eth interface number)
Fail-on: Command rejected, configured key does not appear in running-config, no form fails to restore the default, or default value is not the agg-eth interface number

08d7477835899c37.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with identical ES (identifier 0 <type0-value>, load-balancing-mode single-active, service-carving highest-random-weight) on agg-eth interfaces; remote PE3 imports Type-1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes and verifies ESI Label extended community redundancy mode = 1; IXIA attached to PE3 generates 10 Gbps bi-directional L2 traffic to CE behind the ES.
Action: IXIA starts traffic load; verify PE3 forwards all traffic via Primary DF (PE1 or PE2); administratively shut the Primary DF's ES interface; measure convergence time until traffic resumes via backup DF; collect `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` and `show evpn mac-table` on PE3 throughout failover.
Verify: PE3's `show evpn ethernet-segment` output confirms ES redundancy mode = Single-Active; failover convergence time (last packet loss to first forwarded packet); packet loss count; confirm traffic never dual-homed (only one PE forwards for a given MAC at any moment).
08d7477835899c37.expectation:
Pass:    Convergence time ≤ 3 seconds (es-waiting-time default); packet loss < 0.01% of 10 Gbps × convergence window; remote PE3 deduces Single-Active mode from imported Type-1 routes; all MAC addresses reachable via exactly one PE (Primary DF) pre-failover and exactly one PE (backup DF) post-failover.
Fail-on: Convergence > 3 sec; packet loss ≥ 0.01%; PE3 does not recognize Single-Active mode (e.g., load-balances or dual-homes traffic); traffic black-holed > 3 sec after ES interface shutdown; PE3 installs MAC routes from both PEs simultaneously (violates Single-Active MUST).

09048ce419505c60.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT; configure `ethernet-segment` on agg-eth interface with `identifier 1` (type 4 default) and `load-balancing-mode single-active` (default); DUT is designated forwarder for BUM on ES; verify Type 3 IMET routes with P-tunnel attribute advertising downstream-assigned label for ingress replication; IXIA emulates remote PE injecting BUM via MPLS (transport label + EVPN downstream label from P-tunnel attribute).
Action: IXIA sends continuous BUM traffic for ≥24 h: unknown unicast (randomized dst MACs NOT in `show l2-services mac-table`), broadcast (FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF), multicast (01:00:5E:xx:xx:xx); monitor hourly: memory, DF status, split-horizon state (RFC7432bis §8.3), counters.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` (DF stable, no flaps); `show l2-services mac-table` (unknown dst MACs NOT learned, confirming RFC7432bis §13.2.1 default no-lookup behavior); `show evpn route type 3` (IMET stable); `show platform process memory` (flat); `show interface agg-eth statistics` (BUM egress to ES monotonic, no drops).
09048ce419505c60.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (ES multi-homing) + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
09048ce419505c60.expectation:
Pass:    BUM traffic flooded to ES without destination MAC address lookup for 24 h; unknown MACs remain unlearned; DF status stable (no flaps); memory growth <2%; MPLS ingress and agg-eth egress counters monotonic; split-horizon enforced; no packet loss.
Fail-on: Packet loss >0.01%, unknown dst MACs incorrectly learned (violates §13.2.1 default no-lookup), DF flap, memory growth ≥5%, counter freeze/rollback, split-horizon violation (loopback detected).

091434228cd28be3.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT (AS 65001) with EVPN neighbor PE1 (AS 64520, private) exchanging L2VPN EVPN routes. DUT learns at least one EVPN route from PE1 with AS_PATH containing 64520. Configure second neighbor PE2 (AS 65100, public) under af-l2vpn evpn to test outbound advertisement.
Action: Navigate to configuration / routing / bgp / 65001 / neighbor <PE2-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn, configure `private-as remove`, commit. Clear BGP soft outbound to PE2 to trigger route re-advertisement.
Verify: Capture outbound BGP UPDATE messages to PE2 via tcpdump or IXIA BGP analyzer. Decode AS_PATH attribute in L2VPN EVPN NLRIs to confirm private AS numbers are stripped.
091434228cd28be3.expectation:
Pass:    AS_PATH in outbound UPDATE messages to PE2 contains no AS numbers in ranges 64512..65534 or 4200000000..4294967294; routes successfully propagated to PE2; `show running-config` persists `private-as remove`.
Fail-on: Any private AS number (64512..65534 or 4200000000..4294967294) appears in AS_PATH to PE2, configuration commit rejected, or BGP session to PE2 drops.

0927fa4f1505bc8e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'evpn-mac-learn' with `evpn evpn-mac-learn service-type vlan-based`; attach 2 local CE-facing interfaces (e.g., Eth1/1, Eth1/2) to the bridge-domain; configure `mac-aging-time 300` (5 min); ensure no control-plane MAC learning config exists (per spec, not supported).
Action: From IXIA, send 50 data frames per interface with unique source MACs (00:AA:00:00:01:01–00:AA:00:00:01:32 on Eth1/1; 00:BB:00:00:01:01–00:BB:00:00:01:32 on Eth1/2); send at 10 fps; separately send 10 LACP BPDUs with source MAC 00:CC:00:00:00:01 on Eth1/1 to verify LACP exclusion.
Verify: Execute `show l2-services mac-table evpn evpn-mac-learn` within 10s; confirm 100 data-plane learned MACs (50/interface) with correct ingress ports; verify LACP source MAC 00:CC:00:00:00:01 is NOT present in table; check `show l2-services vpls evpn-mac-learn` shows no control-plane learned entries.
0927fa4f1505bc8e.expectation:
Pass:    100 MACs learned via data-plane (Type=Dynamic or Data-Plane) within 10s; each MAC mapped to correct ingress interface; LACP source MAC absent from table; no control-plane MAC entries in VPLS table.
Fail-on: MACs not learned after 30s, or Type=Control-Plane, or LACP MAC 00:CC:00:00:00:01 appears in MAC table, or crash/ERR log during learning.

09818ed653b475a3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Type 1 (LACP-based) Ethernet segment on both DUT and neighbor PE: add `ethernet-segment` to `agg-eth 0`, enable `auto-discovery` with matching `import-rt` and `export-rt` under the EVPN instance, establish BGP EVPN peering, verify ESI auto-discovery routes (RFC7432bis §5) exchanged and DF elected. Start IXIA continuous traffic through the multihomed CE.
Action: On the current DF PE, perform a control-plane process restart (platform-specific EVPN/BGP daemon reset or `restart bgp` equivalent).
Verify: Check ESI operational state recovers on restarted PE (show ethernet-segment, show running-config agg-eth 0). Verify ESI auto-discovery routes re-advertised in BGP EVPN table (show bgp evpn). Confirm DF re-election completes. Measure traffic loss duration from IXIA counters. Confirm standby PE maintained forwarding during DF recovery.
09818ed653b475a3.expectation:
Pass:    ESI state recovers to operational, auto-discovery routes re-advertised, DF election completes, no manual intervention required, IXIA reports traffic loss ≤ 1 s
Fail-on: ESI stuck in down/error state, auto-discovery routes missing from BGP table, DF election does not complete, or continuous traffic loss > 1 s without recovery

0a0cf1141887058c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 with `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based`, RD 1.1.1.1:100, `export-rt 65000:100` and `import-rt 65000:100`; configure `interface agg-eth 1` with `ethernet-segment` and 10-octet ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99; set BGP IPv4 router-id 1.1.1.1; establish EVPN peering with PE2 and exchange route-target 65000:100.
Action: Enable `auto-discovery`, bring up agg-eth 1; capture Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) route on PE2 using `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 rd 1.1.1.1:100 detail` and extract NLRI fields: RD, ESI, Ethernet Tag ID, and MP_REACH_NLRI Next Hop.
Verify: Parse advertised route on PE2; confirm RD = 1.1.1.1:100 (§7.9 compliance), ESI field = exactly 10 octets with value 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 (§5 compliance), MP_REACH_NLRI Next Hop = 1.1.1.1 (§8.4.1 MUST), and Ethernet Tag ID present (§6).
0a0cf1141887058c.expectation:
Pass:    Type 1 route with RD = 1.1.1.1:100, ESI = 10-octet 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, Next Hop = 1.1.1.1 (PE1's IPv4 address), Ethernet Tag ID present and valid.
Fail-on: No Type 1 route advertised for non-zero ESI, RD mismatch, ESI != 10 octets or incorrect value, Next Hop != PE1 address (1.1.1.1), or Ethernet Tag ID missing/malformed.

0a74a885286bc26c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN VLAN-based service on PE1 and PE2; attach IXIA port P1 to PE1 access interface. Send frames from MAC M1 via P1; verify `show evpn mac address-table` on remote PE3 learns M1 via PE1's VTEP.
Action: Stop traffic from P1. Physically move P1 from PE1 to PE2 access interface; resume M1 frames via PE2. Capture BGP session on PE3 (`tcpdump -i <uplink> port 179`); measure time T from first M1 frame seen on PE2 uplink until `show evpn mac address-table` on PE3 switches M1 to PE2. Repeat move 6 times in 180 s window to test sticky MAC threshold.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on PE3 after first move: M1's Type 2 route from PE2 carries MAC Mobility extended community with sequence ≥ 1; PE1's previous advertisement withdrawn. T < 1 s (fast convergence). After 6 moves: `show evpn duplicate-mac` flags M1 as sticky or duplicate MAC.
0a74a885286bc26c.expectation:
Pass:    MAC Mobility sequence increments with each move; PE1 withdraws obsolete route within 5 s; PE3 updates FIB to PE2 in < 1 s (fast convergence); sticky MAC detection activates after threshold (> 5 moves / 180 s per typical implementation).
Fail-on: Sequence number static or absent; no withdrawal; FIB convergence > 2 s; or no sticky MAC flag after 6+ rapid moves.

0acbf06601807c0a.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) dual-homed to same CE; both PEs configure identical ESI (e.g., type 0: 01.23.45.67.89.ab.cd.ef.00) with load-balancing-mode all-active on DUT (`interface agg-eth 1; ethernet-segment; identifier 0 01.23.45.67.89.ab.cd.ef.00; load-balancing-mode all-active`) and equivalent on 3rd-party; MPLS backbone + iBGP EVPN session established between DUT, 3rd-party PE, and remote PE (IXIA acting as remote PE).
Action: Bring up ES on both PEs; CE sends ARP/MAC (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55) into EVI 100; capture BGP UPDATE messages at remote PE showing Ethernet A-D per ES routes from both PEs; decode ESI Label extended community and verify "Multihoming redundancy mode" field = 0x00 (All-Active); capture MAC/IP Advertisement routes from both PEs with their MPLS labels.
Verify: On remote PE, show BGP EVPN table for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55; confirm two next-hops (DUT + 3rd-party) both installed; send bidirectional traffic from remote PE to MAC; capture MPLS packets at DUT and 3rd-party ingress; verify (a) traffic ECMP load-balances across both PEs, (b) MPLS label stack matches label advertised in MAC/IP Advertisement route from the receiving PE.
0acbf06601807c0a.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) + IXIA (emulates CE + remote PE with BGP EVPN speaker and traffic generator)
0acbf06601807c0a.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes with ESI Label extended community byte 1 = 0x00; remote PE BGP table shows MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 with two ECMP next-hops (DUT IP + 3rd-party IP); ≥40% of test flows traverse each PE (within 60/40 split tolerance); captured MPLS label in forwarded packet = label from that PE's MAC/IP Advertisement route.
Fail-on: ESI Label extended community ≠ 0x00, remote PE installs only one next-hop (Single-Active behavior), traffic uses only one PE (<10% on the other), or MPLS label mismatch (label not from MAC/IP route).

0ad7ac7e327af862.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES1 (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on 3 PEs using Highest-Preference DF algorithm: PE1 preference 200, PE2 preference 150, PE3 preference 100 (set under `ethernet-segment` with DF algorithm and per-ES preference parameters per RFC9785 §4.1). Verify PE1 elected DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment esi <ESI> detail`. IXIA sends 1 Gbps bidirectional L2 traffic through ES1.
Action: On PE1 (current DF), execute `restart process evpn-mgr` to reset EVPN control-plane process. Capture `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 detail` output on all 3 PEs during transition. Monitor IXIA packet loss counters.
Verify: DF role transitions from PE1 → PE2 (next-highest preference). Confirm PE2 shows "DF role: DF" and PE1/PE3 show "DF role: non-DF" in show command. Measure traffic convergence time (time to zero packet loss) in IXIA capture.
0ad7ac7e327af862.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE (minimum 3 PEs total in same ES1; all PEs must support RFC9785 DF Election Extended Community with Preference parameter)
0ad7ac7e327af862.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 becomes new DF (DF election follows descending preference order 200→150→100); traffic loss duration ≤ 1 s; all 3 PEs converge to consistent DF state (PE2=DF, PE1/PE3=non-DF) within 5 s
Fail-on: Wrong PE elected DF (PE3 selected, violating preference ordering); traffic loss > 1 s; ES stuck in DF-election-pending or inconsistent DF state across PEs > 10 s; PE1 re-preempts PE2 without operator intervention (violates Don't Preempt if configured)

0afd080baf423bea.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn test-flood service-type vlan-based` with two ACs in VLAN 100; commit without `unknow-mac-flooding` configuration in mode `configuration / l2-services / evpn test-flood`.
Action: Send Ethernet frames with unknown destination MAC `00:00:00:99:99:99` from IXIA to ingress AC; capture egress. Configure `unknow-mac-flooding disable`; commit; resend frames. Issue `no unknow-mac-flooding`; commit; resend frames.
Verify: Default (unconfigured): unknown MACs flood to egress AC, `show running-config l2-services evpn test-flood` omits the parameter. After `disable`: flooding stops, config shows `unknow-mac-flooding disable`. After `no` form: flooding resumes, config omits line (default restored).
0afd080baf423bea.expectation:
Pass:    Unconfigured and post-`no` states both flood unknown MACs (default = enable); running-config omits line in both cases; `unknow-mac-flooding disable` blocks flooding and appears in config.
Fail-on: Unknown MACs not flooded when unconfigured or after `no unknow-mac-flooding`, or `no` form leaves `unknow-mac-flooding enable` in config (non-default), or `disable` fails to block flooding.

0b228527f67e6ef8.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP EVPN configured (router bgp, af l2vpn evpn enabled); aggregated interface `agg-eth 0` created with member links x-eth 0/0/1 and x-eth 0/0/2 (LACP bundle); no prior ethernet-segment configuration.
Action: `configure` → `interface agg-eth 0` → `ethernet-segment` → verify mode prompt changes to ethernet-segment context → `commit`.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` includes the ethernet-segment configuration block; re-enter the mode and confirm ESI sub-commands (`identifier`, load-balancing options) are accessible.
0b228527f67e6ef8.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
0b228527f67e6ef8.expectation:
Pass:    `ethernet-segment` command accepted, running-config persists the ethernet-segment block under interface agg-eth 0, mode allows ESI parameter configuration (identifier, load-balancing)
Fail-on: Command rejected (syntax error / invalid mode), commit failure, ethernet-segment block missing from running-config, or ESI sub-commands inaccessible

0b23a12e978ee58d.action_steps:
Setup:  Under `configuration / l2-services`, create `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based` with normalized VLAN-ID 4 on interface x-eth 0/0/1 (input VLAN 100 maps to EVPN VLAN 4); configure `auto-discovery` with `import-rt` and `export-rt`. Start IXIA sending 1000 pps VLAN-100-tagged frames into x-eth 0/0/1, receiving on remote PE via EVPN.
Action: While traffic flows: (1) enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1`, change normalized VLAN-ID from 4 to 6, `commit`, wait 2 s; (2) revert normalized VLAN-ID back to 4, `commit`. Poll IXIA loss counters every 100 ms.
Verify: Query IXIA loss counters across both transitions; run `show running-config | section l2-services` and `show l2-services evpn evpn1 detail` after each commit to confirm new normalized VLAN-ID is active and VLAN replacement is operational.
0b23a12e978ee58d.expectation:
Pass:    IXIA reports zero packet loss during both config changes; `show running-config` reflects new normalized VLAN-ID within ≤ 1 s; input VLAN 100 frames continue to be replaced with active normalized VLAN-ID and forwarded through EVPN.
Fail-on: Any packet loss (> 0 frames), or config change not active within 1 s, or VLAN replacement stops working (frames dropped or forwarded with wrong VLAN-ID).

0b605bb6a3bece5c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (EVI 100, RD 1:100, RT 100:100) on DUT and neighbor PE; establish MP-BGP EVPN peering; attach IXIA port emulating host (MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55) to remote PE EVPN instance.
Action: Send ARP/data frames from IXIA-emulated host on remote PE to trigger local MAC learning; remote PE advertises BGP EVPN Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) route with MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 to DUT.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` on DUT displays Type 2 route with MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from remote PE; `show l2-services mac-table` shows MAC entry with 'remote' or 'EVPN' flag and VXLAN VNI or MPLS label; send bi-directional traffic between DUT-local host and 00:11:22:33:44:55 and capture successful forwarding via EVPN data-plane (VXLAN or MPLS).
0b605bb6a3bece5c.expectation:
Pass:    DUT installs remote MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from Type 2 route into MAC table with remote/EVPN flag; MAC entry points to EVPN tunnel (VXLAN VNI or MPLS label); bi-directional traffic forwarding achieves 0% loss.
Fail-on: Type 2 route received but MAC not installed in `show l2-services mac-table`, or MAC installed with incorrect flag (local instead of remote), or traffic to remote MAC experiences >0.1% loss, or MAC appears on wrong VLAN/VNI.

0b7181224fbdb1ce.action_steps:
Setup:  Create EVPN instance evpn-100 (service-type vlan-based); attach interface Ethernet1/1/1; configure `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface Ethernet1/1/1` under evpn-100; commit and verify static entry appears in `show evpn evpn-100 mac-table`.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-100` mode; execute `no mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-100` contains no mac-address-static line; `show evpn evpn-100 mac-table` does not list 00:11:22:33:44:55 or shows it removed; send Ethernet frame sourced from 00:11:22:33:44:55 via IXIA on Ethernet1/1/1 → MAC is learned dynamically (type: dynamic).
0b7181224fbdb1ce.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC binding 00:11:22:33:44:55 removed from running-config and operational MAC table; dynamic MAC learning resumes for that address (IXIA frame produces dynamic entry).
Fail-on: Static MAC entry persists in running-config or MAC table; MAC remains locked to Ethernet1/1/1; dynamic learning of 00:11:22:33:44:55 fails or MAC stuck in stale/static state.

0ba2533c3a0a3455.action_steps:
Setup:  Multi-homed Ethernet Segment configured on DUT agg-eth 0 and neighbor PE; EVPN instance with auto-discovery, import-rt, export-rt configured; Don't Preempt capability enabled on the Ethernet Segment; IXIA sending bidirectional traffic to multi-homed CE; verify neighbor PE is current DF.
Action: Kill the EVPN/BGP control-plane process on DUT (platform-specific debug command); wait for automatic process restart and BGP/EVPN reconvergence.
Verify: System logs confirm process restart; `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` confirms Ethernet Segment config persists; EVPN show commands confirm Don't Preempt capability advertised in DF Election Extended Community; verify neighbor PE remains DF (DUT is non-DF); IXIA reports traffic interruption duration.
0ba2533c3a0a3455.expectation:
Pass:    Control-plane process restarts; DUT rejoins Ethernet Segment without preempting existing DF; traffic interruption ≤ control-plane convergence bound (typically ≤ 3 s for EVPN route withdrawal/readvertisement).
Fail-on: Process fails to restart, or DUT preempts existing DF after restart (violates RFC9785 §4.3 non-revertive behavior), or traffic interruption exceeds documented HA recovery bound.

0ba61d92414db56e.action_steps:
