Setup:  Configure two PEs multi-homing a CE via LACP on the same ES; configure EVPN instance with `auto-discovery` on both PEs; configure `import-rt` and `export-rt`; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` between PEs.
Action: Bring up ES interfaces on both PEs; both advertise Type 1 per-ES route (ET=MAX-ET, MPLS Label=0, ESI Label EC) and per-EVI route(s); capture BGP UPDATEs between PE↔PE; extract ESI Label from ESI Label Extended Community.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 detail`; confirm ESI Label value matches in both PEs' Type 1 per-ES routes for the ES (RFC7432bis §8.2.1 MUST); if values differ, verify 'Mismatched Configuration' alarm raised, routes saved but not selected; when resolved, alarm clears.
0ba61d92414db56e.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
0ba61d92414db56e.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise Type 1 per-ES route with ET=MAX-ET, ESI Label EC; ESI Label value identical across PEs (RFC7432bis §8.2.1 MUST); if mismatch, alarm raised and routes quarantined (SFS delta behavior; RFC left MUST violations unspecified).
Fail-on: ESI Label EC missing; ESI Label mismatch but no alarm (failure to enforce SFS delta); or mismatched routes used in forwarding (falling back to undefined RFC base behavior).

0bcdef38e1ea85e1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed vES1 on DUT (PE1, IP 10.0.0.1) and neighbor PE2 (IP 10.0.0.2) using `interface agg-eth / ethernet-segment`, set DF algorithm to Highest-Preference with PE1 preference=200 + Don't Preempt enabled, PE2 preference=100. Configure EVPN service `evpn vES1 service-type vlan-based` with auto-discovery, export-rt/import-rt matching. Verify PE1 elected DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment df-election`. Start IXIA bidirectional MAC learning traffic (100 Mbps) through the ES.
Action: Power-cycle PE1 (current DF) via PDU harness. Monitor BGP EVPN Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) routes and DF state on PE2 during outage. Wait for PE1 full boot and BGP session re-establishment (check `show bgp evpn summary`).
Verify: During outage, PE2 assumes DF role (`show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE2 shows DF=local). After PE1 recovery, BGP session up, PE1 Type-4 route readvertises preference=200 + Don't Preempt in DF Election Extended Community (RFC9785 §4.1), but PE2 remains DF per Don't Preempt semantics. Confirm via `show bgp evpn routes type 4` on both PEs and `show evpn ethernet-segment df-election` shows PE2=DF, PE1=backup. IXIA reports traffic forwarding throughout (failover to PE2, continues via PE2 post-recovery).
0bcdef38e1ea85e1.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
0bcdef38e1ea85e1.expectation:
Pass:    (1) PE2 becomes DF within convergence window during PE1 outage; (2) After PE1 boot, BGP EVPN session re-establishes, PE1 readvertises Type-4 with pref=200+Don't Preempt, but PE2 remains DF (no preemption per RFC9785 §4.1 tie-break rule); (3) PE1 ethernet-segment config intact; (4) IXIA traffic loss ≤ convergence window, zero loss post-failover.
Fail-on: PE1 preempts PE2 after recovery (violates Don't Preempt); lost/corrupted ethernet-segment config on PE1; BGP EVPN session fails to re-establish; PE2 does not assume DF during outage; continuous traffic loss post-recovery; missing or incorrect DF Election Extended Community in Type-4 routes.

0bd5ffcc803a3c6a.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT (AS 65001) peered with IXIA (acting as PE in AS 65002) via BGP with af-l2vpn evpn enabled; no allow-as-in configured initially.
Action: On DUT, configure allow-as-in with count=1 under the IXIA neighbor's af-l2vpn evpn context and commit. IXIA advertises EVPN Type-2 MAC/IP routes: first with AS_PATH=[65002, 65001] (local AS once), then with AS_PATH=[65002, 65001, 65001] (local AS twice).
Verify: Issue `show running-config` to confirm allow-as-in count=1 is persisted; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` to confirm routes with AS_PATH=[65002, 65001] are present in RIB; verify routes with AS_PATH=[65002, 65001, 65001] are absent from `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output (rejected per loop detection).
0bd5ffcc803a3c6a.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN routes with DUT's AS appearing exactly once in AS_PATH are accepted; routes with AS appearing twice are rejected per loop detection beyond the configured count.
Fail-on: Routes with single local-AS occurrence are rejected, or routes with double occurrence are accepted, or allow-as-in configuration is not accepted or persisted.

0be3784f12e9f4c9.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (PE1=DUT, PE2=neighbor) in EVPN instance EVI 100; VPLS service configured; CE1→PE1, CE2→PE2; BGP EVPN peering established.
Action: IXIA sends Ethernet frame from CE1 with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55. PE1 advertises BGP EVPN Type-2 route (MAC/IP Advertisement, RFC7432bis §9.2) to PE2.
Verify: On PE2, `show evpn mac evi 100` displays MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 with Type=Remote, Next-hop=PE1. `show l2-services vpls mac-table` confirms remote entry. Send traffic from CE2 destined to 00:11:22:33:44:55; IXIA capture at CE1 shows frame arrival.
0be3784f12e9f4c9.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 installs remote MAC entry learned via BGP Type-2 route; MAC table shows Type=Remote, Next-hop=PE1; traffic from CE2 to remote MAC forwards successfully (§2.6.3, RFC7432bis §9.2).
Fail-on: MAC table on PE2 empty, Type=Local instead of Remote, Next-hop incorrect/missing, or traffic black-holed/flooded.

0be8ee62c7f63e7d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP neighbor under `af-l2vpn evpn` with `inbound-soft-reconfiguration` enabled. Establish BGP session; peer advertises multiple EVPN type-2 MAC/IP routes.
Action: Record initial route count via `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`. Apply an inbound route-map to the neighbor that filters routes by RT or MAC prefix. Trigger inbound soft-refresh to re-evaluate cached routes against the new policy, without BGP session reset.
Verify: `show bgp neighbor <neighbor-ip>` shows session state = Established, uptime unchanged from before policy change. `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows route count reduced per new policy. Check neighbor statistics for zero session resets.
0be8ee62c7f63e7d.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session remains Established throughout; route count decreases per new inbound route-map; session uptime counter does not reset; neighbor statistics show zero session resets.
Fail-on: Session state transitions to Idle/Active/Connect, or session uptime resets to zero, or route count unchanged despite filtering policy, or `inbound-soft-reconfiguration` command rejected at config time.

0c15067756c012ed.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs in multi-homing: PE1 & PE2 each with `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88`, ES1 with `load-balancing-mode single-active` (default), ES2 with `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `service-carving highest-random-weight`, `configuration / l2-services / evpn / es-waiting-time 3` (default). IXIA sends continuous unicast traffic (1 Gbps, mixed frame sizes) to multi-homed CEs behind both ES.
Action: Run for 24 hours. Monitor DF election stability for single-active ES, traffic distribution for all-active ES. Sample `show platform process memory`, `show evpn ethernet-segment`, and interface counters hourly.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms ESI operational, DF stable on ES1, both PEs active on ES2. `show interface agg-eth 1 statistics` on single-active ES shows unicast Rx only on DF (non-DF counters = 0), on all-active ES shows distributed load (counter ratio 40:60 to 60:40). `show platform process memory` flat. Syslog clean (no ES flaps, no route churn).
0c15067756c012ed.equipment: Two routers (PE1, PE2 in EVPN multi-homing) + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
0c15067756c012ed.expectation:
Pass:    Single-active ES forwards unicast only via DF (non-DF Rx unicast counters = 0); all-active ES load-balances unicast across PEs (counter ratio within 40:60–60:40 range); DF election count unchanged over 24 h; memory growth < 5%; no route withdrawals.
Fail-on: Non-DF receives unicast on single-active ES; all-active traffic goes to one PE only (ratio > 90:10); memory growth ≥ 5%; DF re-election > 0 without trigger; ES state flaps; route churn in `show evpn instance`.

0c4b7463fad0fc11.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN EVI with MP2MP P-multicast tunnel (RSVP-TE P2MP or mLDP) on three PEs; attach PE1 and PE2 to the same Ethernet Segment (shared ESI); enable DCB label allocation for ESI labels.
Action: Bring up the EVPN instance on all PEs; trigger ESI label advertisement via `configuration / l2-services / evpn / advertise-mac enable`; capture BGP UPDATE messages (AFI=25, SAFI=70) carrying Ethernet A-D per-ES routes (type 1) with ESI label extended community.
Verify: Extract ESI label values from type 1 routes advertised by PE1 and PE2 for the shared ESI; confirm both PEs use identical ESI label and that the label falls within the configured DCB range; decode PMSI Tunnel Attribute in type 3 routes to verify DCB label (if aggregate trees used) instead of upstream-assigned label.
0c4b7463fad0fc11.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (3-PE topology) + IXIA traffic gen + packet capture (Wireshark/tcpdump for BGP UPDATE decode)
0c4b7463fad0fc11.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 and PE2 advertise identical ESI label for the shared Ethernet Segment, and the label value is within the DCB range (RFC7432bis §19 MUST); PMSI Tunnel Attribute contains DCB label if aggregate P-multicast tree is configured.
Fail-on: PE1 and PE2 advertise different ESI labels for the same ESI, or ESI label is outside DCB range, or MP2MP tunnel uses upstream-allocated label instead of DCB label.

0cc1ed9f17550f3b.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with an EVPN ethernet-segment configured under `configuration / interface / agg-eth`; run `show running-config interface agg-eth <id> ethernet-segment` and confirm no explicit service-carving statement appears (default carving service in effect).
Action: Enter ethernet-segment configuration mode and issue the service-carving command; commit. Then issue `no service-carving`; commit.
Verify: Run `show running-config interface agg-eth <id> ethernet-segment` after each commit: the first shows an explicit service-carving statement; the second shows no such statement (default restored).
0cc1ed9f17550f3b.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
0cc1ed9f17550f3b.expectation:
Pass:    service-carving command accepts configuration and appears in running-config after commit; `no service-carving` removes the explicit statement and running-config returns to default state (no explicit service-carving line).
Fail-on: service-carving configuration rejected, or `no` form does not remove the explicit statement, or running-config retains an explicit service-carving value after `no` command.

0d024958add788e6.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that single-active ESI with primary/backup DF signaling recovers gracefully from primary PE control-plane failure, with the backup PE assuming forwarding within es-waiting-time to minimize traffic loss.
Method: Configure two PEs with shared ESI in single-active mode, generate continuous IXIA traffic through the primary DF, forcibly restart the EVPN control-plane process on the primary, and measure backup PE takeover time and traffic outage duration.
Setup: Two PEs (PE1, PE2) with identical multi-homing config: `interface agg-eth 0` → `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 <shared-type0-value>`, `load-balancing-mode single-active`, `service-carving default`. LACP partner (CE) dual-homed to both PEs. Remote PE3 peers BGP EVPN with PE1 and PE2. IXIA at remote site generating 1 Gbps bidirectional traffic to/from CE MACs.
Action:
1. Verify DF election: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE1 and PE2 (one DF, one Non-DF).
2. Verify aliasing: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on PE3 shows two paths (PE1, PE2 next-hops) for CE MAC routes.
3. Identify primary DF's EVPN process: `show platform process | include evpn`.
4. Restart that process using platform-specific reset command; monitor IXIA loss histogram and `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs during recovery.
Verify:
1. Backup PE transitions from Non-DF to DF within 3 s (es-waiting-time default): `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms state change.
2. IXIA loss histogram records traffic outage ≤ 3 s.
3. Primary PE recovers within 30 s; DF election re-stabilizes to one DF, one Non-DF.
4. Aliasing persists: PE3 continues to see two paths for CE MACs in `show bgp l2vpn evpn`.
5. Single-active forwarding verified: only the DF forwards traffic (LACP active-standby state aligns with DF election).
0d024958add788e6.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
0d024958add788e6.expectation:
Pass:    Backup assumes DF role within 3 s of primary failure; traffic outage ≤ 3 s (one es-waiting-time window); primary recovers and DF election stabilizes within 30 s; aliasing (two BGP paths) and single-active forwarding (only DF forwards) maintained throughout.
Fail-on: Backup doesn't assume DF; traffic outage > 3 s; primary doesn't recover; split-brain (both or neither PE claims DF); aliasing breaks (PE3 loses one or both paths); or both PEs forward simultaneously (single-active mode violated).

0d1a1363d7b2f11b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment on DUT `agg-eth 0` with ESI type 0; configure the same ESI on neighbor PE `agg-eth 0`; establish iBGP EVPN session with `address-family l2vpn evpn`; create a VLAN-based EVPN instance; attach IXIA to simulate a dual-homed CE.
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` mode; issue `service-carving highest-random-weight`; commit. Wait for DF election to complete (both PEs exchange ES routes via BGP).
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays `service-carving highest-random-weight` under ethernet-segment; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` indicates DF election status showing one PE elected as DF based on highest weight; send L2 traffic from IXIA CE and confirm only the DF PE forwards frames to the EVPN core.
0d1a1363d7b2f11b.expectation:
Pass:    `service-carving highest-random-weight` accepted and persists in running-config; DF election completes with the PE having highest random weight elected as DF for each service; only the DF forwards traffic from the CE.
Fail-on: Configuration rejected at commit; DF election does not complete (both or neither PE shows as DF); default carving method is used despite explicit config; or both PEs forward CE traffic (split-horizon violation).

0d8efc1c5a97f1e1.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology (PE1 = DUT, PE2 = neighbor) in same EVI/VLAN. On PE1, configure `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5` (M=180s, N=5). Attach syslog collector to capture operator alerts.
Action: Inject host with MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 on PE1 access port; PE1 learns locally and advertises BGP MAC/IP route. Move same MAC to PE2 access port (PE2 advertises, PE1 receives and re-learns remotely). Repeat move back-and-forth 5 times within 180-second window. On 5th move, duplication threshold exceeded.
Verify: PE1 generates operator alert (syslog severity warning or higher). Capture `show evpn mac address-table` (MAC marked duplicate/frozen), `show bgp l2vpn evpn` (no new UPDATE sent for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 after detection), syslog entry. Trigger 6th move — confirm PE1 does NOT send/process further BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes for that MAC. Reconfigure with `move-interval 60 move-count 3` and re-test to verify M and N configurability per RFC7432bis §15.1.
0d8efc1c5a97f1e1.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
0d8efc1c5a97f1e1.expectation:
Pass:    After 5th move within 180s, PE1 raises operator alert; MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 frozen; no BGP UPDATE sent for 6th move; M (`move-interval`) and N (`move-count`) successfully reconfigured and detection behavior changes accordingly.
Fail-on: No alert after threshold exceeded; BGP continues advertising MAC moves beyond N; sequence number keeps incrementing; `move-interval` or `move-count` parameters reject valid values or have no effect.

0d97465d240c3799.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT + neighbor PE) multihomed to a CE via IXIA; assign same ESI (e.g., 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11) under `interface agg-eth-1-1; ethernet-segment` on both; configure matching EVI with `import-rt`/`export-rt`; verify both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes (BGP EVPN route type 1, ESI=01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, no EVI field) via `show bgp evpn ethernet-segment`.
Action: Shut down the PE-CE link on DUT (`interface agg-eth-1-1; shutdown`) to simulate connectivity failure; wait 5 seconds.
Verify: On DUT run `show bgp evpn routes sent` and confirm Ethernet A-D per ES routes for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 are withdrawn (withdrawn flag set or routes absent); on neighbor PE run `show bgp evpn routes received` and `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` to confirm DUT's next-hop removed from the ES next-hop list; send IXIA traffic to CE and verify it arrives only via surviving PE link (neighbor PE's path).
0d97465d240c3799.expectation:
Pass:    Within 5 seconds of link shutdown, DUT withdraws all Ethernet A-D per ES routes for the failed ESI; neighbor PE's `show evpn ethernet-segment` no longer lists DUT's system-IP in the next-hop set; IXIA traffic to CE continues with 0% loss via the surviving PE.
Fail-on: Ethernet A-D per ES routes remain advertised >10 seconds after shutdown; neighbor PE still forwards traffic to DUT's next-hop causing black-hole; or traffic loss >1%.

0dbebaf27245c462.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on two PEs with 10K MAC entries pre-learned in MACT (5K local CEs per PE via attachment circuits, 5K remote via EVPN type-2 routes). Use `configuration / l2-services / evpn` with default `es-waiting-time 3`, and `interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment` with `load-balancing-mode single-active` (default). IXIA generates known unicast traffic (64B, 128B, 512B, 1518B frames).
Action: Ramp IXIA bidirectional L2 traffic from 1 Gbps to line-rate (10 Gbps) in 1 Gbps steps; each step sustained for 60 seconds. Traffic uses known destination MACs (already in MACT). Measure per-step: forwarding throughput (Mpps), average latency (μs), MACT lookup rate (lookups/sec), and frame loss (%).
Verify: Run `show l2-services evpn mac-table` and `show l2-services evpn statistics` on both PEs after each step; capture IXIA per-flow latency histograms and loss counters.
0dbebaf27245c462.expectation:
Pass:    Throughput ≥ line-rate (14.88 Mpps at 64B, 812 kpps at 1518B) with zero frame loss; average latency ≤ 50 μs; MACT lookup rate sustains traffic rate at all frame sizes; all 10K MAC entries remain stable in `show l2-services evpn mac-table` (no flapping).
Fail-on: Any frame loss > 0.01% at sustained rate; latency > 50 μs; throughput < 95% of line-rate; MAC entry flapping (adds/deletes) during stable traffic; MACT lookup failures or timeouts in syslog.

0dd86e45c597e0e7.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN instance (e.g., `evpn1` service-type vlan-based) configured and operational; interface `x-eth 0/0/1` is a member of the EVPN; no prior static MAC entries.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1` mode, issue `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config` includes the static MAC entry under the EVPN instance; the MAC table (via device-specific show command) displays MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 as type=static with egress interface `x-eth 0/0/1`.
0dd86e45c597e0e7.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
0dd86e45c597e0e7.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC configuration accepted at commit, persists in running-config, and appears in MAC table with type=static and correct interface binding.
Fail-on: Commit rejected, or static MAC absent from running-config after commit, or MAC not present in MAC table, or MAC type is not static, or interface binding incorrect.

0ea28d6b0f7503ef.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with local AS 65001, `evpn evpn-v100 service-type vlan-based` for VLAN 100, and BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE (`af-l2vpn evpn` enabled under neighbor config); do NOT configure manual `export-rt` — rely on auto-derivation per RFC7432bis §7.10.1. Connect IXIA as CE on VLAN 100 access port.
Action: Send L2 frames from IXIA CE (VLAN 100) to trigger MAC/IP Advertisement route (Type 2); simultaneously capture BGP traffic on DUT↔PE link (tcpdump -i <pe-facing-interface> 'tcp port 179' -w bgp-capture.pcap).
Verify: Open bgp-capture.pcap in Wireshark, locate BGP UPDATE with EVPN NLRI Type 2; decode RT Extended Community (Type Code 0x0002): Global Administrator = 65001, Local Administrator = 0x00000064 (VLAN 100 in bits [11:0], bits [31:12] zero); cross-check RT on DUT with `show bgp evpn route-type 2`.
0ea28d6b0f7503ef.expectation:
Pass:    RT Extended Community in BGP UPDATE encodes 65001:100 per RFC7432bis §7.10.1: Global Admin field = AS number (65001), Local Admin = 0x00000064 (VLAN 100 in lowest 12 bits, upper 20 bits zero).
Fail-on: RT absent from UPDATE, Global Admin ≠ 65001, Local Admin ≠ 0x00000064, or any bit in positions [31:12] of Local Admin non-zero.

0f027c59260fe53c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES1 (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on DUT aggregated-ethernet-1 and neighbor PE with identical ESI; enable DF election "Don't Preempt" capability in extended community; trigger failover so neighbor PE holds DF role for all Ethernet Tags in ES1; IXIA sending bidirectional L2 traffic (1 Gbps, 64-byte frames) through ES1 multi-homed endpoints.
Action: Execute platform control-plane process reset on DUT (e.g., `reset protocol bgp evpn` or equivalent process restart command); allow DUT to re-establish ES1 peering and rejoin the Ethernet Segment.
Verify: Query `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 detail` and `show evpn ethernet-segment df-election` to confirm neighbor PE remains DF for all Ethernet Tags; verify DUT rejoins as non-DF; capture IXIA Rx frame count and timestamp deltas to measure traffic interruption duration.
0f027c59260fe53c.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor PE remains DF for ES1 after DUT control-plane recovery (no preemption observed); DUT state shows non-DF role; IXIA reports traffic loss ≤ 200 ms and zero out-of-order frames post-recovery; DF election extended community reflects "Don't Preempt" capability active.
Fail-on: DUT preempts neighbor PE and claims DF role for any Ethernet Tag in ES1; traffic outage > 1 s; DUT or neighbor PE fall back to default DF election algorithm (RFC7432 modulo-based) instead of maintaining configured algorithm with "Don't Preempt"; BGP EVPN session fails to re-establish within 10 s.

0f0cf426a259e8fa.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn EVP1 service-type vlan-based` with L2 MTU 9000, control-word enabled, flow-label enabled. Establish BGP neighbor, enable `af-l2vpn evpn`, configure ingress-replication for BUM.
Action: Trigger Inclusive Multicast route (Type 3) generation; capture BGP UPDATE on neighbor PE. Parse Extended Communities for Type 0x06 Sub-type 0x04 (L2-Attr).
Verify: Decode L2-Attr Extended Community on Type 3 route: L2 MTU field = 9000 (octets 5-6), C flag (bit 14) matches control-word config, F flag (bit 15) matches flow-label config, P flag (bit 14 of octet 3) = 0, B flag (bit 15 of octet 3) = 0, all MBZ fields = 0 per RFC7432bis §7.11.1 Type 3 encoding rules.
0f0cf426a259e8fa.expectation:
Pass:    Type 3 route carries L2-Attr EC with MTU=9000, C/F flags reflect config, P=0 B=0 (per-ESI-and-EVI attributes zeroed on Type 3), all MBZ=0
Fail-on: P or B flags non-zero on Type 3 route, MTU field zero when MTU configured non-zero, C/F flags contradict config, or any MBZ field non-zero

0f46502c7d759dcf.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with identical 9-octet ESI on `agg-eth 0` interfaces (`ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 <type0-value>`, `load-balancing-mode single-active`), both connecting to a multi-homed CE. Configure PE3 as remote PE in the same EVPN instance with BGP peering to PE1 and PE2. Start IXIA continuous known-unicast traffic from PE3 toward a MAC behind the CE.
Action: (1) On PE3, verify via `show evpn ethernet-segment` and BGP EVPN route inspection that PE3 has learned Ethernet A-D per ES (route-type 1), MAC/IP Advertisement (route-type 2), and Inclusive Multicast (route-type 3) routes from both PE1 and PE2 (aliasing paths established). (2) Identify which PE is the current BGP best path for each route type. (3) Administratively shut down the active PE's `agg-eth 0` interface. (4) Wait up to (`es-waiting-time` + 3 s) for BGP route withdrawal and convergence.
Verify: (1) `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE3 now shows the backup PE as the active next-hop for the ESI. (2) BGP best-path selection has promoted the backup PE's routes for all three route types. (3) IXIA loss histogram confirms traffic interruption ≤ 1 s, then sustained recovery (aliasing succeeded).
0f46502c7d759dcf.expectation:
Pass:    PE3 switches to the backup PE within ≤ 1 s; best-path selection installs the backup PE's Ethernet A-D per ES, MAC/IP, and Inclusive Multicast routes; IXIA traffic recovers with ≤ 1 s loss (aliasing per RFC7432bis §8.4 and best-path selection per §7.13.x verified).
Fail-on: No PE switch, PE3 selects wrong next-hop, recovery > 1 s, any route type's best-path selection fails, or sustained blackhole (continuous loss ≥ 10 s).

0f6ddb45f039a713.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP instance with VRF, add neighbor with remote AS; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under the neighbor; wait for session to establish.
Action: Execute `show bgp neighbors brief` to list all neighbors; execute `show bgp neighbors detail <neighbor-ip>` targeting the configured neighbor.
Verify: `brief` displays one row per neighbor with columns: neighbor IP, remote AS, messages received/sent, uptime, state (Established); `detail` displays complete neighbor information including BGP session parameters, capabilities exchanged, timers, and per-address-family state for `af-l2vpn evpn` (active, routes received/sent).
0f6ddb45f039a713.expectation:
Pass:    `brief` command returns tabular output with neighbor IP, AS, state, and counters; `detail` command returns structured output containing BGP session state, negotiated capabilities, and `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family operational status.
Fail-on: Command unrecognized, output missing configured neighbor, `detail` output omits `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family section, or session state reported incorrectly.

0f7e2f8c02a16391.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-bundle` on x-eth 0/0/1 with bundle VLANs 100,200,300; configure `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 64500:1`, `export-rt 64500:1`; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; verify `show evpn instance evpn1` operational.
Action: From IXIA connected to x-eth 0/0/1, send 100 frames each with VLAN 100, 200, 300 (in-bundle baseline) plus 100 frames with VLAN 999 (out-of-bundle test); capture egress on DUT PE-facing interface and neighbor PE ingress.
Verify: Check `show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics` ingress/egress counters and `show evpn instance evpn1 detail`; confirm VLANs 100/200/300 forwarded (300 egress frames total), VLAN 999 dropped (zero egress, ≥100 input-discard counter).
0f7e2f8c02a16391.expectation:
Pass:    In-bundle VLANs (100/200/300) show 100 frames each forwarded to neighbor PE (300 total egress); out-of-bundle VLAN 999 shows zero egress frames and input-discard counter ≥100 on x-eth 0/0/1.
Fail-on: Any VLAN 999 frame forwarded (egress count > 0 or neighbor PE receives VLAN 999); OR in-bundle VLANs dropped (egress < 100 frames per VLAN).

0f7e96f10a7d2ab3.action_steps:
Problem: Primary and backup DF signaling in single-active ESI must encode the correct L2-Attr extended community P bit so remote PEs identify the designated forwarder.
Method: Configure two PEs with shared ESI and explicit service-carving preference-based DF election, capture BGP Type 1 route advertisements between them, and decode L2-Attr extended community P bit to confirm primary vs backup role encoding.
Setup: Two PEs share an ESI to one CE via agg-eth 0 interface. Configure `ethernet-segment` with `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (same ESI on both), `load-balancing-mode single-active`, and `service-carving highest-preference preference <value>` with PE1 preference > PE2 to force deterministic DF election.
Action: Bring up the ESI on both PEs, establish BGP l2vpn evpn peering between them, and capture BGP UPDATEs containing Type 1 (per-EVI Ethernet A-D) routes on the PE-PE link. Decode the L2-Attr extended community in the captured packets to examine the P bit state.
Verify: Captured Type 1 routes show L2-Attr extended community with P bit indicating primary vs backup role per RFC7432bis §8.6. `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms DF role assignment matches the preference hierarchy. `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` displays received routes with extended community attributes.
0f7e96f10a7d2ab3.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
0f7e96f10a7d2ab3.expectation:
Pass: L2-Attr extended community present in Type 1 routes with P bit correctly encoding primary/backup distinction per RFC7432bis §8.6; DF role from `show evpn ethernet-segment` matches service-carving preference order (higher preference is DF).
Fail-on: L2-Attr extended community missing, P bit state does not distinguish primary from backup, or DF election result contradicts configured preference hierarchy.

0fa5526ad9f3f137.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs multihomed to ES-1 (ESI Type 1: 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:01:02:03) via IXIA LAG; enable BGP RT Constraint (RFC4684) on both PEs (`af-rtfilter` under `configuration/routing/bgp/neighbor`); configure DUT `import-rt` policy to accept only ES-Import RT derived from ESI-1 (06:02:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff); configure neighbor PE with second ES-2 (ESI Type 1: 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:07:08:09) not multihomed to DUT.
Action: Neighbor PE advertises BGP EVPN route-type 4 (Ethernet Segment routes) for both ESI-1 and ESI-2 with auto-derived ES-Import RT extended communities (Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x02, high-order 6 octets of ESI); DUT advertises RT Constraint membership NLRI constraining to ESI-1's ES-Import RT only; capture BGP UPDATE messages showing RT Constraint filtering in action.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail` on DUT shows ESI-1 ES route with ES-Import RT 06:02:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff imported; ESI-2 ES route (ES-Import RT 06:02:11:22:33:44:55:66) absent from DUT BGP table; `show bgp rt-constraint` confirms DUT advertised RT membership limiting to ESI-1's ES-Import RT; neighbor PE debug logs confirm ES route for ESI-2 suppressed to DUT per RT Constraint.
0fa5526ad9f3f137.expectation:
Pass:    DUT imports ES route for ESI-1 (matching RT Constraint policy) and filters ES route for ESI-2 (non-matching ES-Import RT); `show bgp rt-constraint` on both PEs shows RT membership NLRIs exchanged; RT Constraint procedures apply to ES-Import RT extended community per RFC4684.
Fail-on: DUT imports both ES routes regardless of RT Constraint policy (RFC4684 not applied to ES-Import RT), or DUT imports neither ES route, or BGP RT Constraint session fails to establish, or RT membership NLRIs not exchanged.

0fc338e3c0afb5bf.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS instance under `configuration / l2-services / vpls <vpls-name>` and set `mac-aging-time 600` (non-default value, assuming default is 300 seconds). Commit the configuration.
Action: Execute `copy running-config startup-config` (or vendor equivalent). Reload the DUT via `reload` command. After boot, enter the same VPLS context and execute `no mac-aging-time` to test default restoration. Commit and verify.
Verify: Post-reload, `show running-config` section `l2-services / vpls <vpls-name>` contains `mac-aging-time 600`. After `no mac-aging-time`, verify `show running-config` either omits the line (implicit default) or shows `mac-aging-time 300` (explicit default). Confirm `show mac-address-table aging-time` or equivalent reflects the configured/default values.
0fc338e3c0afb5bf.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
0fc338e3c0afb5bf.expectation:
Pass:    `mac-aging-time 600` persists byte-identical across reload in running-config; MAC table uses 600-second aging. `no mac-aging-time` restores default (300 seconds, or vendor-documented default); MAC table aging updates to default.
Fail-on: `mac-aging-time` value lost, corrupted, or reverts to default after reload without `no` command. `no mac-aging-time` does not restore documented default, leaves aging disabled, or requires DUT restart to take effect.

0fca38d7da877b00.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure aggregate interface `agg-eth 0` with member physical interfaces; ensure interface is administratively up; commit configuration.
Action: Enter `configure`, navigate to `interface agg-eth 0`, issue `ethernet-segment` to enable EVPN multi-homing and enter ethernet-segment sub-configuration mode, then `commit`.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays `ethernet-segment` configuration block; re-enter `configure / interface agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` to confirm sub-configuration context is accessible.
0fca38d7da877b00.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
0fca38d7da877b00.expectation:
Pass:    `ethernet-segment` command accepted without error; commit succeeds; running-config contains `ethernet-segment` block under `interface agg-eth 0`; ethernet-segment sub-configuration mode is accessible
Fail-on: Command rejected with syntax or prerequisite error; commit fails; `ethernet-segment` missing from running-config; ethernet-segment sub-mode inaccessible

0fdc9b2f9aa4fb63.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-aware-bundle, auto-discovery enabled with initial export-rt value; configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0; attach BD; IXIA sends L2 traffic through DUT for ≥ 1 minute.
Action: Modify export-rt under auto-discovery to a different route-target value; commit the change.
Verify: Capture BGP EVPN updates at neighbor PE showing route-type 1 withdrawal/re-advertisement with new RT; check IXIA loss counters; confirm `show running-config` reflects new export-rt.
0fdc9b2f9aa4fb63.expectation:
Pass:    Ethernet A-D per EVI route (type 1) re-advertised with new RT within ≤ 10 s; IXIA reports zero packet loss; route preserves RD, ESI, ET ID (VLAN-ID for vlan-aware-bundle service-type), MPLS Label LT2 per RFC7432bis §8.4.
Fail-on: Any packet loss, or route update not visible at neighbor PE within 10 s, or route structure corrupted (missing/changed ESI, ET ID, or Label fields).

10018f669bca52e1.action_steps:
Setup:  On DUT configure two vlan-aware-bundle EVPN instances on x-eth 0/0/1: `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` for VLANs 1-3 with `import-rt 65000:100` / `export-rt 65000:100`; `evpn evpn2 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` for VLANs 4-5 with `import-rt 65000:200` / `export-rt 65000:200`. Enable `auto-discovery` and `advertise-mac enable` on both. Establish BGP EVPN peering (AF l2vpn evpn) to neighbor PE.
Action: From IXIA connected to x-eth 0/0/1, send BUM frames (broadcast dst ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff, unique src MACs per VLAN) with VLAN tags: 2 (in evpn1 range), 5 (in evpn2 range), 10, 20 (outside any configured bundle) at 100 fps each for 30 seconds.
Verify: `show evpn evpn1 mac-table` (expect learned MACs from VLAN 2 only), `show evpn evpn2 mac-table` (expect VLAN 5 only), `show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics` (ingress-discard counter increments by ~6000 for VLANs 10+20), `show bgp l2vpn evpn routes` on DUT and neighbor PE (RT-2 MAC/IP routes present only for VLANs 2, 5; RTs 65000:100, 65000:200; no routes for VLANs 10, 20).
10018f669bca52e1.expectation:
Pass:    evpn1 learns MACs from VLAN 2; evpn2 learns MACs from VLAN 5; ingress-discard counter increments by 6000 frames (VLANs 10, 20 dropped at input); BGP advertises RT-2 routes for learned MACs in VLANs 2, 5 with correct RTs; neighbor PE receives only routes for configured VLANs; no MAC entries for VLANs 10, 20 in any EVPN instance.
Fail-on: MAC entries for VLAN 10 or 20 appear in evpn1/evpn2 mac-table; ingress-discard counter does not increment or increments incorrectly; BGP advertises RT-2 routes for VLANs 10, 20; frames with configured VLANs (2, 5) are dropped; cross-contamination between evpn1 and evpn2 (VLAN 2 MACs in evpn2 or vice versa).

1021e8964399f74d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance with agg-eth interface: `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88.99; load-balancing-mode single-active`. Configure neighbor PE with matching ESI. IXIA generates bidirectional MAC unicast traffic (50 local CE MACs via attachment circuit + 50 remote PE MACs via EVPN). Pre-verify `show l2-services evpn mac-table` shows 100 entries with correct next-hop types (local ifIndex for local CEs, remote IP for remote PEs).
Action: Kill EVPN control-plane process (e.g., `kill -9 $(pidof evpnd)` or platform debug command). Start timer. Monitor MACT state and IXIA Tx/Rx counters during process restart and MACT repopulation.
Verify: `show system processes | grep evpn` confirms process restart. `show l2-services evpn mac-table` confirms entry count (100), VLAN-ID/MAC keys, ESI values, and next-hop types (local attachment circuit ifIndex vs. remote IP). IXIA measures traffic loss duration.
1021e8964399f74d.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE (remote PE required to populate remote next-hop IP entries in MACT; IXIA generates local and remote-direction traffic)
1021e8964399f74d.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN process restarts within 10 sec; MAC Unicast Forwarding Table fully repopulated (all 100 entries with correct VLAN-ID, MAC, ESI, next-hop type) within es-waiting-time (3 sec default) + 2 sec convergence margin = 5 sec total; IXIA-measured traffic loss ≤ 5 sec; no next-hop type corruption (local/remote swap).
Fail-on: Process fails to restart, MACT missing entries after 30 sec, incorrect next-hop types (local ifIndex appearing for remote MAC or vice versa), VLAN-ID/MAC key mismatch, ESI field corrupted/null, or traffic loss > 5 sec.

1082b9e08da21293.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance "EVPN-PB-TEST" with service-type port-based on LAG agg-lag 1 and Ethernet x-eth 0/0/1; enable auto-discovery with import-rt 65000:100 and export-rt 65000:100; save to startup-config; stage new image on ONIE server.
Action: Execute `onie-install <new-image-url>` and wait for upgrade + reboot.
Verify: Run `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN-PB-TEST` to confirm service-type port-based, auto-discovery, import-rt 65000:100, export-rt 65000:100, and both LAG agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 bindings are intact; replay the full EVPN config block (copy/paste from saved pre-upgrade config) to verify no syntax errors.
1082b9e08da21293.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN instance "EVPN-PB-TEST" with service-type port-based persists; auto-discovery enabled; import-rt 65000:100 and export-rt 65000:100 present; LAG agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 remain bound; config re-application succeeds with no CLI errors.
Fail-on: service-type changes or becomes undefined; auto-discovery disabled or route targets lost; interface bindings cleared; config re-application returns "% Incomplete command" or syntax error.

108cddab650550ec.action_steps:
Setup:  Create EVPN instance (`evpn evpn-1` under `configuration / l2-services`), commit; `show running-config` should contain no explicit `es-waiting-time` (default = 3 seconds per requirement).
Action: Configure `es-waiting-time 10`, commit; then issue `no es-waiting-time`, commit.
Verify: After first commit, `show running-config` displays `es-waiting-time 10` under evpn-1; after `no` form, explicit line is absent (default restored).
108cddab650550ec.expectation:
Pass:    Configured value (10) appears in running-config; `no` form removes explicit config, restoring 3-second default.
Fail-on: Command rejected, configured value (10) doesn't appear in running-config, `no` form doesn't remove explicit line, or CLI error.

112c1950cc64816b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure three ethernet segments on DUT: ES1 with ESI type 0 (manual 10-byte value), ES2 with ESI type 1 (LACP-based LAG to neighbor PE), ES3 with ESI type 4 (router-ID based single-home interface). Configure EVPN instance (vlan-aware-bundle) binding to all three ESs, enable BGP EVPN with neighbor PE.
Action: Bring up EVPN, verify BGP session established, verify EVPN type-1 and type-4 routes advertised. Collect tech-support bundle.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: `show evpn ethernet-segment` (all three ESIs present with type field 0/1/4), `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` (multi-homing status for type 1, single-home for type 4), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` and `route-type 4` (AD-per-ES and ES routes), running-config (ES interface bindings, ESI values), syslog entries for ES discovery.
112c1950cc64816b.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains `show evpn ethernet-segment` output listing all three ESI types (0, 1, 4) with 10-byte ESI values in hex, multi-homing flag set for type 1, single-home for type 4, and running-config showing ES-to-interface bindings.
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn ethernet-segment` output, ESI type field not displayed, fewer than 3 ES entries captured, or missing BGP EVPN route-type 1/4 outputs.

116ae2ca97c125c0.action_steps:
Setup:  Multi-home customer VLAN 100 to Exaware PE1 and Cisco PE2 via LAG with `identifier 0 01.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.11`, `load-balancing-mode all-active` on both sides. Configure remote PE3 (not on ES) for EVPN instance 100 with ingress-replication; attach IXIA to customer segment.
Action: IXIA sends broadcast ARP (DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) into VLAN 100. Simultaneously capture MPLS on PE1→PE2 link and PE1→PE3 link. Monitor Cisco PE2 LAG interface for forwarding.
Verify: Parse BGP UPDATE from PE2 for type-1 (A-D per ES) route containing ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 and ESI Label Extended Community with label value (e.g., 24000). In PE1→PE2 capture, decode MPLS: expect 3+ labels with bottom-1 = ESI label 24000. In PE1→PE3 capture, decode MPLS: expect 2 labels (transport + VPN), no ESI label. On Cisco PE2 LAG interface counters: TX must NOT increment (split-horizon blocks forward-to-ES). Customer capture: exactly 1 ARP frame.
116ae2ca97c125c0.expectation:
Pass:    Cisco PE2 advertises ESI label (16..1048575) in BGP type-1 route; Exaware PE1 pushes this label in BUM replicas to PE2; Cisco PE2 does not forward ESI-labeled packets to local LAG; PE1 sends no ESI label in BUM to PE3; customer sees no duplicate; Cisco accepts Exaware ESI label encoding without BGP error.
Fail-on: PE2 type-1 route omits ESI Label Extended Community; PE1 omits ESI label when sending BUM to PE2; Cisco PE2 TX counter on LAG increments (forwarded back, loop risk); PE1 includes ESI label in BUM to PE3; customer captures >1 copy of ARP; Cisco logs "malformed ESI label" or sends BGP NOTIFICATION.

11764ed2c70dc57b.action_steps:
Setup:  Create `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` on x-eth 0/0/1 for VLAN-IDs 1-100 (per Example 1 in spec); configure `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, `export-rt` under evpn1; IXIA port attached to x-eth 0/0/1 sends BUM frames: broadcast (DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF), unknown-unicast (DA=00:11:22:33:44:55 not in FDB), multicast (DA=01:00:5E:01:01:01).
Action: IXIA transmits 1000 pps per BUM type on VLAN 50 (in-bundle) and VLAN 200 (out-of-bundle) for 10 seconds (30k frames/VLAN total).
Verify: `show l2-services evpn evpn1 statistics` for accept/drop counts; packet capture at egress; `show interfaces x-eth 0/0/1` for VLAN-filter drop counter.
11764ed2c70dc57b.expectation:
Pass:    VLAN 50 frames: ≥30k accepted, visible in evpn1 stats; VLAN 200 frames: ≥30k dropped, drop counter incremented, zero egress, no VLAN 200 MACs in evpn1 MAC table
Fail-on: VLAN 200 frames forwarded/bridged; VLAN 200 MAC entries in evpn1 FDB; drop counter = 0; crash or state corruption

1189c6239fd5120c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-based, attach CE-facing interface; configure mac-aging-time 300 under l2-services/vpls. Start IXIA bidirectional L2 traffic (100 flows, 1000 pps) to trigger Data-Plane MAC learning on the CE interface.
Action: While traffic flows, execute `mac-aging-time 600` under configuration/l2-services/vpls, wait 5s, then execute `mac-aging-time 300`. Capture IXIA loss counters and timestamp of `show running-config | include mac-aging-time` updates.
Verify: `show mac-address-table` displays MAC entries learned via data-plane (not control-plane flags); `show running-config` reflects mac-aging-time 600 → 300 changes; IXIA Rx counters match Tx counters (zero loss).
1189c6239fd5120c.expectation:
Pass:    Both mac-aging-time modifications complete within ≤ 1 s of CLI commit; IXIA reports zero packet loss across both changes; Data-Plane MAC learning continues (entries present in show mac-address-table without control-plane markers).
Fail-on: Packet loss > 0 during either config change, or mac-aging-time not updated in show running-config within 1 s, or MAC entries disappear/switch to Control-Plane learning, or LACP-learned MACs appear in the table.

11990a9a4e0bc1f1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance (VNI 100, RD 65001:100, RT 100:100); establish eBGP session with IXIA emulating neighbor PE (AS 65002); enable advertise-mac.
Action: IXIA injects three malformed BGP UPDATEs containing Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes: (a) Default Gateway extended community with transitive bit incorrectly set to 0, (b) Type 2 NLRI with length field = 0x00 (underflow), (c) MAC Mobility extended community with reserved Flag bits 0x80 set. Inject one valid Type 2 route (MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, IP 10.1.1.1/32, VNI 100, valid Default Gateway EC) as control.
Verify: show bgp evpn route (only valid Type 2 route present in RIB); show bgp neighbor <ixia-ip> (Rcvd Updates Error counter ≥ 3); show l2-services evpn mac-table (only 00:11:22:33:44:55 learned); syslog | grep "malformed\|invalid" (3 distinct parse errors logged); show bgp neighbor <ixia-ip> state (session Established).
11990a9a4e0bc1f1.expectation:
Pass:    All three malformed Type 2 routes rejected at BGP UPDATE parse; BGP session remains Established; Rcvd Updates Error counter increments by exactly 3; syslog contains "invalid extended community" or "malformed NLRI length" for each injection; valid Type 2 route installed in MAC table; no malformed routes re-advertised to other BGP peers (verify via IXIA capture on alternate peer port if present).
Fail-on: BGP session transitions to Idle/Active; any malformed route appears in RIB (show bgp evpn route) or MAC table; DUT crash/reload; Update Errors counter = 0 or < 3; syslog silent (no parse error logged); valid Type 2 route rejected; malformed route observed in BGP UPDATE to 3rd peer.

11a2bc7a026a68e2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with default parameters (es-waiting-time defaults to 3 seconds, load-balancing-mode defaults to single-active). Configure agg-eth interface with ethernet-segment using default identifier type 4. Establish EVPN neighbor session with PE.
Action: Send unicast MAC frames from IXIA (local CE) with specific VLAN-ID and MAC addresses to trigger local MAC learning. Inject EVPN Type-2 routes from neighbor PE with distinct MAC addresses to trigger remote MAC learning. Generate bidirectional traffic to populate MACT.
Verify: Execute `show l2-services evpn mac-table` and `show l2-services evpn mac-table detail` to inspect MACT entries. Confirm local MAC entries reference attachment circuit ifIndex as next hop. Confirm remote MAC entries reference neighbor PE IP as next hop. Validate each entry contains VLAN-ID (key), MAC Address (key), ESI, and Next Hop fields.
11a2bc7a026a68e2.expectation:
Pass:    One MACT created per EVPN instance; local MAC entries show attachment circuit ifIndex as local next hop type; remote MAC entries show PE IP as remote next hop type; all entries contain VLAN-ID, MAC Address, ESI, Next Hop fields.
Fail-on: MACT not created when EVPN instance configured; MAC entries missing any mandatory field (VLAN-ID, MAC Address, ESI, Next Hop); local/remote next hop types swapped or incorrect; multiple MACTs per EVPN instance.

11b664a2975cb8bc.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that tech-support captures evidence of VLAN-ID filtering behavior when BUM frames with VLAN-IDs outside the configured vlan-aware-bundle range are received.
Method: Configure a vlan-aware-bundle EVI for a specific VLAN range, send BUM traffic with both in-range and out-of-range VLAN-IDs, collect tech-support bundle, and verify it contains VLAN membership configuration, drop counters for out-of-range frames, and MAC learning only for in-range VLANs.
Setup: Two-PE MPLS topology with BGP EVPN operational. Configure vlan-aware-bundle EVI on DUT for VLAN-IDs 10-20 on access port x-eth 0/0/1. Neighbor PE configured with matching import/export route targets.
Action: Send BUM frames (broadcast ARP) via IXIA CE emulator with VLAN 15 (in-range) and VLAN 25 (out-of-range). Run `tech-support` command on DUT and save bundle off-box.
Verify: Open bundle and confirm presence of: (1) `show evpn evi` output showing vlan-aware-bundle configuration and VLAN-IDs 10-20 membership, (2) `show evpn mac address-table` showing MAC learning only for VLAN 15, (3) interface or EVI drop/discard counters for VLAN 25 frames, (4) `show running-config` section with the EVI and VLAN range configuration, (5) kernel/FIB tables reflecting the VLAN filter state.
11b664a2975cb8bc.expectation:
Pass: Tech-support bundle contains all five verification items, with drop counters > 0 for VLAN 25 and MAC entries present only for VLAN 15.
Fail-on: Bundle missing drop counters, or bundle shows MAC learning for VLAN 25, or bundle missing VLAN membership configuration, or bundle incomplete (missing show output or FIB state).

11caa804e602336e.action_steps:
Setup:  Create agg-eth 10, enter configuration mode → `interface agg-eth 10`, configure `lacp-key 500` (non-default value), commit.
Action: Issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 10` to confirm `lacp-key 500` is present; then issue `no lacp-key` under the interface, commit, and re-check running-config.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 10` before `no lacp-key` displays `lacp-key 500`; after `no lacp-key`, the line is absent or shows `lacp-key 10` (interface number default); `show lacp internal` confirms the interface uses administrative key 10.
11caa804e602336e.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
11caa804e602336e.expectation:
Pass:    Custom `lacp-key 500` accepted and persists in running-config; `no lacp-key` removes the explicit line and LACP administrative key reverts to interface number (10) observable in `show lacp internal`.
Fail-on: Custom key rejected, `no lacp-key` fails to restore default, or default administrative key is not the agg-eth interface number.

11e4a6b56586a9ec.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface `agg-eth 5` with member link `x-eth 0/0/1` under `configuration / interface / agg-eth`. Do not configure lacp-key. Enable LACP on the interface and commit.
Action: Check the LACP Administrative Key value in operational state (should equal 5, the interface number). Then configure lacp-key explicitly to value 5 under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 5`, commit, and verify idempotency via running-config.
Verify: `show lacp interface agg-eth 5` or `show lacp internal` displays Administrative Key = 5; `show running-config interface agg-eth 5` before and after explicit lacp-key configuration.
11e4a6b56586a9ec.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
11e4a6b56586a9ec.expectation:
Pass:    LACP Administrative Key defaults to 5 (the interface number for `agg-eth 5`). After explicitly configuring lacp-key to 5, `show running-config` omits the lacp-key line (idempotent default behavior).
Fail-on: LACP Administrative Key is not 5, or explicitly setting lacp-key to 5 adds the line to `show running-config` (non-idempotent).

11eac10990e614df.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with All-Active ES: interface agg-eth / ethernet-segment / load-balancing-mode all-active, identifier 0 <type0-value>. Establish P2MP MPLS LSPs for BUM transport. Verify DUT (Non-DF) advertises Ethernet A-D per ES route with upstream-assigned ESI label; verify neighbor PE (connected to same ESI) programs label in context label space for DUT and forwarding entry blocks packets with this label from being sent onto the ES (RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2 split-horizon). IXIA sends BUM traffic over P2MP LSP.
Action: Power-cycle DUT via PDU harness while BUM traffic is active.
Verify: Post-boot: show ethernet-segment confirms load-balancing-mode all-active and identifier 0 restored; show bgp evpn routes type 1 confirms Ethernet A-D per ES route re-advertised with ESI label; neighbor PE show mpls forwarding confirms label re-installed with split-horizon rule (no egress to ES interface); IXIA verifies BUM traffic resumes with no duplicate frames on ES.
11eac10990e614df.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (same ESI) + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
11eac10990e614df.expectation:
Pass:    DUT re-advertises Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) with upstream-assigned ESI label; neighbor PE re-programs label with split-horizon (must NOT forward onto ES per §8.3.1.2); BUM traffic resumes; no duplicates on ES after es-waiting-time (default 3 s).
Fail-on: Lost ES config, Ethernet A-D route missing/wrong ESI label, neighbor PE forwards BUM back to ES (split-horizon violation), BUM blackhole >10 s, or duplicate frames observed on ES.

1239a85aa2a06ec3.action_steps:
Problem: Validate ESI generation for all three supported types: Type 0 manual (multi-homing), Type 1 LACP-derived (multi-homing), Type 4 auto-default (single-homing).
Method: Test Type 0 and Type 1 on a dual-homed LAG between two PEs and one CE; test Type 4 on a single-homed interface.
Setup: Two PEs with MPLS core and BGP EVPN peering; CE1 dual-homed via LACP LAG to both PEs; CE2 single-homed to PE1; vlan-based EVPN instance on each PE.
Action: Configure ethernet-segment on both PEs' LAG interfaces: (a) Type 0 with matching manual 9-octet ESI; verify LAG up and ES route; (b) Reconfigure to Type 1 LACP-derived ESI; verify ES route; (c) On PE1 single-homed interface to CE2, configure ethernet-segment without explicit ESI to trigger Type 4 auto-default from router-id+ifIndex.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs for Type 0/1 (ESI must match), on PE1 for Type 4 (auto-generated ESI present); BGP EVPN table for ES route advertisement; LAG and single-homed interface operational status.
1239a85aa2a06ec3.expectation:
Pass:    Type 0 and Type 1: identical ESI on both PEs, ES route advertised, LAG up; Type 4: auto-derived ESI present, interface up.
Fail-on: ESI mismatch for Type 0/1 between PEs; ES route missing for multi-homed types; Type 4 fails to auto-generate ESI; interfaces fail to come up.

124456703f863c60.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 with member interfaces; connect a member port to IXIA configured for LACP partner role with frame capture enabled.
Action: Under `interface agg-eth 0`, apply lacp-system-mac with a custom MAC address (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55); commit configuration; allow LACP negotiation to complete; capture DUT-transmitted LACP PDUs.
Verify: Decode captured LACP PDU Actor System ID field; confirm it equals the configured custom MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55). Execute `no lacp-system-mac`; commit; capture next LACP PDU; verify Actor System ID now matches the agg-eth 0 interface MAC address.
124456703f863c60.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (IXIA as LACP partner + PDU capture/decode)
124456703f863c60.expectation:
Pass:    LACP Actor System ID reflects custom MAC when configured; reverts to agg-eth 0 interface MAC after `no lacp-system-mac`
Fail-on: Actor System ID unchanged after custom MAC configured, or unchanged after `no` command (stuck on custom MAC)

12889cb6205d0ae9.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN instance (`evpn EVPN1 service-type vlan-based`) and one ES configured via CLI; BGP EVPN neighbor peering established; Route Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) advertised with RD, ESI key, ET ID = MAX-ET, MPLS Label = 0, and ESI Label Extended Community (LT4 label for <EVI, ES>); NETCONF client authenticated.
Action: Use NETCONF `<edit-config>` to modify `auto-discovery`, `import-rt route-target`, `export-rt route-target` under `configuration/l2-services/evpn/EVPN1`; capture Route Type 1 update with `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 <ESI>` before/after; query NETCONF `<get-config>` and compare to CLI `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN1`.
Verify: NETCONF `<get-config>` matches CLI; Route Type 1 ESI label value (LT4 type: <EVI, ES>) identical pre/post NETCONF change (RFC7432bis ch.8.2.1 MUST); RD, ESI, ET ID = MAX-ET, MPLS Label = 0 fields unchanged; no mismatch alarm triggered; EVPN YANG capability in NETCONF `<hello>`.
12889cb6205d0ae9.expectation:
Pass:    NETCONF and CLI produce identical Route Type 1 advertisements: same RD, ESI key, ET ID = MAX-ET, MPLS Label = 0, ESI Label Extended Community value; ESI label (LT4: <EVI, ES>) consistent across transports per RFC7432bis ch.8.2.1; `show evpn` output identical; NETCONF `<get-config>` roundtrips without loss.
Fail-on: NETCONF `<edit-config>` rejects valid auto-discovery/import-rt/export-rt config; ESI label value changes after NETCONF modification (RFC7432bis ch.8.2.1 violation); Route Type 1 fields diverge between CLI/NETCONF views; mismatch alarm raised despite identical ESI labels; YANG model missing for Route Type 1 parameters.

12a199fa5da8b9c3.action_steps:
Setup:  Create route-policy "EVPN-IN-POLICY" that matches routes with extended community RT 65000:100 and sets local-preference to 200. Configure BGP neighbor 10.1.1.2 in mode `configuration / routing / bgp / 65000 / neighbor / 10.1.1.2 / af-l2vpn / evpn` with no policy attached.
Action: Attach route-policy "EVPN-IN-POLICY" to the inbound direction for neighbor 10.1.1.2 within the EVPN address-family. Neighbor advertises EVPN Type 2 MAC/IP routes, some with RT 65000:100.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms routes from neighbor 10.1.1.2 with RT 65000:100 display local-preference 200; routes without RT 65000:100 retain default local-preference 100; `show running-config` displays the policy attachment.
12a199fa5da8b9c3.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (to advertise EVPN routes subject to policy evaluation)
12a199fa5da8b9c3.expectation:
Pass:    Route-policy attachment succeeds at commit; inbound policy evaluation modifies route attributes per the policy rules; only matching routes affected
Fail-on: Policy attachment rejected OR policy not evaluated (local-preference unchanged for matching routes) OR policy applied globally instead of per-neighbor

12e0c59b343c07f5.action_steps:
Setup:
1. Two-PE topology with MPLS backbone; configure BGP peering between PE1 and PE2.
2. Create VLAN-based EVPN instance on both PEs with RD 1:1, import-rt 100:1, export-rt 100:1.
3. On PE1, under BGP neighbor config enter `af-l2vpn evpn` context; apply route-reflector-client, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, and maximum-prefix knobs per REQ#20.
4. Bind access port x-eth 0/0/1 on PE1 to EVPN; connect CE; generate 10 MAC addresses on VLAN 100.

Action:
1. Bring up BGP EVPN session; verify with `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`.
2. Baseline: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` (route count), `show evpn evi` (local MAC count), `show evpn mac address-table`.
3. Start IXIA bidirectional traffic at 100 Mbps for ≥ 3 min; re-read all counters.
4. Apply weight and allow-as-in in af-l2vpn evpn context; confirm session stability with `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`.

Verify:
1. BGP session up; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` reflects soft-reconfiguration and RR-client status.
2. PE1 learns 10 local MACs (`show evpn evi`); PE1 receives remote MACs from PE2 via BGP (`show bgp l2vpn evpn`).
3. EVPN MAC counters, BGP route counters, and interface TX/RX counters all increment monotonically.
4. Applied weight and allow-as-in take effect; BGP session remains established; traffic continues forwarding.
12e0c59b343c07f5.expectation:
Pass:    All BGP common commands (route-reflector-client, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, weight, allow-as-in, capability, private-as, group) function in af-l2vpn evpn context; BGP session stable; local/remote MAC learning works; counters increment correctly; traffic forwards without loss.
Fail-on: Any BGP command rejected or non-functional, session flaps, MAC learning fails, counters freeze, or traffic drops.

1324a630ab5279e5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP neighbor <neighbor-ip> under af-l2vpn evpn; enable capability negotiation for ORF Prefix-List and Route Refresh. Configure IXIA as a BGP peer advertising these capabilities.
Action: Commit configuration on DUT; establish the BGP session. Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <neighbor-ip>` to display negotiated capabilities.
Verify: Inspect the capabilities section of the output. Confirm both ORF Prefix-List (RFC 5291) and Route Refresh (RFC 2918) are listed under advertised and received capabilities; session state is Established.
1324a630ab5279e5.expectation:
Pass:    Both ORF Prefix-List and Route Refresh capabilities are successfully negotiated (advertised and received); BGP session reaches Established state within the expected convergence time.
Fail-on: One or both capabilities are absent from the negotiated set; session stalls in OpenSent/OpenConfirm due to capability mismatch; BGP logs show capability-related errors.

133a7ccf5fefeb46.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT ethernet-segment with ESI Type 4 (Router-ID based, format 04:RouterID:Discriminator); enter configuration/l2-services/evpn and set advertise-mac enable; create EVPN instance EVI 100; neighbor PE in same EVI with configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor af-l2vpn evpn enabled; IXIA connected to DUT access port.
Action: IXIA sends Ethernet frames with source MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff; DUT learns MAC and generates MAC/IP Advertisement route (Type 2); run show evpn routes type mac-ip on DUT; run show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 detail on neighbor PE; optionally capture BGP UPDATE with tcpdump port 179.
Verify: DUT output shows MAC route with ESI field's most significant byte = 0x04 (Type 4); neighbor PE received route shows 10-octet ESI starting with 0x04; parse NLRI: RD(8)|ESI(10)|EthTag(4)|MAC-len(1)|MAC(6)|IP-len(1)|IP(0/4/16); confirm ESI byte-0 is in valid range [0,5]; repeat test with ESI Types 0,1,2,3,5 to confirm all valid types work.
133a7ccf5fefeb46.expectation:
Pass:    MAC/IP Advertisement route NLRI has ESI most significant byte = 0x04 (valid, within [0,5] inclusive); route advertised from DUT; neighbor PE accepts route with no malformed-NLRI error; show evpn routes on both PEs displays ESI Type correctly; traffic forwarding succeeds; all ESI Types 0-5 tested successfully.
Fail-on: ESI Type byte in NLRI is 0x06 or higher (outside [0,5]); route not generated or advertised; neighbor PE rejects UPDATE with malformed NLRI error; show commands display incorrect ESI Type or missing route; MAC not learned; forwarding fails.

13d9cf8f9f06d2d8.action_steps:
Setup:  Under `configuration / l2-services`, create two EVPN instances: `evpn EVPN-A service-type vlan-based` and `evpn EVPN-B service-type vlan-bundle`; commit.
Action: Execute `show evpn summary` from operational mode.
Verify: Output lists both EVPN-A and EVPN-B with their respective service types (vlan-based, vlan-bundle); note any summary fields (admin/operational state, route counts, MAC counts); confirm instance count is 2.
13d9cf8f9f06d2d8.expectation:
Pass:    Command executes successfully; output displays both configured EVPN instances (EVPN-A with vlan-based, EVPN-B with vlan-bundle); no missing or extra instances.
Fail-on: Command fails or not found, any configured instance missing from output, service-type mismatch, or phantom instances displayed.

13e5c08f6746f556.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (PE1, PE2) with shared ESI on `interface agg-eth 0` to one CE; on each PE under `configuration / interface agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment`, configure `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (same 9-octet ESI on both), `load-balancing-mode single-active`, and `service-carving highest-preference preference 100` on PE1, `service-carving highest-preference preference 50` on PE2. Bring up LACP; verify PE1 is primary DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment`.
Action: Start tcpdump on PE1↔PE2 BGP link (port 179). Issue `shutdown` on PE1 `interface agg-eth 0` to force DF withdrawal. Start IXIA bidirectional traffic toward CE MAC addresses. Wait for PE2 to assume DF role per `show evpn ethernet-segment`.
Verify: Decode captured Type 1 (per-EVI A-D) routes in tcpdump: confirm primary/backup DF signalling per RFC7432bis-§8.6 (check L2-Attr extended community for proper DF designation). Check `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` on both PEs shows primary on PE1, backup on PE2 initially, then primary on PE2 after failover. Confirm IXIA traffic converges to PE2 with zero dropped frames and no duplicate forwarding.
13e5c08f6746f556.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
13e5c08f6746f556.expectation:
Pass:    Type 1 routes advertise with proper primary/backup DF signalling per RFC7432bis-§8.6; PE2 assumes DF role within default `es-waiting-time` (3 s); IXIA traffic converges cleanly with zero simultaneous forwarding by both PEs.
Fail-on: DF signalling missing or incorrect in Type 1 L2-Attr extended community; both PEs forward traffic simultaneously post-failover (single-active violation); traffic black-holed; DF transition takes >3 s; Type 1 routes not advertised.

147c7b6bd285b607.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `lacp-key` on agg-eth 0 with a non-default value (e.g., 100); commit; verify configured key appears in `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`.
Action: Enter configuration mode for agg-eth 0; issue `no lacp-key`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` no longer contains `lacp-key` statement; verify via operational LACP show command that administrative key has reverted to default (interface number 0); if interface is part of an ethernet-segment, confirm ES identifier derivation updates correctly.
147c7b6bd285b607.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
147c7b6bd285b607.expectation:
Pass:    `lacp-key` removed from running-config; LACP administrative key reverts to interface number (0 for agg-eth 0); LACP adjacency remains stable; ES state (if configured) consistent with new key
Fail-on: `lacp-key` persists in running-config, LACP administrative key remains at non-default value, or stale key value affects ES identifier or EVPN route advertisement

149227bb8438979c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment` on DUT with ESI types 0 (locally assigned), 1 (LACP-based), and 5 (ASN-based). Enable `auto-discovery` under `configuration / l2-services / vpls` with import-rt/export-rt. Establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE advertising A-D routes (type 1). IXIA generates bidirectional traffic through multi-homed segments.
Action: Execute control-plane process restart (e.g., `restart protocol bgp` or platform-specific EVPN daemon reset). During recovery window, neighbor PE advertises A-D route NLRIs with ESI most-significant-byte (MSB) values 0, 1, 3, 5 (valid, within 0-5 range) and MSB 6, 7, 255 (invalid, out-of-range per RFC7432bis §7.1).
Verify: Use `show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show bgp evpn route-type 1`, and syslog to confirm: A-D routes with ESI MSB 0-5 are accepted and installed in routing table; A-D routes with ESI MSB 6, 7, 255 are rejected with error log (e.g., "ESI type out of range"); locally configured ethernet-segments re-establish to active state; IXIA traffic loss counters show ≤1s disruption.
149227bb8438979c.expectation:
Pass:    All A-D routes with ESI MSB 0-5 (inclusive) accepted and installed post-recovery; A-D routes with ESI MSB 6, 7, 255 rejected with syslog message indicating invalid ESI type; all ethernet-segments return to operational state within 30s; traffic loss ≤1s.
Fail-on: Invalid ESI type (MSB 6-255) accepted into BGP table or forwarding state; valid ESI types 0-5 rejected; no recovery after 60s; traffic outage >1s; ethernet-segment state corrupted or stuck in "down" after recovery.

149643076255476e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT as single-homed (access interface with no ethernet-segment config). Configure 3rd-party PE as multihomed: ethernet-segment with identifier 0 and load-balancing-mode all-active on agg-eth interface. Establish BGP L2VPN-EVPN session.

Action: From multihomed 3rd-party PE, advertise Ethernet A-D per-ES (Type 1) and per-EVI (Type 1) routes with ESI label and Layer 2 Attributes extended community. Capture OPEN and UPDATE messages on PE↔PE link. On DUT: `show bgp neighbor` (verify L2VPN-EVPN, Layer 2 Attributes, Non-Revertive capabilities exchanged); `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`; `show bgp l2vpn evpn`. Send bidirectional traffic via IXIA through multihomed ES and single-homed segment.

Verify: Captured OPEN confirms all three capabilities negotiated. `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` displays Type 1 routes with ESI and label. `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms routes installed. No NOTIFICATION from DUT. Traffic forwards correctly; DUT performs aliasing (accepts frames from any PE backing the ES).
149643076255476e.expectation:
Pass:    DUT (single-homed) negotiates capabilities (L2-attributes, non-revertive), accepts and installs Type 1 routes with ESI from multihomed PE, forwards traffic using aliasing. Confirms SFS §2.7.9 MUST requirement (strengthens RFC7432bis ch.8.7 from SHOULD to MUST).

Fail-on: DUT fails capability negotiation, rejects Type 1 routes, sends NOTIFICATION, or drops traffic from multihomed ES — violates SFS mandatory multihoming procedure support (would be RFC-compliant under ch.8.7 SHOULD but SFS requires MUST).

14acbf409d3d1bc7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 100 on DUT, PE2, PE3 with NO explicit ingress-replication config (test default behavior); verify unknown-mac-flooding enable (default); establish BGP EVPN peering and VXLAN tunnels; attach IXIA as CE behind DUT in bridge-domain 10.
Action: IXIA sends broadcast ARP request (DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF, SIP=192.168.1.1); DUT must replicate to all remote PEs (PE2, PE3) via default flood mechanism.
Verify: Capture VXLAN-encapsulated frames at PE2 and PE3 tunnel ingress; show l2-services evpn instance 100 statistics on DUT for BUM TX counter.
14acbf409d3d1bc7.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs + IXIA traffic gen
14acbf409d3d1bc7.expectation:
Pass:    Both PE2 and PE3 receive exactly one VXLAN copy of the broadcast ARP; DUT BUM TX counter = 2 (per RFC7432bis §16.1 MUST: broadcast sent to all remote PEs).
Fail-on: Any remote PE does not receive the broadcast, or BUM TX counter ≠ 2, or feature requires explicit config to activate.

1510129033b8c634.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-name service-type vlan-based` with `auto-discovery` enabled; establish BGP L2VPN EVPN address-family session to neighbor PE; send traffic from IXIA to generate MAC learning and EVPN route advertisement.
Action: Execute `show fib evpn-neighbor` and record the neighbor entries, identifiers, and forwarding state indicators.
Verify: Each remote PE that has advertised EVPN routes appears in output; re-execute command to verify idempotent output with stable EVPN state.
1510129033b8c634.expectation:
Pass:    All active EVPN neighbor PEs listed in FIB output with identifying information and forwarding state reflecting learned routes.
Fail-on: Missing neighbor entry for configured and active EVPN peer, empty output when routes are exchanged, or inconsistent output on stable topology.

15acd0d4a14c00b9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed Ethernet Segment with identical ESI on DUT and neighbor PE; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbors; set `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in `/configuration/l2-services/evpn`; configure MP2MP tunnel (Ingress Replication or P-multicast tree) for EVPN instance.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN session between DUT and neighbor PE; exchange Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) and Type 3 (Inclusive Multicast) routes; send BUM traffic from IXIA through multi-homed CE to trigger multicast forwarding via PMSI tunnel.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` on both PEs to extract ESI label values (must be identical); `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3` to extract PMSI Tunnel Attribute label; `show mpls forwarding-table` to confirm labels are from DCB range (not upstream-allocated); packet capture on P-multicast tunnel to verify MPLS label stack; `show evpn ethernet-segment` to confirm multi-homing operational; IXIA BUM traffic counters (Tx vs. Rx, no duplication).
15acd0d4a14c00b9.expectation:
Pass:    ESI labels in Type 1 routes are identical on DUT and neighbor PE and allocated from Domain-wide Common Block (DCB) range; PMSI Tunnel Attribute in Type 3 routes uses DCB label (not upstream-allocated); BUM traffic forwarded without duplication (IXIA Rx count = Tx count × egress PE count); `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows ES status Up on both PEs.
Fail-on: ESI labels differ between DUT and neighbor PE on same ES (violates RFC7432bis §19 MUST); ESI or PMSI labels allocated from upstream range instead of DCB; BUM traffic duplicated (Rx > expected) or black-holed (Rx < expected); multi-homing or multicast forwarding failure (ES status Down, no Type 1/3 routes exchanged).

15b3c96bd0ca69d9.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (PE1, PE2) in same Ethernet Segment; configure `ethernet-segment` under `interface agg-eth`, enable `evpn evpn-name service-type vlan-aware-bundle`, configure Highest-Preference algorithm on both PEs, then apply identical local policy override (e.g., explicit DF preference value) on both PEs. IXIA generates BUM traffic at documented rate targeting 1000 Ethernet Tags on the ES.
Action: Trigger DF election by bringing up the ES, measure initial DF election convergence time. Generate sustained BUM traffic (per RFC9136 profile), then fail the active DF (shutdown interface or isolate PE), measure DF switchover convergence time and traffic loss during failover event.
Verify: On both PEs run `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` to confirm both PEs show same local-policy-overridden DF election result. Measure DF election convergence ≤ spec value, BUM throughput matches injected rate (± spec tolerance), latency within bounds, and traffic loss during DF switchover ≤ maximum allowed per SLA.
15b3c96bd0ca69d9.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs show identical local policy override in DF election output; DF election convergence time ≤ X ms (spec/SLA value); BUM throughput within ±Y% of injected rate; latency ≤ Z ms; traffic loss during DF switchover ≤ W packets/frames.
Fail-on: PEs show inconsistent local policy in DF election (mismatched elected DF); DF election convergence exceeds spec; throughput degradation beyond tolerance; latency spikes above bound; traffic loss exceeds maximum allowed during failover.

15da3b6889b2464e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP AS 65001, VRF `evpn-test`, neighbor 10.0.0.2 under `configuration / routing / bgp / 65001 / vrf / evpn-test / neighbor / 10.0.0.2 / af-l2vpn / evpn`.
Action: Issue `allow-as-in 5`, commit, verify in running-config. Repeat with boundary values: `allow-as-in 1`, `allow-as-in 10`. Test invalid inputs: `allow-as-in 0`, `allow-as-in 11`, `allow-as-in -1`, `allow-as-in abc` (expect parse rejection).
Verify: `show running-config routing bgp 65001 vrf evpn-test neighbor 10.0.0.2` after each valid commit shows the configured value; each invalid input triggers immediate CLI parse error message.
15da3b6889b2464e.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
15da3b6889b2464e.expectation:
Pass:    Values 1, 5, 10 commit successfully and appear in running-config under the neighbor's af-l2vpn evpn context; values 0, 11, -1, and non-integer 'abc' all rejected with CLI parse error naming the parameter.
Fail-on: Any invalid value silently commits, BGP neighbor session crashes, or running-config contains malformed `allow-as-in` entry.

15f2d6ce397cb4da.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with one Ethernet Segment using ESI type 1 (LACP-based, most significant byte = 0x01), establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE, verify ES route type 4 is advertised.
Action: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4`, `show ethernet-segment`, `show running-config | include ethernet-segment`, then collect full tech-support bundle via CLI or NETCONF.
Verify: Parse tech-support archive for: BGP EVPN route type 4 entries showing 10-octet ESI with first byte in range 0x00–0x05, `show ethernet-segment` output with ESI and operational state, running-config containing ESI configuration, syslog entries for ES up/down events.
15f2d6ce397cb4da.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (to advertise/receive ES routes) + IXIA
15f2d6ce397cb4da.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` output with RD + 10-octet ESI (first byte 0x01) + originating router IP; `show ethernet-segment` output with ESI type, identifier, and oper-status; running-config section with `ethernet-segment` and `esi` commands; syslog entries timestamped to ES activation.
Fail-on: Missing BGP EVPN route type 4 table, missing `show ethernet-segment` output, ESI field not present in captured routes, or running-config missing ESI configuration stanza.

15f4003be0e15cb5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homing: Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE both connect to same customer site via ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99; configure `ethernet-segment` under `agg-eth` on both; EVPN instance with matching import-rt/export-rt; ensure one PE is DF, other non-DF for VLAN under test.
Action: Bring up ethernet-segment on both PEs; capture BGP EVPN route exchange (filter Type 1 = Ethernet A-D per ES routes); inject BUM frame from CE behind non-DF PE; capture MPLS-encapsulated BUM packet between non-DF and DF PEs using IXIA tap or port mirror.
Verify: On both PEs: `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` confirms LT4 label allocated for ESI; `show bgp evpn route-type 1` shows Type 1 route with ESI Label Extended Community matching local LT4; packet capture shows BUM forwarded non-DF→DF has label stack [LT3 (broadcast label for EVI), LT4 (ESI label)] in that order; 3rd-party PE processes inbound LT4 and does NOT replicate back to originating ES.
15f4003be0e15cb5.expectation:
Pass:    Both DUT and 3rd-party PE advertise Type 1 routes containing ESI Label Extended Community (RFC7432bis §7.1); captured BUM packet shows two-label stack (LT3 outer, LT4 inner, LT4 value = advertised ESI label); 3rd-party PE honors LT4 split-horizon (no loop back to same ES); bidirectional BUM forwarding succeeds.
Fail-on: Type 1 route missing ESI Label Extended Community; BUM packet has incorrect label stack (LT4 missing, wrong position, or wrong value ≠ advertised ESI label); 3rd-party PE drops BUM or loops it back to originating ES; route encoding mismatch causes BGP session flap or route rejection.

162a136884eb4b08.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance EVI-100, Ingress Replication BUM mode; set `advertise-mac enable` (default) under `configuration/l2-services/evpn`; establish BGP session with PE2, enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor`; configure IXIA port mirror on PE-facing interface for BGP capture.
Action: Activate EVI-100 on DUT; capture BGP UPDATE containing IMET route (Route Type 3, NLRI format: RD:Ethernet-Tag-ID:IP-address-length:Originating-Router-IP) advertised by DUT to PE2.
