Verify: Decode PMSI Tunnel attribute (Path Attribute type code 22, RFC6514§5) in Wireshark: extract Flag field (1 octet), Tunnel Type field (1 octet), MPLS Label field (3 octets), Tunnel Identifier field (4 octets for IPv4); confirm Flag=0x00, Tunnel-Type=0x06 (Ingress Replication), MPLS-Label=valid LT3 label (≥16, ≤1048575), Tunnel-Identifier=DUT router-id/loopback IP.
162a136884eb4b08.expectation:
Pass:    IMET route contains PMSI Tunnel attribute with Flag=0x00, Tunnel-Type=0x06 (Ingress Replication per RFC6514§5), MPLS-Label in range [16–1048575] (valid LT3), Tunnel-Identifier matches DUT originating router IP address
Fail-on: PMSI Tunnel attribute (type 22) missing from IMET UPDATE; Flag≠0x00; Tunnel-Type≠0x06; MPLS-Label=0 or out-of-range; Tunnel-Identifier≠DUT router-id; attribute length or encoding violates RFC6514§5

162b8ca3811be0b2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE with EVPN instance (VNI 1000), enable `advertise-mac enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`. Establish iBGP session with IXIA acting as neighbor PE, enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor`.
Action: IXIA injects three MAC/IP Advertisement routes (EVPN Type 2 NLRI, RFC7432bis §7.2) with ESI most-significant byte set to invalid values: 0x06, 0x07, 0xFF (outside the mandated 0x00–0x05 range). Each route carries a unique MAC address.
Verify: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type macip` to confirm malformed routes absent; check `show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics` for malformed-NLRI counter ≥3; inspect syslog for ESI validation failure entries; confirm `show l2-services mac-table` does not list the injected MAC addresses.
162b8ca3811be0b2.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (IXIA configured as BGP EVPN peer for NLRI fault injection)
162b8ca3811be0b2.expectation:
Pass:    All three malformed routes (ESI MSB 0x06, 0x07, 0xFF) rejected; malformed-NLRI error counter increments by ≥3; syslog records ESI range violation (RFC7432bis §7.2, valid 0x00–0x05); MAC table contains zero entries from the injected MACs.
Fail-on: Any malformed route appears in `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output or MAC table; error counter does not increment; syslog silent on ESI validation; BGP session flap or DUT crash.

162b93e1801e64bf.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with agg-eth interface, enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth <id> / ethernet-segment` mode; ensure ESI is configured (e.g., `esi 00:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11`).
Action: Issue `service-carving default`; commit. Display running-config for this ethernet-segment. Reconfigure with `service-carving highest-random-weight`; commit. Display running-config again. Issue `no service-carving`; commit; display running-config a third time.
Verify: After each commit, run `show running-config interface agg-eth <id> ethernet-segment` and `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment detail` (or equivalent) to confirm the active DF election method matches the last committed choice and that the default is restored after `no service-carving`.
162b93e1801e64bf.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
162b93e1801e64bf.expectation:
Pass:    Only one service-carving method (default | highest-random-weight | highest-preference | lowest-preference) appears in running-config after each commit; the second commit fully replaces the first; `no service-carving` restores 'default' as documented.
Fail-on: Two or more service-carving methods appear simultaneously in running-config; old method persists in show commands after reconfiguration; `no service-carving` leaves a non-default method active; commit rejected for a valid method choice.

1655323367bd0d79.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with reserved ESI (identifier 0 <type0-value>) on agg-eth 0 and non-reserved ESI (identifier 1) on agg-eth 1. Establish EVPN BGP peering to neighbor PE. Configure es-waiting-time to default 3 seconds.
Action: From neighbor PE, advertise MAC/IP Advertisement routes for both ESIs. During route advertisement, trigger BGP session flap (clear bgp neighbor or shutdown/no shutdown BGP session). Observe route arrival sequence: send MAC/IP routes before and after Ethernet A-D per ES routes in different convergence cycles.
Verify: `show evpn mac`, `show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` (MAC/IP), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` (A-D per ES). Capture route processing timestamps relative to BGP session up events.
1655323367bd0d79.expectation:
Pass:    Reserved-ESI MAC (identifier 0) installs forwarding state immediately upon receiving MAC/IP Advertisement route, independent of A-D per ES route presence or BGP convergence state. Non-reserved ESI (identifier 1) MAC installs forwarding state only when both MAC/IP Advertisement AND A-D per ES routes received, even if routes arrive across separate BGP convergence cycles or out of order.
Fail-on: Reserved-ESI MAC delayed waiting for A-D per ES route, OR non-reserved ESI MAC installs before A-D per ES route arrives, OR forwarding state inconsistent after BGP session recovery, OR route resolution violates RFC7432bis §9.2.2 during convergence events.

16a6c8022a36d99b.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN instance; agg-eth 0 with ethernet-segment configured; BGP peering to IXIA established; agg-eth 0 administratively down.
Action: Configure `es-waiting-time` to 10 seconds under configuration / l2-services / evpn; commit. Bring agg-eth 0 up at time T0; IXIA records T1 when first ES route BGP UPDATE arrives. Repeat test with `no es-waiting-time` to verify default (3s) timing.
Verify: `show running-config` displays the configured value; measure delay (T1 - T0) for both test cases (10s configured, 3s default).
16a6c8022a36d99b.expectation:
Pass:    Configured value persists in running-config; measured delay with 10s config is 10±1s; `no` form restores default behavior (measured delay 3±1s).
Fail-on: Command rejected at commit; or delay is always ~3s regardless of configured value; or `no es-waiting-time` fails to restore default; or no ES routes advertised within 30s of interface-up.

174a4c093ea16171.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE MPLS topology; establish BGP EVPN neighbor session (PE1↔PE2); on PE1 create EVPN instance with `evpn evi-1 service-type vlan-based`, configure RD, `import-rt 65000:1`, `export-rt 65000:1`, `auto-discovery`, bind access interface; attach CE single-homed to PE1; IXIA sends bidirectional traffic (1 Gbps, known-unicast + unknown-unicast, PE1↔PE2) for ≥ 60 s baseline.
Action: On PE1 in BGP neighbor config (`configuration routing bgp vrf neighbor`), enter `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family; configure each BGP parameter (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) with appropriate values while traffic continues; commit after each; observe `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`, `show evpn evi`, `show running-config`.
Verify: All eight BGP commands accepted under `af-l2vpn evpn`; BGP session remains Established; local MACs learned from CE ingress traffic; remote MACs learned via BGP Type-2 routes; IXIA reports zero frame loss across all commits.
174a4c093ea16171.expectation:
Pass:    All BGP commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) configurable under `af-l2vpn evpn`; EVPN instance operational; RD configured; local and remote MAC learning functional; zero traffic interruption.
Fail-on: Any BGP command rejected with syntax error, BGP session transitions to Idle/Active, MAC learning fails, or IXIA loss > 0 packets.

184d303613d5cf7c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance and `interface agg-eth X / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 / load-balancing-mode all-active`. Establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE. IXIA sends traffic from MAC M1 homed to the ethernet-segment.
Action: Trigger MAC move (IXIA shifts M1 traffic to neighbor PE, incrementing MAC Mobility sequence number). During active MAC Mobility event, power-cycle the DUT via PDU harness. Wait for full boot + BGP EVPN session re-establishment (check `show bgp evpn summary`). Force a second MAC move (M1 back to DUT) to verify sequence number advancement.
Verify: `show evpn mac | include M1` confirms M1 present with sequence number > pre-reboot value. `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows ES operational. `show l2-services forwarding-table` has correct unicast destination for M1. Repeat MAC move; sequence number increments without duplicate MAC alarm.
184d303613d5cf7c.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
184d303613d5cf7c.expectation:
Pass:    MAC Mobility detection resumes post-reboot; moved MAC M1 appears in `show evpn mac` with incremented sequence number; subsequent MAC moves continue to increment sequence correctly; no duplicate MAC entries or stale routes.
Fail-on: MAC M1 stuck with pre-reboot sequence number, MAC Mobility detection broken (duplicate MACs not detected/sequence frozen), ethernet-segment not operational, or forwarding plane sends traffic to wrong PE.

1850f3e3f6ee6938.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs each with `ethernet-segment` on `agg-eth 0` (same ESI); create two VPLS instances (e.g., EVI 100 with `export-rt 65000:100`, EVI 200 with `export-rt 65000:200`) both associated with the same ethernet-segment; establish BGP `af-l2vpn evpn` session between the PEs.
Action: Enable the ethernet-segment on both PEs; start tcpdump or BGP capture on the inter-PE link to record BGP UPDATE messages containing EVPN route type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES).
Verify: Decode captured BGP UPDATEs; for each route type 1 NLRI with the configured ESI, inspect the path attributes for Route Target extended community (Type 0x00 0x02); confirm the union of RTs across all type 1 routes from this ES includes every RT configured on the associated EVPN instances (65000:100, 65000:200).
1850f3e3f6ee6938.expectation:
Pass:    Every Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) carries at least one RT extended community in its path attributes; the complete set of type 1 routes advertised for the ES includes all RTs from every EVPN instance sharing that ES ({65000:100, 65000:200}).
Fail-on: Any type 1 route with zero RT extended communities in the UPDATE, or the union of RTs across all type 1 routes missing an RT that was configured via `export-rt` on an EVPN instance bound to the ES.

18b12b7d750cc027.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE multi-homed to same CE; configure matching ESI (type 1) on both PEs' agg-eth interfaces; DUT: `ethernet-segment; identifier 1 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88; load-balancing-mode single-active`; establish BGP EVPN peering; IXIA injecting bidirectional traffic via CE.
Action: Bring up EVPN instance on both PEs; trigger DF election (default service-carving); DUT advertises Ethernet A-D per ES route-type 4 with ESI Label extended community.
Verify: On DUT: `show bgp evpn route-type 4` confirms ESI Label ext-community Multihoming redundancy mode field = 1; on 3rd-party PE: confirm route imported and ES deduced as Single-Active (vendor-specific show command); IXIA confirms only DF forwards traffic from CE (non-DF blocks).
18b12b7d750cc027.expectation:
Pass:    DUT advertises route-type 4 with redundancy mode = 1 in ESI Label ext-community; 3rd-party PE imports route and treats ES as Single-Active; DF election completes (`show evpn ethernet-segment` shows one PE as DF, one as non-DF); IXIA traffic flows only through DF, 0 packets via non-DF.
Fail-on: Redundancy mode field in ext-community ≠ 1, 3rd-party PE rejects route or treats ES as All-Active, both PEs forward traffic simultaneously, or DF election fails/times out beyond `es-waiting-time` (default 3 s).

18de5c76311dea2e.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: PE1 and PE2 multi-homed to CE1 via agg-eth with `ethernet-segment identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88` and `load-balancing-mode all-active`; PE3 single-homed to CE2. Configure EVPN instance with ingress-replication on all PEs. Verify DF election via `show evpn ethernet-segment` and capture ESI label advertised by DF in Ethernet A-D per-ES route (BGP UPDATE community).
Action: From CE2 (connected to ingress PE3), inject broadcast ARP or unknown-unicast frame via IXIA. Capture MPLS-encapsulated BUM packet copies on PE3→PE1, PE3→PE2, and PE3→any-other-PE links using tcpdump with `-s0 -w bum_capture.pcap` filtering on UDP 4789 or MPLS label range.
Verify: Wireshark decode of each capture: extract outer MPLS label stack. On DF PE (PE1 or PE2), run `show mpls forwarding-table label <ESI-label>` to confirm local programming and that disposition action is `pop-and-drop` or equivalent (no ES forwarding). On DF PE, capture ES-facing interface to confirm zero BUM leakage despite receiving packet with its own ESI label.
18de5c76311dea2e.expectation:
Pass:    BUM copy to DF PE contains ESI label matching the DF's advertised per-ES label; DF PE mpls forwarding entry for that label has action preventing ES forwarding; tcpdump on DF's ES-facing agg-eth shows no packet egress. BUM copy to non-DF PE (if same ES) has no ESI label. BUM copy to any PE not attached to source ES has no ESI label.
Fail-on: ESI label absent in copy to DF PE; ESI label present in copy to PE not on source ES; DF PE forwards received BUM packet onto the ES (violating split-horizon); `show mpls forwarding-table` lacks entry for advertised ESI label or entry allows ES forwarding.

192eeb47e0996513.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE with same ESI (identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09) on agg-eth interfaces; configure service-carving default on DUT, service-carving highest-random-weight (or omit DF Election Extended Community) on 3rd-party PE; establish BGP EVPN peering; attach multi-homed CE via LAG; create EVPN instance with VLANs 100-110.
Action: Bring up agg-eth interfaces; capture BGP updates showing DUT advertises ES Route (Type 4) with DF Election Extended Community DF Alg=0x00, 3rd-party advertises DF Alg=0x01 or no Extended Community; wait es-waiting-time (default 3 sec) for DF election; send bidirectional IXIA traffic from CE across VLANs 100-110.
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment on DUT displays "DF Algorithm: Default (fallback)"; show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 confirms ES routes exchanged; tcpdump on both PEs' core-facing interfaces to verify only one PE forwards CE→core per VLAN (per RFC7432bis ch.8.5 modulo ESI label); IXIA reports no duplicate frames in core.
192eeb47e0996513.expectation:
Pass:    DUT detects DF Alg mismatch (advertised 0x00, received 0x01 or missing Extended Community) and falls back to Default algorithm per RFC7432bis ch.8.5; DF election completes within es-waiting-time; only elected DF forwards CE→core traffic for each VLAN; zero duplicate frames; BGP session stable.
Fail-on: DUT does not fall back to Default algorithm (show evpn ethernet-segment still reports non-Default); DF election fails or exceeds es-waiting-time + 10 sec; both PEs forward same flow (split-brain, >0 duplicates at IXIA) or neither forwards (black-hole, 100% loss); ES Route (Type 4) rejected or not installed in BGP RIB.

197b71bae6a3f745.action_steps:
Setup:  Create interface agg-eth 0 and enter its configuration mode. Note that the default LACP Administrative Key is the interface number (0).
Action: Configure the lacp-key command with a custom value (e.g., 100) under interface agg-eth 0 and commit. Run `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` to verify. Then issue the no form (`no lacp-key`), commit, and check running-config again.
Verify: After applying the custom value, `show running-config` displays the lacp-key configuration line. After `no lacp-key`, the configuration line is absent, indicating the default (interface number) is restored.
197b71bae6a3f745.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
197b71bae6a3f745.expectation:
Pass:    Custom lacp-key value is accepted, appears in `show running-config`, and is successfully removed by `no lacp-key`. Configuration reverts to default behavior.
Fail-on: Custom value is rejected or not shown in running-config; `no lacp-key` fails or leaves the configuration line present; command generates an error.

19d607f0f698f6fb.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as iBGP route reflector with two neighbors (A and B) in the same AS; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on both neighbors; issue `route-reflector-client` under neighbor-A's `af-l2vpn evpn` submode; leave neighbor-B as standard iBGP peer. Bring up BGP sessions.
Action: From neighbor-B, advertise an EVPN route (Type-2 MAC/IP or Type-3 Inclusive Multicast) to DUT. DUT applies route-reflector rules and reflects the route to neighbor-A.
Verify: On neighbor-A, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn` with detail option or capture BGP UPDATE messages to confirm the route carries ORIGINATOR_ID = neighbor-B's router-id and CLUSTER_LIST containing DUT's cluster-id. Verify neighbor-B does not receive a reflection.
19d607f0f698f6fb.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (iBGP peers for route-reflection topology)
19d607f0f698f6fb.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor-A receives the EVPN route with ORIGINATOR_ID set to neighbor-B's router-id and CLUSTER_LIST containing DUT's cluster-id; neighbor-B does not receive reflection (split-horizon enforced).
Fail-on: Route not reflected to neighbor-A, OR ORIGINATOR_ID attribute missing or != neighbor-B's router-id, OR CLUSTER_LIST attribute missing or does not contain DUT's cluster-id, OR route reflected to neighbor-B (split-horizon violation).

1a02300fafb20cb8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0 with identifier 0 <type0-value> (or identifier 1 or default type 4) and load-balancing-mode (single-active or all-active). Configure neighbor PE with matching ES identifier on its agg-eth interface. Establish BGP EVPN session between DUT and neighbor PE. Verify Ethernet A-D per-ES routes (type 1) advertised in BGP EVPN table.
Action: Power-cycle DUT via lab PDU while ES operational, per-ES routes advertised, and remote PE receiving those routes.
Verify: After full boot: show running-config interface agg-eth 0 confirms ethernet-segment block present with original identifier type/value and load-balancing-mode; BGP EVPN session re-established; Ethernet A-D per-ES route (type 1) for this ES re-advertised and visible on neighbor PE; functional test: administratively disable agg-eth 0 on DUT, verify neighbor PE withdraws DUT from next-hop set and redirects traffic per RFC7432bis ch.8.4 Aliasing.
1a02300fafb20cb8.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
1a02300fafb20cb8.expectation:
Pass:    ES config intact (identifier, load-balancing-mode); BGP session up; per-ES route re-advertised; Aliasing redirection works (neighbor PE reroutes traffic when DUT ES link down)
Fail-on: Lost ES config, BGP down >es-waiting-time + 30 s, missing per-ES route, or neighbor PE fails to redirect traffic on ES link failure

1a5ed8d0cb587781.action_steps:
Problem: Tech-support must capture MAC duplication detection state when a MAC oscillates between PEs and triggers the freeze, so operators can diagnose the duplicate MAC source and BGP route suppression per RFC7432bis §15.1.
Method: Configure detection thresholds, inject duplicate MAC traffic from two IXIA ports behind different PEs to trigger the freeze, run tech-support, and confirm the bundle contains the frozen MAC state, BGP withdrawal evidence, configured thresholds, and operator alerts.
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology with shared EVI. Configure `host mac-address-duplication-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 180` on both PEs (M=180s, N=5 moves per RFC7432bis §15.1 configurability requirement). Two IXIA ports, each behind a different PE, both configured to source the same MAC address.
Action: Generate traffic from both IXIA ports with the duplicate MAC to trigger 5 moves within 180 seconds. Wait for the PE to detect duplication, freeze the MAC, and stop sending BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes per RFC7432bis §15.1. Run `tech-support` and save the bundle off-box.
Verify: Open the bundle and confirm it contains: (1) MAC duplication state showing the frozen MAC (via `show evpn mac address-table` or equivalent duplicate-detection output), (2) `show bgp l2vpn evpn` showing the MAC/IP route is suppressed or withdrawn for that MAC, (3) `show running-config` displaying the configured `move-interval 180`, `move-count 5`, and `freeze-time 180` values, (4) syslog or alarm entries alerting the operator about the duplication event and freeze action.
1a5ed8d0cb587781.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
1a5ed8d0cb587781.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains frozen MAC state, BGP MAC/IP Advertisement route suppression evidence for that MAC address, configured detection thresholds (move-interval and move-count per RFC7432bis §15.1), and operator alert logs showing the duplication event.
Fail-on: Missing MAC duplication state output, missing BGP route withdrawal evidence, missing syslog/alarm entries for the duplication event, or missing configuration section showing `move-interval`/`move-count`/`freeze-time` parameters.

1a709fbc5db1c6a4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure aggregate interface `agg-eth 0` and enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment` mode.
Action: Issue `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (9-octet hex, e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88); commit. Change to `identifier 1`; commit. Change to `identifier 4`; commit. Finally issue `no identifier`; commit to restore type 4 default.
Verify: After each commit, `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays the configured identifier line under ethernet-segment (or absence of explicit config when default applies). If available, `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms ESI type and value match the configuration.
1a709fbc5db1c6a4.expectation:
Pass:    All identifier forms (type 0, 1, 4) are accepted; each commit succeeds; running-config reflects current identifier setting; `no identifier` restores type 4 default (no explicit identifier line or documented default ESI)
Fail-on: Syntax error on any identifier command; commit rejection; running-config shows incorrect ESI type or stale value; ethernet-segment fails to initialize with configured identifier

1acfc8aa13e5bdaa.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that PEs with mismatched DF Election algorithms fall back to Default (RFC7432bis §8.5) to prevent DF split-brain in all-active multi-homing.
Method: Build a two-PE all-active ES with mismatched `service-carving` values, capture BGP route advertisements, and verify DF election converges using Default algorithm.
Setup: Two PEs (PE1, PE2) connect to the same CE via LACP LAG. Configure EVI on both. PE1: `interface agg-eth 0`, `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 1`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `service-carving highest-preference preference 50000`. PE2: `interface agg-eth 0`, `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 1`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `service-carving default`.
Action: Bring up the ES on both PEs; both derive identical ESI from LACP; PE1 advertises Type 4 (ES) route with DF Election Extended Community 'DF Alg'=Highest Preference; PE2 advertises Type 4 with 'DF Alg'=Default; both advertise Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes with ES-Import RT Extended Community; PEs detect algorithm mismatch and fall back to Default (RFC7432bis §8.5); DF election converges.
Verify: Capture BGP: Type 4 routes carry DF Election EC with differing 'DF Alg' values; Type 1 routes carry ES-Import RT EC. `show evpn ethernet-segment detail`: both PEs report identical ESI, fallback to Default confirmed. `show evpn df`: exactly one DF per VLAN using Default algorithm. Traffic: known-unicast load-shared across both PEs; BUM forwarded only by DF; non-DF blocks BUM.
1acfc8aa13e5bdaa.expectation:
Pass: Both PEs detect algorithm mismatch (highest-preference vs default) and fall back to Default algorithm (RFC7432bis §8.5) per EVPNS-REQ#120 MUST; Type 1 and Type 4 routes correctly advertised with ES-Import RT EC (REQ#250, #260, #300); exactly one DF per VLAN; known-unicast load-shared (REQ#150); BUM only from DF.
Fail-on: PEs do not fall back (continue using mismatched algorithms), DF split-brain (two DFs elected), missing ES-Import RT EC in Type 1, or non-DF forwards BUM onto access.

1ada2446a9421607.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure all-active multi-homing ES1 (ESI 00:01:02:03:04:05:06:07:08:09) on LAG1, configure EVPN instance EVI-100 with Flow Label capability (F-bit=1). Configure neighbor PE with matching ES1 and F-bit=1. Attach IXIA to LAG1; DUT learns local MAC-A (IXIA), remote MAC-B on neighbor PE.
Action: Send 1000 pps known unicast from IXIA MAC-A → MAC-B (forces EVPN unicast lookup to F-bit=1 neighbor). Send 100 pps broadcast from IXIA (forces BUM replication with ESI label to neighbor PE). Capture MPLS packets on inter-PE link.
Verify: Run "show evpn instance EVI-100 detail" and "show bgp l2vpn evpn summary" to confirm F-bit=1 learned from neighbor PE. Decode MPLS label stacks in captured packets: known unicast = [transport][VPN-unicast][Flow-Label][payload]; BUM = [transport][VPN-BUM][ESI-label][payload].
1ada2446a9421607.expectation:
Pass:    100% of known unicast packets contain Flow Label after VPN label; 100% of BUM packets contain ESI label only after VPN label (no Flow Label); per RFC7432bis §18.1 "if the VPN label is for known unicast, then the next label MUST be a flow label and if the VPN label is for BUM traffic, then the next label MUST be an ESI label".
Fail-on: Any Flow Label in BUM packets, or any known unicast missing Flow Label to F-bit=1 peer, or ESI label in unicast stack, or wrong label ordering.

1af46e619715cfd3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with `ethernet-segment` on `agg-eth 0` using `identifier 0 <type0-value>` and `load-balancing-mode all-active`. Configure equivalent all-active ES on 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) connected to same dual-homed CE. IXIA simulates the CE, initially sending from DUT-facing link.
Action: IXIA sends frames with MAC M1 from DUT side; DUT advertises type-2 route. Stop DUT-side traffic; start from 3rd-party PE side so PE detects local MAC move and advertises type-2 route with MAC Mobility extended community (seq=1). Move MAC back to DUT; DUT advertises type-2 route with MAC Mobility seq=2.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 2` on both PEs displays MAC Mobility extended community with correct sequence numbers (1, then 2). Capture BGP UPDATE messages on both sides; confirm extended community encoding per RFC7432bis ch.15. IXIA sends traffic destined to M1 after each move; measure delivery to active PE.
1af46e619715cfd3.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs correctly generate MAC Mobility extended community when MAC moves; sequence numbers increment (0→1→2); routes accepted by peer; IXIA traffic reaches M1 at correct PE within 1 second of each move.
Fail-on: MAC Mobility extended community missing or malformed in cross-vendor direction, sequence number does not increment, route rejected by peer, or traffic black-holed >2 seconds after MAC move.

1af770f3a2739d4a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `router bgp 65000` with neighbor 192.0.2.2 (remote-as 65001); under `neighbor 192.0.2.2` add `af-l2vpn evpn`; commit; verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows 192.0.2.2 in Established state and `show bgp neighbor 192.0.2.2` lists L2VPN/EVPN (AFI 25 SAFI 70) in advertised capabilities.
Action: Enter `router bgp 65000 / neighbor 192.0.2.2`; issue `no af-l2vpn evpn`; commit; execute `clear bgp 192.0.2.2 soft in` to refresh without tearing down the session.
Verify: `show running-config router bgp` confirms `af-l2vpn evpn` absent under neighbor 192.0.2.2; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` no longer lists 192.0.2.2; `show bgp neighbor 192.0.2.2` shows AFI/SAFI list excludes L2VPN/EVPN; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` returns empty (all routes from 192.0.2.2 withdrawn); BGP session state remains Established for any other configured AFI/SAFIs.
1af770f3a2739d4a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (e.g., second AxWare router or Cisco/Juniper PE to establish BGP and verify EVPN AFI/SAFI capability negotiation and route withdrawal)
1af770f3a2739d4a.expectation:
Pass:    `af-l2vpn evpn` removed from running-config; neighbor 192.0.2.2 disappears from `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`; zero EVPN routes sourced from 192.0.2.2 in RIB; L2VPN/EVPN capability absent from `show bgp neighbor` advertised caps; session stays up for other AFI/SAFIs.
Fail-on: `af-l2vpn evpn` persists in config after commit; 192.0.2.2 still appears in `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` with State=Established; stale EVPN routes remain in RIB; L2VPN/EVPN capability still advertised; or BGP session transitions to Idle/Active unexpectedly.

1b8cfa1eebf24ba9.action_steps:
Setup:  Connect DUT interface ae0 and neighbor PE interface ae0 to IXIA LAG emulating dual-homed CE. Configure DUT: `interface agg-eth ae0 / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 / load-balancing-mode all-active / service-carving highest-random-weight`. Configure neighbor PE with matching ESI and `service-carving highest-random-weight`. Provision EVPN instance 100 with VLANs 10-12 on both PEs; establish BGP EVPN peering.
Action: Bring up ae0 on both PEs. Send BUM traffic from IXIA on VLANs 10, 11, 12 (broadcast frames, 100 fps each VLAN). Wait for DF election to complete (default es-waiting-time 3 seconds).
Verify: Capture BGP UPDATE from DUT containing Ethernet Segment Route (Type 4) for ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88; parse DF Election Extended Community and confirm DF Alg = 0x01 (HRW per RFC8584 §3). Execute `show evpn ethernet-segment identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 detail` on both PEs; confirm each VLAN 10-12 has exactly one DF (DUT or neighbor per HRW hash). Monitor IXIA Rx: single copy per BUM frame, no duplication.
1b8cfa1eebf24ba9.expectation:
Pass:    DUT advertises ES Route Type 4 with DF Election Extended Community present, DF Alg = 0x01 (highest-random-weight, RFC8584 §3). Each VLAN 10-12 maps to exactly one DF (either DUT or neighbor PE per HRW result). IXIA receives single copy of each BUM frame, 0% duplication, 0% loss.
Fail-on: ES Route Type 4 missing DF Election Extended Community, or DF Alg field ≠ 0x01. Both PEs show DF role for same VLAN (IXIA sees duplicate frames). Neither PE is DF for a VLAN (IXIA Rx rate = 0 for that VLAN). `show evpn ethernet-segment` reports "DF election failed" or ES operationally down.

1c8c0176b15f6b5e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `agg-eth 0` under configuration / interface.
Action: Under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0`, issue `lacp-key 500`, commit, save running-config; then issue `no lacp-key`, commit, save running-config again.
Verify: First `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays `lacp-key 500`; second capture shows either no lacp-key line (default applies) or explicit value matching the interface number (0).
1c8c0176b15f6b5e.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
1c8c0176b15f6b5e.expectation:
Pass:    Custom LACP Administrative Key (500) is accepted, committed, and persisted in running-config; `no lacp-key` restores the default (interface number 0); both states correctly reflected.
Fail-on: Command syntax error, custom key not persisted in running-config, `no` form rejected or does not restore default, or default value is not the interface number.

1cf1b23a8be37191.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT; establish BGP EVPN peering (af-l2vpn evpn) with test PE; configure multi-homed CE with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99.
Action: From test PE/IXIA, send BGP UPDATE for AD-per-ES route (Type 1, ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) containing two ESI Label ECs (label 16000 first, then 17000 second in same UPDATE); repeat test with duplicate ES Import RT ECs (RT 65000:100 twice) and duplicate MAC Mobility ECs (seq 5, then seq 8).
Verify: show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 detail; show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 — inspect extended communities in route; confirm installed ESI Label is 16000 (first), ES Import RT shows single 65000:100, MAC Mobility seq is 5; BGP session remains up.
1cf1b23a8be37191.expectation:
Pass:    DUT installs first EC instance only (ESI Label 16000, first RT 65000:100, first seq 5); subsequent duplicate ECs ignored per RFC7432bis §7.14.2; BGP UPDATE accepted; no Treat-as-withdraw triggered; BUM split-horizon enforcement uses label 16000.
Fail-on: Second EC value honored (label 17000 installed, seq 8 used); UPDATE rejected or treated-as-withdraw; BGP session flaps; duplicate ECs cause route-install failure or conflicting forwarding state.

1d245b0376da4212.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 (DUT), PE2, and CE in EVPN topology; CE connected to PE2 via `agg-eth-1`. On PE2 configure `interface agg-eth-1 / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09`. Verify PE1 `l2-services / evpn / es-waiting-time` = 3 sec (default).
Action: Test ESI=0: Remove PE2 ethernet-segment config (ESI defaults to 0), send IXIA packet from CE to trigger MAC learning, observe PE1 MAC table installation timing. Test non-reserved ESI: Restore PE2 `identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09`, clear MAC tables, delay PE2 Ethernet A-D per ES route advertisement (shut/no-shut ethernet-segment) by 5 sec after MAC/IP Advertisement sent, monitor PE1 MAC installation.
Verify: On PE1: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 detail` (check ESI field = 0 or 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`, `show l2-services forwarding-table evpn mac-address <CE-MAC>`. Compare BGP route reception timestamps (tcpdump or debug log) with MAC table insertion time.
1d245b0376da4212.expectation:
Pass:    ESI=0 test: PE1 installs MAC forwarding state immediately (within 1 sec) upon MAC/IP Advertisement reception alone, no dependency on Ethernet A-D per ES route. Non-reserved ESI test: PE1 installs MAC only after BOTH MAC/IP Advertisement AND Ethernet A-D per ES route received; no installation during the 5-sec delay window.
Fail-on: ESI=0: MAC installation blocked or delayed waiting for non-existent Ethernet A-D route. Non-reserved ESI: MAC installed in PE1 forwarding table before Ethernet A-D per ES route arrives; forwarding state active with incomplete route set (MAC/IP Advertisement only).

1d40ecbeb402f97e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (RD 65000:100, import-rt 65000:100) with BD-1; set control-word enable, unknown-mac-flooding enable (both defaults); configure BGP neighbor with af-l2vpn evpn to peer with neighbor PE.
Action: Activate EVPN instance and BD-1; establish BGP session; trigger Type 3 route advertisement.
Verify: Capture BGP UPDATE on IXIA or neighbor PE; decode Type 3 route: RD=65000:100, ET ID=BD-1, Originating Router IP=DUT loopback, PMSI Tunnel Attr (Flag=0, Type=6 Ingress Replication, MPLS Label LT3, Tunnel ID=DUT loopback), L2-Attr Ext Comm (L2 MTU, Flow Label flag, Control Word flag=enabled), RTs=65000:100, Next Hop=DUT BGP router-id.
1d40ecbeb402f97e.expectation:
Pass:    Type 3 route contains all mandatory fields per spec: RD (key), ET ID (key), Originating Router IP (key), PMSI Tunnel Attr (Flag=0, Type=6, Label LT3 allocated, Tunnel ID=Originating Router IP), L2-Attr Ext Comm (L2 MTU, Flow Label, Control Word=enabled), RTs, Next Hop=advertising PE IP; one route per BD.
Fail-on: Missing mandatory field (RD, ET ID, Originating Router IP, PMSI attr, L2-Attr, RT); PMSI Type≠6 or Flag≠0; Tunnel ID≠Originating Router IP; Next Hop≠advertising PE IP; Control Word flag incorrect; LT3 label not allocated; route count ≠ 1 for BD-1.

1d4fbb195207c2b5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface `agg-eth 10` with no explicit `lacp-key`; verify default LACP administrative key is 10 (matches interface number) via `show running-config interface agg-eth 10`.
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth / agg-eth 10`; issue `lacp-key 2048`; commit. Test boundaries: `lacp-key 0` (min valid), `lacp-key 65535` (max valid). Test invalid inputs: `lacp-key 65536` (above range), `lacp-key -1` (below range), `lacp-key abc` (non-integer). Issue `no lacp-key` to restore default.
Verify: After each valid commit, `show running-config interface agg-eth 10` displays the configured key value; invalid inputs rejected at CLI parse time with error message. After `no lacp-key`, running-config omits the line and key behavior reverts to default (interface number 10).
1d4fbb195207c2b5.expectation:
Pass:    Valid keys (0, 2048, 65535) accepted, committed, and reflected in running-config; each invalid input (65536, -1, abc) rejected immediately with CLI parse error naming the parameter or valid range; `no lacp-key` removes the config line and restores default behavior
Fail-on: Invalid value commits and appears in running-config, CLI accepts out-of-range integer without error, parser crashes or hangs on malformed input, or `no lacp-key` fails to restore default (key != interface number)

1d89d11dc2fd5ab0.action_steps:
Setup:  Enter configuration mode; navigate to l2-services context; configure three EVPN instances: `evpn test-vlan-based service-type vlan-based`, `evpn test-vlan-bundle service-type vlan-bundle`, `evpn test-vlan-aware service-type vlan-aware-bundle`; commit configuration and exit to operational mode.
Action: Execute `show evpn summary` at the operational prompt; record all displayed fields (instance name, service type, operational status, summary counters); wait 10 seconds; execute `show evpn summary` again.
Verify: Output contains all three configured instances (test-vlan-based, test-vlan-bundle, test-vlan-aware); service-type field shows vlan-based, vlan-bundle, and vlan-aware-bundle respectively; operational status and summary data are displayed; second execution produces identical output.
1d89d11dc2fd5ab0.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
1d89d11dc2fd5ab0.expectation:
Pass:    All configured EVPN instances appear in summary with accurate service-type and operational status; output is well-formed and idempotent.
Fail-on: Any instance missing, service-type mismatch or missing, status incorrect, malformed output, or variation between executions on stable configuration.

1e0a27c95978446c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth interface with `service-carving highest-preference` (default preference 32767) and a second ES with `service-carving lowest-preference preference 100`. Bring up EVPN multi-homing with neighbor PE advertising ES route-type 4 for both segments.
Action: Trigger DF election by enabling both Ethernet Segments, wait for DF state convergence, then collect `tech-support` bundle.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: `show running-config` (service-carving lines with/without explicit preference), `show evpn ethernet-segment` (DF role + preference value = 32767 for default, 100 for explicit), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` (DF Election Extended Community octets encode preference correctly per RFC9785 §3 format), and syslog entries for DF election events referencing preference algorithm.
1e0a27c95978446c.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (or IXIA emulating PE for BGP ES route exchange)
1e0a27c95978446c.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains ES config with preference values (32767 default when omitted, 100 when set), BGP ES routes showing DF Election Extended Community with correct preference encoding in last two octets, and DF election logs naming highest/lowest-preference algorithm.
Fail-on: Default preference != 32767 when `service-carving highest-preference` has no explicit preference parameter, or preference out-of-range 0-65535, or BGP route-type 4 missing DF Election Extended Community, or tech-support missing ES/DF state output.

1e185fa7e942a1e4.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN instance 'evpn-100' operationally up; IXIA ports connected to two DUT interfaces in the same bridge domain; MAC duplication detection initially disabled.
Action: Configure `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-100`; commit. IXIA sends L2 frames with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from Port-A (10 moves in 60 seconds), then from Port-B (same MAC).
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-100 | include duplicate-detection`; `show evpn mac-duplication-detection` or `show evpn mac 00:11:22:33:44:55`; syslog for duplicate MAC alerts; MAC frozen after move-count threshold crossed.
1e185fa7e942a1e4.expectation:
Pass:    Config persists in running-config; when source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 moves ≥ 5 times within 180 s, DUT logs duplicate detection, freezes the MAC for 300 s (no forwarding/learning), releases after freeze-time expires.
Fail-on: Commit rejected; duplicate MAC not detected after exceeding move-count; MAC not frozen or freeze-time != 300 s; no syslog alert; `no host mac-address-duplicate-detection` fails to restore default.

1e43fdc781a5cb86.action_steps:
Setup:  On DUT: assign ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 to interface eth1 (`interface eth1; ethernet-segment; esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11`), create EVPN instance (`l2-services; evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle; auto-discovery; export-rt 65000:100; import-rt 65000:100`), enable BGP EVPN AF (`routing; bgp; neighbor 192.0.2.1; af-l2vpn evpn`). On neighbor PE: assign same ESI to interface, configure reciprocal BGP EVPN peering. IXIA: connect as CE to both DUT eth1 and neighbor PE eth1 (ES multi-homing).
Action: Commit config to trigger Type 4 route advertisement with default DF election parameters (Alg 0, bitmap unconfigured); IXIA sends BUM traffic (multicast MAC 01:00:5E:00:00:01, VLAN 100, 1000 pps) from CE side; capture BGP UPDATE with Type 4 route on neighbor PE.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11` on both DUT and neighbor PE displays Type 4 routes with ES-Import RT extended community (value = high 6 octets of ESI: 11:11:11:11:11:11) and DF Election Extended Community; parse DF Election community: bitmap = 32767 (0x7FFF), DF Alg = 0; `show bgp summary`: session Established, Type 4 routes exchanged; traffic: only one PE (the DF) forwards IXIA BUM to core (check `show interface statistics` on both PEs, expect Tx on DF, no Tx on non-DF).
1e43fdc781a5cb86.expectation:
Pass:    Both DUT and neighbor PE advertise Type 4 routes for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 with ES-Import RT 11:11:11:11:11:11; DF Election Extended Community bitmap = 32767 (0x7FFF) in all Type 4 routes (MUST: default value per spec); DF Alg = 0; BGP session Established, routes in `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 received-routes` on each peer; exactly one PE forwards IXIA BUM traffic to core (DF election correct).
Fail-on: Type 4 route missing on either PE; DF Election bitmap ≠ 32767 when unconfigured; bitmap outside 2-octet range [0, 65535]; BGP session not Established; both PEs forward BUM (DF election conflict) or neither forwards (traffic loss); Type 4 route not received by peer.

1e4af2ead50123ca.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology with EVI 100 (VNI 10100, VLAN 100) configured per RFC7432bis ch.13; 'advertise-mac enable' and 'control-word enable' on both PEs; iBGP EVPN neighbor UP; 1000 local MAC addresses advertised per PE via BGP EVPN RT-2 routes; IXIA 10GbE ports emulate CEs.
Action: IXIA generates bidirectional known-unicast L2 traffic at line rate (1518-byte frames, ~9.877 Gbps per direction) for 300 seconds; destination MACs match advertised RT-2 routes, forcing DUT to perform VXLAN encap → tunnel forwarding → remote PE decap per RFC7432bis unicast forwarding rules.
Verify: Capture IXIA throughput (Tx/Rx frames, Mbps), latency distribution (min/avg/P95/P99), frame loss per direction; confirm DUT MAC table via 'show l2-services evpn mac-table evi 100' and VXLAN tunnel counters via 'show l2-services evpn vxlan-tunnel statistics'.
1e4af2ead50123ca.expectation:
Pass:    Aggregate throughput ≥ 19.73 Gbps bidirectional (≥ 99.9% line rate for 1518B @ 10GbE), frame loss < 0.001% per direction (< 2,440 frames per direction), latency P99 < 500 μs
Fail-on: Throughput < 19.73 Gbps, frame loss ≥ 0.001% in either direction, or latency P99 ≥ 500 μs — any metric miss fails the test

1e54b17395018a90.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with agg-eth attachment circuit; configure ethernet-segment with identifier type 0 (9-octet hex value 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09), load-balancing-mode single-active, es-waiting-time 3 seconds; establish BGP EVPN peering with IXIA (remote PE emulation); IXIA advertises 10 valid MAC/IP routes to populate MACT.
Action: IXIA injects malformed EVPN Route Type 2 advertisements: (1) all-zeros MAC, (2) multicast MAC as source, (3) MPLS label in reserved range 0–15, (4) VLAN-ID > 4095, (5) malformed ESI (wrong length), (6) reserved bits set in NLRI, (7) TLV length mismatch.
Verify: Run `show l2-services evpn mac-table` and confirm only 10 valid entries present (VLAN-ID, MAC Address, ESI, remote Next-Hop IP); check `show evpn statistics` for discard counter increments (7 events); check syslog for 7 discard messages with reason codes; confirm BGP session still established.
1e54b17395018a90.expectation:
Pass:    All 7 malformed variants dropped; MACT contains exactly 10 valid entries with correct VLAN-ID (key), MAC Address (key), ESI, and remote Next-Hop IP; error counter increments by 7; syslog shows 7 entries; no crash or BGP flap.
Fail-on: Any malformed entry appears in MACT output; crash, reload, or BGP session down; valid MAC count ≠ 10; silent discard (missing syslog or counter); malformed Route Type 2 propagated to other BGP peers.

1e79784ec1b83947.action_steps:
Setup:  Create EVPN service 'evpn100' with service-type vlan-based under configuration/l2-services; ensure physical interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 exists and is administratively down.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn100` mode; execute `interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1`; commit; capture `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn100`; then execute `no interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1`; commit; capture same show command.
Verify: After add: running-config displays 'interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1' under evpn100 service. After removal: interface line absent from running-config; no orphaned sub-config remains.
1e79784ec1b83947.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
1e79784ec1b83947.expectation:
Pass:    Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 appears in `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn100` after add-commit; disappears completely after no-commit; both commits return success with no warnings.
Fail-on: Interface not present after first commit, still listed after removal commit, commit rejected with error, or orphaned attachment-circuit state visible in `show evpn evpn100 detail`.

1ea7f446c4e4f984.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE in All-Active redundancy mode (`load-balancing-mode all-active`) with same ESI type 0 identifier on agg-eth interfaces; configure EVPN instance with ingress-replication; establish BGP EVPN session between PEs.
Action: Verify both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes with downstream ESI labels via `show bgp evpn route-type 1`; inject BUM frame from CE attached to the ES; IXIA sends MPLS-encapsulated BUM packet directly to DUT using DUT's advertised ESI label; capture packets on ES-facing interface and on core-facing interface.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms ESI label allocation; packet trace shows Non-DF includes DF's ESI label when forwarding BUM to DF; packet with DUT's own ESI label arrives at DUT but does NOT appear on ES-facing interface capture (split-horizon enforced).
1ea7f446c4e4f984.expectation:
Pass:    ESI label present in Ethernet A-D per ES route (route-type 1); BUM packet received with DUT's advertised ESI label is NOT forwarded onto the ES (0 packets on ES-facing capture); Non-DF → DF BUM copy includes DF's ESI label.
Fail-on: No ESI label in Ethernet A-D per ES route for All-Active ES; packet with DUT's ESI label is forwarded back to ES (loop observed); ESI label missing in Non-DF → DF BUM traffic; ESI label included in BUM sent to PE not on the same ES.

1ee9c8c6734cb39c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 with EVPN instance 100, Ethernet Segment ES1 (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00) on port1, and BGP EVPN neighbor to PE2. Configure PE2 with same EVI 100, BGP peering to PE1. Ensure PE2 does NOT advertise MAC/IP (Type-2) route with LT1 for target MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (suppress or withdraw it).
Action: On PE1, verify LT2 label allocation per ES via `show l2-services evpn instance 100 ethernet-segment ES1 detail`. Confirm LT2 advertisement in `show bgp evpn route type 1` (Ethernet A-D per-EVI for ES1). Send known-unicast L2 frame from IXIA (attached to PE2) with dest-MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF. Capture MPLS label stack between PE2 and PE1.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route type 1 rd <PE1-RD> esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00` on PE2 displays PE1's Ethernet A-D per-EVI route with LT2 label value. MPLS capture shows packet carries LT2 label (not LT1). Packet arrives on PE1 ES1 port1, correct ET derived from LT2.
1ee9c8c6734cb39c.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 advertises Type-1 route with LT2 label per RFC7432bis §7.1; forwarded packet from PE2 uses LT2 in MPLS stack per RFC7432bis §8.4; traffic delivered to correct ES (ES1) and ET (0 or sub-interface as configured).
Fail-on: No LT2 label in Type-1 route advertisement, packet uses LT1 despite LT1 not being advertised by remote, traffic delivered to wrong ES or dropped, or LT2 label does not map to ES1 on ingress PE.

1f148f42455a2b7b.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish three BGP peerings with L2VPN EVPN address-family enabled. Configure prefix limit of 10 on first peer (default teardown behavior), 5 with warning-only mode on second peer, 8 with restart interval 2 minutes on third peer.
Action: From first peer, advertise 11 EVPN Type-2 (MAC/IP) routes to exceed its limit. From second peer, advertise 6 routes. From third peer, advertise 9 routes. Start tcpdump to capture BGP messages and monitor syslog. Observe for 3 minutes.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows first peer in Idle state; tcpdump confirms NOTIFICATION code 6 sub-code 1 sent to first peer. Second peer remains ESTABLISHED with syslog message warning of prefix limit exceeded; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms exactly 5 routes accepted from this peer. Third peer tears down, then automatically re-establishes within 2 minutes.
1f148f42455a2b7b.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (BGP/EVPN route advertisement capability)
1f148f42455a2b7b.expectation:
Pass:    First peer enters Idle with NOTIFICATION 6/1. Second peer stays ESTABLISHED, logs prefix-limit warning, caps at 5 routes. Third peer tears down, restarts within 2 min, and re-establishes. No peer accepts routes beyond its configured limit
Fail-on: Any peer accepts routes beyond limit, NOTIFICATION code/sub-code incorrect, warning-only mode tears down session, restart mode fails to reconnect or violates 2-minute interval

1f1b0facd528e533.action_steps:
Problem: diagnostic bundles must capture Route Type 4 Ethernet Segment routes with their ES-Import RT extended communities to enable troubleshooting of multi-homing split-horizon and aliasing failures.
Method: configure multi-homed CE with ESI on two PEs, trigger Route Type 4 advertisement, collect tech-support bundle, and verify it contains BGP EVPN detail showing the ES-Import RT derived from the ESI's high-order 6 octets per RFC7432bis §7.6.

Setup: Two PEs multi-home a CE via LAG (configure ethernet-segment under agg-eth 0 on each PE with identical ESI, e.g., type 1 LACP-based). Establish EVPN BGP peering (af-l2vpn evpn) between PEs.

Action: Verify Route Type 4 is advertised (`show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4`). Run tech-support (or the documented diagnostic bundle command). Save bundle off-box and extract.

Verify: Bundle contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail` output showing ES-Import RT extended community. Inspect that the ES-Import RT value matches the high-order 6 octets of the configured ESI. Bundle contains running-config with ethernet-segment stanza showing the ESI. Bundle contains Ethernet Segment route details covering EVPNS-REQ#250, EVPNS-REQ#260, EVPNS-REQ#300, and RFC7432bis §7.4, §7.6, §8.1.1.
1f1b0facd528e533.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail` with ES-Import RT extended community present and matching the high-order 6 octets of the ESI from the running-config. Bundle includes Route Type 1 and Route Type 4 details for the Ethernet Segment.
Fail-on: Missing Route Type 4 BGP detail output, ES-Import RT extended community absent, ES-Import RT value does not match ESI high-order 6 octets, or ethernet-segment configuration missing from bundle.

1f383767cd053db3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with `af-l2vpn evpn` toward neighbor PE; establish P2MP MPLS LSP (RSVP-TE P2MP = PMSI type 2, or mLDP P2MP = PMSI type 6) as Inclusive P-tunnel; neighbor PE advertises PMSI Tunnel attribute in Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag route (RT-3, per RFC7432bis §11); configure `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on DUT; connect two local ACs to IXIA ports P1 and P2.
Action: Neighbor PE injects three Ethernet frames over the P2MP LSP: frame 1 with unicast DA 00:00:00:11:11:11 and SA 00:00:00:AA:AA:AA, frame 2 with broadcast DA FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF and SA 00:00:00:BB:BB:BB, frame 3 with multicast DA 01:00:5E:01:01:01 and SA 00:00:00:CC:CC:CC.
Verify: IXIA captures frames on P1 and P2; verify each port receives 3 frames; run `show l2-services evpn mac-table` and confirm MAC addresses 00:00:00:AA:AA:AA, 00:00:00:BB:BB:BB, 00:00:00:CC:CC:CC are absent (no learning from P2MP LSP); verify frame 1 floods to both ACs per RFC7432bis §12.2 MUST 2 (unicast DA treated as unknown unicast).
1f383767cd053db3.expectation:
Pass:    IXIA P1 receives frames 1, 2, 3; IXIA P2 receives frames 1, 2, 3 (flood behavior); MAC table contains 0 entries for source MACs from P2MP LSP; unicast frame 1 floods despite unicast DA (§12.2 compliance).
Fail-on: Either IXIA port receives fewer than 3 frames, or frame 1 delivered to only one AC (FDB unicast lookup instead of unknown-unicast flood), or source MACs 00:00:00:AA:AA:AA / BB:BB:BB / CC:CC:CC appear in MAC table, or frames dropped.

1f5098ef8f8c20ab.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as EVPN PE with instance (vlan 100, RD 65000:100, RT 65000:100), loopback0 10.0.0.1 as router-id. Configure neighbor PE with BGP EVPN AF peering.
Action: On DUT: `configuration/l2-services/evpn` set `advertise-mac enable`, `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; `configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor` enable `af-l2vpn evpn`. Capture BGP UPDATE containing route type 3 on wire. Trigger MAC advertisement to also generate route type 2.
Verify: Decode captured route type 3 NLRI: RD, Ethernet Tag ID, Originating Router's IP Address, IP Address Length. On neighbor PE: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 detail` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 detail` to cross-check Originating Router IP field consistency.
1f5098ef8f8c20ab.expectation:
Pass:    Route type 3 has RD = 65000:100 (Type 0/1/2 format per RFC7432bis §7.9), Originating Router's IP = 10.0.0.1 (DUT loopback), IP Address Length = 32 bits. Route type 2 from same DUT also has Originating Router's IP = 10.0.0.1. Repeat test with IPv6 loopback to verify IP Address Length = 128.
Fail-on: RD = 0:0 or malformed, Originating Router's IP = 0.0.0.0 or missing, IP Address Length ≠ 32 (IPv4) or ≠ 128 (IPv6), Originating Router IP inconsistent between route types 2 and 3 from same DUT, or Originating Router IP changes when advertising routes for different EVIs.

1f52705ecc012050.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure loopback 0 with IP 10.0.0.1/32; create EVPN instance (config: l2-services / evpn evpn-up-test service-type vlan-based); capture auto-generated RD via show evpn instance evpn-up-test (expect Type 1 format: 10.0.0.1:<instance-number> per RFC7432bis ch.7.9); record exact RD value; stage next ONIE image on server.
Action: Run onie-install upgrade <image-url>; wait for reboot and system ready.
Verify: show evpn instance evpn-up-test confirms RD unchanged (Type 1, Administrator subfield = 10.0.0.1, Assigned Number = same instance ID); show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-up-test shows no manual route-distinguisher statement; EVPN instance operational.
1f52705ecc012050.expectation:
Pass:    Auto-generated RD preserved exactly (Type 1, loopback 0 IP 10.0.0.1, same Assigned Number as pre-upgrade); no manual RD override in config; EVPN instance operational; running-config replays without errors.
Fail-on: RD format changed (not Type 1 or different IP/instance-number), manual RD statement appears in config, EVPN instance down, or config replay errors mentioning route-distinguisher or l2-services/evpn.

1f6840b272b06a0b.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that a single-homing Exaware PE implements mandatory multihoming procedures (ES/A-D route processing, aliasing, mass withdrawal) when interoperating with a multihomed 3rd-party PE, per SFS §2.7.9 MUST (overrides RFC7432bis ch.8.7 SHOULD).
Method: Configure DUT without `ethernet-segment`, configure Cisco/Juniper PE pair with multihomed ES, exchange BGP EVPN routes (types 1, 4), drive traffic to the ES, shut down one PE, and verify aliasing and convergence within default `es-waiting-time` 3 seconds.
Setup: Exaware DUT configured as single-homing PE (EVI 100, access `agg-eth 0` without `ethernet-segment`); 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) two instances forming multihomed ES with identical ESI type 0; BGP EVPN sessions up with `af-l2vpn evpn` capability negotiated; MPLS transport operational.
Action: On DUT: configure EVI 100, attach `agg-eth 0` without `ethernet-segment`. On 3rd-party: configure multihomed ES (same ESI on two PEs), advertise ES route (type 4) and A-D routes (type 1 per-ES and per-EVI), inject MAC/IP route (type 2) for destination in the ES. Drive L2 traffic from DUT to destination MAC. Shut down one 3rd-party PE to trigger mass withdrawal. Capture BGP updates and forwarding.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` on DUT lists ES route from the multihomed ES. `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` lists A-D per-ES and per-EVI routes from both 3rd-party PEs. `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` shows MAC/IP route with aliasing (next-hop can be either PE). Traffic forwards via either PE (aliasing active). Upon mass withdrawal: routes for down PE withdrawn; traffic shifts to remaining PE within `es-waiting-time` default 3 seconds; no black-hole.
1f6840b272b06a0b.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper, two instances for multihomed ES) + IXIA traffic generator
1f6840b272b06a0b.expectation:
Pass: DUT accepts and processes ES route (type 4) and A-D routes (type 1) from multihomed 3rd-party PE; traffic aliases to either PE in the ES; mass withdrawal converges correctly (routes removed, traffic shifts within 3 seconds); per SFS §2.7.9 MUST (overrides RFC7432bis ch.8.7 SHOULD for single-homing PEs); BGP capability negotiation succeeds.
Fail-on: DUT rejects ES or A-D routes (NOTIFICATION or route not in RIB); aliasing fails (traffic hard-pinned to one PE despite both reachable); mass withdrawal does not converge (traffic black-holes or convergence exceeds `es-waiting-time` default 3 seconds); or BGP session fails during capability negotiation.

1f8fb3c3db6b1136.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (EVI 100, VLAN 100) on DUT and neighbor PE. Configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth interfaces with `identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88` and `load-balancing-mode all-active` on both PEs. Connect IXIA to both PEs via access ports in VLAN 100.
Action: IXIA sends 100 frames with source MAC 00:00:00:11:11:11 from DUT-facing port. Wait 5 seconds. Stop traffic. IXIA immediately sends 100 frames with same source MAC 00:00:00:11:11:11 from neighbor-PE-facing port (simulating MAC move).
Verify: On DUT, `show evpn mac` confirms MAC learned locally (seq=0), then learned via neighbor PE (seq≥1). `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows MAC/IP Advertisement route from neighbor PE includes MAC Mobility Extended Community (type 0x06) with sequence number incremented. Traffic to 00:00:00:11:11:11 forwarded via VXLAN tunnel to neighbor PE.
1f8fb3c3db6b1136.expectation:
Pass:    DUT advertises MAC route with MAC Mobility Extended Community when MAC initially learned (seq=0). Neighbor PE advertises same MAC with incremented sequence (seq=1) after move. DUT updates MAC table and forwards traffic to new location within es-waiting-time (3s default per spec §2.7.6). BGP route from neighbor PE contains MAC Mobility EC per RFC7432bis ch.15.
Fail-on: MAC Mobility Extended Community absent from route updates. Sequence number does not increment (remains 0 after move). Traffic black-holed or forwarded to stale location >3s after move. Duplicate traffic sent to both PEs.

1fa03baa3ef35a74.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN instance evpn-1 configured with `mac-address-static aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1`, BGP EVPN peering established with neighbor PE, Type-2 route for static MAC verified in `show bgp l2vpn evpn`.
Action: Add second static MAC `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface GigabitEthernet0/0/2`, then remove and re-add first MAC. Execute `show tech-support` or equivalent diagnostic bundle command.
Verify: Extract and parse tech-support archive: grep running-config for `mac-address-static` entries, locate `show evpn mac` output containing both MACs marked as static, find `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output with Type-2 routes for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff and 00:11:22:33:44:55, check syslog section for MAC add/remove events.
1fa03baa3ef35a74.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains (1) both `mac-address-static` commands in running-config, (2) `show evpn mac` table listing aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff on Gi0/0/1 and 00:11:22:33:44:55 on Gi0/0/2 with static flag, (3) `show bgp l2vpn evpn` with Type-2 routes [RD]:[0]:[0]:[48]:[MAC] for both MACs, (4) syslog entries timestamped to add/remove actions.
Fail-on: Running-config missing `mac-address-static` lines, `show evpn mac` absent or missing static MACs, no Type-2 routes in `show bgp l2vpn evpn`, or tech-support missing EVPN-specific show commands (show evpn, show bgp l2vpn).

1faf0f2be87ed3ff.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: DUT and neighbor PE dual-homed to CE1 via LAG with shared ESI `identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09`, both configured with `load-balancing-mode all-active` under `ethernet-segment`; remote PE3 with same EVPN instance (EVI), iBGP peering to DUT and neighbor PE.
Action: Bring up LAG on DUT and neighbor PE; wait for Ethernet A-D per-ES route (type 1) advertisement. Generate MAC learning on CE1 (ARP or data traffic) so DUT and neighbor PE both advertise MAC/IP routes (type 2) for the same MAC. At PE3: capture BGP UPDATEs with tcpdump, filter NLRI type 1. Parse ESI Label Extended Community (Type 0x06 Subtype 0x01) and extract flags byte bit 0 (Single-Active/All-Active). Issue `show evpn mac-address <MAC>` at PE3.
Verify: Decode captured type 1 routes: confirm ESI Label EC flags byte bit 0 = 0 (All-Active mode). At PE3, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` displays both routes with redundancy mode=All-Active. `show evpn mac-address <MAC>` shows two next-hops (DUT IP, neighbor PE IP) with ECMP; each next-hop uses the MPLS label from that PE's MAC/IP Advertisement route.
1faf0f2be87ed3ff.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + remote PE3 (three routers minimum) + CE1 traffic source (IXIA or host)
1faf0f2be87ed3ff.expectation:
Pass:    Both Ethernet A-D per-ES routes carry ESI Label EC with flags[0]=0 (All-Active deduced by PE3); PE3 installs the learned MAC with exactly two ECMP next-hops (one per PE); each next-hop's label stack matches the label advertised in that PE's type 2 route.
Fail-on: Any Ethernet A-D per-ES route has flags[0]=1 or EC missing; PE3 installs single next-hop only; next-hop label does not match type 2 advertised label; PE3 does not deduce All-Active.

1fd93cf579c3a4e3.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with VLAN 100 and interface xe-0/0/1 ready. NETCONF client authenticated via SSH port 830, <hello> exchanged with :evpn:1.0 capability confirmed. IXIA port1 connected to DUT xe-0/0/1.
Action: NETCONF <edit-config>: create evpn instance 'evpn-ce1' service-type vlan-based, vlan 100, mac-aging-time 300, bind interface xe-0/0/1. NETCONF <get-config> source=running filter=/l2-services/evpn. IXIA sends 10 L2 frames src-MAC 00:00:00:00:01:01 through 00:00:00:00:01:0A + 1 LACP BPDU src-MAC 00:00:00:00:FF:FF to xe-0/0/1.
Verify: NETCONF <rpc-reply> for get-config contains evpn-ce1 with mac-aging-time=300 and xe-0/0/1 binding. CLI 'show mac-address-table evpn evpn-ce1' lists the 10 data-plane MACs as type=learned. LACP src-MAC 00:00:00:00:FF:FF absent from table. CLI 'show evpn evpn-ce1' output matches NETCONF retrieved parameters.
1fd93cf579c3a4e3.equipment: DUT + NETCONF client + IXIA traffic gen
1fd93cf579c3a4e3.expectation:
Pass:    NETCONF edit-config returns <ok/>, get-config retrieves evpn-ce1 with mac-aging-time=300 and xe-0/0/1, show mac-address-table displays exactly 10 MACs (00:00:00:00:01:01–0A) type=learned, LACP MAC (00:00:00:00:FF:FF) absent, CLI and NETCONF views byte-identical.
Fail-on: NETCONF rejects edit-config or get-config returns wrong/missing evpn-ce1 parameters, fewer than 10 data-plane MACs in table, LACP src-MAC appears in learned entries, CLI 'show evpn' contradicts NETCONF config, mac-aging-time not applied.

205dc9c043ca1393.action_steps:
Setup:  Under configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment, configure a non-default identifier (type 0 or type 1, not the default type 4); commit and save to startup configuration.
Action: Reload the DUT (graceful reboot or power-cycle).
Verify: Post-boot, `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` contains the identifier line unchanged; EVPN operational state displays the ESI with the configured type and value (not all-zeros, not type 4 default).
205dc9c043ca1393.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
205dc9c043ca1393.expectation:
Pass:    Identifier configuration survives reload byte-identical; operational ESI programmed with saved type and value.
Fail-on: Identifier missing post-reload, reverted to default type 4, or operational ESI shows all-zeros / default.

20776e058ee53e55.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP AS 65001, router-id 1.1.1.1; create `interface agg-eth 1` without LACP or `ethernet-segment` sub-command.
Action: Configure `l2-services / evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based`, bind agg-eth 1 as attachment-circuit, commit; observe response and EVPN state.
Verify: Commit status; `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` (no ethernet-segment); `show l2-services evpn evpn-1` (oper state); `show evpn ethernet-segment`; syslog for config errors.
20776e058ee53e55.equipment: DUT only (configuration validation via CLI and show commands)
20776e058ee53e55.expectation:
Pass:    Commit fails with error 'ethernet-segment configuration mandatory for EVPN ES' or EVPN instance enters oper-down with alarm 'incomplete ES config'; no spurious UP state.
Fail-on: EVPN instance shows oper-up without ethernet-segment/LACP on agg-eth 1, or accepts invalid config silently but is non-functional, or system crash.

20af462482cf4207.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `interface agg-eth 0` and enter `ethernet-segment` mode; leave `identifier` unconfigured.
Action: Commit the configuration.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` must show no explicit identifier line; examine ethernet-segment operational state to confirm ESI first octet is 0x04 (type 4, router-ID based per documented default).
20af462482cf4207.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
20af462482cf4207.expectation:
Pass:    ESI defaults to type 4; running-config omits identifier line; explicit type-4 configuration (if syntax allows) is idempotent.
Fail-on: ESI type ≠ 4, or ESI unassigned, or explicit default config alters running-config.

210ecf5725906618.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that port-based EVPN service type configuration is accepted on both LAG and physical Ethernet interfaces as documented in the vendor SFS example.
Method: Configure two port-based EVPN instances (one on LAG agg-lag 1, one on Ethernet x-eth 0/0/1) with auto-discovery and route-targets, verify BGP AD routes exchanged and traffic forwards correctly.
Setup: Two-PE topology over MPLS; iBGP EVPN session established; agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 configured as L2 access interfaces on PE1 with corresponding interfaces on PE2.
Action: On PE1: enter configuration l2-services mode; issue `evpn evi-100 service-type port-based`, bind agg-lag 1 as service interface, configure `auto-discovery` with `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`, commit; issue `evpn evi-101 service-type port-based`, bind x-eth 0/0/1 as service interface, configure `auto-discovery` with `import-rt 65000:101` and `export-rt 65000:101`, commit. Configure matching EVIs on PE2 with same RTs. IXIA sends bidirectional L2 traffic on both access circuits at 100 Mbps per circuit for 60 seconds.
Verify: `show evpn evi` displays both instances with service-type port-based and correct interface bindings (agg-lag 1 for evi-100, x-eth 0/0/1 for evi-101); `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows Type-1 AD routes for both EVIs; IXIA reports bidirectional traffic statistics with 0% loss.
210ecf5725906618.expectation:
Pass: Both EVPN configurations commit successfully; `show evpn evi` lists evi-100 on agg-lag 1 and evi-101 on x-eth 0/0/1, both with service-type port-based; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` displays Type-1 AD routes for both EVIs with correct RTs (65000:100 and 65000:101); IXIA reports 0% packet loss on both circuits.
Fail-on: Configuration rejected for either LAG or Ethernet interface type; commit error stating interface type incompatibility; no Type-1 AD routes advertised for either EVI; IXIA reports packet loss exceeding 0.01% on either circuit.

2161a94922a21719.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP AS 65001 in VRF evpn-test with peer-group EVPN-PEERS and neighbor 192.0.2.2 (neighbor PE AS 65002) under af-l2vpn evpn address-family. Configure EVPN instance evpn-100 service-type vlan-based. Bring neighbor to Established state.
Action: Under `routing bgp vrf evpn-test neighbor 192.0.2.2 af-l2vpn evpn`, apply: `group EVPN-PEERS`, `allow-as-in 2`, `inbound-soft-reconfiguration`, `maximum-prefix 8000 80`, `private-as remove-all`, `route-reflector-client`, `weight 300`. Collect tech-support bundle.
Verify: Parse tech-support output for: `show run routing bgp vrf evpn-test` (neighbor config section), `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor 192.0.2.2` (operational state), `show bgp l2vpn evpn vrf evpn-test summary` (session status), and syslog entries for BGP neighbor state changes.
2161a94922a21719.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains `show run routing bgp vrf evpn-test` with all seven BGP common CLI commands (group EVPN-PEERS, allow-as-in 2, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix 8000 80, private-as remove-all, route-reflector-client, weight 300) under af-l2vpn evpn neighbor context; `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor 192.0.2.2` displays weight=300, RouteReflectorClient flag, MaxPrefix=8000(80%); syslog shows neighbor Established with L2VPN/EVPN capability negotiated.
Fail-on: Tech-support missing `show run routing bgp` neighbor af-l2vpn evpn section, configured BGP common CLI commands absent from running-config, `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor` output missing, operational state contradicts config (wrong weight/max-prefix/RR-client status), or no EVPN capability in BGP logs.

2167f50659189647.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT (`evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`), attach `ethernet-segment` to `agg-eth 0`, configure `import-rt` under a vpls instance. Establish iBGP session with neighbor PE advertising type 4 (ES) routes for a matching ESI.
Action: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment` on DUT.
Verify: Output displays the Ethernet Segments Table with at least one type 4 route entry showing ESI, originating router RID, local-preference, and import status. Cross-check that the displayed ESI matches the neighbor PE's advertised ES route.
2167f50659189647.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (iBGP EVPN session) + IXIA traffic gen
2167f50659189647.expectation:
Pass:    Command returns table containing type 4 route(s) with fields ESI, originating router, local-preference, and next-hop; imported routes from neighbor PE visible.
Fail-on: Command errors, returns empty table when neighbor is advertising type 4 routes, or omits mandatory ES route attributes (ESI, originating router).
2167f50659189647.req_id: CLI:show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment
2167f50659189647.sub_category: show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment

219e4b666cc0cc56.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with three ethernet-segments: (1) ESI Type 0 (operator value 00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:01) on Eth0/1, (2) ESI Type 1 on LAG1 (DUT auto-derives from LACP; IXIA sends System-MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF, Port-Key 0x0123), (3) ESI Type 4 (Router-ID 192.168.1.1, discriminator 1) on Eth0/3. Create EVPN instance `evpn EVPN-TEST service-type vlan-aware-bundle`, enable `auto-discovery`, configure `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`. Configure 3rd-party PE (Cisco IOS-XR) with matching ESI values on corresponding interfaces, EVPN instance, BGP EVPN AFI/SAFI.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN session between DUT and 3rd-party PE via `af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor config. Activate all three ethernet-segments on DUT. Start LACP from IXIA to form Type 1 ESI on LAG1. Capture BGP UPDATE messages with Wireshark at BGP session.
Verify: On DUT: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` for received Ethernet A-D routes from 3rd-party PE with ESI Type 0/1/4. On Cisco PE: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` for received routes from DUT. Decode ESI field in captured BGP NLRI: octet 0 = Type (0x00/0x01/0x04), octets 1-9 = type-specific value per RFC 7432 § 5.
219e4b666cc0cc56.expectation:
Pass:    Both DUT and 3rd-party PE successfully exchange and install Ethernet A-D per-ES routes (EVPN route-type 1) for all three ESI types. ESI encoding validated in Wireshark: Type 0 = 0x00 + 9-octet operator value, Type 1 = 0x01 + 6-octet CE LACP MAC + 2-octet Port-Key + 1-octet reserved, Type 4 = 0x04 + 4-octet Router-ID + 4-octet discriminator + 1-octet reserved. Bidirectional route acceptance confirmed via show commands on both PEs.
Fail-on: Any ESI type rejected by either PE (BGP notification code 3 subcode 10, UPDATE malformed), route not installed in EVPN table, ESI encoding mismatch (wrong Type byte or incorrect value field structure), or missing Ethernet A-D route for any of the three ESI types.

21e4b7d72b4d8e19.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (`evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-based`); configure BGP peer under `af-l2vpn evpn`; attach `x-eth 0/0/1`; configure the non-default state (MAC advertising disabled); learn local MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 via traffic injection.
Action: Restore default configuration (advertising enabled, per requirement) by removing the suppress configuration; commit; observe BGP UPDATE containing MAC/IP Advertisement route sent to peer.
Verify: Peer's `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` output shows MAC/IP route for 00:11:22:33:44:55; route includes correct RD, RT, MAC address, and MPLS label; `show running-config` on DUT shows no explicit advertise-mac suppression (default state).
21e4b7d72b4d8e19.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
21e4b7d72b4d8e19.expectation:
Pass:    Peer receives and installs MAC/IP Advertisement route (type 2) for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55; route format valid (RD, RT, label present); DUT config shows default (no explicit suppress line); BGP session remains stable.
Fail-on: Peer receives no MAC routes after re-enable; MAC route missing required fields (no label, invalid RD/RT, or malformed NLRI); running-config still shows non-default state; or BGP session flaps during config change.

22460bc078c8ffd4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment (ESI type 0, value 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:00) on DUT and neighbor PE agg-eth interfaces using `ethernet-segment` command; enable DF algorithm highest-preference (RFC9785 §4.2); apply consistent local policy override (df-preference 200) on both PEs to override default signaled preference; create EVPN instance `evpn vpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` with `auto-discovery`, matching import-rt/export-rt; verify DUT elected as DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment` and route type 4 advertisement in `show bgp evpn route-type 4`; start IXIA bidirectional traffic 1 Gbps through DUT.
Action: Kill EVPN/BGP control process on DUT (e.g., bgpd or evpnd) via platform debug command `debug process kill <pid>`; observe BGP neighbor down, route type 4 withdrawal, DF failover to neighbor PE during outage.
Verify: Process auto-restarts (verify via `show system processes`); BGP session re-establishes; route type 4 re-advertised with ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:00; verify df-preference 200 still configured on both PEs (check running-config); confirm DF re-election completes (may return to DUT or stay on neighbor PE per algorithm); measure traffic interruption duration via IXIA dropped-frame counters.
22460bc078c8ffd4.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts ≤ 10 s; BGP session re-establishes; route type 4 re-advertised; DF re-election completes with consistent local policy (df-preference 200 on both PEs, no config divergence post-recovery); traffic interruption ≤ 5 s (standard EVPN HA convergence bound).
Fail-on: Process fails to restart; route type 4 not re-advertised after 30 s; df-preference inconsistency detected (DUT ≠ neighbor PE value) post-recovery; DF election stuck; traffic interruption > 5 s or permanent traffic loss.

22652be91e5ee1e4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (advertise-mac enable) and BGP with af-l2vpn evpn enabled toward IXIA neighbor. IXIA BGP peer established, capable of injecting crafted Route Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) NLRI.
Action: IXIA sends UPDATE with MAC/IP Advertisement routes violating RFC7432bis §9.2.1: (a) MAC Address Length = 40 bits (not 48), (b) IP Address Length = 24 bits (not 0/32/128), (c) malformed RD (5-octet instead of 8-octet), (d) ESI length = 8 octets (not 10). Send one variant per UPDATE.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` (peer Established), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route` (zero routes installed from IXIA), `show log | include "EVPN|BGP"` (UPDATE error per variant: "MAC len 40 != 48", "IP len invalid", "RD malformed", "ESI len != 10"), `show system counters bgp` (UPDATE Error counter += 4).
22652be91e5ee1e4.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (IXIA with BGP EVPN NLRI fault-injection: adjustable MAC/IP Address Length fields, RD/ESI octet manipulation)
22652be91e5ee1e4.expectation:
Pass:    All 4 malformed variants rejected per RFC7606 treat-as-withdraw; BGP session stable; error counter increments by 4; syslog contains one entry per variant identifying the specific encoding violation (MAC len, IP len, RD format, ESI len); zero EVPN routes installed from IXIA peer.
Fail-on: Any malformed route appears in `show bgp l2vpn evpn route` or local MAC table; BGP session transitions to Idle/Active (session reset); DUT crash/core dump; silent discard (syslog missing expected error); error propagated to downstream PE via Route Reflector.

226a2c37ce0939f2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES1 (ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:01) multi-homed between PE1 and PE2. Configure DUT as PE3 with BGP EVPN peering to PE1 and PE2 (af-l2vpn evpn enabled per neighbor). PE1 advertises Ethernet A-D per EVI route (type 1) for ES1 with EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community (MTU=1500, P-flag=1).
