Verify: NETCONF <get-config> returns service-type=vlan-based, normalized-vlan=4 for evpn-vlan4 and =6 for evpn-vlan6; CLI 'show l2-services evpn instance evpn-vlan4' matches NETCONF view; 'show evpn mac' or bridge MAC table displays learned MACs with VLAN 4 (from VLAN 100 input) and VLAN 6 (from VLAN 200 input), confirming VLAN-ID replacement.
2d3170339c48fa6f.equipment: DUT + NETCONF client + IXIA traffic gen
2d3170339c48fa6f.expectation:
Pass:    NETCONF <edit-config> accepts vlan-based service-type with normalized-vlan leaf values 4 and 6; <get-config> retrieves them; CLI output matches NETCONF data; MAC learning shows VLAN 100→4 and 200→6 replacement in bridge table.
Fail-on: NETCONF schema lacks vlan-based enum or normalized-vlan leaf; <edit-config> rejected; <get-config> diverges from configured values; CLI/NETCONF views show different VLAN-IDs or service-type; or MAC table shows original VLAN tags 100/200 instead of normalized 4/6 (VLAN-ID not replaced).

2d8f72d76281ce58.action_steps:
Setup:  Enter configuration mode; under `l2-services`, configure `evpn EVPN-INST-1 service-type vlan-based` and `evpn EVPN-INST-2 service-type vlan-aware-bundle`; commit.
Action: Run `show evpn global` and capture full output. Run `show evpn global | include vlan-based`. Run `show evpn global | exclude EVPN-INST-2`. Run `show evpn global | count`.
Verify: Unfiltered output displays both EVPN-INST-1 (service-type vlan-based) and EVPN-INST-2 (service-type vlan-aware-bundle). `| include vlan-based` returns only lines containing that token. `| exclude EVPN-INST-2` omits all lines with EVPN-INST-2. `| count` returns total line count.
2d8f72d76281ce58.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
2d8f72d76281ce58.expectation:
Pass:    `show evpn global` displays both EVPN instances with correct service-types; pipe filters (include, exclude, count) operate per CLI filter semantics.
Fail-on: EVPN instance missing from output, service-type incorrect or not displayed, pipe filter returns wrong subset or rejects command, or command fails.

2dac0d264834e33c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` on x-eth 0/0/1 with VLAN range 10-20; bind to VPLS with auto-discovery/RT. IXIA sends BUM (broadcast) frames tagged with VLANs 5, 10-20, 25, 30 at 1000 pps per VLAN; traffic flows ≥ 1 minute.
Action: While traffic flows, modify EVPN instance: change VLAN range from 10-20 to 15-30 via `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1 / vlan-range 15-30`; commit.
Verify: Capture IXIA Rx stats per VLAN. Run `show evpn evpn1` and `show l2-services vpls` to confirm new VLAN range active. Check DUT interface counters (`show interfaces x-eth 0/0/1 statistics`) for drops.
2dac0d264834e33c.expectation:
Pass:    VLANs 15-20 (overlap) have zero loss during change. VLANs 10-14 are forwarded before change, dropped after. VLANs 21-30 are dropped before change, forwarded after. VLANs 5, 25 remain dropped throughout (not in either bundle). Config visible in `show running-config` within ≤ 1 s.
Fail-on: Packet loss > 0 for VLANs 15-20, or VLANs outside new bundle (5, 10-14, 25) are forwarded post-change, or config change takes > 1 s.

2df9e37788f70ad2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-aware-bundle spanning multiple VLANs (e.g., VLANs 10, 20, 30). Configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0 on both DUT and neighbor PE; configure auto-discovery with import-rt and export-rt. Confirm DF election completes; record which VLANs DUT is DF for (should reflect modulo using numerically lowest VLAN 10 per RFC7432bis ch.8.5). Start continuous BUM traffic from IXIA to multihomed CE.
Action: Power-cycle DUT via PDU harness while DF election and BUM traffic forwarding are active.
Verify: Post-reboot: (1) show running-config confirms evpn service-type vlan-aware-bundle and ethernet-segment configs intact; (2) show evpn commands confirm DF election reconverged with DUT as DF for same VLANs as pre-power-cycle; (3) IXIA verifies BUM traffic forwarding resumes matching pre-power-cycle pattern (no duplicates or blackholes); (4) DF role consistency confirms continued use of numerically lowest VLAN tag in modulo.
2df9e37788f70ad2.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
2df9e37788f70ad2.expectation:
Pass:    DF election reconverges within 60 seconds with consistent DF role per VLAN (same as pre-power-cycle); BUM traffic forwarding resumes correctly (IXIA shows <1% loss, no duplicates); configuration intact; behavior confirms RFC7432bis ch.8.5 compliance (numerically lowest VLAN tag in modulo).
Fail-on: DF election fails to converge, DF role changes for any VLAN (indicating wrong modulo input), BUM traffic loss ≥1% or duplicates, or configuration lost.

2e655adbe7171fee.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure all-active multi-homed Ethernet Segment (same ESI) on DUT and neighbor PE; enable MP2MP MPLS LSPs (mLDP or P2MP) for EVPN instance; configure BGP EVPN peering with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled; set `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in EVPN instance.
Action: Bring up ES and EVPN peering; capture BGP EVPN Ethernet Auto-Discovery (EAD) routes from both PEs; inject BUM traffic from IXIA via DUT; capture packet at egress neighbor PE.
Verify: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type ead` and `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI>` on both PEs; confirm ESI label value is identical on DUT and neighbor PE; confirm label is from DCB allocation range (per local label manager); capture encapsulated BUM packet and verify ESI label matches advertised value; verify neighbor PE identifies source ES correctly in forwarding table.
2e655adbe7171fee.expectation:
Pass:    DUT and neighbor PE both advertise identical ESI label for the shared ES in EAD routes; ESI label is allocated from DCB range; egress PE identifies source ES from ESI label in received BUM packets (confirmed via `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` and packet capture).
Fail-on: DUT and neighbor PE advertise different ESI labels for same ES, or ESI label value is not from DCB range, or egress PE cannot identify source ES from received BUM packet ESI label, or BUM traffic loops/black-holes.

2e661290902ef336.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT: `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor to PE2, `import-rt 65000:100` / `export-rt 65000:100`, local Ethernet segment to IXIA (CE simulator); confirm `advertise-mac enable` (default); clear MAC table.
Action: From IXIA CE-facing port, send: (1) DHCP Discover src-MAC 00:00:00:00:00:01, (2) ARP Request (target-IP=DUT-IP) src-MAC 00:00:00:00:00:02, (3) ARP Request (target-IP=peer-IP) src-MAC 00:00:00:00:00:03, (4) ICMP Echo src-MAC 00:00:00:00:00:04.
Verify: Run `show l2-services evpn mac-table instance <EVI-name>`; confirm all four source MACs present with origin=local, type=dynamic.
2e661290902ef336.expectation:
Pass:    All four MACs (00:00:00:00:00:01–04) in MAC table as local dynamic entries; data-plane learning per IEEE 802.1D active by default (RFC7432bis §9.1 MUST requirement).
Fail-on: Any source MAC missing from table, entries show origin≠local or type≠dynamic, or explicit local-learning enable command required beyond standard EVPN instance configuration.

2f8d5e11e3b7d060.action_steps:
Setup:  Three PEs in EVPN topology: PE1 and PE2 multihome ES1 (ESI type 1, auto-derived ES-Import RT 0x06:02:0011:2233:4455 from high-order 6 octets of ESI); PE3 advertises ES2 (ESI type 0, manual ES-Import RT 0x06:02:AABB:CCDD:EEFF). Enable RT Constraint (RFC4684) on PE1 BGP session via `configuration / routing / bgp / neighbor <PE2-addr> / af-l2vpn evpn` with RT filtering; configure PE1 to constrain ES-Import RT to 0x06:02:0011:2233:4455 only.
Action: PE1 sends RT Constraint message limiting accepted ES-Import RT; PE2 and PE3 advertise Ethernet Segment routes (Type 4) with their respective ES-Import RTs (0x06:02:0011:2233:4455 and 0x06:02:AABB:CCDD:EEFF). Issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` on PE1; capture BGP UPDATE messages via packet trace.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` on PE1; `show bgp l2vpn evpn rt-constraint` or BGP capability negotiation; packet capture confirms RT Constraint advertisement (Type 132) and selective ES route acceptance.
2f8d5e11e3b7d060.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (EVPN-capable) + packet capture tool for BGP UPDATE verification
2f8d5e11e3b7d060.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 BGP table contains Type 4 ES route for ES1 (ES-Import RT 0x06:02:0011:2233:4455, Sub-Type 0x02); no Type 4 route for ES2 (mismatched RT 0x06:02:AABB:CCDD:EEFF); RT Constraint capability negotiated and active per RFC4684 §4; PE1 installs ES1 route and completes DF election for ES1.
Fail-on: PE1 imports ES2 route despite RT Constraint mismatch, RT Constraint not negotiated/advertised (no Type 132 message), or ES1 route incorrectly filtered when ES-Import RT matches constraint.

2fab35a25e84b35d.action_steps:
Setup:  On DUT and neighbor PE, configure EVPN instance with EVI 100 mapped to VLAN 100; verify advertise-mac=enable (default), unknown-mac-flooding=enable (default), control-word=default; establish BGP EVPN peering; attach IXIA port A to DUT VLAN 100, port B to neighbor-PE VLAN 100.
Action: IXIA port A sends frame src-MAC=A1 dst-MAC=B1 (unknown); DUT floods per RFC7432bis ch.13.2; neighbor PE learns B1 locally and advertises RT2; DUT installs B1 in MAC table; IXIA port B sends return traffic src-MAC=B1 dst-MAC=A1 (now known unicast).
Verify: `show bgp evpn routes type 2 evi 100` on DUT shows RT2 for B1 from neighbor-PE; `show l2-services evpn mac-table evi 100` shows B1 learned via BGP; IXIA confirms known-unicast return traffic received at port A with 0 loss (not flooded).
2fab35a25e84b35d.expectation:
Pass:    RT2 routes exchanged; known unicast forwarded directly to destination PE per RT2 next-hop; traffic Rx at correct port with 0% loss; MAC table shows remote MACs learned via BGP.
Fail-on: No RT2 routes advertised/received; known unicast flooded instead of directly forwarded; packet loss > 0.01%; MAC table does not show remote MACs or shows incorrect next-hop; control-word mismatch prevents forwarding.

2fd63c8ef41607e7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` on x-eth 0/0/1 with VLAN-IDs 1-3; enable auto-discovery, set import-rt/export-rt to establish EVPN instance with neighbor PE. IXIA connected to x-eth 0/0/1 sending tagged BUM frames.
Action: IXIA sends (a) broadcast frames (MAC DA FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) tagged VLAN 2 (in-bundle) and VLAN 10 (out-of-bundle); (b) unknown-unicast frames tagged VLAN 3 (in-bundle) and VLAN 20 (out-of-bundle); (c) multicast frames (MAC DA 01:00:5E:xx:xx:xx) tagged VLAN 1 (in-bundle) and VLAN 100 (out-of-bundle). Repeat 100 frames per VLAN-ID.
Verify: `show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics` for VLAN-filtered drop counters; `show evpn evpn1 mac-table` confirms only VLAN 1-3 MACs learned; IXIA capture on neighbor PE link confirms zero frames received with VLAN tags 10, 20, 100.
2fd63c8ef41607e7.expectation:
Pass:    BUM frames with VLAN-IDs 10, 20, 100 are dropped at ingress (300 dropped frames total on x-eth 0/0/1 VLAN-filter counters); VLAN 1-3 BUM frames (300 total) forwarded to EVPN core and visible in neighbor PE MAC table or as flooded traffic.
Fail-on: Any BUM frame with VLAN-ID outside 1-3 appears in `show evpn evpn1 mac-table`, or IXIA captures VLAN 10/20/100 frames egressing toward neighbor PE, or VLAN-filter drop counter remains zero while out-of-bundle frames sent.

2ff01e0ecb734836.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology; configure EVPN instance on each PE with `advertise-mac enable` (default), BGP neighbors with `af-l2vpn evpn`; IXIA scale rig connected to each PE to generate MAC learning and known unicast traffic across 10K unique MAC addresses.
Action: IXIA learns 10K MACs to trigger LT1 label allocation per MACT (RFC7432 ch.7.2); measure LT1 allocation rate (MACs/sec); send bidirectional known unicast traffic between learned MACs at line rate; measure throughput (Gbps, pps), latency (μs), and packet loss for LT1-labeled packets (RFC7432bis ch.8.4).
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac` confirms 10K MACs with LT1 labels allocated; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` shows MAC/IP Advertisement routes with LT1 labels; IXIA reports throughput ≥ line rate, latency, and 0% packet loss; measure BGP UPDATE processing time for all 10K routes.
2ff01e0ecb734836.expectation:
Pass:    LT1 allocation rate ≥ 1000 MACs/sec; throughput ≥ 10 Gbps (line rate) with 0% packet loss; latency ≤ 100 μs; BGP convergence ≤ 60 sec for 10K MAC/IP routes; all 10K LT1 labels visible in MACT and BGP table.
Fail-on: LT1 allocation < 1000 MACs/sec, throughput < 10 Gbps, packet loss > 0%, latency > 100 μs, BGP convergence > 60 sec, or any missing LT1 labels in MACT/BGP routes.

3034e1a623d460e7.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (DUT + neighbor PE) in same EVPN instance multihoming a CE via LACP; configure `ethernet-segment` under `agg-eth 0` on both PEs with identical ESI containing CE's LACP System MAC in high-order 6 octets; enable `auto-discovery` for the EVPN.
Action: Establish LACP between CE and both PEs; bring up BGP EVPN peering; initiate test traffic from IXIA through the multihomed CE.
Verify: `show bgp evpn` to confirm both PEs advertise ESI-encoded routes; decode ESI to verify CE LACP System MAC in high-order 6 octets; verify auto-discovery discovers remote PE; confirm DF election completed (one PE is DF).
3034e1a623d460e7.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
3034e1a623d460e7.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise identical ESI; ESI high-order 6 octets encode CE LACP System MAC per RFC7432bis ch.5; auto-discovery discovers remote PE; DF election designates one PE as DF; traffic forwarded without loss.
Fail-on: ESI mismatch between PEs; CE LACP System MAC absent/misplaced in ESI; auto-discovery fails to discover remote PE; DF election fails; traffic blackholed or looped.

30ee97f2b3678027.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with `advertise-mac enable` (default); establish BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled; learn at least one MAC address in the DUT's MACT (via IXIA traffic on access port); enable packet capture for BGP updates and MPLS-encapsulated data plane.
Action: Verify DUT advertises MAC/IP Advertisement Route (RT-2) to neighbor PE containing MPLS Label1 field (LT1) per RFC7432 ch.7.2; from neighbor PE, send known unicast traffic to the advertised MAC; capture MPLS label stack on ingress packets at DUT.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` confirms RT-2 with MPLS label value; `show evpn mac-table` shows MAC entry with assigned LT1 label; ingress packet capture at DUT shows MPLS label matching advertised LT1; `show interface <evpn-ifl> counters` increments for forwarded unicast.
30ee97f2b3678027.expectation:
Pass:    RT-2 route contains valid MPLS Label1 (LT1) field (20-bit label, non-reserved range); neighbor PE encapsulates packets with advertised LT1 label; DUT performs label lookup per RFC7432bis ch.8.4, maps LT1 to correct MACT, and forwards frame to access port; zero label-mismatch drops.
Fail-on: RT-2 missing MPLS Label1 field; neighbor PE omits LT1 label in MPLS stack; DUT drops packets with "unknown label" error; DUT uses wrong MACT for LT1 lookup; label value in received packet differs from advertised LT1.

30f51debf9729e42.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS instance with auto-discovery enabled under `configuration / l2-services / vpls / auto-discovery`; no export-rt statements configured yet.
Action: Issue `export-rt 100:1` and commit. Issue `export-rt 200:2` and commit to build a list. Issue `show running-config l2-services vpls` to verify both values. Issue `no export-rt 100:1` and commit.
Verify: After adding both values, `show running-config` displays `export-rt 100:1` and `export-rt 200:2` under the auto-discovery stanza. After `no export-rt 100:1`, only `export-rt 200:2` remains.
30f51debf9729e42.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
30f51debf9729e42.expectation:
Pass:    Multiple export-rt route-target values are accepted and stored as a list; each value is individually removable via `no export-rt <route-target>`.
Fail-on: Command rejects valid route-target syntax (e.g., ASN:value format), values not persisted to running-config, `no` form fails to remove the specified entry, or duplicate values cause config errors.

3108dad4f63005ae.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES on DUT and neighbor PE with identifier type 0 (manual ESI 01.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.11), load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving highest-random-weight. Create EVPN instance with VLAN-aware bundle (VLANs 100-110, 11 VLANs for HRW service carving). IXIA generates continuous bidirectional traffic (100 Mbps per VLAN) to multi-homed MAC addresses.
Action: Run `show evpn ethernet-segment service-carving` to identify current DF assignments. Kill the EVPN/BGP control-plane process on DUT using platform debug command while traffic is active. Monitor traffic loss and DF re-election timing.
Verify: Process auto-restarts; run `show evpn ethernet-segment service-carving` to confirm DF re-assignments per HRW algorithm. Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` to verify ES Route includes DF Election Extended Community with DF Alg = 0x02 (HRW per RFC8584 ch.3). Measure traffic interruption duration per VLAN via IXIA.
3108dad4f63005ae.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen (process-kill harness)
3108dad4f63005ae.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN process restarts automatically within 10s; DF re-election completes using HRW algorithm (service-carving output shows per-VLAN DF assignments matching RFC8584 ch.3 HRW hash); ES Route (type 4) advertises DF Election Extended Community with DF Alg = 0x02; traffic loss per VLAN ≤ 5s (es-waiting-time 3s default + 2s convergence margin).
Fail-on: Process crash with no auto-restart; DF election stuck or incorrectly falls back to default algorithm (RFC7432bis ch.8.5); traffic loss > 5s on any VLAN; missing or incorrect DF Election Extended Community in ES Route (type 4); DF Alg ≠ 0x02 in BGP advertisement.

3135f26493e94a8b.action_steps:
Setup:  Best Path Selection already exercised via prerequisite rows (RT-2 MAC-IP routes for same MAC advertised from 2+ PEs with different BGP path attributes: local-pref, AS_PATH, or MED); advertise-mac enabled, control-word default, unknown-mac-flooding enabled in EVI running-config.
Action: Execute `tech-support evpn <evi-id>` or platform-specific equivalent; bundle must capture: `show evpn routes <evi-id> route-type 2 detail`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`, `show evpn mac-table <evi-id>`, `show running-config l2-services evpn`, `show log | match evpn`, `show fib evpn <evi-id>`.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: (1) show evpn routes RT-2 detail listing ≥2 paths for same MAC with path attributes (local-pref/AS_PATH/MED) and one best-path indicator (e.g., '>' or 'best' flag) per RFC7432bis ch.7.13; (2) MAC table next-hop matching best-path PE; (3) FIB entry consistent with selected next-hop; (4) syslog route-update entries; (5) running-config EVPN section showing advertise-mac/control-word/unknown-mac-flooding.
3135f26493e94a8b.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains all 6 listed show commands; RT-2 detail displays ≥2 candidate paths with path attributes (local-pref/AS_PATH/MED) and best-path indicator ('>' or 'best'); MAC table and FIB next-hop match best-path PE; running-config EVPN section present.
Fail-on: Any required show command missing, <2 RT-2 candidates visible, no best-path indicator, MAC table next-hop ≠ best-path PE, FIB entry inconsistent with selected path, or running-config EVPN section absent.

31a4fa267e652d09.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN multihomed ES: configure agg-eth interface, assign non-zero ESI, enter mode `configuration / interface / agg-eth <id> / ethernet-segment`; attach EVPN instance with ≥3 VLANs to test service distribution via DF election.
Action: Configure service-carving DF election: (a) `service-carving 200` (explicit preference); (b) verify read-back; (c) `no service-carving` (restore default); (d) test boundaries 0, 65535; (e) reject 65536 (out-of-range), -1 (negative), xyz (non-integer).
Verify: Valid values commit and appear in `show running-config interface agg-eth <id>` as `service-carving <N>`; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` confirms DF election method is service-carving with configured preference; `no` form removes explicit line; each invalid input rejected with parse error before commit.
31a4fa267e652d09.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
31a4fa267e652d09.expectation:
Pass:    Preference values 0..65535 accepted and persisted; ES DF election uses service-carving method with correct weight; `no service-carving` restores default (no explicit config line); all out-of-range/malformed inputs rejected at CLI parse
Fail-on: Invalid preference accepted into running-config, DF election method not service-carving after explicit config, valid value rejected, `no` form leaves stale entry, or CLI crash/hang

31cb6e68043f5c16.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with P2MP MPLS LSP as PMSI tunnel; PE1 advertises IMET route with PMSI Tunnel attribute pointing to P2MP LSP root at PE1, leaves at PE2 (DUT) and PE3; verify configuration l2-services evpn unknown-mac-flooding enable on PE2; attach IXIA to PE2 access port.
Action: From PE1, inject three frames onto the P2MP LSP: (1) dst MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF (broadcast), (2) dst MAC 01:00:5E:01:02:03 (multicast), (3) dst MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE (unicast, unknown in PE2 MAC table); capture on IXIA at PE2 access.
Verify: show l2-services evpn statistics; show l2-services evpn mac-table; confirm PE2 floods all three frames to IXIA: (1) as broadcast, (2) as multicast, (3) as unknown unicast (MAC lookup bypassed for P2MP LSP packets, not forwarded as known unicast).
31cb6e68043f5c16.expectation:
Pass:    All frames received via P2MP LSP treated as BUM: broadcast flooded to all access ports, multicast flooded to group members, unicast MAC flooded as unknown unicast (no MAC lookup).
Fail-on: PE2 performs MAC table lookup for unicast dst MAC on P2MP LSP (treats as known unicast), drops P2MP LSP packets, or fails to classify/flood as BUM.

31d1a85795644344.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with `agg-eth 0` under the same ESI using `identifier 0 <type0-value>` and `load-balancing-mode all-active` to enable aliasing. Attach multi-homed CE to both PEs. Configure EVPN instance and BGP peering to remote PE3.
Action: From PE3, initiate known-unicast traffic to a MAC behind the multi-homed CE. On PE3, verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn` displays multiple paths (from PE1 and PE2) with identical ESI. Administratively disable PE1's `agg-eth 0` interface. Observe traffic continuity and convergence time.
Verify: Capture `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on PE3 before and after PE1 failure. Confirm PE3 installs aliased routes (same ESI, different next-hops) pre-failure and switches to PE2-only path post-failure. Measure packet loss duration with IXIA traffic counters.
31d1a85795644344.expectation:
Pass:    PE3 learns and installs aliased paths (RFC7432bis ch.8.4 aliasing) from both PEs pre-failure; traffic fails over to PE2 within ≤ 3 seconds (default `es-waiting-time`); zero duplicate frames observed on CE during switchover.
Fail-on: PE3 does not install backup path from PE2, or black-hole duration exceeds 3 seconds, or duplicate frames delivered to CE during convergence window.

31f77fdac6a499c7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS instance vpls-100 with interface x-eth 0/0/1; add static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 to interface x-eth 0/0/1 via `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1` under l2-services / vpls / vpls-100; commit.
Action: Navigate to configuration / l2-services / vpls / vpls-100; execute `no mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-100` no longer contains `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55`; `show mac-address-table vpls vpls-100` does not list 00:11:22:33:44:55.
31f77fdac6a499c7.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
31f77fdac6a499c7.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 removed from running-config and MAC address table for VPLS vpls-100.
Fail-on: Static MAC entry persists in running-config or MAC address table after `no` command and commit.

320095c8f36f598e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP neighbor under routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip>; enter af-l2vpn evpn; configure capability negotiation for the neighbor.
Action: Commit; establish BGP session with the neighbor PE that supports ORF Prefix-List and Route Refresh capabilities.
Verify: `show bgp neighbor <neighbor-ip>` displays Established state and confirms ORF Prefix-List and Route Refresh are negotiated for the L2VPN EVPN AF.
320095c8f36f598e.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session Established with ORF Prefix-List (RFC 5291) and Route Refresh (RFC 2918) capabilities active for the L2VPN EVPN address-family
Fail-on: Session fails, capabilities not negotiated, or capability list missing ORF/Route-Refresh flags for the AF

320e397086d44ef8.action_steps:
Problem: Validate the mandatory fallback to Default Algorithm when PEs in an all-active multi-homed ES advertise incompatible DF election algorithms via Type 4 routes.
Method: Configure two PEs with shared LACP-based ESI but mismatched service-carving algorithms (PE1 using highest-preference, PE2 using default), capture BGP Type 1 and Type 4 route exchanges, and verify both PEs fall back to RFC7432bis-§8.5 modulo-based service carving.
Setup: Two PEs (PE1, PE2) connected to same CE via LACP LAG; shared ESI type 1 configured on both; BGP EVPN peering established between PEs; EVI provisioned on both.
Action: PE1: configure `interface agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment / identifier 1 / load-balancing-mode all-active / service-carving highest-preference preference 100`. PE2: configure `interface agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment / identifier 1 / load-balancing-mode all-active / service-carving default`. Bring up both ESs. Capture BGP updates showing Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES, EVPNS-REQ#260) and Type 4 (Ethernet Segment Route, EVPNS-REQ#300) advertisements with DF Election Extended Community. Drive EVPN L2 traffic from IXIA through CE to both PEs.
Verify: BGP capture shows PE1 Type 4 with DF Election EC (DF Alg = highest-preference), PE2 Type 4 with DF Alg = default. `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs confirms both have fallen back to Default Algorithm (RFC7432bis-§8.5 modulo function). ES-Import Route Target correctly generated per RFC7432bis-§7.6 (EVPNS-REQ#250). Traffic distribution follows Default Algorithm service carving, not highest-preference.
320e397086d44ef8.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
320e397086d44ef8.expectation:
Pass: Both PEs detect algorithm mismatch in Type 4 DF Election EC and fall back to Default Algorithm per RFC7432bis-§8.5; traffic forwarding matches modulo-based service carving; Type 1 and Type 4 routes correctly advertised; ES-Import RT generated per RFC7432bis-§7.6.
Fail-on: PE1 continues using highest-preference algorithm; traffic distribution follows highest-preference instead of Default Algorithm; Type 1 or Type 4 routes missing or malformed; ES-Import RT incorrect or missing.

327e7fbefcf76f76.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 multi-homed to CE with `load-balancing-mode all-active` and identical `identifier 0 <type0-value>` on their `agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment`. Configure P2MP MPLS LSPs for BUM forwarding. Verify Ethernet A-D per ES routes with upstream-assigned ESI labels are advertised.
Action: IXIA (simulating remote PE3) sends MPLS BUM packet with PE1's ESI label as outer label, replicated via P2MP to PE1 and PE2.
Verify: `tcpdump` on `agg-eth 0` at both PEs shows no egress to the shared ES. `show mpls forwarding-table` confirms ESI label programmed in context of PE1 with split-horizon forwarding behavior.
327e7fbefcf76f76.expectation:
Pass:    ESI label installed in PE1 and PE2 MPLS tables; BUM with that label NOT forwarded onto the Ethernet segment.
Fail-on: BUM packet with ESI label egresses on the shared ES interface, or ESI label missing from co-located PE forwarding tables.

32c48d47a056e185.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed ES on DUT agg-eth 1 (`ethernet-segment; identifier 0 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:00; service-carving highest-random-weight; load-balancing-mode all-active`) and on neighbor PE with identical ESI/algorithm; attach to EVPN instance VLAN 100; verify `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows DUT as DF; IXIA sending 1000 pps bidirectional via ES.
Action: Reset EVPN control-plane process on DUT (kill process or vendor restart command); capture BGP peering session for ES route type 4 withdrawal/re-advertisement; monitor DF Election Extended Community (DF Alg field = HRW, per RFC8584 §3).
Verify: Process auto-restarts; ES route type 4 re-advertised within `es-waiting-time` (3 s default) with DF Election Extended Community; `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows stable single-DF state; IXIA traffic outage ≤ 3 s; no duplicate frames.
32c48d47a056e185.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (same vendor) + IXIA traffic gen
32c48d47a056e185.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN process auto-recovers; ES route type 4 with DF Election Extended Community (DF Alg = HRW) re-advertised ≤ 3 s; DF election reconverges to single DF; traffic outage ≤ 3 s; no duplicate forwarding.
Fail-on: Process fails to auto-restart; ES route type 4 missing DF Election Extended Community after recovery; DF state inconsistent (both/neither DF) after 10 s; traffic outage > 5 s; persistent duplication or black-holing.

33c7403cbac7dfff.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure loopback 0 with IPv4 10.1.1.1. Create EVPN instance "evpn-test" (vlan-aware-bundle) under configuration/l2-services; configure auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt.
Action: Verify EVPN instance is operational, then collect tech-support bundle including: show evpn summary, show bgp l2vpn evpn, show running-config l2-services, show logging evpn.
Verify: Parse tech-support archive for: (a) RD in show bgp output matches Type 1 format <loopback0-IP>:<instance-number> (10.1.1.1:X), (b) running-config captures evpn-test stanza, (c) syslog shows EVPN instance creation, (d) no RD manually configured (auto-generated only).
33c7403cbac7dfff.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains show bgp l2vpn evpn output displaying RD = 10.1.1.1:<auto-assigned-number> (Type 1 per RFC7432bis 7.9), running-config with evpn-test definition, and EVPN instance-up syslog entry.
Fail-on: RD missing from BGP table, RD Type ≠ 1, Administrator field ≠ loopback 0 IPv4, or incomplete archive (missing show/config/log sections).

33d9b4db199b1ce4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance with `host mac-address-duplication-detection` enabled. Attach two interfaces (e.g., x-eth 0/0/1 and x-eth 0/0/2) to the same EVPN bridge domain.
Action: Configure IXIA to continuously send frames with source MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 from port-A to DUT x-eth 0/0/1 at 100 fps. After 10 seconds, start sending frames with the same source MAC from port-B to DUT x-eth 0/0/2 at 100 fps (both streams running simultaneously).
Verify: Monitor syslog and check MAC learning tables; the DUT should detect the same host MAC appearing from multiple interfaces concurrently and trigger duplicate detection.
33d9b4db199b1ce4.expectation:
Pass:    Duplicate MAC detection alert appears in syslog within 10 seconds of both streams active; MAC may be blocked, withdrawn from EVPN advertisements, or flagged as duplicate.
Fail-on: No duplicate detection triggered; MAC remains active from both interfaces without any system response or alert.

34063915db330c62.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance and ethernet-segment (non-reserved ESI type 0, e.g., 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09); configure neighbor PE (or IXIA emulating PE) to advertise MAC/IP Advertisement routes with ESI=0 and with the same non-reserved ESI; set es-waiting-time to 5 seconds to widen the crash window.
Action: Neighbor PE advertises MAC/IP route (route type 2) with non-reserved ESI but delays the Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) by 3 seconds; during the es-waiting-time window (before ES route arrives), kill the DUT's BGP or EVPN control-plane process (e.g., `debug process-crash evpn-mgr`); let process restart and neighbor complete route advertisement (both type 1 and type 2).
Verify: Process restarts within documented bound; inspect `show bgp l2vpn evpn route` for both route types; inspect `show evpn mac` and forwarding table (`show l2-services forwarding-table`) to confirm ESI=0 MAC installed immediately (solo), non-reserved ESI MAC installed only after both type 1 + type 2 received post-recovery; measure traffic loss ≤ es-waiting-time + process-restart bound.
34063915db330c62.equipment: DUT + IXIA (emulating neighbor PE with BGP EVPN route injection and timing control for type 1/type 2 route sequencing) + process-kill harness
34063915db330c62.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts ≤ 10 seconds; ESI=0 MAC/IP routes install forwarding state without ES route dependency; non-reserved ESI MAC/IP routes install forwarding state only after both MAC/IP Advertisement route AND Ethernet A-D per ES route received post-recovery; traffic loss ≤ (es-waiting-time + restart bound).
Fail-on: Process does not restart; non-reserved ESI MAC/IP route installs forwarding before ES route arrives (MUST violation); ESI=0 MAC/IP route blocked or waits for ES route (MUST violation); forwarding table corruption or missing entries post-recovery; traffic loss > (es-waiting-time + 10 sec).

344478ec34c1acb8.action_steps:
Problem: BGP parameters (route-reflector-client, maximum-prefix) configured under af-l2vpn evpn may not apply to EVPN NLRI exchange or may break MAC learning.
Method: Build two-PE MPLS topology, configure route-reflector-client under af-l2vpn evpn, establish vlan-based EVPN service, attach CEs, drive traffic, and verify EVPN route reflection and MAC learning.
Setup:  PE1 and PE2 interconnected via MPLS core; iBGP session active between them. CE1 single-homed to PE1 via an access interface; CE2 single-homed to PE2. Both CEs configured for VLAN 100.
Action: On PE1: under BGP neighbor configuration for PE2, enter af-l2vpn evpn address family and enable route-reflector-client. Under l2-services, configure `evpn <name> service-type vlan-based`, set RD, enable `auto-discovery`, configure `import-rt 65000:1` and `export-rt 65000:1`, and bind the access interface to the EVPN instance. On PE2: apply similar EVPN configuration without the RR client attribute. Commit configurations on both PEs. From CE1, send 100 Mbps unicast traffic toward CE2; from CE2, send return traffic to CE1.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on both PEs displays EVPN type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) and type 3 (Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag) routes exchanged, with route-reflector-client attribute honored for PE1's neighbor. `show evpn evi` confirms the EVPN instance is operationally up with service-type vlan-based and RD active. `show evpn mac` shows CE1's MAC address learned locally on PE1 and learned remotely (from BGP) on PE2; CE2's MAC learned locally on PE2 and remotely on PE1. Interface counters on both PEs indicate bidirectional traffic forwarding with zero drops.
344478ec34c1acb8.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN routes (type 2 and type 3) exchanged successfully with BGP route-reflector client configuration honored; RD and route targets operational; local MAC addresses learned from directly attached CEs on each PE; remote MAC addresses learned via BGP EVPN NLRI; bidirectional L2 traffic forwarding between CEs with no packet loss.
Fail-on: EVPN routes not advertised or received; route-reflector-client configuration not applied or causing BGP session failure; RD or route target mismatch preventing route import/export; local MAC learning failure on either PE; remote MAC learning failure on either PE; traffic loss, drops, or black-holing in either direction.

347993836d45b4cb.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on six agg-eth interfaces (agg-eth 0 through agg-eth 5), each with a distinct ESI type (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) covering RFC7432bis §7.1 valid range; enable auto-discovery under EVPN/VPLS instance; establish iBGP EVPN session to neighbor PE; IXIA generating continuous bidirectional L2 traffic (1000 flows, 100 Mbps aggregate) distributed across all six segments.
Action: Sustain steady-state operation for ≥ 24 consecutive hours with continuous traffic injection.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` for six Ethernet A-D routes (one per ESI type); `show running-config interface agg-eth` for configuration stability; `show platform process memory` sampled hourly; `show interface agg-eth <0..5>` for Tx/Rx counters at T=0h, T=12h, T=24h.
347993836d45b4cb.equipment: DUT + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h) + neighbor PE
347993836d45b4cb.expectation:
Pass:    Six Ethernet A-D routes (ESI types 0-5) continuously present over 24 h with zero BGP withdraw/update events; memory usage variance ≤ 5% across hourly samples; Tx/Rx counters increase monotonically by expected traffic volume (within 2%); BGP session uptime = test duration.
Fail-on: Missing/withdrawn A-D route at any checkpoint; BGP route update events for A-D routes; memory growth >10% from baseline; counter anomaly (freeze, decrement, >5% deviation); BGP session down/reset; ESI MSB outside 0x00-0x05 range in advertised routes.

347a2db1e4805d0e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0 with ESI Type 1 (most significant byte = 0x01, valid per §7.1 range 0-5). Establish EVPN BGP peering with neighbor PE; verify Ethernet A-D routes advertised. Start continuous traffic over agg-eth 0.
Action: Flap agg-eth 0 interface via `shutdown` then `no shutdown`. Monitor BGP UPDATE messages during route withdrawal and re-advertisement.
Verify: Capture BGP packets (`tcpdump -i <mgmt-if> port 179 -w /tmp/bgp.pcap`) during flap. Decode Ethernet A-D route NLRI; verify ESI field most significant byte remains 0x01 (within mandatory 0-5 range per §7.1) in re-advertised routes. Confirm traffic recovers.
347a2db1e4805d0e.expectation:
Pass:    After interface flap, re-advertised Ethernet A-D routes contain ESI with most significant byte in valid range (0-5); traffic converges.
Fail-on: Ethernet A-D route ESI field most significant byte > 0x05, or routes not properly withdrawn/re-advertised, or traffic does not recover.

349475859f2fdf81.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in `configuration/l2-services/evpn` mode (default); DUT advertises IMET (type 3) route with P-tunnel downstream MPLS label L; verify label L via `show bgp evpn route type 3 | include P-tunnel`; IXIA connected via neighbor PE.
Action: IXIA generates 5 Mpps aggregate (10,000 unknown unicast MAC DAs + multicast + broadcast) for 300 sec; neighbor PE encapsulates with MPLS label L and forwards to DUT; DUT must treat all label-L packets as BUM per RFC7432bis §12.1.
Verify: `show mpls forwarding-table label L` displays increasing hit count; `show l2-services evpn statistics` BUM counters increment; IXIA at DUT local CEs captures all flooded frames; record throughput, p99 latency, loss rate.
349475859f2fdf81.expectation:
Pass:    DUT sustains ≥ 5 Mpps with 0% packet loss; p99 latency ≤ 10 ms; all MPLS label-L packets treated as BUM; unicast MACs treated as unknown unicast (flooded per RFC7432bis §12.1 MUST); counters accurate.
Fail-on: Loss > 0%; latency > 10 ms; throughput < 5 Mpps; label-L packet not treated as BUM; unicast MAC not flooded; counter mismatch > 0.01%.

3498b38930a514ac.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 as sticky (static flag) on DUT EVI 100 ES1; advertise via BGP with MAC Mobility ext-community (static=1, seq=0). Configure second learning path via ES2 on same PE.
Action: Inject same MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 from ES2 to trigger RFC7432bis §15.2 MUST alert (sticky MAC conflict); verify operator alert in syslog; collect tech-support bundle (show tech-support evpn or equivalent).
Verify: Examine tech-support archive for: show evpn mac (sticky entry with static flag), show evpn routes type 2 (RT2 with MAC Mobility ext-community static=1 seq=0), syslog entries containing sticky MAC violation alert for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01, running-config showing sticky MAC configuration, show evpn instance 100 (kernel/FIB state).
3498b38930a514ac.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (or IXIA simulating second PE for ES2) + syslog collector
3498b38930a514ac.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains sticky MAC config for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01, RT2 route showing MAC Mobility ext-community with static flag=1 and seq=0, operator alert in syslog stating "sticky MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 learned from conflicting source" (or vendor-equivalent wording per RFC §15.2 MUST alert), and FIB entries for EVI 100.
Fail-on: Missing sticky MAC operator alert in syslog, missing MAC Mobility ext-community static flag in show evpn routes, or tech-support lacks show evpn mac / show evpn instance output.

34a4b4002a0649f2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE multi-homed to CE via `ethernet-segment` on agg-eth interface; create `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` with VLANs 10, 20, 30 (to test numerically-lowest requirement). Verify DUT is elected DF using `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` (DF election uses VLAN 10, the numerically lowest tag, in modulo function). IXIA sends BUM traffic on VLAN 10.
Action: Kill EVPN control-plane process on DF (DUT) using `kill -9 $(pidof evpn-mgr)` or platform equivalent; neighbor PE becomes DF during outage.
Verify: `show system processes | grep evpn` confirms process restart within 3 sec; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows DF re-election with VLAN 10 still used in modulo; `show evpn mac-table` repopulates; IXIA packet counters show BUM traffic loss ≤ 3 sec.
34a4b4002a0649f2.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen (multi-homed CE topology; process-kill harness)
34a4b4002a0649f2.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN process auto-restarts; DF re-election completes using numerically lowest Ethernet tag (VLAN 10) per RFC7432bis §8.5; BUM traffic interruption ≤ 3 sec; zero packet loss post-recovery
Fail-on: Process does not restart; DF election uses VLAN 20 or 30 (violates MUST); BUM traffic interruption > 3 sec; MAC table does not reconverge; persistent traffic black-holing

34b3891b9896ad04.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed ethernet segment with ESI Type 1 or 2 on DUT and neighbor PE; enable `auto-discovery` under an EVPN instance; establish iBGP EVPN peering (`af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor).
Action: Verify DUT advertises BGP Type 4 (Ethernet Segment) route with ES-Import RT extended community; if RT Constraint (RFC4684) is implemented, enable it on the BGP session; collect tech-support bundle (e.g., `show tech-support evpn` or equivalent).
Verify: Extract from tech-support archive: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type ethernet-segment` (Type 4 routes with ES-Import RT), `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` (ESI config and ES-Import RT derivation), `show bgp l2vpn evpn rt-constraint` (RT Constraint state if applicable), `show evpn evi` (auto-discovery config), running-config for ESI and `import-rt`/`export-rt` under `auto-discovery`, syslog entries for multi-homing DF election and ES route import events.
34b3891b9896ad04.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: (1) Type 4 route showing ES-Import RT extended community (Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x02) with 6-octet value derived from high-order portion of the ESI; (2) ESI Type (1/2/3) and automatic derivation proof; (3) RT import filter showing ES-Import RT imported by all PEs on the same ES; (4) if RT Constraint enabled per RFC4684, evidence that RT Constraint procedures apply to ES-Import RT (e.g., RT-Constrain NLRI for ES-Import RT, route filtering based on RT Constraint).
Fail-on: Type 4 routes missing ES-Import RT extended community, ES-Import RT not derived from ESI high-order 6 octets (for Types 1/2/3), or RT Constraint implemented but NOT applied to ES-Import RT (violates RFC4684 MUST).

34b5a4b733aa7435.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT1, DUT2) in BGP EVPN topology; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbors; configure 100 EVPN instances per PE with unique RD (per RFC7432bis §7) and VNI; set DUT1 originating router IP to IPv4 loopback, DUT2 to IPv6 loopback; enable `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` per EVI; IXIA connected to both PEs generates 10 Gbps BUM traffic (64-byte frames) across 50 VNIs.
Action: Measure IMET (Type-3) route advertisement convergence from EVI activation to remote PE installation (capture BGP UPDATE timestamps); sustain BUM traffic load for 5 minutes; capture all BGP EVPN routes with `show bgp l2vpn evpn route type imet detail` to extract RD format and originating router IP fields; measure BUM forwarding throughput/latency via IXIA counters.
Verify: Parse BGP output to confirm all 200 IMET routes (100 per PE) have RD matching §7 format, originating router IP equals configured loopback and remains identical across all routes from each PE; verify IPv4/IPv6 address-family independence; confirm IXIA reports ≥9.9 Gbps average throughput with <0.5 ms mean latency, zero frame loss.
34b5a4b733aa7435.expectation:
Pass:    IMET route convergence ≤300 ms (EVI config commit to BGP ESTABLISHED on remote PE); originating router IP field is 100% consistent per PE across all 100 routes and matches loopback address; BUM forwarding ≥9.9 Gbps sustained (99% line rate for 10G link) with mean latency <0.5 ms and zero packet loss over 5-minute window.
Fail-on: Convergence >300 ms; any IMET route with originating router IP ≠ PE loopback or inconsistent across routes from same PE; BUM throughput <9.8 Gbps or any packet loss >0%; RD format violation (not per §7); originating router IP tied to BGP next-hop address-family (violates RFC7432bis §11.1 independence requirement).

35493c0953d9a38b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure vlan-based EVPN instance on DUT under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`. Establish BGP peering with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled to neighbor PE. Generate local MAC addresses via IXIA-connected host traffic.
Action: With default configuration (no explicit `advertise-mac` command), verify DUT advertises MACs via BGP Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes. Configure `no advertise-mac` to disable advertisement, observe BGP Withdraw messages. Re-enable with `advertise-mac`, observe BGP Update messages with MAC routes.
Verify: On neighbor PE, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` lists DUT's MAC routes when enabled/default, shows empty or routes withdrawn when disabled. Capture BGP Update/Withdraw packets. On DUT, `show running-config` confirms presence/absence of `no advertise-mac`.
35493c0953d9a38b.expectation:
Pass:    Default behavior (documented as 'enable') advertises local MACs via BGP Type 2 routes; `no advertise-mac` stops advertisement and sends Withdraw for existing MAC routes; re-enabling sends Update with MAC routes.
Fail-on: MACs advertised when `no advertise-mac` configured, or MACs not advertised at default, or BGP Withdraw/Update not sent on configuration change, or `show running-config` does not reflect config state.

35818dbc7137b8c0.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT in clean config, create `agg-eth 1` with at least one member port under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1`, commit; verify no `lacp-key` line in `show running-config interface agg-eth 1`.
Action: Configure `lacp-key 500`, commit; verify line appears in running-config and `show lacp interface agg-eth 1 detail` shows Administrative Key = 500; issue `no lacp-key`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` omits `lacp-key`; `show lacp interface agg-eth 1 detail` shows Key = 1 (interface number); configure `lacp-key 1` explicitly and verify it does NOT appear in running-config (idempotent check).
35818dbc7137b8c0.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
35818dbc7137b8c0.expectation:
Pass:    Default Administrative Key equals agg-eth interface number (agg-eth 1 → Key 1); `no lacp-key` restores this default; explicit config of default value (`lacp-key 1`) is idempotent (omitted from running-config).
Fail-on: Default Key ≠ interface number, `no lacp-key` fails to restore default, or `lacp-key 1` appears in running-config when explicitly set (non-idempotent).

362a24160a0dfbb2.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that when a host MAC conflicts with an IRB default-gateway MAC in the same subnet, the PE advertising the host MAC withdraws its route and raises an alarm.
Method: Configure DUT with a host using MAC M, neighbor PE with IRB using the same MAC M (with Default Gateway extended community), and verify DUT withdraws its route and raises an alarm when it receives the neighbor's default-gateway advertisement.
Setup:  Configure two PEs in the same EVPN instance/subnet. Neighbor PE has IRB interface with MAC M (advertises with Default Gateway extended community). DUT is configured with `advertise-mac enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn` and `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled on the BGP neighbor.
Action: Attach IXIA-simulated host with MAC M to DUT. DUT advertises this MAC route without Default Gateway extended community. Observe DUT's response when it receives neighbor PE's route for MAC M with Default Gateway extended community.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn mac` confirms DUT has withdrawn its MAC route for M. `show logging` contains alarm about MAC conflict between host and default gateway. Only neighbor PE's route for M (with Default Gateway extended community) remains in DUT's BGP table. No MAC Mobility extended community is present on routes carrying Default Gateway extended community.
362a24160a0dfbb2.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
362a24160a0dfbb2.expectation:
Pass:    DUT withdraws its host-MAC route for M upon receiving neighbor PE's route with Default Gateway extended community, raises alarm about the MAC conflict, and only neighbor PE's route remains in BGP table; no MAC Mobility extended community appears on routes with Default Gateway extended community.
Fail-on: DUT does not withdraw its route, no alarm is raised, both routes coexist, or MAC Mobility extended community is present on a route carrying Default Gateway extended community.
362a24160a0dfbb2.sub_category: RFC7432bis §10.1.1 — Best Path Selection for Default Gateway

363fce9566997e45.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with iBGP AS <asn> in VRF <vrf>; iBGP neighbors PE1 and PE2 established; `af-l2vpn evpn` activated on both; PE1 advertising EVPN type-2 (MAC/IP) routes.
Action: Navigate to `configuration / routing / bgp <asn> / vrf <vrf> / neighbor <pe2-ip> / af-l2vpn evpn`, issue `route-reflector-client`, then `commit`.
Verify: `show running-config routing bgp` confirms `route-reflector-client` under vrf <vrf> / neighbor <pe2-ip> / af-l2vpn evpn; on PE2, `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` for routes originated by PE1 reveals ORIGINATOR_ID (PE1's router-id) and CLUSTER_LIST (DUT's cluster-id).
363fce9566997e45.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs (PE1 originating EVPN routes, PE2 configured as RR client)
363fce9566997e45.expectation:
Pass:    `route-reflector-client` accepted and persists in running-config; EVPN routes reflected to PE2 carry ORIGINATOR_ID and CLUSTER_LIST per RFC 4456
Fail-on: Commit rejected, or reflected routes on PE2 missing ORIGINATOR_ID or CLUSTER_LIST attributes, or PE2 receives no reflected routes

36a826a24fe4cbc1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance (EVI 100, non-zero ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, import-rt 65000:100). Configure two neighbor PEs advertising Ethernet A-D per-EVI routes (BGP EVPN type-1) for the same ESI: PE1 includes EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community (P=1, B=0); PE2 omits it.
Action: Establish `af-l2vpn evpn` BGP sessions with both PEs. Both PEs advertise type-1 routes with identical standard BGP attributes (same local-pref, AS-path length). Trigger best-path selection by receiving both routes.
Verify: Run `show bgp evpn route-type 1 esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11` and `show evpn ethernet-segment` to inspect A-D per-EVI routes and selected paths. Confirm PE1's route is chosen as best-path. Send bidirectional traffic via IXIA; verify DUT forwards to PE1 (aliasing) and uses P/B flags per RFC8214 §8 for backup.
36a826a24fe4cbc1.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects PE1's route (with EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community) as best-path per RFC7432bis §7.13.2 ordered criteria, even though PE2's route has equivalent standard BGP metrics. P flag (P=1) and B flag (B=0) are honored for aliasing and backup path per RFC8214. Traffic follows selected path.
Fail-on: DUT selects PE2's route (without Layer 2 Attributes extended community), applies RFC7432bis §7.13.2 criteria out of order, ignores P/B flags in multi-homing forwarding decision, or best-path selection is inconsistent across route refreshes.

36b758225ab14b45.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT booted; under `configuration / l2-services`, configure two EVPN instances: `evpn EVP1 service-type vlan-based` and `evpn EVP2 service-type vlan-aware-bundle`; commit configuration.
Action: Execute `show evpn summary`.
Verify: Output contains both instance names (EVP1, EVP2) with their respective service types; verify output is structured (table or list with clear field labels).
36b758225ab14b45.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
36b758225ab14b45.expectation:
Pass:    Command executes successfully; output displays both EVPN instances with their configured service types in a structured, readable format.
Fail-on: Command fails with error, output is empty when instances exist, or either instance name/service-type is absent from the summary.

36ea3aa9ef03b550.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 2 PEs (PE1=DUT, PE2) multi-homed to same CE using identical ESI under `interface agg-eth` → `ethernet-segment`. Enable Highest-Preference Algorithm for DF election. Configure identical local policy override on both PEs to force PE2 as DF (e.g., static DF preference=200 on PE2, preference=100 on PE1). Verify PE2 is elected DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs. IXIA sends BUM traffic; confirm forwarding via PE2 only.
Action: Issue platform-specific control-plane process restart on PE2 (current DF): `restart process evpn-mgr` or equivalent. Monitor DF election state on PE1 during PE2 downtime.
Verify: After PE2 control-plane recovers, capture `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show evpn instance` on both PEs. Confirm local policy override still configured identically on PE1 and PE2 (preference values unchanged). Verify PE2 re-elected as DF. Check BUM traffic forwarding resumes via PE2. Measure convergence time from process restart to traffic resume.
36ea3aa9ef03b550.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
36ea3aa9ef03b550.expectation:
Pass:    Local policy override remains identical on both PEs after recovery (PE2 preference=200, PE1 preference=100); PE2 re-elected as DF; BUM traffic forwarding resumes via PE2 within 10 seconds of process restart; no DF flapping.
Fail-on: Policy inconsistency detected post-recovery (different preference values on PE1 vs PE2); wrong PE elected as DF; DF role flaps >2 times; traffic loss >10s; manual intervention required to restore policy consistency.

38345f5a2e8441f0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with ES multi-homing (ESI type 0); enable advertise-mac; verify DUT allocates LT2 label per ES and advertises via BGP Type-1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI, RFC7432bis §7.1) route to IXIA-emulated remote PE.
Action: IXIA injects five malformed LT2 variants while maintaining valid LT2 traffic baseline: (a) MPLS label=3 (reserved range 0-15), (b) label=1048576 (exceeds 20-bit range), (c) BoS=1 with non-zero ET in payload, (d) label stack depth=9 (LT2 at position 8), (e) BGP Type-1 UPDATE with MPLS Label TLV length=4 (expected 3). Send 100 malformed pkts/variant + 500 valid LT2 packets.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn instance <name> labels` confirms LT2 label unchanged; `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment` shows ES operational; `show interfaces <ES-interface> statistics` input-errors += 500 (5 variants × 100); `show log | include LT2` yields 5 syslog entries naming label value and drop reason; IXIA RX counter = 500 valid packets.
38345f5a2e8441f0.expectation:
Pass:    Input-errors counter = 500; 5 distinct syslog entries (one per variant, each with label value/TLV field and drop reason); IXIA receives exactly 500 valid LT2 packets; ES remains up; DUT CPU < 10% throughout test.
Fail-on: Any malformed packet forwarded to ES (IXIA RX > 500); fewer than 5 syslog entries; valid packet loss (IXIA RX < 500); ES flaps; LT2 label withdrawn from BGP; crash or CPU > 80%.

38cc22adb9a2cc96.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT baseline config; confirm no existing EVPN instances (`show running-config l2-services | include evpn` → empty output). Four mutex service-type values available: vlan-based, vlan-bundle, vlan-aware-bundle, port-based. This row tests vlan-aware-bundle.
Action: Create instance: `configure terminal` → `l2-services` → `evpn EVPN-TEST service-type vlan-aware-bundle` → `commit` → `exit`. Then remove: `configure terminal` → `l2-services` → `no evpn EVPN-TEST` → `commit` → `exit`.
Verify: Post-creation: `show running-config l2-services` must contain `evpn EVPN-TEST service-type vlan-aware-bundle`. Post-removal: same command must NOT contain string `evpn EVPN-TEST`; if `show evpn instance` command exists, verify it does not list EVPN-TEST.
38cc22adb9a2cc96.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
38cc22adb9a2cc96.expectation:
Pass:    Both commit operations succeed (no errors). Post-creation: `show running-config l2-services` contains `evpn EVPN-TEST service-type vlan-aware-bundle`. Post-removal: same output does NOT contain `evpn EVPN-TEST`.
Fail-on: Commit returns error; post-creation: `evpn EVPN-TEST` missing from running-config or service-type ≠ vlan-aware-bundle; post-removal: `evpn EVPN-TEST` still present in running-config or `show evpn instance` still lists EVPN-TEST.

394bf9c87f073841.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN-MPLS topology (DUT + neighbor PE) with F-bit=1 advertised in BGP EVPN extended community; four equal-cost MPLS LSPs between PEs; IXIA connected to both PEs, pre-programmed with 1000 distinct known-unicast L2 flows (varied src/dst MAC+IP for entropy).
Action: IXIA sends 1000 concurrent known unicast flows at aggregate line rate for 60s. Capture MPLS packets on all four LSPs simultaneously. Measure per-LSP packet count, aggregate throughput, end-to-end latency (p50/p99), and packet loss.
Verify: Decode MPLS label stack in captured packets: confirm Flow Label (3rd label after Tunnel+VPN) is present in 100% of known unicast packets. Sample 100 distinct flows and verify each uses a unique Flow Label value (entropy check). Calculate ECMP distribution: coefficient of variation (σ/μ) of packet counts across the four LSPs. Confirm aggregate throughput ≥ 95% of (4 × link rate), latency p99 ≤ 5ms, loss = 0%.
394bf9c87f073841.expectation:
Pass:    F-bit=1 advertised by both PEs in BGP EVPN updates; 100% of known unicast packets contain Flow Label as 3rd MPLS label; sampled flows (n=100) use distinct Flow Label values; ECMP distribution CV ≤ 0.10; throughput ≥ 95% of max; latency p99 ≤ 5ms; zero packet loss.
Fail-on: F-bit=0 or missing in BGP EVPN advertisements; any known unicast packet missing Flow Label; Flow Label values are not unique across sampled flows (no entropy); ECMP CV > 0.10 (unbalanced load indicating poor Flow Label hashing); throughput < 95%; latency p99 > 5ms; any packet loss > 0.

39679ea8d8f7a359.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with VLAN 100; configure `agg-eth 0` with `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 <type0-value>`, `load-balancing-mode single-active`; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; connect IXIA as local CE on the attachment circuit.
Action: IXIA sends unicast frame (src MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, dst MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE, VLAN 100) to DUT. Neighbor PE advertises MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE via BGP EVPN Type-2 route with EVPN label L1. Send return unicast frame from neighbor PE to DUT (dst MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, encapsulated with EVPN label L1).
Verify: Display the MAC Unicast Forwarding Table to confirm entries keyed by (VLAN-ID, MAC address) with documented fields: ESI and next-hop (local entry: attachment circuit ifIndex; remote entry: neighbor PE IP). Capture traffic on attachment circuit and PE-PE link to confirm bidirectional forwarding.
39679ea8d8f7a359.expectation:
Pass:    MACT contains both entries with complete documented structure—VLAN-ID (100), MAC address, ESI, next-hop type (local attachment circuit ifIndex for MAC learned from CE; remote PE IP for MAC learned via BGP EVPN). All frames forwarded correctly without drops.
Fail-on: MACT missing entries, incorrect or missing field values (VLAN-ID, MAC address, ESI, next-hop type/value mismatch), frames dropped/flooded/misforwarded to wrong next hop.

3a0771ddd79b20c5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and remote PE with EVPN instance; establish BGP peering with F-bit=1 (Flow Label capable). Run known unicast and BUM traffic to trigger Flow Label allocation and ESI label forwarding per RFC7432bis §18.1.
Action: Collect tech-support: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` (F-bit Extended Community), `show mpls forwarding-table` (flow label for unicast, ESI label for BUM), `show evpn instance`, `show evpn mac`, running-config (routing/bgp, l2-services/evpn sections), syslog grep "flow.label|F-bit|EVPN".
Verify: BGP output includes F-bit=1 Extended Community from remote PE; MPLS FIB shows flow label for known unicast, ESI label (no flow label) for BUM; EVPN/BGP config sections present; syslog contains Flow Label negotiation events.
3a0771ddd79b20c5.expectation:
Pass:    All show commands collected; F-bit=1 in BGP Extended Community; flow label in MPLS label stack for known unicast; ESI label (no flow label) for BUM per RFC7432bis §18.1 "MUST NOT use for BUM"; EVPN/BGP/MPLS config sections included; syslog entries confirm Flow Label handling.
Fail-on: Missing `show bgp l2vpn evpn` or F-bit Extended Community; missing `show mpls forwarding-table` or flow label absent for known unicast; flow label present for BUM (RFC7432bis §18.1 violation "MUST NOT"); missing EVPN/BGP config sections; no syslog evidence.

3a92ca9fc35e1ea8.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT + Cisco IOS-XR (or Juniper) PE both connected to same CE via agg-eth; configure identical ESI (identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09), load-balancing-mode single-active, on both PEs; establish MP-BGP EVPN session; verify both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per-ES (route type 1, RD:ESI:Ether-Tag) for the ESI via show bgp evpn routes; IXIA sends continuous traffic toward MAC addresses learned from CE.
Action: Shut down interface agg-eth on DUT; observe DUT withdraws Ethernet A-D per-ES route within es-waiting-time (default 3s per l2-services evpn es-waiting-time); continue IXIA traffic toward MACs previously reachable via DUT.
Verify: show bgp evpn routes type 1 on Cisco PE confirms DUT route withdrawn; show ethernet-segment on Cisco PE shows ESI now single-homed to Cisco; IXIA measures packet loss during failover window; show interface agg-eth counters on Cisco PE confirms traffic now aliases via Cisco path; repeat test with roles reversed (Cisco fails, DUT aliases).
3a92ca9fc35e1ea8.expectation:
Pass:    DUT withdraws Ethernet A-D per-ES route within 3s (es-waiting-time); Cisco PE updates aliasing next-hop to itself; IXIA measures ≤0.1% packet loss; bidirectional traffic forwards via aliasing path; route encoding interoperates (both directions tested); RFC7432bis ch.8.4 aliasing behavior verified.
Fail-on: Ethernet A-D per-ES route not withdrawn or delayed >3s; traffic black-holed (>1% loss 10s post-failure); Cisco PE rejects DUT route encoding (BGP UPDATE error); or aliasing logic fails (next-hop not updated, traffic still attempts failed path).

3aed6097239fa419.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure vlan-based EVPN service `evpn1` with BGP auto-discovery; add interface `x-eth 0/0/1` to the bridge domain.
Action: Navigate to `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1`; configure `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`; commit. From IXIA, send L2 frames destined to MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55.
Verify: MAC table shows static entry 00:11:22:33:44:55 bound to `x-eth 0/0/1`; verify frames destined to that MAC egress on `x-eth 0/0/1`; send frames with source-MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from another port and confirm static entry is not overwritten.
3aed6097239fa419.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 appears in MAC table bound to `x-eth 0/0/1`; all traffic to that destination MAC egresses on the configured interface; static entry persists despite source-MAC learning attempts.
Fail-on: Static MAC missing; traffic to that MAC floods, drops, or egresses incorrectly; dynamic learning overwrites the static entry.

3b055c82df7636ba.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `advertise-mac enable` and `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor. Configure multi-homing ESI (10-octet) on DUT access interface. Establish BGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE. Deploy IXIA host on multi-homed segment.
Action: Send IPv4 and IPv6 traffic from IXIA host to trigger MAC learning. Enable BGP UPDATE capture on neighbor PE for route type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement).
Verify: Decode captured route type 2 NLRI: RD field format matches Section 7 encoding (Type 0/1/2), MAC Address Length = 48 bits, MAC field = exactly 6 octets, IP Address field = 4 octets (IPv4) or 16 octets (IPv6), ESI field = 10 octets matching configured multi-homing ESI.
3b055c82df7636ba.expectation:
Pass:    Route type 2 advertised with: (a) RD per Section 7, (b) MAC = 6 octets, (c) IP = 4 octets for IPv4 / 16 octets for IPv6, (d) ESI = configured 10-octet value. Multi-homing and MAC advertisement coexist without route withdrawal.
Fail-on: MAC encoded with ≠ 6 octets; IP encoded with ≠ 4 octets (IPv4) or ≠ 16 octets (IPv6); RD missing/malformed; ESI field absent or ≠ configured value; routes withdrawn after multi-homing enabled.

3b1106a12694cfd9.action_steps:
Setup:  Under `configuration / l2-services`, configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`, attach AC on `x-eth 0/0/1 vlan 100`. Establish BGP EVPN AF peering to neighbor PE; verify baseline MAC learning and forwarding.
Action: Issue `control-word enable`, commit. Send 50 tagged L2 frames (VLAN 100) via IXIA to the AC. Reconfigure with `control-word disable`, commit, send 50 frames. Issue `no control-word`, commit, send 50 frames.
Verify: Capture MPLS-encapsulated packets on core-facing interface during each traffic burst; decode payload to confirm presence/absence of 4-byte Control Word field between label stack and Ethernet header. Check `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn1`.
3b1106a12694cfd9.expectation:
Pass:    With `control-word enable`, captured EVPN packets include the 4-byte Control Word; with `control-word disable` or `no control-word` (default), Control Word is absent. Running-config shows explicit `control-word enable` or `control-word disable` when configured.
Fail-on: Control Word present when disabled, absent when enabled, syntax error on configuration, or running-config does not persist `enable`/`disable` state when explicitly set.

3b17c19823e3bf5f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT as single-homed PE (no `ethernet-segment` on access interface); configure EVI with L2VPN EVPN. Configure 3rd-party PE as multihomed PE with ESI (using `identifier` equivalent) on LAG, same EVI. Establish BGP session with L2VPN-EVPN address-family; verify capability negotiation (Layer 2 Attributes extended community, Non-Revertive Capability).
Action: Advertise Ethernet A-D per-ES and per-EVI routes (type 1) from 3rd-party multihomed PE. Inject EVPN type-2 MAC/IP traffic via IXIA through 3rd-party PE. Observe DUT processing Ethernet A-D routes and installing aliased/backup paths. Collect `tech-support` bundle from DUT.
Verify: Bundle contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn` showing received Ethernet A-D routes with ESI, `show bgp neighbor` showing negotiated EVPN capabilities, `show running-config` confirming DUT is single-homed (no `ethernet-segment` stanza), and forwarding tables showing aliased next-hops for MAC addresses reachable via multihomed segment.
3b17c19823e3bf5f.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle demonstrates single-homed DUT MUST process multihoming procedures (Ethernet A-D routes, aliasing, ESI-based split-horizon) from 3rd-party multihomed PE per EVPNS-REQ#190, overriding RFC7432bis ch.8.7 SHOULD. Capabilities negotiated; forwarding via aliased paths operational.
Fail-on: DUT skips or rejects Ethernet A-D processing, no aliased paths installed, or DUT treats multihoming support as optional (RFC7432bis ch.8.7 SHOULD behavior). Missing capability negotiation or forwarding evidence.

3b708ba845f8e8d5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an ethernet-segment with shared ESI under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0`. Establish EVPN peering with a neighbor PE connected to the same multi-homed segment. Both PEs advertise EVPN Type-4 ES routes for the shared ESI.
Action: Configure the service-carving option under the ethernet-segment context to specify a non-default carving service for Default Forwarder Election. Commit the configuration.
Verify: Run `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` to confirm the service-carving line is present. Run `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` to verify the DF election method reflects the configured carving service (not the default). Confirm DF role (DF or non-DF) is assigned per the configured method.
3b708ba845f8e8d5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
3b708ba845f8e8d5.expectation:
Pass:    service-carving configuration commits successfully and appears in running-config. The `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` output displays the configured DF election method (not 'default carving service'). DF role assignment is active for the ethernet-segment.
Fail-on: Command is rejected at commit, service-carving line is missing from running-config after commit, or the DF election method remains 'default carving service' despite configuration.

3b74ad075113e210.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN VPLS with DUT as ingress PE (DF for local ESI-A), neighbor PE as non-DF egress PE for remote multi-homed ESI-B (00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99). Verify neighbor PE advertises ESI label 5000 via BGP EVPN route-type 1 (Ethernet AD per-ES). Enable `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in configuration/l2-services/evpn. IXIA attached to single-homed CE port on DUT.
Action: IXIA injects malformed BUM variants into EVPN: (a) broadcast (DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) with corrupted Ethernet FCS, (b) unknown-unicast with Ethernet length/type field = 0x0000 (invalid), (c) oversized multicast frame (L2 MTU + 200 bytes), (d) BUM frame with MPLS label already present in payload (unexpected encapsulation). Monitor DUT replication behavior toward non-DF neighbor PE.
Verify: Execute `show l2-services evpn statistics` on DUT (check for l2-input-discard or bum-validation-error counters). Check `show logging | include EVPN` for syslog entries. Packet capture on MPLS core link to neighbor PE: confirm zero malformed frames forwarded (with or without ESI label 5000 in MPLS stack).
3b74ad075113e210.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + neighbor PE
3b74ad075113e210.expectation:
Pass:    All malformed BUM variants (a)-(d) dropped at DUT L2 ingress before EVPN replication stage; error counter (e.g., "evpn-bum-input-discards") increments by ≥ 4; syslog contains entries "EVPN: BUM packet validation failed" with specific failure reason; packet capture shows 0 frames with ESI label 5000 sent to non-DF PE
Fail-on: Any malformed variant encapsulated with ESI label 5000 and forwarded to non-DF neighbor PE, DUT process crash (evpn_mgr or l2_fwd), silent discard (counter = 0 and no syslog), or corrupted frame reaches neighbor PE and triggers downstream failure

3b9c1385613ac3ea.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with Eth1 (standalone), Eth2 in LACP LAG1, Eth3 (single-home); NETCONF client authenticated; EVPN YANG capability verified.
Action: NETCONF edit-config (operation=merge): configure /l2-services/evpn/ethernet-segment[name='ES0']/esi/type-0/value='00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00' on Eth1; /ethernet-segment[name='ES1']/esi/type-1 on LAG1 (LACP-derived); /ethernet-segment[name='ES4']/esi/type-4 on Eth3 (router-ID). Negative: type-1 on Eth1 (non-LACP) → rpc-error expected; type-4 on LAG1 → rpc-error expected.
Verify: get-config //ethernet-segment returns three entries, esi/type in {0,1,4}; CLI "show evpn ethernet-segment" shows ES0/ES1/ES4 with correct type and interface; both negative tests return rpc-error with error-tag=operation-failed or invalid-value.
3b9c1385613ac3ea.expectation:
Pass:    All three ESI types accepted via NETCONF with constraints enforced (Type 1 requires LACP LAG, Type 4 requires single-home); get-config and CLI views identical; negative tests rejected with rpc-error; YANG model exposes type-0, type-1, type-4 containers.
Fail-on: Type 1 accepted on non-LACP interface; Type 4 accepted on LAG; CLI ESI type diverges from NETCONF datastore; YANG model missing any type node; capability not advertised; edit-config ignored.

3c3809947edc736c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance; configure `agg-eth 0` interface with `ethernet-segment` enabled; commit configuration to create at least one ES entry.
Action: Issue `show evpn ethernet-segments` at the operational prompt; repeat the command after 10 seconds with no configuration changes.
Verify: First execution displays table output including the configured `agg-eth 0` Ethernet Segment; second execution produces identical output (confirms idempotence).
3c3809947edc736c.expectation:
Pass:    Command succeeds; output contains table with ESI and interface columns; configured `agg-eth 0` ES entry appears; repeated invocations yield identical output.
Fail-on: Syntax error, output missing configured ES, parser malfunction, or output inconsistency between invocations on unchanged system.

3d82423eb0ae0a1a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment ES-1 (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) multi-homed to PE1 and PE2; configure MP2MP MPLS LSP (mLDP or P2MP LDP) for BUM transport; enable EVPN instance with `unknown-mac-flooding enable`.
Action: Examine BGP EVPN Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per-ES) routes from PE1 and PE2 using `show bgp evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99`; send broadcast frame from CE attached to ES-1 (ingress via PE1); capture MPLS-encapsulated BUM packet at remote PE3.
Verify: Confirm PE1 and PE2 advertise identical ESI label for ES-1 in their Type 1 routes; verify ESI label value is from platform DCB range (downstream context-based allocation); decode MPLS label stack on captured packet at PE3 to confirm ESI label matches the advertised value and identifies source ES-1.
3d82423eb0ae0a1a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (multi-homing ES-1) + remote PE + IXIA traffic gen + packet capture
3d82423eb0ae0a1a.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 and PE2 both advertise the same ESI label (e.g., label 5000) for ES-1; ESI label is allocated from DCB range per platform documentation; BUM packet at PE3 contains matching ESI label in MPLS stack, allowing PE3 to identify source ES-1.
Fail-on: PE1 and PE2 advertise different ESI labels for the same ES-1; ESI label not from DCB range; ESI label missing, mismatched, or incorrect in BUM packet MPLS stack at PE3.

3d840033eab4f15d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance "EVPN-100" with `control-word enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn EVPN-100`; establish BGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE; bind AC interface; send tagged MAC learning traffic via IXIA; capture egress trunk and confirm MPLS-encapsulated EVPN MAC packets contain 4-byte control word (between label stack and Ethernet header).
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn EVPN-100`, issue `no control-word`, commit; continue traffic.
Verify: Issue `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN-100` (control-word line absent); `show evpn EVPN-100 detail` reports control-word disabled or default; capture egress MPLS packets: 4-byte control word no longer present between label stack and payload.
3d840033eab4f15d.expectation:
Pass:    Running-config contains no `control-word` line; `show evpn` operational state reports disabled/default; packet captures after commit show EVPN MAC frames without control word (label stack → Ethernet directly, no 4-byte CW); MAC forwarding continues; no BGP session flap.
Fail-on: `control-word` remains in running-config; `show evpn` still reports enabled; egress packets still carry 4-byte control word after commit; commit fails; traffic blackhole or neighbor session reset.

3d98fbbd2293d20d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance "evpn-mac-test" (service-type vlan-based) with RD 65000:100, import-rt/export-rt 65000:100, attach interface GigabitEthernet0/1. Establish iBGP EVPN session with neighbor PE (10.0.0.2, AS 65000).
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-mac-test` mode and execute `mac-address-static 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee interface GigabitEthernet0/1`. Commit config.
Verify: On DUT: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-mac-test` (static MAC present), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-ip` (Type-2 route for 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee with RD 65000:100). On neighbor PE: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-ip` (Type-2 route received with next-hop = DUT loopback).
3d98fbbd2293d20d.expectation:
Pass:    Running-config shows `mac-address-static 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee interface GigabitEthernet0/1`. DUT originates BGP EVPN Type-2 route (MAC/IP Advertisement) for MAC 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee, RD 65000:100, RT 65000:100. Neighbor PE's BGP table contains this route.
Fail-on: Config command rejected or not persisted after commit. DUT does not generate Type-2 route for the static MAC. Neighbor PE does not receive the advertised route within 30 seconds of commit.

3dbda9e2275b4812.action_steps:
Setup: Configure EVPN instance on DUT with established BGP peering to neighbor PE; send L2 frames from IXIA to DUT so DUT learns MAC addresses in the EVPN.
Action: Enter the EVPN configuration mode, issue `no advertise-mac`, and commit; wait 30 seconds and capture the BGP route table. Then remove the `no` form (restoring default enabled behavior), commit, wait 30 seconds, and capture the route table again.
Verify: Check `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output on DUT and neighbor PE for EVPN Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes; inspect running-config for the command state; optionally capture BGP UPDATE messages.
3dbda9e2275b4812.expectation:
Pass: With `no advertise-mac` configured, the DUT does not advertise local MAC addresses; zero Type 2 routes appear in DUT BGP RIB for local MACs and neighbor PE does not receive them. With default (no command present, or explicit `advertise-mac`), all local MAC addresses appear as Type 2 routes in BGP on DUT and are received by neighbor PE.
Fail-on: Local MAC addresses appear in BGP Type 2 routes when `no advertise-mac` is configured, OR local MAC addresses do not appear in Type 2 routes when advertisement is enabled (default).

3e7582b06b676bed.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed ES on DUT and neighbor PE with intentionally mismatched DF algorithms and LACP System MACs; enable `host mac-address-duplicate-detection` with move-count 3, freeze-time 180; configure static MAC via `mac-address-static`; attach syslog collector.
