Action: Bring up multi-homed link to trigger ES misconfiguration alarm; move a MAC between PE nodes >3 times within move-interval to trigger duplicate-MAC temporary freeze; wait freeze-time to trigger permanent freeze alarm; send traffic from static MAC location from different PE IP to trigger static MAC relearning alarm; collect via `show logging` and syslog.
Verify: Parse alarm messages for exact ESI value, agg-eth interface, source IP-address, LACP System MAC, DF Algorithm name; confirm temporary freeze alarm cites RFC7432bis ch.15.1 and includes MAC-address; confirm permanent freeze alarm after freeze-time expiry; confirm static MAC relearning alarm cites RFC7432bis ch.15.2 and includes MAC-address + source ip-address.
3e7582b06b676bed.expectation:
Pass:    All 4 alarm types raised with complete detail: Multi-homed ES misconfiguration alarm contains ESI, agg-eth n, IP-address, LACP MAC, DF Algorithm; duplicate MAC temporary freeze alarm appears on move-count+1 move within move-interval; permanent freeze alarm appears after freeze-time seconds; static MAC relearning alarm contains MAC-address and source ip-address per RFC7432bis 15.1/15.2.
Fail-on: Any alarm missing, incomplete (lacks ESI/MAC/IP/algorithm field), or cites wrong RFC section; duplicate MAC frozen before move-count threshold or not frozen at all; static MAC relearning proceeds without alarm; permanent freeze alarm missing after freeze-time expiry.

3f1ad02aacd6527c.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish BGP session between DUT and remote PE with L2VPN EVPN address-family enabled; configure maximum-prefix with limit of 100 for this neighbor (default behavior: tear down session when exceeded); remote PE initially advertises 50 EVPN type-2 MAC/IP routes.
Action: Remote PE advertises 51 additional EVPN routes, bringing total to 101 and exceeding the configured threshold.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows neighbor transitioned to Idle or down state; packet capture confirms BGP NOTIFICATION with error code 6 (Cease) sub-code 1 (Maximum Number of Prefixes Reached); syslog contains maximum-prefix violation for this neighbor.
3f1ad02aacd6527c.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session tears down when 101st prefix arrives (exceeding limit of 100); NOTIFICATION code 6 sub-code 1 sent to remote PE; event logged to syslog; session stays down until manual clear.
Fail-on: Session remains Established after exceeding limit, or NOTIFICATION has incorrect code/sub-code, or no syslog entry, or session auto-recovers without restart timer configured.

3f3a1e1cc0462813.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE connected over MPLS; BGP EVPN session (AFI/SAFI 25/70) established; configure `advertise-mac enable` on DUT in `configuration / l2-services / evpn` mode; attach VLAN 100 to EVPN instance; assign RD per RFC7432bis §7 format (Type 0/1).
Action: Inject ARP request from IXIA (IPv4 10.1.1.10 MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55) into VLAN 100 on DUT to trigger MAC learning; capture BGP UPDATE message carrying EVPN route type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) sent from DUT to 3rd-party PE using Wireshark/tcpdump on peering link.
Verify: Decode EVPN NLRI on 3rd-party PE: confirm RD field = 8 octets (Type 0: <2-octet ASN>:<4-octet number> or Type 1: <4-octet IP>:<2-octet number>); MAC Address Length = 48 bits; MAC Address = 6 octets (00:11:22:33:44:55); IP Address Length = 32 bits; IP Address = 4 octets (0x0a010164). Check `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on Cisco or `show route table <vrf>.evpn.0` on Juniper for installed route.
3f3a1e1cc0462813.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Cisco IOS-XR / Juniper Junos) + IXIA traffic gen
3f3a1e1cc0462813.expectation:
Pass:    3rd-party PE accepts EVPN route type 2 with no BGP Notification; MAC/IP entry 00:11:22:33:44:55 / 10.1.1.10 visible in PE's EVPN table; RD encoded as 8 octets per RFC7432bis §7.9; MAC = exactly 6 octets; IP = exactly 4 octets (IPv4) or 16 octets (IPv6 if tested).
Fail-on: BGP UPDATE rejected (Notification code 3 subcode 10 or malformed attribute error); route not installed; MAC Address Length ≠ 48 bits; MAC field ≠ 6 octets; IP Address field ≠ 4 or 16 octets; RD malformed or missing.

3f3d66cbdb709a01.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that a locally-configured static MAC address is advertised via BGP EVPN Type 2 routes and installed as a static entry on remote PEs, preventing dynamic re-learning of the same MAC.
Method: Configure `mac-address-static` on PE1 in a two-PE EVPN topology, capture the BGP Type 2 advertisement, verify PE2 installs it as static, and test that PE2 rejects a subsequent dynamic learning attempt from its local access interface.
Setup: Configure two PEs (PE1, PE2) in a common EVPN instance using `evpn EVPN-100 service-type vlan-based` under `configuration / l2-services`. Assign access interfaces (`x-eth 0/0/1` on PE1, `x-eth 0/0/2` on PE2) to the EVPN; establish BGP session between PEs with L2VPN EVPN address family enabled.
Action: On PE1, navigate to `configuration / l2-services / evpn EVPN-100` and issue `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`; commit. Capture BGP UPDATE messages between PE1 and PE2 using tcpdump or IXIA. On PE2, check `show evpn mac address-table` for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55. From IXIA attached to PE2's `x-eth 0/0/2`, send a frame with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 to trigger a dynamic learning attempt on PE2.
Verify: Confirm PE1's BGP UPDATE contains a Type 2 route advertising MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 (decode captured packet). Confirm PE2's MAC table shows 00:11:22:33:44:55 as a static/remote entry learned from PE1 via `show evpn mac address-table`. Confirm that after the dynamic learning attempt, PE2 does not update the MAC table entry to point to its local access interface (the entry remains static and remote).
3f3d66cbdb709a01.expectation:
Pass: Type 2 route advertised by PE1 carrying MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55; PE2 installs it as a static entry; PE2 rejects dynamic learning of the same MAC from its local access side.
Fail-on: No Type 2 route advertised, PE2 does not install the MAC, PE2 installs it as dynamic instead of static, or PE2 accepts dynamic learning that overrides the static entry.

3f4dde71ac838788.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure basic BGP peering between DUT and PE (neighbor IPs, AS numbers, base session parameters); verify session established without L2VPN EVPN address-family.
Action: DUT: navigate to `configuration / routing / bgp / neighbor <peer-ip>` mode, issue `af-l2vpn evpn`, commit. PE: enable equivalent L2VPN EVPN address-family for DUT neighbor, commit.
Verify: `show bgp neighbors <peer-ip>` on DUT displays L2VPN EVPN among negotiated capabilities; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms neighbor is listed and session active.
3f4dde71ac838788.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session remains UP; DUT and PE both show L2VPN EVPN AFI/SAFI successfully negotiated
Fail-on: BGP session resets without recovering, L2VPN EVPN missing from negotiated capabilities, or CLI rejects the command

3f5e26e3081f18ac.action_steps:
Setup:  Create an EVPN service instance under `configuration / l2-services / evpn <evpn-name>` with `service-type vlan-based`. Configure mac-limit to restrict MAC learning to 10 addresses. Attach access interface `x-eth 0/0/1` to the EVPN service. Commit.
Action: From IXIA connected to `x-eth 0/0/1`, transmit frames with 15 unique source MAC addresses (00:00:00:00:00:01 through 00:00:00:00:00:0F) at 100 pps for 30 seconds.
Verify: Display the MAC address table for the EVPN instance. Count learned MAC addresses; verify the count does not exceed 10.
3f5e26e3081f18ac.expectation:
Pass:    MAC address table contains at most 10 learned MACs; the limit is enforced and additional MACs beyond the 10th are not learned.
Fail-on: More than 10 MAC addresses are learned, indicating the configured mac-limit is not enforced.

3f6864861062e127.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth interface with ethernet-segment identifier (type 0 or default type 4, e.g., 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88), load-balancing-mode single-active, attach to an EVI; establish BGP EVPN peering with a neighbor PE sharing the same ESI (multi-homed CE scenario).
Action: Verify ES operational status is up; confirm Ethernet A-D per-EVI routes (Type 1, per RFC7432bis §8.4) are advertised in BGP EVPN table with the configured ESI; execute tech-support collection command.
Verify: Inspect tech-support bundle for: ES operational state and DF election status (show evpn ethernet-segment or equivalent), BGP Type 1 routes with ESI match (show bgp evpn route type 1 or equivalent), running-config ethernet-segment block (identifier type/value, load-balancing-mode, service-carving), syslog ES state-change and DF election entries, FIB forwarding entries for the EVI.
3f6864861062e127.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: ES status showing operational state and DF role; BGP Type 1 routes advertising the ESI for the EVI (aliasing path active per RFC7432bis §8.4); complete ethernet-segment configuration (identifier type/value, load-balancing-mode, service-carving if set); syslog entries for ES bring-up, DF election, and state transitions; FIB entries for MACs on the multi-homed segment.
Fail-on: Missing any of: ES operational status output, Type 1 route advertisements in BGP table, ethernet-segment config block in running-config, ES-related syslog entries (DF election, state changes), or FIB forwarding state for the aliased segment.

3f97cdc89dc6466d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS instance under `l2-services`, bind two x-eth ports, commit. Start IXIA bi-directional L2 traffic (100 fps, 64-byte frames) for 30 seconds to populate the MAC table with 10 unique source MACs. Stop traffic and verify 10 learned entries via `show mac-address-table`.
Action: Execute `mac-aging-time 120`, commit. Mark time T=0. At T=140s, issue `show mac-address-table` and record learned-entry count. Execute `no mac-aging-time`, commit. Issue `show running-config` and verify the mac-aging-time line is absent.
Verify: At T=140s, `show mac-address-table` displays zero learned entries (all MACs aged out per 120s timer). After `no mac-aging-time`, `show running-config` contains no mac-aging-time configuration for this VPLS instance.
3f97cdc89dc6466d.expectation:
Pass:    All 10 learned MAC addresses are removed from the MAC table by T=140s (120s configured aging time + 20s tolerance). The `no mac-aging-time` command removes the configuration line from `show running-config`.
Fail-on: Any learned MAC address persists in `show mac-address-table` beyond T=140s, or `no mac-aging-time` does not remove the configuration from running-config.

3fc4d2babea493da.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that all four required EVPN alarm types (ES misconfiguration, temporary MAC freeze, permanent MAC freeze, static MAC relearning) are generated and captured in tech-support bundles for post-mortem analysis.
Method: Build a multi-homed EVPN setup, trigger all four alarm conditions by misconfiguring DF algorithm, exceeding MAC duplication thresholds, and moving a static MAC, then collect tech-support bundle and verify it contains all alarm records and syslog entries.
Setup: Multi-homed EVPN with DUT and neighbor PE both attached to the same ES via agg-eth 0. Configure host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-count 5 freeze-time 180 on an EVI. Configure mac-address-static AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 interface agg-eth 0 on the same EVI. Syslog collector configured to receive alarm messages from the DUT.
Action: (1) Trigger ES misconfiguration by changing DF algorithm on the DUT to mismatch the neighbor PE setting for the shared ES. (2) Trigger MAC duplication using IXIA: send traffic causing the same MAC to move between interfaces more than 5 times (exceeding move-count), first to freeze temporarily, then continue to trigger permanent freeze. (3) Trigger static MAC relearning by sending IXIA traffic from MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 arriving on a different interface than agg-eth 0. (4) Wait 60 seconds for alarms to propagate to syslog. (5) Run tech-support on the DUT and save the bundle off-box.
Verify: Open the tech-support bundle and confirm: show alarms output includes Multi-homed ES misconfiguration for ESI <esi-value>(agg-eth 0): from <neighbor-ip>, LACP System MAC <MAC-address>, DF Algorithm <algorithm>; show alarms includes Duplicated MAC address <mac-address> is frozen temporarily; show alarms includes Duplicated MAC address <mac-address> is frozen permanently; show alarms includes Relearning of static MAC address AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 from <remote-ip>; syslog entries corresponding to each of the four alarms; running-config showing host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-count 5 freeze-time 180 and mac-address-static AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01; EVPN MAC table showing frozen MAC entries.
3fc4d2babea493da.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA + syslog collector
3fc4d2babea493da.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains all four alarm types in show alarms with accurate ESI value, agg-eth 0 interface, IP addresses, LACP System MAC, and DF algorithm details; corresponding syslog entries present; running-config and EVPN MAC table included.
Fail-on: Any of the four required alarms missing from show alarms or syslog; alarm details (ESI value, agg-eth interface, IP addresses, DF algorithm, MAC addresses) incomplete or incorrect; bundle missing running-config or EVPN MAC table.

401f5864250030e5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and PE2 with identical ESI (identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88) on agg-eth interfaces; set load-balancing-mode all-active. Establish EVPN BGP session. Verify both advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes with downstream-assigned ESI labels via `show bgp evpn route-type 1`.
Action: Inject BUM packet (broadcast MAC DA) from IXIA into ES via DUT. DUT replicates using ingress replication to PE2 (same ES) and PE3 (different ES). Capture MPLS-encapsulated copies on wire to each egress PE.
Verify: Parse captured packets: copy to PE2 MUST include ESI label advertised by PE2 in its A-D per ES route; copy to PE3 MUST NOT include ESI label. At PE2, verify packet received with ESI label is NOT forwarded to local agg-eth interface (check `show interface agg-eth counters` and IXIA Rx on ES). Confirm ESI label present in `show mpls forwarding-table` with action drop-to-CE.
401f5864250030e5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (both on same ES) + IXIA traffic gen
401f5864250030e5.expectation:
Pass:    ESI label appears in A-D per ES route (type 1); BUM packet to same-ES egress PE contains that ESI label in MPLS stack; BUM packet to different-ES egress PE has no ESI label; PE2 does not forward received labeled BUM back to ES (agg-eth Tx counter static, IXIA sees no loop).
Fail-on: ESI label missing from type-1 route; BUM packet to same-ES PE missing ESI label (split-horizon not enforced, loop risk); labeled BUM forwarded back to ES at egress PE (agg-eth Tx increments, IXIA sees duplicate); ESI label absent from MPLS table or action allows forwarding to ES.

4039a0d751d10702.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure vES across two PEs (PE1 and PE2) in multi-home to same CE; set PE1 `ethernet-segment` Preference=200 with "Don't Preempt" enabled, PE2 Preference=100; configure matching `evpn` instance with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, `export-rt` on both; IXIA sends bidirectional L2 traffic (1 Gbps) through the ES.
Action: Run for ≥24 hours with PE1 expected as active DF (highest preference + Don't Preempt); poll `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show evpn instance detail` every 30 min to log DF state; run `show platform process memory` hourly.
Verify: Parse logs to confirm PE1 remained DF with no role transitions; confirm PE1 ES interface counters monotonic (no resets); memory delta <2% on both PEs; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows zero flaps.
4039a0d751d10702.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
4039a0d751d10702.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 (Preference=200, Don't Preempt set) is DF for entire 24 h with zero DF transitions; memory variance ≤2% on both PEs; traffic counters monotonically increasing; BGP EVPN neighbor state stable.
Fail-on: Any DF role swap between PE1 and PE2; memory growth >5% on either PE; counter resets or freeze on ES interface; EVPN route flaps or neighbor down events.

406289517a97381d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP with local AS 65001 (2-byte private AS, range 64512-65534) and establish L2VPN EVPN peering with a neighbor in AS 100 (public AS). Configure an EVPN instance (vlan-based or vlan-aware-bundle per the `evpn` command) and ensure MAC/IP routes are ready to advertise. Configure `private-as` under the neighbor's L2VPN EVPN address-family context.
Action: Commit configuration to activate `private-as`. Advertise locally originated EVPN routes (Type 2 MAC/IP Advertisement) to the neighbor. Capture the AS_PATH of outbound routes using `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor <neighbor-ip> advertised-routes` or tcpdump on the BGP session (port 179).
Verify: Inspect the AS_PATH attribute in advertised EVPN routes. Confirm that private AS 65001 is absent from the AS_PATH.
406289517a97381d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for BGP L2VPN EVPN peering and AS_PATH verification)
406289517a97381d.expectation:
Pass:    AS_PATH in outbound EVPN updates does not contain private AS numbers in ranges 64512-65534 or 4200000000-4294967294. Locally originated routes have AS_PATH empty (private local AS stripped) or containing only public AS numbers.
Fail-on: Private AS 65001 (or any AS in the specified private ranges) appears in the AS_PATH of outbound updates, or AS_PATH is malformed.

40a2bde61cb92a00.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment (ESI type 0x00) on agg-eth interfaces on DUT and neighbor PE; configure `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` with `auto-discovery`, matching `import-rt` / `export-rt`; apply local DF election policy override (e.g., force-highest-preference) consistently on both PEs per RFC 9785 §4.2.
Action: Bring up the ES; verify DF election completes via `show evpn ethernet-segment` showing DF status; collect tech-support from both DUT and neighbor PE using `show tech-support` or equivalent.
Verify: Parse tech-support bundles for: `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` (DF per VLAN, preference values), `show bgp evpn route-type 1` and `route-type 4` (ES-Import RT, ESI consistency), running-config `ethernet-segment` and `evpn` stanzas, syslog grep 'DF election|Ethernet.Segment'.
40a2bde61cb92a00.expectation:
Pass:    Both tech-support bundles contain: (a) identical DF policy config in running-config, (b) `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` showing consistent elected DF per Ethernet Tag, (c) BGP Type 1 (per-ES A-D) and Type 4 (ES route) with matching ESI and ES-Import extended community (per RFC 7432), (d) syslog confirming DF election success with no policy mismatch alarms.
Fail-on: Inconsistent DF policy between PEs in running-config, missing `show evpn ethernet-segment` output, missing Type 1 or Type 4 routes, DF election failure syslog, or alarm indicating policy inconsistency per RFC 9785 §4.2 MUST requirement.

418b3b4e79ea8955.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (PE1, PE2) with identical ESI on agg-eth interfaces and `load-balancing-mode single-active`; configure EVPN instance with BD; remote PE3 imports Ethernet A-D per ES routes; IXIA sends L2 traffic toward multi-homed CE.
Action: Identify Primary DF PE via `show evpn ethernet-segment <esi> detail` (check DF role); kill EVPN control-plane process on Primary DF PE (platform-specific debug command, e.g., `debug platform process-kill evpn-mgr`).
Verify: On PE3: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` confirms Ethernet A-D per ES routes have ESI Label extended community with redundancy-mode=1; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` shows MAC/IP routes reachable via new Primary DF only; `show evpn ethernet-segment remote` confirms PE3 deduced Single-Active mode; on new Primary DF: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` confirms Ethernet A-D per EVI route has L2-Attr extended community with P-bit=1; IXIA reports traffic recovery.
418b3b4e79ea8955.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE (2 PEs for ES multi-homing + 1 remote PE + IXIA traffic gen + process-kill harness)
418b3b4e79ea8955.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts within es-waiting-time (default 3s); backup PE becomes new Primary DF; remote PE3 imports routes with redundancy-mode=1 in ESI Label ext-community and correctly deduces Single-Active mode; new Primary DF advertises Ethernet A-D per EVI route with P-bit=1 in L2-Attr ext-community; traffic interruption ≤ es-waiting-time + DF election time (typically 3s + <2s).
Fail-on: Process does not restart; redundancy-mode ≠ 1 in ESI Label ext-community; remote PE3 fails to deduce Single-Active mode or load-balances across both PEs; P-bit not set in new Primary DF's per-EVI routes; traffic interruption > 10s or permanent black-hole.

41db976cc4780b03.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (VNI 1000, Ethernet Tag 100) with `unknown-mac-flooding enable` and `advertise-mac enable` under configuration/l2-services/evpn; establish BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE with `af-l2vpn evpn`; verify Type-3 IMET route advertised.
Action: IXIA (acting as CE) sends Ethernet frame with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 (new) and destination MAC 99:88:77:66:55:44 (unknown) on DUT CE-facing port with Ethernet Tag 100.
Verify: Check `show l2-services evpn mac-table` for source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55; check `show l2-services evpn forwarding` confirms flood-list includes neighbor PE; IXIA at neighbor PE captures the flooded frame; `show l2-services evpn statistics` unknown-unicast-flood counter increments.
41db976cc4780b03.expectation:
Pass:    Source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 appears in local MAC table; frame flooded to neighbor PE via EVPN Type-3 IMET route; neighbor PE IXIA receives frame with original dest MAC 99:88:77:66:55:44; unknown-unicast-flood counter ≥ 1.
Fail-on: Source MAC absent from mac-table after 5 seconds; frame dropped (flood counter = 0); neighbor PE IXIA receives zero frames; dest MAC lookup skipped (no flood despite unknown-mac-flooding enable).

41e203879875ab3a.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT in mode `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` with no explicit ESI configured (type 4 default per CLI doc).
Action: Execute `identifier ?` to display ESI type options and help text. Execute `iden` + TAB to verify command completion to `identifier`. Execute `identifier ` followed by `?` to list ESI type syntaxes (should include at minimum type 4, the documented default). Note whether help indicates default type.
Verify: `?` lists `identifier` with description 'set Ethernet segment identifier (ESI) value' or equivalent. TAB completes uniquely to `identifier`. Sub-token `?` after `identifier ` enumerates all ESI type forms per the CLI doc Parameters Table, and help text indicates type 4 is the default.
41e203879875ab3a.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
41e203879875ab3a.expectation:
Pass:    `identifier` is discoverable via `?` and TAB, sub-token help lists all documented ESI types including the type 4 default, and help text matches the CLI doc description.
Fail-on: Command not found via `?`, TAB completion fails, sub-token help omits documented ESI types or type 4 default, or description text does not mention ESI/Ethernet segment identifier.

41f17f5f2d7de9dc.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with matching ESI (`interface agg-eth 0; ethernet-segment; identifier 1`), `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `service-carving default`. Both PEs advertise Type 1, Type 4 (ES route), and ES-Import RT. IXIA traffic flows through the all-active path ≥ 1 minute at line rate.
Action: On PE1: change to `service-carving highest-random-weight`; commit. Capture Type 4 BGP update; verify DF Election Extended Community 'DF Alg' field reflects HRW (RFC8584 §3). On PE2: configure `service-carving highest-preference preference 30000` (mismatch); both PEs must fall back to Default per RFC7432bis §8.5. Revert PE1 to `service-carving default`.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms active algorithm (HRW on PE1 before mismatch, Default on both after). `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` shows DF Election Extended Community with correct 'DF Alg' values at each stage. IXIA loss histogram during each change. After revert, running-config matches original.
41f17f5f2d7de9dc.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner (two PEs with BGP EVPN, CE with LACP for shared ESI, IXIA for continuous traffic and per-second loss measurement)
41f17f5f2d7de9dc.expectation:
Pass:    Algorithm change PE1→HRW completes hitlessly (≤0.01% loss per all-active MUST); Type 4 DF Alg field updates within 1 s; when PE2 advertises different algorithm, both PEs fall back to Default within es-waiting-time (3 s default per CLI doc); revert restores original config byte-for-byte.
Fail-on: Loss >0.01% during homogeneous algorithm change, PEs do not fall back to Default when DF Alg values mismatch (violates the fallback MUST), DF Alg field not updated in Type 4 route, or revert leaves config drift.

420ff9ec4edb2c56.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT and neighbor PE) with matching ESI using `identifier 1` and `load-balancing-mode single-active` on `agg-eth 0`. Verify primary DF elected and backup DF signaled via ES routes (RFC7432bis §8.6). IXIA sends bidirectional traffic through the ES.
Action: Trigger control-plane reset on the primary DF PE (use platform reset CLI) while traffic is active. Time the failover.
Verify: Confirm backup DF was pre-signaled in ES route advertisements before reset. After reset, verify backup DF takes over forwarding and measure traffic loss/convergence.
420ff9ec4edb2c56.expectation:
Pass:    Backup DF signaled per RFC7432bis §8.6 before reset; backup DF activates and forwards traffic; convergence ≤ 3 s (es-waiting-time default); traffic loss < 1 s
Fail-on: No backup DF signaling observed; convergence > 3 s; or traffic outage > 1 s

422b946dbfff00cd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS instance `vpls test-vpls` under `configuration / l2-services`; attach at least one AC interface; capture the default MAC aging time via `show mac-address-table aging-time` or equivalent.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / vpls test-vpls` and issue `mac-aging-time 600`. Commit. Generate traffic from IXIA to learn a MAC address in the VPLS. Wait 600 seconds (10 minutes) without traffic. Issue `no mac-aging-time` and commit; verify default (typically 300s) is restored in running-config.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls test-vpls` displays `mac-aging-time 600` after first commit, and omits it (or shows default) after `no` form. `show mac-address-table` confirms the learned MAC is flushed after the configured 600s interval (not before, not significantly later). After `no` form, verify aging reverts to the default interval documented in the platform manual.
422b946dbfff00cd.expectation:
Pass:    `mac-aging-time 600` accepted without commit error; running-config includes the line; learned MAC entry ages out within 600s ± 10s tolerance; `no mac-aging-time` removes the explicit config and operational behavior reverts to default aging time.
Fail-on: Commit rejects `mac-aging-time` with valid integer; configured value does not persist after reload; MAC entry ages out significantly earlier (< 580s) or later (> 620s) than configured; `no` form fails to restore default, or default value undefined in `show` output.

426b09b7a47595b3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT, neighbor) in All-Active multi-homing on shared ES (identifier 0 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01, load-balancing-mode all-active) with P2MP MPLS LSPs enabled for BUM. DUT advertises upstream-assigned ESI label L1 in Ethernet A-D per ES route; neighbor PE programs L1 in DUT's context label space. IXIA connected to neighbor PE sends BUM traffic.
Action: IXIA injects P2MP MPLS packets with ESI label L1 and malformed headers: (a) TTL=0, (b) reserved label value 0x000000, (c) S-bit=1 mid-stack, (d) EXP bits=0x7 (reserved), (e) label stack depth >10, (f) truncated header (< 4 bytes after label). Each variant sent as separate stream, 100 pps, 10 seconds.
Verify: On neighbor PE: `show mpls forwarding-table label <L1>` confirms label programmed with action "receive, do not forward to ES"; `show interface agg-eth <ES-interface> statistics` shows zero egress packets matching malformed streams; `show log messages | match "MPLS.*malformed|TTL.*expired"` captures drop events with label L1; `show system errors mpls` displays counters mpls_invalid_ttl, mpls_malformed_header, mpls_reserved_label incremented by ~600 each.
426b09b7a47595b3.expectation:
Pass:    All six malformed variants dropped at MPLS label processing (before ES lookup); error counters increment by packet count (±5%); syslog entries present for each drop type ("MPLS: TTL expired, label <L1>", "MPLS: malformed header, label <L1>"); zero packets egress ES interface; DUT remains stable (no core dump, control-plane reachable).
Fail-on: Any malformed packet forwarded to ES interface (violates RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2 MUST NOT forward to originating ES), missing error counters (indicates silent drop without accounting), system crash/reload, control-plane timeout >5s, or syslog missing drop reason (un-diagnosable failure mode).

433f4ea4c2e301ea.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with two EVPN attachment circuits: `agg-eth 0` with reserved ESI (no `ethernet-segment` config, defaults to ESI=0) and `agg-eth 1` with non-reserved ESI (`ethernet-segment; identifier 0 <type0-value>`). Neighbor PE advertises MAC/IP Advertisement routes for both ESI contexts. IXIA generates continuous bidirectional traffic (100 Mbps mixed L2/L3) to MAC addresses in both segments. Baseline: `show evpn mac`, `show evpn esi`, `show evpn route type 2`, `show platform process memory`.
Action: Run for ≥24 hours with hourly PE-to-CE link flaps (shut/no shut on both `agg-eth 0` and `agg-eth 1`). Neighbor PE controlled to send MAC/IP Advertisement routes immediately but delay Ethernet A-D per ES routes by 10 seconds for the non-reserved ESI segment. Monitor route installation timestamps via `show evpn route type 2 detail` and `show evpn route type 1` after each flap cycle. Sample memory and alarm log hourly.
Verify: ESI=0 MAC entries installed immediately (<1 sec) upon receiving MAC/IP Advertisement route alone, with no dependency on Ethernet A-D per ES routes. Non-reserved ESI MAC entries installed only after BOTH MAC/IP Advertisement route AND Ethernet A-D per ES routes received (10+ sec delay observable). Route resolution timing stable across all 24 flap cycles (σ <5%). Forwarding state at hour 24 matches baseline. Memory growth ≤5%. Zero unexpected alarms.
433f4ea4c2e301ea.equipment: DUT + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h) + neighbor PE with route advertisement control
433f4ea4c2e301ea.expectation:
Pass:    ESI=0 MAC forwarding installed within 1 sec of MAC/IP Advertisement route alone (no wait for Ethernet A-D per ES). Non-reserved ESI MAC forwarding installed only when both MAC/IP Advertisement route and Ethernet A-D per ES routes present (10+ sec delay confirms dependency). Route resolution timing variance <5% across 24h. Memory growth ≤5%. Traffic loss only during expected convergence windows (<3 sec per `es-waiting-time` default).
Fail-on: ESI=0 MAC installation delayed waiting for Ethernet A-D per ES routes (violates RFC7432bis §9.2.2 MUST for reserved ESI). Non-reserved ESI MAC installed before Ethernet A-D per ES routes arrive (violates RFC7432bis §9.2.2 MUST for non-reserved ESI). Route resolution timing drift >5% over 24h. Memory growth >5%. Unexpected packet loss or alarms.

43425483d7f94a8b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure evpn (vlan-aware-bundle service type); configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0; map VLANs 100, 200, 300 to the segment; enable auto-discovery with export-rt and import-rt. IXIA emulates remote PE advertising both Type 1 and Type 4 routes.
Action: IXIA sends continuous bidirectional L2 traffic across VLANs 100/200/300 for ≥ 24 hours at 50% line rate. Monitor BGP EVPN route table continuously.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` polled hourly; confirm Type 1 routes contain RD, ESI, ET ID = VLAN-ID, and Label LT2 for all three VLANs; `show platform process memory` polled hourly; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows zero session resets; IXIA Rx/Tx counters polled hourly.
43425483d7f94a8b.expectation:
Pass:    Type 1 routes (per RFC7432bis ch.8.4) advertised continuously for 24 h with correct fields (RD, ESI, ET ID, Label LT2); Type 4 routes present throughout (prerequisite for forwarding); memory stable (±5%); zero BGP session resets; IXIA counters monotonic with <0.001% loss
Fail-on: Type 1 route withdrawal, flap, or field corruption; Type 4 route absence; memory growth >5%; any BGP session reset; traffic loss >0.1% or counter freeze

439f1b3964355881.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT + Cisco/Juniper PE connected over MPLS; configure BGP EVPN peering, create 3 VPLS instances (EVI 100, 200, 300) with `l2-services evpn` and `advertise-mac enable`, configure DUT loopback as BGP router-id; run packet capture on 3rd-party PE.
Action: On DUT, enable `routing bgp vrf <vrf> neighbor <peer-ip> af-l2vpn evpn` for all 3 EVIs; trigger Type 3 (IMET) route advertisement; decode captured BGP UPDATE messages to extract RD, Originating Router IP Address (ORIA), and IP Address Length fields from all Type 3 routes.
Verify: Parse Type 3 routes: RD format matches RFC7432bis §7 encoding (Type 0/1/2); ORIA = DUT loopback IP; ORIA identical across all 3 EVIs; 3rd-party PE installs routes in `show bgp l2vpn evpn` table; inject unknown-MAC BUM frame from IXIA behind DUT, confirm replication to 3rd-party PE attached CE; repeat test with DUT using IPv4 loopback and again with IPv6 loopback (both must interoperate).
439f1b3964355881.expectation:
Pass:    All Type 3 routes accepted by 3rd-party PE; RD conforms to §7; ORIA = DUT loopback and consistent across EVI 100/200/300; BUM traffic replicates correctly in both IPv4 and IPv6 ORIA scenarios; no BGP NOTIFICATION errors.
Fail-on: 3rd-party PE rejects Type 3 route with NOTIFICATION (malformed RD or ORIA); ORIA differs between any two EVIs; RD format violates §7 encoding rules; BUM traffic not replicated to 3rd-party PE; interop fails when switching ORIA address family (IPv4 ↔ IPv6).

43a75a0893e0e2c4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'evpn-test1' with service-type vlan-bundle on x-eth 0/0/1, bind VLAN bundle VLANs 100-110, set import-rt/export-rt 65000:100, enable advertise-mac. Configure matching EVPN instance on neighbor PE with RT 65000:100.
Action: From IXIA, send 100 frames each with VLAN tags 100, 105, 110 (in bundle) and 50, 200, 999 (not in bundle); use distinct source MAC per VLAN (MAC-A/100, MAC-B/105, MAC-C/110, MAC-X/50, MAC-Y/200, MAC-Z/999).
Verify: `show l2-services evpn evpn-test1` confirms vlan-bundle config; `show l2-services mac-table evpn evpn-test1` for MAC learning; `show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics` for VLAN drop counters.
43a75a0893e0e2c4.expectation:
Pass:    MACs from VLANs 100, 105, 110 (MAC-A, MAC-B, MAC-C) learned in evpn-test1 MAC table; MACs from VLANs 50, 200, 999 (MAC-X, MAC-Y, MAC-Z) absent; interface drops increment by 300 frames (100 × 3 non-member VLANs).
Fail-on: Any MAC from non-member VLANs (50/200/999) appears in evpn-test1 MAC table, or drop counter does not increment for invalid VLAN frames.

43b050bea3a1135c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT in EVPN multi-homing mode with non-zero ESI (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:01) and `advertise-mac enable` (default). Configure two neighbor PEs both advertising the same Ethernet A-D per EVI route (RT-2) for the ESI: PE1 sends route WITH EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community (MTU=1500, P=1, B=0); PE2 sends route WITHOUT Layer 2 Attributes (P=0, B=1). Enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under DUT's BGP neighbor config for both PEs; use `import-rt <route-target>` to accept both routes.
Action: Trigger best-path selection by receiving both routes simultaneously. Inject bidirectional traffic from IXIA destined to MAC addresses in the Ethernet Segment to force aliasing/backup path decisions.
Verify: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 <ESI>` and `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI>` to confirm DUT selected PE1's route (with Layer 2 Attributes extended community) as best path per RFC7432bis §7.13.2 criterion 1.a (routes without the extended community are removed from consideration). Verify P/B flags (P=1, B=0) from PE1's selected route govern aliasing/backup forwarding; confirm traffic uses PE1's next-hop.
43b050bea3a1135c.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (PE1, PE2 for path diversity) + IXIA traffic gen
43b050bea3a1135c.expectation:
Pass:    DUT applies RFC7432bis §7.13.2 selection criteria in the specified order: removes PE2's route from consideration (no Layer 2 Attributes extended community), selects PE1's route as best path, and honors its P/B flags (P=1, B=0) for multi-homing forwarding. Traffic forwards via PE1's next-hop.
Fail-on: DUT selects PE2's route (without Layer 2 Attributes extended community) as best path, OR applies selection criteria out of order, OR ignores P/B flags from the selected route, OR traffic forwards to PE2 instead of PE1, OR multi-homing aliasing/backup behavior does not reflect the best-path selection result.

43b1466c8f49ea47.action_steps:
Setup:  Under `configuration/l2-services/evpn`, set `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default) and `advertise-mac enable`; establish BGP EVPN session with IXIA (neighbor `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled); verify DUT advertises IMET route-type-3 with ingress-replication P-tunnel label (e.g., 10000) via `show routing bgp routes evpn route-type 3`; prime IXIA to craft malformed MPLS packets with label 10000.
Action: IXIA sends 6 fault types (10 packets each) with MPLS label 10000: (1) MPLS TC bits = 7 (reserved), (2) MPLS TTL = 0, (3) inner Ethernet frame truncated to 32B, (4) inner dst MAC = 00:00:00:00:00:00, (5) MPLS payload missing Ethernet header (only 8B after pop), (6) inner frame 9500B (exceeds MTU).
Verify: `show l2-services evpn statistics` shows ingress-error counter ≥ 60; `show log` contains 6 syslog entries 'Malformed packet on ingress-replication label 10000'; DUT SSH/ping responsive; `show l2-services evpn mac-table` has no 00:00:00:00:00:00 entry; IXIA sniffer on local AC captures zero flooded packets.
43b1466c8f49ea47.expectation:
Pass:    Ingress-error counter increments by ≥ 60 (10 pkts × 6 types); 6 distinct syslog error entries reference label 10000; no invalid MAC in mac-table; zero malformed packets forwarded to AC or remote PE; DUT remains stable (SSH/ping OK).
Fail-on: DUT crash or unresponsive; any malformed packet forwarded to AC or PE; ingress-error counter = 0 or < 60; no syslog entry; MAC 00:00:00:00:00:00 appears in mac-table; valid ingress-replication traffic fails post-test (stale state).

43c6d083ce54d55b.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that a host moving between PEs triggers MAC re-advertisement with incremented sequence, preventing traffic blackholing from stale FIB or incorrect sticky-MAC treatment.
Method: Configure two-PE EVPN topology, move host from PE1 to PE2, verify MAC Mobility extended community sequence increment, prompt BGP and FIB convergence, and absence of sticky-MAC flag.
Setup: Configure EVPN instance on PE1 and PE2 with shared parameters, attach host H1 to PE1's access interface (e.g., `agg-eth 0`), verify H1's MAC is learned locally on PE1 and advertised via BGP Type 2 route to PE2.
Action: Disconnect H1 from PE1's interface and connect to PE2's interface; send frames from H1 on PE2's side to trigger MAC learning and BGP advertisement from PE2.
Verify: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on PE2 to confirm it advertises H1's MAC with MAC Mobility extended community sequence incremented from PE1's previous value; execute same command on PE1 to confirm it withdraws its previous advertisement and installs PE2's route; execute `show evpn mac address-table` on PE1 to confirm H1 now points to PE2 as remote egress; execute `show evpn duplicate-mac` to verify no sticky-MAC flag is set.
43c6d083ce54d55b.expectation:
Pass:    MAC Mobility sequence increments by 1 relative to PE1's previous advertisement, PE1 withdraws its old route and installs PE2's route promptly (within seconds), PE1's MAC table shows H1 as remote via PE2, no sticky-MAC flag on H1.
Fail-on: Sequence does not increment, PE1 and PE2 both advertise H1 concurrently (no withdrawal from PE1), PE1's MAC table remains stale (shows H1 as local or via wrong PE), or sticky-MAC flag incorrectly applied.

43f8d8c17a73dc00.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with ethernet-segment under configuration/interface/agg-eth 0 toward the same CE; enable af-l2vpn evpn on BGP neighbors; verify both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes; start continuous IXIA traffic through both PEs.
Action: On PE1, issue `interface agg-eth 0; shutdown` to simulate PE-to-CE link failure; capture BGP UPDATE messages at PE2 and IXIA packet captures.
Verify: PE1 withdraws Ethernet A-D per ES routes (check `show bgp evpn routes`); PE2 removes PE1 from ES next-hop set (check `show evpn ethernet-segment`); MAC/IP Advertisement route withdrawals arrive with timestamps spanning 0 to MAC ageout timer; PE2 continues forwarding all traffic.
43f8d8c17a73dc00.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 withdraws all Ethernet A-D per ES routes within convergence window after link-down; remote PE2 prunes PE1 from aliasing set; MAC/IP withdrawals arrive staggered (≥2 distinct timestamps if ageout >0); zero packet loss on surviving PE2.
Fail-on: Ethernet A-D per ES routes remain in remote PE2's table >5 seconds after link-down; PE2 still sends traffic toward failed PE1; all MAC/IP withdrawals arrive in same BGP UPDATE (no stagger); multi-homing state breaks on PE2 (advertise-mac stops).

441afa77780a7033.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed ES on agg-eth interface (DUT and neighbor PE); configure EVPN instance with vlan-aware-bundle service-type; enable auto-discovery; set DF election algorithm to preference-based with Don't Preempt capability on DUT; trigger failover by shutting down DUT's agg-eth, wait for neighbor PE to become DF; bring DUT's agg-eth back up.
Action: After DUT rejoins ES (neighbor PE remains DF due to Don't Preempt), collect tech-support bundle.
Verify: Tech-support must contain: `show evpn ethernet-segment <ES-ID> detail` (DF role = non-DF, Don't Preempt capability present), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type ethernet-ad` (Type-4 routes with DF Election Extended Community Don't Preempt bit set), running-config (`interface agg-eth` with `ethernet-segment`, `l2-services evpn` with `auto-discovery`), syslog entries (DF election triggered, non-revertive decision logged), `show forwarding evpn <evpn-name>` (FIB for EVI).
441afa77780a7033.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
441afa77780a7033.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains DF state showing DUT as non-DF post-recovery, BGP Type-4 AD routes with DF Election Extended Community Don't Preempt bit, ES config, DF election syslog entries proving non-revertive behavior, and EVI FIB state.
Fail-on: Missing DF state output, BGP Type-4 routes without DF Election Extended Community, no syslog evidence of DF election events, or missing ES/EVPN configuration.

44825cbbf347bd90.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP local-as 65001, neighbor 192.0.2.1 remote-as 65002, and `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf default / neighbor 192.0.2.1`; establish session with IXIA BGP peer advertising EVPN routes; verify EVPN route exchange with `show bgp l2vpn evpn` showing received prefixes.
Action: Execute `write memory` to save configuration; reload DUT with `reload`; after boot completes and BGP process starts, wait for session re-establishment.
Verify: Run `show running-config` to confirm `af-l2vpn evpn` present under neighbor 192.0.2.1; run `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` to confirm neighbor Established with same prefix count as pre-reload; run `show bgp neighbor 192.0.2.1` to confirm EVPN capability negotiated.
44825cbbf347bd90.expectation:
Pass:    `af-l2vpn evpn` configuration persists byte-for-byte in running-config post-reload; BGP session re-establishes with L2VPN EVPN AFI/SAFI (25/70) negotiated; EVPN routes automatically re-received (prefix count matches pre-reload baseline ±10%).
Fail-on: `af-l2vpn evpn` missing or altered post-reload; BGP session establishes without EVPN capability; EVPN route count drops to zero or differs by >50% from baseline; manual CLI intervention required to restore EVPN functionality.

449702eded7c202d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with AS 65001; create EVPN instance 'evpn-vlan100' with service-type vlan-based for VLAN 100; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE.
Action: Enable advertise-mac on the EVPN instance; trigger MAC route advertisement by bringing up a CE attachment circuit on VLAN 100; capture BGP UPDATE from DUT advertising EVPN Type-2 (MAC/IP) routes.
Verify: Issue 'show bgp evpn routes' and 'show l2-services evpn evpn-vlan100 detail' on DUT; decode the RT in the captured BGP UPDATE: Global Administrator field = 65001, Local Administrator field = 0x000000000064 (decimal 100 in lowest 12 bits, upper 52 bits zero).
449702eded7c202d.expectation:
Pass:    RT is auto-derived as 65001:100; BGP UPDATE wire format shows RT with Global Admin = 65001 (AS number) and Local Admin = 0x000000000064 (VLAN 100 encoded in lowest 12 bits, bits 12-63 all zero).
Fail-on: RT not auto-derived (manual RT required), or VLAN ID encoded incorrectly (e.g., shifted into upper bits), or any non-zero bits in Local Admin field bits 12-63.

44b565b5b26ad7eb.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs multihomed to same CE via LAG; on each PE's agg-eth interface configure `ethernet-segment` with Type 1 ESI (LACP-based, RFC7432bis §5), enable `evpn <name> service-type vlan-aware-bundle` with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, `export-rt`; send EVPN traffic through both PEs.
Action: Flap one PE's ES link to trigger DF re-election, then collect tech-support bundle on both PEs using vendor command (e.g., `show tech-support evpn`).
Verify: Parse tech-support archives for: `show evpn ethernet-segment` (ESI, DF state, redundancy mode), `show evpn ethernet-segment esi <value>` (LACP System MAC in high-order 6 octets of ESI Value), `show l2-services evpn <name> auto-discovery` (import-rt/export-rt, discovered remote PEs), running-config `ethernet-segment` stanza, syslog entries for DF election events.
44b565b5b26ad7eb.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
44b565b5b26ad7eb.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains all five show command outputs with populated data; ESI Value field encodes LACP System MAC in octets 0–5 (RFC7432bis §5); DF election syslog present; auto-discovery shows peer PE; running-config includes `ethernet-segment` under agg-eth.
Fail-on: Missing any of: ES show output, auto-discovery state/config, DF election logs; ESI Value does not match LACP System MAC encoding (high-order 6 octets); running-config missing `ethernet-segment` or `auto-discovery` stanzas.

44ce07092b112053.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) as EVPN PEs with iBGP session, enable BGP EVPN address family (AFI 25, SAFI 70 per RFC7432bis section 7). Configure one EVI (EVPN Instance) on each PE with matching Route Target extended community; attach one IXIA port to each PE.
Action: From IXIA port attached to DUT, send one ARP request (SRC_MAC=00:11:22:33:44:55, SRC_IP=10.1.1.10). DUT learns MAC and must advertise BGP EVPN Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) route per RFC7432bis section 7.2 encoding with mandatory fields: RD, Ethernet Tag ID, MAC length=48, MAC address, IP length=32, IP address, MPLS label.
Verify: On 3rd-party PE, execute "show bgp l2vpn evpn" or "show bgp evpn route-type 2". Confirm Type 2 route received with all RFC7432bis section 7.2 mandatory fields: RD, Ethernet Tag ID=0 (or EVI-assigned), 48-bit MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55), 32-bit IP (10.1.1.10), MPLS label, RT extended community. Verify route installs in PE's MAC-VRF forwarding table. Send return traffic from 3rd-party PE's IXIA port to learned MAC; confirm DUT receives traffic over EVPN MPLS tunnel with advertised label.
44ce07092b112053.expectation:
Pass:    3rd-party PE successfully parses and accepts Type 2 route with correct RFC7432bis section 7.2 encoding (RD, Ethernet Tag ID, MAC address, IP address, MPLS label all present and valid); route installs in MAC-VRF; return traffic forwarded via EVPN tunnel reaches DUT with correct MPLS label.
Fail-on: BGP EVPN session fails to establish (AFI 25/SAFI 70 not negotiated per RFC7432bis section 7), or DUT does not advertise Type 2 route after MAC learning, or route has malformed encoding (missing/incorrect mandatory fields per section 7.2), or 3rd-party PE rejects route with BGP error, or EVPN tunnel forwarding fails.

4518ac9e386473ec.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `advertise-mac enable` and iBGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled. IXIA BGP speaker peers with DUT, prepared to advertise Type-3 IMET routes.
Action: IXIA injects BGP UPDATEs containing EVPN Type-3 (IMET) routes with malformed PMSI Tunnel attributes: (a) invalid Tunnel Type = 255 (reserved, not 6), (b) truncated attribute (length field = 10 but payload < 10 bytes), (c) Flag = 0x80 (reserved bit set, not 0), (d) MPLS Label > 2^20 (invalid encoding). Each variant sent in separate UPDATE.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route type imet` to confirm malformed routes NOT installed in RIB; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms BGP session remains Established; `show logging | include PMSI` captures attribute parsing errors; IXIA confirms no BGP NOTIFICATION received (treat-as-withdraw per RFC4271).
4518ac9e386473ec.expectation:
Pass:    All four malformed IMET routes discarded (absent from `show bgp l2vpn evpn route type imet`); BGP session stays Established; error log contains "PMSI Tunnel attribute invalid" or similar for each variant; no DUT crash or memory leak after 100 malformed route cycles.
Fail-on: Any malformed route installed in RIB; BGP session reset/flaps; missing error log entry for any variant; DUT crash/restart; or malformed PMSI attribute re-advertised to route-reflector or other iBGP peer.

4555262bb95b3c8f.action_steps:
Problem: Verify that a single-homing PE correctly processes multihoming procedures (Ethernet A-D route installation, ESI label application, split-horizon filtering) when peering with a 3rd-party multihoming PE, as mandated by SFS§2.7.9 which strengthens RFC7432bis §8.7 from SHOULD to MUST.
Method: Build BGP EVPN peering between DUT (single-homed, no ethernet-segment configured) and 3rd-party multihoming PE advertising Ethernet A-D routes with ESI labels, drive BUM and unicast traffic via IXIA, capture tech-support bundle, and verify it contains evidence of mandatory multihoming procedure processing.
Setup: DUT configured with EVI on single-homed interfaces (no `ethernet-segment` under `interface agg-eth`); 3rd-party PE configured as multihoming PE advertising Type 1 (per-ES) and Type 4 (per-EVI) Ethernet A-D routes with ESI labels. BGP EVPN session established with Layer 2 Attributes (RFC7432bis §7.11.2) and Non-Revertive (RFC9785 §4.3) capabilities negotiated.
Action: IXIA sends BUM and unicast traffic through the topology. Verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn` displays received Ethernet A-D routes with ESI label values from multihoming peer. Capture tech-support bundle during active traffic.
Verify: Bundle contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output showing Type 1 (per-ES) and Type 4 (per-EVI) Ethernet A-D routes with ESI label values; `show bgp neighbor` output showing Layer 2 Attributes and Non-Revertive capabilities in negotiated capability list; forwarding tables showing ESI label application for split-horizon filtering; running-config and any syslog/alarm entries from the test run.
4555262bb95b3c8f.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle demonstrates DUT (single-homed PE) installed Ethernet A-D routes from multihoming peer, applied ESI labels for split-horizon filtering, and negotiated required BGP capabilities, confirming MUST compliance per SFS§2.7.9 (not RFC7432bis §8.7 optional SHOULD).
Fail-on: Bundle lacks Ethernet A-D route entries in BGP table, ESI label state absent from forwarding tables, or capabilities missing, indicating multihoming procedures were ignored and device reverted to RFC7432bis §8.7 SHOULD behavior where single-homing PEs may omit these procedures.

4583b641c42f5186.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT (PE1) and neighbor PE (PE2) with identical ESI (e.g., ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on interfaces toward multi-homed CE; establish EVPN instance and BGP EVPN peering between both PEs; IXIA emulates CE with LAG to both PEs.
Action: IXIA sends 1000 Ethernet frames from CE via PE1; PE1 encapsulates with EVPN MPLS label stack including ESI label and forwards to PE2.
Verify: On PE2, run `show evpn ethernet-segment` to confirm ESI operational; capture egress interface toward shared ES — confirm zero frames forwarded back to that ES; verify PE2 forwards traffic only to remote destinations (non-ES interfaces).
4583b641c42f5186.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 receives frames with ESI label matching local ESI and drops them (split horizon applied); zero frames egress PE2 interface toward shared ES; `show l2-services evpn forwarding-table` shows no looped MACs.
Fail-on: PE2 forwards one or more frames back to the shared ES interface (loop detected); packet capture shows frames egressing ES-facing port; MAC forwarding table shows duplicate entries indicating loop.

45bcc6b8743b2f12.action_steps:
Problem: Ensure that tech-support captures all critical EVPN ingress-replication state (IMET routes, PMSI attributes, BUM replication lists) required to diagnose forwarding failures in a multi-PE topology.
Method: Execute tech-support on PE1 in an active 3-PE ingress-replication topology with BUM traffic, extract the bundle, and verify it contains BGP EVPN Type 3 routes, PMSI tunnel attributes, replication target lists, MAC tables, and kernel forwarding state.
Setup: Three-PE EVPN topology (PE1, PE2, PE3) with ingress-replication tunnel type configured; BUM traffic source on access interface at PE1; all PEs advertising Route Type 3 IMET routes; BUM traffic actively forwarded via ingress-replication.
Action: Run `tech-support` command on PE1; save the diagnostic bundle off-box; extract and inspect bundle contents for EVPN-specific evidence.
Verify: Bundle contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output (Type 3 IMET routes), `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` (PMSI Tunnel attributes), `show evpn mac address-table`, `show configuration l2-services evpn`, ingress-replication state, BUM replication lists, kernel forwarding tables for the EVI, and any alarms/syslog entries from the test period.
45bcc6b8743b2f12.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains: Route Type 3 IMET routes with PMSI Tunnel attributes, ingress-replication state showing PE2 and PE3 as replication targets, BUM forwarding tables, EVPN MAC address table with learned entries, running config for l2-services/evpn with advertise-mac and unknown-mac-flooding settings, and logs from the test period.
Fail-on: Missing any of: Type 3 IMET routes, PMSI Tunnel attribute details, ingress-replication target list, BUM replication state, EVPN MAC table, or l2-services/evpn running configuration.

45c103f5846296fc.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology with iBGP peering; configure EVPN instance `evpn-test` on both routers; on DUT navigate to `configuration / routing / bgp / neighbor <peer-ip>`, enter `af-l2vpn evpn` submode; create BGP peer-group `rr-clients` at global BGP level for group command testing.
Action: In af-l2vpn evpn submode, configure each BGP common command: `route-reflector-client`, `maximum-prefix 100 80` (limit 100, warning-threshold 80%), `weight 200`, `allow-as-in 2`, `inbound-soft-reconfiguration`, `group rr-clients`, `private-as remove`. On neighbor PE, advertise 50 EVPN type-2 MAC/IP routes initially, then scale to 105 routes. Execute `show running-config routing bgp neighbor <peer-ip>`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <peer-ip> detail`.
Verify: All commands accepted without CLI error; `show running-config` displays commands under `neighbor <peer-ip> / af-l2vpn evpn` stanza; `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <peer-ip> detail` reports "Route-Reflector Client: Yes", "Inbound soft reconfiguration: Enabled", "Maximum prefixes allowed: 100 (threshold 80%)"; routes from this neighbor show "weight 200" in `show bgp l2vpn evpn <route> detail`; syslog warning logged at 80 prefixes; BGP session reset or new routes rejected when 100 exceeded.
45c103f5846296fc.expectation:
Pass:    All eight commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) accepted in af-l2vpn evpn neighbor context; configuration persists in running-config under correct neighbor stanza; route-reflector-client status visible in `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors` detail; maximum-prefix enforces limit with warning at 80 routes and action (session reset or route rejection) at 100; weight 200 applied to all routes from neighbor (visible in route detail output); inbound-soft-reconfiguration stores pre-policy routes (verifiable via `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <peer-ip> received-routes`).
Fail-on: Any command rejected with syntax error or "invalid in this context"; command accepted but absent from `show running-config routing bgp`; neighbor detail shows "Route-Reflector Client: No" after explicit configuration; 105 routes accepted without maximum-prefix enforcement; routes display default weight 0 instead of configured 200; `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <peer-ip> received-routes` returns error despite inbound-soft-reconfiguration enabled.

45eeb98ffb02fef3.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT bare; NETCONF client authenticated; IXIA port connected to DUT x-eth 0/0/1.
Action: NETCONF <edit-config> creates EVPN instance 'evpn40' with service-type vlan-bundle, bundle VLANs 100,200 on x-eth 0/0/1; IXIA transmits 1000 tagged frames VLAN 100, 1000 tagged frames VLAN 300.
Verify: NETCONF <get-config> returns service-type vlan-bundle with VLANs 100,200; CLI 'show evpn evpn40' confirms vlan-bundle operational; CLI 'show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics' shows VLAN 100 frames accepted, VLAN 300 frames in ingress-discard counter.
45eeb98ffb02fef3.equipment: DUT + NETCONF client + IXIA traffic gen
45eeb98ffb02fef3.expectation:
Pass:    NETCONF config persists across get-config; VLAN 100 Rx count = 1000; VLAN 300 ingress-discard count = 1000, Rx count = 0; CLI/NETCONF views match.
Fail-on: VLAN 300 frames forwarded (Rx > 0); NETCONF schema rejection; ingress-discard counter does not increment for VLAN 300; CLI shows vlan-bundle but NETCONF get-config does not.

46c6bd5a79f3ac75.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle on x-eth 0/0/1 for VLAN-IDs 1-3; start IXIA broadcast traffic with VLAN tags 2 (in-bundle), 10, 100 (out-of-bundle) at 1000 fps for 60 seconds.
Action: Collect tech-support after traffic completes; capture must include show evpn evpn1, show l2-services evpn evpn1, show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics, show logging, running-config, and kernel FIB/forwarding tables for VLAN filtering.
Verify: Extract tech-support archive; confirm presence of all listed show commands, interface drop counters for VLAN 10 and 100, accept/forward counters for VLAN 2, running-config section `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1` with service-type vlan-aware-bundle and VLAN-IDs 1-3.
46c6bd5a79f3ac75.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains evpn1 config (service-type vlan-aware-bundle, VLAN-IDs 1-3), x-eth 0/0/1 statistics showing ≥60,000 drops for VLAN 10 frames and ≥60,000 drops for VLAN 100 frames, ≥60,000 accepted frames for VLAN 2, all standard show outputs and logs present.
Fail-on: Missing any required show command, missing interface drop statistics, drop counters are zero or <60,000 for out-of-bundle VLANs 10/100 (indicating BUM frames were not dropped per MUST requirement), or running-config missing evpn1 VLAN-ID range.

46ff55ecee514919.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance under `configuration / l2-services / evpn` with `advertise-mac enable`, `control-word enable`. Establish VXLAN tunnel to neighbor PE; install known MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 via EVPN RT-2 route pointing to remote PE VNI 5000.
Action: IXIA injects 5 unicast packet variants destined to 00:11:22:33:44:55: (a) VXLAN header with reserved bits 4-7 set (non-zero), (b) control-word with first nibble ≠ 0000, (c) VNI field = 9999 (mismatch), (d) inner Ethernet frame with corrupted FCS, (e) inner frame 9200 bytes exceeding MTU.
Verify: Check `show l2-services evpn statistics` for ingress-discard / malformed-packet counters; `show log` filtered for EVPN error events; capture at neighbor PE confirms zero malformed packets received; ping from valid source to 00:11:22:33:44:55 still succeeds (FDB intact).
46ff55ecee514919.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + neighbor PE
46ff55ecee514919.expectation:
Pass:    Ingress-discard counter increments by ≥5 (one per variant); syslog contains 5 malformed-packet error entries (VXLAN-reserved-bits, control-word-invalid, VNI-mismatch, FCS-error, MTU-exceeded); neighbor PE capture shows 0 malformed packets; DUT remains stable (no reload); legitimate ping succeeds.
Fail-on: Any malformed packet forwarded to neighbor PE; DUT crash or process restart; syslog missing any of the 5 error types; FDB entry for 00:11:22:33:44:55 deleted or corrupted; legitimate unicast traffic (ping) fails post-injection.

47550fec673e9eb9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and PE1 with identical ESI on `agg-eth 0`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`; configure P2MP LSPs for BUM replication; configure PE2 (remote) without the ESI; establish BGP EVPN peering; verify non-DF PE advertises A-D per ES route with upstream-assigned ESI label.
Action: From PE2 (remote), send BUM traffic via P2MP LSP encapsulated with the upstream-assigned ESI label learned from PE1's A-D per ES route; monitor packet flow at the DUT.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` on DUT confirms import of A-D per ES route with upstream ESI label; `show mpls forwarding-table label <value>` shows label programmed in PE1's context; packet capture on DUT's `agg-eth 0` shows zero egress frames for BUM received with this label.
47550fec673e9eb9.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (same ESI all-active) + remote PE + IXIA + P2MP MPLS LSP support
47550fec673e9eb9.expectation:
Pass:    DUT programs upstream-assigned ESI label from PE1 per RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2; BUM received with this label is NOT forwarded onto `agg-eth 0` (split-horizon enforced); interface egress counter = 0 for this flow.
Fail-on: Label not programmed, or BUM forwarded onto `agg-eth 0` (split-horizon violation), or local CE receives duplicate frames from both local DF and P2MP path.

478798b75d4d1e6b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn <evpn-name> service-type vlan-based` under `configuration / l2-services`. Configure aggregate interface `agg-eth 0` and enable `ethernet-segment` under `configuration / interface / agg-eth`. Commit.
Action: Execute `show evpn ethernet-segments` and record structured output. Run `show evpn ethernet-segments | include agg-eth` to filter for aggregate interfaces. Run `show evpn ethernet-segments | count` to verify row count.
Verify: Unfiltered output displays configured Ethernet Segment for `agg-eth 0`. Pipe `| include agg-eth` returns only lines containing 'agg-eth'. `| count` returns integer row count or clear unsupported message.
478798b75d4d1e6b.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
478798b75d4d1e6b.expectation:
Pass:    Command returns structured output showing Ethernet Segment(s) with interface names and ES operational data. Pipe filters behave per documented semantics.
Fail-on: Command fails, returns empty when ES configured, omits `agg-eth 0`, or pipe filter returns wrong subset / breaks output structure.

479978f56c1f6977.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `interface agg-eth 0` and enter its `ethernet-segment` sub-mode. Issue the `identifier` command with a non-default type and value per the CLI reference (document states default is type 4). Commit and verify the line appears in `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`.
Action: Save the running configuration to startup-config (`write memory`), then reload the DUT and wait for the system to reach operational state.
Verify: Re-examine `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` and confirm the `ethernet-segment` stanza contains the exact `identifier` configuration line; EVPN services using this ethernet-segment become operational without manual re-entry of commands.
479978f56c1f6977.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
479978f56c1f6977.expectation:
Pass:    The non-default `identifier` configuration persists byte-for-byte across reload; EVPN services using this ethernet-segment auto-resume to Up operational state.
Fail-on: Post-reload, the `identifier` is absent, has reverted to the type 4 default, appears modified, or EVPN services fail to automatically resume.

479fbe37cee6c796.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT and neighbor PE with `configuration / l2-services / evpn` (advertise-mac enable). Establish iBGP EVPN session using `af-l2vpn evpn` between peers. Attach IXIA to DUT AC interface, learn one MAC address into DUT's MACT.
Action: Verify DUT advertises MAC/IP route (Type 2) with LT1 label to neighbor PE using `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and `show evpn mac`. Send known-unicast frame from neighbor PE side to learned MAC; packet-capture on core link to confirm LT1 label is pushed (RFC7432bis §8.4).
Verify: Parse BGP Type-2 route for MPLS Label field = LT1 (RFC7432 §7.2 NLRI encoding); packet capture shows outer label = LT1; DUT forwards frame into correct MACT (MAC destination reachable); counters in `show evpn statistics` increment for label-switched known-unicast.
479fbe37cee6c796.expectation:
Pass:    DUT allocates unique LT1 per MACT, advertises it in MAC/IP route NLRI Label field; ingress PE pushes LT1 when forwarding known unicast to DUT; DUT uses LT1 to select correct MACT and delivers frame.
Fail-on: MAC/IP route carries label 0 (implicit null) or no label; packet capture shows no MPLS label or wrong label value; traffic dropped or forwarded to wrong MACT; `show evpn mac` lists MAC but BGP route missing LT1.

47b84ed1782fc5f3.action_steps:
Setup:  DCB labels exercised via P-multicast tree aggregation (≥2 BDs on ingress PE) or MP2MP tunnels with ESI labels allocated from DCB; at least one Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag route with PMSI Tunnel Attribute carrying DCB label.
Action: Collect tech-support output including: show l2-services evpn pmsi-tunnel, show l2-services evpn esi-label, show routing bgp evpn inclusive-multicast, show l2-services evpn forwarding-table, running-config for BDs using DCB aggregation.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: PMSI Tunnel Attribute label allocation method (DCB vs. upstream), ESI label range confirmation (all PEs on same ES use identical label from DCB block), P-multicast aggregation membership (which BDs share the tree), syslog entries for DCB allocation events.
47b84ed1782fc5f3.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support shows DCB labels in PMSI Tunnel Attribute for aggregated P-multicast trees, ESI labels from DCB block with same value across all PEs on the ES, running-config confirms multi-BD aggregation, BGP route dumps show Inclusive Multicast routes with DCB label encoding.
Fail-on: PMSI Tunnel Attribute uses upstream-allocated labels instead of DCB, ESI labels differ across PEs on same Ethernet Segment, missing P-multicast aggregation state, or DCB label allocation mode not observable in show commands/logs.

486a5afae06fd7df.action_steps:
Setup:  Four PE routers in EVPN topology (PE1-PE4), each with IXIA port; configure EVI with ingress replication mode (no P2MP), `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on all PEs.
Action: IXIA at PE1 generates broadcast frames at incremental rates (100 fps → 10K fps); monitor ingress replication list on PE1 (show l2-services evpn replication-list), verify PE1 replicates each broadcast frame to PE2, PE3, PE4 via individual unicast VXLAN tunnels.
Verify: IXIA ports at PE2/PE3/PE4 capture all broadcast frames; measure per-PE replication latency (PE1 Tx timestamp to PEx Rx); check `show l2-services evpn statistics` for BUM replication counters and verify zero drops across all remote PEs.
486a5afae06fd7df.equipment: Four PE routers (DUT as PE1 + 3 neighbor PEs as PE2/PE3/PE4) + IXIA scale rig with 4 ports (one per PE) for Tx/Rx verification and latency measurement
486a5afae06fd7df.expectation:
Pass:    100% of broadcast packets received at all 3 remote PEs (PE2, PE3, PE4) with zero loss; per-PE replication latency ≤ 50 ms at 10K fps sustained load; ingress replication counter increments match IXIA Tx count × 3 PEs.
Fail-on: Any broadcast packet missing at any remote PE (< 100% delivery); packet loss > 0%; any PE excluded from replication list; latency > 50 ms; replication count mismatch indicating dropped replicas.

48ff84d2b9fb2801.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure one Ethernet segment (`ethernet-segment` under `agg-eth 0`) shared by two EVPN instances (VPLS 100 and VPLS 200), each with distinct RTs configured via `import-rt` and `export-rt` in `auto-discovery` mode; establish BGP EVPN peering with a neighbor PE via `af-l2vpn evpn`.
Action: Activate the Ethernet segment (no shutdown on `agg-eth 0`), verify Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) advertisement, then collect tech-support bundle.
Verify: Inspect `show bgp evpn routes`, `show running-config`, and captured BGP UPDATE messages in tech-support output.
48ff84d2b9fb2801.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (to observe advertised Ethernet A-D routes with RT communities in BGP updates)
48ff84d2b9fb2801.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains Ethernet A-D per ES routes (type 1) with RT extended communities listing the union of all RTs from VPLS 100 and VPLS 200; running-config shows ES-to-EVI mappings and per-EVI RT assignments; BGP route dumps include the RT path attributes per RFC7432bis §8.2.1.1.
Fail-on: Ethernet A-D per ES routes missing RT communities, RT set incomplete (missing an EVI's RT), or tech-support lacks BGP EVPN route details / running-config sections for the ES and VPLS instances.

49020352eafc196c.action_steps:
Setup: Two-PE topology (PE1, PE2) over MPLS core with LDP; BGP IPv4 unicast session established between PE loopbacks; CE1 single-homed to PE1 on agg-eth 0.
Action: On PE1: configure `evpn evi-1 service-type vlan-based` with RD 65000:1; enable `auto-discovery`; set `import-rt 65000:1` and `export-rt 65000:1`; bind agg-eth 0 to evi-1; commit. Under BGP neighbor PE2 configuration, enter `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family context and configure all eight BGP common commands per REQ#20: allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group. On PE2: configure matching EVPN instance. From CE1, send traffic with 100 distinct source MAC addresses (1000 frames per MAC).
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` displays EVPN Type-2 MAC/IP routes exchanged between PE1 and PE2; `show bgp neighbors` output confirms all eight BGP common commands are active in the af-l2vpn evpn context for PE2 neighbor; `show evpn mac evi evi-1` reports ≥100 local MAC entries on PE1 and ≥100 remote MAC entries learned from PE2; `show evpn evi` shows evi-1 operational; `show running-config routing bgp` persists all configured BGP commands.
49020352eafc196c.expectation:
Pass: All eight BGP common commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) successfully configured and accepted in af-l2vpn evpn neighbor context; EVPN Type-2 routes exchanged in both directions; local MAC learning achieves ≥100 entries; remote MAC learning achieves ≥100 entries; all commands persist in running-config; BGP session remains stable throughout test.
Fail-on: Any of the eight BGP common commands rejected or generate error when configured in af-l2vpn evpn context; EVPN Type-2 routes not advertised or received; local MAC count <100 or remote MAC count <100; configured commands absent from running-config after commit; BGP session flaps or goes down during configuration or traffic.

49e5d4541863c65f.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: multi-home CE1 to PE2 and PE3 with ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, Eth-Tag 0; DUT peers to both; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on all neighbors.
Action: PE2 advertises type-1 (A-D per EVI) WITH EVPN Layer 2 Attributes EC and P=0; PE3 advertises type-1 WITHOUT L2 Attr EC but better AS-PATH; capture `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 detail` for selected path.
Verify: Criterion 1.1 applied first — DUT discards PE3 route (no L2 Attr EC) even though AS-PATH is shorter; best-path = PE2 route; then inject second route from PE2 with P=1 (aliasing) and verify DUT updates forwarding per criterion 1.2 (P-flag triggers load-balance per RFC8214 §9.2).
49e5d4541863c65f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects PE2 route (has L2 Attr EC) over PE3 (no EC, better AS-PATH) — proving criterion 1.1 precedes standard BGP tie-break; when P=1 route received, aliasing behavior enabled (traffic load-balances across PEs for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11).
Fail-on: DUT selects PE3 route (criterion ordering violated); or ignores P-flag after L2 Attr EC filtering; or applies standard BGP best-path before removing non-EC routes.

4a04a3301611690c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with AS 65000, create EVPN instance 'EVPN-100' service-type vlan-based for VLAN 100, enable BGP neighbor with af-l2vpn evpn (do NOT manually configure export-rt/import-rt to trigger auto-derivation). Configure neighbor PE with matching import-rt 65000:100.
Action: Commit config, enable advertise-mac on EVPN-100, capture BGP UPDATE on neighbor PE for route-type 2 (MAC/IP) or route-type 3 (IMET) carrying the auto-derived RT.
Verify: Decode Extended Community RT in captured UPDATE: Global Administrator = 65000 (2-octet AS format) and Local Administrator bits [0:11] = 100 (0x064), bits [12:31] = 0x00000. Run 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route' on neighbor PE and confirm route imported with RT 65000:100.
4a04a3301611690c.expectation:
Pass:    RT Extended Community format is <AS>:<VLAN> where AS=65000 and VLAN=100, encoded per RFC4360 (2-octet AS: Global Admin = 65000, Local Admin = 0x00000064). Neighbor PE imports the route using auto-derived RT 65000:100.
Fail-on: RT is 0:0, RT Global Admin ≠ 65000, RT Local Admin ≠ 100, or upper 20 bits of Local Admin are non-zero. Route rejected at neighbor PE due to RT mismatch.

4a495bb54e3eb781.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that tech-support captures sufficient diagnostic state to troubleshoot aliasing failures when traffic must redirect to a backup PE after a multi-homed link failure.
Method: Configure multi-homed ES topology, trigger link failure, collect tech-support bundle, and verify it contains ES state, A-D per ES route advertisements, and aliased next-hop forwarding entries.
Setup: Multi-homed CE attached via agg-eth 0 to PE1 and PE2, both configured with `ethernet-segment` and identical `identifier 0 <type0-value>` under the interface. Remote PE3 advertises MAC/IP routes for destinations behind the CE. Both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes per RFC7432bis §8.4.
Action: Shut down PE1's agg-eth 0 interface. Remote PE3 traffic to the CE should immediately alias to PE2 without waiting for MAC re-learning. Run the tech-support command, save the bundle, and inspect contents.
Verify: Bundle contains `show evpn ethernet-segment` output showing both PEs' ES state and DF election, `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output showing A-D per ES route advertisements from PE1 and PE2, `show evpn mac address-table` entries, running-config with the ES configuration, syslog entries for the agg-eth 0 link-down event, and kernel forwarding tables showing aliased next-hop entries pointing to PE2.
4a495bb54e3eb781.expectation:
Pass: Tech-support bundle includes complete ES state (ESI value, load-balancing mode, DF role), A-D per ES route advertisements from both PEs, MAC table entries with remote PE next-hops, link-down event in syslog, and aliased forwarding entries that enabled immediate traffic redirect to PE2 per RFC7432bis §8.4.
Fail-on: Missing ES configuration or state, missing A-D per ES routes, missing failover event logs, or missing aliased next-hop forwarding entries.

4a90c77c0446c3e9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 PE routers in EVI 100; each advertises IMET route (type 3) for BD 10 with distinct BGP attributes (PE1: MED=50, PE2: MED=100 + AS_PATH prepended, PE3: higher IGP metric); DUT = PE1; set `advertise-mac enable`, `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on all; IXIA scale rig generates BUM traffic + route churn.
Action: IXIA injects 10,000 IMET route updates/min across 500 EVIs while sending 1 Gbps BUM traffic; every 60 sec modify MED/AS_PATH on neighbor PEs to trigger best path recalculation (e.g., change PE2 MED to 25, forcing path switch); measure convergence time from BGP UPDATE receipt to FIB update.
Verify: Run `show l2-services evpn routes type 3` and `show router bgp routes evpn inclusive` to confirm selected path matches RFC7432bis §7.13.1 step 5 criteria order (LOCAL_PREF > AS_PATH > MED > IGP metric > router-ID); capture convergence time per path change event; log CPU % during updates; verify BUM traffic forwarding via correct IMET next-hop.
4a90c77c0446c3e9.expectation:
Pass:    Best path recalculation + FIB update completes in < 1 second per event; selected path always reflects correct criteria precedence (e.g., lower MED wins only if LOCAL_PREF and AS_PATH equal); CPU utilization < 40% during 10K updates/min; BUM traffic loss < 0.1% during convergence windows.
Fail-on: Convergence > 1 sec; wrong path selected (criteria order violated — e.g., MED chosen over shorter AS_PATH); CPU > 80%; traffic loss > 1%; any best path oscillation or route flapping detected in logs.

4a9d526b77d04cca.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as EVPN PE with VPLS instance; configure 'af-l2vpn evpn' under BGP neighbor, set 'import-rt' and 'export-rt' matching neighbor PE. Establish BGP EVPN session to test neighbor PE capable of injecting Type-1 (Ethernet A-D Per EVI) routes with controlled Extended Community attributes.
Action: From neighbor PE, advertise Ethernet A-D Per EVI route (NLRI: RD + ESI + Ethernet Tag) WITHOUT L2-Attr Extended Community (Type 0x800A); capture DUT's route processing. Then advertise same route WITH L2-Attr Extended Community; capture again.
Verify: Use 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1' and packet capture to confirm: (a) route without L2-Attr ExtComm triggers RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 aliasing (ESI-based next-hop resolution only), (b) route with L2-Attr ExtComm triggers RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 (DUT extracts Control Flags, L2 MTU, and applies enhanced aliasing). Check 'show l2-services evpn' for correct ESI state and DF election reflecting the applied RFC version.
4a9d526b77d04cca.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (capable of BGP EVPN Type-1 injection with programmable Extended Communities) + IXIA for traffic validation
4a9d526b77d04cca.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts both route variants. Route without L2-Attr ExtComm → aliasing per RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 (ESI next-hop set only).
