Action: Start packet capture on PE1↔PE2 BGP session; bring up ES on both PEs; capture BGP UPDATE containing Ethernet Segment route (type 4); decode DF Election Extended Community (Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x06 per RFC8584) in the route.
Verify: Extract octets 6-7 (Preference field per RFC9785 §3) from the DF Election EC in each PE's ES route; confirm PE1 sends 0x7FFF (32767), PE2 sends 0xC350 (50000).
564227de8f22be5a.expectation:
Pass:    PE1's ES route carries DF Alg=0x03 (Highest-Preference) with Preference=0x7FFF (32767 decimal) in EC octets 6-7 when preference not configured; PE2's ES route shows Preference=0xC350 (50000 decimal) matching the configured value; both decode to valid uint16 in range 0–65535.
Fail-on: PE1's default preference ≠ 32767; PE2's preference ≠ 50000; preference field is 0x0000 or outside 0–65535 range; or DF Election EC missing/malformed when algorithm is Highest-Preference.

565731b04abde9d3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with ingress-replication P-tunnel; DUT is designated forwarder for Ethernet Segment in single-active mode (default load-balancing-mode); IXIA sends unknown unicast frames (dest MAC not in DUT MAC table) at 100 Mbps toward the ES.
Action: While IXIA traffic is running, restart the DUT's EVPN control-plane process (platform-specific reset command); monitor BUM packet forwarding during and after process recovery.
Verify: Capture MPLS-encapsulated BUM traffic egressing to remote PE; confirm no MAC table lookup is performed (default behavior per RFC7432bis §13.2.1); measure time from process restart to BUM forwarding restoration; check split-horizon (no looped frames).
565731b04abde9d3.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
565731b04abde9d3.expectation:
Pass:    BUM forwarding resumes within es-waiting-time (3 s default) + control-plane convergence; unknown unicast frames are flooded to remote PE via ingress-replication without destination MAC lookup; packet loss ≤ 1% over 10 s measurement window post-recovery.
Fail-on: BUM traffic not restored after 5 s, PE performs MAC lookup (violates default), packet loss > 5%, permanent blackhole, or manual config changes required to restore forwarding.

5682fc613d72c549.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with identical ES (interface agg-eth ethernet-segment identifier 0 AA.BB.CC.DD.EE.FF.00.00.01, load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving highest-random-weight); attach both to same EVI; capture BGP Ethernet A-D per EVI routes from both PEs showing L2-Attr extended community with P bit set.
Action: Reset DUT EVPN control-plane (clear bgp evpn * or restart evpn process) while IXIA sends bidirectional traffic; monitor DF re-election and route re-advertisement.
Verify: Capture post-recovery Ethernet A-D per EVI routes from both DUT and neighbor PE; decode L2-Attr extended community P bit (must be 1 for All-Active); verify B bit reflects elected DF role; measure traffic convergence time.
5682fc613d72c549.expectation:
Pass:    Both DUT and neighbor PE advertise L2-Attr extended community with P bit=1 in all Ethernet A-D per EVI routes after recovery; DF election completes within es-waiting-time (default 3 s); traffic convergence ≤ 5 s.
Fail-on: Any PE in the All-Active redundancy group advertises P bit=0, missing L2-Attr extended community in post-recovery routes, DF state ambiguous (both or neither claim Primary), or traffic outage > 5 s.

56dbe8488d3a24de.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based`, map to VID 100 on AC interface, set `import-rt/export-rt 65000:100`. IXIA emulates peer PE, establishes BGP EVPN session.
Action: IXIA sends (1) MAC/IP Advertisement routes with Ethernet Tag ID=100 (RFC7432bis §6.1 mandates 0); (2) IMET routes with Tag ID=0xFFFF; (3) data frames with VID=0 (priority-tagged), VID=4095 (reserved), VID=5000 (exceeds valid range 1–4094), and corrupted 802.1Q headers (invalid TPID 0x0000, truncated tag).
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-table evpn-100` confirms zero MAC entries from malformed routes. `show router bgp routes evpn` shows rejected routes. `show l2-services evpn evpn-100 statistics errors` increments discard counters. Syslog contains "Ethernet Tag ID must be 0 for VLAN-based service" and "invalid VID" messages. DUT control-plane remains responsive.
56dbe8488d3a24de.expectation:
Pass:    All routes with Ethernet Tag ID ≠ 0 rejected (zero MAC entries installed from IXIA peer). All frames with invalid VID (0, 4095, out-of-range) or corrupted 802.1Q tag dropped at ingress (frame discard counter >0). Syslog entries logged. No DUT crash or control-plane hang.
Fail-on: Routes with Tag ID ≠ 0 installed in MAC-VRF table; malformed frames forwarded to local AC or MPLS core; DUT crash/reboot; error counters stuck at 0; no syslog messages; BGP session flaps.

57265a8846c1d2ec.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 MAC-VRFs (evpn evpn-100, evpn-200, evpn-300) on DUT with Type 1 RDs 10.0.0.1:100, 10.0.0.1:200, 10.0.0.1:300; configure matching MAC-VRFs on Cisco/Juniper PE with RDs 10.0.0.2:100, 10.0.0.2:200, 10.0.0.2:300; establish BGP EVPN session (af-l2vpn evpn) and set advertise-mac enable.
Action: Advertise MAC/IP routes (EVPN Type 2) from each MAC-VRF by attaching hosts or injecting routes; capture BGP UPDATE messages showing NLRI with RD field; verify show bgp evpn route output on both PEs.
Verify: On DUT: show bgp evpn route | grep RD confirms each MAC-VRF's routes carry its assigned RD; on 3rd-party PE: show bgp l2vpn evpn (Cisco) or show route table bgp.evpn.0 (Juniper) displays routes with DUT's RDs 10.0.0.1:100/200/300; confirm no RD collision across MAC-VRFs; send bidirectional L2 traffic via IXIA and verify FIB lookup uses correct RD+MAC.
57265a8846c1d2ec.expectation:
Pass:    All Type 2 routes from DUT MAC-VRF evpn-100 carry RD 10.0.0.1:100, evpn-200 → 10.0.0.1:200, evpn-300 → 10.0.0.1:300; 3rd-party PE installs routes in per-VRF RIB with no RD conflict errors; packet capture shows RD in BGP NLRI matches source MAC-VRF; bidirectional L2 forwarding succeeds at line rate.
Fail-on: Two MAC-VRFs advertise routes with same RD; 3rd-party PE logs RD collision or rejects routes; BGP UPDATE missing RD field or carries RD ≠ configured MAC-VRF RD; data plane black-holes due to ambiguous RD.

5745325b10b8b8fc.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Bundle-Ether10 as an L2 interface: enter `interface Bundle-Ether10`, then `l2-transport` to enable L2 mode; verify via `show running-config interface Bundle-Ether10` that l2-transport is present and no VPLS/bridge-domain/L2VPN services are bound.
Action: From configuration mode, enter `interface Bundle-Ether10`, then issue `ethernet-segment` to enter ES configuration sub-mode; configure an ESI using Type-0 (operator-configured, e.g., `identifier type 0 0011.2233.4455.6677.8899`); exit and `commit`.
Verify: Commit succeeds; `show running-config interface Bundle-Ether10` displays the `ethernet-segment` configuration block with the Type-0 ESI; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` lists Bundle-Ether10 with the configured ESI and operational state Up.
5745325b10b8b8fc.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
5745325b10b8b8fc.expectation:
Pass:    Commit completes successfully; running-config contains `ethernet-segment` block under Bundle-Ether10 with ESI Type-0 identifier 0011.2233.4455.6677.8899; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows Bundle-Ether10 with matching ESI, status Up, and multi-homing mode active.
Fail-on: Commit rejected with error; `ethernet-segment` block missing or incomplete in running-config; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` does not list Bundle-Ether10, ESI is absent/malformed, or operational state is Down/NotConfigured.

575fd2397eb2d891.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN instance configured (service-type vlan-based or vlan-bundle), two PEs in same EVI via BGP EVPN auto-discovery; IXIA connected to DUT CE-facing port; MAC forwarding table empty for test DA.
Action: Configure `unknow-mac-flooding disable` under configuration / l2-services / evpn, commit; send frames with unknown MAC DA from IXIA; capture on PE2 uplink (expect zero rx). Reconfigure `unknow-mac-flooding enable`, commit, resend traffic (expect flood to PE2). Issue `no unknow-mac-flooding`, commit, verify default (enable) restored and flooding resumes.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `unknow-mac-flooding enable | disable` persists under l2-services / evpn; packet capture on PE2 uplink shows zero frames when disabled, non-zero when enabled; `no` form removes explicit line, flooding behavior reverts to enable.
575fd2397eb2d891.expectation:
Pass:    `disable` → unknown MAC frames not forwarded to remote PE (PE2 capture shows 0 rx); `enable` → frames flooded to all PEs in EVI (PE2 capture shows >0 rx); `no unknow-mac-flooding` → config line removed, default enable restored
Fail-on: Command parse error, commit rejected, or forwarding mismatch (disable set but PE2 receives frames, or enable set but PE2 receives zero frames)

57ce658fa9036767.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP AS 65001 with neighbor 10.1.1.2 (remote AS 65002) under `configuration / routing / bgp`; configure EVPN instance with `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based` under `configuration / l2-services`; verify BGP session reaches Established state with `show bgp summary`.
Action: Navigate to `configuration / routing / bgp / neighbor 10.1.1.2`, issue `af-l2vpn evpn`, commit configuration. Observe BGP session re-negotiates capabilities (visible in syslog or `show bgp neighbor` output). Then issue `no af-l2vpn evpn`, commit, and verify capability removal.
Verify: After enabling: `show bgp neighbor 10.1.1.2` displays L2VPN EVPN in negotiated capabilities (AFI/SAFI 25/70 or equivalent vendor-specific string); `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows neighbor with active EVPN session count > 0 if routes available. After disabling: L2VPN EVPN capability absent from `show bgp neighbor 10.1.1.2`, no EVPN routes from 10.1.1.2 remain in `show bgp l2vpn evpn`.
57ce658fa9036767.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (to establish BGP peering and verify L2VPN EVPN AFI/SAFI capability negotiation)
57ce658fa9036767.expectation:
Pass:    `af-l2vpn evpn` command accepted under neighbor context, BGP re-negotiates with L2VPN EVPN capability present in capabilities list, EVPN routes exchanged if neighbor advertises them. `no af-l2vpn evpn` cleanly removes address-family without disrupting IPv4/IPv6 unicast sessions, all EVPN routes from that neighbor withdrawn.
Fail-on: Command syntax error, BGP capabilities list does not include L2VPN EVPN after commit, EVPN routes not advertised/received despite capability negotiated, `no` form causes full BGP session reset (session state transitions to Idle), or address-family configuration remnants persist in running-config after `no` form.

58317d0927f43653.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT in `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment` mode with ESI configured (e.g., `identifier 0 <mac-address>`), no `service-carving` configured (default state).
Action: Issue `service-carving ?` to inspect help listing; TAB-complete by typing `serv` + TAB; after completion, type space and `?` to list parameters; configure `service-carving` per documented parameters (or standalone if no params); commit; capture `show running-config interface agg-eth 1`; issue `no service-carving`; commit; re-capture running-config.
Verify: `?` lists `service-carving` with description referencing Default Forwarder Election method; TAB completes uniquely or offers documented options; parameter help lists all CLI doc parameters (zero invented tokens); running-config shows configured service-carving under ethernet-segment; after `no service-carving`, config reverts to default (line absent or explicit default marker).
58317d0927f43653.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
58317d0927f43653.expectation:
Pass:    `service-carving` discoverable with DF-election context in help; TAB completion works; parameter help exactly matches CLI doc; config persists after commit; `no` form restores documented default (absence or default keyword).
Fail-on: Help absent or lacks DF context; TAB fails; parameter mismatch vs. CLI doc; config not persisted; `no` form does not revert to default state.

587fedbfa67c4fac.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN VPLS instance (EVI 100, RD 1:100, import-rt/export-rt 100:100) on DUT and 3rd-party PE with Ingress Replication enabled; configure `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor; verify `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (defaults); confirm BGP EVPN session up.
Action: Activate EVPN instance on DUT; capture BGP UPDATE messages exchanged with 3rd-party PE; trigger Type-3 IMET route advertisement; inject BUM traffic (broadcast ARP) from IXIA via both PEs.
Verify: Wireshark decode of DUT's Type-3 route: PMSI Tunnel attribute present with Tunnel Type=6 (Ingress Replication), Flags=0x00, MPLS Label=valid LT3 label (non-zero, not reserved range 0-15), Tunnel Identifier=DUT's loopback/originating IP per RFC6514 §5; on 3rd-party PE CLI: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3` confirms route accepted; IXIA captures BUM frames replicated bidirectionally.
587fedbfa67c4fac.expectation:
Pass:    DUT's Type-3 route carries PMSI attribute with Type=6, Flags=0x00, LT3 label in valid range (≥16), Tunnel ID equals DUT loopback IP; Cisco/Juniper PE accepts route (status: valid, best) and installs in EVPN RIB; BUM traffic replicates bidirectionally with 0% loss.
Fail-on: PMSI attribute absent, Tunnel Type ≠ 6, Flags ≠ 0x00, MPLS Label ≤ 15 or 0xFFFFF (reserved), Tunnel ID mismatch (≠ DUT loopback), 3rd-party PE rejects route (BGP notification code 3 subcode 9 'Optional Attribute Error'), or unidirectional BUM black-hole.

58addc8ffd2a82fc.action_steps:
Setup:  Multi-homed CE (IXIA) dual-homed to DUT (PE1) and neighbor PE2 via LAG; configure ethernet-segment with same ESI (e.g., Type 0) on both agg-eth interfaces; af-l2vpn evpn BGP session active; advertise-mac enabled; ≥10 MACs advertised in EVI; BGP EVPN capture running on PE1↔PE2 link.
Action: Issue 'shutdown' on DUT's agg-eth interface to trigger PE-CE link failure (or 'no evpn' under VPLS for EVI decommissioning).
Verify: Capture BGP withdrawals: Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES, route-type=1, RFC7432bis §7.1) with matching RD+ESI; all Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement, route-type=2) for MACs on failed ES; on PE2, 'show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI>' drops PE1's Originator-IP; PE2's MPLS table removes PE1's label for ES.
58addc8ffd2a82fc.expectation:
Pass:    Type 1 (A-D per ES) withdrawn ≤5 sec post link-down; all Type 2 (MAC/IP) withdrawn within MAC ageout timer (0–300 sec per RFC7432bis §17.3 SHOULD); PE2 removes PE1 from ECMP next-hop set; IXIA traffic converges to PE2.
Fail-on: Type 1 route persists >10 sec after shutdown; MAC/IP routes remain beyond MAC ageout timer; PE2 forwards to stale PE1 next-hop (IXIA: >1% loss in steady-state post-failover).

58b13e39d8bf3c63.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed ethernet-segment on DUT and neighbor PE: both share ESI (e.g., `identifier 0 <type0-value>`), system-mac, and connect to same CE via `agg-eth 0`; configure EVPN instance with `auto-discovery` enabled.
Action: On DUT, enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` mode; issue `service-carving` command; commit; verify DF election result via `show evpn ethernet-segment detail`; issue `no service-carving`; commit and re-verify.
Verify: After `service-carving`: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays the `service-carving` line; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows DF state (DF or non-DF) and election method in use. After `no service-carving`: config line removed, DF election reverts to default method (confirm via same show commands).
58b13e39d8bf3c63.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (both must support EVPN multi-homing to observe DF election state)
58b13e39d8bf3c63.expectation:
Pass:    `service-carving` command accepted; running-config persists the line; DF election completes (one PE is DF); `no service-carving` removes config and restores default DF election behavior
Fail-on: Command rejected with syntax error, `service-carving` line absent from running-config after commit, DF election does not occur, or `no` form fails to remove configuration

58bc5cfc2957696d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT and neighbor PE, enable `advertise-mac enable` (default under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`), establish BGP EVPN peering via `af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor config, attach CE interfaces, IXIA sends frames to populate DUT MACT with at least one MAC.
Action: Run `show bgp evpn route type 2` to confirm DUT advertises Type-2 routes with non-zero LT1 labels (RFC7432 §7.2). IXIA injects known-unicast traffic from neighbor PE to DUT targeting the advertised MAC; capture ingress packet to verify LT1 label in MPLS header (RFC7432bis §8.4). Execute `show tech-support` to collect diagnostic bundle.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: `show bgp evpn route type 2` output with LT1 label values, `show l2-services evpn` MACT entries, `show mpls forwarding-table` showing LT1 label → MACT bindings, `show running-config` confirming `advertise-mac enable`, syslog entries for LT1 label allocation/deallocation.
58bc5cfc2957696d.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains all of: Type-2 routes with non-zero LT1 label field, EVPN MACT state, MPLS forwarding table with LT1 label → MACT bindings, running-config showing `advertise-mac enable`, LT1-related syslog entries.
Fail-on: Any required output missing, LT1 label field is zero or absent in Type-2 routes, or MPLS forwarding table does not contain LT1 label entries for the MACT.

58dd671dc22bd04c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 4 PEs with 2 Ethernet Segments (ES-1 multi-homed to PE1+PE2, ES-2 multi-homed to PE3+PE4); enable MP2MP MPLS LSPs for BUM forwarding; configure EVPN with "unknown-mac-flooding enable" (mode: configuration/l2-services/evpn); verify ESI labels from DCB (show evpn ethernet-segment detail) and identical per ES; IXIA scale rig injects 1 Gbps aggregate BUM traffic (broadcast + unknown unicast, 10k MAC flows).
Action: IXIA sends BUM traffic from CEs attached to ES-1 and ES-2 for 300 seconds; measure per-flow throughput, latency (min/avg/p99), packet loss; capture show mpls forwarding-table, show evpn mac-table, show system cpu/memory every 30 seconds.
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment detail confirms ES-1 ESI label identical on PE1 and PE2, ES-2 ESI label identical on PE3 and PE4, both from DCB range; IXIA reports throughput ≥ 980 Mbps, p99 latency ≤ 5 ms, packet loss ≤ 0.001%; show interface statistics confirms no drops; CPU ≤ 80%, memory ≤ 90%.
58dd671dc22bd04c.equipment: Four PEs (two multi-homed ES pairs) + IXIA scale rig
58dd671dc22bd04c.expectation:
Pass:    BUM forwarding throughput ≥ 98% of injected rate (≥ 980 Mbps), p99 latency ≤ 5 ms, packet loss ≤ 0.001%, ESI labels from DCB and identical per ES across multi-homed PEs, CPU ≤ 80%, memory ≤ 90%
Fail-on: Throughput < 980 Mbps, p99 latency > 5 ms, packet loss > 0.001%, ESI labels differ across PEs in same ES or not from DCB range, CPU > 80%, or memory > 90%

5910d9068594619c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT (EVI 100, Ethernet Tag 0, VLAN 10) with BGP neighbor PE enabled for af-l2vpn evpn. Use default values: advertise-mac enable, unknown-mac-flooding enable (RFC7432bis §13.1 requirements). IXIA emulates CE on access port in VLAN 10.
Action: IXIA sends packet src MAC=00:11:AA:AA:AA:AA dst MAC=00:22:BB:BB:BB:BB (not advertised by any PE). Neighbor PE advertises MAC 00:33:CC:CC:CC:CC via BGP EVPN Type-2 route. IXIA sends second packet src MAC=00:11:AA:AA:AA:AA dst MAC=00:33:CC:CC:CC:CC.
Verify: show l2-services mac-table [EVI 100] confirms 00:11:AA:AA:AA:AA learned on local access interface. Packet capture on DUT-to-PE link: first packet encapsulated and flooded per IMET route Type-3, second packet encapsulated and unicast per Type-2 route to neighbor PE.
5910d9068594619c.expectation:
Pass:    Source MAC 00:11:AA:AA:AA:AA learned and visible in show l2-services mac-table with local interface. Unknown dest MAC 00:22:BB:BB:BB:BB triggers BUM flood to neighbor PE via IMET route (Type-3). Known dest MAC 00:33:CC:CC:CC:CC triggers unicast to neighbor PE via Type-2 route. No drops in show l2-services evpn statistics.
Fail-on: Source MAC not learned from CE packet, unknown unicast not flooded to neighbor PE (violates RFC7432bis §13.1 MUST flood when unknown-mac-flooding enabled), known MAC not unicast forwarded, or packet drops.

59295d5491839e22.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance and enable auto-discovery. On neighbor PE, advertise EVPN routes with route-target 65000:100 (target set) and 65000:200 (control set).
Action: In mode `configuration / l2-services / vpls / auto-discovery`, issue `import-rt 65000:100` and commit. Verify route import behavior. Issue `no import-rt 65000:100` and commit.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `import-rt 65000:100` present after configuration and removed after `no` form. Inspect EVPN route table to confirm only routes with RT 65000:100 are imported (routes with RT 65000:200 filtered out). After removal, verify import filter is removed.
59295d5491839e22.expectation:
Pass:    `import-rt 65000:100` command accepts the route-target format; only EVPN routes with matching route-target 65000:100 are imported; routes with RT 65000:200 are filtered; `no import-rt 65000:100` removes configuration; import filter is removed from running-config.
Fail-on: Command rejects the route-target format; routes with non-matching RT (65000:200) are imported when filter is active; routes with matching RT are filtered out; `no` form fails or configuration persists.

5941566c2722c84f.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT agg-eth with `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 <9-octet-hex>; load-balancing-mode single-active` on VLAN 100 EVPN instance; Cisco/Juniper PE with equivalent single-active ES; MPLS transport + BGP EVPN session established; IXIA emulates CEs on both attachment circuits.
Action: IXIA at local CE (DUT side) sends MAC frames → DUT learns MAC in MACT with local next-hop = attachment circuit ifIndex. IXIA at remote CE (Cisco side) sends frames → DUT receives EVPN Type-2 route + frame with EVPN label (LT1), learns remote MAC in MACT with remote next-hop = Cisco PE IP. Forward known unicast bidirectionally.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-table` on DUT: entries keyed by VLAN-ID 100 + MAC, ESI populated, local MACs → attachment circuit ifIndex, remote MACs → Cisco PE IP. Cisco PE shows DUT's MACs learned. Unicast forwarded, no flooding.
5941566c2722c84f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT MACT contains entries with VLAN-ID + MAC keys; local MACs have attachment circuit ifIndex next-hop; remote MACs have 3rd-party PE IP next-hop; bidirectional known unicast forwarding succeeds; no flooding for learned MACs.
Fail-on: DUT floods known unicast, MACT lookup fails, next-hop type incorrect (local/remote swapped), Cisco PE rejects DUT Type-2 routes, label mismatch, or one-way traffic black-hole.

596159bbe11b3853.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance using `evpn evpn-name service-type vlan-based`; enable `auto-discovery` with `import-rt` and `export-rt` configured; add `ethernet-segment` on interface `agg-eth 0` multihomed to neighbor PE; start IXIA traffic flowing through the Ethernet segment; verify DF election completes and Type-1 auto-discovery routes (RFC7432bis §5) are advertised to neighbor PE and visible in BGP RIB.
Action: Kill the EVPN control-plane process on DUT while traffic is active; observe for automatic process restart.
Verify: Issue `show running-config` to confirm `ethernet-segment` configuration persists; verify ESI state is re-established using show commands; check BGP RIB for re-advertisement of Type-1 auto-discovery routes; verify DF election completes and DF role is consistent; measure end-to-end service interruption duration from kill to full traffic restoration; check neighbor PE EVPN table for absence of duplicate MAC entries.
596159bbe11b3853.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + neighbor PE
596159bbe11b3853.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN control-plane process restarts automatically; all Ethernet segment state (ESI, DF election, auto-discovery) fully recovered; Type-1 routes re-advertised within BGP convergence window; DF election completes with consistent result; service interruption ≤ documented HA recovery bound; zero duplicate MAC advertisements detected.
Fail-on: Process fails to restart or enters failed state; Ethernet segment state lost or corrupted; Type-1 auto-discovery routes missing or incomplete in BGP RIB; DF election fails, times out, or produces split-brain scenario; service interruption exceeds documented HA bound; duplicate MAC entries or route inconsistencies observed at neighbor PE.

5986c2f7bcf3cc4a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE in EVPN topology over MPLS; create matching EVI with P-multicast tree (e.g., Ingress Replication or mLDP); enable unknown-mac-flooding on DUT to trigger IMET route advertisement.
Action: Advertise IMET route (BGP EVPN type 3) from both PEs; capture BGP UPDATE on wire; generate broadcast/unknown-unicast frames from IXIA attached to DUT and 3rd-party PE.
Verify: Parse captured IMET route for PMSI Tunnel attribute (RFC6514); confirm attribute contains Tunnel Type, Tunnel Identifier (tree identity per RFC7432bis §11.2), and MPLS label field; verify label is unique per BD (not reused across EVIs); confirm BUM data plane packets carry the advertised MPLS label in encapsulation.
5986c2f7bcf3cc4a.expectation:
Pass:    IMET routes from both DUT and 3rd-party PE carry PMSI Tunnel attribute with all three mandatory fields (Tunnel Type, Tunnel Identifier, MPLS label); label value is unique per BD and matches label in BUM packet encaps; BUM traffic forwarded bidirectionally without drops.
Fail-on: IMET route missing PMSI Tunnel attribute, or attribute missing Tunnel Identifier / MPLS label field, or label collision across BDs, or 3rd-party PE rejects route (BGP Notification), or BUM traffic black-holed.

59a4c5e6cdc4b306.action_steps:
Setup:  Under `configuration / interface / agg-eth.1 / ethernet-segment`, configure `service-carving highest-preference 45000` (explicit). Under `agg-eth.2 / ethernet-segment`, configure `service-carving lowest-preference` (no preference param → RFC9785 default 32767). Establish BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE; verify ES type-4 routes advertise DF Election Extended Community with correct preference values.
Action: Power-cycle DUT via lab PDU harness; wait for full boot completion.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth.1 ethernet-segment` confirms `service-carving highest-preference 45000`; `agg-eth.2` confirms `service-carving lowest-preference` (no explicit preference); `show bgp evpn route-type 4` decodes DF Election Extended Community: ES1 pref=45000, ES2 pref=32767; BGP session Established.
59a4c5e6cdc4b306.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for BGP EVPN session and ES route verification) + power-cycle harness
59a4c5e6cdc4b306.expectation:
Pass:    Both service-carving configs intact post-reboot; explicit preference 45000 retained in ES1; ES2 advertises default preference 32767 per RFC9785 §3 MUST; BGP EVPN session re-establishes; DF Election Extended Community present and correct in both ES routes.
Fail-on: service-carving config lost or reverted, explicit preference ≠ 45000 after reboot, default preference ≠ 32767 in BGP advertisement, DF Election Extended Community missing from ES route, or BGP session fails to reach Established state.

59d1544880e18282.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'evpn-norm' with service-type vlan-based; attach x-eth 0/0/1 with ingress VLAN translation (tag 200 → normalized 100) and x-eth 0/0/2 with native VLAN 100; connect IXIA ports to both interfaces.
Action: Transmit 1000 tagged Ethernet frames from IXIA (VLAN 200, dst MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55) into x-eth 0/0/1; capture egress traffic on x-eth 0/0/2.
Verify: Run 'show evpn evpn-norm detail' to confirm VLAN normalization config; inspect IXIA packet capture on x-eth 0/0/2 interface — all received frames must show outer VLAN tag = 100; check 'show interface x-eth 0/0/1 counters' for zero input drops.
59d1544880e18282.expectation:
Pass:    100% of captured frames on x-eth 0/0/2 carry VLAN tag 100 (replaced from ingress VLAN 200); interface counters show 1000 Rx on x-eth 0/0/1, 1000 Tx on x-eth 0/0/2, zero drops.
Fail-on: VLAN tag remains 200 in egress frames, or frame count mismatch (drops > 0), or 'show evpn' reports normalization config rejected/inactive.

59ef9bd821f66bd4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE as multihomed PEs for the same Ethernet Segment (assign ESI type 0 or 3 on both). Enable EVPN instance with `auto-discovery`, `export-rt`, and `import-rt` commands. Establish BGP EVPN peering using `af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor config. Verify auto-discovery (Type-1) routes are exchanged.
Action: Send BUM traffic from IXIA to CE attached to multihomed ES to exercise split-horizon filtering. Collect tech-support from DUT using `show tech-support evpn` or platform equivalent command.
Verify: Extract tech-support archive and confirm it includes: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` output, `show bgp l2vpn evpn <route-id> detail` displaying ESI Label Extended Community with Type=0x06 Sub-Type=0x01 encoding, Flags octet (RED field bits 6-7), and ESI Label value (3-octet, high-order 20 bits); running-config showing ESI, auto-discovery, import-rt, export-rt; syslog entries for auto-discovery route events; `show evpn ethernet-segment` output.
59ef9bd821f66bd4.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains BGP EVPN Type-1 route details showing ESI Label Extended Community with Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01; ESI Label value present (non-zero if split-horizon active, may be zero per §7.5 if no filtering needed); Flags octet includes RED=00 for All-Active mode; running-config includes ESI configuration and auto-discovery commands; syslog shows route add/withdraw events; no extended community parsing errors.
Fail-on: Auto-discovery route output missing from tech-support, ESI Label Extended Community absent in route details, Type/Sub-Type not 0x06/0x01, ESI Label field missing or not 3-octet encoded, RED flags incorrect or absent, running-config missing ESI or auto-discovery configuration, or syslog errors indicating malformed extended community.

5a55e2e56f3e28c0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as single-homing PE (no ethernet-segment on attachment interface); configure neighbor PE with `ethernet-segment / identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 / load-balancing-mode all-active`; establish EVPN BGP session; verify DUT receives Type 1 (AD per-EVI) and Type 4 (ES) routes from multihoming PE.
Action: Reset EVPN control-plane process on DUT (platform-specific CLI, e.g., `restart protocol bgp` or equivalent) while IXIA sends bidirectional traffic (100 Mbps, 64-byte frames) through both single-homed DUT and multihomed neighbor PE to shared EVPN destination.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms ES routes re-learned; `show bgp evpn route-type 1` and `route-type 4` show full route table restored; `show interface <attachment-port> statistics` confirms traffic forwarding resumes; aliasing/backup-path procedures remain active (DUT honors multihoming neighbor's AD routes).
5a55e2e56f3e28c0.equipment: DUT (single-homing PE) + neighbor PE (multihoming-capable, Exaware or 3rd-party) + IXIA traffic gen
5a55e2e56f3e28c0.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN session re-establishes within es-waiting-time (3 s default); all Type 1/Type 4 routes from multihoming PE re-appear in DUT routing table; traffic convergence ≤ es-waiting-time + 2 s (≤ 5 s total); DUT continues to process multihoming procedures (aliasing, DF election) from neighbor PE after recovery.
Fail-on: EVPN session stuck or takes > 10 s to recover; missing Type 1 or Type 4 routes after recovery; traffic blackhole > 5 s; DUT ignores multihoming AD routes post-reset (treats multihomed CE as unreachable despite Type 1/4 routes present).

5a5e6e14f783a8e8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT interface agg-eth 1 with `ethernet-segment / identifier 1 / load-balancing-mode single-active` (default) and attach to EVI 100; peer neighbor PE2 via iBGP with matching EVI 100 config; IXIA emulates dual-homed CE with ports to DUT and PE2.
Action: Commit DUT config to advertise Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1, RFC7432bis §7.1); on PE2 capture BGP UPDATE: `tcpdump -i eth0 -s0 -w /tmp/type1.pcap port 179`; decode ESI Label Extended Community (type 0x06 sub-type 0x01, RFC7432bis §7.5) with tshark.
Verify: Confirm ESI Label EC flags bit 0 = 1 (Single-Active per RFC7432bis §14.1.1); on PE2 run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 detail` and `show evpn ethernet-segment` to verify ES mode = Single-Active; IXIA sends L2 frames from CE towards core via both PEs; verify only DF forwards (non-DF drops frames; capture at core shows only DF source MAC).
5a5e6e14f783a8e8.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Label EC flags[0] = 1 in pcap; PE2 CLI confirms redundancy mode Single-Active; IXIA traffic from CE exits only via DF (single copy at core, non-DF drops confirmed via PE2 interface counters).
Fail-on: Flags[0] = 0 or ESI Label EC absent; PE2 interprets ES as All-Active; both PEs forward CE traffic (duplicate frames at core or load-balancing observed).

5ada3ce12a79104f.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with aggregate ethernet interface `agg-eth 0` created and admin-up; no prior `ethernet-segment` configuration under that interface.
Action: Navigate to `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0` and issue `ethernet-segment`; verify CLI prompt changes to indicate ethernet-segment sub-mode; exit sub-mode and issue `commit`.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays `ethernet-segment` stanza; re-enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` to confirm sub-mode is accessible.
5ada3ce12a79104f.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
5ada3ce12a79104f.expectation:
Pass:    `ethernet-segment` command accepted without syntax error; CLI enters ethernet-segment sub-mode (prompt reflects mode path); configuration persists in running-config after commit; sub-mode re-enterable.
Fail-on: Command rejected with syntax error; sub-mode not entered (no prompt change); `ethernet-segment` absent from running-config post-commit; subsequent attempt to enter sub-mode fails.

5b00f00bc0be6cc2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance (e.g., `evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-aware-bundle`) with a non-default `mac-aging-time 600` (seconds) under `configuration / l2-services / evpn test-evpn`; commit and verify active via `show evpn instance test-evpn | include aging`.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn test-evpn`, issue `no mac-aging-time`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn test-evpn` contains no `mac-aging-time` statement; `show evpn instance test-evpn` or `show evpn mac-address-table instance test-evpn` displays the documented default aging time (typically 300 seconds); `show evpn mac-address-table instance test-evpn` confirms learned MACs now age per default timer.
5b00f00bc0be6cc2.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
5b00f00bc0be6cc2.expectation:
Pass:    `mac-aging-time` statement absent from `show running-config l2-services evpn test-evpn`; aging time in `show evpn instance` matches documented default (300s or vendor-specified); any previously learned MAC entries age out per default timer (observable via repeated `show evpn mac-address-table` at default-interval boundaries).
Fail-on: `mac-aging-time` statement persists in running-config after `no` command; aging time in operational state remains at 600s or other non-default value; MAC entries fail to age per default timer; commit rejected or operational inconsistency between control-plane config and data-plane forwarding table.

5b2c81dfe57111d5.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT in mode `configuration / l2-services / vpls / auto-discovery` with no `import-rt` configured.
Action: Issue `import-rt 65000:100`, then `import-rt 65000:200` to configure a list of import route-targets. Commit and capture `show running-config l2-services`. Then issue `no import-rt 65000:100`, commit, and re-capture running-config.
Verify: After first commit, both `import-rt 65000:100` and `import-rt 65000:200` appear under the auto-discovery stanza. After `no import-rt 65000:100`, only 65000:200 remains.
5b2c81dfe57111d5.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
5b2c81dfe57111d5.expectation:
Pass:    Each `import-rt <route-target>` line persists in running-config; `no import-rt <route-target>` removes only the specified RT, leaving others intact.
Fail-on: Configuration rejected, RT values not persisted, or `no` form removes all RTs instead of only the specified one.

5b533b0cd8357817.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT + neighbor PE) in the same Ethernet Segment with different Preference values (e.g., PE1 pref=200, PE2 pref=100) and one with "Don't Preempt" enabled; trigger DF election by bringing up the ES on both PEs.
Action: Change Preference on the current non-DF PE to a higher value, or toggle the "Don't Preempt" capability; wait for DF re-election; collect tech-support bundle via CLI.
Verify: Examine tech-support for: `show evpn ethernet-segment` output showing ESI, DF role, Preference value, and Don't Preempt state per PE; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` showing ES routes with DF Election Extended Community (Pref, DF algorithm, Preempt bit); running-config containing `ethernet-segment` stanza with Preference and preemption settings; syslog entries for DF election transitions ("DF elected", "DF preempted", PE IP of winner).
5b533b0cd8357817.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (both must support EVPN ES with DF Election Extended Community; neighbor PE can be same vendor or RFC-compliant 3rd party)
5b533b0cd8357817.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: (1) ES config showing per-ES Preference values, (2) BGP Type-4 routes with DF Election Extended Community attributes (Preference, DF Alg=2 or 3, Don't Preempt bit), (3) DF state per EVI/ES showing which PE is active DF and runner-up Preference ordering, (4) syslog entries recording DF role changes with winning PE IP and tie-break reason (Don't Preempt vs. lowest IP).
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn ethernet-segment` output, missing DF Election Extended Community in BGP routes, no Preference values in config, or absent DF transition logs when election was triggered.

5cb27e71d2bee926.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN vlan-aware-bundle service; configure ethernet-segment on DUT interface with ESI Type 0 value 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, all-active multi-homing mode, connected to neighbor PE in same ES; establish BGP EVPN peering with auto-discovery; verify `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays Type 0, ESI value, and DF election role; start IXIA bidirectional L2 unicast traffic (100 Mbps, 512-byte frames) through the multi-homed segment for ≥ 1 minute.
Action: Modify ESI Type 0 value to AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:11:22:33 via CLI (e.g., `identifier type 0 value AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:11:22:33`) and commit; wait 10 s; then change ESI type from Type 0 to Type 4 (single-home) by reconfiguring the interface with `identifier type 4` and commit.
Verify: Poll IXIA Tx/Rx counters during both config changes; capture `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` immediately after each commit to confirm ESI type and value updated within ≤ 1 s; verify BGP EVPN Type 1 (AD per-ESI) and Type 4 (ES route) withdrawn and re-advertised with new ESI after first change, and withdrawn entirely after Type 4 (single-home) applied.
5cb27e71d2bee926.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for multi-homing validation) + IXIA traffic gen
5cb27e71d2bee926.expectation:
Pass:    Zero packet loss during Type 0 ESI value change; `show evpn ethernet-segment` reflects new ESI value within 1 s; BGP Type 1/Type 4 routes re-advertised with updated ESI within 2 s. Type 0 → Type 4 change may cause brief reconvergence (≤ 5 s loss acceptable as multi-homing is disabled).
Fail-on: Packet loss during Type 0 value modification > 0, or new ESI value not active in `show evpn ethernet-segment` within 1 s of commit, or BGP routes not updated with new ESI, or Type 0 → Type 4 transition causes > 5 s traffic loss.

5cc0e4dec38f4050.action_steps:
Setup:  Under `interface agg-eth 0`, configure `ethernet-segment` with ESI and required attributes, omitting the `load-balancing-mode` statement; commit.
Action: Execute `show running-config interface agg-eth 0 ethernet-segment` and `show evpn ethernet-segment interface agg-eth 0`.
Verify: Running-config displays no `load-balancing-mode` line (confirming it's using default); operational show command indicates effective mode is single-active.
5cc0e4dec38f4050.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
5cc0e4dec38f4050.expectation:
Pass:    Ethernet-segment operates with single-active mode when `load-balancing-mode` is unconfigured, per documented default behavior.
Fail-on: Effective mode is not single-active, ES fails to initialize without explicit mode config, or show command indicates no/undefined mode.

5d0366a697ae2840.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco IOS-XR PE in EVPN instance 100, BGP EVPN session established. Cisco PE advertises Type-2 route for MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01 in VLAN 100 WITHOUT Default Gateway extended community (simulating host MAC learned locally). DUT configures IRB interface with same MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01 for subnet 10.1.1.0/24.
Action: DUT advertises Type-2 route for MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01 WITH Default Gateway extended community (type 0x0F) via `advertise-mac enable`. Capture BGP UPDATEs on both PEs. Monitor Cisco PE for route withdrawal.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 2 mac 00:00:5e:00:01:01` on DUT confirms route with Default Gateway ext comm 0x0F present, no MAC Mobility ext comm attached. Cisco `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms withdrawal of non-DG route. Cisco logs alarm "duplicate default-gateway MAC detected". Packet capture shows BGP UPDATE with NLRI withdrawal from Cisco within 30 seconds.
5d0366a697ae2840.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Cisco IOS-XR or Juniper) + packet capture (Wireshark/tcpdump for BGP UPDATEs)
5d0366a697ae2840.expectation:
Pass:    Cisco PE withdraws its MAC route within 30s of receiving DUT's route with DG ext comm; alarm logged on Cisco; only DUT's route with DG ext comm 0x0F remains in both PEs' EVPN tables; no MAC Mobility ext comm (type 0x00) present on DUT's DG route.
Fail-on: Both routes coexist after 30s, DUT route rejected by Cisco, no withdrawal UPDATE sent, no alarm raised, or MAC Mobility ext comm incorrectly attached to DUT's DG route.

5d5c1d60c2a686a8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP with `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family on DUT and neighbor PE; configure neighbor relationship under `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip>` then enter `af-l2vpn evpn` mode without configuring explicit capability negotiation parameters.
Action: Establish BGP session between DUT and neighbor PE; allow capability negotiation to complete during session establishment.
Verify: Inspect `show bgp neighbor <neighbor-ip>` output for advertised and received capabilities; confirm Route Refresh capability (RFC 2918) is advertised; confirm ORF Prefix-List capability (RFC 5291) is NOT advertised; verify `show running-config` omits capability configuration lines in the af-l2vpn evpn context.
5d5c1d60c2a686a8.expectation:
Pass:    Route Refresh capability appears in advertised capabilities; ORF Prefix-List does NOT appear; BGP session establishes successfully; running-config contains no explicit capability lines.
Fail-on: Route Refresh capability missing from advertisement, or ORF Prefix-List capability present in advertisement, or session establishment fails, or unexpected capability configuration appears in running-config.

5d80740b0e44f580.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE with BGP EVPN session established; EVPN instance configured on DUT (e.g., `evpn test1 service-type vlan-based`); attachment circuit bound; MAC routes advertised.
Action: On DUT, navigate to `configuration / l2-services / evpn <evpn-name>` mode, issue `control-word enable`, commit. Generate L2 unicast frames across the EVPN to trigger MAC packet forwarding.
Verify: `show running-config` displays `control-word enable` under the EVPN instance. Capture MPLS-encapsulated packets on inter-PE link via IXIA; decode MPLS label stack and inspect payload immediately after bottom-of-stack label — verify presence (or absence) of 4-byte Control Word field before inner Ethernet header.
5d80740b0e44f580.expectation:
Pass:    With `control-word enable`: every captured EVPN MAC packet contains 4-byte Control Word (0x0000xxxx sequence field) between MPLS labels and inner frame. With `disable` or default (no command): no Control Word, inner MAC follows label directly.
Fail-on: Control Word presence contradicts running-config; commit syntax error; EVPN session drops after config change; neighbor PE rejects MAC routes or logs capability mismatch.

5ed8fb404de9cd8a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-bundle` in `configuration / l2-services` mode; bind VLANs 100-105 to the bundle on x-eth 0/0/1; connect IXIA to DUT port, neighbor PE to receive valid traffic.
Action: IXIA transmits 10 Gbps mixed traffic: 50% frames tagged VLAN 100-105 (valid bundle members), 50% frames tagged VLAN 200-205 (outside bundle); sustain for 60 seconds; measure forwarded frame count per VLAN, throughput, and latency.
Verify: Execute `show evpn evpn-100 statistics` and capture IXIA RX counters at neighbor PE; confirm zero frames with VLAN 200-205 egress DUT; `show system cpu` to verify control-plane CPU < 50% during test.
5ed8fb404de9cd8a.expectation:
Pass:    Valid VLAN (100-105) traffic forwarded at ≥ 9.95 Gbps (≤ 0.5% loss), latency < 10 μs; invalid VLAN (200-205) frames 100% dropped (0 frames received at neighbor PE); DUT CPU < 50% throughout test.
Fail-on: Any frame with VLAN 200-205 forwarded to neighbor PE; valid VLAN loss > 0.5%; throughput < 9.95 Gbps; latency ≥ 10 μs; CPU ≥ 50%.

5ee97e99c9491a29.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as EVPN PE with no local attachment to ESI 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09. Configure remote PE to advertise MAC/IP route (type-2) for MAC 00:00:00:11:11:11 with ESI 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09 and corresponding Ethernet A-D per ES route (type-4) for same ESI. IXIA sends continuous traffic (100 Mbps) to MAC 00:00:00:11:11:11.
Action: Shutdown BGP session to remote PE, wait 5s, then re-enable (no shutdown). Capture BGP update sequence during reconvergence. Poll `show evpn mac` every 100ms and `show bgp evpn route-type {2|4}` to correlate MAC installation timing with route-type reception.
Verify: During partial route reception (only type-2 or only type-4 present), `show evpn mac` returns no entry for MAC 00:00:00:11:11:11. MAC installed only after both type-2 and type-4 routes received for ESI 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09. Traffic resumes within convergence bound.
5ee97e99c9491a29.expectation:
Pass:    MAC 00:00:00:11:11:11 appears in FIB only when both route types present; traffic converges within es-waiting-time (3s default) + BGP reconvergence (≤5s) = 8s. No MAC entry during partial routes.
Fail-on: MAC installed when only type-2 route present (before type-4 arrives), OR traffic loss > 10s, OR MAC not withdrawn during BGP shutdown, OR MAC remains after routes withdrawn.

5efdbb0aa1ebd190.action_steps:
Setup:  Create aggregation interface agg-eth 0 with at least one member interface; enable LACP on the LAG.
Action: Check the LAG interface's MAC address and the LACP system MAC (via show lacp and show interface commands); configure lacp-system-mac to a non-default MAC (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55); verify the change; issue 'no lacp-system-mac'; verify restoration.
Verify: Initial LACP system MAC equals the LAG interface MAC; after lacp-system-mac configuration, LACP advertises the configured MAC; after 'no lacp-system-mac', LACP system MAC reverts to LAG MAC; `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` omits lacp-system-mac when at default.
5efdbb0aa1ebd190.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
5efdbb0aa1ebd190.expectation:
Pass:    Default LACP system MAC equals LAG interface MAC; 'no lacp-system-mac' restores LAG MAC; running-config omits lacp-system-mac at default.
Fail-on: LACP system MAC at default differs from LAG MAC, or 'no' form fails to restore default, or default appears in running-config.

5f037b27160bdc07.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN instance (vlan-based) operational between PE1 (DUT) and PE2 with BGP EVPN session established. PE1 has access interface x-eth 0/0/1 participating in the EVPN.
Action: On PE1 navigate to the EVPN instance and issue `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`; commit. On the PE1↔PE2 link capture BGP packets (tcpdump or IXIA). Query `show evpn mac address-table` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on both PEs.
Verify: PE1 local MAC table shows `00:11:22:33:44:55` bound to x-eth 0/0/1 with static flag. Captured BGP UPDATE contains Type-2 NLRI advertising MAC `00:11:22:33:44:55`. PE2 BGP table and MAC table both reflect the received entry.
5f037b27160bdc07.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC configuration accepted; Type-2 route advertised from PE1 carrying MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55; remote PE2 receives the route and installs MAC entry pointing toward PE1. Local and remote show commands confirm presence.
Fail-on: Configuration rejected; commit fails; no Type-2 route generated; remote PE does not receive or install the MAC; BGP capture shows no UPDATE for this MAC or malformed Type-2 NLRI.

5f08fe1e272d9345.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT in all-active multi-homed EVPN: Ethernet Segment ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 with two member interfaces (eth1, eth2), EVPN instance 100, VNI 100, unknown-mac-flooding enable (default). Neighbor PE configured with same ESI. Establish MP-BGP AF-L2VPN EVPN session between DUT and neighbor PE. Verify Type-1 Ethernet A-D per-ES routes exchanged, each carrying unique MPLS label LT4 per interface (eth1 → LT4_label1, eth2 → LT4_label2).
Action: From neighbor PE, inject BUM packet (broadcast MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF or unknown-unicast) into EVPN instance with MPLS label stack containing LT4_label1 (eth1's label). Repeat with second BUM packet using LT4_label2 (eth2's label).
Verify: Capture on DUT: first packet (LT4_label1) floods to eth2 only, NOT to eth1; second packet (LT4_label2) floods to eth1 only, NOT to eth2. Use 'show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99' to confirm BUM split horizon table populated with ifIndex-to-LT4 mappings. Verify Type-1 routes contain correct LT4 label values.
5f08fe1e272d9345.expectation:
Pass:    BUM frames flood to all ES member interfaces except the one identified by incoming LT4 label (split horizon filter active). Type-1 A-D per-ES routes advertised with one LT4 label per ES interface. BUM split horizon table shows eth1 → LT4_label1, eth2 → LT4_label2.
Fail-on: BUM frame with LT4=X floods back to interface X (split horizon broken), or BUM traffic not flooded to any ES interface, or Type-1 route missing/incorrect LT4 label field, or split horizon table empty/incomplete.

5f1da3f85815c013.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE both configured with `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300`; EVPN instance active with shared RT; IXIA attached to both PEs presenting MAC 00:00:5E:00:53:01.
Action: IXIA triggers 6 MAC moves (exceeding N=5) within M=180 seconds by alternating ARP/traffic source between DUT-facing and PE-facing ports; each move causes local MAC learning and BGP MAC/IP Advertisement route update.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-duplication-status` on DUT lists 00:00:5E:00:53:01 as duplicate/frozen; `show log | match duplicate-mac` confirms operator alert logged; `show bgp evpn routes mac 00:00:5E:00:53:01` returns no route (withdrawn); 3rd-party PE CLI (e.g., Cisco `show l2vpn evpn mac dup`) also shows duplication detected; traffic for other MAC addresses continues forwarding.
5f1da3f85815c013.expectation:
Pass:    DUT detects 6th move, logs duplicate-MAC alert with timestamp, withdraws BGP MAC/IP route for 00:00:5E:00:53:01, freezes MAC processing for 300s; 3rd-party PE independently detects duplication; no BGP session flap or route storm.
Fail-on: DUT continues advertising 00:00:5E:00:53:01 after threshold exceeded, or no alert logged, or MAC Mobility sequence number increments beyond configured move-count, or 3rd-party PE rejects/blackholes all EVPN routes from DUT, or freeze-time not honored.

5f214098cd5e9209.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two EVPN instances on DUT with separate MACTs (via vlan/bridge-domain mapping); enable advertise-mac (default); configure Ethernet-Segment with ESI for multi-homing; establish BGP af-l2vpn evpn session with neighbor PE; learn MACs in both MACTs via IXIA.
Action: Verify distinct LT1 labels allocated per MACT in `show bgp evpn route-type 2`; send known unicast from neighbor PE with LT1 label matching MACT-1; send second stream with LT1 for MACT-2; trigger ES link-down to verify multi-homing reroute preserves LT1 forwarding.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 2` confirms unique LT1 per MACT (RFC7432 §7.2); packet capture shows correct LT1 in MPLS stack (RFC7432bis §8.4); `show l2-services evpn mac-table` shows MACs in correct MACT post-failover; `show ethernet-segment` confirms DF election unaffected.
5f214098cd5e9209.expectation:
Pass:    Each MACT advertises unique LT1 label in MAC/IP routes per RFC7432 §7.2; inbound labeled packets forwarded to correct MACT per LT1 value; multi-homing failover completes with 0 MAC loss and LT1 labels unchanged.
Fail-on: Same LT1 label across multiple MACTs; wrong MACT selected on ingress; multi-homing DF flap or state down; MAC flush during LT1-labeled traffic.

5f32da32635f0c60.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP neighbor with L2VPN EVPN address family; create a route-policy that denies EVPN routes matching a specific route-target; attach the policy to inbound EVPN updates from this neighbor.
Action: Establish BGP session; neighbor advertises MAC/IP routes: some with permitted route-targets, some with denied route-targets.
Verify: `show l2vpn evpn mac`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirm only permitted routes installed; denied routes not in local L2VPN table; `show running-config` shows policy attached.
5f32da32635f0c60.expectation:
Pass:    Inbound policy correctly filters EVPN routes; only permitted-RT routes installed in local MAC/IP table; denied routes not present.
Fail-on: Denied-RT routes installed in local table; policy has no filtering effect; all received routes installed.

5f49bf7884500700.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE connected via IP/MPLS core; both have VPLS instance `vpls-ad-test` pre-configured without auto-discovery; IXIA connected to customer-facing ports on both PEs.
Action: On DUT, enter `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-ad-test` mode, issue `auto-discovery`, then `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100`, commit. On neighbor PE, configure matching `auto-discovery` with identical route-targets and commit. Send L2 unicast traffic (100 Mbps, 64-byte frames) bidirectionally from IXIA for 60 seconds.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-ad-test` on DUT displays the `auto-discovery` stanza with `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`. Check BGP EVPN routes or VPLS operational state to confirm neighbor PE is discovered. IXIA reports zero packet loss.
5f49bf7884500700.expectation:
Pass:    Running-config contains `auto-discovery` with import-rt and export-rt set to 65000:100; neighbor PE discovered via auto-discovery (BGP EVPN routes received); IXIA traffic forwarded with 0% loss.
Fail-on: `auto-discovery` stanza absent or incomplete in running-config, neighbor PE not discovered after 90 seconds, or IXIA reports >0.01% packet loss.

5f78b8ca6f2f6273.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn1` with `service-type vlan-bundle` on interface x-eth 0/0/1, bundle VLANs 100–110; IXIA sends continuous streams tagged VLAN 100, 105, 110 (in-bundle) and VLAN 200, 300 (out-of-bundle) at 1000 pps each for ≥ 1 minute; verify baseline: VLAN 100/105/110 forwarded, VLAN 200/300 dropped.
Action: While traffic flows, modify `evpn1` bundle membership: remove VLAN 105, add VLAN 200 using `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1` then update VLAN membership commands; confirm with `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn1`.
Verify: IXIA counters show VLAN 105 Rx drops to zero within ≤ 1 s, VLAN 200 Rx begins within ≤ 1 s; VLAN 100/110 streams report zero loss during transition; `show l2-services evpn evpn1` reflects updated VLAN list.
5f78b8ca6f2f6273.expectation:
Pass:    After bundle modification, frames with VLAN-ID not in updated bundle (105, 300) show 0 packets received at IXIA; frames with VLAN-ID in updated bundle (100, 110, 200) forwarded with no loss; config change applied within ≤ 1 s; MUST verified: out-of-bundle VLAN traffic dropped.
Fail-on: Out-of-bundle VLAN (105 or 300) receives > 0 packets post-change; in-bundle VLAN (100, 110, 200) experiences > 0 packet loss; config change takes > 1 s to take effect; VLAN 200 not forwarded after being added to bundle.

5f8c3d8cd824ba34.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE EVPN topology; DUT configured with `advertise-mac enable` (configuration/l2-services/evpn) and `af-l2vpn evpn` neighbors to PE1, PE2. PE1 advertises MAC M1 (Type 2 route) with Default Gateway extended community (Type 0x06 Sub 0x0d, RFC7432 §10.1) but longer AS-path [64512 64513]; PE2 advertises same M1 without Default Gateway ext-comm, shorter AS-path [64512].
Action: Inject both BGP UPDATEs to DUT; capture control-plane packets (tcpdump/Wireshark); force BGP best-path re-selection.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route <M1>` marks PE1's route as best ('>' indicator); `show evpn mac <M1>` shows PE1's MPLS label/next-hop in use; packet capture confirms Default Gateway ext-comm presence in PE1's UPDATE, absence in PE2's.
5f8c3d8cd824ba34.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs (to advertise competing Type 2 routes with/without Default Gateway ext-comm) + IXIA (to verify data-plane forwarding follows best-path)
5f8c3d8cd824ba34.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects PE1's route with Default Gateway extended community per RFC7432bis §7.13.1 criterion 1, overriding AS-path preference (criteria ordered).
Fail-on: DUT selects PE2's route (missing Default Gateway ext-comm) based on AS-path length, or both routes installed as ECMP.

5f8e6f90c3f437ca.action_steps:
Problem: Validate hitless DF election algorithm changes during all-active traffic and automatic fallback to Default when peer PEs advertise incompatible DF Alg values.
Method: Flow IXIA traffic through two PEs sharing an ESI, change PE1 from default to highest-random-weight service-carving, configure PE2 with incompatible highest-preference to trigger RFC7432bis-§8.5 fallback, monitor loss and DF state transitions.
Setup: PE1 and PE2 each connect to the same CE via LACP LAG; configure `interface agg-eth 1` / `ethernet-segment` / `identifier 1` / `load-balancing-mode all-active` / `service-carving default` on both PEs; bring up EVI; IXIA flows traffic through the multi-homed CE for ≥ 1 minute at line rate.
Action: On PE1, change to `service-carving highest-random-weight` and commit while traffic continues; verify BGP Type 4 (ES Route, EVPNS-REQ#300) advertises HRW algorithm in DF Election Extended Community; on PE2, change to `service-carving highest-preference preference 20000` creating incompatibility; observe PE1 detects mismatch and falls back to `default` algorithm per RFC7432bis-§8.5 and EVPNS-REQ#120.
Verify: IXIA reports zero packet loss during all configuration changes; `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE1 shows DF algorithm transitions: default → highest-random-weight → default (after fallback); BGP Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES, EVPNS-REQ#260) and Type 4 routes update within 1 second; PE1's Type 4 DF Election Extended Community reflects fallback to Default algorithm; ES-Import Route Target (EVPNS-REQ#250) remains stable.
5f8e6f90c3f437ca.expectation:
Pass:    All service-carving changes apply within 1 second with zero traffic loss; PE1 automatically falls back to Default algorithm (EVPNS-REQ#120 MUST, RFC7432bis-§8.5) when PE2 advertises incompatible highest-preference DF Alg value; Type 1 and Type 4 routes reflect correct DF state throughout; all-active load-balancing (EVPNS-REQ#150) continues without disruption.
Fail-on: Any packet loss during configuration changes; DF algorithm not updated within 1 second; PE1 fails to fall back to Default when receiving incompatible DF Alg from PE2; Type 4 DF Election Extended Community does not reflect fallback; or traffic forwarding breaks.

607f492efc0cef93.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology (DUT as PE1, neighbor as PE2) multi-homed to CE via LAG; configure ES ESI=01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 on both PEs (under `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment`); set DF algorithm to Highest-Preference on both.
Action: On DUT: configure Preference=200, enable Don't Preempt capability. On PE2: configure Preference=200, disable Don't Preempt. Capture BGP EVPN Route Type 4 advertisements on PE1↔PE2 link. Bring up all ES LAG member interfaces.
Verify: Decode DF Election Extended Community (RFC9785 §5) in Route Type 4 from both PEs. Confirm DUT advertises Preference=200 with Don't Preempt bit=1; PE2 advertises Preference=200 with Don't Preempt bit=0. Run `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11` to confirm DUT holds DF role.
607f492efc0cef93.expectation:
Pass:    DUT elected as DF per RFC9785 §4.1 tiebreaker rule (both PEs have Preference=200; DUT wins via Don't Preempt capability set). Route Type 4 from DUT carries DF Election EC with Preference=200, Don't Preempt=1. Route Type 4 from PE2 carries Preference=200, Don't Preempt=0.
Fail-on: DF Election EC missing from Route Type 4; Preference in EC ≠ 200; Don't Preempt bit state in EC does not match configuration; PE2 elected as DF (violates §4.1 selection); DF election non-convergent after 10 sec.

60ceeda16d82b2bf.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs multihome to a CE via agg-eth with identical ES identifier type 0. PE1: `service-carving highest-preference preference 40000`. PE2: `service-carving highest-preference preference 20000`. Both: `load-balancing-mode all-active`. Establish EVPN VLAN-based service; IXIA sends bidirectional traffic through the ES.
Action: Shut the PE1 agg-eth interface (current DF, preference 40000) to force DF re-election while traffic is active.
Verify: Issue `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE2 to confirm PE2 DF status and preference value 20000. Monitor IXIA packet loss counter and convergence duration.
60ceeda16d82b2bf.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 becomes DF (DF field = local, preference = 20000 in show output); traffic converges within 3 seconds (es-waiting-time default); packet loss ≤ 50 ms per RFC7432 mass-withdrawal.
Fail-on: PE2 does not become DF, convergence time > 3 seconds, or permanent traffic loss (> 100 consecutive lost packets).

613ee1abac53b4e4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure syslog collector. Enable MAC duplication detection: `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 60` in `configuration/l2-services/evpn`. Configure EVPN instance with bridge-domain.
Action: Trigger MAC duplication alarm ON by injecting duplicate MAC movement exceeding move-count threshold (6 moves in <180s) → verify warning syslog sent. Disable detection: `no host mac-address-duplicate-detection` → verify informational syslog sent. Re-enable detection → verify warning syslog sent. Collect tech-support bundle.
Verify: Parse tech-support bundle for syslog entries matching alarm state transitions (OFF=>ON = warning severity, ON=>OFF = informational severity). Confirm bundle contains `show host mac-address-duplicate-detection`, `show l2-services evpn`, running-config with MAC duplication settings.
613ee1abac53b4e4.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains ≥2 syslog entries for MAC duplication alarm: (1) OFF=>ON with severity=warning, (2) ON=>OFF with severity=informational. Bundle includes show command output + running-config snapshot.
Fail-on: Missing syslog entries, wrong severity mapping (e.g., informational for OFF=>ON), or missing `show host mac-address-duplicate-detection` output in bundle.

615476154faaaea4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `ethernet-segment` under `interface agg-eth 1` with an ESI (e.g., `identifier type 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88.99`), associate the interface with an EVPN instance, and verify EVPN Type-1 (A-D per-EVI) and Type-4 (ES) routes are advertised.
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1`, issue `no ethernet-segment`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` contains no `ethernet-segment` block; `show evpn ethernet-segment` reports no ESI for agg-eth 1; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms Type-1/Type-4 routes for the ESI are withdrawn; `show l2vpn forwarding` shows no multi-homing state for agg-eth 1.
615476154faaaea4.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
615476154faaaea4.expectation:
Pass:    `ethernet-segment` block removed from running-config; ESI de-registered (not in `show evpn ethernet-segment`); EVPN Type-1 and Type-4 routes for the ESI withdrawn from BGP; no stale multi-homing state in `show l2vpn forwarding`.
Fail-on: `ethernet-segment` or ESI remains in running-config; ESI still listed in `show evpn ethernet-segment`; Type-1/Type-4 routes not withdrawn; or multi-homing DF/non-DF state persists in `show l2vpn forwarding`.

6181353c51231323.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn100 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` on x-eth 0/0/1 bound to VLAN-IDs 100-105; establish BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE; commit.
Action: IXIA attached to x-eth 0/0/1 transmits broadcast frame (dst ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff, src aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01) tagged VLAN 200 (not in configured bundle); send 1000 frames at 10 fps.
Verify: `show evpn mac address-table` lists zero MAC entries for VLAN 200; `show interface x-eth 0/0/1 counters` confirms ingress drops incremented; neighbor PE `show evpn route` shows no type-2 or type-3 routes for VLAN 200 from DUT.
6181353c51231323.expectation:
Pass:    BUM frame with VLAN-ID 200 (outside bundle range 100-105) is dropped at ingress; no MAC learning or EVPN route advertisement occurs.
Fail-on: MAC entry for VLAN 200 appears in DUT MAC table, or type-2/type-3 route for VLAN 200 is advertised to neighbor PE, or frame egresses to MPLS core.

618897f1dc92eaaa.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVI 100 with local attachment circuit (agg-eth 1, VLAN 10, ethernet-segment with identifier type 0, load-balancing-mode single-active) and remote PE neighbor; learn at least 2 local MACs from IXIA on agg-eth 1 and 2 remote MACs from remote PE EVPN routes.
Action: Collect tech-support output after MACT is populated with both local and remote entries.
Verify: Parse tech-support for `show l2-services evpn mac-table instance 100`, `show l2-services evpn instance 100`, running-config (ethernet-segment identifier, load-balancing-mode), kernel MAC forwarding tables for EVI 100.
618897f1dc92eaaa.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains MACT entries showing VLAN-ID 10, MAC addresses, ESI values, local next-hop type (agg-eth 1 ifIndex), remote next-hop type (neighbor PE IP), plus running-config with ethernet-segment identifier/load-balancing-mode settings, plus kernel FIB for EVI 100.
Fail-on: Missing `show l2-services evpn mac-table` output, missing local or remote next-hop type entries in MACT, missing ESI field, missing ethernet-segment config, or missing kernel FIB tables for EVI 100.

6192362a9ccd9307.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment on DUT with ESI type values spanning valid range (type 0x00 arbitrary, 0x01 LACP, 0x04 router-ID, 0x05 AS-based) under l2-services/evpn; establish iBGP peering with neighbor PE; enable af-l2vpn evpn on neighbor.
Action: Activate each ES configuration; trigger ES route (type 4) advertisement by bringing up the ES; capture BGP UPDATE messages on PE↔PE link for each ESI type; attempt to configure invalid ESI type 0x06 or higher if CLI permits.
Verify: Decode route type 4 NLRI from each captured UPDATE; extract 10-octet ESI field; confirm byte 0 (most significant) equals configured type value and is in range [0x00..0x05]; verify DUT rejects or ignores any peer-advertised ES route with ESI byte 0 > 0x05.
6192362a9ccd9307.expectation:
Pass:    All advertised ES routes carry ESI with byte 0 ∈ {0x00, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05} matching configured type; DUT discards peer ES routes with ESI byte 0 ≥ 0x06.
Fail-on: DUT advertises ES route with ESI byte 0 > 0x05, or accepts/installs peer ES route violating the 0–5 type constraint without logging a malformed NLRI error.

61929741ab30ad71.action_steps:
Problem: validate that tech-support captures complete diagnostic evidence when ESI label mismatch occurs across PEs multi-homing to the same Ethernet Segment, exercising the SFS alarm and route-hold behavior (EVPNS-REQ#260 delta over RFC7432bis §8.2.1).
Method: configure two-PE multi-homed ES, engineer ESI label mismatch in Type 1 per-ES advertisements, trigger vendor mismatch alarm, collect tech-support bundle, and verify it contains Type 1 routes (per-ES and per-EVI), ESI label values, alarm entries, and forwarding state.
Setup: Two PEs multi-home to CE via LACP; configure `evpn` under `l2-services`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt` and `export-rt`, establish BGP `af-l2vpn evpn` sessions; configure different ESI label allocations on the two PEs for the shared ES to induce mismatch.
Action: Allow BGP to converge and SFS mismatch alarm to fire; run `tech-support` command; save bundle off-box and extract contents.
Verify: Bundle contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn` listing Ethernet A-D per ES routes (ET ID = MAX-ET, MPLS Label = 0) and per-EVI routes; `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` shows ESI label extended community values differing between PEs; alarm log includes mismatch entry ("Mismatched Configuration" per EVPNS-REQ#260 SFS requirement) with timestamp; bundle includes `show evpn mac address-table`, running-config with `evpn`, `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, `export-rt`, `af-l2vpn evpn` config, and kernel forwarding tables for EVI/ES.
61929741ab30ad71.expectation:
Pass: tech-support bundle contains complete Type 1 route evidence—Ethernet A-D per ES and per-EVI routes per RFC7432bis §8.2.1 and EVPNS-REQ#260/270, ESI label extended community values visible in detail view, mismatch alarm log entry documenting detection and route-hold state ("Save the route and don't use it" per EVPNS-REQ#260), and forwarding state for affected EVI/ES.
Fail-on: missing `show bgp l2vpn evpn` or detail output, ESI label values not visible in extended communities, no alarm log entry for label mismatch, missing `auto-discovery` or `af-l2vpn evpn` running-config, or absent kernel FIB state for ES.

620ca73176006cb3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance 100 with BD/VLAN 100, RD 65000:100, export-rt 65000:100; configure neighbor PE with import-rt 65000:100 and af-l2vpn evpn; establish MP-BGP session; verify advertise-mac enable and unknown-mac-flooding enable (spec defaults).
Action: Activate EVPN instance on DUT; capture BGP UPDATE for IMET route (type 3, RFC7432bis ch.7.3) sent to neighbor PE; note LT3 label value in PMSI Tunnel attribute; inject unknown-destination Ethernet frame from IXIA on DUT VLAN 100 access port.
Verify: show evpn instance 100 label confirms LT3 label allocated for BD 100; show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 on neighbor PE displays IMET with PMSI Tunnel attribute containing LT3 label; packet capture on MPLS inter-PE link shows BUM frame with outer MPLS label = advertised LT3 label.
620ca73176006cb3.expectation:
Pass:    One LT3 label allocated per BD (visible in show evpn instance label), IMET route carries PMSI Tunnel attribute with LT3 label, BUM MPLS outer label matches advertised LT3
Fail-on: LT3 label absent for BD 100, or IMET PMSI Tunnel attribute missing/empty, or BUM MPLS outer label ≠ LT3 label, or IMET route not sent to neighbor PE

62fbcf11c77090ae.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure port-channel 1 with member ports, set `lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55`, bring up LACP session with IXIA partner, verify IXIA captures LACP PDUs showing System ID = 00:11:22:33:44:55.
Action: Issue `no lacp-system-mac` under port-channel 1 configuration; commit; observe LACP re-negotiation on wire.
Verify: `show running-config interface port-channel 1` has no `lacp-system-mac` line; `show lacp neighbor` shows session remains up; capture new LACP PDUs and confirm System ID field = port-channel 1 MAC address (the default LAG MAC).
62fbcf11c77090ae.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (IXIA configured as LACP partner on member port peer links, with PDU capture enabled)
62fbcf11c77090ae.expectation:
Pass:    `lacp-system-mac` removed from config, LACP PDU System ID reverts to port-channel interface MAC, LACP session stays COLLECTING_DISTRIBUTING, IXIA confirms MAC change within 3 × LACP slow interval (90 s).
Fail-on: `lacp-system-mac` persists in running-config, LACP PDUs still carry 00:11:22:33:44:55, LACP session flaps to DEFAULTED/EXPIRED, or System ID in PDU does not match port-channel MAC from `show interface port-channel 1`.

63074db25640adf9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT in EVI 100 with ingress replication for BUM, VXLAN tunnels to 2+ neighbor PEs. Verify IMET Type-3 routes received from all remote PEs (`show evpn routes type 3`). Send broadcast frame from CE to populate forwarding state.
Action: Collect tech-support on DUT: `show tech-support evpn instance 100` or equivalent diagnostic bundle.
Verify: Parse tech-support archive for: `show evpn routes type 3` (Type-3 IMET route from each remote PE with tunnel endpoint), `show evpn instance 100` (ingress replication list), running-config (`unknown-mac-flooding enable`, `advertise-mac enable`), syslog (EVPN BGP session establishment with remote PEs), forwarding tables showing BUM replication entries with N next-hops (one per remote PE).
63074db25640adf9.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains Type-3 IMET routes from all N remote PEs, ingress replication list with N entries, BUM forwarding entry showing replication to N next-hops, config showing `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default), EVPN session UP logs.
Fail-on: IMET routes missing or < N entries, ingress replication list incomplete, BUM forwarding table absent or < N next-hops, or EVPN config/session logs not captured in bundle.

6329fc66b4d51061.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN with advertise-mac enabled; establish iBGP EVPN session with IXIA (emulating PE peer); IXIA advertises valid EVPN Type-2 route for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 with LOCAL_PREF 100.
Action: IXIA injects malformed Type-2 updates for same MAC with higher LOCAL_PREF 200 but corrupted NLRI: (a) invalid NLRI length field 0xFF, (b) reserved ESI type 0xFF, (c) oversized label stack (4 labels), (d) reserved flag bits 0x80 set in route-type octet — forcing DUT best-path evaluation of malformed candidates.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn mac` confirms only valid route (LOCAL_PREF 100) installed; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` session remains Established; `show log | match EVPN` captures malformed-NLRI errors; BGP error counter increments.
6329fc66b4d51061.expectation:
Pass:    Valid route with LOCAL_PREF 100 remains best path; all four malformed variants absent from RIB; error counter ≥4; syslog entries present for each malformed NLRI; BGP session stable (no NOTIFICATION sent).
Fail-on: Malformed route selected as best path despite corruption, BGP session reset, daemon crash, missing syslog/counter, or malformed route propagated to Route Reflector.

633bcb354639e9f0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as ingress PE with EVPN instance (unknown-mac-flooding enable); configure Ethernet Segment on 2 egress PEs (one DF, one non-DF) multi-homed to same site; verify non-DF PE advertises ESI label in BGP type-1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) or type-4 (Ethernet Segment) routes.
Action: Send BUM traffic from IXIA through ingress PE to multi-homed ES; collect tech-support from DUT including show l2-services evpn, show bgp l2vpn evpn routes (type-1/type-4 with ESI label), show evpn forwarding-table, show mpls forwarding-table, packet captures of replicated BUM packets to non-DF PE.
Verify: Inspect tech-support for ESI label allocation in non-DF BGP routes; BUM replication list entries showing non-DF PE target with ESI label included; MPLS forwarding entries or packet traces confirming ESI label in MPLS stack for copies sent to non-DF per RFC7432bis 8.3.1.1 ingress replication rule.
633bcb354639e9f0.equipment: DUT (ingress PE) + 2 neighbor PEs (DF + non-DF for multi-homed ES) + IXIA traffic gen
633bcb354639e9f0.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support shows non-DF ESI label in BGP EVPN routes (type-1/type-4); ingress PE replication list includes non-DF PE with ESI label; packet captures or FIB dumps confirm ESI label in MPLS stack for BUM packets to non-DF egress PE.
Fail-on: Missing ESI label in non-DF replication entries, missing show evpn or show bgp output in tech-support, missing packet captures, or ESI label absent from MPLS stack in BUM replicas to non-DF PE.

63c56820404a922d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment with ESI and `load-balancing-mode` explicitly set to non-default; attach `agg-eth 0`; verify neighbor PE with same ESI forms multi-homed segment.
Action: Execute `no load-balancing-mode` under the Ethernet Segment configuration; commit.
Verify: Execute `show running-config` (confirms `load-balancing-mode` removed); `show evpn ethernet-segment` (displays single-active state); send IXIA traffic to multi-homed CE; capture on both PEs to confirm only Designated Forwarder forwards.
63c56820404a922d.expectation:
Pass:    Configuration reverts to default (absent from `show running-config`); ES operates in single-active mode; traffic forwarded exclusively by DF; non-DF PE drops ingress traffic.
Fail-on: `load-balancing-mode` remains in running-config; ES stays in non-default mode; multiple PEs forward simultaneously; or DF election corrupted.

63de72feb1c84568.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based` with normalized VLAN-ID 4 on x-eth 0/0/1 (maps input VLAN 10→4), enable auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt 65000:100; send baseline traffic with VLAN 10, capture egress showing VLAN 4; stage upgrade image.
Action: Execute onie-install upgrade; after reboot, verify `show running-config l2-services` contains evpn1 vlan-based config and auto-discovery RTs; send identical test frames tagged VLAN 10 on x-eth 0/0/1.
Verify: `show evpn evpn1` confirms operational with service-type vlan-based; packet capture on egress shows VLAN 4 (replacement still active); `show version` confirms new image; no config-replay errors in `show logging`.
63de72feb1c84568.expectation:
Pass:    Upgraded image preserves evpn1 vlan-based config including VLAN-ID normalization (10→4) and auto-discovery route-targets; post-upgrade traffic with input VLAN 10 egresses as VLAN 4 matching pre-upgrade behavior.
Fail-on: l2-services/evpn config missing or VLAN mapping lost after upgrade; egress frames show VLAN 10 (replacement broken); evpn1 status not operational; upgrade rollback or boot failure.

63e8e88c6c1314b0.action_steps:
