Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with identical ESI (e.g., `identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88`) on agg-eth interfaces to shared multihomed CE. Enable EVPN instance, verify Route Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) advertised from both PEs. IXIA generates continuous traffic destined to CE MAC addresses, currently forwarding through DUT's local agg-eth.
Action: Execute `configuration / interface / agg-eth / shutdown` on DUT to simulate local path failure. Monitor Route Type 1 withdrawal from DUT's BGP updates. Measure traffic loss duration on IXIA.
Verify: Query `show evpn ethernet-segment` (ES state down on DUT), `show evpn route type 1` (DUT's route withdrawn, neighbor PE's route remains), `show interface agg-eth` (admin down). Confirm IXIA traffic continues via neighbor PE (aliasing path active), convergence time < es-waiting-time (default 3 sec), packet loss < 50ms per RFC7432bis §8 fast convergence.
63e8e88c6c1314b0.expectation:
Pass:    DUT withdraws Route Type 1 within 1 sec of agg-eth shutdown; traffic converges to neighbor PE aliasing path within es-waiting-time (3 sec default); total packet loss < 50ms; `show evpn ethernet-segment` reflects ES operationally down on DUT while neighbor PE ES remains up.
Fail-on: Traffic blackhole lasting > es-waiting-time; aliasing path not activated (complete traffic drop with no recovery via neighbor PE); Route Type 1 not withdrawn or stale; sustained packet loss after convergence window.

641b5bab279dfb0c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed ES (ESI type 0) on Exaware DUT agg-eth interface with `load-balancing-mode all-active` and `service-carving highest-preference` (no explicit preference → default 32767). Configure same ESI on 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) with highest-preference and explicit preference 40000. Establish BGP EVPN peering.
Action: Bring up the agg-eth interfaces and BGP session; capture ES route (type 4) advertisements from both PEs; extract the DF Election Extended Community (type 0x06, subtype 0x06) octets 6-7 (preference field).
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on DUT for DF role per VLAN; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` to confirm ES route received from peer with preference 40000; packet capture confirms DUT's ES route carries preference=32767 (0x7FFF) in DF Election Extended Community.
641b5bab279dfb0c.expectation:
Pass:    DUT advertises preference=32767 when not explicitly configured; 3rd-party PE's preference=40000 is received and parsed; DF election converges (higher preference wins per VLAN in all-active mode); ES route format conforms to RFC9785 §3 (octets 6-7 = preference in network byte order).
Fail-on: DUT advertises preference ≠ 32767 by default, or rejects valid preference in range 0-65535 from peer, or DF election does not converge within es-waiting-time, or DF Election Extended Community malformed (wrong type/subtype or byte order).

6456fb8286a488e9.action_steps:
Setup:  Connect Exaware DUT and Cisco IOS-XR PE via MPLS core; establish iBGP EVPN address-family session. On DUT: configure EVPN instance using `evpn` and `auto-discovery` with `export-rt` and `import-rt`; configure `ethernet-segment` on `agg-eth 0`. On Cisco: configure equivalent EVPN instance with ethernet-segment on bundle interface.
Action: Enable ethernet-segment configuration on `agg-eth 0` to trigger BGP advertisement of Ethernet A-D per EVI route (route-type 1). Verify Cisco PE also advertises route-type 1.
Verify: Capture BGP UPDATE via `tcpdump -i <core-if> port 179 -w /tmp/bgp.pcap`. Decode EVPN NLRI in Wireshark to confirm: (1) RD field = 8 octets, (2) ESI field = 10 octets, (3) Ethernet Tag ID present, (4) Next Hop = advertising PE's loopback IPv4 or IPv6. Run `show bgp evpn route-type 1` on both PEs to verify routes installed. Send IXIA unicast traffic between CEs to validate forwarding.
6456fb8286a488e9.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs exchange route-type 1 with RD (8 bytes), ESI (10 bytes), Next Hop per RFC7432bis §8.4.1; routes in `show bgp evpn route-type 1` with "valid" status; IXIA traffic forwarded with 0% loss
Fail-on: BGP notification "malformed EVPN NLRI", ESI length ≠ 10 octets in capture, Next Hop = 0.0.0.0, or IXIA >0% frame loss

6458c9cae5480b59.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed Ethernet Segment ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 with DUT as initial DF and neighbor PE as backup; enable "Don't Preempt" capability in DF Election Extended Community on both PEs (`ethernet-segment` under agg-eth interface); IXIA sends bidirectional 1 Gbps L2 traffic through ES; confirm DUT is DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99`.
Action: Every 2 hours for 24 h: administratively shut neighbor PE's ES interface, wait 5 min, restore with "Don't Preempt" enabled. At hour 12: bring neighbor PE back with mismatched DF algorithm (HRW if default is mod-based per RFC7432) to trigger fallback. IXIA traffic continuous.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` confirms "Don't Preempt" capability set; `show evpn ethernet-segment esi <ESI>` shows DUT remains DF after each neighbor return (hours 0-12, 14-24); at hour 12, all PEs fall back to default algorithm; `show platform process memory` RSS ±5%; `show evpn ethernet-segment counters` monotonic; IXIA reports loss.
6458c9cae5480b59.equipment: Two routers (multi-homed ES: DUT + neighbor PE) + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
6458c9cae5480b59.expectation:
Pass:    DUT remains DF through all non-preemptive neighbor PE returns ("Don't Preempt" honored); algorithm mismatch at hour 12 triggers RFC7432 default fallback on all PEs per MUST; memory stable (RSS change <10% over 24 h); counters monotonic, no resets; IXIA loss <0.1% over 24 h; BGP EVPN route churn only during mismatch fallback event.
Fail-on: Neighbor PE preempts DUT as DF on return (violates non-revertive behavior); algorithm mismatch does NOT trigger RFC7432 fallback; memory growth >10%; counter resets or freeze; IXIA loss >0.1%; unexpected route withdrawals.

64923c8587da9136.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES on PE1 and PE2 with `load-balancing-mode all-active` and matching ESI `identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88`; configure DUT as remote PE3 importing Ethernet A-D per ES routes from both; establish eBGP EVPN sessions (PE1 ↔ PE3, PE2 ↔ PE3); attach CE with LACP lag to PE1+PE2.
Action: Advertise MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01 from both PE1 and PE2 in EVI 100 via MAC/IP Advertisement routes with ESI Label ext-community redundancy mode = 0; trigger BGP route churn by flapping PE1's session or modifying route attributes; send continuous L2 traffic from IXIA (behind PE3) destined to 00:00:5e:00:01:01 during convergence.
Verify: On PE3, `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms All-Active mode deduced for ESI 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88; `show evpn mac` displays ECMP next hops (both PE1 and PE2 labels); `show mpls forwarding-table` includes label stacks from MAC/IP Advertisement routes; IXIA traffic counters show load-balancing across PE1 and PE2; during BGP flap, zero packet loss and traffic continues via remaining PE.
64923c8587da9136.equipment: DUT (remote PE3) + two neighbor PEs (PE1, PE2 for multi-homed ES) + IXIA traffic gen + LACP-capable CE switch
64923c8587da9136.expectation:
Pass:    Remote PE3 deduces All-Active from redundancy mode = 0 in all received Ethernet A-D per ES routes; installs ECMP next hops using MAC/IP Advertisement route labels; traffic ECMP hashes across PE1 and PE2 (±10% distribution); during BGP session flap of one PE, convergence completes within es-waiting-time (default 3 s) with zero frame loss.
Fail-on: PE3 installs single next hop instead of ECMP; traffic blackholes or persists to flapped PE beyond 3 s; wrong label stack applied (e.g., Ethernet A-D per ES label instead of MAC/IP Advertisement label); PE3 show commands indicate Single-Active mode or missing ESI.

65b85a00ef43db3a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as multihomed PE in All-Active Ethernet Segment (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) with neighbor PE, attach EVI to the ES, establish BGP EVPN peering, inject BUM traffic from dual-homed CE to trigger DF election and split-horizon forwarding.
Action: Collect tech-support bundle via `show tech-support` or NETCONF <get-tech-support> after DF election completes and BUM traffic forwarding is active.
Verify: Parse tech-support archive for: `show evpn ethernet-segment` (DF role, ES-Import RT), `show evpn route type 1 esi <ESI>` (AD-per-EVI with ESI), `show evpn route type 2` (MAC routes with ESI label), running-config for ES/EVI, syslogs for DF election events, L2 forwarding tables showing split-horizon group or ESI label handling.
65b85a00ef43db3a.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains ESI operational state (DF/non-DF role), Type-1 AD-per-EVI route with ESI value, Type-2 MAC/IP routes with ESI label allocation, split-horizon forwarding group or ESI-based loop prevention entries in FIB, syslogs for DF election transitions.
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn ethernet-segment` output, missing ESI label in Type-2 route dump, missing DF election state, or missing split-horizon forwarding plane entries in L2 tables.

65c049a31ea5e649.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with `load-balancing-mode all-active` on `agg-eth 0`, both using the same ESI (`identifier 0 <type0-value>`); configure PE3 without this ESI; enable ingress replication for BUM traffic; connect IXIA to CE attached to the multi-homed ES.
Action: IXIA transmits continuous BUM traffic (broadcast and unknown-unicast frames) at moderate rate for ≥ 24 hours; traffic enters EVPN via the Non-DF PE, exercising ESI label inclusion (Non-DF → DF copy) and exclusion (any ingress PE → non-ES PE3) per RFC7432bis ch.8.3.1.1.
Verify: Hourly verification of ESI label in platform MPLS label space; monitor `agg-eth 0` receive counters for zero ingress packets (split-horizon enforcement); monitor EVPN BUM replication counters and remote PE receive statistics for continuous delivery; track process memory usage.
65c049a31ea5e649.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (PE1, PE2 multi-homed to same ES; PE3 standalone) + IXIA continuous BUM traffic (≥ 24 h)
65c049a31ea5e649.expectation:
Pass:    ESI label remains in platform label space throughout 24 h; `agg-eth 0` shows zero ingress BUM packets (forwarding entry correctly drops packets with ESI label); BUM traffic continuously delivered to DF PE (with ESI label) and non-ES PE3 (without ESI label); memory usage flat (≤ 5% drift); all replication and receive counters monotonically increase.
Fail-on: ESI label absent from platform label space; any ingress packets on `agg-eth 0` (split-horizon violation); BUM traffic loss or starvation at any remote PE; memory growth > 5%; any counter freeze, decrement, or reset.

65e94f87109bc582.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN instance (EVI 100) with P2MP MPLS LSP for BUM; PE1 advertises Inclusive Multicast route type 3 with PMSI Tunnel attribute (P2MP mLDP, label=5000); PE2 joins P2MP LSP; both PEs configure `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in `configuration/l2-services/evpn`; PE2 local MAC table empty (no 00:11:22:33:44:55 entry).
Action: From PE1, encapsulate and send three frames over P2MP LSP label 5000 to PE2: (a) broadcast MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF, (b) multicast MAC 01:00:5E:01:01:01, (c) unicast MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 (unknown to PE2). Capture on PE2's local AC.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-table evi 100` on PE2 (confirm 00:11:22:33:44:55 still absent); `show l2-services evpn statistics evi 100` (BUM Rx counter increments by 3); packet capture on PE2 AC shows all three frames flooded locally.
65e94f87109bc582.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 floods all three frames to local ACs; frame (c) with unicast MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 is treated as unknown unicast per RFC7432bis §12.2 MUST (not dropped); BUM Rx counter = 3.
Fail-on: Unicast MAC frame (c) dropped or not flooded; broadcast/multicast frames not delivered; BUM Rx counter ≠ 3; PE2 does not process packets received on P2MP LSP label 5000.

66040c1e12df9309.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT booted with factory-default config; navigate to `configuration / l2-services` mode.
Action: Create four EVPN instances, one per service type: `evpn EVPN-VB service-type vlan-based`, `evpn EVPN-VBU service-type vlan-bundle`, `evpn EVPN-VAB service-type vlan-aware-bundle`, `evpn EVPN-PB service-type port-based`. Issue `commit`. Verify EVPN config mode is accessible for one instance. Test removal: `no evpn EVPN-VB`, `commit`.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn` lists all four instances with correct service types; EVPN config mode accessible and functional; after `no evpn EVPN-VB`, only three instances remain in running-config.
66040c1e12df9309.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
66040c1e12df9309.expectation:
Pass:    All four service types (vlan-based, vlan-bundle, vlan-aware-bundle, port-based) create distinct EVPN instances; running-config shows `evpn <name> service-type <type>` for each; EVPN config mode reachable; `no evpn` removes only the specified instance
Fail-on: Any service type rejected, service type mismatch in running-config, EVPN config mode unreachable, or `no evpn` fails or affects wrong instance

664d9d36177cfa47.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT: loopback 192.0.2.1/32, BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE (`af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor`), create EVI 100 and 200 under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`, ensure `advertise-mac enable` (default).
Action: Activate both EVPN instances; DUT originates Type-3 (IMET) routes per RFC7432bis §11.1; on neighbor PE run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 detail` to capture both routes.
Verify: Decode each Type-3 NLRI (EVI 100, EVI 200); extract and compare: RD format, Originating Router IP Address, IP Address Length (bits), Ethernet Tag ID.
664d9d36177cfa47.expectation:
Pass:    Both Type-3 routes show: RD in valid format per §7 (ASN:nn or IP:nn), Originating Router IP = 192.0.2.1 in both EVIs (§11.1 MUST: "remain the same...across all EVIs"), IP Address Length = 32 bits (IPv4), all mandatory fields present.
Fail-on: Originating Router IP inconsistent between EVI 100 and 200, IP Address Length ≠ 32 for IPv4, RD format invalid, or Type-3 NLRI missing mandatory fields (RD, Originating Router IP, IP Address Length).

669d5ce10880f521.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (PE1=DF, PE2=non-DF on multi-homed ES1 ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99); local CE on each PE; configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`, `advertise-mac enable`, `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; IXIA scale rig connected to CEs generating 10 Gbps mixed traffic (64–1518B frames): 60% unicast known MAC, 30% BUM, 5% MPLS-encapsulated LT1 (per-MAC label), 5% MPLS-encapsulated LT2 (per-ES label).
Action: Inject traffic over 300s: (a) CE1→PE1→CE1 (local loopback, expect 100% drop); (b) CE2→PE1→CE1 via MACT (expect forward); (c) BUM from CE2→PE2(non-DF)→ES1 (expect drop); (d) LT1 traffic with valid MACT entry (expect forward); (e) LT1 traffic with MACT entry deleted mid-test (expect drop); (f) LT2 traffic to ES1 (expect forward via ES). Capture per-stream throughput, latency (avg/p99), loss.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn evpn1 mac-table` confirms MACT population; `show l2-services evpn evpn1 statistics` shows per-path forwarding/drop counters; IXIA reports validate throughput ≥9.8 Gbps on valid paths, latency ≤10 ms, 0% loss on valid paths, 100% drop on invalid paths (a, c, e).
669d5ce10880f521.expectation:
Pass:    Aggregate throughput ≥9.8 Gbps (98% line rate) on valid forwarding paths (b, d, f); per-stream latency ≤10 ms (p99); 0% packet loss on valid paths; 100% drop rate on invalid paths (local CE loopback, BUM to non-DF ES, LT1 with missing MACT); MACT/BUM database lookup latency ≤1 µs per vendor spec; no forwarding table overflow or control-plane impact.
Fail-on: Throughput <9.5 Gbps on valid paths; latency >10 ms (p99); packet loss >0.01% on valid paths; any frames forwarded on invalid paths (CE1→CE1 loopback detected in IXIA, BUM forwarded to non-DF ES1, LT1 forwarded with missing MACT entry); lookup latency >5 µs; MACT table overflow; control-plane CPU >80% sustained.

66e1923eec51dc5d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as BGP route reflector with VRF; configure PE2 as standard iBGP peer; attach IXIA to PE2; establish iBGP session between DUT and PE2.
Action: Under neighbor PE1's `af-l2vpn evpn` address family, configure `route-reflector-client`; establish iBGP session; IXIA sends L2 traffic to PE2, triggering EVPN type 2 MAC/IP advertisements from PE2 to DUT.
Verify: On PE1, inspect BGP L2VPN EVPN routes (e.g., `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and route details); confirm reflected routes include ORIGINATOR_ID attribute (PE2's router-id) and CLUSTER_LIST attribute (DUT's cluster-id).
66e1923eec51dc5d.equipment: DUT (route reflector) + PE1 (RR client) + PE2 (route originator) + IXIA (attached to PE2)
66e1923eec51dc5d.expectation:
Pass:    Reflected routes on PE1 contain ORIGINATOR_ID attribute equal to PE2's router-id and CLUSTER_LIST attribute containing DUT's cluster-id.
Fail-on: Reflected routes missing ORIGINATOR_ID or CLUSTER_LIST attributes, attribute values incorrect, or routes not reflected to client at all.

67583acf103bc5d3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure route-policy rp-test: deny MAC/IP routes with community 65000:999, permit all others. Configure BGP neighbor 192.0.2.1 under VRF customer-1, address family L2VPN EVPN.
Action: In the BGP neighbor's af-l2vpn evpn configuration context, attach route-policy rp-test to the inbound direction. Commit the configuration. Have neighbor 192.0.2.1 advertise 6 EVPN type-2 routes: 3 tagged with community 65000:999, 3 without.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn vrf customer-1` displays exactly 3 MAC/IP routes. `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` confirms none have community 65000:999. `show running-config routing bgp` shows route-policy rp-test attached inbound for neighbor 192.0.2.1's L2VPN EVPN address family.
67583acf103bc5d3.equipment: DUT + IXIA (acting as BGP neighbor advertising controlled EVPN routes)
67583acf103bc5d3.expectation:
Pass:    Inbound route-policy rp-test is active. Exactly 3 routes are installed in the EVPN RIB (those without community 65000:999). Routes with the denied community are filtered before installation.
Fail-on: All 6 routes appear in RIB (policy not applied), or 0 routes installed (policy blocks all), or policy attachment rejected, or running-config does not reference the policy.

67ca6161ce74523a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT (RD 65000:100, export-rt/import-rt 65000:100), enable BGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE, verify advertise-mac is enabled (default). Attach IXIA-emulated host to DUT access port in VLAN mapped to EVPN instance.
Action: Send Ethernet frame from IXIA host (MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, IP 10.1.1.10) to trigger local MAC learning. Capture BGP UPDATE on neighbor PE using packet analyzer or `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-ip detail`.
Verify: BGP Type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) route received at neighbor PE contains all mandatory fields: RD=65000:100, ESI (0 for single-homed or actual ESI), Ethernet Tag ID=0, MAC Address=00:11:22:33:44:55, IP Address=10.1.1.10, MPLS Label1 (LT1), MPLS L3 Label, Sequence Number, Route Targets=65000:100, Next Hop=DUT PE loopback IP. If MAC is static, verify Static Flag is set.
67ca6161ce74523a.expectation:
Pass:    Type-2 route contains all 11 mandatory fields (RD, ESI, ET ID, MAC, IP, Label1, L3 Label, Sequence Number, RTs, Static Flag if applicable), Next Hop equals DUT PE IP address, and neighbor PE installs the route in its EVPN table.
Fail-on: Any mandatory field missing or zero when it should have a value (e.g., RD=0:0, Label1=0 for non-per-EVPN-instance label allocation, RTs empty), Next Hop ≠ DUT PE IP, or neighbor PE rejects the route.

67f3be01768fec5a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS instance under `configuration / l2-services / vpls` with at least one attachment circuit (AC). Select a test MAC address (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55) and an interface (e.g., `x-eth 0/0/1`).
Action: Under the VPLS instance, configure the static MAC binding the test MAC to the interface. Commit. From IXIA, send Ethernet frames with destination MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 into a different AC of the same VPLS.
Verify: `show mac-address-table` or equivalent displays the static MAC entry with the configured interface and a 'static' or 'permanent' flag. IXIA captures confirm frames egress only on `x-eth 0/0/1`, not flooded to other ACs.
67f3be01768fec5a.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC entry appears in the MAC table with 'static' flag, bound to the specified interface; incoming frames to that MAC are forwarded only to that interface, not flooded
Fail-on: Static MAC not installed, frames to the static MAC are flooded to all ACs, or commit rejects the configuration

682df28d0c6d20d2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVI 100 with 'control-word enable' (configuration/l2-services/evpn) and MTU 1500. Configure neighbor PE with EVI 100, control-word disable, MTU 9000. Establish BGP EVPN peering (af-l2vpn evpn) and exchange EVPN routes with L2-Attr Extended Community.
Action: Wait for BGP route processing to detect L2 attribute mismatch (received C-flag=0 vs local enable, MTU 9000 vs 1500). Collect: show bgp evpn summary, show l2-services evpn evi 100 detail, show bgp evpn routes received-routes neighbor <peer-IP>, show running-config l2-services evpn control-word, show alarms | include 'L2-Attr|mismatch', show log messages filtered for RFC7432bis §7.11.2 violations.
Verify: Tech-support bundle contains received L2-Attr Extended Community (MTU=9000, C-flag=0), local config (control-word enable, MTU=1500), syslog entry indicating attribute mismatch per RFC7432bis §7.11.2 with reason (control-word and MTU both mismatched), and show l2-services output proving neighbor PE IP is NOT in nexthop/destination list for EVI 100.
682df28d0c6d20d2.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: received L2-Attr with non-zero MTU≠1500 and C-flag≠local control-word state, mismatch alarm/log citing RFC7432bis §7.11.2 mandatory checks, running-config showing local control-word enable and MTU 1500, and EVI 100 forwarding state excluding the neighbor PE IP from destination set per MUST NOT requirement.
Fail-on: Missing received L2-Attr extended community in BGP output, no mismatch alarm/log despite mismatched attributes, or neighbor PE incorrectly added to EVI 100 forwarding destinations (violates RFC7432bis §7.11.2 MUST NOT).

684d03d2cf861347.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VLAN-Aware Bundle EVPN (evpn evpn-name service-type vlan-aware-bundle) on two PEs with multihomed CE via Ethernet Segment (ethernet-segment on agg-eth); assign VLANs 10, 20, 30 to EVI; verify DF election uses VLAN 10 (numerically lowest tag per RFC7432bis §8.5) via show evpn ethernet-segment detail.
Action: IXIA sends continuous BUM traffic (broadcast/multicast, 100 pps) and unknown unicast (1000 pps) across all three VLANs for ≥ 24 hours; monitor DF role stability and no re-election events.
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment detail (DF role unchanged on same PE), show platform process memory (RSS/VSZ trend), show evpn mac (MAC count monotonic), IXIA RX counters (zero loss), syslog (no DF failover or election errors).
684d03d2cf861347.expectation:
Pass:    DF role stable on single PE for full 24 h; VLAN 10 confirmed in DF modulo function (lowest tag); BUM and unknown unicast forwarded by DF only; zero packet loss; memory RSS growth < 2%; MAC table and traffic counters monotonically increasing.
Fail-on: DF role flaps or splits across PEs; DF election uses VLAN ≠ 10 in modulo; packet loss > 0.01%; memory RSS growth > 5%; counter rollback, freeze, or wrap; DF election crash, hung state, or syslog errors.

686579c9b27da1b8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with agg-eth to dual-homed CE. On both PEs: enter "configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment", set "identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88", "load-balancing-mode single-active". Configure EVPN instance with LAG member. Wait es-waiting-time (default 3s).
Action: Verify Type-4 ESI route and Type-1 per-ES Ethernet A-D route via "show bgp evpn route-type 4" and "route-type 1". IXIA sends 1000 pps L2 BUM + unicast traffic through CE to remote destination. Check DF election and Tx counters on both PE core-facing interfaces. Reconfigure "load-balancing-mode all-active" on both PEs, resend traffic.
Verify: "show evpn ethernet-segment" displays mode (single-active → all-active), ESI 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88, DF status. Check Tx packet counters on both PE core-facing interfaces. Confirm IXIA Rx rate matches Tx (loss ≤ 0.1%).
686579c9b27da1b8.expectation:
Pass:    Single-active: only DF PE shows incrementing Tx counters; non-DF counters = 0. All-active: both PEs show incrementing Tx counters (load-balanced per flow hash). Type-4 ESI route present with ESI 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88; Type-1 per-ES A-D routes present from both PEs. Traffic loss ≤ 0.1% after es-waiting-time (3s default). Both modes operational per RFC7432bis ch.14.
Fail-on: Single-active: both PEs forward traffic (Tx counters > 0 on both) or neither forwards. All-active: only one PE forwards or zero PEs forward. Type-4 or Type-1 routes missing, or ESI value mismatch. Traffic loss > 0.1% during steady state. "show evpn ethernet-segment" does not reflect configured mode or DF status incorrect.

686bece36b1fe75b.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 (DF) and PE2 (Non-DF) dual-homed to CE1 via agg-eth interface with `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88; load-balancing-mode all-active`. PE3 is single-homed. EVI 100 configured with ingress replication for BUM. Verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` shows PE1 advertises ESI label L1.
Action: CE1 (IXIA-emulated) injects broadcast ARP into EVI 100 via LACP link; frame arrives at PE2 (Non-DF). PE2 performs ingress replication to PE1 and PE3. Capture MPLS packets at PE1 ingress (from PE2) and PE3 ingress (from PE2). Monitor PE1's agg-eth interface toward CE1 for egress traffic.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` confirms PE1 advertises ESI label L1. `show mpls forwarding-table label L1` on PE1 shows label programmed in platform label space with action preventing ES forwarding (e.g., pop-and-drop-to-ES or split-horizon flag). Packet capture at PE1: MPLS stack includes label L1. Packet capture at PE3: no ESI label in stack. `show interface agg-eth 1 counters` on PE1 shows zero egress during test. CE1 receives exactly one ARP reply (no loop).
686bece36b1fe75b.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs + IXIA (LACP CE emulation and packet capture)
686bece36b1fe75b.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 advertises ESI label L1 in Ethernet A-D per ES route-type 1. PE2 includes L1 in replicated BUM copy to PE1 (both on ES), omits ESI label in copy to PE3 (not on ES). PE1's MPLS forwarding table entry for L1 is programmed in platform label space with forwarding action that prevents ES egress. No BUM loop; CE1 receives broadcast once.
Fail-on: ESI label L1 missing from PE1's route-type 1 advertisement. PE2 omits L1 when replicating to PE1, or includes ESI label when replicating to PE3. Label L1 not programmed in PE1's platform label space, or forwarding entry allows forwarding to ES. PE1 forwards received labeled BUM onto agg-eth toward CE1 (loop). CE1 receives duplicate broadcasts.

68dff3acc0c5f405.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with Ethernet Segment under aggregate interface `agg-eth 1`; enter `interface agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment` mode; set `es-waiting-time 8` (non-default); commit.
Action: Issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 1 ethernet-segment` and `show ethernet-segment detail`.
Verify: Running-config contains `es-waiting-time 8`; operational show command displays 8-second waiting time. Test default: issue `no es-waiting-time`, commit, confirm config and operational state revert to 3 seconds.
68dff3acc0c5f405.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
68dff3acc0c5f405.expectation:
Pass:    `es-waiting-time 8` present in running-config; operational state shows 8 seconds; `no es-waiting-time` restores default 3 seconds in both running-config and show output.
Fail-on: Configured value not in running-config; operational state incorrect (not 8 sec, or not 3 sec after no command); default not restored; commit failure.

69491c8b4ea31d84.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with same ESI (identifier 0 type0-value 01.23.45.67.89.ab.cd.ef.01) on agg-eth; enable load-balancing-mode all-active and service-carving highest-random-weight on both; establish EVPN VXLAN with 500 VLANs; IXIA generates continuous L2 traffic (1 Gbps) to multi-homed destination MAC addresses across all VLANs.
Action: Run for ≥ 24 hours; monitor `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` every 15 min (log DF state per VLAN and advertised DF Election Extended Community 'DF Alg' = HRW); capture `show platform process memory` hourly.
Verify: Parse logs to confirm zero DF re-election events (no VLAN DF ownership changes absent topology event); confirm ES route type 4 consistently carries DF Election Extended Community with 'DF Alg' = 0x02 (HRW per RFC8584 ch.3); memory RSS growth <2% over 24 h; IXIA reports zero frame loss and monotonic Rx counters.
69491c8b4ea31d84.equipment: Two routers (both DUTs) + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
69491c8b4ea31d84.expectation:
Pass:    DF ownership per VLAN stable for 24 h (zero flaps); ES route type 4 always advertises DF Election Extended Community 'DF Alg' = 0x02 (HRW); memory growth <2%; zero packet loss; counters monotonic.
Fail-on: Any DF re-election without link/node failure; missing or incorrect DF Election Extended Community (wrong 'DF Alg' value or absent); memory RSS growth ≥2%; packet loss >0 or counter freeze/rollback.

69b7923dde8173ec.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-count 5 move-interval 180` in `configuration / l2-services / evpn`; configure syslog destination (e.g., `logging host <syslog-server-ip>`); attach host to EVI 100 via two different PEs to prime MAC-move condition.
Action: Inject same MAC address from two locations to trigger duplicate-MAC alarm (exceed move-count threshold); capture syslog feed at collector; then stop duplicate injection and wait for freeze-time expiry to clear alarm.
Verify: Run `show alarms` to confirm MAC-duplication alarm transitions from OFF→ON; syslog collector receives message with severity=warning (level 4) when alarm triggers; when condition clears, `show alarms` shows alarm OFF and syslog receives severity=informational (level 6).
69b7923dde8173ec.expectation:
Pass:    Alarm OFF→ON generates syslog with severity=warning (level 4); alarm ON→OFF generates syslog with severity=informational (level 6); both messages contain alarm identifier and timestamp.
Fail-on: No syslog message on alarm state change, wrong severity level (not warning/informational), missing alarm identifier in syslog, or alarm state not reflected in `show alarms`.

6a175faec775b4b5.action_steps:
Setup:  Create agg-eth 0 and enter configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment mode.
Action: Configure an ESI using the identifier command. Commit configuration. Verify identifier appears in `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`. Issue `no identifier` and commit. Verify type 4 ESI default is restored in running-config.
Verify: After initial commit, running-config displays the configured identifier statement. After `no identifier` commit, running-config shows type 4 as the default ESI (either implicit or explicit per implementation).
6a175faec775b4b5.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
6a175faec775b4b5.expectation:
Pass:    identifier command successfully configures an ESI value; configuration persists in running-config after commit; `no identifier` restores the documented type 4 default ESI.
Fail-on: identifier command rejects configuration; configured ESI doesn't persist after commit; `no identifier` fails to execute; or default after `no identifier` is not type 4.

6a27424996b2000c.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology with multihomed CE connected via agg-eth on both sides. On each PE: `interface agg-eth 1` → `ethernet-segment` → `identifier 0 AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:00:01` + `load-balancing-mode all-active`. Configure EVPN instance (EVI 100) MAC-VRF. IXIA emulates CE hosts.
Action: IXIA generates traffic from N unique source MACs (N = documented MAC mobility ceiling per `show evpn scale-limits` or vendor spec table). Cyclically move MAC attachment point PE1→PE2→PE1 at rate of 100 MACs/sec; run for ≥ 5 minutes to reach steady-state scale.
Verify: `show evpn mac` on both PEs shows sequence number incrementing on each move; `show bgp evpn route-type 2` displays MAC/IP routes with MAC Mobility extended community (seq > 0); `show evpn mac-mobility statistics` confirms move count; no duplicate MAC entries in table post-convergence.
6a27424996b2000c.expectation:
Pass:    N MACs remain mobile for 5 min without table corruption; sequence numbers advance correctly on each move (no stuck or regressed values); MAC convergence time ≤ 500 ms per move; CPU < 80%, memory delta < 10% from baseline.
Fail-on: MAC table inconsistency (same MAC with conflicting sequence numbers or duplicate entries across PEs after convergence); crash, OOM, or control-plane hang; sequence number not incrementing on move; per-MAC convergence > 1 sec or > 2× baseline; packet loss > 0.1% during move.

6a29310a593afe47.action_steps:
Setup:  Create an EVPN instance (`evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-based` under configuration / l2-services). Attach two ports to the EVPN domain.
Action: Enter configuration / l2-services / evpn test-evpn mode and issue `host mac-address-duplication-detection`. Commit the configuration.
Verify: `show running-config` includes the command under the EVPN instance. Generate traffic from IXIA with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 arriving on port 1, then send the same source MAC from port 2. Check `show evpn mac` and device logs for duplicate MAC detection events.
6a29310a593afe47.expectation:
Pass:    Command accepted and persisted in running-config; when the same MAC address appears from two different ports, DUT detects and logs the duplicate condition
Fail-on: Command rejected at commit, or duplicate MAC scenario (same MAC from multiple ports) produces no detection event or log entry

6a35b2245fa30983.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment` with minimal ES config (ESI, lacp system-id); do not set `es-waiting-time`.
Action: (1) Issue `show ethernet-segment detail` and record waiting-time field (must show 3 sec default). (2) Configure `es-waiting-time 3` under the ethernet-segment; verify `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` omits the line. (3) Configure `es-waiting-time 5`; verify line appears. (4) Issue `no es-waiting-time`; confirm reversion to 3 sec.
Verify: `show ethernet-segment detail` displays waiting-time value; `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` omits `es-waiting-time` when set to default, displays non-default values.
6a35b2245fa30983.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
6a35b2245fa30983.expectation:
Pass:    Unconfigured ES defaults to 3 sec per `show ethernet-segment detail`; explicit `es-waiting-time 3` omitted from `show running-config` (idempotent); non-default values (e.g., 5) appear in config; `no es-waiting-time` restores 3 sec.
Fail-on: Default ≠ 3 sec, or explicit config of default (3 sec) appears in running-config, or `no` form fails to restore 3 sec, or command rejected under ethernet-segment mode.

6a6c720d1dbc2488.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT has EVPN instance evpn-1 configured (vlan-based service-type), BGP neighbor relationship established with PE2, IXIA sends L2 frames to generate at least one learned MAC on DUT EVI. Default state: advertise-mac not explicitly configured (default = enable).
Action: (1) Capture BGP routes on PE2 via `show bgp l2vpn evpn` — expect RT-2 (MAC/IP) routes from DUT. (2) On DUT: `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-1 / no advertise-mac`, commit. (3) Re-capture BGP routes on PE2 — expect zero MAC routes from DUT. (4) On DUT: `advertise-mac`, commit. (5) Re-capture BGP routes on PE2.
Verify: `show running-config | include advertise-mac` reflects current setting; PE2 BGP table shows presence/absence of RT-2 routes matching DUT's learned MACs.
6a6c720d1dbc2488.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + neighbor PE
6a6c720d1dbc2488.expectation:
Pass:    With `advertise-mac` (or absent = default enable): PE2 receives RT-2 routes for DUT's local MACs within 5 seconds of MAC learning. With `no advertise-mac`: PE2 BGP table has zero MAC routes from DUT, existing routes withdrawn within BGP hold-time.
Fail-on: Command rejected, or `advertise-mac` enabled but PE2 never receives RT-2 routes, or `no advertise-mac` but DUT still advertises MAC routes, or config does not persist after commit.

6a86bd52e8e28a63.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 100 (RD 65000:100) on DUT with loopback0 192.0.2.1/32 (IPv4) as PE identifier. Establish iBGP EVPN session using IPv6 next-hop (af-l2vpn evpn) to neighbor PE. Configure ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on ethernet1/1 for all-active multi-homing. Enable advertise-mac and unknown-mac-flooding.
Action: DUT advertises IMET (Type 3) and Ethernet A-D (Type 1/4) routes with IPv6 next-hop but IPv4 Originating Router IP. On neighbor PE, capture BGP EVPN updates and parse Originating Router IP field vs. next-hop field. Send BUM traffic from IXIA through ethernet1/1.
Verify: Neighbor PE BGP table: IMET route RD=65000:100, Originating Router IP=192.0.2.1 (IPv4), next-hop=IPv6. DUT show evpn routes: all route types (1,2,3,4) use Originating Router IP=192.0.2.1 across all EVIs. IXIA receives replicated BUM traffic. No duplicate MAC alarms.
6a86bd52e8e28a63.expectation:
Pass:    IMET route RD set per RFC7432bis §7; Originating Router IP=192.0.2.1 (IPv4 loopback), independent of IPv6 next-hop; same IP in all EVPN route types across all EVIs; multi-homing operational (Type 1/4 present, BUM traffic replicated).
Fail-on: Originating Router IP ≠ 192.0.2.1, set to zero, or inconsistent across route types/EVIs; Originating Router IP changes based on next-hop type; RD incorrect; multi-homing broken (ESI routes absent, duplicate MAC counters >0); BUM traffic not replicated.

6a99523512e1c120.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on PE1 (DUT) and PE2 with same EVPN instance and DF algorithm set to non-revertive mode (Don't Preempt capability enabled); PE1 initially elected as DF for all Ethernet Tags.
Action: Administratively shut down PE1's ES interface or disconnect ES link; verify PE2 becomes DF; start IXIA sending BUM frames (unknown unicast dst MAC) into ES; bring PE1's ES interface back up with original preference intact.
Verify: Issue `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 detail` on both PEs; inspect BGP EVPN routes (Type 4) from PE1 for DF Election Extended Community with Don't Preempt bit set; capture BUM frame forwarding on IXIA (frames continue via PE2, zero duplication); confirm PE2 remains DF per `show evpn instance <name> df-election`.
6a99523512e1c120.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 remains DF for all Ethernet Tags after PE1 returns; PE1 stays non-DF; PE1's Type-4 route carries DF Election Extended Community with Don't Preempt capability bit set (RFC9785 §4.3); BUM traffic stable through PE2 with zero loss or duplication.
Fail-on: PE1 preempts PE2 and becomes DF again (revertive behavior); DF Election Extended Community missing Don't Preempt bit; BUM traffic loss >0.1% or any duplication during PE1 return; split-brain (both PEs claim DF role for same Ethernet Tag).

6a9cb0e420154550.action_steps:
Setup:  On PE1 (DUT) and PE2, configure interface agg-eth with ethernet-segment identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88, load-balancing-mode all-active; configure l2-services evpn instance; establish BGP EVPN peering; IXIA ports A+B connect to PE1+PE2 LAG (CE), port C sends remote traffic.
Action: IXIA CE advertises MAC M1 via LAG; verify show evpn route type 4 lists both PE1 and PE2 as attached VTEPs; IXIA port C sends 10,000 packets (100 pps) destined to M1.
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment (ES Up on PE1, PE2; DF/NDF roles assigned); show evpn route type 4 (ES route with PE1+PE2 VTEP IPs); show evpn route type 2 M1; IXIA CE receives traffic via both PE paths.
6a9cb0e420154550.expectation:
Pass:    Type 4 Ethernet Segment route advertised by both PE1 and PE2 listing all attached PEs per RFC7432bis §8.4; ES Up; traffic from remote to M1 delivered via both PE1 and PE2 (aliasing active); ≥9,990/10,000 packets delivered (≥99.9%).
Fail-on: Type 4 route missing, incomplete (<2 PEs listed), or not received; ES Down on either PE; traffic to M1 uses only PE1 path (aliasing broken); packet loss >0.1%; show commands return error or ES status mismatch.

6aa60b48d2f1c590.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs in the same Ethernet Segment on interface `agg-eth 0` using `ethernet-segment`, with a VLAN-aware EVPN instance (`evpn evpn-name service-type vlan-aware-bundle`, `auto-discovery`) and 10 VLANs (Ethernet Tags 1-10). Configure Highest-Preference DF election on both PEs with an identical local policy override on each PE to satisfy RFC9785 §4.2 consistency requirement; verify BGP session is up and Route Type 4 (ES) and Route Type 1 (AD per-EVI) are advertised.
Action: From IXIA, send BUM traffic on all 10 VLANs to the multi-homed ES; use tcpdump to capture Route Type 4 from both PEs and extract the DF Election Extended Community Preference field; monitor both PEs' egress interfaces with IXIA to identify which PE forwards BUM traffic for each VLAN.
Verify: Route Type 4 from both PEs carries identical Preference values (matching the local policy override); the same PE is DF for all 10 Ethernet Tags; BUM traffic for all VLANs egresses only from the DF PE (non-DF does not forward).
6aa60b48d2f1c590.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise identical overridden Preference in Route Type 4; same PE elected DF for all 10 Ethernet Tags; BUM forwarding exclusive to DF PE; local policy override consistent across PEs per RFC9785 §4.2 MUST.
Fail-on: PEs advertise different Preference values in Route Type 4; different PEs elected DF for different Ethernet Tags; BUM traffic duplicated (both PEs forward) or blackholed (neither forwards); DF election split-brain.

6af1bc4ba7b994c1.action_steps:
Setup:  Create `agg-eth 1` under `configuration / interface`; commit. Capture the default LACP system MAC from `show lacp interface agg-eth 1` (should equal the LAG's own MAC address).
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1`, issue `lacp-system-mac 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE`, commit. Verify the new MAC. Then issue `no lacp-system-mac`, commit. Test rejection of invalid values: `lacp-system-mac not-a-mac`, `lacp-system-mac ZZ:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF` (invalid hex), `lacp-system-mac 11:22:33` (truncated format).
Verify: After setting 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE: `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` contains `lacp-system-mac 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE` and `show lacp interface agg-eth 1` displays system MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE. After `no` form: running-config has no explicit `lacp-system-mac` line; `show lacp` displays the default LAG MAC address. Each invalid value triggers a parse error at CLI input time.
6af1bc4ba7b994c1.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
6af1bc4ba7b994c1.expectation:
Pass:    Valid MAC (00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE) accepted, visible in both running-config and LACP operational state. `no lacp-system-mac` removes config line and restores default LAG MAC in operational state. All three invalid formats (literal string, invalid hex digit ZZ, truncated MAC) rejected with parse-time CLI error messages.
Fail-on: Invalid MAC format silently accepted or committed; `no` form does not restore default LAG MAC; valid MAC accepted in config but not reflected in LACP protocol state; DUT crashes, hangs, or leaves partial/corrupt config entry.

6b032bafbb90d455.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS instance vpls-200 (config / l2-services / vpls vpls-200), attach interface ge-0/0/1 as AC, bring VPLS up. Confirm `show vpls vpls-200` shows state=up.
Action: Under config / l2-services / vpls vpls-200, execute `mac-address-static 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01 interface ge-0/0/1`; commit. Execute `copy running-config startup-config`; execute `reload` and wait for full boot.
Verify: Post-reload, `show running-config` section l2-services / vpls vpls-200 contains the mac-address-static line unchanged; `show vpls vpls-200 mac-table` lists 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01 with type=static and interface ge-0/0/1.
6b032bafbb90d455.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
6b032bafbb90d455.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01 persists in running-config across reload and is automatically re-installed in VPLS vpls-200 MAC table as static entry on ge-0/0/1.
Fail-on: Static MAC config absent post-reload, or present in config but not installed in MAC table (type=static check fails), or requires manual re-apply.

6b2f9537d6bba5d1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure identical Ethernet Segments on DUT and neighbor PE (both on `agg-eth 0` with matching ESI). Attach to an EVPN instance. Do not configure `load-balancing-mode`.
Action: Commit configuration on both PEs; bring up the ES. Send bidirectional traffic from IXIA through the multi-homed segment.
Verify: `show running-config` on both PEs omits `load-balancing-mode` under ethernet-segment context; verify only the elected designated-forwarder forwards traffic while non-DF blocks; operational state reports single-active mode.
6b2f9537d6bba5d1.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs default to single-active mode; only DF forwards traffic; non-DF blocks; running-config omits the parameter.
Fail-on: Both PEs forward simultaneously (all-active behavior), or running-config shows explicit default value (non-idempotent), or mode differs between PEs.

6b48634bd714c274.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with MP2MP tunnel (RSVP-TE P2MP or mLDP) and multi-homed Ethernet Segment ESI 01:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:01; enable DCB label allocation for ESI labels (per RFC7432bis §19); establish BGP EVPN session between DUT and IXIA.
Action: IXIA injects four malformed BGP EVPN route variants: (1) ESI Label route (type 4) with label value 10 (reserved MPLS range 0-15), (2) ESI Label with value 1048576 (exceeds 2^20-1 max), (3) ESI Label extended community with reserved bits set to 0xFF, (4) PMSI Tunnel Attribute in Inclusive Multicast route with DCB label field = 0 (reserved).
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` (malformed routes not installed or marked invalid), `show evpn ethernet-segment` (DUT ES state unaffected), `show log | grep "DCB\|ESI"` (syslog entries for each rejection), `show system counters protocol-errors evpn` (error counter increments by 4), `show bgp neighbors` (session remains Established).
6b48634bd714c274.expectation:
Pass:    All four malformed DCB label variants rejected; protocol-error counter increments by 4; syslog contains distinct entries "Invalid ESI Label from <IXIA-IP>: label=10 (reserved range)", "label=1048576 exceeds 20-bit space", "reserved bits non-zero in ESI Label extended community", "PMSI DCB label=0 invalid"; BGP session stable; DUT Ethernet Segment remains operational with correct DCB label in use.
Fail-on: Any malformed label accepted into RIB or installed in forwarding table; DUT crash, memory leak, or BGP session flap; missing or incomplete error logging; malformed label forwarded to downstream peers; split-horizon filtering broken due to invalid ESI label processing.

6b5cb52fda697c07.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure aggregated Ethernet interface `agg-eth 0` on the DUT; enter `ethernet-segment` configuration mode within that interface context.
Action: (a) Configure ESI type 0 with a valid 9-octet hex value: `identifier 0 01.23.45.67.89.ab.cd.ef.00`. Commit and verify. (b) Test invalid type 0 values: non-hex characters (`identifier 0 01.GG.45.67.89.ab.cd.ef.00`), incorrect octet count (`identifier 0 01.23.45`), and extra octets (`identifier 0 01.23.45.67.89.ab.cd.ef.00.11`). (c) Issue `no identifier` to restore default (type 4).
Verify: Valid type 0 identifier appears in `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` and `show ethernet-segment` output; invalid formats are rejected at parse time with CLI error messages; `no identifier` removes explicit config and restores default ESI type.
6b5cb52fda697c07.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console or SSH)
6b5cb52fda697c07.expectation:
Pass:    Type 0 identifier accepts exactly 9 octets in hex format xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx (0-9, a-f); invalid octet counts, non-hex chars, and malformed input rejected before commit with parse errors; `no identifier` restores default configuration.
Fail-on: Valid 9-octet hex rejected; invalid format accepted and committed; ESI not readable via show commands; `no identifier` fails to restore default; or feature crashes on malformed input.

6b9d2f37b8da6946.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN instance "evpn-test" (vlan-based, VNI 1000, RD/RT configured), interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 in bridge domain; NETCONF client authenticated; neighbor PE with BGP EVPN peering established.
Action: Via NETCONF, configure static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 on interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 in evpn-test using `<mac-address-static>` YANG element; commit.
Verify: CLI `show evpn mac evpn-name evpn-test` shows MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 with static flag; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows Type-2 route for this MAC with VNI label; NETCONF get-config returns `<mac-address-static>` entry; neighbor PE receives BGP Type-2 route.
6b9d2f37b8da6946.expectation:
Pass:    NETCONF commit succeeds; static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 present in both NETCONF datastore and CLI output bound to GigabitEthernet0/0/1; BGP EVPN Type-2 route advertised to neighbor with correct MAC, local label, and ESI 0.
Fail-on: NETCONF schema error or commit failure, static MAC absent from CLI after commit, no Type-2 route in BGP table, or NETCONF/CLI views diverge on MAC address or interface binding.

6c4fa16f6a2f24c0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS instance; add interface `x-eth 0/0/1` as a member. Enter mode `configuration / l2-services / vpls`.
Action: Issue `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`; commit. Issue `no mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55`; commit.
Verify: After first commit, inspect VPLS MAC table for static entry 00:11:22:33:44:55 on x-eth 0/0/1. After second commit, verify entry removed. Check `show running-config` for presence/absence of static MAC config.
6c4fa16f6a2f24c0.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
6c4fa16f6a2f24c0.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 is installed and does not age out; cleanly removed via `no` form.
Fail-on: MAC not installed, survives `no` command, or ages out (dynamic treatment).

6cab505d3a256e74.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT (AS 65001) with BGP neighbor 10.0.0.2 in `configuration / routing / bgp 65001 / vrf default / neighbor 10.0.0.2 / af-l2vpn evpn`. Baseline: without `allow-as-in`, confirm neighbor's EVPN route with AS_PATH [65001, 65002] is rejected (AS_PATH loop check active).
Action: Issue `allow-as-in 2` under af-l2vpn evpn; commit. Neighbor advertises three EVPN Type-2 routes with AS_PATH: (a) [65001, 65002], (b) [65001, 65001, 65002], (c) [65001, 65001, 65001, 65002].
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms routes (a) and (b) accepted (1-2 occurrences ≤ count); route (c) rejected (3 occurrences > count). `show running-config` displays `allow-as-in 2` under the neighbor af-l2vpn evpn context.
6cab505d3a256e74.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (BGP EVPN speaker emulation)
6cab505d3a256e74.expectation:
Pass:    Routes with local AS appearing ≤2 times in AS_PATH installed in BGP RIB; routes with 3+ occurrences rejected; running-config persists the setting
Fail-on: Routes with ≤2 local-AS occurrences rejected (bypass not active), routes with 3+ occurrences accepted (count not enforced), or commit rejected

6ce9b64445335929.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with ESI multi-homing (same ethernet-segment identifier type 0, load-balancing-mode single-active). Configure MAC-VRF with EVPN instance on both PEs. IXIA generates traffic from MAC M1 alternating attachment points to trigger MAC mobility (sequence number increments in RT-2 routes).
Action: While MAC mobility is active (M1 flapping, sequence numbers incrementing), kill the EVPN control-plane process on DUT (platform debug command: e.g., kill -9 <l2vpnd-pid> or equivalent). Observe MAC mobility sequence number before crash.
Verify: Process auto-restarts. Issue 'show evpn mac-table', 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2' to confirm MAC M1 present with sequence number ≥ pre-crash value. RT-2 routes re-advertised with correct MAC mobility sequence. IXIA traffic resumes.
6ce9b64445335929.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (same ESI) + IXIA traffic gen (process-kill harness)
6ce9b64445335929.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts within es-waiting-time (default 3 sec). MAC M1 present in mac-table with sequence number ≥ pre-crash value. RT-2 routes contain MAC mobility extended community with correct sequence. Traffic loss ≤ es-waiting-time + 1 sec.
Fail-on: Process does not restart, MAC M1 missing or stuck with stale sequence number, duplicate MAC entries on both PEs, or traffic loss > 4 sec.

6d0f010f2f19220d.action_steps:
Setup:  IXIA-emulated PE1 advertises MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 as sticky via EVPN Route Type 2 with MAC Mobility ext-community (static=1, seq=0); DUT learns via `af-l2vpn evpn` neighbor and installs route.
Action: IXIA injects: (1) BGP UPDATE same MAC, static=1 but seq=7 (violates §15.2 seq=0 for sticky); (2) BGP UPDATE corrupted MAC Mobility ext-community TLV (length 0x0A vs. valid 0x08); (3) Ethernet frame sticky MAC as SA from DUT local AC (triggers §15.2 local learning conflict).
Verify: `show bgp evpn route type 2` excludes malformed routes; `show log` operator alert for sticky MAC conflict; `show bgp statistics` error counter >0; `show l2-services mac-table` sticky MAC from PE1 unchanged; conflicting-SA frames dropped if configured; no crash.
6d0f010f2f19220d.expectation:
Pass:    Malformed BGP UPDATEs rejected (not in RIB); BGP error counter >0; syslog operator alert present for sticky MAC conflict (malformed seq or local learning); legitimate sticky MAC route from PE1 intact; optional drop for conflicting-SA frames; no crash.
Fail-on: Malformed route installed in RIB; no operator alert in syslog; crash or BGP session drop; sticky MAC route withdrawn or overwritten; conflicting-SA frames forwarded without alert.

6d542b73016d2778.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE configured with BGP ASN, reachable loopbacks, and EVPN address-family enabled under `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn`. BGP session established.
Action: On DUT, issue `capability orf-prefix-list send` (or `receive`, `both`, or `route-refresh`). Commit. Perform soft-reset or wait for capability re-negotiation: `clear bgp neighbor <neighbor-ip> soft`.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `capability` line under the neighbor af-l2vpn evpn stanza. `show bgp neighbor <neighbor-ip> capabilities` (or equivalent) lists the negotiated capability: ORF SEND/RECEIVE or Route-Refresh in the advertised/received capability table.
6d542b73016d2778.expectation:
Pass:    Running-config persists `capability {orf-prefix-list send|receive|both|route-refresh}`; `show bgp neighbor` output confirms the capability was advertised by DUT and acknowledged by peer (appears in negotiated capabilities list). BGP session remains ESTABLISHED.
Fail-on: Commit rejected; BGP session drops to IDLE/ACTIVE after config; capability absent from negotiated list; or peer logs unsupported-capability notification.

6d7f1bf9b972d210.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance with ingress-replication BUM mode; establish iBGP peering with neighbor PE; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` and `advertise-mac enable` on DUT; start packet capture on PE for BGP updates.
Action: Activate the EVPN instance on DUT (triggers Type 3 Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag Route advertisement); capture BGP UPDATE messages containing NLRI AFI=25/SAFI=70 route-type 3.
Verify: Decode captured Type 3 route; confirm PMSI Tunnel attribute (code 22) is present with Flags=0x00, Tunnel Type=6 (Ingress Replication), MPLS Label field populated with LT3 label value, Tunnel Identifier=DUT loopback/RD originator IP address.
6d7f1bf9b972d210.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (iBGP peer) + packet capture (Wireshark/tcpdump on PE or mirror port)
6d7f1bf9b972d210.expectation:
Pass:    Every Type 3 route from DUT contains PMSI Tunnel attribute; attribute fields match RFC6514 §5 Ingress Replication encoding (Type=6, Flags=0, Label=valid LT3, Tunnel ID=originating router IP).
Fail-on: Type 3 route lacks PMSI Tunnel attribute, OR Tunnel Type ≠ 6, OR Flags ≠ 0x00, OR Tunnel Identifier ≠ DUT originator IP, OR MPLS Label field is zero/invalid.

6db87e0cde3fd56a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed Ethernet Segment on PE1 (DUT) and PE2: create EVPN instance via `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based`, enable `auto-discovery`, assign ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 to CE-facing interface on both PEs; establish iBGP EVPN peering using `af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor config; set RD/RT with `import-rt` / `export-rt`.
Action: Activate the multi-homed ES; capture type-1 routes on both PEs using `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99`; parse MP_REACH_NLRI for ET ID (expect 0xFFFFFFFF), MPLS Label (expect 0), and ESI Label Extended Community value; inject mismatch by altering PE2's EVPN label allocation or manually advertising a type-1 route with different ESI Label.
Verify: Confirm ESI Label Extended Community matches across all type-1 routes for the ES when initially converged (RFC7432bis ch.8.2.1); upon mismatch injection, verify alarm appears in `show alarms` (e.g., "Mismatched Configuration" or "ESI Label Mismatch"); check that mismatched route exists in BGP RIB (`show bgp l2vpn evpn`) but is excluded from active Ethernet Segment state (`show evpn ethernet-segment`); send bidirectional IXIA traffic via CE to confirm split-horizon filtering still functions; resolve mismatch (synchronize labels) and verify alarm clears.
6db87e0cde3fd56a.expectation:
Pass:    All type-1 routes for ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 carry identical ESI Label Extended Community value per RFC7432bis ch.8.2.1 MUST; no alarm when consistent; when mismatch injected, alarm raised and mismatched route saved in BGP RIB but not installed in forwarding/aliasing tables; split-horizon filtering remains operational (no looping); alarm clears when all routes converge to same ESI Label.
Fail-on: Type-1 routes with different ESI Labels do not trigger alarm; mismatched route is installed in active Ethernet Segment state and used for aliasing/split-horizon; alarm persists after label values synchronized; IXIA traffic loops via multi-homed CE.

6e83bac9941c7a2f.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT (PE1) and neighbor PE2 multi-homed to CE via ESI Type-1 (auto-derives ES-Import RT from 6-octet prefix). IXIA simulates route reflector with RFC4684 RT Constraint enabled, initially not advertising interest in the derived ES-Import RT.
Action: Configure ESI on DUT (`evpn` service, `auto-discovery`, derived `import-rt` under `l2-services/vpls/auto-discovery`). DUT advertises Type 4 ES route with ES-Import RT EC (0x06:0x02). On IXIA, send RT Constraint route (AFI=1, SAFI=132) containing the ES-Import RT value. Capture BGP on DUT↔IXIA link.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 esi <value>` on IXIA/PE3. Before RT Constraint match: zero Type 4 routes from DUT. After RT Constraint UPDATE: Type 4 route appears. Pcap confirms RT Constraint UPDATE precedes Type 4 route advertisement per RFC4684 filtering.
6e83bac9941c7a2f.expectation:
Pass:    IXIA PE3 does NOT receive DUT's Type 4 ES route when RT Constraint excludes ES-Import RT. After IXIA advertises RT Constraint including ES-Import RT, DUT sends Type 4 route within BGP advertisement interval. Withdraw RT Constraint: Type 4 route withdrawn.
Fail-on: IXIA receives Type 4 ES route despite RT Constraint excluding ES-Import RT, or DUT fails to send Type 4 route when RT Constraint explicitly includes ES-Import RT (RFC4684 procedures not applied).

6e84bd0fb9e08f01.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that route resolution after control-plane restart correctly distinguishes reserved ESI (0/MAX-ESI) requiring only MAC/IP Advertisement route from non-reserved ESI requiring both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes before installing forwarding state.
Method: Configure neighbor PEs with single-homed (ESI=0) and multi-homed (non-reserved ESI with `identifier 0 <type0-value>`) segments, kill DUT's EVPN process under traffic, capture route re-advertisement timing via debug logs, verify forwarding state installs per RFC mandate.
Setup: Neighbor PE1 has single-homed segment (ESI=0) and multi-homed segment (shared ESI with PE2 using `identifier 0 <type0-value>`). DUT receives MAC/IP Advertisement routes and Ethernet A-D per ES routes from both PEs. IXIA traffic targets learned MACs on both segment types.
Action: Enable BGP EVPN debug on DUT. Identify EVPN process via `show platform process` and kill it. Monitor route re-reception: PEs re-advertise MAC/IP Advertisement routes (both ESI types) and Ethernet A-D per ES routes (non-reserved ESI). Track `show evpn mac` output during recovery.
Verify: `show evpn mac` confirms ESI=0 MACs appear immediately after MAC/IP Advertisement route (no wait for Ethernet A-D per ES). Non-reserved ESI MACs appear only after both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes received. Debug logs confirm sequence. Process restarts ≤ 5 s; traffic recovers ≤ 30 s; `show alarms` clears.
6e84bd0fb9e08f01.expectation:
Pass: ESI=0 MACs install on MAC/IP Advertisement alone; non-reserved ESI MACs install only after both route types; process restarts ≤ 5 s; traffic recovers ≤ 30 s; interruption ≤ 1 s.
Fail-on: Non-reserved ESI MACs install before Ethernet A-D per ES routes, or ESI=0 MACs wait unnecessarily, or process fails to restart or exceeds 5 s, or traffic recovery exceeds 30 s or interruption exceeds 1 s.

6e91c51a9f819ec9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with Ethernet Segment (ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11) on interface; set DF election algorithm (0=Default, 1=HRW, 2=Highest-Pref, 3=Lowest-Pref) and DF preference value (test default 32767 and custom value in 0–65535 range); establish BGP EVPN peering to trigger ES Route (type 4) advertisement.
Action: Verify type-4 route advertised via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4`; flap ES interface to trigger DF re-election (generates syslog); execute `show tech-support` command.
Verify: Parse tech-support bundle for: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` output (RD, ESI, Originating Router IP), ES-Import RT Extended Community (Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x02, high-order 6 octets matching configured ESI), DF Election Extended Community (algorithm 0–3, preference 0–65535), running-config showing ESI/DF configuration, ES operational state (DF role, local DF winner), and syslog entries for DF election events.
6e91c51a9f819ec9.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` with all mandatory fields (RD, ESI, Originating Router IP, ES-Import RT 0x06:0x02 derived from ESI high-order 6 octets, DF Election EC with algorithm field 0–3 and preference field showing configured value or default 32767), running-config with ESI and DF election settings, ES state showing DF role and election result, syslog entries timestamped to interface flap showing DF re-election.
Fail-on: Missing type-4 route section in tech-support, DF preference value outside 0–65535 range, ES-Import RT not matching ESI derivation rule (high-order 6 octets), DF Election EC missing or showing invalid algorithm (>3), no ESI in running-config, or missing DF operational state.

6ea0c285f0cd350f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure LAG on DUT with `interface agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 / load-balancing-mode all-active`; configure identical ESI on 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper); both PEs connect to same CE; EVPN instance active, BGP session up; IXIA sends bidirectional L2 traffic through CE to validate active-active forwarding.
Action: Shut down DUT uplink or clear `clear bgp l2vpn evpn` to trigger Fast Convergence; capture BGP UPDATEs on wire; monitor IXIA traffic counters for packet loss and convergence time.
Verify: Wireshark/tcpdump captures Ethernet A-D per ES route (route type 4) with withdraw flag from DUT per RFC7432bis §8.2; 3rd-party PE processes mass withdraw (check `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on Cisco or equivalent); `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows ESI down on DUT, up on 3rd-party PE; IXIA reports convergence time and loss.
6ea0c285f0cd350f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT sends single Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 4) with withdraw flag for the ESI; 3rd-party PE accepts and converges; traffic fails over to 3rd-party PE within 3s (default `es-waiting-time`) + BGP propagation delay (<1s); IXIA measures ≤50 ms traffic interruption and zero sustained loss.
Fail-on: No type-4 route sent, 3rd-party PE logs BGP error or ignores update, convergence >5s, or IXIA reports >1% packet loss after 10s.

6ea9edf75d54e0c8.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish BGP session between DUT and IXIA-emulated PE; configure `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor <IXIA-peer>`; verify session reaches ESTABLISHED state.
Action: IXIA injects BGP UPDATE with MP_REACH_NLRI containing EVPN route type 2, where the final NLRI's Length field = 1 (violates 2-octet minimum per §7). Repeat with UPDATE where NLRI Length field = 20 but actual encoded NLRI is 15 octets (length inconsistency per §7.14.1 third MUST).
Verify: Capture BGP NOTIFICATION message sent by DUT (UPDATE Message Error subcode); poll `show bgp neighbor <IXIA-peer>` to confirm state = IDLE; check `show bgp evpn statistics` for malformed-NLRI or parse-error counter increment.
6ea9edf75d54e0c8.equipment: DUT + IXIA (with BGP protocol emulation and UPDATE message crafting capability)
6ea9edf75d54e0c8.expectation:
Pass:    DUT resets BGP session ≤ 5 seconds after receiving malformed NLRI; BGP NOTIFICATION with UPDATE Error code sent; session state = IDLE; parse-error counter increments by 1 per malformed UPDATE.
Fail-on: Session remains ESTABLISHED after malformed NLRI received; no NOTIFICATION sent; DUT processes invalid NLRI (e.g., installs route to RIB); parse-error counter remains 0.

6eb959658e23d656.action_steps:
Setup:  On DUT agg-eth interface: configure ethernet-segment with identifier 0 <9-octet-hex> and load-balancing-mode single-active (default); configure identical ES on neighbor PE; establish BGP EVPN peering; create EVI/BD on both PEs attached to ES; verify Type-1 routes advertised/imported and DF election complete after es-waiting-time (default 3 sec).
Action: On DUT and remote PE, execute tech-support collection command after ES is operationally up and Type-1 routes are exchanged; archive outputs.
Verify: Extract tech-support archives; verify presence of: show evpn ethernet-segment (ES in Single-Active mode, DF role), show evpn route type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES routes), show evpn route detail (ESI Label ext-community redundancy-mode=1), show running-config (load-balancing-mode single-active or default), syslog (ES operational events, DF election, es-waiting-time expiry), and on remote PE: show evpn ethernet-segment confirming ES deduced as Single-Active from imported routes.
6eb959658e23d656.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support from DUT contains: ES config with load-balancing-mode single-active (or default), Type-1 routes, ESI Label ext-community redundancy-mode=1, DF election logs; remote PE tech-support contains: imported Type-1 routes with same ext-community, show evpn ethernet-segment output confirming ES is marked Single-Active (deduced from redundancy-mode=1).
Fail-on: Tech-support missing show evpn ethernet-segment, missing Type-1 routes, ESI Label ext-community absent or redundancy-mode ≠ 1, or remote PE ES database shows mode as All-Active/unknown instead of Single-Active (failed to deduce correct mode per RFC7432bis §14.1.1).

6ed918d1dbc8d5f1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT and neighbor PE) in same EVPN instance with shared VLAN 100 and route-target 65000:100. On DUT: `configuration / l2-services / evpn` → `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300`.
Action: Using IXIA, send traffic with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from port attached to DUT (move 1), then from port attached to neighbor PE (move 2), alternating 6 times within 180 seconds to exceed the N=5 threshold. Capture BGP session between DUT and route reflector.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-table` on DUT confirms MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 is marked duplicate/frozen. BGP capture shows DUT stops sending BGP UPDATE messages (NLRI=MAC/IP Advertisement route, MAC=00:11:22:33:44:55) after 6th move. `show log` or syslog collector confirms operator alert for duplicate MAC. Verify move-interval and move-count are configurable (test with M=180, N=5 and M=300, N=3).
6ed918d1dbc8d5f1.expectation:
Pass:    After 6th MAC move within 180s, DUT detects duplication, logs operator alert (syslog/SNMP trap), withdraws or stops sending BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes for 00:11:22:33:44:55, and freezes MAC for 300s. M (move-interval) and N (move-count) are configurable per RFC MUST.
Fail-on: DUT continues advertising duplicate MAC in BGP after threshold (N=5 moves in M=180s) exceeded, no operator alert logged, MAC not frozen, sequence number increments beyond N, or move-interval/move-count not configurable.

6f41001a99d9dd86.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance via `configuration / l2-services / evpn <name> service-type vlan-aware-bundle`; verify auto-generated RD shows Type 1 with loopback 0 IPv4 (e.g., `1:10.0.0.1:<instance>`) via `show l2-services evpn <name>`; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; IXIA injects L2 traffic through EVPN instance for ≥ 1 minute.
Action: Modify loopback 0 IPv4 address (`configuration / interface loopback 0 / ipv4 address <new-ip>/32`) while traffic flows.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn <name>` displays new RD with Type 1 + new loopback IP within ≤ 1 s; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms routes withdrawn and re-advertised with new RD; IXIA reports packet loss.
6f41001a99d9dd86.expectation:
Pass:    RD auto-regenerates to Type 1 format `1:<new_loopback_ip>:<instance_number>` within ≤ 1 s; BGP EVPN routes updated with new RD; zero packet loss during transition; spec MUST: "Type Field: 1, Administrator subfield: the IPv4 address configured on loopback 0, Assigned Number subfield: the unique EVPN Instance number."
Fail-on: RD retains old loopback IP or wrong Type field (!= 1); traffic loss > 0 packets; BGP session flaps; EVPN routes not updated with new RD; config change takes > 1 s to apply.

6f4553fb62cbfea4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with BD on DUT; set `control-word enable` and `advertise-mac enable` under `configuration/l2-services/evpn`; establish BGP EVPN peering with `af-l2vpn evpn` to neighbor PE; configure IXIA to capture BGP UPDATE messages on the DUT↔PE link.
Action: Activate the EVPN instance and bring up the Broadcast Domain; trigger Type 3 (Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag) route advertisement; capture the BGP UPDATE containing the Type 3 route at IXIA.
Verify: Decode the captured BGP UPDATE; confirm Route Type = 3; validate all mandatory fields: RD (present), ET ID (present), Originating Router's IP Address (= DUT loopback), PMSI Tunnel Attribute (Flag=0, Type=6 for Ingress Replication, MPLS Label assigned, Tunnel Identifier = Originating Router's IP), L2-Attr Extended Community (L2 MTU value, Flow Label flag, Control Word flag = enabled per DUT config), RT (matches configured import-rt), Next-Hop (= DUT BGP router-id); cross-check on neighbor PE with `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3`.
6f4553fb62cbfea4.expectation:
Pass:    BGP UPDATE contains Type 3 route with all mandatory fields correctly encoded: RD present, ET ID present, Originating Router's IP = DUT loopback, PMSI Tunnel Attribute (Flag=0, Type=6, MPLS Label assigned, Tunnel Identifier = Originating Router's IP), L2-Attr Extended Community present (L2 MTU populated, Flow Label flag set, Control Word flag = enabled), RT matches configuration, Next-Hop = DUT router-id; neighbor PE installs the route.
Fail-on: Any mandatory field missing; PMSI Type ≠ 6; PMSI Flag ≠ 0; Tunnel Identifier ≠ Originating Router's IP; Next-Hop ≠ DUT router-id; L2-Attr Extended Community absent or incomplete; Control Word flag contradicts DUT `control-word` setting; or no Type 3 route advertised per Broadcast Domain.

6f54092b3459cc4d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT and neighbor PE, both multi-homed to the same Ethernet Segment; configure `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, and `export-rt` per documented syntax.
Action: On DUT, configure `service-carving` under the EVPN instance; commit. Inject BUM traffic from IXIA toward the multi-homed CE.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays DF election method and per-service DF assignment; `show running-config` contains `service-carving`; packet capture at CE confirms BUM traffic arrives once (no duplication).
6f54092b3459cc4d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
6f54092b3459cc4d.expectation:
Pass:    `service-carving` accepted; DF election uses carving service method (not default method); BUM traffic delivered exactly once
Fail-on: Configuration rejected, DF election shows default method instead of carving service, or duplicate BUM frames at CE

6f830667ffd41250.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT configured as single-homing PE (no `ethernet-segment` on any interface). 3rd-party PE (Cisco) configured as multihoming PE: `agg-eth 0` with `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 <type0-value>`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`. BGP EVPN session established with both sides advertising af-l2vpn evpn capability.

Action: Configure `af-l2vpn evpn` neighbor on DUT pointing to 3rd-party. On 3rd-party, advertise Ethernet Segment route (Type 4) and per-ES Ethernet A-D route (Type 1) with non-zero ESI. Capture BGP OPEN messages to verify L2 Attributes Extended Community capability (RFC7432bis §7.11.2) and Non-Revertive capability (RFC9785 §4.3) are exchanged. Advertise MAC/IP routes (Type 2) from 3rd-party with ESI label for split-horizon. Drive IXIA traffic toward MAC behind 3rd-party multihomed segment; fail primary path on 3rd-party to test aliasing.

Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on DUT lists Type 1 and Type 4 routes from 3rd-party with non-zero ESI (not rejected). `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms L2 Attributes and Non-Revertive capabilities present in negotiation. DUT honors split-horizon (does not forward IXIA traffic back to same ES) and aliasing (switches to backup path when primary down) per RFC7432bis ch.8.7.
6f830667ffd41250.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts and processes multihoming routes (Type 1/4 with ESI) from 3rd-party multihoming PE; L2 Attributes and Non-Revertive capabilities negotiated in OPEN; split-horizon and aliasing enforced. This validates EVPNS-REQ#190 MUST enforcement (overrides RFC7432bis ch.8.7 base SHOULD — single-homing PE must support multihoming procedures for interop).

Fail-on: DUT rejects Type 1 or Type 4 routes with non-zero ESI, L2 Attributes or Non-Revertive capability missing in OPEN, split-horizon not honored (traffic loops), aliasing not honored (no backup path switch), or DUT ignores multihoming procedures (reverts to RFC base SHOULD behavior).

6fcd795ed4d42389.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (PE1, PE2) multi-homed to CE via agg-eth with same ESI (identifier 0 type0-value xx.xx...), load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving highest-random-weight. Configure EVPN instance (EVI 100) on both.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN peering. Capture BGP UPDATE containing Ethernet A-D per EVI route (type 1, per RFC7432bis §7.1) from both PEs using tcpdump. Decode L2-Attr extended community (type 0x0F subtype 0x04 per RFC7432bis §8.6).
Verify: Extract L2-Attr extended community from each PE's route. Parse P bit (Primary DF) and B bit (Backup DF) fields. Confirm P bit = 1 for BOTH PE1 and PE2 per all-active MUST rule.
6fcd795ed4d42389.expectation:
Pass:    L2-Attr extended community present in Ethernet A-D per EVI routes from both PEs. P bit = 1 for ALL PEs in the redundancy group (PE1 and PE2) when all-active mode used. B bit reflects elected backup role if applicable. EC type code matches RFC7432bis.
Fail-on: L2-Attr EC missing from either PE's route. P bit = 0 for any PE in all-active ES redundancy group. Invalid P/B bit encoding (both zero when DF elected, or P=0 with B=1).

6fdfd1441438d748.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT and neighbor PE) multi-homed to a shared CE. On DUT's `agg-eth 0` and neighbor PE's CE-facing interface, configure identical 10-octet ESI using `ethernet-segment`. Establish BGP EVPN peering with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled.
Action: On DUT, enable `ethernet-segment` under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0` and configure the ESI value. On neighbor PE, configure matching ESI on its CE-facing interface. Capture BGP UPDATE from DUT containing Type 4 (Ethernet Segment Route) NLRI. Parse the UPDATE to extract the ES-Import RT extended community and the 10-octet ESI value from the NLRI.
Verify: From the captured Type 4 route, verify NLRI contains RD + 10-octet ESI + Originating Router's IP Address. Locate the ES-Import RT extended community in the route attributes. Verify the ES-Import RT value equals the high-order 6 octets (bytes 0-5) of the configured ESI per RFC7432bis ch.7.6. Check `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` on both PEs to confirm route advertisement and import.
6fdfd1441438d748.expectation:
Pass:    Type 4 route carries ES-Import RT extended community with value exactly matching high-order 6 octets of ESI (per RFC7432bis ch.7.6), and neighbor PE receives the route.
Fail-on: ES-Import RT extended community missing from Type 4 route, or RT value doesn't match ESI's first 6 octets, or neighbor PE doesn't receive/import the route, or Type 4 NLRI structure incorrect.

701c3f0122d5209d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure all-active multi-homed Ethernet Segment (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) between DUT and neighbor PE, both connected to same CE via LAG. Verify ESI label allocated in EVPN instance via `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99`.
Action: Inject broadcast frame from CE into DUT's ES member interface. DUT advertises via EVPN type-2/type-3 routes with ESI label set. Capture all packets on neighbor PE's CE-facing interface (same ESI).
Verify: Use `show evpn forwarding-table` to confirm ESI label present in routes. Packet capture on neighbor PE's ES interface shows zero frames matching source MAC (split-horizon dropped per RFC7432bis §8.3).
701c3f0122d5209d.expectation:
Pass:    Broadcast frame originated from local ES does NOT appear on neighbor PE's interface to same ES; ESI label correctly identifies source segment; `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays "split-horizon: active".
Fail-on: Broadcast frame loops back to CE via neighbor PE (same ESI), or ESI label absent/zero in type-2/type-3 routes, or forwarding loop detected.

703a3157559acec5.action_steps:
