Setup:  CE1 dual-homed to Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE via same ESI (all-active mode); CE2 single-homed to DUT. Both PEs in same EVI, BGP EVPN session up, `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on DUT. Verify DF/non-DF election complete; confirm type 4 Ethernet A-D per-ES routes exchanged (ESI labels advertised by non-DF PE).
Action: IXIA behind CE2 sends broadcast frame (dest MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) into DUT. DUT (ingress PE, DF or non-DF) performs ingress replication to all remote PEs including the 3rd-party non-DF PE for CE1's ES.
Verify: Packet capture on link between DUT and 3rd-party non-DF PE: confirm MPLS label stack contains [transport-label][VPN-label][ESI-label]. On 3rd-party PE: verify frame NOT forwarded to CE1 (check interface TX counters, no increment). Check DUT `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` to confirm ESI label value matches captured label.
703a3157559acec5.expectation:
Pass:    Captured BUM packet to non-DF PE contains ESI label (from type 4 route) in MPLS stack; 3rd-party non-DF PE performs split-horizon filtering (frame not sent to CE1); ESI label value in wire capture matches BGP route type 4 advertisement from non-DF PE.
Fail-on: ESI label absent from MPLS stack to non-DF PE; 3rd-party PE forwards BUM frame to CE1 (loop/duplicate); ESI label value mismatch; interop failure (type 4 routes not accepted or ESI label not honored).

704698afdf963468.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: PE1 (private ASN 64600) peers with DUT (public ASN 65100), DUT peers with PE2 (public ASN 65200). PE1 advertises EVPN Type-2 MAC/IP routes (AS_PATH: 64600). Configure L2VPN EVPN address-family on all peers.
Action: On DUT, navigate to `configuration / routing / bgp / 65100 / neighbor / <PE2-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn` and configure `private-as remove`; commit. Capture BGP UPDATEs sent to PE2 and verify AS_PATH excludes 64600. Reconfigure with `private-as replace`; commit. Capture BGP UPDATEs and verify AS_PATH now contains 65100 in place of 64600.
Verify: On PE2, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and inspect AS_PATH of routes learned from DUT. On DUT, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <PE2-ip> advertised-routes` and `show running-config routing bgp` to confirm active choice. Use tcpdump or BGP debug on DUT→PE2 link to capture UPDATE messages showing AS_PATH manipulation (remove: AS_PATH empty or only 65100; replace: AS_PATH contains 65100 where 64600 was).
704698afdf963468.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (PE1 with private ASN, PE2 public) + packet capture tool or IXIA for BGP UPDATE inspection
704698afdf963468.expectation:
Pass:    With `remove` active, all outbound L2VPN EVPN UPDATEs to PE2 have AS_PATH free of private AS numbers (64512..65534, 4200000000..4294967294); AS_PATH is either empty or contains only DUT's public ASN. With `replace` active, AS_PATH shows DUT's ASN (65100) in place of each private AS occurrence. Only one choice present in running-config at a time; second commit cleanly replaces the first.
Fail-on: Private AS 64600 appears in AS_PATH seen by PE2 when either `remove` or `replace` is configured. Both `remove` and `replace` appear in running-config simultaneously. Configuration change doesn't take effect after commit (PE2 continues seeing unmodified AS_PATH).

70ad50b4c47332ae.action_steps:
Setup:  All-Active ES exercised in Basic Functionality rows; DUT (as remote PE) has received Ethernet A-D per ES routes with redundancy mode 0 from multiple PEs; MAC/IP Advertisement routes imported; multi-path next-hops installed per RFC7432bis §14.1.2.
Action: Execute tech-support collection on DUT.
Verify: Tech-support contains: (1) received Ethernet A-D per ES routes with ESI Label extended community redundancy mode = 0, (2) MAC/IP Advertisement routes from all PEs in the ES, (3) next-hop sets constructed using all advertising PEs, (4) label stack entries from MAC/IP Advertisement route labels, (5) multi-path forwarding table entries, (6) syslog for route processing and All-Active deduction.
70ad50b4c47332ae.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support shows all Ethernet A-D per ES routes with redundancy mode 0, DUT deduced All-Active mode, multi-path next-hops constructed from MAC/IP Advertisement routes per §14.1.2, label stacks match MAC/IP route labels, forwarding table has multi-path entries.
Fail-on: Any redundancy mode ≠ 0 in Ethernet A-D per ES routes, missing multi-path next-hop construction, incorrect label stack, or single-path forwarding for multi-homed MACs.

70d938ab05bb58a4.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT EVPN instance EVI 100 with MTU 9000, Control Word enabled, multihomed ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 to CE; 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) EVPN instance EVI 100, same ESI; BGP EVPN session established; IXIA generates bidirectional L2 traffic.
Action: DUT advertises Inclusive Multicast route (type 3) and Ethernet A-D per EVI route (type 1) for EVI 100; capture both routes at 3rd-party PE; decode L2-Attr Extended Community (Type 0x06 / Sub-type 0x04) in each route type; 3rd-party PE also advertises both route types back to DUT.
Verify: On DUT show bgp l2vpn evpn route type 3 and type 1; on 3rd-party use vendor-specific route decode; verify Inclusive Multicast route carries MTU=9000, C=1, F=0 or 1, P=0, B=0; verify Ethernet A-D per EVI route carries P=1 or B=1, MTU=0, C=0, F=0; confirm both PEs install each other's routes; IXIA traffic forwards bidirectionally.
70d938ab05bb58a4.expectation:
Pass:    DUT Inclusive Multicast route encodes L2-Attr with MTU=9000, Control Word flag set, P=0 B=0, MBZ fields zero; Ethernet A-D per EVI encodes P or B flag, MTU=0 Control Word=0 Flow Label=0, MBZ zero; 3rd-party PE accepts both; DUT accepts 3rd-party routes with same partitioning; IXIA shows zero packet loss.
Fail-on: L2-Attr Extended Community missing or carries MTU in Ethernet A-D route, or carries P/B in Inclusive Multicast route, or MBZ fields non-zero, or either PE rejects the other's encoding, or route not installed, or traffic black-holed.

70e2251033e4ef63.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance, Ethernet Segment ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on interface, and iBGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE. Configure `auto-discovery` with ES-Import RT under l2-services/vpls, enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbor.
Action: Bring up the Ethernet Segment without explicit DF election preference (test default 32767). Issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` on DUT. Configure DF election preference value (e.g., `df-preference 1024` under ethernet-segment if CLI supports) and verify accepted range rejects 65536, accepts 65535.
Verify: On neighbor PE, capture received Type 4 route via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4`; decode DF Election Extended Community bitmap field = 32767 (default case) or configured value. Confirm ES-Import RT matches high-order 6 octets of ESI (00:11:22:33:44:55).
70e2251033e4ef63.expectation:
Pass:    Type 4 route advertised with ES-Import RT = 00:11:22:33:44:55, DF Election Extended Community present with bitmap field = 32767 when unconfigured, and = configured value when set within 0–65535. CLI rejects out-of-range values (e.g., 65536, -1).
Fail-on: Type 4 route missing after ES up, ES-Import RT ≠ ESI high-order 6 octets, DF Election Extended Community absent, bitmap field ≠ 32767 on default config, bitmap field outside 0–65535 accepted, or neighbor PE does not receive route.

7129f75e7b4b6abc.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (evpn evpn-name with auto-discovery, import-rt/export-rt). Configure agg-eth 0 with ethernet-segment (ESI ≠ 0, 10-octet value). Establish multi-homing with neighbor PE sharing same ESI. Start bidirectional traffic from IXIA through multi-homed segment.
Action: Execute shutdown on agg-eth 0, wait 5 seconds, then no shutdown to trigger mass-withdrawal and re-advertisement of Ethernet A-D per EVI routes.
Verify: Capture BGP updates showing Route Type 1 withdrawal with matching 10-octet ESI, then re-advertisement. Validate advertised RD matches DUT configuration, Next Hop equals DUT PE address (IPv4 or IPv6). Measure traffic loss via IXIA counters.
7129f75e7b4b6abc.expectation:
Pass:    Routes withdraw within 1 sec of down-event, re-advertise within 1 sec of up-event with correct RD, ESI (10-octet), and Next Hop. Traffic recovers via neighbor PE aliasing within sub-second. IXIA loss < 1%.
Fail-on: Missing or incorrect RD/ESI/Next-Hop in re-advertised routes, convergence > 3 seconds, IXIA loss > 5%, or stale forwarding state after recovery.

7148e2f58ef16945.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with ingress-replication (no multicast tunnel); advertise-mac enable, unknown-mac-flooding enable (both defaults). Establish BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE. Verify DUT advertises IMET route (type 3) with P-tunnel attribute containing ingress-replication downstream label L. Attach two local EVPN ACs to DUT; neighbor PE has traffic generator.
Action: From neighbor PE, send three labeled packets to DUT using label L: (1) broadcast frame (DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF), (2) multicast frame (DA=01:00:5E:01:01:01), (3) unknown unicast frame (DA=00:11:22:33:44:55, MAC not in DUT mac-table). Inject each at 10 pps for 10 seconds.
Verify: Run `show l2-services evpn mac-table` to confirm 00:11:22:33:44:55 is absent. Capture traffic on both local ACs; confirm all three frame types are flooded to both ACs. Check `show l2-services evpn statistics` for BUM counters increment by 300 (3 types × 10 pps × 10 s). For unknown unicast specifically, verify no MAC learning occurred (DA still absent from mac-table), proving DUT treated it as unknown unicast per §12.1.
7148e2f58ef16945.expectation:
Pass:    All three traffic types (broadcast/multicast/unknown-unicast) received on label L are flooded to local ACs. Unknown unicast MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55) remains absent from mac-table post-test, confirming DUT treated it as unknown unicast flood, not as a learned unicast entry. BUM counter delta matches injected frame count (±2%).
Fail-on: Unknown unicast frame is dropped, or DUT incorrectly learns DA MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55 appears in mac-table as a known unicast entry), or labeled traffic is not flooded to local ACs, or BUM counter does not increment.

716d5622d43f5440.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that MAC Mobility sequence counters and move-tracking state survive EVPN control-plane restart, preventing duplicate-MAC loops or black-holes when a MAC moves after the process recovers.
Method: Configure two-PE EVPN topology, trigger MAC move to increment sequence number, restart EVPN process, trigger second move, and confirm sequence continues correctly and convergence meets timing target.
Setup: Two-PE EVPN topology with EVI 100 configured on both PE1 and PE2; ES configured on both PEs with `identifier 0 <type0-value>` or `identifier 1` and default `es-waiting-time` (3 seconds); host H1 (MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01) initially attached to PE1 agg-eth 0 in EVI 100; IXIA generating continuous traffic to H1.
Action: 1) Move H1 to PE2 agg-eth 0 (first MAC mobility event). 2) Record MAC/IP advertisement sequence number via `show evpn mac address-table` or `show bgp l2vpn evpn`. 3) Identify EVPN/BGP control process via `show platform process | include evpn`. 4) Restart the identified process. 5) Wait for process recovery. 6) Move H1 back to PE1 agg-eth 0 (second mobility event).
Verify: 1) Process restarts within ≤ 5 s of restart command. 2) `show evpn mac address-table` recovers H1's MAC entry with correct PE2 location within ≤ 30 s. 3) Second MAC move shows sequence number incremented from pre-restart value (not reset to 0 or 1). 4) IXIA traffic histogram shows convergence ≤ 4 s per move event (es-waiting-time + 1 s margin). 5) No duplicate MAC alarms via `show evpn mac address-table` or syslog.
716d5622d43f5440.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts ≤ 5 s; MAC table recovers ≤ 30 s with correct location; MAC mobility sequence number preserved across restart and increments correctly on second move; traffic convergence ≤ 4 s per move; no duplicate MAC alarms during or after restart.
Fail-on: Process restart > 5 s, MAC table recovery > 30 s, sequence number reset to 0, duplicate MAC alarm logged, traffic black-hole > 4 s, or no auto-recovery.

7199702abbe5af9b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT and 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) with identical ESI via 'identifier 0 <type0-value>' (9 octets hex) under 'configuration/interface/agg-eth/ethernet-segment'; set 'load-balancing-mode all-active' (default is single-active); same EVI 100; establish MP-BGP EVPN session; attach CE to both PEs via agg-eth.
Action: Bring up ethernet-segment on both PEs; use 'es-waiting-time 3' (default); trigger Ethernet A-D per EVI (route-type 1) advertisement; capture BGP UPDATEs via tcpdump or 'debug bgp updates' to extract L2-Attr extended community per RFC7432bis §8.6.
Verify: Decode L2-Attr extended community in received Ethernet A-D per EVI routes from both PEs; confirm P bit=1 for ALL PEs in the redundancy group (RFC7432bis §8.6 MUST for All-Active mode); run 'show evpn ethernet-segment' for DF role; run 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1' for route attributes; send bidirectional IXIA traffic to validate All-Active load-balancing across both PEs.
7199702abbe5af9b.expectation:
Pass:    Both DUT and 3rd-party PE advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI routes with L2-Attr extended community where P bit=1 for all PEs in the redundancy group (All-Active mode per RFC7432bis §8.6); routes accepted mutually; DF election completes with both PEs in forwarding state; IXIA traffic load-balances bidirectionally with no drops.
Fail-on: Any PE in the redundancy group advertises L2-Attr with P bit=0 in All-Active mode (violates RFC7432bis §8.6 MUST); route rejected; DF election fails or shows Single-Active behavior (only one PE forwards); traffic single-paths or black-holes.

71cb21b9ebdfedfe.action_steps:
Setup:  Enter `configure / l2-services`; create `evpn EVPN-001 service-type vlan-based` and `evpn EVPN-002 service-type vlan-bundle`; commit and return to operational mode.
Action: Issue `show evpn global` to display all instances; issue `show evpn global name EVPN-001` to verify per-instance filtering.
Verify: First output lists both EVPN-001 and EVPN-002 with their service types; second output shows only EVPN-001; repeat both commands to confirm idempotence.
71cb21b9ebdfedfe.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
71cb21b9ebdfedfe.expectation:
Pass:    `show evpn global` displays both instances with correct service-type labels (vlan-based, vlan-bundle); filtered query returns only the named instance; identical output on repeated invocation.
Fail-on: Missing instances, incorrect or missing service-type field, parser error, or output drift between successive queries.

71e2021ab86cf23f.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco PE both multihomed to CE via agg-eth; configure `ethernet-segment` with `identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88` on both PEs; EVPN instance 100, BGP EVPN session up.
Action: On Cisco PE, advertise MAC/IP Advertisement route (RT-2) for MAC1 with ESI=00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 but suppress Ethernet A-D per ES route (RT-1); wait 10 seconds; release RT-1. Monitor DUT route table and MAC installation timing. Reset: configure Cisco to advertise MAC2 with ESI=0; verify immediate installation.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 2` and `show bgp evpn route-type 1` on DUT; `show evpn mac` for MAC installation timestamps; packet capture to confirm RT-2 received before RT-1 in non-reserved ESI case.
71e2021ab86cf23f.expectation:
Pass:    Non-reserved ESI (00.11.22…): MAC1 NOT in DUT forwarding table after RT-2 alone; MAC1 installed within 1 second of RT-1 receipt. ESI=0: MAC2 installed immediately upon RT-2 receipt without RT-1 dependency.
Fail-on: MAC1 installed before RT-1 received, OR MAC2 installation delayed beyond 1 second waiting for RT-1, OR DUT rejects Cisco's route encoding.

720cfd341c5c0fd8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN/VPLS service with `mac-limit 100`; commit; verify the limit is present in `show running-config`.
Action: Navigate to the service's configuration context; issue `no mac-limit`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms mac-limit removed; send 150+ unique source MACs via IXIA to the service; verify all are learned in the service's MAC table.
720cfd341c5c0fd8.expectation:
Pass:    mac-limit removed from config; service learns >100 MACs without limit enforcement
Fail-on: mac-limit persists in running-config, learning capped at 100, or MAC table flushed on config change

721e690335d2e394.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that a single-homing PE enforces mandatory (MUST, not SHOULD) support for multihoming procedures—Ethernet A-D route processing, ESI split-horizon filtering, DF election attribute handling—when interoperating with a 3rd-party multihoming PE, per the SFS upgrade from SHOULD to MUST in RFC7432bis §8.7.
Method: Configure DUT as single-homing PE (no `ethernet-segment` on ACs) and 3rd-party PE as multihoming PE (with `ethernet-segment` on LAG); establish BGP EVPN; have 3rd-party advertise Ethernet A-D routes with EVPN Layer 2 Attributes (DF election per RFC7432bis §7.11.2) and Non-Revertive Capability (RFC9785 §4.3); verify DUT installs and honors all multihoming constructs.
Setup: DUT configured with EVPN EVI 100, single-homed AC on x-eth 0/0/1 (no `ethernet-segment` configured). 3rd-party PE configured with EVI 100, multihomed LAG with `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 1`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`.
Action: Bring up BGP EVPN session between DUT and 3rd-party PE. 3rd-party PE advertises Ethernet A-D per-ES route (type 4), Ethernet A-D per-EVI route (type 1), MAC/IP routes (type 2) with ESI populated, EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community (C/P/B flags for DF election), and Non-Revertive Capability. Send bidirectional L2 traffic via IXIA through the multihomed CE toward DUT.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on DUT confirms Ethernet A-D routes (types 1, 4) are installed from 3rd-party PE. DUT performs split-horizon: traffic sourced from the CE's ESI is not forwarded back to that ESI. DUT honors DF election attributes and Non-Revertive Capability. Capture BGP UPDATEs to confirm extended community attributes.
721e690335d2e394.expectation:
Pass:    DUT installs Ethernet A-D routes (types 1, 4), performs split-horizon filtering on the ESI, honors EVPN Layer 2 Attributes (DF election parameters) and Non-Revertive Capability; bidirectional traffic forwards correctly; SFS MUST compliance (mandatory support) is demonstrated.
Fail-on: DUT rejects or ignores Ethernet A-D routes, omits split-horizon on ESI, ignores DF election or Non-Revertive attributes, or logs warnings indicating optional (SHOULD) support; this indicates fallback to RFC7432bis §8.7 base behavior where single-homing PE support was SHOULD.

723aa299306badb0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure three PEs in multi-homed EVPN-ES (all-active load-balancing-mode). PE1: `service-carving lowest-preference preference 100`. PE2: `service-carving lowest-preference` (no explicit preference → default 32767 per RFC9785§3). PE3: `service-carving lowest-preference preference 200`. IXIA attached to CE sends continuous BUM + unicast traffic (1 Gbps mixed profile).
Action: Run traffic for 24 hours. Monitor DF election state via `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` every 15 min (verify PE1 elected DF, preference values 100/32767/200 stable). Capture BGP updates for DF Election Extended Community (RFC9785§3 format: last 2 octets = preference in network byte order).
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms PE1=DF, PE2 preference=32767, PE3 non-DF throughout run. `show platform process memory evpnd` flat (±2% variance). IXIA reports zero frame loss, BUM forwarding only from DF. BGP capture confirms no unexpected DF Election Extended Community updates.
723aa299306badb0.equipment: Three routers (PE1, PE2, PE3) + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
723aa299306badb0.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 remains non-DF for entire 24 h (32767 > 200 in lowest-preference). Default preference 32767 observed in both CLI (`show evpn ethernet-segment`) and BGP attribute (captured DF Election Extended Community). Memory stable (evpnd RSS ±2%). Zero frame loss. No spurious DF re-elections.
Fail-on: PE2 becomes DF at any point (violates lowest-preference with default=32767). Preference value drifts from 32767 in BGP advertisements or CLI. Memory growth >5%. Frame loss >0.001%. DF election flaps without link/node failure trigger.

728f762416ccc4b4.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT (AS 65001) has BGP neighbor 10.1.1.2 (remote AS 65002) configured with `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family enabled in VRF default. BGP session is Established. No `allow-as-in` configured.
Action: In mode `configuration routing bgp 65001 vrf default neighbor 10.1.1.2 af-l2vpn evpn`, configure `allow-as-in 2` and commit. From neighbor PE (10.1.1.2), advertise EVPN Type-2 routes with AS_PATH: Route-A [65002], Route-B [65002, 65001], Route-C [65002, 65001, 65001], Route-D [65002, 65001, 65001, 65001].
Verify: `show running-config routing bgp 65001 vrf default neighbor 10.1.1.2` includes `allow-as-in 2`. `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows Route-A, Route-B, Route-C installed; Route-D filtered.
728f762416ccc4b4.expectation:
Pass:    `allow-as-in 2` persists in running-config. Routes with local AS appearing 0-2 times in AS_PATH are accepted into BGP table. Routes with local AS appearing >2 times are filtered by AS_PATH loop check.
Fail-on: Command rejected or not persisted, or all routes accepted (loop check bypassed entirely), or Route-C rejected despite count=2, or Route-D accepted despite count>2

7310190adc55d4d0.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP neighbor configured under `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn`; neighbor PE advertising EVPN Type-2 routes with RT 65001:100 (target: to be denied) and RT 65001:200 (target: to be permitted); pre-configure route-policy `test-policy-deny-rt100` that denies routes with RT 65001:100 and permits all others.
Action: Navigate to `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn`, configure `policy test-policy-deny-rt100 in`, commit, and perform soft-clear inbound (`clear bgp <neighbor-ip> soft in`) to refresh routes.
Verify: (1) `show running-config routing bgp` confirms `policy test-policy-deny-rt100 in` under af-l2vpn evpn neighbor context. (2) `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` shows routes with RT 65001:200 present; routes with RT 65001:100 absent. (3) `show evpn mac` shows MAC entries only from permitted routes. (4) `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor <neighbor-ip>` shows session established and routes received count.
7310190adc55d4d0.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (or IXIA BGP stack) advertising EVPN Type-2 routes with multiple RT values
7310190adc55d4d0.expectation:
Pass:    Policy persists in running-config; routes with RT 65001:100 are absent from `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and `show evpn mac` (filtered by inbound policy); routes with RT 65001:200 are present and installed; BGP session remains up.
Fail-on: Policy not in config; RT 65001:100 routes appear in BGP RIB or MAC table (policy not working); RT 65001:200 routes missing (over-filtering); or BGP session down.

7357f360666ee4f2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure both DUT and neighbor PE in common EVPN instance; on each, configure `interface agg-eth 0` → `ethernet-segment` → `identifier 0 <type0-value>` with matching ESI; omit `service-carving` command to test default behavior.
Action: Commit on both PEs; start BGP capture; after 4 seconds issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` and `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs; send bidirectional L2 traffic from IXIA (simulating multi-homed CE).
Verify: Inspect DF Election Extended Community in captured Ethernet Segment Routes; confirm DF Alg = Default (RFC7432bis ch.8.5); verify one PE elected DF, other non-DF; traffic forwarded only by DF.
7357f360666ee4f2.expectation:
Pass:    DF Election Extended Community advertised by both PEs with DF Alg = Default (RFC7432bis ch.8.5); exactly one PE in DF role; IXIA traffic egresses only via DF PE.
Fail-on: DF Election Extended Community absent, DF Alg ≠ Default, both PEs claim DF (split-brain), or neither forwards (blackhole).

736b1b9542389b83.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN VPLS instance on DUT and neighbor PE; establish BGP EVPN peering; verify BGP EVPN NLRI exchange shows F-bit=1 (Flow Label capable) from neighbor PE using `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` and NLRI details.
Action: Generate known unicast traffic from IXIA through DUT to remote CE behind neighbor PE; capture MPLS-encapsulated packets on inter-PE link; repeat test with neighbor PE configured to advertise F-bit=0; trigger BUM traffic (unknown destination MAC) and capture packets.
Verify: Use `show mpls forwarding-table` and packet captures to confirm: (1) when F=1 received, DUT inserts 20-bit Flow Label (RFC 5462) after VPN label with S=0 for known unicast; (2) when F=0 received, VPN label has S=1 and no Flow Label follows; (3) BUM packets have ESI label after VPN label, never Flow Label.
736b1b9542389b83.expectation:
Pass:    With F=1: 100% of known unicast packets contain label stack [Transport label | VPN label (S=0) | Flow label (S=1)]; with F=0: 0% contain Flow label, stack is [Transport | VPN (S=1)]; BUM packets: 0% contain Flow label, all have [Transport | VPN (S=0) | ESI label].
Fail-on: Flow label present in any packet when remote F=0; Flow label absent when remote F=1 and DUT is Flow-capable; Flow label present in BUM packets; label stack order violates RFC7432bis §18.1 (VPN label not preceding Flow/ESI label).

73a0ce5bd9c277c2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT (current image) in 'configuration / l2-services' mode: 'evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-bundle' with VLANs 100, 200 bound to x-eth 0/0/1; under 'vpls / auto-discovery' set 'import-rt 65000:100' and 'export-rt 65000:100'. Configure matching EVPN on neighbor PE. Generate IXIA L2 streams to DUT x-eth 0/0/1: VLAN 100 (1000 pps, 60 s), VLAN 200 (1000 pps, 60 s), VLAN 300 (500 pps, 60 s, non-configured VLAN). Verify pre-upgrade: 'show evpn evpn-1' operational, VLANs 100/200 forwarded to neighbor PE (IXIA rx ≥ 59,000 frames each), VLAN 300 dropped ('show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics' ingress-drop counter ≥ 29,500, neighbor PE VLAN 300 rx = 0).
Action: Stage next image on ONIE server; run 'onie-install <image-url>' from DUT; wait for reboot and login; verify 'show version' confirms new image.
Verify: 'show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-1' displays preserved vlan-bundle service-type with VLANs 100, 200; 'show evpn evpn-1 detail' shows operational state up, auto-discovery active; clear counters; re-run IXIA 30-second traffic burst; VLANs 100/200 forwarded post-upgrade (neighbor PE IXIA port rx ≥ 29,000 frames each), VLAN 300 dropped post-upgrade (DUT interface ingress-drop counter ≥ 14,500, neighbor PE VLAN 300 rx = 0).
73a0ce5bd9c277c2.equipment: DUT + ONIE image server + IXIA traffic gen + neighbor PE
73a0ce5bd9c277c2.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN vlan-bundle config with VLANs 100, 200 preserved in running-config; post-upgrade VLAN 100/200 traffic forwarded (≥ 99% delivery to neighbor PE); VLAN 300 frames dropped at DUT ingress (0 frames reach neighbor PE, DUT drop counter matches IXIA VLAN 300 tx count ± 1%).
Fail-on: Config lost or requires manual re-entry; VLAN 300 frames forwarded post-upgrade (any VLAN 300 frames received at neighbor PE); EVPN instance fails to reach operational state; running-config replay produces errors; drop behavior regressed (VLAN 300 not dropped).

7487e5c05eaabf05.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT PE1 with IRB interface for subnet 10.1.1.0/24, default gateway MAC 00:00:5E:00:53:01, advertising EVPN MAC route with Default Gateway extended community. Configure neighbor PE2 in same EVPN instance. Connect IXIA to PE2 to inject traffic that causes PE2 to learn and advertise the same MAC 00:00:5E:00:53:01 as a host MAC (without Default Gateway extended community).
Action: Start IXIA traffic with source MAC 00:00:5E:00:53:01 toward PE2. Verify PE2 learns the MAC locally and advertises EVPN Type-2 route without Default Gateway extended community. Wait for BGP convergence so PE2 receives PE1's Type-2 route with Default Gateway extended community for the same MAC.
Verify: On PE2, execute `show bgp evpn mac 00:00:5E:00:53:01` to confirm the locally-originated route is withdrawn. Execute `show alarms` or `show log` on PE2 to confirm default gateway MAC conflict alarm is raised. On PE1, execute `show bgp evpn mac 00:00:5E:00:53:01` to confirm only PE1's route with Default Gateway extended community remains active.
7487e5c05eaabf05.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 withdraws its MAC route for 00:00:5E:00:53:01 within BGP convergence time (≤10 seconds after receiving PE1's route with Default Gateway extended community). PE2 raises alarm indicating default gateway MAC conflict. Only PE1's route with Default Gateway extended community remains in the EVPN MAC table across both PEs. MAC Mobility extended community is not present on either route.
Fail-on: PE2 continues advertising conflicting MAC route after receiving PE1's Default Gateway route. No alarm raised on PE2. Both MAC routes remain active simultaneously. MAC Mobility extended community is attached to or processed on routes carrying Default Gateway extended community.

74aa844202618852.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE in All-Active mode on shared ES: on both, 'configure / interface agg-eth 1/1 / ethernet-segment' → 'identifier 0 <9-octet-value>' → 'load-balancing-mode all-active'; configure EVPN instance with ingress replication; IXIA generates broadcast frames at 1000 pps from CE behind shared ES.
Action: While BUM traffic flows, on DUT execute 'configure / interface agg-eth 1/1 / shutdown', wait 2 seconds, then 'no shutdown'; start convergence timer.
Verify: 'show evpn ethernet-segment' reports ES status=up and DF/Non-DF roles assigned; 'show bgp l2vpn evpn' confirms both PEs re-advertise Ethernet A-D per ES (type-1) routes with ESI label; packet capture between DUT and neighbor PE shows BUM packets include ESI label in MPLS stack; 'show mpls forwarding-table label <ESI-label>' on neighbor PE shows forwarding action does NOT point to agg-eth (split-horizon enforced); IXIA Rx counter shows traffic resumes; stop timer when loss <1% for 5s.
74aa844202618852.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
74aa844202618852.expectation:
Pass:    ES reconverges within 3× es-waiting-time (default 9s total); type-1 routes re-advertised with ESI label by both PEs within 9s; neighbor PE enforces split-horizon (no forward to source ES); IXIA records ≤5% packet loss during convergence.
Fail-on: ESI label absent from type-1 route >10s after interface up; neighbor PE forwards BUM with ESI label back to source ES (loop detected); convergence >15s; >10% packet loss.

74fe0c561cea8580.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs in same Ethernet Segment with multi-homed CE connected via agg-eth 0 on each PE; set `identifier 0 <type0-value>` matching on both; configure EVPN instance; IXIA generates bidirectional unicast traffic at 10 Gbps.
Action: Test 1: verify default `load-balancing-mode single-active` behavior, measure throughput/latency for 60 s. Test 2: change to `load-balancing-mode all-active` (mutex choice), measure throughput/latency and per-PE traffic distribution for 60 s. Test 3: with all-active, configure `service-carving highest-random-weight`, measure per-service distribution.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms ES mode and service-carving config on both PEs; collect TX/RX byte counters from agg-eth 0 on both routers; IXIA reports throughput/latency/packet loss.
74fe0c561cea8580.expectation:
Pass:    Single-active (default): only one PE forwards, standby PE 0% traffic, throughput ≥ 9.5 Gbps, latency ≤ 10 ms, 0% loss. All-active: both PEs forward with 40–60% distribution per RFC7432bis ch.14, aggregate throughput ≥ 9.5 Gbps, latency ≤ 10 ms, 0% loss. Service-carving: distribution matches highest-random-weight algorithm.
Fail-on: Single-active forwarding on both PEs; all-active imbalance outside 40–60%; throughput < 9 Gbps; latency > 15 ms; packet loss > 0.01%; service-carving mismatch.

7527031318116e4f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure the DUT as a BGP route reflector with an iBGP session to neighbor A (the client) and a separate iBGP session to neighbor B (route source). Enable route-reflector-client for neighbor A under the af-l2vpn evpn address family context.
Action: Neighbor B advertises an L2VPN EVPN route (e.g., type 2 MAC/IP Advertisement route). Capture or inspect the route as received by neighbor A.
Verify: On neighbor A, inspect the received route using BGP show commands or packet capture to confirm ORIGINATOR_ID attribute (set to neighbor B's router-id) and CLUSTER_LIST attribute (containing DUT's cluster-id) are present.
7527031318116e4f.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor A receives the reflected route with ORIGINATOR_ID and CLUSTER_LIST attributes correctly populated; a non-client neighbor would not receive these attributes on the same route
Fail-on: Neighbor A receives routes without ORIGINATOR_ID or CLUSTER_LIST, routes are not reflected at all, or attributes are malformed

759682959b4f66ed.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn <evpn-name> service-type vlan-based`, BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn`, and `import-rt <route-target>`. Peer advertises routes of types 1 (Ethernet A-D), 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement), 3 (Inclusive Multicast) matching the import policy.
Action: Issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi` (all EVIs), `show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi evi-name <evpn-name>` (filtered), and both `brief` and `detail` variants.
Verify: Output contains all three route types with RD, RT, next-hop attributes; `brief` shows condensed format, `detail` shows extended attributes; evi-name filter displays only routes for the specified EVI.
759682959b4f66ed.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (to advertise EVPN routes)
759682959b4f66ed.expectation:
Pass:    Each route advertised by the peer (types 1, 2, 3) appears with matching route distinguisher, route target, and next-hop; `brief` omits extended attributes present in `detail`; evi-name filter shows only routes imported into the specified EVI.
Fail-on: Route advertised with matching import-rt missing from output, route type/RD/RT/next-hop mismatch with advertised values, `brief` and `detail` produce identical output, or evi-name filter includes routes from other EVIs.
759682959b4f66ed.req_id: CLI:show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi
759682959b4f66ed.sub_category: show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi

75bfc4250b1aee2d.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology with EVPN iBGP session (enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor`). Configure Ethernet Segment on DUT with ESI type 1: 01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01 (LACP-based per RFC7432 §5). Set `advertise-mac enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`. IXIA attached to ES member interface.
Action: Send broadcast frame from IXIA (SMAC=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF) to trigger MAC learning and route type 2 advertisement. Capture BGP UPDATE on PE↔PE link using Wireshark/tcpdump port 179. Parse EVPN NLRI: locate 10-octet ESI field (follows 8-octet RD in route type 2 structure).
Verify: Decode ESI byte[0] from captured route type 2; confirm = 0x01. Repeat test iterations with ESI types 0, 3, 4, 5 (e.g., type 4 Router-ID: 04:00:00:00:C0:A8:01:01:00:00). Negative test: configure ESI 06:06:06:06:06:06:06:06:06:06 or FF:FF:...; verify DUT CLI rejects or omits from BGP advertisement.
75bfc4250b1aee2d.expectation:
Pass:    All advertised MAC/IP Advertisement routes carry ESI with most significant byte (ESI[0]) ∈ {0x00, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05}. Invalid ESI types (>5) rejected at config or not advertised in BGP.
Fail-on: Captured BGP route type 2 contains ESI[0] = 0x06 or higher (e.g., 0xFF). DUT accepts and propagates ESI type > 5.

76440f897ac2f71b.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that a single-homing PE implements the mandatory multihoming procedures required for interoperating with multihoming PEs, per SFS §2.7.9 which upgrades RFC7432bis §8.7 from SHOULD to MUST.
Method: Configure DUT as single-homing PE (no ES) and 3rd-party PE with multihoming (ES, all-active); advertise Ethernet A-D routes and MAC/IP routes with ES-Import from the 3rd party; verify DUT processes them correctly (split-horizon, mass-withdraw recognition, L2 Attributes, Non-Revertive capability).
Setup: DUT with EVIs configured but no `ethernet-segment` on any interface (single-homing mode). 3rd-party PE with ES on an aggregate interface using `load-balancing-mode all-active`, backed by multiple PEs. BGP EVPN session established between DUT and 3rd-party PE.
Action: From 3rd-party PE, advertise Ethernet A-D per-ES (type 4), A-D per-EVI (type 1), and MAC/IP routes with ES-Import extended community, L2 Attributes extended community (RFC7432bis §7.11.2), and Non-Revertive capability (RFC9785 §4.3). Send traffic from DUT toward a MAC behind the multihoming ES. Capture BGP UPDATEs and data-plane frames via tcpdump on PE-PE link and access ports.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on DUT shows installed Ethernet A-D routes with ES-Import RT. `show bgp neighbor` confirms L2 Attributes and Non-Revertive capability exchanged. DUT honors split-horizon: frames not forwarded to the matching ES-Import RT. DUT recognizes mass-withdraw when A-D per-ES is withdrawn. Decoded BGP UPDATEs contain L2 Attributes extended community and Non-Revertive capability.
76440f897ac2f71b.expectation:
Pass: DUT installs Ethernet A-D routes (type 1 and 4); split-horizon enforced; L2 Attributes and Non-Revertive capability negotiated per RFC7432bis §7.11.2 and RFC9785 §4.3; mass-withdraw recognized.
Fail-on: DUT rejects Ethernet A-D routes (violates SFS §2.7.9 MUST; this overrides RFC7432bis §8.7 SHOULD), OR forwards frames to ES-Import RT (split-horizon broken), OR missing L2 Attributes or Non-Revertive capability.

7666f19e1e42d6d1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN BD 100 under `configuration/l2-services/evpn` with export-rt 65000:100, `advertise-mac enable` (default), `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default for BUM). Configure BGP neighbor PE with `af-l2vpn evpn`. Configure PE to import RT 65000:100.
Action: Activate BD 100 (`no shutdown`); DUT originates Type-3 (Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag) route per RFC7432bis §11.2 with default P-tunnel (no explicit multicast tree or ingress-replication config).
Verify: On PE, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 detail` or capture BGP UPDATE; decode Type-3 NLRI to confirm PMSI Tunnel attribute (RFC6514 type 22) present with Tunnel Type field and MPLS Label field bound uniquely to BD 100.
7666f19e1e42d6d1.expectation:
Pass:    Type-3 route carries PMSI Tunnel attribute with valid Tunnel Type (e.g., 6=Ingress Replication) and MPLS label unique to BD 100 per RFC7432bis §11.2.
Fail-on: Type-3 route missing PMSI Tunnel attribute, or attribute lacks Tunnel Type or MPLS Label, or label not unique across BDs, or BGP UPDATE rejected by PE.

766cc23e24d37eb5.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 (DF) and PE2 (non-DF for VLAN 100) multi-home CE1 via ESI; PE3 ingress with `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default). Verify PE2 advertises ESI label using `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn`; IXIA on PE3 (TX) and CE1 (RX).
Action: IXIA generates broadcast frames at 50K pps (64-byte, sustained 300 sec) from PE3's local segment; PE3 replicates via Ingress Replication to PE1/PE2. Capture MPLS packets on PE3→PE2 link using IXIA.
Verify: Packet capture confirms PE2's ESI label present in MPLS stack (below VPN label) for all PE3→PE2 BUM packets per RFC7432bis §8.3.1.1; `show l2-services vpls statistics` reports ≥50K pps replication rate. IXIA at CE1 shows zero duplicates (PE2 drops based on ESI label, PE1 forwards) and average latency ≤10 ms.
766cc23e24d37eb5.equipment: Multi-homing topology: 2 egress PEs (PE1 DF, PE2 non-DF) + 1 ingress PE (PE3) + IXIA scale rig with MPLS capture capability
766cc23e24d37eb5.expectation:
Pass:    100% of PE3→PE2 BUM packets contain PE2's ESI label in MPLS stack; sustained replication ≥50K pps; average latency ≤10 ms; zero duplicate frames at CE1.
Fail-on: Any PE3→PE2 packet missing ESI label; replication rate drops below 50K pps; average latency exceeds 10 ms; any duplicate frame detected at CE1.

7716c16583f39eee.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology sharing EVI; configure `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300` on DUT to set M=180s, N=5. Syslog collector configured and reachable. IXIA ports attached: port-1 behind DUT, port-2 behind neighbor PE, both using MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 as source in the shared VLAN.
Action: Start traffic from IXIA port-1; after MAC is learned on DUT, alternate traffic between port-1 and port-2 to trigger five MAC mobility events within 180 seconds. After the 5th move, check `show alarms` on DUT, inspect syslog at collector, and use BGP route monitoring (tcpdump or platform show commands) to confirm DUT stops advertising MAC/IP routes for AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01.
Verify: Alarm raised with appropriate severity (warning/critical) for MAC duplication. Syslog entry contains MAC address AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01, event type (duplication detected), and threshold values (M=180, N=5). BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes for AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 cease from DUT. Frozen state persists until operator intervention (corrective action such as clear command or config change).
7716c16583f39eee.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA (two ports, duplicate MAC) + syslog collector
7716c16583f39eee.expectation:
Pass:    M and N parameters configurable; detection triggers after exactly N=5 moves within M=180s; alarm raised; syslog entry present with all required fields; DUT stops BGP processing for duplicate MAC; freeze persists until operator action.
Fail-on: No alarm raised, no syslog entry, BGP continues advertising the duplicate MAC, detection triggers at wrong threshold, or freeze ends without operator intervention.

77399f04b90e866a.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT: configure BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor`; set `advertise-mac enable` under `configuration/l2-services/evpn`; create VPLS instance with auto-discovery, `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`. 3rd-party PE (Cisco or Juniper) with matching EVPN and RT config, BGP EVPN session established.
Action: (1) Baseline: 3rd-party PE advertises Route Type 2 (MAC/IP) and Type 3 (IMET) via MP_REACH_NLRI, observe DUT processing. (2) Error injection via IXIA BGP emulator: send UPDATE with MP_REACH containing NLRI where Length field = 1 (violates ≥2 minimum per §7.14.1); send UPDATE with NLRI Length = 20 but actual encoded NLRI totals 15 octets (inconsistent).
Verify: Baseline: `show bgp evpn routes` displays Type 2/3 from 3rd-party; `show l2-services vpls mac-table` shows learned MACs. Error cases: BGP session resets (Established → Idle), syslog entry for NLRI parse error citing §7.14.1, NOTIFICATION sent (UPDATE Message Error, code 3), session re-establishes after fault clears.
77399f04b90e866a.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) + IXIA (BGP EVPN emulation for byte-level crafting of malformed MP_REACH/MP_UNREACH NLRIs)
77399f04b90e866a.expectation:
Pass:    Valid Route Type 2/3 NLRIs from 3rd-party PE correctly parsed and installed; MAC learning works. Malformed NLRI (Length < 2 or Length inconsistent with actual NLRI size) triggers BGP session reset with UPDATE Error NOTIFICATION per RFC7432bis §7.14.1; session recovers when valid UPDATEs resume.
Fail-on: DUT accepts malformed NLRI without resetting session, crashes/hangs on parse error, or rejects valid RFC7432bis-compliant NLRI from 3rd-party PE.

773c1a1cc8cf1b19.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'evpn-pb-100' with service-type port-based, attach LAG agg-lag 1 and Ethernet x-eth 0/0/1, configure auto-discovery with import-rt 65000:100 export-rt 65000:100, verify instance is operationally up (show evpn evpn-pb-100).
Action: Collect tech-support bundle via CLI (tech-support or equivalent vendor command), extract and parse the archive.
Verify: Tech-support contains: (1) running-config showing 'evpn evpn-pb-100 service-type port-based' with interface attachments and route-targets, (2) show evpn detail output with operational state and service-type, (3) show l2-services output for the EVI, (4) BGP EVPN routes (type-1 AD per-EVI, type-3 IMET), (5) kernel forwarding tables (bridge FDB for attached interfaces), (6) syslog entries for EVPN instance creation and state transitions.
773c1a1cc8cf1b19.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle includes all 6 elements: running-config with port-based EVPN instance config, show evpn detail with service-type and state, show l2-services, BGP routes (type-1/type-3), kernel bridge FDB, and relevant syslog entries.
Fail-on: Missing any of: running-config for port-based EVPN, show evpn detail output, BGP EVPN route advertisements, kernel forwarding tables for the bridge-domain, or syslog entries for the instance.

773f8f07e79cee9b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-test service-type vlan-based` with a local CE-facing interface (e.g., Ethernet1/1) attached to EVPN bridge domain; configure `mac-aging-time 300` to allow verification window.
Action: From IXIA, send 10 Ethernet frames with unique source MACs (00:11:22:33:44:01 through 00:11:22:33:44:0A) to DUT Ethernet1/1. Separately send LACP frames (source MAC 01:80:c2:00:00:02) to verify exclusion.
Verify: Issue `show evpn evpn-test mac` and `show mac-address-table evpn-test`; confirm all 10 test MACs appear with learning-type "data-plane" or "dynamic" and interface Ethernet1/1; verify LACP MAC 01:80:c2:00:00:02 is absent from table.
773f8f07e79cee9b.expectation:
Pass:    All 10 test source MACs appear in EVPN MAC table as data-plane learned entries; LACP MAC (01:80:c2:00:00:02) is absent; MAC table count increments by exactly 10.
Fail-on: Zero MACs learned after traffic, any test MAC shows learning-type "control-plane" or "BGP", LACP MAC appears in learned table, or MAC learning counter remains frozen.

774404590e63257a.action_steps:
Problem: validate that tech-support captures sufficient evidence to diagnose L2-Attr Extended Community presence in received Ethernet A-D routes, which determines whether RFC7432 or RFC7432bis Single-Active behavior applies, and to correlate that with MAC/IP advertisement and learning state.
Method: build two-PE EVPN with multi-homed ES, drive MAC learning via Type 2 routes, capture tech-support bundle during active operation, and verify bundle contains BGP route details with L2-Attr attributes and MAC forwarding tables.
Setup: Two-PE EVPN topology with multi-homed ES; configure `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbors, `advertise-mac enable` on both PEs, and matching `import-rt` / `export-rt` for the EVI. Attach CE to both PEs and drive traffic to learn MAC addresses.
Action: Let Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVPN Instance) and Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes propagate between PEs. Run `tech-support` command on PE1 during active MAC learning. Save the bundle off-box and extract it.
Verify: Inspect bundle for: (1) `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` output showing received Type 1 routes with or without L2-Attr Extended Community attributes; (2) Type 2 routes with MAC addresses, IP addresses (if any), and MPLS labels LT1/LT2/LT3/LT4; (3) `show evpn mac address-table` output showing learned MAC entries; (4) running-config with `af-l2vpn evpn`, `advertise-mac`, `import-rt`, and `export-rt` configuration; (5) kernel forwarding tables (FIB) for the EVI and ES.
774404590e63257a.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` explicitly showing L2-Attr Extended Community presence or absence in received Ethernet A-D routes, Type 2 route details with all four label types (LT1/LT2/LT3/LT4), MAC address table with learned entries, full EVPN configuration, and FIB state.
Fail-on: Missing `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail`, missing extended community attributes in Type 1 route display, missing Type 2 route details, missing MAC address table, missing configuration sections, or missing FIB/forwarding tables.

774dbe4586b89f8a.action_steps:
Problem: Validate ESI Label mismatch detection and handling when multiple PEs advertise type-1 A-D routes for the same ES with different ESI labels (SFS §3.1 extends RFC7432bis §8.2.1 with quarantine and alarm requirements).

Method: Multi-home a CE to PE1 and PE2 with identical ESI; misconfigure PE2 with a different ESI label than PE1; observe DUT (PE3) via BGP packet capture and CLI to verify quarantine behavior and alarm lifecycle.

Setup: Three-PE BGP EVPN topology with DUT (PE3) peered to PE1 and PE2. CE dual-homed to PE1 (agg-eth 0) and PE2 (agg-eth 0) via LACP with ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11. Configure `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based` with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100` on all PEs. Configure PE1 with ESI label 5000; PE2 with ESI label 6000 (deliberate mismatch). Enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on all BGP neighbors.

Action: Bring up agg-eth 0 on PE1 and PE2. Capture BGP UPDATEs at DUT for route-type 1 (per-ES: ET=MAX-ET, MPLS Label=0; per-EVI: ET=100). Decode ESI Label Extended Community from both PE1→DUT and PE2→DUT advertisements. Issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`, `show evpn ethernet-segment detail`, `show log` on DUT. After verification, correct PE2 to ESI label 5000, wait for BGP convergence, re-check alarm and route status.

Verify: DUT receives four type-1 routes: two per-ES (one from PE1, one from PE2) and two per-EVI (one from PE1, one from PE2), each pair with identical ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 but different ESI labels (5000 vs 6000) per RFC7432bis §7.1, §8.4.1. DUT installs first-received routes (PE1, label 5000) per RFC7432bis §7.13.2 but quarantines PE2's routes (marked unusable, not eligible for path selection) per SFS §3.1. Alarm logged: "ESI label mismatch for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11" or equivalent. `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows single active ESI label 5000, PE2's label 6000 flagged as conflicting. After PE2 correction to label 5000, alarm clears, PE2's routes become active, and both PEs' routes are used per RFC7432bis §8.2.1.1 RT handling.
774dbe4586b89f8a.equipment: DUT (PE3) + two neighbor PEs (PE1, PE2) + multi-homed CE with LACP + packet capture
774dbe4586b89f8a.expectation:
Pass: DUT detects ESI label mismatch across type-1 A-D routes for the same ES (RFC7432bis §8.2.1), quarantines routes with non-matching ESI label (SFS §3.1), raises alarm, and clears alarm when labels converge. Both per-ES and per-EVI variants handled identically.

Fail-on: DUT installs both routes despite label mismatch (violates RFC7432bis §8.2.1 consistency), OR no alarm raised when mismatch detected, OR alarm persists after label convergence, OR quarantined routes are used for forwarding before mismatch resolution (violates SFS §3.1 quarantine behavior).

775205b6d8c9d325.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with the same ESI using `identifier 0 <type0-value>` on agg-eth 0. Set `load-balancing-mode all-active` on both. Set `service-carving highest-random-weight` on PE1, `service-carving default` on PE2. Attach CE via LACP LAG to both PEs. Configure EVI and bring up BGP EVPN peering.
Action: Verify PE1 advertises Ethernet Segment Route (4) with DF Election Extended Community 'DF Alg'=HRW per RFC8584-§3, and Ethernet A-D Per ES Route (1) with ES-Import RT per RFC7432bis-§7.6. Verify PE1 receives PE2's Route (4) with DF Alg=Default. Inject IXIA traffic via CE on VLAN 10; verify the elected DF forwards without loops.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 4` shows PE1 advertises DF Alg=HRW and PE2 advertises DF Alg=Default (mismatch detected). `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms exactly one PE is DF for VLAN 10 (DF election completed using fallback to Default per RFC7432bis-§8.5). IXIA traffic forwards through the single DF; no duplicate frames.
775205b6d8c9d325.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 supports and advertises HRW in Route (4) DF Election Extended Community; receives PE2's differing DF Alg (Default); falls back to Default Algorithm per RFC7432bis-§8.5; exactly one PE becomes DF for VLAN 10; traffic forwards without loops.
Fail-on: PE1 does not support HRW (or highest-preference/lowest-preference per EVPNS-REQ#120); does not advertise DF Election Extended Community; does not fall back when DF Alg mismatches; both or neither PE becomes DF; or traffic loops/blackholes.

77b28adfc58f8258.action_steps:
Setup:  Three PEs: PE1 and PE2 multi-homed to same ES with `load-balancing-mode all-active`, P2MP MPLS LSPs configured for BUM distribution, PE3 remote PE importing the Ethernet A-D per ES routes. IXIA traffic generator attached to ES and remote site.
Action: IXIA sends BUM traffic (1000 multicast flows, 5 Gbps aggregate) from ES toward remote site. Verify PE1 advertises upstream assigned ESI label in Ethernet A-D per ES route; PE2 and PE3 program the label in PE1's context label space. Measure BUM throughput, latency (IXIA timestamping), and duplicate frame count on ES.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` (ESI label value), `show mpls forwarding-table label <ESI-label>` on PE2/PE3 (context label space, action = forward to remote but NOT to local ES), IXIA RX counters at remote site and ES receiver.
77b28adfc58f8258.expectation:
Pass:    BUM throughput ≥ 4.95 Gbps (99% of offered load), latency ≤ 10 ms (P99), zero duplicate frames received on ES (verified via IXIA sequence numbers), PE2 forwarding entry for PE1's ESI label shows no local ES egress interface.
Fail-on: Throughput < 99%, latency > 10 ms, any duplicate frames on ES (indicates PE2 forwarded back to ES in violation of the MUST NOT requirement), or ESI label not programmed in PE1 context label space on PE2/PE3.

77c6f2df3fad433d.action_steps:
Setup:
1. Configure BGP on DUT with neighbor PE; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor configuration.
2. Create at least one EVPN instance using `evpn <evpn-name> service-type vlan-based` on DUT.
3. Neighbor PE configured to advertise EVPN Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) and Type 3 (Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag) routes with RD and RT matching DUT's import policy.

Action:
1. Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <neighbor-ip> received routes`.
2. Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <neighbor-ip> advertised routes`.

Verify:
1. `received routes` output displays EVPN routes with Route Type field (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5), RD, RT list, BGP next-hop, and route-type-specific attributes (MAC address + IP for Type 2; originator IP for Type 3; ESI for Type 1/4).
2. `advertised routes` output shows routes DUT is sending to the neighbor (if DUT has local EVPN routes).
3. Cross-check route count with `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` neighbor statistics.
77c6f2df3fad433d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE configured to advertise EVPN routes
77c6f2df3fad433d.expectation:
Pass:    Both commands execute successfully. Received routes display includes at least the routes advertised by neighbor PE, with all mandatory fields (Route Type, RD, RT, next-hop) populated. Route-specific attributes (MAC/IP for Type 2, originator for Type 3) are present.
Fail-on: Command returns error, displays empty output when neighbor has advertised routes, omits mandatory EVPN fields, or shows routes not advertised by the neighbor.
77c6f2df3fad433d.req_id: CLI:show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors advertised/received routes
77c6f2df3fad433d.sub_category: show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors advertised/received routes

7840614258ed674e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with DUT and neighbor PE both attached to same ethernet-segment (all-active or single-active); generate traffic from IXIA-attached host so MAC is learned on DUT, then move host attachment to trigger MAC Mobility event (MAC re-appears behind neighbor PE).
Action: After MAC move completes and EVPN routes re-advertised with incremented MAC Mobility sequence number, collect tech-support bundle on DUT using vendor-provided command.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: `show evpn mac-address` output with sequence numbers, MAC move events in syslog referencing RFC7432bis sequence increment, BGP EVPN MAC/IP Advertisement routes showing updated sequence field, and FIB entries reflecting new PE attachment point.
7840614258ed674e.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains MAC Mobility sequence numbers in EVPN routes (sequence > 0 after move), syslog entries documenting MAC move event with old/new PE identifiers, and FIB state consistent with final MAC location.
Fail-on: Tech-support missing MAC sequence data in routes, no MAC Mobility events in syslog, FIB entries stale or pointing to wrong PE, or running-config omitted ethernet-segment configuration for multihomed interface.

78427f5eec9a100b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT interface agg-eth-1: `interface agg-eth-1; ethernet-segment; identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09; load-balancing-mode all-active; service-carving highest-preference`. Configure neighbor PE with same ESI and preference 20000. Establish iBGP EVPN peering (AFI/SAFI l2vpn/evpn) between DUT and PE. Attach LACP LAG to both PEs toward CE. Start IXIA BUM + unicast traffic from CE.
Action: On DUT under ethernet-segment mode, test `service-carving highest-preference preference <val>` for boundary values: 0, 1, 32767, 65535. Attempt 65536 (must reject). Test default: `no service-carving; service-carving highest-preference` (omit preference keyword). After each change: `commit; clear bgp evpn neighbor <PE-IP> soft`. Final test: set DUT preference=10000, PE=30000, then induce BGP convergence: `clear bgp neighbor <PE-IP>` (hard reset), monitor DF re-election.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI> detail` displays configured/default preference value. `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` shows ES route with DF Preference. Wireshark capture: BGP UPDATE for ES route (type 4), DF Election Extended Community (type 0x06 subtype 0x06), octets 6-7 encode preference in network byte order. `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI> designated-forwarder` confirms DF winner per preference before and after BGP session restore. Monitor IXIA traffic loss during convergence.
78427f5eec9a100b.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (EVPN/BGP-capable, same vendor or interop) + IXIA traffic gen (emulates CE, sends tagged BUM+unicast) + LACP-capable switch or IXIA port-channel (forms agg-eth LAG)
78427f5eec9a100b.expectation:
Pass:    CLI accepts preference values 0, 1, 32767, 65535; rejects 65536 with error. When preference keyword omitted, show output and BGP capture confirm default=32767. BGP ES route DF Election Ext-Comm octets 6-7 match configured value (big-endian u16). DF election selects PE with highest preference (DUT=10000 loses to PE=30000). After BGP session reset, DF re-converges to same winner within 3× es-waiting-time; IXIA traffic loss <1s.
Fail-on: CLI accepts value >65535 or rejects valid 0-65535 value. Default ≠ 32767 in show/capture. BGP ES route omits DF Election Ext-Comm or preference field is zero/corrupted. DF election result contradicts preference ordering. DF fails to reconverge after BGP session restore, or IXIA traffic blackhole >5s, or DF flip-flops between PEs.

78428632684e74df.action_steps:
Setup: Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-based; establish BGP EVPN peering; inject traffic via IXIA to learn local MAC on DUT and remote MAC from neighbor PE.
Action: Execute `show evpn mac address-table`.
Verify: Table displays both local and remote MAC entries with columns for MAC address, VLAN/instance, type (local/remote), and interface or remote-PE.
78428632684e74df.expectation:
Pass: Table contains both local and remote MAC entries, each with type indication and VLAN association.
Fail-on: Command errors, empty output when MACs exist, missing entry type, or incorrect entry type for known MAC.

789b1ab2fef4a107.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE1 with `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` for VLANs 10,20,30; configure multihomed `ethernet-segment` on agg-eth interface with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:01. IXIA emulates PE2 on same ESI via BGP EVPN peering.
Action: IXIA injects malformed Ethernet A-D per EVI routes (Type 1): (a) corrupted Ethernet Tag = 0xFFFFFFFF, (b) truncated route missing ESI field, (c) invalid DF algorithm extended community value 0xFF, (d) reserved bits set in Ethernet Tag ID. Inject 4 variants sequentially.
Verify: Execute `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirming DUT remains DF using modulo with VLAN 10 (lowest tag); `show bgp evpn` confirms zero malformed routes in table; `show system logging` displays 4 distinct malformed-route rejection entries; check EVPN error counter increments by 4.
789b1ab2fef4a107.expectation:
Pass:    All 4 malformed Type 1 route variants dropped; DUT DF status stable with modulo based on VLAN 10; EVPN route error counter = 4; syslog shows 4 rejection entries with BGP update error codes.
Fail-on: DUT crash or DF flap; DF election attempts to use corrupted tag 0xFFFFFFFF in modulo; any malformed route appears in `show bgp evpn` output; fewer than 4 syslog entries; error counter unchanged.

78e7bac17e847dfa.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two EVPN instances: `evpn evpn-vlan1 service-type vlan-based` and `evpn evpn-vab1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle`. Commit configuration.
Action: Execute `show evpn broadcast-domains` at the operational prompt.
Verify: Capture the output; confirm it lists both evpn-vlan1 and evpn-vab1 with their respective service types (vlan-based, vlan-aware-bundle) and operational status.
78e7bac17e847dfa.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
78e7bac17e847dfa.expectation:
Pass:    Output displays all configured broadcast domains (evpn-vlan1, evpn-vab1) with correct service-type labels and non-error status.
Fail-on: Configured broadcast domain missing from output, or service-type field shows incorrect value / unknown.

78e9425519a7f8c3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE1 with EVPN EVI using ingress replication mode (per RFC7432bis §16.1); establish VXLAN tunnels to 3 remote PEs (PE2, PE3, PE4) all in same EVI; set `configuration/l2-services/evpn advertise-mac enable` (default) and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default); attach IXIA traffic generator to DUT host-facing port in EVPN bridge domain.
Action: From IXIA, transmit single broadcast frame (dest MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF, e.g., ARP request) into DUT's EVPN-attached segment; capture egress VXLAN-encapsulated traffic at PE2, PE3, PE4 VTEP interfaces.
Verify: Execute `show l2-services evpn replication-list` on DUT to confirm all 3 remote PE VTEPs present; verify packet captures at PE2, PE3, PE4 each show exactly 1 VXLAN-encapsulated copy of the broadcast frame with matching inner Ethernet payload; check `show l2-services evpn statistics` for broadcast replication counter = 3.
78e9425519a7f8c3.equipment: DUT + 3 neighbor PEs + IXIA traffic gen (4-PE EVPN topology for all-remote ingress replication validation)
78e9425519a7f8c3.expectation:
Pass:    DUT ingress-replicates the single broadcast frame to all 3 remote PEs (3 unicast VXLAN copies); PE2, PE3, PE4 each receive 1 copy; replication counter = 3; satisfies RFC7432bis §16.1 MUST: "A given broadcast packet must be sent to all the remote PEs."
Fail-on: One or more remote PEs (PE2/PE3/PE4) do not receive the broadcast frame; replication counter < 3; replication-list incomplete; broadcast dropped or sent via non-ingress-replication path.

79476b96aa613fd0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` and `auto-discovery` enabled; establish IXIA as BGP EVPN peer.
Action: IXIA transmits BGP UPDATEs containing malformed Ethernet A-D per EVI routes (route type 1): ESI length ≠ 10 octets (e.g., 8 or 12 bytes), RD length ≠ 8 octets, Next Hop missing from MP_REACH_NLRI, reserved bits set in NLRI.
Verify: `show bgp evpn summary` (session stable), `show bgp evpn routes type 1` (no malformed routes installed), `show logging` (UPDATE error per variant), `show bgp evpn error-counters` (incremented).
79476b96aa613fd0.expectation:
Pass:    All four malformed variants rejected; BGP session remains established; error counter increments once per variant; syslog records specific error (e.g., "EVPN route type 1: ESI length invalid").
Fail-on: Crash, BGP session reset, any malformed A-D route appears in `show bgp evpn routes type 1`, error counter unchanged, or no syslog entry.

795056bfeb9c4688.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with advertise-mac enable, import-rt/export-rt configured; learn at least one MAC/IP locally (static or dynamic); optionally trigger a MAC move between ESIs to exercise MAC Mobility Extended Community and Sequence Number fields.
Action: After EVPN instance advertises Type 2 routes, collect tech-support including show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-ip, show mac-table, show evpn instance, running-config (advertise-mac, import-rt, export-rt), and syslog entries for MAC mobility/duplication events.
Verify: Parse tech-support output for Type 2 route structure: confirm presence of RD, ESI, ET-ID, MAC Address, IP Address, MPLS Label (LT1), L3 Label, RTs, Next-Hop=PE-IP; if MAC move triggered, confirm MAC Mobility Extended Community and incremented Sequence Number.
795056bfeb9c4688.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains show bgp l2vpn evpn commands displaying all mandatory Type 2 fields (RD, ESI, ET-ID, MAC, IP, Label LT1, L3 Label, RTs, Next-Hop); MAC table correlates learned MACs to advertised routes; running-config shows advertise-mac enable + RTs; if MAC mobility exercised, Extended Community and Sequence Number present in route.
Fail-on: Type 2 route fields incomplete (missing ESI, labels, RTs, or Next-Hop ≠ PE IP); MAC table/route mismatch; advertise-mac disabled in config; MAC Mobility Extended Community absent after relearning; Static Flag or Sequence Number fields not captured.

797cb4d699bb68b9.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT booted, CLI session via SSH. Enter `configure`, then `l2-services`. Create EVPN instance: `evpn TEST-EVPN service-type vlan-based`. Confirm `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default) to activate BUM routing. Commit configuration, exit to operational prompt.
Action: Execute `show evpn bum routing-table` to display all BUM routes. Execute `show evpn bum routing-table name TEST-EVPN` to filter by instance name. Re-execute base command to confirm idempotence on stable state.
Verify: Output renders without error, displays table with headers for BUM routing information (EVPN name, VLAN ID, route type, next-hop, label). Entries (if present) correspond to configured EVPN instances with BUM flooding enabled. Name-filtered output returns only TEST-EVPN entries. Repeated execution produces identical output.
797cb4d699bb68b9.expectation:
Pass:    `show evpn bum routing-table` executes successfully, produces well-formed tabular output with appropriate headers, name filter returns correct subset, command is idempotent on stable state.
Fail-on: CLI parser error, malformed output structure, incorrect name filtering, or output drift on stable system.

79866e3b8daaec72.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with all-active multi-homing ESI 01:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:AA on local CE-facing interface; configure two EVPN BGP peers (PE1 10.0.0.1, PE2 10.0.0.2) with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled under neighbor config; apply `import-rt 65000:100` for VPLS instance; enable `advertise-mac enable` in EVPN config; PE1 and PE2 both advertise MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 / IP 192.168.50.1 with RT 65000:100—PE1 includes Default Gateway extended community (type 0x030D), PE2 omits it.
Action: From IXIA connected to DUT's CE interface, send continuous traffic (100 pps) to 192.168.50.1; verify DUT selects PE1's route per RFC7432bis §7.13.1 criterion #1 (Default Gateway preference); administratively shut PE1's BGP session; capture re-convergence to PE2; re-enable PE1 and verify reversion; confirm local multi-homing ESI state remains stable throughout.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-ip 192.168.50.1` shows PE1 as best path (marked '>') when both routes present; `show evpn mac-table mac 00:11:22:33:44:55` confirms next-hop matches PE1's tunnel endpoint before/after failover cycle; `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:AA` shows local ESI continuously UP (DF election stable); IXIA traffic report shows packet loss per transition.
79866e3b8daaec72.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE (requires 2 neighbor PEs to advertise competing MAC/IP routes with/without Default Gateway community)
79866e3b8daaec72.expectation:
Pass:    DUT applies RFC7432bis §7.13.1 ordered criteria: selects PE1's route (with Default Gateway community) over PE2's route when both available; failover to PE2 and recovery to PE1 each complete with ≤50ms traffic interruption (≤5 packets at 100 pps); local multi-homing ESI remains UP with no DF re-election during remote best-path changes; MAC forwarding table updates match BGP RIB best-path.
Fail-on: DUT selects PE2's route as best while PE1's Default Gateway route is reachable (violates criterion order); convergence >100ms; local ESI flaps or DF election triggers during remote path change; BGP RIB and MAC table inconsistent; DUT load-balances between PE1/PE2 instead of selecting single best path.

7994ef1bd612937c.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE EVPN instance with P2MP MPLS LSP as inclusive tunnel (PMSI Tunnel attribute advertised in Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag route per RFC7432bis §11); configure `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default); IXIA connected to PE1.
Action: IXIA transmits 10 Gbps unknown unicast traffic (64-byte frames, 1000 unique destination MACs not in PE MAC tables) through PE1; measure per-PE throughput (Gbps, PPS), latency (min/avg/max/p99), and packet loss over 300-second sustained window; traffic replicates via P2MP LSP to PE2/PE3.
Verify: `show evpn statistics`, `show mpls lsp p2mp <lsp-id>`, `show l2-services vpls mac-table`, IXIA Rx counters at PE2/PE3 attachment circuits; confirm P2MP LSP replication to all remote PEs and unicast MAC treatment per RFC7432bis §12.2.
7994ef1bd612937c.expectation:
Pass:    Throughput ≥ 9.5 Gbps (≥ 95% line rate) sustained, latency avg ≤ 5 ms and p99 ≤ 20 ms, packet loss = 0%, all unknown unicast replicated via P2MP LSP to both PE2 and PE3.
Fail-on: Throughput < 9.5 Gbps, latency avg > 5 ms or p99 > 20 ms, packet loss > 0.01%, traffic not replicated via P2MP LSP (e.g., dropped or sent via incorrect tunnel type).

7a0344a82769fec6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as remote PE with EVPN instance peering to IXIA. Configure IXIA to emulate 2 PEs advertising Ethernet A-D per ES routes for shared ES (same ESI), with ESI Label ext-community redundancy mode = 0 (All-Active), plus valid MAC/IP Advertisement routes with MPLS label stacks.
Action: IXIA sends malformed EVPN route variants: (a) Ethernet A-D per ES with corrupted ESI Label ext-community (invalid attribute length, redundancy mode value >2, truncated TLV), (b) MAC/IP Advertisement with malformed MPLS label stack (label value >1048575, invalid S-bit encoding, empty label stack), (c) Ethernet A-D per ES with reserved bits set in ESI Label ext-community flags field.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms BGP sessions up; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows only valid routes installed; `show logging` captures parse error for each malformed update; IXIA sends Ethernet frames to advertised MACs and verifies 0% loss.
7a0344a82769fec6.expectation:
Pass:    DUT rejects all malformed routes at BGP NLRI parse stage; BGP sessions remain established; syslog contains error entry for each malformed update ("invalid ESI Label ext-community" or "malformed MPLS label"); EVPN RIB contains only valid routes; Ethernet frame forwarding 0% loss.
Fail-on: DUT crash, BGP session reset, malformed route appears in EVPN RIB, frame loss >0.1%, or no syslog/counter evidence of route rejection.

7a15e3143c99ee47.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT (receiving PE) peered with neighbor PE via BGP L2VPN EVPN; configure multi-homed agg-eth 0 on DUT with non-reserved ESI (identifier 1). Neighbor PE advertises MAC/IP routes: some with reserved ESI (0 or MAX-ESI per §9.2.2), others with non-reserved ESI matching DUT's agg-eth 0 identifier. Neighbor PE also advertises Ethernet A-D per ES routes for the non-reserved ESI. IXIA generates continuous traffic toward advertised MAC addresses.
Action: Flap PE-to-CE link on neighbor PE (bring down then up the multi-homed interface) to trigger ES route withdrawal and re-advertisement per §17.3. During route reconvergence on DUT, run tech-support bundle command. Extract bundle off-box.
Verify: Bundle contains: EVPN MAC table, EVPN ethernet-segment table, BGP L2VPN EVPN route RIB, show running-config, BGP/EVPN event logs with timestamps. Reserved ESI (0/MAX-ESI) entries: forwarding state installed from MAC/IP Advertisement route alone. Non-reserved ESI entries: forwarding state installed only after BOTH MAC/IP Advertisement route AND Ethernet A-D per ES routes present in RIB.
7a15e3143c99ee47.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
7a15e3143c99ee47.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle demonstrates RFC7432bis-§9.2.2 route resolution compliance during PE-to-CE failure recovery (§17.3): reserved ESI (0/MAX-ESI) forwarding installed from MAC/IP Advertisement route alone; non-reserved ESI forwarding installed only after both MAC/IP Advertisement route and Ethernet A-D per ES routes received; failure/recovery event sequence logged with timestamps.
Fail-on: Non-reserved ESI forwarding installed before Ethernet A-D per ES routes present; reserved ESI forwarding blocked awaiting Ethernet A-D routes; missing EVPN show outputs, BGP route RIB, or failure/recovery event logs.

7a633b090647cab7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with identical ESI (ethernet-segment identifier 0 <esi-value>) on agg-eth interfaces facing multi-homed CE; set load-balancing-mode all-active on both; establish EVPN instance and learn 100 MAC addresses from IXIA-emulated CE hosts.
Action: Disable PE1's agg-eth interface to trigger mass MAC mobility to PE2; after 5 seconds re-enable PE1's interface; repeat flap 2 more times with 2-second intervals to force rapid MAC re-mobility while IXIA maintains continuous traffic.
Verify: Run `show evpn mac` on both PEs after final convergence (wait 3 × es-waiting-time = 9 sec default); check MAC Mobility sequence numbers >1 for moved MACs; inspect BGP `show bgp l2vpn evpn` route updates for MAC Mobility extended community (0x06); measure per-flap traffic loss via IXIA counters; compare MAC tables across PEs for consistency.
7a633b090647cab7.equipment: Two routers + IXIA (PE1 and PE2 with multi-homed ESI config; IXIA emulates CE and traffic endpoints)
7a633b090647cab7.expectation:
Pass:    All 100 MACs show sequence number ≥3 (one per move); MAC/IP routes carry MAC Mobility extended community; traffic loss ≤1 sec per flap; MAC tables identical on PE1 and PE2 within 9 sec of final flap.
Fail-on: Any MAC with sequence number stuck at 0 or not incremented; missing extended community 0x06 in route updates; traffic blackhole >1 sec for any flap; MAC table mismatch between PEs persisting beyond 3 × es-waiting-time (9 sec).

7aad86da4ef56961.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure all-active ES (ethernet-segment / identifier 0 <9-octet-value> / load-balancing-mode all-active) on DUT and neighbor PE; establish EVI with ingress-replication; DUT is Non-DF, neighbor PE is DF for EVI-1; remote PE not attached to ES; IXIA attached to ES sends BUM traffic into EVI-1.
Action: Reset DUT control-plane (platform restart evpn-process or equivalent) while IXIA transmits BUM at 1000 pps; wait for ES re-convergence (≤ es-waiting-time default 3 s + BGP route re-advertisement).
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment <ES-id> confirms ESI label re-advertised in Ethernet A-D per ES route; capture BUM packet from DUT to DF neighbor PE contains DF's ESI label in MPLS stack; capture BUM to remote non-ES PE has no ESI label; DF's MPLS forwarding drops ESI-labeled BUM (IXIA RX counter = 0 looped frames).
7aad86da4ef56961.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (all-active ES peer) + remote PE (non-ES) + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
7aad86da4ef56961.expectation:
Pass:    ESI label re-advertised within 10 s; Non-DF DUT includes DF's ESI label in BUM to DF; no ESI label in BUM to non-ES PE; DF does not forward ESI-labeled BUM back to ES (IXIA RX = 0)
Fail-on: ESI label missing after recovery, BUM looped to ES (IXIA RX > 0), ESI label sent to non-ES PE, or Non-DF omits ESI label when sending to DF

7ad656df6e64907e.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with a VPLS instance configured under `configuration / l2-services / vpls`.
Action: Enter the VPLS instance, issue `auto-discovery`, configure `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:200`, and commit. Then issue `no auto-discovery` and commit.
Verify: After first commit, `show running-config l2-services` displays the auto-discovery section with both import-rt and export-rt entries; after `no auto-discovery`, the entire auto-discovery section is absent from running-config.
7ad656df6e64907e.expectation:
Pass:    `auto-discovery` command accepts import-rt and export-rt sub-commands and persists to running-config; `no auto-discovery` atomically removes the entire auto-discovery section including all route-target attributes
Fail-on: Sub-commands rejected, `no auto-discovery` leaves orphaned route-target entries in the VPLS instance, or auto-discovery configuration fails to persist across commit

7ae2c1cc4f9d11cd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP neighbor; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under that neighbor; verify session reaches Established state via `show bgp summary`.
Action: Execute `show bgp neighbors brief` and capture output; execute `show bgp neighbors detail <neighbor-ip>` for the configured peer; run `show bgp neighbors detail | include l2vpn` to filter EVPN address-family lines; run `show bgp neighbors brief | count` to test count filter.
Verify: `brief` displays neighbor IP, AS number, state (Established), uptime in tabular format; `detail` contains an 'Address Family: l2vpn evpn' (or 'af-l2vpn evpn') section showing advertised/received prefix counts and capabilities; `| include l2vpn` returns only lines with that token; `| count` returns integer matching line count of unfiltered brief output.
7ae2c1cc4f9d11cd.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN session required)
7ae2c1cc4f9d11cd.expectation:
Pass:    Both command variants execute without error; `detail` shows EVPN address-family block with route counters (0 or higher); output filters (include, count) return correct subsets.
Fail-on: Command rejected; no output when BGP session is up; EVPN address-family section missing from `detail` output; filter pipes return wrong subset, produce syntax error, or break columnar alignment.

7aec37661c0b71c5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `ethernet-segment` under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0`; leave es-waiting-time unconfigured (requirement default: 3 seconds).
Action: Set es-waiting-time to a non-default value, commit; verify value appears in `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`; then issue `no es-waiting-time`, commit.
Verify: After first commit, running-config contains explicit es-waiting-time configuration; after `no` form, es-waiting-time line removed and 3-second default restored.
7aec37661c0b71c5.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
7aec37661c0b71c5.expectation:
Pass:    es-waiting-time accepts non-default configuration and `no` form cleanly restores 3-second default.
