Fail-on: Configuration rejected, value missing from running-config, or `no` form fails to remove explicit config and restore default.

7b1455d62a390cee.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with shared ethernet-segment on agg-eth interface using ESI Type 1 = 01:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:00:01. Enable BGP EVPN peering between PEs (`configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor → af-l2vpn evpn`). Attach the agg-eth to an EVPN instance.
Action: Issue `show router bgp routes evpn ethernet-segment detail` on each PE to inspect advertised Type 4 routes. Optionally capture BGP UPDATE packets with Wireshark/tcpdump filtering on AFI=25/SAFI=70.
Verify: Decode the ES-Import Route Target extended community in the Type 4 route; confirm the 6-octet value = AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (high-order 6 octets of ESI, excluding Type byte and trailing 3 octets).
7b1455d62a390cee.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (both running EVPN) + packet-capture tool (tcpdump/Wireshark) or BGP route reflector for inspection
7b1455d62a390cee.expectation:
Pass:    ES-Import RT extended community in Type 4 route = AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF, exactly matching octets 2–7 of the configured ESI.
Fail-on: ES-Import RT missing from Type 4 advertisement, value != AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF, or Type 4 route not advertised at all.

7b8868af68fb4a94.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology (PE1, PE2) over MPLS core; BGP peering established; CE single-homed to PE1 access interface carrying VLAN 100.
Action: On PE1, navigate to `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor <PE2-loopback>` context, enter `af-l2vpn evpn`, and configure each of the BGP common commands specified in the requirement: allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix (set to 1000), private-as (set to remove), route-reflector-client, weight (set to 100), and group; commit and verify acceptance. Then under `configuration / l2-services`, configure `evpn evi-1 service-type vlan-based`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:1`, `export-rt 65000:1`, bind the access interface VLAN 100 to evi-1; commit. From IXIA through CE, send 1 Gbps bidirectional known-unicast traffic for 60 s.
Verify: `show running-config` displays all eight BGP common commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) persisted under the `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family context for the PE2 neighbor; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows PE2 neighbor in Established state with EVPN capability negotiated; `show evpn evi evi-1` reports operational; `show evpn mac address-table` contains ≥ 1 locally learned MAC (from CE) and ≥ 1 remote MAC (advertised by PE2); IXIA counters show ≥ 0.99 Gbps received bidirectionally with ≤ 0.1% packet loss.
7b8868af68fb4a94.expectation:
Pass:    Commit succeeds for all eight BGP common commands in `af-l2vpn evpn` context; configuration persisted in running-config under the neighbor's address-family stanza; BGP session reaches Established with EVPN capability within 30 s; EVI reaches operational state within 10 s of commit; MAC address-table populates with ≥ 1 local MAC + ≥ 1 remote MAC within 30 s; bidirectional traffic forwarding at ≥ 0.99 Gbps with ≤ 0.1% loss over the 60 s test window.
Fail-on: Any of the eight BGP common commands rejected with syntax error when configured in `af-l2vpn evpn` context, commit fails, BGP session does not negotiate EVPN address-family capability, EVI never reaches operational state, MAC address-table remains empty after 60 s, or packet loss exceeds 1%.

7bb67635b9e9f8b2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100`; configure `ethernet-segment` on `agg-eth 1/0/1` with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:01; attach VLANs 10, 20, 30 as BDs to the EVPN instance; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; IXIA generating traffic via multi-homed CE.
Action: Trigger control-plane process restart on DUT (e.g., `process restart bgp`) while traffic is active; neighbor PE captures BGP updates during recovery.
Verify: Use `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` to confirm 3 Ethernet A-D per EVI routes re-advertised (one per VLAN 10/20/30); verify each route contains RD, ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:01, ET ID = VLAN-ID (10/20/30), unique MPLS Label LT2 per <EVI, ES, ET> tuple, L2-Attr Primary flag, RTs 65000:100, Next Hop = DUT loopback; verify neighbor PE logs show A-D per ED routes (type 4) received before using A-D per EVI for forwarding (RFC7432bis ch.8 requirement); measure traffic convergence.
7bb67635b9e9f8b2.expectation:
Pass:    All 3 Ethernet A-D per EVI routes re-advertised within ≤ 3 s of process restart; each route has correct RD, ESI, ET ID (10/20/30), unique MPLS Label LT2, L2-Attr with Primary flag set, RTs 65000:100; neighbor PE receives both A-D per EVI and A-D per ED routes before forwarding; traffic recovers within documented convergence SLA (e.g., ≤ 5 s).
Fail-on: Missing A-D per EVI route for any VLAN after recovery; incorrect or duplicate MPLS Label LT2; missing L2-Attr Primary/Backup flags; neighbor PE forwards on A-D per EVI before receiving A-D per ED routes (RFC7432bis ch.8 violation); convergence exceeds SLA; sustained packet loss beyond convergence window.

7bca2fb54bfe1a3d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT in private AS 64512 with BGP neighbor 10.0.0.2 (public AS 100). Under af-l2vpn evpn configuration for this neighbor, enable `private-as`. Create a vlan-based EVPN instance with a route-target.
Action: Activate the EVPN instance by attaching an interface or injecting MAC entries; DUT originates MAC/IP advertisement routes (type 2) with AS_PATH starting at private AS 64512 and advertises them to neighbor 10.0.0.2.
Verify: On neighbor PE at 10.0.0.2, examine the BGP L2VPN EVPN table (`show bgp l2vpn evpn` or equivalent); inspect AS_PATH attribute of received type-2 routes to confirm private AS 64512 has been stripped.
7bca2fb54bfe1a3d.expectation:
Pass:    AS_PATH in EVPN routes at neighbor 10.0.0.2 does not contain private AS 64512 (or any AS in ranges 64512-65534, 4200000000-4294967294)
Fail-on: Private AS number 64512 appears in AS_PATH on the neighbor; private-as feature failed to strip it

7c09484f94668469.action_steps:
Problem: validate that when the same MAC address moves between PEs more than N times within M seconds, the detecting PE raises an alert and stops advertising that MAC in BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes, preventing infinite sequence number increment per RFC7432bis §15.1.
Method: build a 2-PE EVPN topology with configurable move-count (N) and move-interval (M) thresholds, force a host MAC to oscillate between PE1 and PE2 access ports exceeding the threshold, capture BGP UPDATEs to confirm advertisement cessation, and verify alarm generation.
Setup: PE1 and PE2 in same EVPN instance (matching EVI, RT import/export), both running BGP EVPN AF. Configure `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 600` on both PEs (5 moves in 180 sec triggers detection, 600 sec freeze). IXIA emulates hosts on PE1 x-eth 0/0/1 and PE2 x-eth 0/0/1 in the same VLAN.
Action: IXIA sends frames with source MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF alternating between PE1-connected port (moves 1, 3, 5, 7) and PE2-connected port (moves 2, 4, 6, 8) at 25-second intervals (6 moves in 125 sec < 180 sec window, exceeding move-count 5). Capture BGP session packets between PE1 and PE2 (tcpdump on transport link or IXIA). Monitor syslog on both PEs and syslog collector.
Verify: After 6th move, check `show evpn mac address-table` on both PEs — MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF must show duplicate/frozen state. Check `show bgp l2vpn evpn` — no new MAC/IP Advertisement routes for AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF after detection. Verify syslog alarm indicating duplicate MAC detected. In BGP capture, confirm MAC/IP Advertisement routes cease after threshold. Trigger 7th and 8th move — confirm no BGP updates sent and MAC remains frozen for 600 sec (freeze-time). Verify `move-interval` and `move-count` values are operator-configurable (test with alternate thresholds: move-interval 120, move-count 3).
7c09484f94668469.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
7c09484f94668469.expectation:
Pass:    After 6th MAC move (exceeding move-count 5 within move-interval 180), both PEs generate duplicate-MAC alarm, freeze MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF in local table, and stop sending/updating/processing BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes for that MAC. `show evpn mac address-table` marks MAC as duplicate. Subsequent moves (7th, 8th) produce no BGP updates. Frozen state persists for freeze-time 600 sec. move-interval and move-count parameters accept operator-configured values.
Fail-on: No alarm after threshold exceeded, or BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes continue to be sent/updated/processed after 6th move, or MAC not frozen in forwarding table, or move-interval / move-count not configurable, or frozen state clears before freeze-time expires.

7c0deed85f358226.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance using `evpn <name> service-type vlan-based`, establish a BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled, and ensure at least one local MAC address exists in the DUT's forwarding table.
Action: In the EVPN instance configuration mode, issue `advertise-mac disable` and commit. Inspect BGP advertisements to the peer. Then issue `no advertise-mac`, commit, and inspect again.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `advertise-mac disable` when set, or shows the setting absent (default enable) after `no advertise-mac`; BGP route table on the neighbor (or `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output on DUT) shows zero type-2 MAC/IP routes when disabled, and local MAC routes present when enabled.
7c0deed85f358226.expectation:
Pass:    `advertise-mac disable` prevents all local MAC addresses from being advertised in BGP EVPN type-2 routes; `no advertise-mac` or default configuration results in local MACs being advertised to the peer.
Fail-on: Type-2 MAC/IP routes for local addresses are sent to the BGP peer when `advertise-mac disable` is configured, or are not sent when enabled.

7ce464698a04c0aa.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 with `evpn test-bundle service-type vlan-bundle` in `configuration / l2-services` mode; bind VLANs 200, 201, 202, 203 to the bundle on interface x-eth 0/0/1; complete BGP EVPN peering and commit.
Action: From IXIA connected to x-eth 0/0/1, send 100-frame burst with unique source MACs on VLAN 201 (in-bundle). Capture `show evpn mac` and interface counters. Then send 100-frame burst with unique source MACs on VLAN 150 (out-of-bundle); capture same counters immediately after.
Verify: Compare MAC table and interface statistics before/after each burst. Check `show evpn evi test-bundle` for VLAN membership. Inspect ingress drop counters on x-eth 0/0/1.
7ce464698a04c0aa.expectation:
Pass:    VLAN 201 frames forwarded normally; 100 MACs learned and visible in `show evpn mac` for test-bundle. VLAN 150 frames dropped; zero MACs learned from VLAN 150; ingress drop counter on x-eth 0/0/1 increments by 100.
Fail-on: Any VLAN 150 frame forwarded, or any VLAN 150 source MAC appears in the EVPN MAC table. OR VLAN 201 frames dropped (false positive).

7d16ec823a44588f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'evpn100' service-type vlan-based; execute `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface GigabitEthernet 0/0/1` under configuration/l2-services/evpn evpn100; configure BGP EVPN auto-discovery with export-rt 65000:100 import-rt 65000:100; start IXIA sending 1000 pps to destination MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 for ≥ 1 min; verify static MAC in `show evpn mac-address static evpn100` and BGP Type-2 route at neighbor PE.
Action: While traffic flows, execute `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface GigabitEthernet 0/0/2` (change interface); wait 2 s; execute `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface GigabitEthernet 0/0/1` (restore).
Verify: IXIA reports 0 packet loss during both interface changes; `show evpn mac-address static evpn100` reflects each interface within ≤ 1 s; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on neighbor PE shows updated Type-2 MAC/IP Advertisement route with new ESI/label.
7d16ec823a44588f.expectation:
Pass:    Zero packet loss during static MAC interface modification; show output updated within 1 s of each change; BGP Type-2 route advertisement reflects interface change at neighbor PE within 1 s; spec MUST: "Configuration and advertising of local MAC addresses MUST be supported."
Fail-on: Packet loss > 0 during config change, or static MAC interface not updated in show output within 1 s, or BGP Type-2 route not updated at neighbor PE within 1 s

7d4aa5690a8fbf78.action_steps:
Setup:  On PE1 and PE2, configure identical ES under interface agg-eth: `identifier 0 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01` and `load-balancing-mode single-active`. Configure DUT (remote PE) in same EVPN instance. IXIA CE multihomed to PE1/PE2 via LACP LAG.
Action: Bring up BGP sessions PE1↔DUT and PE2↔DUT. On DUT, verify received Ethernet A-D per ES routes (route-type 1) via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 <esi>` and confirm ESI Label extended community has "Multihoming redundancy mode" field = 1. Inject MAC from IXIA CE; verify DUT receives MAC/IP Advertisement (route-type 2) from single active PE with L2-Attr extended community P bit = 1.
Verify: On DUT, `show evpn ethernet-segment <esi>` confirms Single-Active mode deduced (not All-Active). Send continuous traffic from IXIA; verify DUT forwards only via active PE (not load-balanced across PE1 and PE2). Confirm zero packet loss during BGP convergence window (default es-waiting-time = 3 seconds).
7d4aa5690a8fbf78.equipment: Three PEs (PE1 and PE2 configured with single-active ES + DUT as remote observer) + IXIA CE (LACP-capable)
7d4aa5690a8fbf78.expectation:
Pass:    DUT correctly deduces Single-Active mode from ESI Label extended community (redundancy mode = 1), forwards traffic to MAC via only the active PE (P bit = 1), and maintains zero packet loss during BGP convergence.
Fail-on: DUT treats ES as All-Active mode, or load-balances traffic across both PE1 and PE2, or drops packets during convergence, or forwards to inactive PE.

7d5c9d797e950f0f.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with a VPLS instance configured; navigate to `configuration / l2-services / vpls` mode for that instance; default MAC aging time in effect; IXIA ports attached to VPLS access interfaces.
Action: Issue `mac-aging-time 120` and commit. From IXIA, send L2 unicast frames to learn 10 distinct source MAC addresses in the VPLS MAC table. Stop traffic. Wait 130 seconds. Then issue `no mac-aging-time` and commit.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `mac-aging-time 120` under the VPLS instance after first commit. MAC table show command confirms all learned MACs aged out after 120-130 s. After `no mac-aging-time`, verify running-config shows default value (explicit parameter absent or default displayed).
7d5c9d797e950f0f.expectation:
Pass:    `mac-aging-time 120` accepted; all MACs aged out within 130 s (aging functional at configured interval); `no mac-aging-time` accepted and default aging behavior restored
Fail-on: Command rejected at commit; any MACs still present in table at 130 s check; `no` form rejected; default not restored in running-config

7d8be64437e05702.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT booted; EVPN instance `evpn-1` created via `evpn evpn-1` under `configuration / l2-services`.
Action: Capture initial `show running-config` (should not show explicit `es-waiting-time`). Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-1`; issue `es-waiting-time 8`; commit; capture running-config. Issue `no es-waiting-time`; commit; capture running-config.
Verify: Initial running-config omits `es-waiting-time`. After `es-waiting-time 8`, config displays the line. After `no es-waiting-time`, line is absent. Default (3 seconds per documentation) can be verified via `show running-config all` (if supported) or operational `show evpn` commands.
7d8be64437e05702.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
7d8be64437e05702.expectation:
Pass:    `es-waiting-time <seconds>` accepted, persists in running-config; `no es-waiting-time` removes explicit config; default is 3 seconds.
Fail-on: Command rejected; value doesn't persist after commit; `no` form fails; default ≠ 3 seconds.

7d955ca2cb08af52.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that tech-support captures complete DF election diagnostic state including the mandatory fallback to Default algorithm when PEs advertise mismatched DF algorithms in their ES Route Type 4 advertisements.
Method: Configure PE1 with `service-carving highest-random-weight` and PE2 with `service-carving highest-preference preference 100` on a shared all-active ESI, drive traffic through the LAG, trigger the RFC8584 algorithm mismatch fallback per EVPNS-REQ#120, then verify tech-support contains DF state, Route Type 4 with DF Election Extended Community, Route Type 1, ES-Import RT, and fallback event evidence.
Setup: Two PEs (PE1, PE2) connected to the same CE via LACP LAG (shared ESI: `identifier 0 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01`). PE1: `load-balancing-mode all-active` + `service-carving highest-random-weight` under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment`. PE2: `load-balancing-mode all-active` + `service-carving highest-preference preference 100`. EVI configured on both PEs with MAC addresses learned and traffic flowing between CEs.
Action: Confirm PE1 and PE2 exchange BGP Route Type 4 (ES Route) with differing DF Election Extended Community 'DF Alg' values (HRW=2 vs Pref=3 per RFC8584). Verify both PEs detect the mismatch and fall back to Default algorithm (RFC7432bis §8.5 modulo). Drive EVPN traffic across the LAG to populate DF state per EVI. Run `tech-support` on PE1, save bundle off-box.
Verify: Bundle contains: (1) `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` with ESI, load-balancing mode, service-carving algorithm; (2) `show evpn df` with per-EVI DF assignment after fallback to Default; (3) `show evpn routes type 4` showing ES Route with DF Election Extended Community field; (4) `show evpn routes type 1` showing Ethernet A-D per ES routes; (5) `show running-config` section with `service-carving highest-random-weight` command; (6) syslog or debug output indicating DF algorithm mismatch detection and fallback to Default; (7) ES-Import RT in BGP tables.
7d955ca2cb08af52.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
7d955ca2cb08af52.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains complete DF election evidence: configured `service-carving` algorithm, Route Type 4 with DF Election Extended Community showing advertised 'DF Alg' value, Route Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES), ES-Import RT per RFC7432bis-§7.6, per-EVI DF assignment from `show evpn df`, and syslog entries confirming the MUST fallback to Default algorithm when detecting the mismatch (EVPNS-REQ#120 behavior that overrides RFC8584's heterogeneous algorithm support by mandating fallback).
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn df` output, missing DF Election Extended Community in Route Type 4, missing Route Type 1 advertisements, missing ES-Import RT, missing syslog evidence of algorithm mismatch detection and fallback, or bundle shows PEs remained on their configured algorithms (HRW/Pref) instead of falling back to Default—indicating the MUST fallback clause failed.

7da9e1f7d83128b0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with shared ESI on agg-eth 0 and `load-balancing-mode single-active`. Establish EVPN instance and LACP to CE.
Action: Verify DF election completes via `show evpn df` (one PE primary, one backup). Collect tech-support bundles from both PEs and save off-box.
Verify: Inspect both bundles for `show evpn df` output confirming distinct DF roles, `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` showing ES state, BGP route dumps containing L2-Attr extended community, and running-config reflecting single-active configuration.
7da9e1f7d83128b0.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support from each PE captures its assigned DF role (primary or backup); BGP routes include RFC7432bis §8.6 L2-Attr signaling; ES configuration is present in running-config.
Fail-on: DF role missing or ambiguous in bundle, BGP routes lack L2-Attr extended community, or both PEs show primary role.

7de82c819d0bbce4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with shared ESI type 0 (00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88), `load-balancing-mode all-active` under `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment`; both PEs advertise EVI 100. DUT (remote PE) peers with PE1 and PE2 via MP-BGP EVPN, imports their routes. IXIA CE port behind PE1/PE2 sends frames with src MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01; PE1 advertises type 2 route with label 100001, PE2 with label 100002.
Action: On DUT: `show bgp evpn route-type 1 esi 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88` → verify both PE1 and PE2 routes carry ESI Label extended community with redundancy mode = 0. `show bgp evpn route-type 2 mac aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01` → confirm 2 routes with distinct labels. `show l2-services mac-table bridge-domain 100 mac aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01` → check next-hop count. Send unicast traffic from IXIA (DUT access port) to MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01; capture MPLS frames on DUT→PE1 and DUT→PE2 core links.
Verify: Ethernet A-D per ES routes from PE1 and PE2 both show redundancy mode = 0 in ESI Label extended community (RFC7432bis §14.1.2). DUT MAC table installs aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 with 2 ECMP next-hops (PE1/label 100001, PE2/label 100002). Captured MPLS packets use label 100001 on PE1 path, 100002 on PE2 path, confirming DUT constructs label stack from received type 2 routes per RFC7432bis §14.1.2 3rd MUST.
7de82c819d0bbce4.equipment: DUT (remote PE) + 2 neighbor PEs (shared ES, All-Active mode) + IXIA traffic gen
7de82c819d0bbce4.expectation:
Pass:    DUT deduces All-Active mode (both type 1 routes show redundancy mode = 0); constructs 2-way ECMP next-hop set for MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 using labels (100001, 100002) from type 2 routes; traffic load-balances with correct per-PE MPLS label stack per RFC7432bis §14.1.2.
Fail-on: DUT installs only 1 next-hop (treats ES as Single-Active despite redundancy mode = 0), or MPLS label in forwarded packets ≠ labels from received type 2 routes, or no load-balancing observed (all traffic to single PE).

7debe5cce0226ff0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (EVI 100, BD 100) with RD 65000:100, RT 100:100, and P-tunnel mode (ingress-replication or mLDP P2MP); enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbor to a receiving PE.
Action: Activate the EVPN instance; capture the BGP EVPN route-type 3 (IMET) advertisement sent to the neighbor PE; parse the PMSI Tunnel attribute (BGP path attribute type 22) from the update.
Verify: Confirm PMSI Tunnel attribute is present in the IMET route and contains (a) tunnel type/identifier (e.g., Ingress Replication tunnel-endpoint=<DUT-loopback>, or mLDP P2MP FEC), and (b) a 20-bit MPLS label value unique to BD 100; repeat for a second EVI 200 and confirm a distinct label.
7debe5cce0226ff0.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN peer) + packet capture (Wireshark/tcpdump on peer or DUT)
7debe5cce0226ff0.expectation:
Pass:    IMET route carries PMSI Tunnel attribute with tunnel-ID field populated (ingress-replication endpoint or P-multicast tree-ID per RFC6514 §5) and a unique MPLS label per BD (label₁₀₀ ≠ label₂₀₀); `show bgp l2vpn evpn route type imet` reflects the label and tunnel-type.
Fail-on: IMET route missing PMSI Tunnel attribute, attribute missing tunnel-ID or label fields, or two BDs share the same label (violates RFC7432bis §11.2 uniqueness requirement).

7e3c425c35ed8704.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that all-active multi-homed PEs with mismatched DF election algorithms fall back to Default algorithm per RFC7432bis §8.5, and that Type 4 routes correctly advertise the DF Election Extended Community.
Method: Configure two PEs with matching ESI and all-active mode but different service-carving algorithms, capture Type 4 BGP routes to decode DF Election EC, and verify both PEs converge to Default algorithm with exactly one DF per VLAN.
Setup: PE1 and PE2 connect to shared CE via LACP LAG; configure `interface agg-eth 0`, `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 1`, `load-balancing-mode all-active` on both; PE1: `service-carving highest-preference preference 40000`; PE2: `service-carving default`; bring up VLAN-aware bundle EVI on both.
Action: Bring ES operational; capture BGP UPDATEs on PE1↔PE2 link; decode Type 4 (ES Route) Extended Communities from both PEs to extract DF Election EC 'DF Alg' field; wait for DF election convergence; generate BUM traffic from CE; capture access-side frames on both PEs.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms Type 4 routes from both PEs with differing DF Alg values in DF Election EC; `show evpn ethernet-segment` reports Default algorithm on both (fallback from mismatch); exactly one DF per VLAN; packet captures show only DF forwards BUM; ES-Import RT EC present in Type 4; Type 1 routes advertised; known unicast load-balanced.
7e3c425c35ed8704.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
7e3c425c35ed8704.expectation:
Pass: Both PEs fall back to Default algorithm (visible in `show evpn ethernet-segment`); one DF per VLAN; only DF forwards BUM; all-active load balancing for known unicast; Type 4 DF Election EC and ES-Import RT present; Type 1 routes advertised.
Fail-on: No fallback to Default when DF Alg mismatch detected; split-brain (multiple DFs) or no DF elected; non-DF forwards BUM; missing DF Election EC or ES-Import RT EC in Type 4; Type 1 routes absent.

7e74811ca0bb0474.action_steps:
Setup:  On pre-upgrade image, configure three ESI types: interface Ethernet1 with ESI type 0 = 00:01:02:03:04:05:06:07:08:09 (operator-configured), Port-Channel10 (members Ethernet2-3, lacp system-priority 100, system-id 00:11:22:33:44:55) with ESI type 1 (LACP-derived), interface Ethernet4 with ESI type 4 (router-id-derived, single-homed). Capture pre-upgrade baseline: show evpn ethernet-segment → record three ESI values; show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 and route-type 4 → record RD, ESI, Ethernet-tag per ES.
Action: Execute onie-install <new-image-URL>; DUT reboots to upgraded image and re-establishes BGP EVPN adjacency with neighbor PE.
Verify: show running-config: ESI type 0 manual value 00:01:02:03:04:05:06:07:08:09 on Ethernet1, Port-Channel10 LACP system-id preserved, ESI type 4 config on Ethernet4 present. show evpn ethernet-segment: three ES entries with ESI values matching pre-upgrade baseline. show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 and route-type 4: routes re-advertised with identical ESI, RD, Ethernet-tag.
7e74811ca0bb0474.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + ONIE image server
7e74811ca0bb0474.expectation:
Pass:    running-config contains all three ESI type configurations unchanged; show evpn ethernet-segment lists three ES with ESI values matching pre-upgrade baseline; BGP neighbor up within 120 sec; route type 1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery per-ES) and route type 4 (Ethernet Segment) re-advertised with identical ESI values within 60 sec of adjacency up; no 'ES down' or 'ESI changed' events in syslog.
Fail-on: Any ESI type config missing, altered, or corrupted in running-config; show evpn ethernet-segment reports fewer than three ES or ESI values differ from baseline; BGP route type 1 or 4 not re-advertised; neighbor PE logs 'ESI mismatch' or route withdrawal; show logging includes 'ES operationally down', 'config replay error', or 'LACP system-id conflict' post-upgrade.

7e8815e7582fd604.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (PE1, PE2) with EVPN instance using `evpn MH-TEST`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100` on both; establish BGP peering with `af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor config; provision access LAG agg-eth 0 to shared CE, agg-eth 1 to CE with LACP enabled, and single-homed x-eth 0/0/1 on PE1.
Action: Configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0 with ESI Type 0 (manual value 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88) on both PEs; configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 1 with ESI Type 1 (LACP-derived); configure ethernet-segment on x-eth 0/0/1 with ESI Type 4 (router-id + ifIndex auto-generation); commit configuration.
Verify: Execute `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` on all PEs; confirm Type 0 displays 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88 on both PE1 and PE2; confirm Type 1 shows LACP-derived ESI on PE1; confirm Type 4 shows auto-generated ESI on PE1; execute `show evpn df` to verify DF election; capture ES route Type 4 advertisements via `show bgp l2vpn evpn` for each ESI.
7e8815e7582fd604.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
7e8815e7582fd604.expectation:
Pass:    All three ESI types (0, 1, 4) successfully configured and operational; Type 0 shows identical manual ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88 on PE1 and PE2; Type 1 shows LACP-derived ESI and LAG operational; Type 4 shows router-id-based ESI; ES route Type 4 advertised for each ethernet-segment.
Fail-on: Any ESI type rejected or not recognized; Type 0 ESI mismatch between PEs; Type 1 fails on non-LACP interface; Type 4 fails on multi-homed interface; missing ES route advertisements; LACP LAG down.

7e8cc99ffdf183fd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with 3 MAC-VRFs (configuration/l2-services/evpn: evpn-1, evpn-2, evpn-3 with service-type vlan-based) without explicit RD; assign export-rt 65000:1, 65000:2, 65000:3; enable advertise-mac per instance. Establish BGP EVPN AF session with neighbor PE (configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor/af-l2vpn evpn); connect IXIA with one L2 port per EVPN instance.
Action: From IXIA, send L2 frames with unique source MACs (MAC-A to evpn-1, MAC-B to evpn-2, MAC-C to evpn-3) to trigger MAC learning and EVPN Type-2 route advertisement. Verify BGP session established on DUT.
Verify: On DUT: `show l2-services evpn evpn-1|evpn-2|evpn-3` to extract auto-generated RD values. On neighbor PE: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` to display received Type-2 routes with RD prefix. Capture BGP UPDATE on neighbor PE and decode NLRI to confirm 8-octet RD field precedes MAC length field (RFC7432bis §7.9). Verify all 3 RDs are distinct and non-zero.
7e8cc99ffdf183fd.expectation:
Pass:    DUT auto-assigns 3 distinct RDs (Type 1 format recommended: PE-loopback-IP:unique-number per RFC4364); each advertised EVPN Type-2 route carries the corresponding MAC-VRF's RD in NLRI; RD encoding is 8 octets per RFC7432bis §7.9; no RD collision across MAC-VRFs.
Fail-on: Two or more MAC-VRFs share identical RD, or RD is 0:0 in BGP UPDATE, or RD field missing from NLRI, or neighbor PE logs RD malformation / rejects routes, or EVPN Type-2 routes not advertised.

7fc781d78fdc61ab.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `advertise-mac enable` (default). On two PEs, create an Ethernet Segment with shared ESI (e.g., 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11), DF election mode preference. Connect IXIA CE to the multi-homed ES port.
Action: From IXIA CE, send three learning triggers per RFC7432bis §9.1: (1) DHCP Discover (source MAC1), (2) ARP Request for DUT's VLAN interface MAC (source MAC2), (3) ARP Request for a remote peer MAC (source MAC3). Then shut/no-shut the ES interface on the non-DF PE to force DF re-election.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-table` (MAC1/2/3 present), `show router bgp routes evpn mac` (Type-2 routes include ESI 01:11:...), `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment` (DF role transitioned, no errors).
7fc781d78fdc61ab.expectation:
Pass:    All three data-plane learning triggers succeed (DHCP, ARP-for-own-MAC, ARP-for-peer per RFC7432bis §9.1 MUSTs); learned MACs in local table; BGP Type-2 routes carry correct ESI from multi-homed ES; DF election completes; new DF re-advertises routes.
Fail-on: MAC learning fails for any trigger type; Type-2 routes missing ESI or both PEs advertise same MAC simultaneously; MAC entries lost during DF switchover; DF election does not converge or errors.

7fd17cfaff1fc993.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP peering between DUT (AS 65001) and IXIA (emulating neighbor PE AS 65002) with af-l2vpn evpn address-family. Configure `allow-as-in 2` under the neighbor af-l2vpn evpn configuration. Prepare four EVPN type-2 routes on IXIA with AS_PATH: (1) [65002], (2) [65002, 65001], (3) [65002, 65001, 65001], (4) [65002, 65001, 65001, 65001].
Action: Advertise all four routes from IXIA to DUT via BGP UPDATE messages. Wait for convergence (~30 seconds).
Verify: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on DUT. Count accepted routes. Inspect AS_PATH attributes in the output. Check BGP logs for rejection messages.
7fd17cfaff1fc993.expectation:
Pass:    Exactly three routes (1, 2, 3) are present in the BGP EVPN table with their complete AS_PATH visible. Route 4 is absent, and BGP logs show AS_PATH loop rejection for it.
Fail-on: Fewer than three routes accepted (routes 2 or 3 rejected despite ≤ 2 AS occurrences), OR four routes accepted (route 4 not rejected despite 3 AS occurrences).

7fdbb03d6d43b265.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE with EVPN instance (advertise-mac enable [default per CLI], import-rt/export-rt configured), attach IXIA ports as CE devices in the EVPN bridge domain.
Action: IXIA CEs send frames from 4 distinct source MACs: (a) DHCP Discover from MAC1, (b) ARP Request for PE gateway MAC from MAC2, (c) ARP Request for peer CE from MAC3, (d) unicast Ethernet frame from MAC4.
Verify: show l2-services evpn mac-table displays all 4 MACs with learn-type local and correct port binding; neighbor PE show router bgp routes evpn mac-ip confirms RT-2 (MAC Advertisement) routes received.
7fdbb03d6d43b265.expectation:
Pass:    All 4 CE MACs learned via data-plane per RFC7432bis §9.1 MUST (standard IEEE Ethernet learning procedures); RT-2 routes advertised with correct RD, RT, ESI, Ethernet-Tag.
Fail-on: Any of the 4 MACs missing from local table, learn-type not local, RT-2 routes not generated/received, or MAC-to-port binding incorrect.

805b99af3f5f99c4.action_steps:
Setup:  Under 'configuration / l2-services', configure 'evpn EVPN-100 service-type vlan-bundle' with VLANs 100,200 attached to x-eth 0/0/1; under 'evpn EVPN-100 / auto-discovery' set 'import-rt 65000:100', 'export-rt 65000:100'; verify 'advertise-mac enable' (default). Establish EVPN BGP peering with neighbor PE (matching RT config).
Action: From IXIA, transmit 1000 fps each of: VLAN 100 (in-bundle), VLAN 150 (not-in-bundle), VLAN 200 (in-bundle), VLAN 300 (not-in-bundle) to DUT interface x-eth 0/0/1.
Verify: Run 'show evpn instance EVPN-100 statistics' and 'show interface x-eth 0/0/1 counters detail' on DUT; capture at neighbor PE ingress to count received frames per VLAN-ID.
805b99af3f5f99c4.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts 2000 fps (VLAN 100+200 combined), drops 2000 fps (VLAN 150+300 combined); neighbor PE receives exactly VLAN 100 and 200 frames only (2000 fps total, zero frames for VLAN 150 or 300). DUT interface drop counters increment by 2000 fps.
Fail-on: Any frames tagged VLAN 150 or 300 reach neighbor PE, or frames tagged VLAN 100 or 200 are dropped by DUT, or DUT drop counters remain zero while transmitting out-of-bundle VLANs 150,300.

8068297262f1f69f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE in same EVPN instance (configuration / l2-services / evpn) with ingress-replication P-tunnel mode; configure ESI multi-homing with identical ESI value on shared Ethernet segment; verify unknown-mac-flooding enable (default); confirm show bgp evpn imet-route displays type-3 IMET routes with ingress-replication labels in P-tunnel attribute.
Action: From IXIA at neighbor PE site, send Ethernet frame dst MAC=00:00:00:AA:BB:CC (unicast, unknown to DUT); neighbor PE encapsulates with DUT's ingress-replication label from IMET route, sends via MPLS core; DUT receives packet with ingress-replication label.
Verify: DUT decapsulates and treats as unknown-unicast per RFC7432bis §12.1 (unicast MAC MUST be treated as unknown-unicast); floods to local CEs including multi-homed segment; show evpn mac-table confirms unknown-unicast flood count increment; show evpn ethernet-segment shows DF unchanged; IXIA on multi-homed segment captures single frame copy (DF logic intact).
8068297262f1f69f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT treats ingress-replication labeled packet as unknown-unicast (RFC7432bis §12.1 MUST); floods to local CEs, not BGP peers; multi-homed segment receives one copy via DF; flood counters increment; no label lookup failure.
Fail-on: Packet dropped or not flooded; re-sent to BGP peers (loop); duplicate or zero frames on ESI segment (DF broken); unicast MAC treated as multicast/broadcast; flood counters unchanged; MPLS label error.

80a97528d2d6894a.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology; MPLS core; BGP EVPN established. On PE1 configure `evpn bundle-1 service-type vlan-bundle` in l2-services mode, bind VLANs 200-203 to the bundle on interface x-eth 0/0/1, commit. Verify bundle EVI is up.
Action: From IXIA send 1000 frames (mix of unicast DMAC and broadcast) tagged with VLAN 100 into x-eth 0/0/1. VLAN 100 is NOT part of the configured 200-203 bundle.
Verify: `show evpn mac address-table` contains zero entries for VLAN 100; `show interface x-eth 0/0/1 counters` shows discard increments matching frame count; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows no Type-2 or Type-3 routes for VLAN 100; tcpdump at PE2 captures zero frames from VLAN 100.
80a97528d2d6894a.expectation:
Pass:    All 1000 VLAN 100 frames dropped at PE1 ingress; MAC table empty for VLAN 100; no BGP routes advertised; PE2 receives nothing.
Fail-on: Any VLAN 100 frame forwarded to PE2, or MAC learning occurs for VLAN 100, or BGP Type-2/Type-3 route advertised for the non-bundle VLAN.

8121697549155fec.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE in MPLS network with BGP EVPN session established; configure identical ESI (e.g., 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on both PEs under `configuration / l2-services / evpn` with ethernet-segment; enable DF election algorithm type 2 (Highest-Preference) WITHOUT explicit preference value to verify default.
Action: Bring up ethernet segment on both PEs; trigger Type 4 route advertisement by enabling `auto-discovery` with `import-rt` / `export-rt` under EVPN instance; confirm BGP EVPN peer session exchanges routes.
Verify: On DUT: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 esi <ESI>`; on 3rd-party: equivalent command (Cisco: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4`, Juniper: `show route table bgp.evpn.0 evpn-ethernet-segment-route`); inspect DF Election Extended Community for algorithm type 2 and preference value; verify ES-Import RT = high-order 6 octets of ESI; confirm both PEs elect identical DF.
8121697549155fec.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise/accept Type 4 routes with DF Election Extended Community present; default DF preference = 32767 in both directions (verified in route detail output); ES-Import RT correctly derived from ESI; identical DF elected (same interface designated on both sides).
Fail-on: Type 4 route rejected by either PE; DF preference != 32767 when not explicitly configured; ES-Import RT mismatch; DF election conflict (both or neither PE claims DF); route missing RD, ESI, or Originating Router IP fields per RFC8584 ch.2.2.

81c3d93682047865.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance using `evpn <name> service-type vlan-based`. Enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP session to neighbor PE. Configure import route-target to accept routes from neighbor. On neighbor PE, advertise EVPN type 1 (Ethernet AD with ESI), type 2 (MAC/IP), and type 3 (IMET) routes.
Action: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi` on DUT.
Verify: Output displays route type 1 entry (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) showing ESI and EVI, route type 2 entry (MAC/IP Advertisement) showing MAC and EVI, route type 3 entry (Inclusive Multicast) showing originator IP and EVI. Confirm only imported routes appear.
81c3d93682047865.expectation:
Pass:    Output contains at least one entry for each of route types 1, 2, and 3; each entry displays the EVI; only routes matching configured import route-target are shown.
Fail-on: Missing any of route types 1, 2, or 3 in output; non-imported routes appear; command error or empty output when imported routes exist in BGP RIB.
81c3d93682047865.req_id: CLI:show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi
81c3d93682047865.sub_category: show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi

81d7ed64b2599bcb.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure IRB interface with default gateway MAC; enable `configuration/l2-services/evpn/advertise-mac enable`; establish BGP peer with `af-l2vpn evpn` in `configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor`.
Action: Use IXIA to inject malformed BGP UPDATEs containing MAC routes with corrupted Default Gateway extended community (invalid type-high/type-low, bad length field, reserved bits set, truncated TLV, or UPDATE carrying both Default Gateway + MAC Mobility extended communities per §10.1.1 prohibition).
Verify: Check `show bgp evpn summary` for peer stability; `show bgp evpn routes` confirms no malformed routes installed in MAC-VRF; syslog for UPDATE error (attribute discard or NOTIFICATION); BGP error counter for malformed optional transitive attribute.
81d7ed64b2599bcb.expectation:
Pass:    Malformed UPDATEs discarded or trigger NOTIFICATION without installing route; BGP session remains established or re-establishes cleanly; error counter increments; syslog records attribute parse failure referencing Default Gateway extended community (type 0x00 0x0d per RFC9251).
Fail-on: DUT crash or BGP process restart; peer stuck in IDLE; malformed route appears in `show bgp evpn routes mac` output; UPDATE with both Default Gateway + MAC Mobility extended communities accepted (violates §10.1.1 SHALL NOT/SHALL be ignored rule); no syslog entry for the invalid attribute.

835ff87f109a6736.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology; configure `af-l2vpn evpn` on both PEs; configure `advertise-mac enable`, `import-rt`, and `export-rt` on DUT EVPN instance; establish multihomed ES on PE1; IXIA primed to inject BGP Route Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement per RFC7432bis §7.2) and Ethernet A-D Per EVPN Instance routes with L2-Attr Extended Community variants.
Action: (1) Inject Ethernet A-D Per EVPN Instance Route without L2-Attr Extended Community; verify DUT applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 redundancy mode behavior. (2) Inject Ethernet A-D with valid L2-Attr Extended Community; verify RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 applies. (3) Inject Route Type 2 (EVPNS-REQ#280) with truncated L2-Attr length field. (4) Inject Route Type 2 with L2-Attr containing reserved bits set. (5) Inject Route Type 2 with oversized L2-Attr (length exceeds standard 8-octet extended community). (6) Send valid Route Type 2 MAC/IP with MPLS label to confirm MAC Unicast Forwarding Table (EVPNS-REQ#350) population and label handling (EVPNS-REQ#310-340) remain intact.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` shows valid routes imported, malformed variants rejected; `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays redundancy mode deduced per RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 (missing L2-Attr) or RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 (valid L2-Attr); `show bgp neighbor errors` increments by exactly +1 per malformed variant; `show log | include community` records structured error per malformed L2-Attr event; `show mac-table` populates only from valid Route Type 2; no crash or BGP session reset.
835ff87f109a6736.expectation:
Pass:    Absent L2-Attr triggers RFC7432 ch.14.1.1; valid L2-Attr triggers RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1; each malformed L2-Attr variant (truncated, reserved-bits-set, oversized) rejected with error counter +1 and syslog entry; valid Route Type 2 populates MAC table with correct MPLS labels; BGP session stable; no crash.
Fail-on: Incorrect RFC behavior selection; malformed L2-Attr accepted into route table or MAC table; missing error counter or syslog for any variant; valid route rejected; crash; BGP session reset; stale or missing MAC entries.

83e1d5f9da420c1f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with agg-eth 0 multi-homed to shared CE via LACP. Configure ethernet-segment with identifier 1 on both PEs' agg-eth 0. Set DUT to load-balancing-mode single-active with service-carving highest-random-weight. Establish EVPN BGP peering between PEs, create two EVIs, and advertise MAC/IP routes from IXIA through CE.
Action: Generate bidirectional L2 traffic from IXIA through CE at 500 pps per EVI. Verify DF election and single-active forwarding via show evpn ethernet-segment. Reconfigure DUT to load-balancing-mode all-active and commit. While mode change occurs, inject 50 new MAC route advertisements via BGP to force route reconvergence.
Verify: Capture show evpn ethernet-segment output and IXIA forwarding counters before and after mode change. Single-active phase: verify one DF elected per EVI per service-carving algorithm, only DF forwards (one PE path active in IXIA). All-active phase: verify both PEs forward with load-balancing per RFC7432bis ch.14 (IXIA counters show traffic split across DUT and neighbor PE).
83e1d5f9da420c1f.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner (CE)
83e1d5f9da420c1f.expectation:
Pass:    Both single-active and all-active modes operate per RFC7432bis ch.14. Single-active: service-carving elects one DF per EVI, only DF forwards, non-DF blocks. All-active: both PEs forward, unicast traffic load-balances (neither PE carries <20% or >80% of flows). Mode transition during BGP reconvergence completes without BGP session reset, total traffic loss <1 second.
Fail-on: Single-active mode shows both PEs forwarding (DF election failed). All-active mode shows only one PE forwarding or >95% traffic on one path (load-balancing not working). BGP session resets during mode change. Traffic blackhole >2 seconds or permanent packet loss.

83f39a2576ddc1bd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-300 service-type vlan-based` under `l2-services`, set MTU 9000, enable Control Word. Configure ESI 01:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:01 on the interface. Under `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:300` and `export-rt 65000:300`. Enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbor to peer PE. Verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows route type 3 (Inclusive Multicast) and route type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI).
Action: Execute `show tech-support` or equivalent CLI command after EVI-300 is operationally up and BGP EVPN routes are active.
Verify: Extract and inspect the tech-support archive for: (1) `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 evpn-300` showing L2-Attr Extended Community 0x06:0x04 with MTU=9000, C=1, F bit set, P=0, B=0; (2) `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 01:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:01` showing L2-Attr Extended Community with P/B bits per ESI config, MTU=0, C=0, F=0; (3) `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-300`; (4) syslog entries for EVPN route advertisement.
83f39a2576ddc1bd.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output for route types 1 and 3 with L2-Attr Extended Community (0x06:0x04) correctly partitioned per RFC7432bis §7.11.1: route type 3 has MTU/C/F populated and P=0/B=0; route type 1 has P/B populated and MTU=0/C=0/F=0. Running-config and EVPN syslogs present.
Fail-on: Missing BGP EVPN output in tech-support, L2-Attr Extended Community absent, incorrect partitioning (MTU≠0 or C≠0 or F≠0 on route type 1, or P≠0 or B≠0 on route type 3), or missing config/logs.

840a324be000e714.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT: `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle`, `auto-discovery`, `export-rt 65000:100`, `import-rt 65000:100`; attach ES on `agg-eth 0` with `ethernet-segment`. Configure 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) with matching EVPN instance, same ESI, same RT. Establish BGP EVPN peer session.
Action: Commit config on DUT; capture BGP UPDATE on wire showing Type-1 route (RD, ESI, ET ID, MPLS Label LT2, L2-Attr extended community with Primary/Backup flags per RFC7432bis ch.8.4). Commit on 3rd-party PE; exchange routes. Inject IXIA traffic from CE behind 3rd-party PE toward MAC behind DUT ES.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on both PEs confirms Type-1 route received with correct encoding. Verify DUT does NOT forward traffic until Type-4 (Ethernet A-D per ES) also received. Once both present, verify bidirectional traffic delivery via IXIA counters and `show evpn mac-table`.
840a324be000e714.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts 3rd-party Type-1 route encoding (RD, ESI, ET ID, MPLS label, L2-Attr extended community) per RFC7432bis ch.8.4; withholds traffic forwarding until associated Type-4 route received; then forwards bidirectionally with 0% loss.
Fail-on: Type-1 route rejected with BGP Notification or encoding parse error, OR traffic forwarded before Type-4 arrives (race condition violation), OR permanent black-hole after both routes present.

8484659cf1a68911.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an ethernet-segment under `configuration / interface / agg-eth <name> / ethernet-segment` with ESI value but omit `load-balancing-mode`; commit the config.
Action: Issue `show running-config interface agg-eth <name>` to confirm no load-balancing-mode line present; then issue `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` to observe operational mode. Configure `load-balancing-mode single-active` explicitly, commit, and re-check `show running-config`.
Verify: Initial running-config omits load-balancing-mode (default applied implicitly); `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` reports mode as single-active; after explicit `load-balancing-mode single-active`, running-config still omits the line (idempotent default).
8484659cf1a68911.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
8484659cf1a68911.expectation:
Pass:    Operational mode is single-active when unconfigured; running-config omits the line both before and after explicit `load-balancing-mode single-active` (idempotent).
Fail-on: Operational mode differs from single-active by default, or explicit single-active config adds a line to running-config (non-idempotent), or `no load-balancing-mode` fails to restore default.

8488894c9cc11148.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN VLAN-based service on DUT with VLAN 100 under `configuration/l2-services/evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based`; configure DUT's BGP AS as 65000; enable multi-homing by assigning the same ESI (00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) to the customer-facing port on both DUT and neighbor PE; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` and `advertise-mac enable`; rely on auto-RT derivation (do not manually configure export-rt/import-rt).
Action: Bring up BGP EVPN peering between DUT and neighbor PE; send bidirectional L2 traffic from IXIA through the multi-homed Ethernet Segment; trigger MAC learning and Type-2 route advertisement.
Verify: On DUT, run `show bgp evpn route-type 2` and decode the RT extended community; confirm Global Administrator = 65000 (2 octets) and Local Administrator = 0x000064 (VLAN 100 in lower 12 bits, upper 20 bits zero); verify `show evpn multi-homing` reports DF election complete and ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 is active on both PEs; confirm IXIA traffic is load-balanced (no >60% imbalance).
8488894c9cc11148.expectation:
Pass:    RT in advertised Type-2 routes matches format <AS>:<0x000000VLAN> (e.g., 65000:100 encoded as Global Admin=65000, Local Admin=0x000064 with upper 20 bits=0); multi-homing ESI routes (Type-1, Type-4) are exchanged; traffic splits ~50/50 across both PEs.
Fail-on: RT Global Admin ≠ configured AS, or VLAN not in lower 12 bits of Local Admin, or upper 20 bits non-zero; DF election fails; ESI routes missing; traffic blackholed or >80% skew to one PE.

849510d9ce3ffe74.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: PE1 and PE2 multi-homed to ES1 (each with 2 local interfaces); remote PE3 sends BUM traffic via LT3 label with LT4 pointing to PE1's ifIndex=10. IXIA generates BUM floods at line rate (10 Gbps baseline). Configure `unknown-mac-flooding enable` and `advertise-mac enable` on all PEs; verify Ethernet A-D per-ES Route (type 1) advertises LT4 labels for each local ES interface.
Action: PE3 injects 10 Gbps BUM traffic encapsulated with MPLS LT3 (ES1 flood label) + LT4 (ifIndex=10). Measure PE1 BUM throughput and verify split horizon: traffic floods to all PE1 ES interfaces (ifIndex 11, 12) EXCEPT ifIndex 10.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn bum-split-horizon` confirms LT4 entry for ifIndex=10. Capture egress counters on PE1 interfaces: ifIndex 10 shows 0 BUM frames, ifIndex 11/12 show ≥99.9% of injected traffic. Measure latency PE3→PE1 local interfaces.
849510d9ce3ffe74.expectation:
Pass:    BUM throughput ≥10 Gbps with <1 ms latency; ifIndex 10 (LT4) receives 0 frames; ifIndex 11/12 receive ≥99.9% of BUM traffic; no packet loss.
Fail-on: Any BUM frames egress ifIndex 10 (split horizon violation), throughput <10 Gbps, latency ≥1 ms, or packet loss >0.1%.

8495f1faaf1ace59.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN vlan-based instance on DUT with neighbor PE. Use IXIA to generate duplicate MAC advertisements (same source MAC from two different sources) to trigger MAC freeze. Verify frozen state: `show evpn frozen mac-addresses` lists at least one frozen MAC.
Action: Execute `clear evpn frozen mac-addresses`.
Verify: Run `show evpn frozen mac-addresses` to confirm no frozen MACs listed. Run `show evpn mac` to verify previously frozen MAC now appears in active MAC table. Check syslog for clear operation confirmation.
8495f1faaf1ace59.expectation:
Pass:    Post-clear, `show evpn frozen mac-addresses` reports no frozen MACs; previously frozen MAC appears in `show evpn mac` active table; syslog confirms clear.
Fail-on: Frozen-MAC list still populated, or cleared MAC absent from active MAC table, or command returns error.

84a0605b18e42a3f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 1 with ethernet-segment (esi-type manual, esi-identifier 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99); ensure the interface and its sub-interfaces are NOT attached to any EVPN instance (per prerequisite).
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment`; issue `load-balancing-mode all-active`; commit. Capture running-config. Then issue `no load-balancing-mode`; commit; capture running-config again.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 1 ethernet-segment` after first commit displays `load-balancing-mode all-active`. After `no load-balancing-mode`, the same show command displays `load-balancing-mode single-active` (explicit) or omits the line (implicit default).
84a0605b18e42a3f.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
84a0605b18e42a3f.expectation:
Pass:    `load-balancing-mode all-active` persists in running-config; `no load-balancing-mode` results in single-active (explicit line or absence indicating default).
Fail-on: Command rejected with error, `all-active` mode not applied to running-config, or `no` command fails to restore single-active default (e.g., all-active still present or unrecognized mode shown).

84ab6cabb33cb31c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0 (identifier 0 <type0-value>, load-balancing-mode single-active). Establish MACT with 100+ MAC entries: local CEs via attachment circuit, remote CEs via neighbor PE EVPN routes.
Action: Issue `shutdown` on agg-eth 0, wait 5 seconds, issue `no shutdown`. Monitor MACT convergence.
Verify: Check MAC table entries are restored with VLAN-ID, MAC Address, ESI, and Next Hop (local/remote types). Verify unicast forwarding to known destination MACs resumes.
84ab6cabb33cb31c.expectation:
Pass:    MACT re-populates with all pre-flap entries (local and remote next-hops); unicast forwarding resumes within es-waiting-time + reconvergence (≤ 10s total).
Fail-on: Missing MACT entries after 10s, stale ESI or next-hop fields, or continued traffic drops to known MACs.

84bc7508ec2cdfff.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 PEs (PE1 10.0.0.1, PE2 10.0.0.2, PE3 10.0.0.3) sharing ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11; under `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment` set DF algorithm = Highest-Preference, Preference = 200 on all 3 PEs; enable "Don't Preempt" capability on PE1 only.
Action: Activate the ES on all PEs; trigger DF election; capture BGP EVPN Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) routes to examine DF Election Extended Community (Preference, DF algorithm, Don't Preempt bit).
Verify: Check `show evpn ethernet-segment <esi>` on each PE for DF/non-DF status; inspect Type-4 route Extended Community to confirm PE1 has Don't Preempt=1, PE2/PE3 have Don't Preempt=0; send broadcast frame from IXIA on ES access port and verify only PE1 replicates to EVPN core.
84bc7508ec2cdfff.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs (same vendor) + IXIA traffic gen
84bc7508ec2cdfff.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 (10.0.0.1) elected DF, despite higher IP, because it advertises Don't Preempt=1 per RFC9785 §4.1 tie-break rule; single DF forwards BUM; all PEs advertise Preference=200 in DF Election Extended Community.
Fail-on: PE2 (10.0.0.2, lowest IP) elected DF, violating Don't Preempt tie-break precedence; multiple PEs forward BUM (split-brain); DF Election Extended Community missing or Don't Preempt bit ignored.

8503a23cef733bdb.action_steps:
Problem: Malformed ESI Label Extended Community in BGP EVPN routes might corrupt the BUM split-horizon table, causing BUM traffic to loop back to the originating multi-homed site or crash the BGP process.
Method: Configure two PEs sharing an ES, establish BGP EVPN peering, inject four variants of BGP routes with corrupted ESI Label Extended Community (wrong type, truncated, reserved bits, invalid label), send BUM traffic from the shared ES, and confirm DUT rejects each malformed route while enforcing split-horizon on valid routes.
Setup: Two PEs share an ES to one CE (ESI configured on both); BGP EVPN peering established; IXIA emulates neighbor PE for route injection and generates BUM traffic from CE.
Action: Inject four BGP EVPN route variants from IXIA: (1) ESI Label Extended Community with wrong type field, (2) truncated ESI Label (< 8 octets total), (3) reserved bits set in flags byte, (4) label value exceeding 1048575. Send BUM traffic from CE.
Verify: `show bgp evpn` confirms malformed routes not installed; `show bgp neighbor` error counter increments by four; packet capture at CE shows zero BUM loop packets (split-horizon enforced); `show log` records each rejection; BGP process stable.
8503a23cef733bdb.expectation:
Pass: All four malformed ESI Label variants rejected; BGP error counter +4; zero BUM loop packets captured; syslog entry per variant; no BGP crash.
Fail-on: Any malformed route in RIB, BUM loop packets detected, error counter ≠ 4, missing syslog entry, or BGP process restart.

85111854b7a033f6.action_steps:
Setup:  Forwarding rules exercised in prior rows (local CE hairpin block, remote→local unicast/BUM, DF/non-DF filtering, LT1/LT2 label forwarding); EVI configured with advertise-mac enable, unknown-mac-flooding enable.
Action: Collect show evpn <evi> mac-table, show evpn <evi> bum-database, show evpn <evi> ethernet-segment, show mpls forwarding-table, show bgp l2vpn evpn routes, show running-config l2-services evpn, show log (EVPN/MAC/DF events); package as tech-support.
Verify: Tech-support contains MACT (remote MACs, no local→local entries), BUM DB (non-DF ES excluded), ES table (DF status), label bindings (LT1→MACT, LT2→ES), BGP routes, config, forwarding logs.
85111854b7a033f6.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support includes complete forwarding evidence: MACT with remote MACs only (zero local CE→same CE), BUM database filtering non-DF ES, ES table with DF election state, label bindings (LT1 to MACT entries, LT2 to ES IDs), BGP EVPN routes (type 1/2/3), config showing advertise-mac enable + unknown-mac-flooding enable, syslog with MAC learning and DF events.
Fail-on: Missing any core table (MACT/BUM DB/ES/labels/BGP), MACT contains local CE→local CE entries (hairpin violation), config shows advertise-mac or unknown-mac-flooding disabled, or no forwarding event logs.

852086e17b2a6888.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (DUT as receiving PE, neighbor as sending PE) in same EVI with shared P2MP MPLS LSP; configure DUT: unknown-mac-flooding enable (default), af-l2vpn evpn under BGP neighbor, import-rt matching EVI route-target; advertise IMET route with PMSI Tunnel attribute specifying P2MP LSP ID; attach IXIA to local AC on DUT.
Action: From neighbor PE, send three Ethernet frames over P2MP LSP: (1) unicast DA 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:01 (not in DUT MAC table), (2) broadcast DA FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF, (3) multicast DA 01:00:5E:02:02:02. All frames arrive at DUT via P2MP LSP.
Verify: DUT CLI: show evpn imet-routes (PMSI Tunnel attribute present with P2MP LSP ID), show mpls p2mp-lsp <id> (Up, packets received > 0), show evpn mac-table (00:AA:BB:CC:DD:01 NOT listed as learned entry), show evpn bum-statistics (unknown-unicast ≥1, broadcast ≥1, multicast ≥1); IXIA on DUT local AC captures all 3 frames (flooded).
852086e17b2a6888.expectation:
Pass:    DUT treats all packets received on P2MP LSP per RFC7432bis §12.2 MUST: (1) unicast MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:01 treated as unknown unicast (flooded to local AC, not installed in MAC table), (2) broadcast flooded, (3) multicast flooded. BUM counters increment, IXIA capture shows 3 frames, P2MP LSP stats show received packets.
Fail-on: Unicast frame with DA 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:01 treated as known unicast (DUT does MAC lookup and drops/unicast-forwards instead of flooding), or any BUM frame not flooded to local AC (IXIA capture missing frames), or DUT logs "invalid PMSI Tunnel attribute" error, or P2MP LSP received-packet counter stuck at zero.

85258e2e5a1f9965.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT (AS 65001) + Cisco/Juniper PE (AS 65002) connected via MPLS; configure `evpn evpn-vlan100 service-type vlan-based` on DUT with VLAN 100, auto-RT derivation enabled (default or explicit); configure matching vlan-based EVPN instance on 3rd-party PE.
Action: Advertise MAC address from DUT side on VLAN 100; capture BGP UPDATE (Type-2 MAC/IP route) on wire between PEs; decode Extended Community RT attribute.
Verify: Wireshark/tcpdump shows RT = 65001:100 with Global Administrator = 0xFDE9 (65001), Local Administrator = 0x00000064 (bits[11:0] = 100, bits[31:12] = 0); `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on 3rd-party confirms route import; send IXIA bidirectional L2 frames (VLAN 100), verify forwarding and MAC learning both directions.
85258e2e5a1f9965.expectation:
Pass:    DUT encodes RT with AS number (65001) in Global Administrator field and VLAN ID (100) in lowest 12 bits of 4-byte Local Administrator field (0x00000064), upper 20 bits zero; 3rd-party PE imports route without errors; IXIA traffic forwarded bidirectionally with <1% loss.
Fail-on: RT encoding violates RFC7432bis §7.10.1 (VLAN not in bits[11:0], bits[31:12] non-zero, wrong AS), or 3rd-party rejects route (BGP notification, no import in RIB), or traffic black-hole/unidirectional (>1% loss or asymmetric forwarding).

857d353897ce8c02.action_steps:
Setup:  FLOW-030 established: multi-homed ES with two PEs; DUT receives Ethernet A-D per EVPN Instance routes (some with L2-Attr Extended Community, some without); Route Type 2 MAC/IP advertisements active with all LT1-4 learning types exercised.
Action: Execute `show tech-support` to collect diagnostic bundle; save and transfer bundle off-box; extract and inspect: `show bgp l2vpn evpn`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` (Extended Communities), `show evpn mac address-table`, running-config (`af-l2vpn evpn`, `advertise-mac`, `import-rt`, `export-rt`), and kernel forwarding tables.
Verify: Bundle contains Route Type 2 advertisements with Extended Communities (L2-Attr presence/absence per route); MAC table with LT1-4 entries; config snapshot; forwarding tables for EVI/ES/MAC; evidence of redundancy mode determination (RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 vs RFC7432bis §14.1.1 based on L2-Attr).
857d353897ce8c02.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle complete: Route Type 2 visible; Extended Communities captured (L2-Attr shown/absent per route); MAC table has all LT1-4 types; config includes EVPN settings; forwarding tables present; redundancy mode selection logic evident.
Fail-on: Missing `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail`; Extended Communities truncated; L2-Attr not captured; MAC table missing any LT1-4; EVPN config absent; no forwarding table dumps; no RFC7432 vs RFC7432bis mode selection evidence.

85b5230792b45d96.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with interfaces x-eth 0/0/1 and agg-lag 1 operationally up; NETCONF client authenticated.
Action: Via NETCONF, create EVPN instance "evpn-pb-1" with service-type=port-based, attach x-eth 0/0/1 and agg-lag 1, configure import-rt 65000:100 and export-rt 65000:100, enable auto-discovery.
Verify: CLI `show evpn evpn-pb-1` confirms service-type port-based; both interfaces listed; route targets 65000:100; auto-discovery on; NETCONF <get-config> returns matching values.
85b5230792b45d96.expectation:
Pass:    NETCONF successfully creates port-based EVPN instance; CLI and NETCONF views show identical service-type, interface list, and route targets; auto-discovery enabled in both.
Fail-on: NETCONF rejects port-based enum; YANG schema missing service-type choice; CLI shows different service-type or missing interfaces; route target mismatch between CLI and NETCONF; auto-discovery state differs.

85d8f6089f922daa.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with broadcast domain, RD, export-rt; configure BGP neighbor with af-l2vpn evpn; set control-word to default, advertise-mac enable, unknown-mac-flooding enable.
Action: Bring up BGP session to neighbor PE; DUT must originate Type-3 route for the broadcast domain.
Verify: On neighbor PE, capture BGP UPDATE and parse Type-3 NLRI fields: RD, ET ID, Originating Router IP, PMSI Tunnel Attribute (Flag=0, Type=6, MPLS Label, Tunnel Identifier=DUT loopback), L2-Attr Extended Community (L2 MTU, Flow Label flag, Control Word flag per config), RTs, Next Hop=DUT peering address.
85d8f6089f922daa.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP L2VPN EVPN capable)
85d8f6089f922daa.expectation:
Pass:    Type-3 route received with all mandatory fields present; PMSI Tunnel Attribute shows Flag=0 and Type=6 (Ingress Replication); Tunnel Identifier=Originating Router IP; L2-Attr Extended Community contains L2 MTU, Flow Label flag, Control Word flag (default state); Next Hop=advertising PE's IP address.
Fail-on: Type-3 route missing or incomplete; PMSI Tunnel Type ≠ 6 or Flag ≠ 0; L2-Attr Extended Community missing L2 MTU, Flow Label flag, or Control Word flag; Next Hop ≠ DUT's peering IP; no route advertised per broadcast domain.

85ee147f62b036fb.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance (`evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-based` under `l2-services`), set `mac-aging-time 600` within that context, commit, and verify `show running-config l2-services evpn test-evpn` displays the configured value.
Action: Enter configuration mode, navigate to the EVPN instance context, issue `no mac-aging-time`, and commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn test-evpn` no longer contains `mac-aging-time`; the instance reverts to default MAC aging behavior.
85ee147f62b036fb.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
85ee147f62b036fb.expectation:
Pass:    The explicit `mac-aging-time` configuration is removed from running-config; default MAC aging time is restored.
Fail-on: `mac-aging-time` persists in the instance running-config, or operational behavior indicates the non-default value remains active.

865a9b62efd73249.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure identical ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on DUT and neighbor PE access interfaces toward IXIA; establish BGP EVPN L2VPN (VLAN 100) between DUT, neighbor PE, and remote PE; IXIA emulates CE dual-homed to DUT and neighbor PE; verify `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` confirms DF election complete on both PEs.
Action: IXIA sends 100 L2 frames (SMAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01, VLAN 100) to DUT's ESI interface; DUT learns MAC locally and advertises BGP RT-2 route; capture egress traffic on (1) DUT→neighbor-PE link (same ESI), (2) DUT→remote-PE MPLS tunnel.
Verify: `show evpn mac-table vlan 100 | match aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01` displays MAC learned on local ESI; `show bgp evpn route-type 2 | match aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01` confirms RT-2 contains ESI field 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99; capture (1) shows 0 frames forwarded (RFC 7432bis §8.3 split horizon—MUST NOT forward to same-ESI neighbor PE); capture (2) shows 100 frames forwarded to remote PE.
865a9b62efd73249.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + remote PE
865a9b62efd73249.expectation:
Pass:    0 frames forwarded to neighbor PE on same ESI; 100 frames forwarded to remote PE; RT-2 advertised with correct ESI field; MAC table entry correct.
Fail-on: ≥1 frame forwarded to neighbor PE on same ESI (violates RFC 7432bis §8.3 MUST split horizon), or RT-2 missing/incorrect ESI, or MAC not learned, or <100 frames reach remote PE.

86c93a29a52d21b1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with ingress replication (not P2MP); PE1 and PE2 exchange IMET routes (type 3) carrying P-tunnel attribute with downstream ingress-replication label; `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on both PEs; local CEs attached.
Action: Send unknown unicast, broadcast, and multicast traffic from CE1; observe ingress-replication label-switched forwarding to PE2; collect tech-support from DUT (PE1) using vendor-specific command (e.g., `show tech-support evpn` or equivalent).
Verify: Parse tech-support archive for: `show bgp evpn routes type 3` (IMET with P-tunnel attribute + label), `show l2-services evpn <evi>` (ingress-replication mode), `show mpls forwarding-table` (label → BUM treatment), running-config (`unknown-mac-flooding enable`), syslog entries for BUM replication, and MAC table showing no learned entry for the unknown unicast destination.
86c93a29a52d21b1.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
86c93a29a52d21b1.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains IMET route with ingress-replication P-tunnel label, MPLS label entry matching that label bound to broadcast/multicast/unknown-unicast treatment, running-config showing `unknown-mac-flooding enable`, and logs confirming unicast MAC addresses in this path were treated as unknown unicast.
Fail-on: Missing IMET type-3 routes, missing MPLS label binding for ingress-replication label, `unknown-mac-flooding disable` in config, or no evidence that unicast destination MAC triggered unknown-unicast forwarding via the ingress-replication label.

886f5ccbdfd3c5f7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an Ethernet Segment on `agg-eth 0` with the minimum required parameters (ESI identifier) but without explicitly setting `load-balancing-mode`. Commit the configuration.
Action: 1. Issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` and verify no `load-balancing-mode` line appears in the Ethernet Segment stanza. 2. Check the operational load-balancing mode for this Ethernet Segment using the appropriate show command and record the value. 3. Enter `configure`, navigate to `interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment`, issue `no load-balancing-mode`, and commit. 4. Re-check the operational load-balancing mode using the same show command.
Verify: (a) Running-config has no `load-balancing-mode` line when unconfigured (implicit default). (b) Operational state displays `single-active` as the load-balancing mode in step 2. (c) `no load-balancing-mode` commit succeeds without error. (d) Operational mode remains `single-active` after step 4.
886f5ccbdfd3c5f7.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
886f5ccbdfd3c5f7.expectation:
Pass:    Default load-balancing mode is `single-active` when `load-balancing-mode` is not explicitly configured. The `no load-balancing-mode` command is accepted and operational mode remains `single-active`.
Fail-on: Default mode is not `single-active`, or `no load-balancing-mode` commit fails with an error, or operational mode changes unexpectedly after the `no` command.

88df5c826acaf662.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs with EVPN instance configured, BGP af-l2vpn evpn peering established. Provision interface x-eth 0/0/1 on both PEs (for Type 0), interface agg-eth 0 (LAG) on both PEs (for Type 1 LACP), and interface x-eth 0/0/2 on PE1 only (for Type 4). Connect all to IXIA as CE with LACP capability.
Action: Configure ESI Type 0 on x-eth 0/0/1 on both PEs with identical manual 9-octet value; configure ESI Type 1 on agg-eth 0 on both PEs (LACP-derived, IXIA forms LACP with LAG); configure ESI Type 4 on x-eth 0/0/2 on PE1 (single-home, default ESI derivation). Bring up all interfaces. Capture BGP UPDATEs between PEs using tcpdump and data-plane frames on IXIA.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs confirms Type 0 ESI value matches on both PEs, Type 1 ESI value matches on both PEs, Type 4 ESI present on PE1 only; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows ES route for each segment; decode BGP captures to verify ESI Type field (octet 0 = 0x00, 0x01, or 0x04) and value encoding in NLRI.
88df5c826acaf662.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE (IXIA acts as CE/LACP partner)
88df5c826acaf662.expectation:
Pass:    All three ESI type configurations accepted by CLI; `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays identical ESI value for Type 0 on both PEs, identical ESI value for Type 1 on both PEs, and single ESI for Type 4 on PE1; all segments show operational status up; BGP capture shows Type field (octet 0) = 0x00 for Type 0 segment, 0x01 for Type 1, 0x04 for Type 4.
Fail-on: CLI rejects any ESI type configuration; ESI value mismatch between PEs for Type 0 or Type 1; Type 4 ESI appears on both PEs instead of PE1 only; any ethernet segment shows operational status down; BGP NLRI Type field incorrect (not 0x00, 0x01, or 0x04 respectively); ES route absent for any segment.

89014176637b2bed.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 (neighbor PE) agg-eth interface: `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx; load-balancing-mode single-active`. Configure DUT as remote PE, BGP EVPN peer to PE1. Attach CE1 to PE1's ES, CE2 to DUT.
Action: Bring up PE1's ES; PE1 advertises Ethernet A-D per ES route with ESI Label extended community, multihoming redundancy mode field = 1. DUT imports route. Send bidirectional traffic between CE1 (behind PE1's ES) and CE2 (behind DUT) via IXIA.
Verify: On DUT: capture BGP UPDATE showing ESI Label ext-community with redundancy mode = 1 (per RFC7432bis §14.1.1). Execute `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI>` confirming ES displayed as single-active mode. Execute `show evpn mac-address-table` showing CE1's MACs learned via PE1 only. IXIA counters confirm traffic from CE2 to CE1 forwarded via PE1.
89014176637b2bed.expectation:
Pass:    DUT correctly parses ESI Label ext-community redundancy mode = 1; deduces ES is single-active per RFC7432bis §14.1.1; `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays single-active; MAC learning and traffic forwarding consistent with single-active semantics (sole next-hop).
Fail-on: DUT fails to parse redundancy mode = 1, or misinterprets as all-active, or `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays incorrect mode, or DUT attempts to load-balance to multiple next-hops for single-active ES.

8920e666cb660d77.action_steps:
Setup:  Create agg-eth 1 with member interfaces x-eth 0/0/1 and x-eth 0/0/2; do not configure lacp-key.
Action: Enable LACP on agg-eth 1; initiate LACP negotiation with IXIA LAG port.
Verify: Check LACP Administrative Key via show commands (expect value = 1, matching the interface number); verify `show running-config` for agg-eth 1 omits lacp-key; explicitly configure lacp-key to 1 and confirm running-config unchanged (idempotent).
8920e666cb660d77.expectation:
Pass:    LACP Administrative Key defaults to agg-eth interface number (1); explicit lacp-key 1 config is idempotent (no line added to running-config).
Fail-on: LACP Administrative Key ≠ 1, or lacp-key 1 appears in running-config after explicit configuration.

895ef84e17fd6798.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure port-based EVPN instance `evpn1` using `evpn evpn1 service-type port-based` with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, and `export-rt` configured; attach the instance to LAG `agg-lag 1` and Ethernet port `x-eth 0/0/1`; establish BGP EVPN neighbor session with remote PE; verify the port-based instance is operationally up on both interfaces.
Action: Run `tech-support` command to generate the diagnostic bundle; copy the bundle file to off-box storage; uncompress and inspect the bundle directory structure and file contents.
Verify: Confirm the bundle contains: (1) `show running-config` output with `evpn evpn1 service-type port-based` configuration including `auto-discovery`, route-targets, and interface bindings to `agg-lag 1` and `x-eth 0/0/1`; (2) `show evpn evi` output listing `evpn1` with service-type port-based and both interfaces; (3) `show bgp l2vpn evpn` showing type-1 AD routes and type-3 IMET routes for the EVI; (4) interface operational state for `agg-lag 1` and `x-eth 0/0/1`.
895ef84e17fd6798.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains complete port-based EVPN configuration (evpn instance with service-type port-based, auto-discovery enabled, route-targets configured, interfaces `agg-lag 1` and `x-eth 0/0/1` bound to the instance) and operational state (EVI up, BGP routes advertised, interface status).
Fail-on: Bundle missing running-config for the port-based EVPN instance, missing `show evpn evi` output, missing interface bindings in the configuration, or missing BGP EVPN route advertisements for the configured instance.

89febb281bdf12d1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as iBGP route reflector (AS 65000); establish iBGP session to PE1 (10.0.0.1) under `af-l2vpn evpn` with `route-reflector-client` enabled; establish second iBGP session to PE2 (10.0.0.2) without RR-client flag; configure EVPN on all routers; bring up BGP sessions.
Action: From PE2, advertise EVPN route-type 2 (MAC/IP) into iBGP; verify DUT receives it via `show bgp l2vpn evpn`.
Verify: On PE1, examine received routes using `show bgp l2vpn evpn` with detail/verbose output; confirm reflected route includes ORIGINATOR_ID attribute (matching PE2's router-ID) and CLUSTER_LIST attribute (containing DUT's cluster-ID).
89febb281bdf12d1.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PE routers (RR topology: DUT as route reflector, PE1 as RR client, PE2 as route originator)
89febb281bdf12d1.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 receives reflected EVPN route with ORIGINATOR_ID equal to PE2's router-ID and CLUSTER_LIST containing DUT's cluster-ID.
Fail-on: Route not reflected to PE1, or ORIGINATOR_ID attribute missing/incorrect, or CLUSTER_LIST attribute missing/incorrect.

8a33d954b02e61d2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE as multihomed PEs for ES with identifier type 0 = 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88, load-balancing-mode all-active on both PEs; configure MAC-VRF EVPN instance on both with es-waiting-time = 3 (default); IXIA simulates multihomed CE, generates continuous bidirectional L2 traffic (100 Mbps) through LAG to both PEs.
Action: Trigger DUT control-plane process restart (e.g., `restart protocol bgp` or platform-specific EVPN daemon kill) while traffic is active; capture BGP UPDATEs at neighbor PE during restart and recovery.
Verify: Inspect neighbor PE BGP capture for Mass Withdraw (RFC7432bis §8.2: Type 4 ES route withdrawn or all MAC routes for the ES withdrawn in single UPDATE); measure traffic outage duration with IXIA; confirm DUT auto-recovers, BGP session re-establishes, and ES returns to all-active operation with restored load-balancing.
8a33d954b02e61d2.expectation:
Pass:    Mass Withdraw sent by DUT (or by neighbor PE upon detecting DUT failure); traffic outage ≤ es-waiting-time (3 s) + BGP reconvergence (typ. 5–7 s total); DUT control-plane auto-recovers, ES returns to all-active, bidirectional traffic resumes with no manual intervention.
Fail-on: No Mass Withdraw observed in BGP capture; traffic outage > 10 s; DUT control-plane does not auto-recover; ES remains operationally down after recovery; traffic black-holes or loops on either PE.

8a96ab1d4a4021c8.action_steps:
