Setup:  DUT: Configure `evpn EVPN-ES1 service-type vlan-based`, set RD to Type 1 format (e.g., 192.0.2.1:100 where 192.0.2.1 is loopback0), configure Ethernet interface with 10-octet ESI 01:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:11:22 (Type-01 LACP-based per RFC7432 §5). 3rd-party PE (Cisco NCS5500 IOS-XR 7.x): configure `evpn evi 1`, assign same ESI to port-channel, establish iBGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family enabled.
Action: On DUT, issue `auto-discovery` under EVPN instance; bring interface to 'up' state to trigger Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) route advertisement. Start packet capture on inter-PE link (IXIA port mirror or tcpdump 'port 179').
Verify: Decode BGP UPDATE Type-4 NLRI: bytes 0-1 of RD = 0x00 0x01 (Type 1), bytes 2-7 = DUT loopback + 2-byte admin field (e.g., 0xC0000201 0x0064 for 192.0.2.1:100). ESI field (10 octets) = 01:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:11:22. Originating Router IP Address = 192.0.2.1 (4 bytes), IP Length = 32 bits. On Cisco: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type es` displays received route with RD 192.0.2.1:100, ESI 01aa.bbcc.ddee.ff00.1122, next-hop 192.0.2.1, status code 'valid' (best path installed).
8a96ab1d4a4021c8.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Cisco IOS-XR 7.x on NCS5500 or Juniper Junos 22.x on MX) + IXIA for BGP wire capture (or tcpdump on management)
8a96ab1d4a4021c8.expectation:
Pass:    Wire capture shows RD Type = 1 (first 2 octets = 0x0001), ESI = exactly 10 octets, Originating Router IP = DUT loopback (non-zero, uniquely identifies DUT per RFC7432bis §8.1.1). Cisco PE accepts route (no BGP notification), installs in Loc-RIB with status 'valid', aliasing function operational for multi-homed segment.
Fail-on: RD Type field ≠ 1 (bytes 0-1 ≠ 0x0001), ESI length ≠ 10 octets (truncated/padded), Originating Router IP = 0.0.0.0 or absent, or Cisco sends BGP UPDATE Error subcode 11 (Malformed NLRI), route missing from `show bgp l2vpn evpn`, or logs "ES route format invalid".

8a9ad16b8aca7124.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP neighbor in mode `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn` without specifying `inbound-soft-reconfiguration`. Establish EVPN session with peer PE; exchange routes.
Action: Modify an inbound route-policy for the neighbor and apply it via soft refresh (no session reset). Monitor BGP protocol messages and memory usage.
Verify: Capture BGP messages showing Route Refresh Request/Response exchange (RFC 2918 capability code 2). Confirm `show running-config` omits `inbound-soft-reconfiguration` under neighbor's `af-l2vpn evpn` context. Memory footprint must not include cached copies of all received NLRIs.
8a9ad16b8aca7124.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP session + packet capture)
8a9ad16b8aca7124.expectation:
Pass:    Route Refresh capability is negotiated and used for policy re-application; no NLRI cache maintained; `show running-config` omits `inbound-soft-reconfiguration`; explicit configuration of the default (disabled) is idempotent.
Fail-on: Soft-reconfiguration cache is active by default, Route Refresh not attempted on policy change, or configuring the documented default alters running-config.

8ab4f04721753eb6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 interface, enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment` mode. No prior identifier config exists (factory default should be type 4 auto-generated ESI).
Action: Verify default behavior: `show ethernet-segment interface agg-eth 0` should display a type 4 ESI derived from router-id. Configure explicit identifier: `identifier 0 <type0-value>` using a valid 10-octet value (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99). Commit. Test restoration: `no identifier`, commit, verify system reverts to type 4.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays identifier statement under ethernet-segment stanza. `show ethernet-segment interface agg-eth 0` shows the ESI value matching the configured identifier (type 0 when explicit, type 4 after `no identifier`).
8ab4f04721753eb6.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
8ab4f04721753eb6.expectation:
Pass:    Before any identifier command, system auto-generates type 4 ESI. After `identifier 0 <type0-value>`, running-config persists the statement and show ethernet-segment displays the configured 10-octet value. `no identifier` removes the statement and restores type 4 auto-generation.
Fail-on: identifier command rejected during commit, configured ESI value doesn't appear in show output, type field incorrect, or `no identifier` fails to restore type 4 default behavior.

8ad387c23156d2c6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT interface agg-eth 1/1 with ethernet-segment identifier and service-carving highest-preference preference 10000; neighbor PE advertises same ESI with preference 20000; establish BGP EVPN session; IXIA sends bidirectional traffic through agg-eth 1/1.
Action: Reset BGP EVPN control-plane (clear bgp evpn * or platform process restart); upon recovery verify DF election re-converges.
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment detail (DF status, configured preference 10000 persists); show bgp evpn route type 4 (decode DF Election Extended Community bytes 6-7 = 0x2710 = 10000); capture BGP UPDATE with tcpdump to confirm Extended Community advertised; IXIA traffic drop duration.
8ad387c23156d2c6.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
8ad387c23156d2c6.expectation:
Pass:    Configured preference 10000 persists after reset; DF Election Extended Community in Ethernet Segment route carries 0x2710 (10000) in bytes 6-7; DUT becomes DF (10000 < 20000 for highest-preference); traffic outage ≤ documented BGP convergence SLA; auto-recovery completes.
Fail-on: Preference reverts to default 32767 after reset; DF Election Extended Community shows wrong value or missing; DF election incorrect (neighbor becomes DF when DUT has higher preference); traffic outage > 1 s; BGP session does not re-establish; feature stuck requiring manual intervention.

8ad682a115ade727.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT and neighbor PE in mode configuration/l2-services/evpn with advertise-mac enable (default), control-word default. Connect IXIA port-A to DUT (simulates CE1), port-B to neighbor PE (simulates CE2). Establish BGP EVPN session between PEs; verify data-plane tunnel (MPLS or VXLAN) is up.
Action: (1) IXIA port-A transmits frame with src MAC=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 (M1) to trigger local MAC learning on DUT. (2) Verify DUT advertises BGP RT-2 route (MAC/IP Advertisement per RFC7432bis) for M1. (3) IXIA port-B transmits 1000 unicast frames (dst MAC=M1, src MAC=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:02, 64B, line-rate).
Verify: show evpn mac-table on DUT: M1 listed as local. show evpn mac-table on neighbor PE: M1 listed as remote with next-hop=DUT. show bgp evpn routes: RT-2 for M1 present. IXIA port-A: 1000/1000 frames received (0% loss). Packet capture between PEs: EVPN-encapsulated frames (VXLAN/MPLS) with inner dst=M1. show evpn statistics: unknown-unicast-flood counter=0.
8ad682a115ade727.expectation:
Pass:    Known unicast forwarding per RFC7432bis ch.13 operational: M1 learned locally, RT-2 advertised, M1 installed on remote PE, 100% frame delivery from CE2→M1 via EVPN tunnel. advertise-mac=enable (default) active, no unknown-unicast flooding for known destination.
Fail-on: Packet loss >0%, M1 absent from neighbor PE MAC table (fallback to BUM flooding), RT-2 route missing/incorrect, EVPN tunnel not forwarding, or unknown-unicast-flood counter >0 for M1 destination.

8ae87b5aadbb0437.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs in same Ethernet Segment on `agg-eth 0` (using `ethernet-segment` command). Configure matching EVPN instance with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, `export-rt`. Apply identical local DF election policy on both PEs to override Highest-Preference algorithm signaled via BGP (RFC9785 §4.2). IXIA sends 1 Gbps bidirectional L2 traffic through multi-homed segment.
Action: Run for ≥24 h under continuous traffic. Poll `show evpn ethernet-segment` hourly on both PEs to log DF election state and effective policy.
Verify: Confirm both PEs show identical DF election result driven by local policy (not by signaled BGP preference); verify no DF election transitions; check traffic counters monotonic; verify `show platform process memory` stable.
8ae87b5aadbb0437.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
8ae87b5aadbb0437.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs consistently elect same DF per local policy override (not signaled preference) throughout 24 h; zero DF election transitions; traffic loss <0.01%; memory delta <5% from baseline.
Fail-on: DF election inconsistency between PEs; DF election ignores local policy and uses signaled preference instead; DF flapping; traffic loss >0.01%; memory growth >5%.

8b0e3412da53f972.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance 100, iBGP sessions to two IXIA-emulated PE routers (PE1 at 10.1.1.1, PE2 at 10.2.2.2); PE1 advertises EVPN RT-2 for MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01 in VNI 100 with LOCAL_PREF 200, PE2 advertises same MAC with LOCAL_PREF 100; enable advertise-mac under configuration/l2-services/evpn.
Action: Verify BGP convergence via show bgp evpn summary; check both RT-2 routes received via show bgp evpn route-type 2; inject 1000 pps L2 traffic from IXIA to MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01; withdraw PE1's route and verify reconvergence.
Verify: show bgp evpn route-type 2 displays best-path marker (>) on PE1's route (before withdrawal), then PE2's (after); show evpn instance 100 mac-table confirms MAC next-hop matches best-path; IXIA reports <0.1% loss during steady-state, <200ms convergence on PE1 withdrawal.
8b0e3412da53f972.equipment: DUT + IXIA (emulating 2 BGP EVPN PE neighbors + L2 traffic generation)
8b0e3412da53f972.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects PE1's route (LOCAL_PREF 200) over PE2's (LOCAL_PREF 100) per RFC7432bis 7.13 / RFC4271 9.1.2; BGP best-path marker on correct route; MAC table next-hop consistent with best-path; traffic forwarded with <0.1% loss; reconvergence within 200ms; after PE1 withdrawal, PE2 becomes best path automatically.
Fail-on: DUT selects PE2's lower-LOCAL_PREF route while PE1 available; or no best-path marked in BGP table; or MAC table next-hop inconsistent with best-path; or traffic loss >0.1% during steady-state; or reconvergence >200ms; or path selection ignores LOCAL_PREF.

8b1d817b168c56d8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP on DUT (AS 65000) with neighbor PE 192.168.1.2 (AS 65001); establish basic EVPN peering; enter `configuration / routing / bgp / neighbor 192.168.1.2 / af-l2vpn evpn` context.
Action: Configure each of the 8 BGP common commands in the af-l2vpn evpn neighbor sub-mode: `allow-as-in 3`, `capability extended-nexthop`, `inbound-soft-reconfiguration`, `maximum-prefix 10000 80 warning-only`, `private-as remove`, `route-reflector-client`, `weight 200`, `group evpn-peers` (where evpn-peers is a pre-configured peer-group). Commit configuration.
Verify: `show running-config routing bgp neighbor 192.168.1.2` displays all 8 commands under af-l2vpn evpn; `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors 192.168.1.2` confirms RR-client status, weight, soft-reconfig state; BGP session stays Established; send EVPN type-2 route from neighbor PE and confirm DUT accepts it with configured weight applied.
8b1d817b168c56d8.expectation:
Pass:    All 8 BGP common commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) accepted in af-l2vpn evpn neighbor context; commands persist in running-config under af-l2vpn evpn stanza; BGP session remains Established; EVPN routes exchanged with weight 200 visible in `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-distinguisher X.X.X.X:Y detail`.
Fail-on: Any of the 8 commands rejected with CLI syntax error or "command not supported in this context"; commands accepted but missing from `show running-config`; BGP session drops to Idle after applying any command; EVPN routes not received or weight/RR-client attributes not applied.

8b209635b2a5b541.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance "EVPN1" with vlan-based service type; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; attach a VLAN and bind to EVPN.
Action: Under `configuration / l2-services / evpn EVPN1`, issue `control-word enable`; commit; generate bidirectional MAC traffic through the EVPN using IXIA.
Verify: Capture EVPN packets on the core-facing link; inspect MPLS encapsulation for control word header presence between MPLS label and Ethernet frame; confirm `show running-config` displays `control-word enable` in the EVPN1 stanza.
8b209635b2a5b541.expectation:
Pass:    Control word header present in all captured EVPN MAC packets exchanged with neighbor PE; `control-word enable` persisted in running-config.
Fail-on: Control word absent from MPLS packet encapsulation despite enabled configuration, or `control-word enable` not present in running-config after commit.

8b226af6a8e1c8c2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 EVPN instances on DUT (e.g., `evpn evpn100 service-type vlan-based`, `evpn evpn200`, `evpn evpn300`) each with unique RD—manual Type 1 format (PE-loopback:X per RFC4364) or auto-assigned. Establish BGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE (`af-l2vpn evpn` in BGP neighbor config), enable `advertise-mac enable` per instance.
Action: Bring up BGP session, inject MAC entries into each EVPN instance (attach CEs or static MACs). Advertise Type 2 (MAC/IP) and Type 3 (IMET) routes. Capture `show l2-services evpn detail` on DUT and `show bgp evpn routes` on neighbor PE.
Verify: On DUT run `show l2-services evpn` to confirm each MAC-VRF lists unique RD (e.g., 10.0.0.1:100, 10.0.0.1:200, 10.0.0.1:300). On neighbor PE parse received EVPN NLRIs—confirm Type 2 and Type 3 routes carry RD matching originating MAC-VRF (cross-check RD in route prefix against DUT config). If auto-assigned, verify Type 1 RD format (IP:number).
8b226af6a8e1c8c2.expectation:
Pass:    All 3 MAC-VRFs display distinct RD values (no duplicates) in show output. Every advertised EVPN route (Type 2, Type 3) carries the RD of its source MAC-VRF per RFC7432bis §7.9. Auto-assigned RDs follow RFC4364 Type 1 format.
Fail-on: ≥2 MAC-VRFs assigned identical RD. EVPN route advertised with RD not matching its MAC-VRF. MAC-VRF operational but RD field null/zero. Neighbor PE rejects routes due to malformed or duplicate RD.

8b2be420c269d94f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with multi-homed ES (same ESI via `identifier 0 <value>`) in All-Active mode (`load-balancing-mode all-active`). Configure EVI instance; establish BGP EVPN session between PEs. Verify both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI routes with L2-Attr extended community P-bit=1.
Action: Start bidirectional traffic via IXIA. Flap DUT agg-eth interface (`shutdown` / `no shutdown`). Monitor BGP UPDATE withdrawals and re-advertisements. Wait es-waiting-time (default 3 sec) for DF re-election.
Verify: Capture re-advertised Ethernet A-D per EVI routes via `show evpn ethernet-auto-discovery evi` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`. Parse L2-Attr extended community for P-bit=1 on both DUT and neighbor PE. Measure traffic convergence time.
8b2be420c269d94f.expectation:
Pass:    After interface recovery and DF re-election, both DUT and neighbor PE advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI routes with L2-Attr extended community P-bit=1 (All-Active MUST per RFC7432bis §8.6). Traffic converges within fast-convergence bound (RFC7432bis §8 mass-withdrawal).
Fail-on: Any PE in All-Active redundancy group advertises L2-Attr with P-bit=0 post-recovery, or traffic blackhole exceeds documented convergence bound, or Ethernet A-D routes remain withdrawn after es-waiting-time + convergence window.

8b4e537e9781a7f4.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP instance, VRF, and one neighbor configured in af-l2vpn evpn address-family (baseline config via CLI or NETCONF); NETCONF client authenticated.
Action: Via NETCONF <edit-config>, configure all 8 BGP common commands in the af-l2vpn evpn neighbor context: allow-as-in 3, capability extended-nexthop, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix 10000 75, private-as remove, route-reflector-client, weight 100, group <peer-group-name>.
Verify: NETCONF <get-config> returns all 8 configured values; CLI 'show running-config routing bgp vrf <vrf> neighbor <ip> af-l2vpn evpn' displays identical settings; no YANG validation errors in NETCONF response.
8b4e537e9781a7f4.expectation:
Pass:    All 8 commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) accepted by NETCONF in af-l2vpn evpn neighbor context; CLI and NETCONF views match character-for-character.
Fail-on: NETCONF <rpc-error> for any of the 8 commands, YANG schema rejects valid BGP neighbor attribute, or CLI output differs from NETCONF <get-config> for any configured parameter.

8b916f222e12441a.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE both multi-homed to same ES (ESI type 0x00); on DUT configure EVPN instance under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`, set `advertise-mac enable` (default), establish MP-BGP session with `af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor config; configure equivalent EVPN instance on 3rd-party PE.
Action: Each PE allocates LT2 label per ES and advertises BGP EVPN Type 1 route (Ethernet A-D per EVPN Instance, RFC7432bis ch.7.1 NLRI); from remote PE, send known unicast frame destined to MAC on multi-homed ES where LT1 (per-MAC label) is not advertised; capture MPLS packet on wire.
Verify: On DUT run `show bgp evpn routes type 1` (or vendor equivalent) to confirm outbound Type 1 route includes non-zero LT2 label; `show evpn ethernet-segment` lists LT2 label allocation per ES; packet capture shows LT2 label in MPLS stack (RFC7432bis ch.8.4); DUT maps incoming LT2 to correct ES/ET; confirm 3rd-party PE accepts DUT's Type 1 route (no BGP errors in log).
8b916f222e12441a.expectation:
Pass:    DUT and 3rd-party PE both advertise Type 1 routes with LT2 ≠ 0; each accepts the other's route (installed in BGP RIB); when LT1 unavailable, remote PE imposes DUT's LT2 label; DUT receives frame and forwards to correct ES; bidirectional traffic succeeds.
Fail-on: Type 1 route rejected (BGP notification/update error), LT2 label = 0 or missing in NLRI, LT2 not used when LT1 absent (packet uses wrong label or dropped), DUT forwards to wrong ES/ET, or one-way black-hole.

8bd22ca72d174efc.action_steps:
Problem: Duplicate MAC addresses across PEs cause infinite MAC Mobility sequence number growth unless detected and suppressed.
Method: Trigger N+1 MAC moves within M seconds using duplicate-MAC IXIA hosts on two PEs, then verify alarm, syslog, and BGP route freeze.
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology sharing an EVI with the same VLAN. Configure `host mac-address-duplication-detection enable move-interval <M> move-count <N> freeze-time <F>` on both PEs with operator-chosen M and N values. Syslog collector configured and reachable. IXIA ports simulate two hosts with identical MAC address 00:11:22:33:44:55, one attached to each PE in the same VLAN.
Action: Send traffic alternately from each IXIA port to trigger MAC moves between PEs. Generate N+1 MAC move events within M seconds to exceed the duplication threshold. Monitor `show alarms` on both PEs and inspect syslog collector output. Check `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on both PEs to confirm BGP MAC/IP Advertisement route suppression for the duplicate MAC.
Verify: Alarm raised on the detecting PE after the Nth move within M seconds. Syslog entry includes duplicate MAC address 00:11:22:33:44:55 and duplication event details. BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes for 00:11:22:33:44:55 are withdrawn or no longer updated (observable via `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and neighbor PE perspective). After freeze-time expires or operator clears the condition, normal BGP route processing resumes.
8bd22ca72d174efc.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
8bd22ca72d174efc.expectation:
Pass:    Alarm raised at configured move-count threshold within move-interval; syslog entry with duplicate MAC details; BGP MAC/IP routes suppressed for freeze-time duration; M and N configurable and honored.
Fail-on: No alarm after N moves in M seconds, or BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes continue to be sent/updated despite duplication detection.

8bf556d343852b39.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT booted with aggregated Ethernet interface `agg-eth 0` created; no prior EVPN multi-homing or `ethernet-segment` configuration on that interface.
Action: Enter `configure`, navigate to `interface agg-eth 0`, issue `ethernet-segment`, exit to top-level config, then `commit`.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` includes `ethernet-segment` as a sub-stanza; re-enter `configure` / `interface agg-eth 0` / `ethernet-segment` to confirm the sub-mode is accessible.
8bf556d343852b39.expectation:
Pass:    `ethernet-segment` command accepted and commits cleanly; stanza persists in running-config; ES sub-mode re-enterable for further configuration.
Fail-on: Syntax error on command entry, commit failure, or `ethernet-segment` stanza missing from running-config post-commit.

8c00e1c55cb8e6dc.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure three Ethernet Segments on DUT: ES1 with ESI Type 0 (arbitrary 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88), ES2 with ESI Type 1 on LACP LAG, ES3 with ESI Type 4 on single-home interface. Configure EVPN instance, enable BGP EVPN to neighbor PE.
Action: Establish BGP session; capture BGP EVPN Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) routes at neighbor PE (tcpdump on TCP:179). Send 100 tagged L2 frames/sec from IXIA through each ES interface for 30 sec.
Verify: Run `show evpn ethernet-segment` to confirm ESI types; decode captured Type-4 routes to verify ESI Type field (byte 0 of 10-byte ESI) equals 0x00, 0x01, 0x04; verify ESI encoding (Type 1: bytes 1-6 = LACP system MAC + bytes 7-9 = port key; Type 4: bytes 1-4 = router-ID); check `show evpn ethernet-segment statistics` for frame counts.
8c00e1c55cb8e6dc.expectation:
Pass:    Three Type-4 routes advertised with correct ESI Type field (0x00 for ES1, 0x01 for ES2, 0x04 for ES3); ESI value encoding matches type spec (Type 1 carries LACP system MAC in bytes 1-6, Type 4 carries router-ID in bytes 1-4); 3000 frames forwarded per ES (100 fps × 30 sec, 0% loss).
Fail-on: Missing Type-4 route for any ESI type; wrong ESI Type field in byte 0; malformed ESI encoding (Type 1 missing LACP MAC or port key, Type 4 missing router-ID in correct position); any frame loss.

8c3fafa10a54a46c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 each with interface agg-eth ae0 facing CE (LACP System MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff). Under each ae0, enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment` and set ESI to type-1 value encoding the CE LACP System MAC in the high-order 6 octets (e.g., 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:01:00). Configure EVPN instance evpn-1 on both PEs with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100`.
Action: Commit configuration on both PEs. Bring up LACP LAG ae0 on each PE toward the CE.
Verify: Run `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE1 and PE2 to confirm identical ESI 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:01:00. Run `show bgp evpn route type 4` to verify ES route advertised with ESI matching the configured value. Run `show evpn designated-forwarder` to confirm one PE elected as DF. Run `show interface agg-eth ae0` to confirm LAG operational.
8c3fafa10a54a46c.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA (IXIA emulates dual-homed CE with LACP System MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff; two PEs form ES multi-homing; verify ES route exchange and DF election between PEs)
8c3fafa10a54a46c.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs display ESI = 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:01:00 in `show evpn ethernet-segment`; BGP EVPN type-4 ES route present with ESI field 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:01:00 and RD matching local PE; DF election shows exactly one PE with DF role; ae0 up/up on both PEs.
Fail-on: ESI value mismatch between PE1 and PE2, type-4 ES route missing or ESI field does not encode aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff in high-order 6 octets, DF status shows 'non-DF' on both PEs or no DF elected, or LAG ae0 down on either PE.

8c94d4d46456d18b.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP EVPN enabled; Ethernet Segment configured on interface (ESI, ES-Import RT); NETCONF client authenticated (ncclient).
Action: Via NETCONF set DF Election Extended Community Bitmap to: (a) omit (verify default 32767), (b) boundary 0, (c) boundary 65535, (d) mid-range 10000. Repeat via CLI if parameter exposed. Trigger BGP Type-4 route advertisement.
Verify: NETCONF get-config returns configured Bitmap; 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail' displays DF Election Extended Community with correct Bitmap; 'show evpn ethernet-segment' reflects value; capture BGP UPDATE and decode DF Election Extended Community (Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x02, RFC8584 ch.2.2) to confirm Bitmap field matches.
8c94d4d46456d18b.expectation:
Pass:    Default Bitmap = 32767 when unconfigured; all values 0/65535/10000 accepted and consistent across NETCONF get-config, CLI show commands, and BGP Type-4 route (decoded extended community).
Fail-on: Default Bitmap ≠ 32767, value outside 0–65535 accepted, NETCONF/CLI divergence, or BGP Type-4 route missing DF Election Extended Community or Bitmap mismatch.

8cdec7822ac87125.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT and neighbor PE (`configuration / l2-services / evpn`, `advertise-mac enable`). Connect both PEs to IXIA port (multi-homed CE) with identical ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00 on Ethernet Segment interfaces. Establish BGP EVPN peering between PEs.
Action: IXIA sends L2 unicast frame (SMAC=00:11:11:11:11:11, DMAC=00:22:22:22:22:22, VLAN 100) to DUT ES interface. DUT advertises EVPN Type-2 route with ESI label to neighbor PE per RFC7432bis §8.3. Neighbor PE receives EVPN route and MPLS-encapsulated frame.
Verify: On neighbor PE: (1) `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00` confirms ESI state; (2) `show evpn mac-table vlan 100` shows learned MAC 00:11:11:11:11:11; (3) ES interface egress counters = 0 (split-horizon blocks forwarding back to same ESI). Capture BGP update to verify ESI label in Type-2 route.
8cdec7822ac87125.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA (IXIA simulates multi-homed CE on shared Ethernet Segment)
8cdec7822ac87125.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor PE applies split-horizon per RFC7432bis §8.3: frame received with ESI label matching local ES is NOT forwarded to that ES. ES interface egress counter = 0. ESI label present in Type-2 route. MAC learned but not forwarded to originating ES.
Fail-on: ES interface egress counter increments (frame forwarded back to same ESI, creating loop). ESI label missing in Type-2 route. IXIA detects duplicate/looped frames.

8cf991da4fb4ce69.action_steps:
Setup:  Multi-home CE to Exaware DUT (interface with ESI config) and Cisco/Juniper PE with matching ESI; configure EVPN instance with `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; establish BGP session with `af-l2vpn evpn` and import-rt; verify Type 1 Ethernet A-D per-ES routes exchanged with LT4 labels.
Action: From remote PE (or IXIA emulating PE), send BUM frame encapsulated with LT3 broadcast label for the EVPN instance; capture at DUT local CE-facing interfaces.
Verify: Use `show evpn instance <id>` and `show evpn ethernet-segment` to confirm BUM split-horizon table entry exists with LT4 label and ifIndex; verify BUM frame flooded to all local interfaces EXCEPT the ESI interface matching the LT4 label in the Type 1 route from 3rd-party PE.
8cf991da4fb4ce69.expectation:
Pass:    DUT builds split-horizon table with one entry per ESI interface (LT4 label + ifIndex present); BUM frame arrives with LT3, floods to local interfaces, but NOT forwarded to interface with matching LT4 ESI; 3rd-party PE accepts DUT's Type 1 routes and reciprocally honors split horizon.
Fail-on: BUM split-horizon table empty or missing LT4 entry; BUM frame looped back to the ESI interface (visible as duplicate frame at CE or MAC move flap); 3rd-party PE rejects DUT's Type 1 route or format mismatch in LT4 label encoding.

8d108267addeada3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `auto-discovery` enabled under l2-services/vpls; configure `ethernet-segment` under `agg-eth 0` with non-zero ESI value; establish BGP EVPN neighbor; capture baseline A-D per EVI routes (route-type 1) showing RD, 10-octet ESI, and Next Hop = PE loopback address; IXIA generating continuous traffic through the ethernet segment.
Action: Reset the BGP or EVPN control-plane process on the DUT (platform-specific process restart CLI); monitor BGP EVPN session re-establishment and route re-advertisement via packet capture or neighbor PE logs.
Verify: Inspect re-advertised A-D per EVI routes on neighbor PE using `show bgp evpn route-type 1` and BGP update captures; confirm RD matches pre-reset value, ESI remains 10-octet and unchanged, Next Hop field = DUT PE address; measure traffic convergence time via IXIA loss counter.
8d108267addeada3.expectation:
Pass:    A-D per EVI routes re-advertised within process recovery time with identical RD, same 10-octet ESI value, Next Hop = DUT PE address; traffic loss ≤ 1 s.
Fail-on: Routes not re-advertised after 60 s; RD, ESI, or Next Hop fields differ from baseline; process does not auto-recover; traffic outage > 1 s.

8d23c65a2d04d7ef.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PE routers in EVPN topology (BGP `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled); create 6 multi-homed Ethernet segments with ESI types 0–5 (Reserved, Auto-generated, LACP-based, MAC-based, Router-ID, AS-based); IXIA scale rig generates MAC learning traffic across all segments.
Action: Trigger 50K MAC/IP Advertisement routes (Type 2) across all ESI types; measure DUT route advertisement rate via `show bgp evpn statistics`; capture BGP UPDATEs on peer PE and decode ESI first octet for all routes; inject negative test with ESI type 6 or 255.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 2 detail` and pcap decode confirm ESI[0] ∈ {0,1,2,3,4,5} for all 50K routes; `show bgp evpn statistics` reports convergence time; peer PE rejects or DUT refuses to generate any route with ESI[0] > 5.
8d23c65a2d04d7ef.expectation:
Pass:    DUT advertises ≥10K MAC/IP routes/sec; all routes have ESI first byte in range 0–5 per RFC7432bis §7.2; convergence to 50K routes within 30 seconds; invalid ESI type 6 rejected (BGP notification or route not generated).
Fail-on: Any MAC/IP Advertisement route observed with ESI[0] = 6–255; route advertisement rate <10K routes/sec; convergence time >30 sec; or DUT accepts/forwards route with out-of-range ESI type.

8d62a8415716afe3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two VPLS instances (e.g., vpls 100 with `export-rt 65000:100`, vpls 200 with `export-rt 65000:200`) under `configuration/l2-services/vpls/auto-discovery`. On agg-eth-1, configure `ethernet-segment esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11` and associate it with both VPLS instances. Enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbor to neighbor PE.
Action: Admin-up agg-eth-1; wait for BGP EVPN session to reach Established and DUT to advertise route type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11.
Verify: On neighbor PE, run `show router bgp routes evpn ethernet-auto-disc detail` and parse the Type 1 route for the target ESI; extract the Route Target extended communities from the Path Attributes.
8d62a8415716afe3.expectation:
Pass:    Type 1 route carries both RT 65000:100 and RT 65000:200 in Extended Communities (entire set of RTs from all EVPN instances the ES belongs to); each route contains ≥1 RT per RFC MUST.
Fail-on: Type 1 route has zero RT extended communities, or carries only a subset of the configured RTs (e.g., only 65000:100 when ES belongs to both VPLS 100 and 200).

8dccd4f6989011d2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with same ESI (identifier 0 type0-value 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01) under agg-eth interface; set `load-balancing-mode all-active` (override default single-active) on both. Configure MAC-VRF on all PEs. Verify remote PE imports Ethernet A-D per ES routes with ESI Label extended community redundancy mode = 0 and deduces All-Active. IXIA sends continuous traffic (100 Mbps) to MACs behind the multi-homed ES.
Action: On DUT, trigger EVPN control-plane process restart (platform-specific reset CLI or simulated crash). Monitor route re-advertisement and traffic continuity.
Verify: Confirm DUT re-advertises Ethernet A-D per ES routes with redundancy mode = 0 in ESI Label extended community (`show evpn route type 1 detail`, inspect extended community fields). Verify remote PE re-deduces All-Active mode post-recovery and maintains ECMP next-hop set for learned MACs (`show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show evpn mac`). Measure packet loss duration via IXIA counters. Check `show evpn ethernet-segment` on DUT confirms load-balancing-mode all-active restored.
8dccd4f6989011d2.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (multi-homed ES, All-Active) + remote PE (RFC7432bis §14.1.2 All-Active deduction verification) + IXIA traffic gen
8dccd4f6989011d2.expectation:
Pass:    DUT auto-recovers All-Active state without manual intervention; packet loss ≤ 1 second; remote PE re-deduces All-Active from redundancy mode = 0 and maintains ECMP across both PEs; all Ethernet A-D per ES routes re-advertised with ESI Label extended community redundancy mode = 0.
Fail-on: Packet loss > 1 second, load-balancing-mode reverts to single-active (default), remote PE loses All-Active deduction (single next-hop only), redundancy mode field ≠ 0 in re-advertised routes, or recovery requires manual reconfiguration.

8e1e8b15529c6142.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN instance configured (`evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based`) with auto-discovery enabled, RD 65000:100, import-rt/export-rt 65000:100, attached to VLAN 100; base EVPN peering operational.
Action: Enter EVPN instance configuration mode; issue `host mac-address duplicate-detection`; commit configuration.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-100` displays `host mac-address duplicate-detection`; `show mac-address-table duplicate-detection` or `show evpn instance evpn-100 detail` confirms duplicate-detection state as 'Enabled'.
8e1e8b15529c6142.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
8e1e8b15529c6142.expectation:
Pass:    `host mac-address duplicate-detection` persisted in running-config for EVPN instance evpn-100; operational state shows duplicate-detection enabled (via show command output containing 'Duplicate Detection: Enabled' or equivalent); no commit errors.
Fail-on: CLI rejects command with syntax error; configuration not persisted after commit and save; show commands report duplicate-detection as 'Disabled' or 'Not Configured'; commit fails with dependency error (missing EVPN prerequisites).

8e35af6ff23d5d0f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with BGP VRF, EVPN instance (vlan-based service), and peer-group 'EVPN-RR' in af-l2vpn evpn mode; add two neighbors (10.1.1.1, 10.1.1.2) to group; establish EVPN peering.
Action: In af-l2vpn evpn context configure: route-reflector-client (peer-group level), weight 200 on 10.1.1.2, maximum-prefix 100 80, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, allow-as-in 2, private-as remove, capability extended-nexthop; send 85 type-2 MAC/IP routes from 10.1.1.2, then 101 total to trigger maximum-prefix.
Verify: show bgp l2vpn evpn summary confirms group membership; show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor 10.1.1.1 detail displays all eight parameters (allow-as-in, capability, soft-reconfig, max-prefix, private-as, RR-client, weight, group); show bgp l2vpn evpn verifies 10.1.1.2 routes preferred by weight 200; syslog warning at 80 routes, session teardown at 101.
8e35af6ff23d5d0f.equipment: DUT + IXIA (emulating two BGP EVPN neighbors with route injection capability)
8e35af6ff23d5d0f.expectation:
Pass:    All eight BGP commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) accepted in af-l2vpn evpn neighbor/peer-group context; show neighbor detail reflects configuration; route-reflector-client adds ORIGINATOR_ID and CLUSTER_LIST; weight 200 routes preferred over default weight; maximum-prefix warning at 80, teardown at 100.
Fail-on: Any command rejected with syntax error in af-l2vpn evpn mode; command accepted but not visible in show run or show neighbor detail; route-reflector-client does not add RR attributes; weight does not influence best-path selection; maximum-prefix threshold not enforced.

8ea36ca5a5eea53c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 00.01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08; load-balancing-mode all-active` on AggEth1. Enable P2MP LSPs for EVPN BUM traffic. PE3 imports EVPN routes but is not connected to this ESI. IXIA injects multicast traffic via PE1's access port. Verify PE1 (Non-DF for some EVIs) advertises Type 1 (A-D per ES) route with upstream-assigned ESI label L1. Verify PE2 programs L1 in PE1's context label space with 'no-forward-to-ESI' action; PE3 programs L1 in PE1's context.
Action: Kill EVPN control-plane process on PE1 (`kill -9 <evpnd-pid>` or platform debug equivalent). Monitor for auto-restart (≤ 30s). Inject BUM traffic continuously via IXIA.
Verify: During outage: PE2 continues BUM forwarding via P2MP LSP without looping to ESI (label L1 forwarding entry blocks ES forward). PE3 retains L1 in forwarding table. After restart: PE1 re-advertises Type 1 route with ESI label; PE2 and PE3 re-program label; BUM forwarding via PE1 resumes. Check `show evpn route type 1 esi 00:01:02:03:04:05:06:07:08`, `show mpls forwarding-table label <L1>`, IXIA loop detection.
8ea36ca5a5eea53c.equipment: PE1 (DUT) + PE2 (neighbor PE) in All-Active ESI with P2MP MPLS + PE3 (remote EVPN PE) + IXIA for BUM injection and loop detection
8ea36ca5a5eea53c.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN process restarts within 30s. During failure, BUM traffic continues via PE2 with zero loops to ESI (MPLS label L1 shows 'context-label, no-ES-forward' action in PE2's FIB). Packet loss ≤ 3s (default `es-waiting-time`). After recovery, PE1's Type 1 route converges within 10s; PE2/PE3 refresh label L1; total BUM outage ≤ 5s.
Fail-on: Process does not restart, PE2 forwards BUM packets back to ESI (IXIA detects duplicate/looped frames), PE3 loses label L1 entry permanently, or BUM service interruption > 10s (exceeds 3× `es-waiting-time` default).

8edb4b30d6894e9e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT and neighbor PE) with matching ESI (identifier 0 <same-value>) on agg-eth interfaces; set `load-balancing-mode all-active` under ethernet-segment on both; establish BGP EVPN peering and configure EVPN instance on both PEs; connect IXIA as dual-homed CE.
Action: Bring up EVPN instance on both PEs; advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI routes; on each PE execute `show bgp evpn route-type 1` to display Ethernet A-D per EVI routes and capture L2-Attr extended community attributes (specifically P bit and B bit values).
Verify: Parse `show bgp evpn route-type 1` output on both PEs; extract L2-Attr extended community from each Ethernet A-D per EVI route; confirm P bit = 1 in L2-Attr for routes originated by both PEs in the All-Active redundancy group.
8edb4b30d6894e9e.expectation:
Pass:    All Ethernet A-D per EVI routes advertised by PEs in the All-Active redundancy group (matching ESI) contain L2-Attr extended community with P bit set to 1 (per RFC7432bis §8.6: "if L2-Attr extended community is included for All-Active mode, then the P bit must be set for all PEs").
Fail-on: Any PE in the All-Active redundancy group advertises Ethernet A-D per EVI route where L2-Attr extended community has P bit = 0, or L2-Attr extended community is absent from the route.

8f033d7799b36aeb.action_steps:
Setup:  Multi-homed ES: DUT (PE1) and neighbor PE (PE2) both advertise shared ESI; configure Don't Preempt capability on PE1 `ethernet-segment` (exact parameter TBD from CLI spec); PE2 is initial DF (higher DF Preference or boot sequence); IXIA emulates multi-homed CE, sends bidirectional traffic.
Action: Power-cycle PE1 using lab PDU while PE2 is active DF; after PE1 boots, BGP EVPN session re-establishes; wait 60s for DF election to stabilize.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment <esi>` confirms PE2 REMAINS DF (not PE1); `show bgp evpn routes type 4 <esi>` shows PE1's Type-4 route carries DF Election Extended Community with Don't Preempt bit=1 (RFC9785 §4.3); IXIA traffic continuous via PE2, no switchover.
8f033d7799b36aeb.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
8f033d7799b36aeb.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 remains DF post-PE1-recovery; PE1 Type-4 route includes DF Election EC with Don't Preempt=1; IXIA traffic via PE2 uninterrupted (<100ms blip at PE1 boot); BGP session up within 60s
Fail-on: PE1 preempts PE2 (becomes new DF); Don't Preempt bit=0 or EC missing in Type-4; traffic blackhole >100ms; BGP session fails to re-establish; PE1 config lost (Don't Preempt capability reverts to default)

8f0a1faca3b0d948.action_steps:
Setup:  Create `agg-eth 1` and verify default LACP Admin Key equals 1 (the interface number).
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1` mode. Configure `lacp-key 200` and commit. Then configure `no lacp-key` and commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` and `show lacp` after each commit — key should change from 1 → 200 → 1.
8f0a1faca3b0d948.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
8f0a1faca3b0d948.expectation:
Pass:    Custom LACP key (200) applies in running-config and LACP state. `no lacp-key` restores the default value (interface number).
Fail-on: `lacp-key 200` rejected, Admin Key remains at default after configuration, or `no lacp-key` fails to restore interface-number default.

8f1f50045c1167e1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `configuration / l2-services / evpn` (advertise-mac enable, control-word enable, unknown-mac-flooding enable); enable BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn`; configure import-rt. IXIA fault-injection script prepared with 6 malformed Type 3 route variants: (a) PMSI Tunnel Type ≠ 6, (b) PMSI Flag ≠ 0, (c) missing L2-Attr extended community, (d) Next Hop ≠ Originating Router IP, (e) invalid ET ID encoding, (f) Tunnel Identifier ≠ Originating Router IP.
Action: Inject each variant via IXIA BGP speaker; send one valid Type 3 route before and after malformed batch to confirm DUT still processes correct updates.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 3` confirms valid routes present, malformed routes absent; `show bgp neighbor <peer> statistics` shows Update Error counter incremented by 6; `show log` contains "BGP Update error" entries referencing Route Type 3 malformed attributes; `show system process` confirms no crash/core.
8f1f50045c1167e1.expectation:
Pass:    All 6 malformed Type 3 variants rejected (not in `show bgp evpn route-type 3`); Update Error counter = 6; syslog shows 6 distinct BGP update errors citing PMSI Tunnel attribute / L2-Attr / Next Hop / ET ID field violations; valid Type 3 routes sent before/after batch are installed; no process restart.
Fail-on: Any malformed route installed in EVPN RIB; Update Error counter < 6; missing syslog entries; BGP session down; DUT crash; valid Type 3 routes rejected after malformed injection.

8f579c274dcba8da.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 in same EVPN instance with ingress-replication mode; verify unknown-mac-flooding enable (default) on PE2; exchange IMET routes and capture the downstream MPLS label L advertised by PE2 in its IMET route P-tunnel attribute (show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3).
Action: From PE1, send three MPLS-encapsulated packets to PE2 with top label = L: (a) inner broadcast MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF, (b) inner multicast MAC 01:00:5E:01:01:01, (c) inner unknown unicast MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 not in PE2's MAC table. Capture PE2 local attachment circuits.
Verify: show l2-services evpn statistics on PE2 for BUM packet counters; show l2-services evpn mac-table confirms 00:11:22:33:44:55 not learned; packet capture on PE2 ACs confirms all three frames flooded to local CEs; for case (c) verify PE2 treats unicast MAC as unknown-unicast (flooded, not dropped).
8f579c274dcba8da.expectation:
Pass:    All three packets replicated to all local ACs on PE2; BUM counters increment by 3; unknown unicast MAC triggers flood (not L2 lookup miss drop); MPLS label L correctly triggers BUM handling per RFC7432bis §12.1.
Fail-on: Packet with label L dropped; unknown unicast MAC (case c) not flooded; label L does not trigger BUM behavior; broadcast/multicast handled differently than unknown-unicast.

8fa8a27c98f411b1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with bridge-domain VLAN 100; configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth with `load-balancing-mode single-active` (default) and `identifier` type 4 (default); establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; attach IXIA as local CE on attachment circuit with VLAN 100.
Action: Send unicast MAC frames from IXIA with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 (local learning); neighbor PE advertises remote MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE via BGP EVPN Type-2 route with EVPN label LT1; send test frames: IXIA→remote MAC, remote PE→local MAC.
Verify: Execute show mac-table or show evpn mac-table commands; confirm entries contain VLAN-ID (100), MAC Address (keys), ESI, Next Hop type; local MAC entry displays attachment circuit ifIndex; remote MAC entry displays neighbor PE IP as primary (single-active) next-hop; capture forwarded frames at destinations (not flooded).
8fa8a27c98f411b1.expectation:
Pass:    MACT displays both local MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55) with VLAN 100, ESI, and attachment circuit ifIndex as Next Hop; remote MAC (00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE) with VLAN 100, ESI, and neighbor PE IP as primary next-hop; unicast frames forwarded per MACT lookup with correct EVPN label LT1 for remote traffic.
Fail-on: MACT missing any field (VLAN-ID, MAC, ESI, Next Hop type); local MAC shows IP instead of ifIndex; remote MAC shows ifIndex instead of primary IP; unicast frames flooded to all attachment circuits or black-holed; EVPN label not applied to remote-bound frames.

900cd5b1181376b4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with multi-homed ES to same CE; DUT with DF algorithm `preference`, neighbor PE with DF algorithm `modulo`, LACP System MAC intentionally mismatched (e.g., 00:00:01:01:01:01 vs. 00:00:02:02:02:02). Configure syslog destination 192.0.2.100. Enable `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300` on DUT EVI 100. Configure `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface eth1/1` on DUT EVI 100.
Action: Bring up multi-homed ES and verify ES misconfiguration alarm raised. From IXIA, send traffic with MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE alternating between port A and port B 6 times within 180 seconds (exceeds move-count=5). Wait 300s for freeze-time expiry, then repeat flapping until permanent freeze threshold exceeded. Send traffic with static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from a non-eth1/1 port to trigger relearning alarm.
Verify: Execute `show alarms` and confirm presence of: (1) "Multi-homed ES misconfiguration for ESI <esi-value>(agg-eth <n>): from <neighbor-ip>, LACP System MAC <MAC>, DF Algorithm <algo>" with specific mismatched values, (2) "Duplicated MAC address 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE is frozen temporarily", (3) "Duplicated MAC address 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE is frozen permanently", (4) "Relearning of static MAC address 00:11:22:33:44:55 from <source-ip>" per RFC7432bis ch.15.1 and 15.2. Verify syslog collector received all four alarm messages with structured fields (timestamp, severity, ESI/MAC/IP/algorithm). Correct ES config (align DF algorithm and LACP System MAC) and clear MAC conditions; verify all alarms clear from `show alarms`.
900cd5b1181376b4.expectation:
Pass:    All four alarm types present in `show alarms` with correct ESI value, interface (agg-eth), neighbor IP, LACP System MAC, DF algorithm name for ES misconfiguration; MAC address and source IP for duplication (temporary at move-count=6, permanent after repeated freeze-time cycles) and static MAC relearning alarms. Syslog entries received at 192.0.2.100 with structured fields matching alarm details. Alarms clear after ES config corrected and MAC conditions resolved.
Fail-on: Any of the four mandatory alarm types missing; alarm text generic or missing specific ESI/MAC/algorithm/IP values; no syslog entries generated; wrong severity level; MAC not frozen after exceeding move-count threshold; permanent freeze not triggered after repeated violations; static MAC relearning not detected when MAC appears from different location; alarm persists in `show alarms` after condition cleared.

9011650efa13cb80.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` with RD 65000:1, export-rt 65000:100, import-rt 65000:100; establish BGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE; attach IXIA port, send traffic to learn 3+ MAC addresses into evpn1 FDB; apply `no advertise-mac` under evpn1, commit; verify neighbor PE `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` has no MAC routes from DUT RD 65000:1.
Action: Remove the disablement: delete `no advertise-mac` line from evpn1 config (revert to default enabled state); commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn1` no longer shows `no advertise-mac` (implicitly enabled by default); `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` on DUT displays 3+ MAC/IP routes with RD 65000:1; neighbor PE `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 neighbor <DUT-IP> received-routes` lists the same MAC routes.
9011650efa13cb80.expectation:
Pass:    Running-config reverts to default (no `no advertise-mac` present), DUT advertises ≥3 BGP EVPN route-type-2 MAC/IP advertisement routes to neighbor PE, neighbor PE's received-routes table shows DUT's RD 65000:1 and RT 65000:100 on those routes.
Fail-on: `no advertise-mac` still in running-config, zero MAC/IP routes in DUT's BGP EVPN table, neighbor PE receives no routes from DUT, or withdrawn routes not refreshed after config change.

9036cfab1a02ed43.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment with identifier 0 <type0-value> on agg-eth 0; set load-balancing-mode all-active. Establish EVPN instance with neighbor PE sharing the same ESI. Use IXIA to inject traffic with 500+ unique MACs moving between DUT and neighbor PE at ≥100 moves/s, generating MAC/IP Advertisement route updates with incrementing sequence numbers (RFC7432bis ch.15).
Action: While MAC mobility events are active, trigger control-plane reset on DUT (platform-specific process restart).
Verify: After recovery, use show commands to confirm MAC entries contain correct sequence numbers matching pre-reset state +Δ; verify EVPN route-table shows MAC/IP Advertisement routes with mobility sequence numbers; check for zero duplicate MAC entries.
9036cfab1a02ed43.expectation:
Pass:    MAC mobility tracking resumes within 3× es-waiting-time default (≤9 s); all 500+ MACs tracked with correct incrementing sequence numbers; zero stuck or duplicate MAC entries; data-plane forwarding continuous (no full outage).
Fail-on: Recovery >10 s, MAC entries stuck with stale sequence numbers, ≥1 duplicate MAC in table, mobility tracking stops, or full data-plane outage >1 s.

90b089c957433434.action_steps:
Setup:  VPLS instance with auto-discovery enabled on DUT; neighbor PE configured to advertise EVPN routes with RT 100:1 (target) and RT 100:2 (noise); BGP L2VPN EVPN peering established.
Action: Configure `import-rt 100:1` under configuration/l2-services/vpls/auto-discovery; commit; observe route filtering; test `no import-rt 100:1` to remove filter.
Verify: `show running-config` displays `import-rt 100:1` in auto-discovery context; EVPN route table contains only routes with RT 100:1; routes with RT 100:2 are absent; after `no import-rt`, all routes imported (no filtering).
90b089c957433434.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for BGP L2VPN EVPN peering)
90b089c957433434.expectation:
Pass:    Only routes matching configured import-rt value (100:1) appear in local EVPN table; non-matching RT (100:2) filtered; `no import-rt` removes filter and allows all routes.
Fail-on: Routes with RT 100:2 imported despite import-rt 100:1 config, OR routes with RT 100:1 rejected, OR `no` form fails to remove the filter.

90b9bce6c27696ef.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that single-active multi-homing with primary/backup DF election prevents both PEs from forwarding simultaneously while enabling fast failover and aliasing.
Method: Configure two PEs sharing an ESI to one CE in single-active mode with explicit service-carving preference, verify Type 1 routes signal primary/backup roles, trigger DF failover by interface shutdown, and confirm only one PE forwards at any time.

Setup: Connect CE to PE1 interface agg-eth 0 and PE2 interface agg-eth 0 via LACP LAG; configure identical ESI identifier (same 9-octet type0-value), load-balancing-mode single-active, service-carving highest-preference with preference 100 on PE1 and preference 50 on PE2; establish EVPN instance with remote PE3.

Action:
1. Bring up EVPN; verify PE1 elected DF (higher preference wins), PE2 in backup; configure PE3 to advertise routes toward the shared ES and confirm aliasing (both PE1 and PE2 valid next-hops from PE3 perspective).
2. Administratively shut down PE1 agg-eth 0; observe PE2 DF election transition; start IXIA bidirectional traffic across the ES during and after transition.

Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays PE1 as DF pre-shutdown, PE2 as DF post-shutdown; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows P bit set in L2-Attr extended community per RFC7432bis ch.8.6 primary/backup signaling; `show evpn df` confirms exactly one PE in DF state at all times; BGP capture of Type 1 (per-EVI Ethernet Auto-Discovery) routes contains primary/backup indicators; IXIA traffic flows only through current DF with no simultaneous forwarding from both PEs; failover completes without traffic black-hole.
90b9bce6c27696ef.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
90b9bce6c27696ef.expectation:
Pass:    DF election selects PE1 initially (higher preference), only that PE forwards traffic, failover to PE2 completes within ≤1s after PE1 shutdown, aliasing paths available from remote PE3 to reach CE via either PE, no traffic duplication at any point.
Fail-on: Both PEs forward simultaneously (split-brain condition), failover convergence >1s, traffic black-holed during transition, or aliasing paths not established.

90ebf6716f1e1e84.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance using `evpn <evpn-name> service-type vlan-based` with multiple access interfaces. Use IXIA to inject Ethernet frames with identical source MAC addresses from different access interfaces to trigger MAC freeze condition.
Action: After freeze event is logged (monitor syslog or event counters), execute `show evpn frozen mac-addresses`.
Verify: Output displays frozen MAC entries with MAC address, evpn-name, freeze reason (duplicate-detected or move-limit), originating interface, and timestamp. Cross-check against `show evpn mac-addresses` to confirm frozen state.
90ebf6716f1e1e84.expectation:
Pass:    Command lists all frozen MACs with complete attributes (MAC address, evpn-name, freeze reason, source interface, timestamp) matching logged freeze events.
Fail-on: Command returns empty when frozen MACs exist, displays non-frozen MACs, omits mandatory fields (MAC/evpn-name/reason), or command errors.

914cc9f67c88e027.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that EVPN unicast forwarding correctly handles malformed data-plane packets (corrupted MAC headers, truncated MPLS labels, invalid control-words) without crashing or forwarding corrupted traffic.
Method: Establish three-PE EVPN topology with ingress-replication for BUM, advertise IMET routes, learn MAC addresses, then inject malformed unicast frames at PE1 and verify drops with proper error accounting.
Setup: Configure PE1, PE2, PE3 with EVPN EVI; advertise Route Type 3 IMET routes with PMSI tunnel attribute (ingress-replication); enable `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on all PEs; establish MAC learning via initial BUM traffic.
Action: (1) Send legitimate unicast traffic from PE1 to learned MAC at PE2 to confirm baseline forwarding per RFC7432bis ch.13. (2) Use IXIA to inject malformed variants: (a) Ethernet frame with invalid destination MAC format, (b) MPLS-encapsulated packet with truncated label stack, (c) packet with `control-word enable` but corrupted control-word sequence, (d) oversized frame exceeding MTU. (3) Monitor PE1 drop counters, PE2/PE3 receive paths, and syslog.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn statistics` on PE1 increments error counters per variant; `show bgp evpn routes type 3` confirms IMET routes stable with PMSI attribute; tcpdump on PE2/PE3 shows zero malformed packets received; `show log | include drop` records drop events; `show system uptime` confirms no restart.
914cc9f67c88e027.expectation:
Pass: Each malformed variant dropped at PE1; error counter +1 per type; IMET routes stable; PE2/PE3 receive zero malformed packets; syslog entry per drop; no crash.
Fail-on: Malformed packet forwarded to peer PE, missing error counter increment, IMET route withdrawal, system restart, or absent syslog entry.

915a556c89453570.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT agg-eth with ethernet-segment, load-balancing-mode all-active, ingress replication for BUM. IXIA emulates remote PE injecting BUM packets to DUT's platform label space.
Action: IXIA sends 5 malformed ESI label variants: (1) label value 0–15 (RFC3032 reserved range), (2) S-bit=1 when inner label present, (3) ESI label below EVI label in stack (wrong order), (4) label points to non-advertised ES identifier, (5) duplicate ESI label in same packet. 100 packets per variant, 1 pps.
Verify: show mpls forwarding-table | grep <ESI-label>, show evpn ethernet-segment <id> detail, show interface agg-eth statistics (input-errors), syslog | grep "ESI label", tcpdump on ES-facing agg-eth for 60 sec.
915a556c89453570.expectation:
Pass:    All 500 packets dropped before ES forwarding lookup; input-errors counter = 500; syslog contains 5 distinct "ESI label validation failed" or "invalid MPLS label" events; tcpdump captures zero packets on agg-eth; no DUT crash/reload.
Fail-on: Any malformed packet forwarded onto Ethernet segment (tcpdump >0 packets), missing or zero input-errors counter, fewer than 5 syslog entries, DUT crash, or packet forwarded to non-ES peer PE in EVPN instance.

9163bcb1f048cd43.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN instance configured, BGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE active; three test interfaces: IF1 in LACP LAG (peer to neighbor PE), IF2 single-homed ethernet, IF3 multi-homed ethernet.
Action: Configure ethernet-segment ES1 with ESI Type 0 (manual 10-byte value 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on IF3; configure ES2 with ESI Type 1 on IF1 (LACP); configure ES3 with ESI Type 4 on IF2 (single-homed). Test constraints: attempt Type 1 on non-LACP interface IF2 (expect rejection), attempt Type 4 on multi-homed interface IF3 (expect rejection).
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment detail (displays ESI Type 0/1/4 and values); show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail on neighbor PE (ESI byte 0 = 0x00/0x01/0x04); verify Type 1 on non-LACP rejected with error; verify Type 4 on multi-homed rejected with error.
9163bcb1f048cd43.expectation:
Pass:    Type 0, 1, 4 ESI configure successfully on appropriate interface types; neighbor PE receives ES (Type 4) routes with correct ESI encoding (byte 0 of 10-byte ESI = 0x00, 0x01, 0x04); Type 1 rejected on non-LACP interfaces; Type 4 rejected on multi-homed interfaces.
Fail-on: ESI type config accepted on invalid interface (Type 1 on non-LACP, Type 4 on multi-homed), or ESI type rejected on valid interface, or ES route missing, or ESI Type encoding incorrect (byte 0 mismatch 0x00/0x01/0x04), or ESI value corrupted.

91698405a565f7db.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance 100 (VXLAN VNI 10100, route-target 100:100); establish MP-BGP EVPN sessions to 3 neighbor PEs; configure neighbors to advertise IMET (Type-3) routes for EVI 100 with differentiated attributes: PE1 local-pref=200, PE2 local-pref=100 AS-path=[65001], PE3 local-pref=100 AS-path=[65001,65001] RID=3.3.3.3.
Action: Query DUT BGP table (`show bgp evpn route type 3 evi 100 detail`) and confirm PE1 selected (local-pref criterion); withdraw PE1 route, re-query and confirm PE2 selected (AS-path criterion); AS-prepend PE2 to match PE3, re-query and confirm PE3 selected (router-ID criterion).
Verify: After each step, `show bgp evpn route type 3 evi 100` displays best-path indicator (`*>`) on expected route; `show l2vpn evpn instance 100 detail` shows active VTEP matching selected PE; BUM traffic capture confirms replication toward correct PE only.
91698405a565f7db.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects IMET route per RFC 7432bis §7.13.1 step-5 order: (1) highest local-pref, (2) shortest AS-path, (3) lowest router-ID; best-path marker and BUM replication update correctly after each attribute change.
Fail-on: DUT selects route with lower local-pref over higher; applies AS-path check before local-pref; selects higher router-ID over lower; or BUM replication mismatches best-path selection.

917c40749f991804.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with flow-label capability enabled (F-bit=1 advertised); set `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in `configuration / l2-services / evpn`; establish BGP EVPN session with IXIA-emulated remote PE. IXIA prepares malformed BUM packets: MPLS stack [Transport Label][VPN Label][Flow Label ELI+EL][Broadcast Ethernet frame], violating RFC7432bis §18.1 (flow label MUST NOT appear on BUM; ESI label expected instead).
Action: IXIA injects 1000 EVPN BUM packets (MAC DA FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) with flow label inserted after VPN label; vary broadcast/unknown-unicast/multicast frames; send at 100 pps to DUT ingress.
Verify: `show evpn statistics` for malformed-packet or flow-label-on-bum counter; `show log | include flow` for syslog entry; `show l2-services vpls mac-table` confirms no MAC learning from malformed frames; `show system status` confirms stability; re-inject legitimate BUM (with ESI label, no flow label) and verify forwarding via `show l2-services vpls flooding-list`.
917c40749f991804.expectation:
Pass:    All 1000 malformed BUM+flow-label packets dropped at ingress; error counter increments to 1000; syslog entry "Flow label on BUM traffic, packet dropped" logged; zero MACs learned from malformed packets; DUT stable (no crash/reboot); legitimate BUM traffic (ESI label, no flow label) forwards correctly.
Fail-on: Any malformed packet forwarded to remote PEs or local attachment circuits; MAC learned from malformed frame; error counter = 0 or < 1000; missing syslog; DUT crash/restart; legitimate BUM traffic also dropped (collateral damage).

9181f3672178e4f5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance EVI 100, ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:01 on multi-homing LAG interface, BGP neighbors 192.0.2.2 and 192.0.2.3 with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled, import-rt/export-rt 65000:100 under VPLS auto-discovery. Configure neighbor PE1 with matching ESI to form all-active redundancy group.
Action: Test case A: Neighbor PE1 advertises Ethernet A-D Per EVPN Instance (Type 1) routes WITHOUT L2-Attr Extended Community (EC type 0x0F omitted). Capture `show bgp evpn routes type 1 evi 100` and verify DUT applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 aliasing/load-balancing behavior via `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:01`. Test case B: Neighbor PE1 re-advertises Type 1 routes WITH L2-Attr Extended Community included. Verify DUT transitions to RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 behavior (enhanced attribute handling).
Verify: `show bgp evpn routes type 1 evi 100` displays received Type 1 routes and L2-Attr EC presence (EC type 0x0F in extended community list). `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms multi-homing state and which RFC behavior is active (original aliasing vs enhanced L2-attribute processing).
9181f3672178e4f5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (with BGP extended community control to advertise/suppress L2-Attr EC type 0x0F in Type 1 routes) + IXIA
9181f3672178e4f5.expectation:
Pass:    When at least one received Type 1 route lacks L2-Attr EC (test case A), DUT applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 original aliasing behavior for backward compatibility. When all received Type 1 routes include L2-Attr EC (test case B), DUT applies RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 enhanced behavior with L2-attribute processing.
Fail-on: DUT applies RFC7432bis behavior when any peer lacks L2-Attr support (breaks backward compatibility), or fails to process L2-Attr when present in all routes, or multi-homing state incorrect

924ea5c683fd1a3a.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs with identical ESI on agg-eth 0 connecting to shared CE via LACP. On each PE: `configure`, `interface agg-eth 0`, `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (same 9-octet value), `load-balancing-mode single-active`, `commit`. BGP EVPN peering established. IXIA connected to remote site, bidirectional traffic to/from CE.
Action: Bring up LACP bundle. Issue `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs to identify primary DF. Verify only primary PE's agg-eth 0 carries traffic (`show interface agg-eth 0 statistics`). From remote site, confirm CE MACs reachable via EVPN routes with both PEs as next-hops (aliasing). Capture BGP Type-4 ES route. Shutdown primary PE's `agg-eth 0`.
Verify: Pre-failure: `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows one PE as DF, other as non-DF. Only primary PE's agg-eth 0 has active TX/RX counters. Remote site sees CE MACs reachable via both PEs (aliasing confirmed). Type-4 ES route present. Post-failure: Within `es-waiting-time` (default 3 s), backup PE becomes DF. IXIA loss histogram shows traffic interruption ≤ 3 s. Backup PE now carries traffic; CE reachability maintained from remote site.
924ea5c683fd1a3a.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
924ea5c683fd1a3a.expectation:
Pass:    DF election succeeds (one primary, one backup). Pre-failure: only primary forwards, aliasing active (remote sees both PEs). Post-failure: backup promotes ≤ 3 s, traffic loss ≤ 3 s, CE reachability preserved.
Fail-on: No DF election, both forward simultaneously in single-active mode, aliasing broken (remote sees only one PE), failover > 3 s, or post-failure traffic blackhole.

925d142087ed0cad.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS instance with member interfaces x-eth 0/0/1 and x-eth 0/0/2. Configure static MAC address 00:11:22:33:44:55 bound to x-eth 0/0/1. Commit the configuration.
Action: Send 100 L2 frames with destination MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 from x-eth 0/0/2. Monitor interface egress counters.
Verify: Run `show running-config | include mac-address-static`. Run `show mac-address-table` and confirm 00:11:22:33:44:55 is listed with type 'static' (not 'learned' or 'dynamic'). Confirm x-eth 0/0/1 egress counter increased by 100, x-eth 0/0/2 egress counter unchanged (no flooding). Wait 600 seconds (exceeding typical MAC aging timeout), re-run `show mac-address-table`, and verify the entry persists.
925d142087ed0cad.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 present in running-config and MAC table with type='static'; all 100 frames forwarded to x-eth 0/0/1 only (unicast, no flood); entry persists after aging timeout expires.
Fail-on: MAC entry absent from MAC table, listed with type='learned' or 'dynamic' instead of 'static', frames flood to x-eth 0/0/2 or other ports, or entry disappears from MAC table after aging timeout.

928a31a394284a54.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance with an Ethernet Segment and set a non-default `load-balancing-mode` (any mode other than single-active); commit and verify active state.
Action: Issue `no load-balancing-mode` under the relevant context; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `load-balancing-mode` line removed; `show evpn ethernet-segment` or equivalent detail output indicates the Ethernet Segment now operates in single-active mode (the documented default); no orphaned mode state in forwarding tables.
928a31a394284a54.expectation:
Pass:    Config line removed; ES behavior reverts to single-active (only one link forwarding per ES at any time); traffic hashes to designated forwarder only
Fail-on: `load-balancing-mode` still present in running-config, ES continues operating in previous mode (e.g. all-active with per-flow load-balancing), or split-brain state where control plane shows single-active but data plane still load-balances

92beac7bf5690534.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance (RD 65000:100, RT 65000:100) with `advertise-mac enable`, BGP peering (af-l2vpn evpn) to neighbor PE, IXIA host connected to access port in VLAN 10.
Action: IXIA sends gratuitous ARP (MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01, IP 10.1.1.101) to trigger local MAC learning on DUT; DUT advertises BGP EVPN Type 2 route to neighbor PE.
Verify: On neighbor PE, capture received Type 2 route via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` and parse: RD=65000:100, ESI (0 if single-homed), ET ID=0, MAC=aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01, IP=10.1.1.101, MPLS Label LT1 (non-zero per-EVI label), L3 Label (VRF label if IP present), Sequence Number=0, RT=65000:100, Next Hop=DUT PE loopback IP.
92beac7bf5690534.expectation:
Pass:    Type 2 route at neighbor PE contains all 11 mandatory fields (RD, ESI, ET ID, MAC, IP, LT1, L3 Label, Seq Num, RT, Next Hop=DUT IP) matching learned MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 / IP 10.1.1.101; LT1 label allocated and non-zero.
Fail-on: Any field missing from Type 2 NLRI, Next Hop ≠ DUT PE IP, LT1 label = 0 or not allocated, or route not advertised within 5 seconds of MAC learning.

92c7c8aac8a8a74a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance (vlan-based service) on the DUT. Generate L2 traffic via IXIA to learn MAC addresses in the EVPN MAC table. Record the current MAC count via `show evpn mac address-table` (expect ≥ 1 dynamic entry).
Action: Execute `clear evpn mac address-table` at the operational prompt.
Verify: Re-run `show evpn mac address-table` and confirm all dynamic (locally-learned) MAC entries are removed. If static or permanent MACs were present, verify they remain. Resume IXIA traffic and confirm MACs are re-learned within 30 seconds.
92c7c8aac8a8a74a.expectation:
Pass:    All dynamic MAC entries disappear from `show evpn mac address-table` after the clear; static/permanent entries (if any) remain; MACs re-learn successfully when traffic resumes.
Fail-on: Any dynamic MAC persists after clear, or static MACs are incorrectly removed, or MACs fail to re-learn after traffic resumes.

92d554297c9ecc76.action_steps:
Setup:  Under configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-test, configure `mac-limit 25`; commit. Bridge IXIA port to DUT access interface within vpls-test.
Action: IXIA transmits L2 unicast frames with unique incrementing source MAC addresses (pattern 00:11:22:33:00:01 through 00:11:22:33:00:32, total 50 unique source MACs). DUT processes frames and learns source MAC addresses.
Verify: Execute `show l2vpn vpls vpls-test mac-table` and count learned entries. Execute `show l2vpn vpls vpls-test statistics` or equivalent to check mac-limit enforcement status.
92d554297c9ecc76.expectation:
Pass:    MAC table contains exactly 25 learned MAC addresses; mac-limit status shows 25/25 (limit reached); frames with source MACs 26–50 do not result in new MAC table entries.
Fail-on: MAC table exceeds 25 entries, OR learning stops before 25 with available capacity, OR limit has no observable enforcement (all 50 MACs installed).

92d73055d8650c02.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVI 100, non-zero ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on ae1; establish BGP EVPN session to 3rd-party PE (Cisco) via `configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor` `af-l2vpn evpn`; configure Cisco route-policy to advertise two Ethernet A-D per EVI routes (route-type 1) for same EVI 100/ESI: Path-A WITH EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community (P=1), Path-B WITHOUT (via community strip), both with identical local-pref/MED.
Action: Advertise both paths from Cisco simultaneously; send IXIA L2 traffic toward EVI 100 to trigger DUT best-path selection and forwarding.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 1 evi 100 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` on DUT; confirm Path-B (no Layer 2 Attributes) NOT in candidate set per RFC7432bis §7.13.2 step 1.1; Path-A selected as best-path; `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` shows P-flag consideration only after Layer 2 Attributes filtering.
92d73055d8650c02.expectation:
Pass:    DUT applies RFC7432bis §7.13.2 criteria in order: filters out Path-B (no Layer 2 Attributes extended community) at step 1.1 BEFORE evaluating P/B flags; Path-A installed as best-path; IXIA traffic forwarded via selected path.
Fail-on: DUT selects Path-B, evaluates P/B flags before Layer 2 Attributes filtering, applies criteria out-of-order, or treats both paths as equal candidates (violates RFC7432bis §7.13.2 MUST ordering).

92eec206a5f735ae.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE with BGP peering; `af-l2vpn evpn` activated under `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip>`. Session Established without optional capabilities.
Action: On DUT under af-l2vpn evpn context, configure `capability` for ORF Prefix-List and Route Refresh. Commit. On neighbor PE, configure matching capabilities. Clear BGP session `clear bgp <neighbor-ip>`.
Verify: `show bgp neighbors <neighbor-ip>` Capabilities section; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` for session state.
92eec206a5f735ae.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (supporting L2VPN EVPN AF)
92eec206a5f735ae.expectation:
Pass:    Session re-establishes; `show bgp neighbors` Capabilities shows ORF Prefix-List (RFC 5291) and Route Refresh (RFC 2918) negotiated for L2VPN EVPN AF.
Fail-on: Session down, Capabilities section missing ORF/Route Refresh, or BGP logs show capability mismatch error.

930fbe5eab83639c.action_steps:
Problem: Duplicate MAC addresses appearing on different PEs in the same VLAN cause continuous MAC moves that risk infinite sequence number increment.
Method: Configure two EVPN PEs with `host mac-address-duplicate-detection` thresholds, use IXIA to alternate traffic from the same source MAC between PE1 and PE2 access ports to exceed the move threshold, verify operator alert and BGP route freeze, and test M and N configurability by changing parameter values and repeating.
Setup:  Two PEs with EVPN in the same EVI, VLAN 100; BGP L2VPN EVPN peering established with `import-rt` and `export-rt` configured; `configure / l2-services / evpn`: `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5`; IXIA attached to PE1 x-eth 0/0/1 and PE2 x-eth 0/0/2; syslog collector monitoring both PEs.
Action: IXIA alternates frames with source MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 between PE1 port and PE2 port, triggering 6 moves within 180 seconds (exceeds threshold); capture BGP updates and syslog; after freeze, change `move-count` to 3, clear duplicate MAC state, repeat with 4 moves to verify new threshold enforces; attempt additional moves post-freeze to confirm BGP does not send updates.
Verify: Syslog shows operator alert for duplicate MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01; `show evpn mac address-table` marks MAC as duplicate/frozen; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows no new MAC/IP Advertisement route updates sent or processed after freeze; sequence number stops incrementing; with `move-count 3`, detection triggers at 4th move (confirming configurability of N); BGP neighbor at PE2 receives no further updates for the frozen MAC; PE1 stops processing MAC move events for that address.
930fbe5eab83639c.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
930fbe5eab83639c.expectation:
Pass:    Operator alert raised when `move-count` exceeded within `move-interval`; PE stops sending, updating, and processing BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes for duplicate MAC per RFC7432bis §15.1; MAC frozen until operator clears state; M (`move-interval`) and N (`move-count`) parameters are configurable and new values enforce correctly (threshold changes from 5 moves to 3 moves when `move-count` modified).
Fail-on: No operator alert generated; BGP continues advertising MAC moves beyond configured threshold; sequence number increments indefinitely; `move-interval` or `move-count` parameter changes have no effect on detection behavior; MAC not frozen or freeze ineffective (BGP updates continue).

9311f687e3f7db94.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with 100 EVPN-VPLS instances using MP2MP P-multicast tree (mLDP or Ingress Replication); enable DCB label allocation for PMSI Tunnel Attribute and ESI labels; configure multihomed Ethernet Segment (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00) on both PEs with ESI labels allocated from DCB; IXIA scale rig connected to both PEs.
Action: On both PEs, configure DCB label range for PMSI (e.g., 16000-17000) and enable ESI label DCB allocation; verify BGP EVPN peering with `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`; IXIA generates BUM traffic across all 100 VPLSs at 1 Gbps aggregate (1000 MAC addresses per VPLS); capture BGP EVPN Type-3 (Inclusive Multicast) routes with `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type imet` and Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) routes with `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type es`; query PE2 MPLS forwarding table with `show mpls forwarding-table` and count unique incoming labels.
Verify: Parse Type-3 routes: confirm PMSI Tunnel Attribute uses DCB labels (16000-17000 range) not per-VPLS upstream-allocated labels; parse Type-4 routes: confirm ESI label is identical on PE1 and PE2 for ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00; measure egress PE (PE2) MPLS label count with DCB enabled vs baseline (without DCB); measure IXIA traffic stats (throughput, latency, packet loss).
9311f687e3f7db94.expectation:
Pass:    DCB label allocation reduces egress PE MPLS label count by ≥50% (e.g., baseline 10K labels for 100 VPLSs → ≤5K labels with DCB); ESI label from DCB is consistent across PE1 and PE2 (same label value in Type-4 routes); throughput ≥990 Mbps (99% of 1 Gbps aggregate), average latency ≤1ms, packet loss ≤0.01%.
Fail-on: Egress PE label count reduction <50% (≥5K labels); ESI label mismatch between PE1 and PE2; throughput <950 Mbps or average latency >2ms or packet loss >0.1%.

9321f6d2f1f14e2a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as EVPN PE with multi-homing (non-zero ESI on at least one Ethernet Segment); establish eBGP peering with IXIA in `af-l2vpn evpn` address family; configure `import-rt` to accept A-D per EVI routes (type 1).
Action: IXIA injects malformed Ethernet A-D per EVI (type 1) routes: (a) invalid ESI encoding (length ≠ 10 octets, ESI type > 5), (b) malformed EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community (wrong length, reserved flags set, invalid P/B flag combinations 11b or 00b for DF election), (c) missing mandatory NLRI fields (Ethernet Tag ID omitted, Label block malformed), (d) reserved bits set in route distinguisher or MPLS label.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms peer up; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` shows zero accepted malformed routes; `show system alarms` or `show log` captures BGP UPDATE error (sub-code 3 or 11 for malformed attribute); DUT remains stable (no crash, no route flapping on valid ESIs).
9321f6d2f1f14e2a.expectation:
Pass:    All malformed A-D per EVI routes rejected (not installed in EVPN RIB); BGP error counter increments for each malformed UPDATE; syslog records "BGP UPDATE error" with sub-code and peer IP; DUT continues to accept well-formed type-1 routes from same peer.
Fail-on: Any malformed route installed in `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`; DUT crash or BGP session reset; malformed route propagated to other PE peers; missing syslog entry; valid routes from same peer rejected after malformed injection.

935d1ac4d586fc79.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs with MPLS core. PE1: `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based` mapped to access VLAN 100, `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100`.
