PE2: `evpn evpn-200 service-type vlan-based` mapped to VLAN 200 (different VID to exercise translation), matching RT config. IXIA ports on both PE access interfaces.
Action: Send tagged frames (VID=100) from IXIA-A → PE1. Capture MPLS-encapsulated traffic between PEs. Capture egress frames at PE2 → IXIA-B. Display `show l2-services evpn evpn-100 routes` and `show l2-services evpn evpn-200 routes` on both PEs.
Verify: Inner Ethernet header in MPLS transport preserves VID=100. PE2 translates to VID=200 on egress. All EVPN Type-2 (MAC/IP) and Type-3 (IMET) routes show Ethernet Tag ID = 0. Frames reach IXIA-B with VID=200, zero loss.
935d1ac4d586fc79.expectation:
Pass:    MPLS transport preserves originating VID=100 per RFC7432bis §6.1 SHOULD. PE2 performs VID translation (100→200) per MUST requirement. All advertised EVPN routes (Type-2, Type-3) carry Ethernet Tag ID = 0 per §6.1 MUST. Frame delivery 100% (0% loss).
Fail-on: VID stripped/rewritten in MPLS encapsulation. VID translation not performed (egress VID=100 instead of 200). Any EVPN route with Ethernet Tag ID ≠ 0. Frame loss > 0 or MAC not learned.

939f12ee29d4886c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT (`advertise-mac enable`, `af-l2vpn evpn` with Cisco/Juniper PE, matching route-targets via `import-rt`). IXIA connected to customer ports on both DUT and 3rd-party PE. Both PEs have L2VPN/EVPN instances in same EVI.
Action: IXIA sends ARP/traffic to learn MACs on both sides. Capture BGP updates from both PEs to verify MAC/IP Advertisement routes (Type 2, RFC7432 ch.7.2) carry LT1 labels in MPLS Label field. IXIA sends known-unicast frames from DUT-side to 3rd-party-side MAC. Capture inter-PE MPLS packets on wire.
Verify: `show evpn mac` (MACs present in both MACTs), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` (Type 2 routes with label values), `show mpls forwarding-table` (LT1 labels installed), packet capture confirms MPLS label stack includes the advertised LT1 label per RFC7432bis ch.8.4.
939f12ee29d4886c.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs exchange Type 2 routes with non-zero LT1 labels; DUT pushes 3rd-party's advertised LT1 onto outbound packets; 3rd-party pushes DUT's LT1 onto inbound packets; bidirectional IXIA traffic ≥99.9% delivery; captured MPLS frames show correct label values matching BGP advertisements.
Fail-on: LT1 label = 0 or missing in BGP route; route rejected due to encoding mismatch; wrong label in data plane (traffic to wrong MACT or black-holed); packet loss >0.1%; one-way forwarding failure.

93d2f76bf5d658e8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and PE2 with identical ESI identifier (type 0) and load-balancing-mode all-active on shared agg-eth interface; configure PE3 importing EVPN routes but not connected to the ES; P2MP MPLS LSP for BUM transport.
Action: Send BUM traffic from IXIA via the All-Active ES; collect tech-support after traffic forwarding.
Verify: Tech-support includes show evpn ethernet-segment, show evpn route type 1, show mpls forwarding-table (context label space for upstream assigned ESI labels), show l2-services forwarding-table (split-horizon check), running-config (ESI and mode).
93d2f76bf5d658e8.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs (shared ES All-Active + import-only) + IXIA for BUM traffic
93d2f76bf5d658e8.expectation:
Pass:    Ethernet A-D per ES routes contain upstream assigned ESI label from both PEs; PE2's MPLS table shows DUT's ESI label in DUT's context space with split-horizon preventing egress to ES; PE3 programs both ESI labels; running-config confirms all-active mode.
Fail-on: Upstream assigned ESI label missing in type 1 routes, label not programmed on remote PEs, split-horizon not enforced (packets forward to originating ES), or mode not all-active.

93d4b786430481b2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 with EVPN instance, IRB interface for subnet 10.1.1.0/24 using default gateway MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01; configure PE2 as BGP neighbor (af-l2vpn evpn enabled) in same EVPN instance.
Action: From PE2 (or IXIA BGP speaker), advertise EVPN Type-2 MAC route for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 WITHOUT Default Gateway extended community; ensure PE1 receives and processes the conflicting route per RFC7432bis §7.13.1 best path rules.
Verify: On PE2: `show bgp evpn route` confirms withdrawal of the host MAC route; `show alarm active` confirms MAC conflict alarm raised. On PE1: `show bgp evpn route mac aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01` shows route with Default Gateway EC and NO MAC Mobility EC; packet capture confirms withdrawn UPDATE from PE2.
93d4b786430481b2.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
93d4b786430481b2.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 withdraws its host MAC route within 5 seconds of receiving PE1's Default Gateway route; alarm ID 'MAC_CONFLICT_WITH_DEFAULT_GW' raised on PE2; PE1's route carries Default Gateway EC, zero MAC Mobility EC; BGP UPDATE from PE2 shows withdrawn NLRI for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01.
Fail-on: PE2 does not withdraw route; no alarm raised; both PE1 (Default Gateway) and PE2 (host MAC) routes remain active; MAC Mobility EC present on PE1's Default Gateway route; traffic to 10.1.1.1 black-holed or load-balanced incorrectly.

9416fa10f9fefc9b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with P2MP MPLS LSP as PMSI tunnel (IMET route type 3 advertised); send BUM traffic to populate MAC table and exercise unknown unicast flooding over the P2MP LSP.
Action: Collect tech-support including: `show bgp evpn route type imet` (verify PMSI Tunnel attribute with P2MP LSP ID), `show mpls p2mp-lsp` (LSP state, labels), `show evpn instance <evi> detail` (BUM flooding list, P2MP tunnel binding), `show l2-services mac-table evi <evi>` (unknown unicast entries), `show running-config l2-services evpn` + `show running-config routing bgp`, syslog entries filtered for EVPN/MPLS events.
Verify: Tech-support bundle contains all outputs above; IMET route shows PMSI Tunnel attribute type 6 (P2MP-MPLS) or type 7 (MP2MP-MPLS) with LSP identifier; P2MP LSP state = Up with label allocated; flooding list maps to the advertised P2MP LSP; MAC table shows unknown unicast MACs flagged for flooding.
9416fa10f9fefc9b.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: (1) IMET route with PMSI Tunnel attribute carrying P2MP/MP2MP LSP ID, (2) P2MP LSP operational with label binding, (3) EVI flooding list referencing the LSP, (4) unknown unicast MAC entries with flood-to-P2MP-LSP indication, (5) complete EVPN/MPLS/BGP config sections, (6) syslog entries for LSP setup and MAC learning events.
Fail-on: Missing PMSI Tunnel attribute in IMET route output, missing P2MP LSP state, missing flooding list binding, or incomplete config/log sections.

94ca4788c8b35c96.action_steps:
Setup:  Create a vlan-based EVPN instance using `evpn <evpn-name> service-type vlan-based` in `configuration / l2-services` mode and enter the EVPN instance sub-mode.
Action: Execute `host mac-address-duplication-detection` and commit. Verify with `show running-config` filtered to the EVPN instance. Save configuration to startup-config, reboot the DUT, and verify the setting persists after reload. Finally, execute `no host mac-address-duplication-detection`, commit, and verify removal from running config.
Verify: After initial commit, `show running-config` displays `host mac-address-duplication-detection` within the EVPN instance stanza. After reboot, the same line remains present. After `no` form and commit, the line is absent from running config.
94ca4788c8b35c96.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH; reboot required)
94ca4788c8b35c96.expectation:
Pass:    Command configures without syntax error under the EVPN instance, persists in startup-config and survives reboot, and is cleanly removed by the documented `no` form.
Fail-on: Syntax error on configuration attempt, setting missing from running or startup config, configuration lost after reboot, or `no` form fails to remove the setting.

94ff9f356317fc81.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn test-evpn1 service-type vlan-based`, attach AC interface, issue `unknow-mac-flooding disable`, commit. IXIA port connected to one AC; generate L2 traffic with unknown destination MAC (not in EVPN MAC table).
Action: In mode `configuration / l2-services / evpn test-evpn1`, issue `no unknow-mac-flooding`; commit. Send 100 frames from IXIA with unknown DA = 00:00:00:00:99:99.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn test-evpn1` contains no `unknow-mac-flooding` line. `show evpn evpn-name test-evpn1 mac` does NOT list 00:00:00:00:99:99. IXIA port on second AC (or all other ACs in broadcast domain) receives all 100 frames (flooded per default enable behavior). Kernel bridge FDB (`bridge fdb show`) shows no static suppress entry for unknown MACs.
94ff9f356317fc81.expectation:
Pass:    `unknow-mac-flooding` absent from running-config; unknown MAC frames flooded to all ACs in EVPN instance (100 Tx = 100 Rx on other AC); behavior matches default `enable` state.
Fail-on: `unknow-mac-flooding disable` persists in config after `no` command, or unknown MAC frames dropped/not flooded (stuck in disabled state), or kernel FDB retains flood-suppression entry.

952d1bfa02b608b0.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT + Cisco/Juniper PE connected via MPLS; BGP EVPN session established (`af-l2vpn evpn` enabled on DUT neighbor config); VPLS instance with `import-rt 65000:100` / `export-rt 65000:100` and `advertise-mac enable`.
Action: Configure 3rd-party PE to inject BGP EVPN routes with duplicate extended communities: Type-1 route with two ESI Label ECs (labels 16777216, 16777217), Type-4 route with two ES Import Route Target ECs (RT 65000:999, 65000:888), Type-2 MAC/IP route with two MAC Mobility ECs (seq 0, seq 1). Capture BGP Update message with Wireshark.
Verify: On DUT: `show bgp evpn summary` (session Up), `show bgp evpn route-type 1` / `route-type 2` / `route-type 4` (routes accepted), `show l2-services evpn routes` (FIB populated). Parse captured Update: confirm DUT used only first EC instance per type (ESI Label=16777216, ES Import RT=65000:999, MAC Mobility seq=0). Send L2 test frames from IXIA behind each PE; verify bidirectional forwarding.
952d1bfa02b608b0.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts all routes despite duplicate ECs; RIB/FIB show routes installed with first EC instance only (ESI Label 16777216, ES Import RT 65000:999, MAC Mobility seq 0); IXIA reports bidirectional L2 forwarding with 0% loss.
Fail-on: DUT withdraws routes (Treat-as-withdraw due to duplicate ECs), or processes wrong EC instance (not the first), or forwarding fails/black-holes, or BGP session flaps.

955bbfa745745414.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE as multi-homed PEs to the same ES (matching ESI under `configuration/interface/agg-eth/ethernet-segment`). Establish EVPN BGP peering with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled. Create an EVI and configure IXIA as CE sending BUM traffic to the multi-homed ES.
Action: Trigger DF election and send BUM traffic from IXIA to the ES; non-DF PE forwards BUM to DF PE with LT3+LT4 label stack. Collect tech-support bundle on DUT after LT4 labels are allocated and BUM forwarding is active.
Verify: Parse tech-support archive for: `show bgp evpn route type 1` output (Ethernet A-D per ES routes with ESI Label Extended Community showing LT4 values), `show evpn ethernet-segment` (DF/non-DF status, ESI), `show mpls forwarding-table` (LT4 label entries), running-config sections for `ethernet-segment` and `af-l2vpn evpn`, syslog entries for ES state changes and DF election.
955bbfa745745414.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle includes Type 1 routes with ESI Label Extended Community values populated, ES operational status (DF/non-DF per ES), LT4 label allocations per ES, MPLS forwarding table entries for LT4 labels, running-config for ethernet-segment and af-l2vpn evpn, and syslog entries capturing ES bring-up and DF election events.
Fail-on: Type 1 routes absent or ESI Label Extended Community field unpopulated, ES operational state missing, LT4 label allocations not captured, MPLS forwarding state incomplete for LT4, or running-config missing ethernet-segment or af-l2vpn evpn configuration.

95be92ae2d228fa5.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT (AS 65000) peered with Cisco IOS-XR PE (AS 65001) over eBGP; MPLS backbone up; EVPN instance 'TEST-EVPN' active on both; neighbor 10.1.1.2 defined under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf default / neighbor 10.1.1.2`.
Action: Enter `af-l2vpn evpn` sub-mode for neighbor 10.1.1.2; sequentially configure: `allow-as-in 2`, `inbound-soft-reconfiguration`, `maximum-prefix 500 80 warning-only`, `route-reflector-client`, `weight 150`, define peer-group 'EVPN-RR' and apply `group EVPN-RR`; commit; from Cisco PE advertise 50 EVPN Type-2 (MAC/IP) and 10 Type-3 (IMET) routes.
Verify: `show running-config routing bgp vrf default neighbor 10.1.1.2 af-l2vpn evpn` lists all six commands; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms Established state post-commit; `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor 10.1.1.2` displays inbound soft-reconfig ON, prefix count 60/500, peer-group EVPN-RR; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows weight 150 on received routes; if RR topology exists, verify DUT reflects routes to other clients.
95be92ae2d228fa5.expectation:
Pass:    Commands allow-as-in, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, route-reflector-client, weight, group all accepted in af-l2vpn evpn neighbor context without CLI error; BGP session remains Established throughout configuration; received EVPN routes display weight 150; prefix count tracks toward 500 limit with 80% warning threshold; RR-client flag set; peer-group membership shown; interop with Cisco PE succeeds (routes installed, data-plane forwards).
Fail-on: Any command rejected with 'not supported in af-l2vpn evpn context' or syntax error; BGP session drops to Idle/Active after commit; EVPN routes not received or weight not applied; maximum-prefix counter not incrementing; route-reflector-client behavior not observable; peer-group not bound; one-way traffic black-hole.

95c1f0d1c1383a3c.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE interconnected via MPLS; configure BGP EVPN address-family on both routers; create matching EVI (e.g., EVI 100, RD 1:100, RT import/export 100:100) on both sides; attach IXIA ports as CE devices to each PE.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN neighborship; advertise local MAC addresses via Type 2 routes; send unicast and BUM traffic from IXIA-CE1 (behind DUT) to IXIA-CE2 (behind 3rd-party PE); capture 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary', 'show evpn mac', 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1/2/3/5' on both PEs.
Verify: BGP session exchanges all RFC7432bis route types (Ethernet Auto-Discovery [Type 1], MAC/IP Advertisement [Type 2], Inclusive Multicast [Type 3], IP Prefix [Type 5]); ESI encoding accepted by both vendors; MAC addresses appear in remote FIB; IXIA confirms bidirectional unicast forwarding and correct BUM replication with zero frame loss.
95c1f0d1c1383a3c.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs parse and install all received RFC7432bis route types; MAC/IP routes cross-installed in FIB; unicast and BUM traffic forwarded with 0% loss in both directions; no BGP EVPN route rejections or format errors in logs.
Fail-on: BGP peer rejects EVPN NLRI with encoding error; route type missing from peer's BGP table; FIB entry missing for remote MAC; traffic black-holed or one-way forwarding; ESI format incompatibility logged.

95d7d425718de778.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology with multi-homed host H1 attached to both PEs via agg-eth 0 configured with `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 <type0-value>; load-balancing-mode single-active`. PE1 initially active (`es-waiting-time` default 3 s). Both PEs learn H1's MAC via BGP with seq=0. IXIA generates continuous traffic to/from H1.
Action: Kill EVPN control-plane process on PE1 via documented platform reset command (`show platform process | include evpn` to identify). After restart completes, trigger ES failover to PE2 (link-down PE1's agg-eth 0). PE2 becomes active, locally learns H1, and must advertise MAC/IP route with incremented seq per RFC7432bis ch.15.
Verify: Process restarts ≤ 5 s. PE2 advertises H1's MAC with seq=1 (incremented from 0). PE1 withdraws previous route and updates forwarding. IXIA traffic blackhole ≤ 10 s. `show evpn mac address-table` on both PEs shows H1 via PE2.
95d7d425718de778.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts ≤ 5 s; PE2 advertises MAC with seq=1 (incremented from initial 0); PE1 forwards to PE2; traffic blackhole ≤ 10 s post-failover; MAC tables consistent.
Fail-on: Process restart > 5 s, MAC sequence not incremented (still 0 or wrong value), traffic blackhole > 10 s, or stale MAC entries after convergence.

962f5a94d0b94028.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure LAG with ethernet-segment in both single-active mode (default) and all-active mode on separate EVIs; set service-carving to highest-random-weight and lowest-preference; configure es-waiting-time to non-default (5 seconds); exercise traffic through both ES modes (unicast load-balancing across PEs in all-active, active/standby in single-active).
Action: Execute CLI `show tech-support` command after ES state is stable and traffic has been forwarded through both single-active and all-active ESI scenarios.
Verify: Parse tech-support bundle for: running-config showing `ethernet-segment`, `load-balancing-mode single-active|all-active`, `service-carving` settings, `es-waiting-time 5`; output of `show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show evpn instance evi <N>`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn` (Type-1, Type-4 routes with ESI label); kernel FIB entries for ESI next-hops; syslog entries for ES state transitions (DF election, mass-withdrawal, aliasing).
962f5a94d0b94028.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains running-config with both single-active and all-active ES instances, non-default es-waiting-time (5 sec), service-carving modes; show output with ESI identifiers (Type-0 format xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx), DF election results, EVPN Type-1/Type-4 routes, ESI label allocations; FIB entries for ESI aliasing; syslog for DF role changes.
Fail-on: Missing ethernet-segment config, missing show evpn ethernet-segment output, missing Type-1 or Type-4 BGP routes, missing ESI labels, or missing DF election logs in syslog.

9669747c4c8cad31.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN service evpn-100 (vlan-based) configured, one AC interface attached; IXIA port connected to AC interface; no existing mac-limit.
Action: `configure terminal` → `l2-services evpn evpn-100` → `mac-limit 100` → `commit`. From IXIA, send L2 frames with 100 unique source MACs, then attempt 101st unique MAC. Capture `show evpn mac evpn-100` after each batch. Issue `no mac-limit`, commit, send frames with 150 unique MACs.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-100` confirms `mac-limit 100`; `show evpn mac evpn-100` reports exactly 100 learned entries (101st rejected). After `no mac-limit`, same show command reports >100 entries learned.
9669747c4c8cad31.expectation:
Pass:    `mac-limit 100` accepted and persists in running-config; exactly 100 MACs learned, 101st MAC frame not added to table; `no mac-limit` removes restriction, allowing >100 MACs to be learned.
Fail-on: Command parse error; commit fails; 101st MAC learned despite limit 100; fewer than 100 MACs learned; `no mac-limit` does not remove restriction; config does not persist across commit.

96be2f9df23a6c07.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance EVI 100, MTU 1500, control-word enable; configure neighbor PE with matching EVI 100, MTU 1500, control-word enable; establish iBGP peering with af-l2vpn evpn enabled between DUT and neighbor PE; configure matching import-rt/export-rt (e.g., 65000:100).
Action: Advertise MAC route (RT-2) from neighbor PE with L2-Attr Extended Community (MTU=1500, C-bit=1 indicating control-word enabled); verify BGP UPDATE carries L2-Attr per RFC8214.
Verify: show bgp evpn route-type 2 (routes from neighbor PE accepted); show l2-services evpn destination (neighbor PE listed as destination for EVI 100); show bgp evpn summary (routes-received > 0); no L2-attribute mismatch warnings in syslog.
96be2f9df23a6c07.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts routes with matching L2 attributes (MTU=1500, control-word=enabled) and adds neighbor PE as EVPN destination for EVI 100; show l2-services evpn destination displays neighbor PE IP; route count in show bgp evpn summary increments.
Fail-on: Routes rejected or neighbor PE missing from EVPN destination table; syslog reports "L2 MTU mismatch" or "control-word mismatch"; BGP session flaps or routes marked invalid.

976835bce2ef0417.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an ethernet-segment under an aggregation interface (mode: `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment`). Record initial service-carving state in running-config.
Action: Configure `service-carving` with a non-default algorithm per CLI documentation; commit. Then execute `no service-carving`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` ethernet-segment section: after first commit, service-carving shows non-default value; after `no` form, service-carving reverts to default (either line absent or explicit default value).
976835bce2ef0417.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
976835bce2ef0417.expectation:
Pass:    `no service-carving` restores default configuration state (requirement: 'default carving service' is used); running-config reflects default
Fail-on: Explicit non-default service-carving remains in running-config after `no` command execution

978d235cf1ef8c29.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that BGP common commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) can be configured in the L2VPN EVPN address-family context and that service-type, RD, and BGP parameters can be modified on-the-fly without disrupting active MAC learning or data plane forwarding.
Method: Establish a working VLAN-based EVPN instance with active local and remote MAC learning, then apply successive configuration changes to BGP common commands, service-type variants (vlan-based, vlan-bundle, vlan-aware-bundle, port-based), and RD while monitoring IXIA traffic for packet loss and BGP session stability.
Setup:
1. Two-PE topology (PE1, PE2) over MPLS/LDP transport; iBGP EVPN session established.
2. CE attached single-homed to PE1 access port x-eth 0/0/1 carrying VLAN 100.
3. On PE1: configure evpn evi-1 service-type vlan-based, RD 65000:1, import-rt 65000:1, export-rt 65000:1; bind access interface; commit.
4. Verify local MAC learning (REQ#90) in `show evpn mac local` and remote MAC learning (REQ#100) in `show evpn mac remote`; confirm BGP route type 2 advertisements present.
5. IXIA generating bidirectional known-unicast traffic PE1↔PE2 at 1 Gbps for ≥90s.
Action:
1. Under `configuration / routing / bgp / neighbor <PE2> / af-l2vpn evpn`, configure each BGP common command from REQ#20: route-reflector-client, allow-as-in, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, weight, capability, group; commit.
2. Change evpn evi-1 RD from 65000:1 to 65000:2 (REQ#70); commit.
3. Cycle service-type: vlan-bundle (REQ#40) → vlan-aware-bundle (REQ#50) → port-based (REQ#60) → vlan-based (REQ#30); commit each change.
4. Modify BGP weight to 100 and maximum-prefix to 1000; commit.
5. Revert all parameters to original values; commit.
Verify:
1. `show running-config` confirms each parameter change persists across commits.
2. `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows BGP session never leaves Established state — zero flaps.
3. `show evpn evi` reflects current service-type and RD value after each modification.
4. `show evpn mac local` and `show evpn mac remote` display stable MAC entries throughout — no unnecessary withdrawals or re-advertisements.
5. IXIA reports zero packet loss for the entire 90s+ test window; capture per-second loss histogram.
6. Final `show running-config` matches original state byte-for-byte.
978d235cf1ef8c29.expectation:
Pass:    All eight requirements verified: (1) All BGP common commands (REQ#20) configure successfully in af-l2vpn evpn context without CLI rejection. (2) All four service-type values — vlan-based (REQ#30), vlan-bundle (REQ#40), vlan-aware-bundle (REQ#50), port-based (REQ#60) — accept on-the-fly changes. (3) RD modification (REQ#70) applies cleanly. (4) Local MAC learning (REQ#90) and remote MAC learning (REQ#100) remain stable. (5) Zero packet loss. (6) Clean revert to original state.
Fail-on: Any BGP common command rejected by CLI in af-l2vpn evpn context, BGP session flaps (Established→Idle/Active→Established), service-type change fails or triggers session reset, MAC routes unnecessarily withdrawn and re-advertised (indicating data-plane disruption), IXIA packet loss >0 packets, or revert leaves configuration drift.

97a6a664ff7468ff.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with VPLS instance "vpls-test-100" configured under `configuration / l2-services / vpls`; BGP EVPN peering established to neighbor PE (AS 65000, RD/RT capability negotiated); no prior `auto-discovery` config in vpls-test-100.
Action: CLI → `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-test-100`, then `auto-discovery`, then `import-rt 65000:100`, then `export-rt 65000:100`, then `commit`. On neighbor PE, configure matching VPLS with `import-rt 65000:100`.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-test-100` displays the `auto-discovery` block with import-rt and export-rt; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on DUT shows Type-1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) routes advertised with RT 65000:100; `show l2vpn vpls name vpls-test-100` lists neighbor PE as a discovered remote endpoint.
97a6a664ff7468ff.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN session for Type-1 route exchange and VPLS auto-discovery validation)
97a6a664ff7468ff.expectation:
Pass:    `auto-discovery` config accepted and persists in running-config; BGP Type-1 (A-D per EVI) routes appear in `show bgp l2vpn evpn` with export-rt 65000:100 extended community; neighbor PE's `show bgp l2vpn evpn` imports the route (RT match); DUT's `show l2vpn vpls` displays neighbor PE IP/ID as auto-discovered member.
Fail-on: Commit error on `auto-discovery` or RT commands; no Type-1 routes in `show bgp l2vpn evpn` after commit; neighbor PE with matching import-rt fails to import route or discover VPLS instance; `auto-discovery` stanza missing from running-config after reload.

97ae865fe89d7a6e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure three-PE topology: PE1 and PE2 multi-homed to CE-A via ESI (single-active mode), PE3 as remote leaf; enable EVPN with `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbors, `advertise-mac enable`, and matching `import-rt` / `export-rt` values across all PEs. Verify PE3 receives Ethernet A-D per EVPN Instance Route (Type 1) from PE1 via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`; capture BGP UPDATE to confirm presence or absence of L2-Attr Extended Community.
Action: From CE-A (attached to PE1/PE2), send ARP request followed by unicast IPv4 traffic (10.0.0.1 → 10.0.0.100, destination behind PE3); trigger MAC learning on PE1. Capture BGP UPDATE at PE3 showing Route Type 2 MAC/IP Advertisement with MPLS label stack (LT1: EVPN label + LT2: VPN label if present). Inject return traffic from PE3 toward CE-A; observe active PE selection (PE1 or PE2). Repeat with IPv6 traffic (2001:db8::1 → 2001:db8::100).
Verify: Parse captured Route Type 1: if L2-Attr Extended Community absent, confirm PE3 applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 redundancy mode inference from route presence; if L2-Attr present with redundancy mode field = 1, confirm PE3 applies RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 (explicit Single-Active detection). Parse Route Type 2: confirm MAC 00:00:00:00:00:01 advertised with IP binding, MPLS labels allocated per LT1/LT2/LT3/LT4 encoding. On PE3: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` displays the MAC/IP route, `show l2-services fdb` lists MAC in forwarding table pointing to correct EVPN next-hop. Confirm unicast traffic reaches destination with zero loss.
97ae865fe89d7a6e.expectation:
Pass:    PE3 correctly interprets L2-Attr presence/absence in Route Type 1, applies RFC7432 or RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 redundancy mode logic accordingly; Route Type 2 MAC/IP routes propagated with valid MPLS labels; PE3 MAC forwarding table populated; unicast traffic forwarded via selected PE with 100% success rate.
Fail-on: PE3 ignores L2-Attr Extended Community or applies wrong RFC version; Route Type 2 not advertised; MPLS label encoding invalid or missing; MAC not learned in PE3 forwarding table; traffic dropped or forwarded via wrong PE.

98542c81525bc46c.action_steps:
Setup:  VLAN-Aware Bundle EVPN instance configured on x-eth 0/0/1 with restricted VLAN range (e.g., VLANs 1-3 only). Functional test completed: ingress BUM frame sent with VLAN-ID outside the configured range (e.g., VLAN 10), triggering drop per REQ#50.
Action: Execute `tech-support` on DUT. Download and extract the bundle archive.
Verify: Inspect for: (1) `show running-config` section containing `evpn` instance with `service-type vlan-aware-bundle` and VLAN membership configuration; (2) `show evpn evi` output listing the instance, service type, and member VLANs; (3) interface statistics for x-eth 0/0/1 showing non-zero drop/discard counters; (4) syslog or event log entries documenting VLAN mismatch during the test window.
98542c81525bc46c.expectation:
Pass:    Bundle contains EVPN vlan-aware-bundle configuration with defined VLAN range, interface drop counters documenting at least one discarded frame for the out-of-range VLAN test, and complete `show evpn evi` output.
Fail-on: Missing EVPN instance in running-config, zero drop counters on ingress interface despite out-of-range VLAN frame, or absent `show evpn` command outputs.

987b6fcb02e5d28d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with af-l2vpn evpn neighbor to PE (session Established), advertise-mac enable (l2-services/evpn), and import-rt/export-rt (l2-services/vpls/auto-discovery). Establish second BGP session from IXIA to DUT. Verify MAC routes advertised to both peers and RT filtering active.
Action: While DUT↔PE exchange valid EVPN routes (MAC advertisement and RT filtering operational), IXIA sends BGP UPDATE with malformed MP_REACH NLRI: final NLRI with Length field = 1 octet (violates RFC7432bis §7.14.1 requirement that every NLRI has minimum 2 octets: Route Type + Length).
Verify: show routing bgp neighbor <IXIA-IP> (state must transition Established → Idle, indicating session reset per §7.14.1); show routing bgp neighbor <PE-IP> (remains Established, unaffected); show routing bgp evpn routes (DUT continues processing valid routes from PE); show l2-services evpn and show l2-services vpls auto-discovery (advertise-mac and RT filtering operational); syslog contains NLRI length validation error and session-reset notification.
987b6fcb02e5d28d.expectation:
Pass:    DUT resets BGP session with IXIA within 10 sec of malformed NLRI (state != Established); PE session unaffected; advertise-mac and import-rt/export-rt continue processing valid routes; log entry references NLRI length < 2 octets and session reset per RFC7432bis §7.14.1.
Fail-on: IXIA session remains Established >10 sec after malformed NLRI, or PE session also resets (collateral damage), or advertise-mac stops advertising, or RT filtering breaks, or DUT crash/hang, or no NLRI parse error logged, or valid EVPN routes from PE are rejected.

98aba16aa9cbd2c9.action_steps:
Setup: Three-PE topology: PE1 (3rd-party Cisco or Juniper) and PE2 multihomed to CE via Ethernet Segment ES1 in single-active redundancy mode; DUT as PE3 (remote). BGP L2VPN EVPN peering established; `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled under `routing bgp neighbor` on DUT. PE1 advertises Ethernet A-D per EVPN Instance (Type 1) routes for ES1.
Action: Scenario 1 (RFC7432 fallback): Configure PE1 to send Type 1 routes WITHOUT L2-Attr Extended Community. IXIA behind CE transmits IPv4 unicast frame (MAC 00:00:00:00:00:01, src 10.0.0.1 → dst 10.0.0.2). Capture BGP UPDATE on PE1→PE3 link. Decode Type 1 route: confirm L2-Attr Extended Community absent. Decode Type 2 MAC/IP Advertisement per RFC7432bis §7.2: RD (8-byte) + ESI (10-byte) + Eth-Tag (4-byte) + MAC length (1-byte, value 0x30 for 48-bit MAC) + MAC (6-byte) + IP Address Length (1-byte, 0x20 for 32-bit IPv4) + IP (4-byte) + MPLS Label1 (3-byte). On DUT: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2`. Scenario 2 (RFC7432bis): Configure PE1 to send Type 1 routes WITH L2-Attr Extended Community ("Multihoming redundancy mode" field = 0x01 for single-active). IXIA transmits IPv6 frame (MAC 00:00:00:00:00:02, src 2001:db8::1 → dst 2001:db8::2). Capture UPDATE. Decode Type 1: confirm L2-Attr present. Decode Type 2: IP Address Length = 0x80 (128-bit IPv6), IP (16-byte).
Verify: DUT applies RFC7432 §14.1.1 in Scenario 1 (no L2-Attr → single-active deduced per RFC7432 logic). DUT applies RFC7432bis §14.1.1 in Scenario 2 (L2-Attr present → redundancy mode field inspected per RFC7432bis logic). `show evpn mac-table` on DUT: both MACs installed with MPLS label mappings. Send reverse traffic from PE3 toward CE; confirm forwarding succeeds on learned labels for both IPv4 and IPv6 entries.
98aba16aa9cbd2c9.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Cisco or Juniper capable of RFC7432/RFC7432bis mode configuration) + IXIA traffic gen
98aba16aa9cbd2c9.expectation:
Pass: Type 1 without L2-Attr → DUT applies RFC7432 §14.1.1; Type 1 with L2-Attr → DUT applies RFC7432bis §14.1.1. Type 2 NLRI encoded per RFC7432bis §7.2 byte-for-byte: MAC length=0x30 (48-bit), IP Address Length=0x20 (IPv4) / 0x80 (IPv6), MPLS labels present, RD and ESI well-formed. Both MACs learned and installed; bidirectional forwarding operational for both address families.
Fail-on: DUT ignores L2-Attr presence/absence (applies wrong RFC version logic), Type 2 encoding deviates from RFC7432bis §7.2 (MAC length ≠ 0x30, IP Address Length ≠ 0x00/0x20/0x80, missing or malformed label, incorrect RD/ESI structure), MACs not installed on DUT, or forwarding fails in either direction.

995c5bc09586bc41.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that the DUT generates and logs the four mandatory EVPN alarms (multi-homed ES misconfiguration, temporary MAC freeze, permanent MAC freeze, static MAC relearning) with correct severity and structured syslog entries containing the documented fields.
Method: Configure multi-homed ES with intentionally mismatched parameters across PEs (ESI/LACP System MAC/DF algorithm), enable mac-address-duplicate-detection with specific thresholds, configure static MACs, drive conflicting MAC learning patterns via IXIA, verify each alarm appears in `show alarms` and syslog with documented details, then resolve each condition and confirm alarms clear.
Setup: DUT with EVPN configured on agg-eth 0, syslog collector reachable. Neighbor PE configured with same agg-eth 0 but mismatched ESI value, LACP System MAC, and DF Algorithm. Configure `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval <interval> move-count <count> freeze-time <time>` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`. Configure `mac-address-static <mac-address> interface <interface>` for one test MAC.
Action: (1) Bring up neighbor PE with mismatched ES parameters to trigger "Multi-homed ES misconfiguration" alarm. (2) Send IXIA traffic moving a MAC between two interfaces repeatedly within move-interval to exceed move-count (temporary freeze). (3) Continue moving the MAC to exceed permanent freeze threshold. (4) Send IXIA traffic with source MAC matching the configured static mac-address from a different interface/PE to trigger "Relearning of static MAC" alarm. After each trigger, poll `show alarms` and inspect syslog feed. Resolve each condition: correct ES config on neighbor PE, clear duplicate detection state, stop conflicting traffic.
Verify: `show alarms` displays all 4 alarm types with documented format: "Multi-homed ES misconfiguration for ESI <esi-value>(agg-eth 0): from <ip-address>, LACP System MAC <MAC-address>, DF Algorithm <algorithm>", "Duplicated MAC address <mac-address> is frozen temporarily", "Duplicated MAC address <mac-address> is frozen permanently", "Relearning of static MAC address <mac-address> from <ip-address>". Syslog entries contain timestamp, severity, facility, feature, reason, and alarm-specific fields (ESI value, IP address, MAC address, DF algorithm). After resolution, poll `show alarms` every 5s to confirm each alarm clears within ≤30s.
995c5bc09586bc41.expectation:
Pass: All 4 alarm types generate with correct severity; syslog entries contain timestamp, severity, facility, feature, reason, plus alarm-specific fields (ESI value, IP address, MAC address, DF algorithm); each alarm clears within ≤30s of resolution event.
Fail-on: Any of the 4 alarms missing from `show alarms` or syslog, wrong severity, syslog entry missing any documented field, or alarm still active >30s after resolution.

9978f5d24ded87b7.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with a VPLS or EVPN instance configured under `configuration / l2-services`.
Action: Navigate to the VPLS/EVPN context, configure `mac-aging-time` to a non-default value, commit; capture `show running-config`; then issue `no mac-aging-time`, commit; capture `show running-config` again.
Verify: Running-config shows the configured mac-aging-time value after first commit; after `no` form, the explicit aging time is absent (default restored).
9978f5d24ded87b7.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
9978f5d24ded87b7.expectation:
Pass:    Configured value persists in running-config; `no` form removes explicit config and restores default behavior
Fail-on: Command rejected, configuration not saved to running-config, `no` form fails to restore default, or state ambiguous

9988d9a4fd4a1a5a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as single-homing PE with no ethernet-segment on any interface. Establish BGP peering with a neighbor PE that has ethernet-segment configured using identifier 0 <type0-value> or identifier 1 on its agg-eth interfaces. Verify DUT receives and processes Ethernet A-D per-ES (route-type 1) and per-EVI (route-type 4) routes from the multihoming PE. Establish continuous bidirectional IXIA traffic through both PEs.
Action: Execute shutdown followed by no shutdown on the DUT's peering interface while the multihoming PE continues advertising Ethernet A-D routes. The multihoming PE may send mass-withdrawal per RFC7432bis ch.8.7 during the flap.
Verify: After interface recovery, use show bgp l2vpn evpn to verify DUT re-learns Ethernet A-D routes from multihoming PE. Measure traffic convergence time from interface up event to full traffic resumption on IXIA. Verify split-horizon and aliasing procedures remain functional.
9988d9a4fd4a1a5a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (with ethernet-segment and identifier configured) + IXIA traffic gen
9988d9a4fd4a1a5a.expectation:
Pass:    DUT successfully re-learns Ethernet A-D routes from multihoming PE post-flap; traffic converges within the default es-waiting-time of 3 seconds (configuration / l2-services / evpn / es-waiting-time); multihoming procedures (aliasing, split-horizon, mass-withdrawal handling per RFC7432bis ch.8.7) remain operational.
Fail-on: Traffic convergence exceeds 3 seconds, or DUT fails to process Ethernet A-D routes after flap, or traffic blackhole persists, or multihoming interoperability is lost.

99910f2b59aee243.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT (PE1) and PE2 multi-homed to ES1 in All-Active mode with P2MP BUM transport: `interface agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01 / load-balancing-mode all-active`. Configure PE3 in same EVI but not attached to ES1. Verify baseline: PE2 and PE3 import DUT's Ethernet A-D per ES route with upstream-assigned ESI label. Start IXIA BUM traffic from PE3 direction toward ES1.
Action: On DUT, execute `shutdown` on `interface agg-eth 1`, wait 2s, then `no shutdown`. Monitor BGP route withdrawal/re-advertisement and MPLS label reprogramming on PE2 and PE3 during es-waiting-time (default 3s).
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on DUT confirms ESI oper-up; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows DUT re-advertised A-D per ES route with upstream ESI label; `show mpls forwarding-table` on PE2 displays label with split-horizon action (MUST NOT forward to ES1 per RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2); `show mpls forwarding-table` on PE3 shows label programmed in DUT context space; IXIA capture at ES1 device shows zero duplicate BUM frames.
99910f2b59aee243.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs (PE2 multi-homed to same ES1, PE3 remote importing EVI routes) + IXIA for BUM traffic generation and ES device capture
99910f2b59aee243.expectation:
Pass:    Within es-waiting-time (3s default) + BGP convergence (total ≤ 6s from interface no-shutdown): DUT re-advertises upstream ESI label in A-D per ES route; PE2 programs it with split-horizon (MUST NOT forward to ES1); PE3 programs it in DUT context space; zero BUM frame duplicates delivered to ES1 (all MUSTs from RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2 satisfied).
Fail-on: ESI label not re-advertised within 6s OR PE2/PE3 fail to reprogram label OR split-horizon violated (duplicate BUM frames detected at ES1 capture point) OR DUT ESI stuck in oper-down state after es-waiting-time expires.

99a347813a8798cd.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP EVPN peering established to a neighbor PE; baseline inbound route-map applied under af-l2vpn evpn address-family; verify initial EVPN routes accepted.
Action: Configure `inbound-soft-reconfiguration` under configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn. Commit. Modify the inbound route-map (e.g., change community filter or AS-path acceptance). Apply the modified policy via `commit` (do NOT issue `clear bgp` or session reset).
Verify: `show running-config` includes `inbound-soft-reconfiguration` under the neighbor af-l2vpn evpn context. `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms session remains Established (uptime does NOT reset to 00:00:xx). `show bgp l2vpn evpn` reflects route re-evaluation per the new policy (accepted/rejected routes change without session bounce).
99a347813a8798cd.expectation:
Pass:    `inbound-soft-reconfiguration` persists in running-config; inbound policy modification takes effect immediately; BGP session uptime unchanged (no flap); received EVPN Type-2/Type-3/Type-5 routes re-evaluated against new policy without neighbor session reset.
Fail-on: Config commit error; BGP session flaps (uptime resets) when policy is modified; routes not re-evaluated until manual `clear bgp <neighbor>` or session bounces; memory exhaustion warning if neighbor sends large prefix count.

9a0968607c03bdfa.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS instance vpls-100 with `auto-discovery`, then `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`; commit.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-100`; issue `no auto-discovery`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-100` confirms no `auto-discovery` block; `show l2vpn evpn database` returns empty or shows no RT entries for vpls-100; `show tech-support l2vpn` reveals no orphaned auto-discovery state.
9a0968607c03bdfa.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
9a0968607c03bdfa.expectation:
Pass:    `auto-discovery` container and all children (import-rt 65000:100, export-rt 65000:100) absent from running-config; vpls-100 exists with no auto-discovery attributes; local EVPN database shows no stale RT entries for vpls-100.
Fail-on: `show running-config` still contains `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, or `export-rt` under vpls-100; `show l2vpn evpn database` shows stale RT 65000:100; commit fails or leaves partial config (e.g., auto-discovery present but RTs missing).

9a0c6f68137b315a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance evpn100 with `service-type port-based`, bind interface x-eth 0/0/1 via `interface x-eth 0/0/1 / ethernet / evpn evpn100`, configure `auto-discovery / import-rt 65000:100 / export-rt 65000:100` under evpn100; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; IXIA sends bidirectional L2 unicast traffic at 1 Gbps through x-eth 0/0/1 for ≥ 1 minute (stable Tx/Rx counters).
Action: Enter `configuration/l2-services/evpn evpn100/auto-discovery`, change `import-rt 65000:200`, commit; wait 2 s; change `export-rt 65000:200`, commit; wait 2 s; revert to `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`, commit after each.
Verify: After each commit, `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn100` displays the new RT value within ≤ 1 s; `show evpn instance evpn100 detail` shows updated import/export RT; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms BGP session remains Established; IXIA GUI shows Tx frame count = Rx frame count (zero loss) across all three modifications.
9a0c6f68137b315a.expectation:
Pass:    Each route-target modification appears in `show running-config` within 1 s of commit; IXIA reports zero dropped frames (Tx count = Rx count ± 0) during all four config changes; BGP EVPN peering stays Established (no Idle/Connect transitions in `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`).
Fail-on: Any packet loss > 0 frames (Tx ≠ Rx on IXIA), or RT config change takes > 1 s to reflect in running-config, or BGP session flaps (FSM state != Established at any verification point).

9a47a53e892f7680.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN fabric with DUT and neighbor PE using `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP; on DUT configure IRB interface for subnet 10.1.1.0/24 with MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff as default gateway with `advertise-mac enable`; on neighbor PE inject MAC route for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff WITHOUT Default Gateway extended community (simulating host MAC).
Action: Establish BGP EVPN adjacency; neighbor PE receives DUT's IRB MAC route (with Default Gateway extended community) for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff.
Verify: `show bgp evpn routes mac aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff` on neighbor PE confirms route withdrawal; `show alarms` displays MAC conflict with default gateway; `show bgp evpn routes` on both PEs confirms MAC Mobility extended community absent from Default Gateway routes and ignored if received.
9a47a53e892f7680.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor PE withdraws its non-gateway MAC route within BGP convergence time (≤5s); alarm raised indicating MAC conflict with default gateway; MAC Mobility extended community not attached to and ignored on Default Gateway routes per §10.1.1.
Fail-on: Neighbor PE retains conflicting host MAC route after receiving DUT's Default Gateway route, or no alarm logged, or MAC Mobility extended community attached to or processed on Default Gateway routes.
9a47a53e892f7680.sub_category: RFC7432bis §10.1.1 — Best Path Selection for Default Gateway

9a5c1aca7e3e035e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN VPLS instance on DUT and 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper); establish BGP EVPN peering over MPLS; configure matching EVI/VNI; attach CE-facing ports; optionally set `mac-aging-time 300` and `mac-limit 1000` in `configuration / l2-services / vpls` on DUT.
Action: From IXIA behind 3rd-party PE, send L2 frames to trigger local MAC learning on 3rd-party PE; 3rd-party PE advertises BGP EVPN Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes per RFC7432bis §9.2; verify DUT receives routes; send return traffic from IXIA behind DUT to remote MACs.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 2` on DUT lists Type 2 routes from 3rd-party PE; `show evpn mac` (or equivalent) shows remote MACs with EVPN/BGP next-hop; data-plane forwarding works bidirectionally; 3rd-party PE logs/show commands confirm it learned DUT's local MACs remotely.
9a5c1aca7e3e035e.expectation:
Pass:    DUT installs remote MACs from 3rd-party PE's Type 2 routes with correct MPLS label or VXLAN encap; bidirectional L2 forwarding succeeds; MAC table respects configured aging/limit; no route parsing errors in DUT logs.
Fail-on: DUT drops or misparses Type 2 routes from 3rd-party PE; remote MACs missing from DUT bridge table; unidirectional or zero traffic forwarding; route encoding mismatch errors; exceeded `mac-limit` not enforced.

9a8e626db43fb426.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two Exaware PEs in all-active ES (same ESI via `ethernet-segment / identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88` and `load-balancing-mode all-active` on both); configure EVI 100 on both; verify both advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI routes with L2-Attr extended community (P bit set).
Action: Power-cycle the primary DF-elected PE using PDU harness; monitor BGP EVPN session down/up on surviving PE; wait for full boot + `es-waiting-time` expiry on rebooted PE.
Verify: On rebooted PE: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88 evi 100` confirms re-advertised route includes L2-Attr extended community with P bit = 1; `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms DF role re-elected; BGP peering to remote PE re-established.
9a8e626db43fb426.equipment: Two routers + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
9a8e626db43fb426.expectation:
Pass:    After power-cycle, rebooted PE re-advertises Ethernet A-D per EVI route with L2-Attr extended community P bit = 1 (all-active mode MUST); DF election converges within es-waiting-time (default 3 s) + BGP hold-timer; both PEs show consistent DF state.
Fail-on: L2-Attr extended community missing in re-advertised route, P bit = 0 in all-active mode, DF election stuck in non-DF state, or BGP EVPN session fails to re-establish within 2× hold-timer.

9ab5bd716573499c.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs multi-home a CE via agg-eth 0; ESI configured under `ethernet-segment` on both. Basic Functionality rows have validated Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) and Type 4 (Ethernet Segment) routes are advertised.
Action: Execute `tech-support` on both PEs; save bundles off-box. From each bundle extract: (1) running-config section for agg-eth 0 showing the 10-octet ESI value, (2) BGP L2VPN EVPN route table showing Type 1 and Type 4 routes, (3) detailed BGP output for Type 4 routes (`show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` or equivalent) displaying extended communities including ES-Import RT.
Verify: For each PE, locate the ESI value in running-config (10 octets, e.g., 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:00:00). In the BGP detail output for Type 4 routes, locate the ES-Import RT extended community. Extract the high-order 6 octets of the ESI (e.g., 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee) and confirm they match the ES-Import RT value shown in the Type 4 route extended community list.
9ab5bd716573499c.expectation:
Pass:    Both PE tech-support bundles contain ESI config under agg-eth 0, Type 1 and Type 4 BGP route advertisements, and the ES-Import RT in each Type 4 route matches the high-order 6 octets of the configured ESI.
Fail-on: Missing ESI in running-config, missing Type 1 or Type 4 routes in BGP output, ES-Import RT extended community absent from Type 4 route detail, or ES-Import RT value does not equal the first 6 octets of the ESI.

9abcf4686838cac1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with Type 1 RD (e.g., 10.0.0.1:100 where 10.0.0.1 is PE loopback), assign a 10-octet ESI (e.g., Type 0 or Type 3) to a port-channel interface, enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbor, bring the multi-homed interface operationally up to trigger Ethernet Segment (Type 4) route advertisement.
Action: After ES route is advertised to BGP peer, collect tech-support bundle using CLI or automated script.
Verify: Inspect tech-support for: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` output showing Type 1 RD (IP:number format), full 10-octet ESI, Originating Router IP field matching PE loopback; `show evpn ethernet-segment` output; running-config with ESI definition and RD; syslog entries for ES discovery/advertisement events.
9abcf4686838cac1.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains Type 4 route with Type 1 RD format (IPv4-address:number per RFC4364), complete 10-octet ESI matching interface config, Originating Router IP = PE loopback, running-config includes `ethernet-segment` and `route-distinguisher` stanzas, syslog shows ES up and route advertised messages.
Fail-on: Type 4 route missing or RD is not Type 1 format (e.g., Type 0 or malformed), ESI field truncated/zero, Originating Router IP absent or mismatched, ES config missing from running-config, or no ES-related syslog entries in bundle.

9afbb91f6715fd1e.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT has VPLS instance `vpls-100` pre-configured under `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-100`; physical interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 exists and is not bound to any other service.
Action: Enter configuration mode, navigate to `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-100`, issue `interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-100` contains `interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1`; `show vpls vpls-100 detail` lists GigabitEthernet0/0/1 as an attachment circuit with oper-state UP.
9afbb91f6715fd1e.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
9afbb91f6715fd1e.expectation:
Pass:    Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 appears in running-config under vpls-100, shows in `show vpls vpls-100 detail` AC list with oper-state UP, commit succeeds with no errors.
Fail-on: Commit error (e.g., "interface already bound to service X", "invalid interface name"), interface absent from `show vpls` output, or oper-state shows DOWN/ERROR when link is physically up.

9b286ee218b8e203.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (`evpn evpn-name service-type vlan-based`) with RD 100:1, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt` / `export-rt`. Configure `ethernet-segment` under `interface agg-eth` with non-zero 10-octet ESI (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99). Establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; verify Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) routes advertised using `show bgp l2vpn evpn`.
Action: Execute `show tech-support` or `show tech-support evpn`.
Verify: Inspect tech-support bundle for BGP Type 1 route table, RD/ESI/Next Hop values, running-config (evpn/auto-discovery/ethernet-segment), and BGP peer logs.
9b286ee218b8e203.expectation:
Pass:    Bundle contains: (a) Type 1 routes with RD per §7, (b) 10-octet ESI in advertised routes, (c) Next Hop = DUT loopback IPv4/IPv6, (d) complete running-config for evpn/ethernet-segment/auto-discovery, (e) BGP update/peer logs.
Fail-on: Missing Type 1 routes in BGP table, ESI absent or ≠ 10 octets, RD format wrong, Next Hop not local PE, or incomplete config/logs.

9b3acd35a1420ae6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based` on DUT. Configure `ethernet-segment` under interface `agg-eth 0`. Establish BGP L2VPN EVPN peering with neighbor PE; neighbor advertises ES routes (type 4) with matching route-targets.
Action: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment` on DUT.
Verify: Output displays imported ES routes with ESI (10-byte identifier), Route Distinguisher, originating PE IP address, and BGP path attributes. Cross-check displayed ESI against neighbor's `ethernet-segment` configuration using `show running-config interface agg-eth` on neighbor.
9b3acd35a1420ae6.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for ES route advertisement and import)
9b3acd35a1420ae6.expectation:
Pass:    Command returns table listing each imported ES route with valid ESI format, RD matching neighbor's configuration, and originating router-id matching neighbor's BGP router-id.
Fail-on: Command fails with error, returns empty table when neighbor advertised ES routes and BGP session is Established, or displays malformed ESI (not 10 bytes) or RD inconsistent with neighbor's advertisements.
9b3acd35a1420ae6.req_id: CLI:show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment
9b3acd35a1420ae6.sub_category: show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment

9b902100fe3508f6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 on DUT and neighbor PE with identical ESI via `identifier 0 <type0-value>`. Attach to VLAN-aware bridge service; establish BGP EVPN session. Run two sub-tests: (A) `load-balancing-mode single-active` on both PEs, (B) `load-balancing-mode all-active` on both PEs. Connect IXIA to dual-homed CE (LAG to both PEs).
Action: Send bidirectional unicast traffic from IXIA to/from remote site. Capture BGP EVPN Type-1 (Ethernet AD per-ES) and Type-4 (ES route) advertisements. Monitor per-interface ingress/egress counters on DUT agg-eth 0 and neighbor PE.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show evpn mac-table`, `show interface agg-eth 0 statistics`. Inspect BGP EVPN Type-4 route for ESI; Type-1 routes for DF election (RFC7432bis ch.14 service carving). Compare forwarding behavior: single-active vs. all-active.
9b902100fe3508f6.expectation:
Pass:    (A) Single-active mode: only the designated forwarder PE increments egress counters for a given service; non-DF PE does not forward. (B) All-active mode: both PEs increment egress counters; traffic is distributed across PEs per aliasing/load-balancing (no duplicate packets). BGP Type-1 and Type-4 routes present; MAC table stable.
Fail-on: (A) Both PEs forward in single-active mode (DF election failure). (B) Only one PE forwards in all-active mode (no load-balancing). MAC flip-flop between PEs, duplicate packets at receiver, or missing Type-1/Type-4 routes.

9b9ea94152309ed3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and PE2: `interface agg-eth 1`, `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 1 <10-byte-ESI>`, `load-balancing-mode single-active` (default). Associate agg-eth with EVI 100. DUT elected DF (verify `show evpn df-election`). Remote PE3 in EVI 100 imports Ethernet A-D per ES routes. IXIA behind PE3 streams 100 Mbps to MAC behind ES.
Action: On DUT: `interface agg-eth 1`, `shutdown`. Wait 5s, then `no shutdown`.
Verify: On PE3: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` displays ESI Label ext-comm "redundancy mode"=1 in routes from PE2. `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI>` confirms "Redundancy: Single-Active". IXIA: measure traffic loss; confirm forwarding via PE2 only (no duplicates).
9b9ea94152309ed3.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (ES partner) + remote PE (route importer) + IXIA traffic analyzer
9b9ea94152309ed3.expectation:
Pass:    PE3 deduces Single-Active mode from ESI Label ext-comm "redundancy mode"=1 in imported Ethernet A-D per ES (type-1) routes before and after failover. Traffic recovers within 3s of DUT shutdown (RFC7432bis §8 mass-withdrawal). Post-recovery: traffic forwarded via PE2 only (Single-Active enforced; IXIA sees no duplicates).
Fail-on: PE3 does not deduce Single-Active at any point, or traffic loss >5s, or DUT and PE2 both forward post-recovery (mode violation), or stale type-1 routes in PE3 BGP after 10s.

9bace050bef98ecd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 with `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based` mapping access VID 100; configure PE2 with same EVPN instance but mapping access VID 200 (VID translation scenario). Establish BGP EVPN session; enable `advertise-mac enable`.
Action: Send 1000 tagged frames (VID 100) from IXIA to PE1; capture MPLS packets between PE1↔PE2 and egress frames at PE2. Collect `show l2-services evpn evpn-100 routes` from both PEs.
Verify: Inspect packet captures for VID preservation in MPLS core and VID translation (100→200) at PE2 egress; confirm all Type-2 MAC/IP and Type-3 IMET routes show Ethernet Tag ID = 0.
9bace050bef98ecd.expectation:
Pass:    MPLS-encapsulated frames carry original VID 100; PE2 egress frames are retagged to VID 200; all EVPN routes (Type-2, Type-3) display Ethernet Tag ID = 0 in BGP UPDATE and CLI output.
Fail-on: VID stripped or modified in MPLS core, no VID translation at disposition PE, or any EVPN route with Ethernet Tag ID ≠ 0.

9bc015d65f4cf88e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP peer under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor`, enable `af-l2vpn evpn`, and configure `allow-as-in 2`. Establish EVPN session with a neighbor PE configured to advertise EVPN routes with the DUT's local AS prepended in AS_PATH (simulating a route reflection or confederation scenario).
Action: Enter `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor / af-l2vpn evpn` context and issue `no allow-as-in`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `allow-as-in` removed from the neighbor's `af-l2vpn evpn` stanza; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows session remains Established; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms routes with local AS in AS_PATH are now rejected (route count drops); no stale EVPN route entries in `show evpn mac` or `show evpn arp-cache`.
9bc015d65f4cf88e.expectation:
Pass:    `allow-as-in` absent from running-config; BGP session stable; routes with local AS in AS_PATH rejected (AS_PATH loop prevention restored); EVPN MAC/ARP tables consistent with accepted routes only.
Fail-on: `allow-as-in` still present in running-config, routes with local AS in AS_PATH still accepted, BGP session flaps, or EVPN forwarding state contains stale entries from previously accepted looped routes.

9c081670fc3e53bd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `agg-eth 0` on DUT; enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` mode; capture baseline running-config showing default ESI (should be type 4 or absent).
Action: Configure an ESI using the `identifier` command with an explicit type parameter per CLI documentation; commit. Capture `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`. Issue `no identifier`; commit. Capture running-config again.
Verify: Initial config shows type 4 ESI or no explicit `identifier` line (default). Post-configuration shows the explicitly set identifier type in running-config. After `no identifier`, running-config reverts to type 4 or the identifier line disappears.
9c081670fc3e53bd.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
9c081670fc3e53bd.expectation:
Pass:    Default ESI is type 4 (or implicit when no identifier configured); explicit `identifier` configuration persists and displays in running-config; `no identifier` restores type 4 default.
Fail-on: Default is not type 4, explicit identifier config doesn't persist post-commit, or `no identifier` leaves non-default ESI configured.

9c6ef52c229eb36b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 with ethernet-segment identifier under configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment.
Action: 1. Within ethernet-segment config mode, set `load-balancing-mode` to a supported non-default value and commit. 2. Run `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` to confirm. 3. Issue `no load-balancing-mode` and commit. 4. Run `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` to verify default restoration.
Verify: After step 2, running-config displays the explicit load-balancing-mode configuration within the ethernet-segment block. After step 4, config shows either no load-balancing-mode line (implicit default) or explicit 'single-active' value.
9c6ef52c229eb36b.expectation:
Pass:    load-balancing-mode accepts valid mode values, persists after commit, and 'no' form restores single-active default per requirement.
Fail-on: Command rejects valid syntax, configured value doesn't appear in running-config, or 'no' form fails to restore single-active default.

9caac470a00d830b.action_steps:
Problem: verify that tech-support captures complete port-based EVPN configuration and operational state (EVI parameters, MAC tables, BGP routes, interface bindings) for troubleshooting.
Method: configure port-based EVPN instance on LAG and Ethernet port per the requirement example, drive bidirectional traffic, collect tech-support bundle, and inspect for config + operational evidence.
Setup: Two-PE topology with MPLS transport and BGP EVPN session established. Configure `evpn <name> service-type port-based` with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, and `export-rt` on DUT. Bind port-based EVI to `agg-eth 1` and `x-eth 0/0/1`. Neighbor PE configured with matching RT.
Action: Send bidirectional L2 traffic through both port-based interfaces (`agg-eth 1` and `x-eth 0/0/1`). Verify MAC learning and forwarding via `show evpn mac address-table` and `show evpn evi`. Execute `tech-support` command and save bundle off-box.
Verify: Extract bundle and confirm it contains: (1) `show evpn evi` output displaying the port-based service-type and bound interfaces, (2) `show evpn mac address-table` with learned MACs, (3) `show running-config` reflecting `service-type port-based` and `auto-discovery` stanzas for `agg-eth 1` and `x-eth 0/0/1`, (4) BGP EVPN route advertisements (RT-2 MAC/IP routes) with the configured import-rt and export-rt, (5) kernel FIB entries for the port-based EVI.
9caac470a00d830b.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains complete configuration (`service-type port-based`, `auto-discovery`, RT config) and operational state (EVI status, MAC table, BGP routes, FIB) for both `agg-eth 1` and `x-eth 0/0/1` port-based bindings.
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn` output, incomplete running-config (port-based stanza absent), missing MAC address-table entries, or missing BGP EVPN routes in the bundle.

9cb9c5596b0769bd.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that MAC forwarding installation during concurrent BGP process recovery and PE-to-CE link failure follows ESI-dependent rules (ESI=0 or MAX-ESI installs from MAC/IP Advertisement alone; non-reserved ESI waits for both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes).
Method: Configure an all-active multi-homed ES (non-reserved ESI, two PEs) and a single-homed site (ESI=0), establish traffic, restart BGP while simultaneously failing a PE-to-CE link, and verify MAC installation timing and ES state convergence.
Setup: CE1 is dual-homed to DUT and neighbor PE via agg-eth 1 (configure `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01`, `load-balancing-mode all-active` on both PEs). CE2 is single-homed to neighbor PE (no ethernet-segment, implicit ESI=0). IXIA on DUT x-eth 0/0/1 generates traffic to MAC1 (CE1) and MAC2 (CE2) at 100 Mbps per flow for ≥ 1 min before test.
Action: Baseline: `show evpn mac-table` on DUT confirms MAC1 (multi-homed, non-reserved ESI) and MAC2 (single-homed, ESI=0) learned remotely via neighbor PE; `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms agg-eth 1 all-active. Simultaneously: (a) restart DUT's BGP process using platform-specific reset CLI, (b) shut down DUT's agg-eth 1 interface. Monitor route re-arrival via packet capture or BGP logging and IXIA loss histogram.
Verify: For MAC2 (ESI=0): BGP logs/capture show only MAC/IP Advertisement route arrives; `show evpn mac-table` re-installs MAC2 immediately after MAC/IP Advertisement (no wait for Ethernet A-D per ES). For MAC1 (non-reserved ESI): BGP logs/capture show both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes arrive; `show evpn mac-table` re-installs MAC1 only after BOTH routes received, not after MAC/IP Advertisement alone. `show evpn ethernet-segment` on DUT shows agg-eth 1 single-active on neighbor PE after link failure. IXIA histogram shows traffic to both CEs recovers via neighbor PE within es-waiting-time (3 s default) + route convergence (≤ 2 s) = total ≤ 5 s outage.
9cb9c5596b0769bd.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
9cb9c5596b0769bd.expectation:
Pass: MAC2 (ESI=0) forwarding installed from MAC/IP Advertisement alone; MAC1 (non-reserved ESI) forwarding installed only after both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES received; agg-eth 1 ES state converges to single-active on neighbor PE; total outage ≤ 5 s; traffic recovered via neighbor PE for both flows.
Fail-on: MAC2 waits for Ethernet A-D per ES before installing, or MAC1 installs forwarding from MAC/IP Advertisement before Ethernet A-D per ES arrives, or ES state doesn't converge to single-active, or outage > 5 s, or traffic not recovered.

9cdfb3d1b894ae41.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `service-type vlan-bundle` on x-eth 0/0/1, bundle members VLANs 10 and 20. IXIA sends 1000 tagged frames each: VLAN 10 (member), VLAN 20 (member), VLAN 30 (non-member).
Action: Allow traffic flow for 60 seconds. Run `tech-support` and save bundle off-box. Extract and inspect: EVPN configuration section, interface statistics, MAC address table, and syslog entries.
Verify: Bundle contains `show running-config | section evpn` showing vlan-bundle with VLANs 10,20. Bundle contains `show interface x-eth 0/0/1` with drop counters incremented by ~1000 for VLAN 30. Bundle contains `show evpn evi` and `show evpn mac address-table` showing MACs learned only for VLANs 10,20.
9cdfb3d1b894ae41.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains vlan-bundle config specifying VLANs 10,20; interface drop counters showing ~1000 drops matching VLAN 30 frame count; EVPN MAC table with zero VLAN 30 entries.
Fail-on: Bundle missing vlan-bundle configuration section, drop counters not incremented for non-member VLAN, or bundle missing `show evpn evi` / `show evpn mac address-table` outputs.

9ce30e17783159e5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs in the same EVPN instance (e.g., EVI 100) with identical ESI (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) multi-homed to a CE; set `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in `configuration / l2-services / evpn` on both PEs. IXIA emulates CE and remote endpoints; baseline: measure line-rate forwarding (10 Gbps) without multi-homing.
Action: IXIA sends 10 Gbps bidirectional traffic from CE to remote MACs; PE1 receives frames from CE, installs local MAC routes, and advertises them; PE2 learns these MAC routes via BGP and applies Split Horizon (MUST NOT forward frames with the same ESI label back to the originating ES). Measure throughput, p99 latency, packet loss, and PE CPU utilization over 300-second test window.
Verify: Execute `show l2-services evpn evi 100 statistics` and `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs to confirm Split Horizon drop counters increment; IXIA traffic analyzer confirms zero duplicate packets received at CE, throughput ≥ 9.5 Gbps (95% line rate), p99 latency ≤ 5 ms, packet loss ≤ 0.01%, and PE CPU ≤ 70%.
9ce30e17783159e5.expectation:
Pass:    Split Horizon drop counters increment on PE2 (confirming RFC7432bis §8.3 filtering active); IXIA reports zero duplicate packets at CE, sustained throughput ≥ 9.5 Gbps, p99 latency ≤ 5 ms, packet loss ≤ 0.01%, and both PEs' CPU ≤ 70% during the 300 s test.
Fail-on: Any duplicate packets observed at CE (Split Horizon not filtering same-ESI frames); throughput < 9.5 Gbps; p99 latency > 5 ms; packet loss > 0.01%; or CPU > 70% on either PE.

9cf9b7cf343c6728.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs in All-Active redundancy mode (`load-balancing-mode all-active`) sharing an ES (same ESI `identifier 0` value); establish EVPN session; designate one as DF, one as Non-DF via `service-carving` or wait for election.
Action: Send BUM traffic (ARP broadcast) from a CE attached to the Non-DF PE; capture ingress replication of BUM packet to the DF PE with ESI label; collect `show tech-support evpn` on both ingress (Non-DF) and egress (DF) PEs.
Verify: Parse tech-support bundle for: `show evpn ethernet-segment` (ESI label advertised in Type-1 route), `show evpn route type 3` (ingress replication IMET routes), platform FIB/LFIB dump showing ESI label → "do not forward to local ES" action, packet capture showing MPLS ESI label inclusion in BUM packet from Non-DF to DF.
9cf9b7cf343c6728.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: (1) ESI label value from Type-1 Ethernet A-D per ES route for the shared ES, (2) platform label-space entry for that ESI label with forwarding action = drop/no-local-forward, (3) IMET route (Type-3) nexthop with ingress replication encap, (4) packet capture or BUM trace showing ESI label encapsulated when Non-DF sends to DF, absent when sending to non-ES PE.
Fail-on: Missing ESI label advertisement in `show evpn route type 1`, missing platform LFIB entry for ESI label, missing packet encap proof of ESI label inclusion/exclusion rules, or incomplete `show evpn ethernet-segment` detail (load-balancing-mode, DF election state).

9db3bbbdbe31f3e3.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP configured in VRF default; neighbor 192.0.2.1 defined under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf default / neighbor 192.0.2.1`; neighbor PE ready to peer.
Action: Enter neighbor mode, issue `af-l2vpn evpn`, commit; wait for BGP adjacency UP; issue `no af-l2vpn evpn`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config bgp vrf default neighbor 192.0.2.1` includes/omits `af-l2vpn evpn` per config state; `show bgp neighbor 192.0.2.1` displays L2VPN EVPN (AFI/SAFI 25/70) in advertised and received capabilities after enable, absent after disable; adjacency remains stable across toggle.
9db3bbbdbe31f3e3.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (both must support BGP EVPN; IXIA Network Tester with BGP EVPN emulation is acceptable alternative to physical PE)
9db3bbbdbe31f3e3.expectation:
Pass:    `af-l2vpn evpn` accepted in VRF default neighbor context; running-config persists the command; BGP OPEN exchanges include AFI 25 SAFI 70 in Multiprotocol Extensions capability; `no af-l2vpn evpn` removes capability from subsequent OPEN (or triggers graceful restart); adjacency does not flap on enable/disable.
Fail-on: Command rejected or silently ignored; capability (25/70) missing from `show bgp neighbor` capabilities list; `no` form leaves stale config or capability; adjacency tears down on AFI/SAFI enable/disable without graceful restart.

9dea73afb8764353.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 1 on DUT and neighbor PE with identical ethernet-segment identifier (type 0: 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88) and `load-balancing-mode all-active`. Attach both to EVI 100 with BD 100. IXIA emulates CE dual-homed to both PEs via LAG. Advertise 100 MAC addresses from a remote PE into EVI 100.
Action: IXIA sends unicast traffic to the 100 remote MACs at 1 Gbps aggregate (varied 5-tuple flows). Capture per-PE egress forwarding statistics over 60 seconds.
Verify: Execute `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 detail` on both PEs (confirm All-Active, both show DF for subset of services or load-balance state). Check IXIA Rx statistics show traffic distributed across both PE uplinks (not 100% via one PE). Confirm `show interface agg-eth 1 statistics` shows balanced egress frame counts.
9dea73afb8764353.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
9dea73afb8764353.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs show load-balancing-mode all-active in ES operational state. Unicast traffic from IXIA is distributed across both PEs with each PE carrying 30–70% of flows (flow-based load balancing per standard LAG hashing). Zero packet loss (IXIA Tx frames = Rx frames ±0.01%).
Fail-on: Only one PE forwards traffic (100% load on one uplink, 0% on the other), or load imbalance outside 30–70% range, or packet loss >0.1%, or ES operationally down, or mode shows single-active despite config.

9df45a302e36bdff.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 100 with `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default) and `advertise-mac enable`; establish ingress-replication transport to 3 remote PEs (10.0.1.2, 10.0.1.3, 10.0.1.4); verify `show l2-services evpn instance 100` reports 3 peers active and IMET routes exchanged.
Action: IXIA injects 100 pps broadcast traffic (dst MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) with 4 fault variants: (a) Ethernet FCS corruption, (b) frame size 9800 bytes (exceeds MTU 9216), (c) VLAN tag VID 4095 (reserved), (d) source MAC = multicast 01:00:5E:00:00:01. Sustain injection for 60 seconds (6000 total malformed broadcasts).
Verify: Run `show l2-services evpn statistics instance 100` for "rx-malformed-bum-dropped" counter (~6000 packets); `show log | include "BUM drop"` for per-fault-type entries; packet capture on uplinks to 10.0.1.2/3/4 shows zero egress frames; `show system resources` confirms CPU <75% and no process restart.
9df45a302e36bdff.expectation:
Pass:    "rx-malformed-bum-dropped" counter increments to 6000 ±50; syslog contains ≥4 distinct entries identifying each fault type (FCS/oversize/reserved-VID/mcast-src) with "dropped malformed BUM"; packet captures on all 3 PE uplinks show 0 malformed broadcasts replicated (RFC7432bis §16.1 violation if any sent); DUT stable (no crash, CPU <75%).
Fail-on: Any malformed broadcast captured egressing to remote PE 10.0.1.2/3/4, drop counter <5900 or unchanged, missing syslog entry for any of the 4 fault types, or DUT crash/reload during or within 5 min after injection.

9dfa24c9087e6b25.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `interface agg-eth 1`; enter `ethernet-segment` sub-mode and configure a valid ESI; remain in the ethernet-segment configuration context.
Action: Issue `load-balancing-mode single-active`; commit; capture `show running-config interface agg-eth 1`. Issue `load-balancing-mode all-active`; commit; capture running-config. Issue `no load-balancing-mode`; commit; capture running-config.
Verify: Inspect each running-config capture to confirm exactly one mode is present (first: single-active, second: all-active, third: single-active). Check operational ES state with `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` to confirm configured mode is active.
9dfa24c9087e6b25.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
9dfa24c9087e6b25.expectation:
Pass:    Each mode change takes effect on commit; only one mode appears in running-config at any time; second mode cleanly replaces first; `no load-balancing-mode` restores single-active default; operational ES state reflects configured mode.
Fail-on: Both modes present simultaneously in running-config, or mode doesn't switch after commit, or `no` form leaves all-active or shows no mode, or operational state doesn't match config.

9e354ee8dfd16aa6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure aggregated interface `agg-eth 0` with member ports (e.g., `x-eth 0/0/1`, `x-eth 0/0/2`); configure `lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55` (or any valid MAC); commit and verify LACP is operational with the custom system MAC.
Action: Enter configuration mode; issue `no lacp-system-mac`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `lacp-system-mac` is absent; verify LACP now uses the `agg-eth 0` interface MAC as system MAC (check LACP operational state or capture LACP PDU); confirm LAG remains operational with no flaps.
9e354ee8dfd16aa6.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
9e354ee8dfd16aa6.expectation:
Pass:    `lacp-system-mac` removed from running-config; LACP system MAC reverted to the LAG interface (`agg-eth 0`) MAC address; LAG stable with no state transitions.
Fail-on: `lacp-system-mac` persists in running-config, or LACP system MAC does not match LAG interface MAC, or system MAC becomes 00:00:00:00:00:00, or LAG flaps/goes down.

9e35a3dfd1d739d7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with `interface agg-eth 0; ethernet-segment; identifier 0 <type0-value>; load-balancing-mode single-active`. Establish BGP EVPN peering with a neighbor PE. Verify DUT advertises Ethernet A-D per ES route (Type 1) with ESI Label extended community redundancy mode field = 1. Verify remote PE imports the route.
Action: Power-cycle the DUT (use lab PDU / smart-PSU harness) while BGP EVPN session is established and Single-Active ES is active.
Verify: After full boot, check `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` for intact ethernet-segment configuration. Verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn` re-advertises Ethernet A-D per ES route with ESI Label extended community redundancy mode = 1. On remote PE, confirm route import and that PE deduces Single-Active mode (check local EVPN database shows ES as Single-Active, not All-Active).
9e35a3dfd1d739d7.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (remote PE role) + power-cycle harness
9e35a3dfd1d739d7.expectation:
Pass:    DUT recovers with ethernet-segment config intact; BGP EVPN session re-establishes; DUT re-advertises Ethernet A-D per ES route with ESI Label extended community redundancy mode = 1; remote PE deduces Single-Active mode (RFC7432bis §14.1.1 compliance).
Fail-on: Lost ethernet-segment config; BGP session fails to re-establish within es-waiting-time + 60 s; DUT fails to advertise Ethernet A-D per ES route; ESI Label extended community missing or redundancy mode ≠ 1; remote PE incorrectly deduces All-Active mode or fails to import route.

9e70045738efb0c7.action_steps:
