Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `advertise-mac enable` in `/configuration/l2-services/evpn`, establish BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE using `af-l2vpn evpn` in `/configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor`, configure Ethernet Segment on DUT with ESI type 1 (LACP-based, byte[0]=0x01) and type 3 (MAC-based, byte[0]=0x03), inject ARP/MAC traffic from IXIA to trigger MAC/IP Advertisement (RT-2) generation.
Action: Generate RT-2 routes with valid ESI byte[0] values (0x00-0x05 per RFC7432bis §7.2 MUST), optionally inject invalid ESI byte[0]=0x06 from neighbor PE and observe rejection, collect full tech-support.
Verify: Parse tech-support for `show bgp l2vpn evpn route type 2` (RT-2 NLRI with ESI field hex dump), `show evpn esi` (ESI type and 10-octet value), `show evpn mac-ip-table`, running-config `/l2-services/evpn` (advertise-mac state), syslog for ESI validation events.
9e70045738efb0c7.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains RT-2 routes with ESI byte[0] in 0x00-0x05 range visible in NLRI hex or structured output, `show evpn esi` lists configured ESI with type field, MAC/IP table correlates to RT-2, running-config shows `advertise-mac enable`, any syslog entry for ESI byte[0] > 0x05 rejection if invalid case tested.
Fail-on: Missing `show bgp l2vpn evpn route type 2` output, ESI field not captured in NLRI dump, `show evpn esi` absent, no MAC/IP table in tech-support, or advertise-mac state missing from running-config.

9ea9d9f00d311bcd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT agg-eth interface: ethernet-segment identifier type0, load-balancing-mode all-active (default=single-active). Establish BGP EVPN to IXIA (remote PE emulator). Verify Ethernet A-D per ES route shows ESI Label extended community redundancy mode = 0. Start L2 traffic through ES.
Action: Hard power-cycle DUT via PDU. Wait for boot + BGP convergence + es-waiting-time (default 3s).
Verify: show running-config: load-balancing-mode all-active intact. show bgp evpn: session up. show evpn ethernet-segment: ES operational. IXIA capture: Ethernet A-D per ES re-advertised with mode = 0. IXIA traffic: forwarding on all next-hops (all-active per RFC7432bis §14.1.2).
9ea9d9f00d311bcd.equipment: DUT + IXIA (emulating remote PE + traffic gen) + power-cycle harness
9ea9d9f00d311bcd.expectation:
Pass:    All-active config persists; Ethernet A-D per ES with redundancy mode = 0 re-advertised within 20s; BGP up; MAC routes present; traffic uses all paths.
Fail-on: Config lost or single-active; Ethernet A-D absent, delayed >30s, or mode != 0; BGP down >60s; single-path forwarding; ES down >30s.

9eb2b80c52d238d4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth-1: `interface agg-eth-1; ethernet-segment; identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09; load-balancing-mode all-active`. Establish iBGP EVPN peering with IXIA emulating neighbor PE.
Action: IXIA injects four malformed BGP EVPN type-1 (Ethernet Segment route) updates: (1) ESI type=15 (reserved/unsupported), (2) ESI type=0 with length=7 octets (violates 9-octet spec), (3) DF election extended community with reserved bits 0x80 set in flags octet, (4) all-active ES route missing split-horizon label or carrying single-active service-carving extended community (conflict per RFC7432bis §14).
Verify: `show bgp evpn summary` (session=Established), `show bgp evpn routes type 1` (count malformed routes in RIB), `show evpn ethernet-segment identifier 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09` (mode still all-active), `show logging | match "EVPN|ESI|malformed"` (count error entries).
9eb2b80c52d238d4.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (IXIA must support BGP EVPN speaker emulation with programmable NLRI malformation)
9eb2b80c52d238d4.expectation:
Pass:    Zero malformed routes in `show bgp evpn routes type 1`; BGP session state=Established; local ES 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09 remains mode=all-active; syslog contains exactly 4 error entries (one per malformed variant, e.g., "invalid ESI type 15", "ESI length mismatch", "reserved DF bits set", "mode conflict").
Fail-on: Any malformed route present in evpn type-1 RIB, BGP session down/flapping, local ES mode changes to single-active or undefined, DUT crash/reload, or syslog has ≠4 related error entries (indicating missed detection or spurious errors).

9eed31a0cacb4d3c.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology (PE1=DUT, PE2=neighbor) in same EVI with BGP signaling; on PE1 configure `configuration / l2-services / evpn / host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300`; verify config accepted; attach IXIA-A to PE1-access, IXIA-B to PE2-access in same VLAN.
Action: From IXIA-A send frame src-MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE → PE1 advertises route type 2 seq=0; from IXIA-B same MAC → PE2 advertises seq=1; repeat alternating to trigger 5 MAC moves within 180 s; on 6th move PE1 detects duplication, withdraws route type 2, freezes MAC, logs alert; then send BGP update from PE2 and verify PE1 ignores it.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-duplication` shows MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE in frozen state on PE1; `show bgp evpn route type 2` confirms PE1 no longer advertises that MAC; syslog contains 'MAC duplication detected: 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE'; PE1 FIB unchanged when PE2 sends subsequent update.
9eed31a0cacb4d3c.expectation:
Pass:    After N=5 moves in M=180 s, PE1 detects duplication and stops sending/updating/processing BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes per RFC7432bis §15.1 'MUST alert operator and stop'; operator alert logged; move-interval, move-count, freeze-time parameters all configurable and enforced.
Fail-on: PE1 continues advertising/processing route type 2 for duplicate MAC post-detection; no syslog alert; config parameters rejected or not enforced; MAC not frozen for freeze-time duration.

9f21af136ea94cbf.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVI 100 with advertise-mac enable under configuration/l2-services/evpn; attach CE that sends ARP requests and DHCP traffic; verify at least 3 MAC addresses learned locally via show l2-fdb mac.
Action: Execute admin tech-support l2-services evpn instance 100 (or equivalent command that captures EVPN instance state, MAC table, BGP EVPN routes, and syslog).
Verify: Parse tech-support archive for: show l2-fdb mac (local SAP entries), show bgp evpn mac (RT-2 routes for learned MACs), show configuration l2-services evpn (advertise-mac enable), syslog entries with MAC learning events, show router fib (MAC-VRF forwarding entries).
9f21af136ea94cbf.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains MAC table with ≥3 locally learned entries (source = local port/SAP), corresponding BGP EVPN MAC/IP routes (NLRI type 2), running-config snippet showing advertise-mac enable, and syslog/debug traces (if enabled) referencing data-plane MAC learn events.
Fail-on: Missing show l2-fdb mac output; MAC table present but no local entries; advertise-mac state not captured; BGP EVPN routes missing for learned MACs; FIB/forwarding tables excluded from archive.

9f2ffcf8e03919ae.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVI 100 (single-homed, ESI=0 implicit) and EVI 200 with agg-eth using `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88; load-balancing-mode all-active`. Configure neighbor PE to advertise MAC/IP Advertisement routes.
Action: Neighbor PE advertises MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 with ESI=0 for EVI 100. For EVI 200, neighbor PE advertises MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE with non-reserved ESI first (no Ethernet A-D route), wait >3s (es-waiting-time default), then send Ethernet A-D per ES route. Collect `tech-support` bundle after route processing.
Verify: Parse tech-support for `show evpn mac-table`, `show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`, running-config `ethernet-segment` stanza with identifier, and syslog showing route-resolution sequence.
9f2ffcf8e03919ae.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support shows MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 (ESI=0) installed in `show evpn mac-table` immediately upon MAC/IP route receipt. MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE (non-reserved ESI) appears in mac-table only after both MAC/IP Advertisement AND Ethernet A-D per ES routes received. Config shows `es-waiting-time 3` (default).
Fail-on: MAC with non-reserved ESI present in forwarding table before Ethernet A-D route arrival, or missing output from `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1/2`, or no syslog evidence of route-resolution wait state.

9f820a9f3b7b5189.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance via `configuration / l2-services / evpn <name> service-type vlan-based`; confirm defaults: `advertise-mac enable`, `unknown-mac-flooding enable`. Attach one CE port to the EVPN instance. IXIA emulates CE on this port.
Action: IXIA sends unicast frame from CE with destination MAC = source MAC (hairpin test), then broadcasts a BUM frame from same CE port.
Verify: Capture on same CE port; run `show l2-services evpn <name> mac-table` to confirm MAC learned; verify zero egress frames on ingress interface.
9f820a9f3b7b5189.expectation:
Pass:    Zero frames egress the ingress CE port; MAC table shows learned entry; no hairpin loop detected in 60-second capture.
Fail-on: Any frame (unicast or BUM) received on same port it was sent from; hairpin loop detected.

9faaa53b71ea5439.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with advertise-mac enable; establish BGP EVPN peering with IXIA; IXIA learns LT1 label from DUT's MAC/IP Advertisement route (RFC7432 §7.2); prime IXIA fault-injection for MPLS label corruption.
Action: IXIA sends known-unicast packets with malformed LT1 labels: (a) reserved label values 0–15, (b) S-bit=0 when LT1 is bottom-of-stack, (c) label value exceeding DUT's allocated range, (d) bit-shifted label (wrong 20-bit alignment), (e) LT1 pointing to non-existent MACT ID.
Verify: `show mpls forwarding-table` confirms no forwarding for malformed labels; `show l2-services evpn statistics` shows label-error counter increments per variant; `show logging` captures "invalid MPLS label" and "unknown MACT" events; BGP session remains Established.
9faaa53b71ea5439.expectation:
Pass:    All 5 variants dropped at ingress; label-error counter increments by ≥5; syslog entries present for each variant type; BGP session stable; no EVPN route withdrawal.
Fail-on: Any malformed packet forwarded into MACT; missing counter increment; missing syslog entry; DUT crash, BGP session flap, or stale FIB entry for invalid label.

9fe9327f7ef0a57c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `auto-discovery` enabled on DUT and neighbor PE; attach multi-homed `agg-eth 0` Ethernet Segment to both PEs with identical ESI; configure multiple VLANs (10, 20, 30) in the EVPN service on both PEs.
Action: Apply service-carving configuration under the appropriate EVPN or ES mode to alter Default Forwarder Election algorithm; commit. Inject BUM (broadcast/unknown-unicast/multicast) traffic from IXIA into each VLAN via the multi-homed link. Then issue `no service-carving`; commit; re-inject BUM traffic.
Verify: Use `show running-config` to confirm service-carving appears and is later removed; monitor DF state per VLAN via `show evpn ethernet-segment` or equivalent; capture on both DUT and neighbor PE which one forwards BUM per VLAN under configured carving vs default algorithm.
9fe9327f7ef0a57c.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
9fe9327f7ef0a57c.expectation:
Pass:    service-carving configuration succeeds and persists in running-config; DF election per VLAN differs between configured carving and default (VLAN-to-PE forwarding mappings change); `no service-carving` restores default carving service with original VLAN-to-PE distribution
Fail-on: CLI rejects service-carving or configuration not persistent; DF election and BUM forwarding behavior per VLAN identical under configured and default carving; `no service-carving` rejected or leaves non-default behavior

a05611ba062dce1a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure neighbor PE with EVPN instance EVI-100, ES 01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01, ET 0; set `advertise-mac disable` under `l2-services/evpn` to suppress Type 2 (MAC/IP) route with LT1, but allow Type 1 Ethernet A-D per-EVI route with LT2 label for the ES. Configure DUT with same EVI-100, `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled under BGP neighbor to peer PE, `import-rt` matching neighbor's export-rt. Attach host MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 to neighbor PE's ES; let DUT learn MAC via data-plane (BUM flooding) without BGP Type 2 advertisement. Verify neighbor PE advertises Type 1 (LT2) but not Type 2 (LT1) via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01 evi 100` on DUT.
Action: Send 1000 pps known unicast traffic from IXIA to DUT (dst MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01, VLAN matching EVI-100). DUT must forward using LT2 label per RFC7432bis §8.4 (remote PE has not advertised LT1 for this MAC). Capture MPLS packets on DUT→neighbor-PE core link.
Verify: Inspect captured MPLS label stack contains LT2 label; cross-check value against neighbor PE's Type 1 route MPLS Label field (RFC7432bis §7.1) via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` on DUT. Confirm `show evpn mac-table evi 100 mac AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01` on DUT shows remote MAC without LT1 label. Verify packets delivered to neighbor PE's ES interface (ESI 01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01, ET 0); check `show interface <ES-iface> statistics` for rx counter +1000.
a05611ba062dce1a.expectation:
Pass:    All 1000 packets forwarded with LT2 label in MPLS stack (RFC7432bis §8.4); LT2 value matches neighbor PE's Type 1 Ethernet A-D per-EVI route MPLS Label field; packets delivered to correct ES (ESI 01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01, ET 0); neighbor PE ES interface rx counters increment by 1000; DUT MAC table shows remote MAC entry without LT1 reference.
Fail-on: Packets dropped; forwarded without MPLS label or with LT1 label (neighbor PE has `advertise-mac disable`, so LT1 unavailable); LT2 value mismatch vs. Type 1 route; delivered to wrong ES or ET; Type 1 route missing LT2 label field; DUT core interface tx ≠ neighbor PE ES interface rx.

a06fd0ec808df3a9.action_steps:
Setup:  Create EVPN instance `evpn evpn-test service-type vlan-aware-bundle`; configure `interface agg-eth 1` with `ethernet-segment` using ESI type 01 (LACP-based, within RFC7432bis §7.1 mandatory range 00–05); enable `auto-discovery` and set `export-rt 65000:100`; establish BGP EVPN session to IXIA PE emulator; verify `show bgp evpn route-type 1` shows Ethernet A-D route with 10-octet ESI (type byte = 01).
Action: Capture Ethernet A-D route NLRI fields (RD + ESI + Ethernet Tag ID + MPLS Label) from `show bgp evpn route-type 1`; execute power-cycle via lab PDU harness; wait for boot completion.
Verify: Run `show evpn`, `show interface agg-eth 1`, `show bgp evpn summary`, `show bgp evpn route-type 1`; confirm ESI type byte still 01, A-D route re-advertised with identical ESI, BGP session state UP.
a06fd0ec808df3a9.equipment: DUT + IXIA (BGP EVPN peer) + power-cycle harness
a06fd0ec808df3a9.expectation:
Pass:    ESI type byte remains 01 (within RFC7432bis §7.1 valid range 00–05); Ethernet A-D route re-advertised with matching 10-octet ESI value; BGP EVPN session re-establishes; A-D route NLRI (RD, ESI, Tag, Label) matches pre-power-cycle capture.
Fail-on: ESI configuration lost or type byte >05; A-D route not re-advertised post-boot; BGP EVPN session down after 120s; A-D route ESI value differs from pre-power-cycle.

a0bde970dc15602a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with shared ES under `interface agg-eth-1/1/1 / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01 / load-balancing-mode single-active / service-carving highest-random-weight`; attach IXIA CE to multihomed LAG; create EVI 100 on both PEs; verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` on remote PE imports Ethernet A-D per ES with ESI Label extended community redundancy-mode=1.
Action: IXIA generates continuous bidirectional L2 traffic (1000 learned MACs, 50% BUM, 50% unicast at 10 Gbps aggregate) for ≥ 24 hours; inject MAC flap event every 30 min (CE LAG member down/up) to exercise DF election and route withdrawals; monitor throughout.
Verify: Every hour: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 | include redundancy-mode` confirms mode=1 stable; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` confirms single Primary DF per BD (P bit set in one PE only); `show l2-services fdb | count` stable; `show platform process memory` flat; `show interface agg-eth-1/1/1 statistics` on secondary PE shows Tx=0 for data frames; IXIA receiver confirms zero duplicate packets.
a0bde970dc15602a.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Label extended community redundancy-mode=1 present in all A-D per ES routes over 24 h; exactly one PE is Primary DF (P bit set) for EVI 100 BD; secondary PE Tx data counters = 0; memory usage within ±5% of baseline; zero duplicate frames at IXIA; MAC table stable at ~1000 entries; DF role stable across 48 flap events.
Fail-on: Both PEs show P bit set (split-brain); secondary PE data-plane Tx > 0; memory growth > 10%; duplicate packets detected at IXIA receiver; ESI Label mode field absent or changed; MAC table leak (growing unbounded); DF election oscillates (> 2 transitions per flap); counter rollback or freeze; process crash or BGP session flap.

a0da910878b8efc9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure service-type vlan-aware-bundle with VLANs 100, 200, 300 in EVI. CE multihomed to DUT and neighbor PE via agg-eth with matching ESI config (ethernet-segment). Verify initial DF using show evpn ethernet-segment. Start continuous traffic on VLANs 100, 200, 300 from IXIA.
Action: Shut down current DF's agg-eth interface to CE, triggering DF re-election. Wait 30s for convergence, then restore interface (no shut) for second failover.
Verify: Check show evpn df-election logs confirm VLAN tag 100 (lowest) referenced in DF calculation. Verify DF re-election completes and traffic resumes on all VLANs. Measure per-failover convergence time via IXIA frame timestamps.
a0da910878b8efc9.expectation:
Pass:    DF re-election uses VLAN 100 per RFC7432bis §8.5 modulo requirement; traffic convergence ≤1s per event (RFC7432bis §8 fast-convergence); zero duplicate frames.
Fail-on: DF logs show VLAN ≠100 used in modulo calculation, OR convergence >1s, OR duplicate/missing frames, OR DF election fails to complete.

a0e5be01def5c792.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with shared Ethernet Segment (ESI type 0, e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on agg-eth interfaces, create EVPN instance "vlan-aware-test" with service-type vlan-aware-bundle covering VLANs 10,20,30. Bring up both PE-CE links to trigger DF election. Verify DF elected for each VLAN using show evpn designated-forwarder.
Action: On the DF PE, execute tech-support collection command (e.g., "show tech-support" or diagnostic bundle generation). Repeat on the non-DF PE. Archive both outputs.
Verify: Extract and inspect both tech-support archives for: (1) show evpn ethernet-segment output with per-EVI DF/non-DF status, (2) show evpn designated-forwarder indicating which PE is DF and confirming VLAN tag 10 (lowest tag) used in modulo calculation for vlan-aware-bundle service, (3) running-config containing ethernet-segment config under agg-eth and evpn service-type vlan-aware-bundle, (4) syslog entries recording DF election events (ES up, Type-4 route Rx/Tx), (5) BUM replication FIB entries present on DF PE, suppressed on non-DF PE.
a0e5be01def5c792.expectation:
Pass:    Both tech-support archives contain: show evpn ethernet-segment with DF state per VLAN; show evpn designated-forwarder confirming lowest VLAN tag (10) used in modulo function for vlan-aware-bundle; running-config with ethernet-segment ESI and service-type vlan-aware-bundle; timestamped DF election syslog entries; BUM FIB on DF PE only.
Fail-on: Missing show evpn ethernet-segment, show evpn designated-forwarder, ethernet-segment config, service-type config, DF election logs, or BUM FIB tables; or incorrect modulo tag (not VLAN 10) shown for vlan-aware-bundle service; or BUM replication state identical on both PEs.

a188bab2bf11165d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVI 100, VNI 10000, bridge-domain BD-100, BGP AS 65000, EVPN peer 192.0.2.2 (neighbor PE), address-family l2vpn evpn activated. Verify BGP session UP. Configure neighbor PE with matching EVI/VNI.
Action: IXIA sends Ethernet frame (SMAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, VLAN 100) into DUT interface attached to BD-100. DUT learns MAC, generates Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) route per RFC7432bis §7.2, and advertises to neighbor PE.
Verify: DUT: show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 | include 00:11:22:33:44:55 (confirm route exists with VNI 10000 label). Neighbor PE: show bgp l2vpn evpn received-routes neighbor <DUT-IP> (confirm Type 2 NLRI with Route Distinguisher, 10-byte ESI, Ethernet Tag=0, MAC, VNI label).
a188bab2bf11165d.expectation:
Pass:    Type 2 NLRI present with RFC7432bis §7.2 mandatory fields: Route Type=2, Length field per encoding, RD (8 bytes), ESI (10 bytes), Ethernet Tag ID=0, MAC Address Length=48 bits, MAC=00:11:22:33:44:55, MPLS Label1=10000. Neighbor PE accepts and installs route.
Fail-on: No Type 2 route after 30 seconds, or NLRI missing/malformed required fields (RD/ESI/Ethernet Tag/MAC/Label), or neighbor PE rejects route with BGP Notification Error 3 Subcode 1 (Malformed NLRI), or route not in neighbor's EVPN table.

a1b3394788db42b8.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE EVPN topology with ingress-replication (FLOW-031 baseline). Configure `advertise-mac enable` on PE1, PE2, PE3. IXIA acts as malformed-update injector.
Action: IXIA establishes BGP session with PE1, advertises Route Type 3 (IMET) for VLAN 100, then injects three malformed Route Type 2 updates: (a) MAC/IP with truncated NLRI (length field = 20, actual = 15 octets), (b) Route Type 2 with reserved Flags bits 6-7 set to 0x3, (c) Route Type 2 with MPLS label stack depth = 25 (exceeds max). IXIA sends one well-formed Route Type 2 for MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff as control.
Verify: `show bgp neighbor statistics` reports 3 update errors. `show l2-services evpn mac-table vlan 100` contains only the well-formed MAC entry (aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff). `show log | include BGP` captures three distinct malformed-update syslog messages with error reasons. PE1 BGP session remains established. DUT does not crash or restart.
a1b3394788db42b8.expectation:
Pass:    Exactly 3 BGP update errors counted; only well-formed MAC installed in VLAN 100 table; 3 syslog entries present with structured reasons; BGP session stable; no crash.
Fail-on: Any malformed Route Type 2 installed in MAC table, BGP session dropped, missing syslog entry, crash, or silent discard (error counter = 0).

a1eb5948b019033b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-fwd-test service-type vlan-based` with two local CEs on access ports (vlan 100, 200); enable `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; establish multi-homed ES to neighbor PE with DUT as non-DF for ES1; populate MACT with known MAC aa:bb:cc:00:00:01 on local CE1, remote MAC dd:ee:ff:00:00:02 via LT1 label 5000.
Action: (1) Inject unicast frame from CE1 (MAC aa:bb:cc:00:00:01) destined to CE1's own MAC—expect hairpin drop. (2) Inject unicast from CE2 to CE1's MAC—expect local forward. (3) Inject frame from remote PE with source MAC dd:ee:ff:00:00:02 to local CE1—expect forward to CE1 only. (4) Inject BUM (broadcast dst FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) from remote PE toward ES1 where DUT is non-DF—expect drop. (5) Inject MPLS frame with LT1 label 5000 and known MAC dd:ee:ff:00:00:02—expect MACT lookup and forward. (6) Inject MPLS frame with LT1 label 5001 and unknown MAC—expect drop per "MACT entry does not exist."
Verify: `show l2-services evpn evpn-fwd-test mac-table` confirms aa:bb:cc:00:00:01 local, dd:ee:ff:00:00:02 remote; `show interface statistics` on CE1, CE2, and ES1 uplink for Tx/Rx/drop counters; IXIA capture confirms no CE1→CE1 hairpin, no BUM to non-DF ES1, LT1-5001 dropped.
a1eb5948b019033b.expectation:
Pass:    (1) Zero CE1→CE1 Tx packets (hairpin blocked). (2) CE2→CE1 unicast Rx=Tx=1. (3) Remote→local unicast forwarded only to local CEs (no remote re-flooding). (4) BUM to non-DF ES1: 0 Tx packets on ES1 uplink. (5) LT1-5000 with known MAC: 1 Rx, 1 Tx to correct local CE. (6) LT1-5001 with unknown MAC: 1 Rx, 0 Tx (dropped per MACT miss).
Fail-on: CE1 Tx counter >0 for hairpin; BUM forwarded to non-DF ES (ES1 Tx >0); remote-to-local unicast leaked back to remote; LT1-5001 forwarded despite missing MACT entry; unknown-mac-flooding=enable yet BUM dropped when DF.

a1ed1fc5ba20ac20.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure same ESI (e.g., identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88) on DUT agg-eth-1/ethernet-segment and on neighbor PE agg-eth-1/ethernet-segment. Set load-balancing-mode single-active and service-carving default on both. Establish EVPN BGP peering between DUT and neighbor PE. Configure IXIA as dual-homed CE connected to both PEs.
Action: Bring up both agg-eth-1 interfaces; wait es-waiting-time (default 3 sec). Capture BGP EVPN advertisements from both DUT and neighbor PE using show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 (ES routes). Examine DF election extended community in Type-4 routes.
Verify: Decode ES route (Type-4) DF Election Extended Community (RFC7432bis §8.6); confirm primary DF PE is signaled with DF bit set, backup DF PE is signaled with backup DF indication. Check show evpn ethernet-segment detail for DF role and backup DF.
a1ed1fc5ba20ac20.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA (IXIA as dual-homed CE)
a1ed1fc5ba20ac20.expectation:
Pass:    BGP Type-4 ES routes from both PEs carry DF Election Extended Community; one PE advertises itself as primary DF (DF bit=1), the other as backup DF. DUT CLI show evpn ethernet-segment displays primary DF IP and backup DF IP. Only primary DF forwards BUM traffic (in single-active mode).
Fail-on: Type-4 routes missing DF Election Extended Community, or only primary DF signaled (no backup DF indication), or both PEs claim primary DF role, or DF election timeout exceeds es-waiting-time.

a22b71d7a7166f9d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn EVI-100 service-type vlan-based` with MTU 9000, enable Control Word and Flow Label; configure multihomed ES with P/B flags; establish BGP peering with neighbor PE using `af-l2vpn evpn` and `auto-discovery` with route-targets.
Action: Trigger advertisement of both Inclusive Multicast route (type 3) and Ethernet A-D per EVI route (type 1); perform BGP route refresh via `clear bgp l2vpn evpn soft` to re-advertise; capture BGP UPDATEs with tcpdump or IXIA.
Verify: Parse captured L2-Attr Extended Community (Type 0x06, Sub-type 0x04) from both route types: for Inclusive Multicast route, confirm MTU=9000, C-flag and F-flag set, P and B flags are zero (MBZ bits); for Ethernet A-D per EVI route, confirm P and B flags match ES config, MTU field is zero (MBZ octets); use `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` to verify advertised attributes.
a22b71d7a7166f9d.expectation:
Pass:    Inclusive Multicast route carries L2-Attr ext-community with MTU=9000, C-flag=1, F-flag=1, P=0, B=0; Ethernet A-D per EVI route carries L2-Attr ext-community with P/B per ESI config, MTU=0, C-flag=0, F-flag=0; both routes accepted by neighbor PE; no BGP errors.
Fail-on: L2-Attr partitioning violated (P or B non-zero in type 3 route, OR MTU/C/F non-zero in type 1 route); routes rejected by peer; BGP session reset; L2-Attr ext-community missing from either route type.

a238a830140cb2db.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 (DUT) and PE2 (neighbor PE) in EVPN instance EVI-100 with BGP peering (af-l2vpn evpn enabled). On PE1, configure static MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 on AC1 with sticky flag (advertised with MAC Mobility ext-comm static=1, seq=0). IXIA port-1 → PE1 AC1, IXIA port-2 → PE2 AC2.
Action: Verify PE2 receives BGP EVPN route type-2 for AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 with MAC Mobility ext-comm (static=1, seq=0) via `show bgp evpn route type 2 mac AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01`. Then IXIA port-2 sends Ethernet frames with SA=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 to trigger local learning on PE2's AC2, creating a conflict with the remote sticky MAC.
Verify: `show logging | include sticky` or `show alarms` on PE2 for operator alert containing MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 and conflict source (local AC2 vs remote PE1). Check `show l2-services mac-table evi 100 mac AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01` for sticky-conflict flag. Capture PE2 drops via `show l2-services statistics | include sticky-violation` (MAY behavior).
a238a830140cb2db.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 generates operator alert (syslog severity ≥ warning, SNMP trap, or alarm) identifying MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01, local AC2, and remote sticky source (PE1). Alert appears within 5 seconds of IXIA frame injection. MAC table shows conflict state (sticky flag + local flag both set, or explicit sticky-violation marker).
Fail-on: No operator alert after 60 seconds despite local learning conflict. PE2 silently overwrites sticky MAC entry with local learning (MAC Mobility seq > 0 in re-advertisement). BGP session to PE1 flaps or PE2 data-plane drops all traffic in EVI-100.

a26390fe8c33156a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (PE1, PE2) with same ESI (01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:00:01) on `agg-eth` interfaces using `configuration/interface/agg-eth/ethernet-segment` mode. Configure EVPN instance with `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`. Verify DF election completes and BGP advertises Type 1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) and Type 4 (Ethernet Segment) routes. Confirm initial state: `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows DF role stable, `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows ESI routes present, baseline `show platform process memory` for EVPN/BGP processes.
Action: IXIA sends bidirectional L2 traffic (unicast + BUM 50/50 mix, 1 Gbps aggregate) to CE through multihomed Ethernet segment for ≥ 24 hours. Traffic exercises DF forwarding for BUM and aliasing for unicast.
Verify: Hourly: `show platform process memory` (track EVPN/L2/BGP process RSS), `show evpn ethernet-segment` (DF role, election count), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` and `route-type 4` (ESI route count). End-of-run: `show interface agg-eth counters` (verify monotonic, zero errors/drops), syslog review (zero DF election flaps or ESI route churn).
a26390fe8c33156a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (both configured as multihomed PEs for same ES) + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
a26390fe8c33156a.expectation:
Pass:    Memory usage ±5% of baseline across all 24 h samples. DF election count in `show evpn ethernet-segment` equals initial count (zero flaps). Type 1 and Type 4 ESI route count stable (±0 routes). Interface counters monotonic with zero errors, zero drops on active DF path. Zero syslog DF or ESI route events.
Fail-on: Memory growth >10% in any EVPN/BGP process. Any DF election flap (election count increments). ESI route withdrawal/re-advertisement detected in BGP table or syslog. Non-zero drops or errors on active forwarding path. Counter rollback or freeze.

a2786c8c3dbf0f95.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with `ethernet-segment` on interface agg-eth 0; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbor; configure neighbor PE on same ES. Verify DUT advertises Ethernet A-D per ES routes (type 1) and MAC/IP Advertisement routes (type 2) to remote BGP speaker.
Action: Shut down interface agg-eth 0 on DUT to simulate PE-to-CE link failure.
Verify: At remote BGP speaker, capture UPDATE messages (tcpdump port 179); confirm withdrawal of all Ethernet A-D per ES routes previously advertised by DUT for the affected ES; verify MAC/IP Advertisement route withdrawals with delay in range [0, MAC ageout timer]; verify neighbor PE no longer uses DUT as next hop for affected routes.
a2786c8c3dbf0f95.expectation:
Pass:    DUT withdraws all Ethernet A-D per ES routes for the failed ES; MAC/IP route withdrawals delayed randomly between 0 and MAC ageout timer per RFC7432bis ch.17.3; neighbor PE removes DUT from next-hop set.
Fail-on: Ethernet A-D per ES routes not withdrawn within BGP hold time, or partial withdrawal only, or MAC/IP routes not withdrawn, or neighbor PE continues forwarding to DUT after convergence.

a2920f24bc5d816b.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs in the same EVPN instance, each with IRB interface for subnet 10.1.1.0/24; PE1 configured with default gateway MAC 00:00:5E:00:01:01 (advertises MAC route without Default Gateway extended community), PE2 configured with same MAC 00:00:5E:00:01:01 on its IRB (advertises with Default Gateway extended community). IXIA captures BGP UPDATEs and timestamps.
Action: Bring up both IRB interfaces; establish EVPN BGP peering; PE1 receives PE2's MAC route with Default Gateway ext-comm; measure time from receipt to PE1's WITHDRAW; scale test with N={1, 10, 50} conflicting IRB MACs.
Verify: Capture PE1 BGP WITHDRAW for conflicting MAC route(s); check PE1 syslog/alarm for Default Gateway MAC conflict; confirm no MAC Mobility extended community on any route carrying Default Gateway ext-comm (both send and receive sides); measure per-conflict detection-to-withdrawal latency.
a2920f24bc5d816b.expectation:
Pass:    For each conflict: PE1 withdraws its MAC route within 100 ms of receiving PE2's route with Default Gateway ext-comm; alarm raised on PE1 (syslog severity ≥ warning); zero routes observed with both Default Gateway + MAC Mobility ext-comms; all N conflicts resolved.
Fail-on: Any MAC route with Default Gateway ext-comm persists on PE1 after 100 ms, missing alarm on PE1, or any route carrying both Default Gateway and MAC Mobility extended communities.

a2d2595ec0b63f0c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance "test-bundle" with `evpn test-bundle service-type vlan-bundle` in l2-services mode; bind VLANs 10,20,30 to x-eth 0/0/1; configure BGP neighbor with EVPN address-family, `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100`; connect IXIA to x-eth 0/0/1.
Action: IXIA sends 1000 frames each with VLAN tags 10, 20, 30 (in bundle), 40, 50 (not in bundle) to DUT; observe BGP EVPN route advertisement to neighbor PE.
Verify: Execute `show l2-services evpn test-bundle mac-table`, `show bgp evpn summary`, `show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics`; check neighbor PE receives EVPN Type-2 MAC/IP routes only for VLANs 10/20/30; confirm ingress discard counter on x-eth 0/0/1 increments by 2000 (VLAN 40 + 50 frames).
a2d2595ec0b63f0c.expectation:
Pass:    VLANs 10/20/30 frames forwarded, MAC addresses learned in `show l2-services evpn test-bundle mac-table`, BGP Type-2 routes for VLANs 10/20/30 advertised to neighbor PE, VLANs 40/50 frames dropped with x-eth 0/0/1 ingress discard counter = 2000, no MAC learning or BGP routes for VLANs 40/50.
Fail-on: VLANs 40 or 50 frames forwarded, or MAC addresses from VLANs 40/50 appear in mac-table, or BGP EVPN routes generated for VLANs 40/50, or ingress discard counter does not increment, or BGP session flaps during traffic.

a3a7ea1e1afd54a8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT + neighbor PE) in multi-homing topology with three Ethernet segments: ESI type 1 (LACP-based, e.g., 01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01), ESI type 0 (arbitrary, 00:11:22:...), and ESI type 5 (AS-based). Enable `advertise-mac enable` under `configuration/l2-services/evpn`. Configure BGP EVPN peering with `af-l2vpn evpn` between PEs. Attempt to configure an invalid ESI with first byte 0x06 or 0xFF.
Action: Generate MAC learning traffic from IXIA CE through each Ethernet segment to trigger MAC/IP Advertisement route (RT-2) origination. Capture BGP EVPN UPDATE messages on the peer PE. Issue `show evpn mac` and `show bgp evpn route-type 2` on both PEs.
Verify: Decode captured RT-2 NLRI, extract the 10-octet ESI field from each MAC/IP Advertisement route. Confirm the first octet (ESI type) is in range 0x00–0x05 for all advertised routes. Verify DUT rejects or refuses to advertise routes for invalid ESI types (≥6). Cross-check `show evpn mac` output displays correct ESI type and value for each learned MAC.
a3a7ea1e1afd54a8.expectation:
Pass:    All MAC/IP Advertisement routes (RT-2) carry ESI with first byte 0x00–0x05; each configured ESI type (0, 1, 5) appears correctly in NLRI; DUT rejects invalid ESI types at config or omits them from BGP updates; `show evpn mac` displays ESI matching configured type and value for each segment.
Fail-on: Any RT-2 BGP UPDATE observed with ESI first octet > 0x05; valid ESI types (0–5) not encoded correctly in NLRI; DUT accepts and advertises ESI type ≥ 6; MAC entries show mismatched or corrupted ESI; multi-homing functionality breaks when MAC advertisement is enabled.

a3d521bd8920e0ae.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PE routers (PE1=DUT, PE2) with MPLS core and BGP EVPN address-family established. PE1 has LAG agg-lag 1 and Ethernet x-eth 0/0/1 connected to IXIA traffic generator.
Action: On PE1: `evpn evi-lag1 service-type port-based`, then `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100`, attach agg-lag 1 as service interface, `commit`. Configure second instance `evpn evi-eth1 service-type port-based` with same route targets, attach x-eth 0/0/1, `commit`. On PE2: mirror the configuration. IXIA sends 100 Mbps untagged L2 traffic through both ACs.
Verify: `show evpn evi` displays both evi-lag1 and evi-eth1 in Up state with service-type port-based. `show running-config l2-services evpn` confirms configuration. IXIA receives bidirectional traffic on both interfaces. `show evpn mac address-table` shows learned MACs on both EVIs.
a3d521bd8920e0ae.expectation:
Pass:    Both port-based EVPN instances (on agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1) reach Up state and forward untagged L2 traffic bidirectionally at line rate; no VLAN filtering at access ports.
Fail-on: Configuration rejected for LAG or Ethernet port types; EVPN instance stuck in Down state; packet loss >0.1%; any VLAN-based filtering applied to port-based service interfaces.

a3e1807b8bf50423.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based` under l2-services; add interface x-eth 0/0/1 as attachment circuit to evpn-1 instance.
Action: Execute the no form of the interface command to remove x-eth 0/0/1 from evpn-1; commit configuration changes.
Verify: `show running-config` no longer lists x-eth 0/0/1 under evpn-1 configuration; operational show commands confirm interface is detached from L2 service.
a3e1807b8bf50423.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
a3e1807b8bf50423.expectation:
Pass:    Interface x-eth 0/0/1 cleanly removed from evpn-1 service; no configuration remnants in running-config; interface available for other service attachments.
Fail-on: Interface configuration persists in running-config under evpn-1, or interface shows operational inconsistencies (e.g., still reports service binding in show output).

a3e24103617d64b9.action_steps:
Setup:  Create a route-policy on DUT that denies MAC/IP routes with RT 65000:100 and permits RT 65000:200. Establish BGP EVPN peering between DUT and neighbor PE with unicast underlay reachability. Configure EVPN instance with vlan-based service on both routers. Navigate to `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn` on DUT.
Action: Apply the route-policy inbound under the BGP EVPN neighbor address-family. From neighbor PE, advertise 10 MAC/IP (Type 2) routes with RT 65000:100 and 10 with RT 65000:200. Check MAC table on DUT to verify only RT 65000:200 routes are installed (inbound policy filtered the others before installation). Remove inbound policy; apply the same policy outbound. Learn 10 MACs locally on DUT with RT 65000:100 and 10 with RT 65000:200. On neighbor PE, verify only RT 65000:200 routes are received (outbound policy filtered the others before NLRI advertisement).
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on DUT displays all received routes in BGP RIB. Check DUT's MAC table (via appropriate show command for l2-services) for installed entries; count should reflect policy filtering. `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on neighbor PE displays routes received from DUT. `show running-config` confirms policy is attached to correct neighbor and direction.
a3e24103617d64b9.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
a3e24103617d64b9.expectation:
Pass:    With inbound policy: DUT receives 20 routes in BGP RIB but installs only 10 (RT 65000:200) in MAC-VRF; 10 denied routes (RT 65000:100) are absent from MAC table, confirming policy evaluated before installation. With outbound policy: neighbor PE receives only 10 routes (RT 65000:200) from DUT; 10 denied routes (RT 65000:100) are not advertised, confirming policy evaluated before NLRI advertisement. Switching policy direction cleanly replaces prior configuration; only one direction is active at a time.
Fail-on: Routes denied by inbound policy appear in MAC table (policy not evaluated or evaluated too late). Routes denied by outbound policy are advertised to neighbor (policy not evaluated or evaluated too late). Both inbound and outbound policy are active simultaneously in running-config. Policy has no effect (all 20 routes pass regardless of RT match).

a4040990710715de.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 advertises sticky MACs (MAC Mobility ext-community static flag=1, seq=0) via BGP EVPN; PE2 peers with PE1 (`af-l2vpn evpn` enabled); IXIA scale rig configured to inject 1000 legitimate flows + 100 sticky MAC violation attempts/sec (same MAC learned locally or from simulated 3rd ES).
Action: IXIA transmits at line rate for 5 minutes; measure sticky MAC violation detection latency, operator alert generation time, frame drop rate for violating MACs, throughput/latency impact on non-sticky traffic.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-table` confirms sticky MACs (static flag=1, seq=0); `show logging` or syslog collector captures operator alerts for each violation; IXIA statistics report per-flow drop counts and latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99).
a4040990710715de.expectation:
Pass:    100% of sticky MAC violations detected; operator alert generated within 500 ms of first violation per MAC; violating frames dropped with <2% throughput impact and <5 ms latency increase on legitimate traffic.
Fail-on: Any sticky MAC violation undetected, operator alert delayed >500 ms, legitimate traffic throughput degraded >2%, or latency increase >5 ms.

a47233afc084d567.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure same ESI (type 0) on DUT and neighbor PE agg-eth interfaces (`ethernet-segment` / `identifier 0 00.00.00.00.00.00.00.00.01`), set `load-balancing-mode single-active` and `service-carving highest-random-weight` on both PEs. Configure EVPN instance, attach L2 service to agg-eth. Verify `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows DF elected, check ES route (type 4) advertises DF Preference extended community (RFC7432bis §8.6).
Action: Identify primary DF with `show evpn ethernet-segment detail`. Power-cycle primary DF PE via PDU harness while IXIA multi-homed CE sends continuous bidirectional traffic.
Verify: Backup DF takes over within es-waiting-time (3s default). After primary DF reboots, verify ES route re-advertised with DF election extended community; `show bgp evpn route-type 4` shows DF Preference and DF Capability bits on both PEs. Check `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms DF re-election completed.
a47233afc084d567.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (both multi-homing same ES) + IXIA traffic gen (simulates multi-homed CE) + power-cycle harness (PDU for primary DF)
a47233afc084d567.expectation:
Pass:    Backup DF takes over within 3s (es-waiting-time); primary DF recovers after reboot, re-participates in DF election, ES routes on both PEs carry DF Preference extended community per RFC7432bis §8.6; IXIA sees zero duplicate frames (single-active enforced).
Fail-on: DF election does not reconverge after power-cycle; ES route missing DF extended community; both PEs forward simultaneously (duplicate frames detected); traffic blackhole > es-waiting-time; lost ES configuration post-reboot.

a487d6465f6c455b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as ingress PE; PE2+PE3 in All-Active on shared ES (identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88, load-balancing-mode all-active), PE2 as DF; PE4 on different ES; all PEs use ingress replication. Verify PE2/PE3 advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes with ESI label.
Action: Send broadcast frame from CE attached to DUT; capture MPLS-encapsulated packets from DUT→PE2 (DF), DUT→PE3 (non-DF same ES), DUT→PE4 (different ES). Parse MPLS label stack on each copy.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms DF/non-DF election; packet captures confirm DUT includes ESI label (matching PE2/PE3 A-D per ES advertisements) in copies to PE2+PE3 but NOT in copy to PE4; `show mpls forwarding esi-label <label>` on PE2/PE3 shows action drops or pops without forwarding to shared ES interface.
a487d6465f6c455b.equipment: DUT + 3 neighbor PEs (PE2+PE3 dual-homed to same ESI type-0, PE4 on separate ES) + IXIA traffic gen + packet capture on PE↔PE links
a487d6465f6c455b.expectation:
Pass:    DUT includes correct ESI label in BUM copies to PE2+PE3 (label value matches A-D per ES route NLRI); NO ESI label in copy to PE4; PE2/PE3 forwarding entry for ESI label does NOT forward to shared ES (split-horizon enforced).
Fail-on: ESI label present in packet to PE4, OR ESI label missing/wrong in packets to PE2/PE3, OR PE2/PE3 `show mpls forwarding` reveals ESI label action forwards to shared ES interface.

a4a84fec164f9ffb.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco PE both connect to IXIA emulating dual-homed CE with LACP (System MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff). Configure BGP EVPN session between DUT and Cisco; on DUT: `interface agg-eth 1`, `ethernet-segment`, ESI type-1 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:00:00; matching ESI on Cisco LAG. Create EVPN instance with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100` on both.
Action: Enable auto-discovery on both PEs; verify AD per-ES route (Type 1) exchange in BGP. Trigger DF election by associating EVPN service with the ethernet-segment. Send bidirectional traffic from IXIA CE through both PEs to remote destination.
Verify: `show bgp evpn summary`, `show bgp evpn route-type 1` (confirm ESI 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:00:00 matches on both sides); `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` (DF election status per VLAN); packet capture on both PE uplinks shows only DF forwards ingress traffic (non-DF drops due to split-horizon).
a4a84fec164f9ffb.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise AD per-ES route with ESI Value high-order 6 octets = aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff; DF election converges (one PE = DF, other = non-DF for same service-VLAN); traffic ingress via CE flows only through DF PE (single active forwarding).
Fail-on: ESI encoding mismatch in received BGP update (Cisco rejects route or uses different ESI format); both PEs claim DF or neither claims DF after 10 s; traffic duplicated or black-holed (both forward or neither forwards).

a4ff667e3d30e0a8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `agg-eth 0` on DUT with member interfaces. Connect to IXIA port configured for LACP LAG formation. Note the default LAG MAC address from `show lacp interface agg-eth 0`.
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth` mode. Configure `lacp-system-mac` with a custom MAC address (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55). Commit and bring up the LACP LAG with IXIA. Capture LACP PDUs to observe the system MAC field. Issue `no lacp-system-mac` to restore default, commit, and re-capture LACP PDUs.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` reflects the custom MAC when configured. Captured LACP PDUs show the custom system MAC in the Actor System field. After `no lacp-system-mac`, `show lacp interface agg-eth 0` and captured PDUs confirm the system MAC reverted to the original LAG MAC address.
a4ff667e3d30e0a8.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (IXIA configured for LACP LAG formation and PDU capture)
a4ff667e3d30e0a8.expectation:
Pass:    `lacp-system-mac` applies the custom MAC to outgoing LACP PDUs (visible in Actor System field), and `no lacp-system-mac` restores the default LAG MAC address in subsequent PDUs.
Fail-on: Custom MAC not reflected in LACP PDU Actor System field, or `no` form fails to revert to the LAG MAC (either retains custom MAC or shows a different address).

a55c6e9a176ef4b1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN Ethernet Segment on DUT agg-eth interface with `service-carving highest-preference` (omit explicit preference parameter to trigger RFC9785 §3 default = 32767). Establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; verify ES route (type 4) advertises DF Election Extended Community with preference = 32767 via `show bgp evpn route-type 4 detail`.
Action: Start IXIA bidirectional traffic (100 Mbps). Kill BGP process on DUT (platform debug command, e.g., `debug process kill bgp`). Monitor BGP session re-establishment and ES route re-advertisement.
Verify: Execute `show bgp evpn summary` (session state), `show ethernet-segment` (ES status), `show bgp evpn route-type 4 detail` (DF Election Extended Community octets 6–7 = preference field). Capture re-advertised ES route; parse preference value. Measure traffic interruption via IXIA packet loss counter.
a55c6e9a176ef4b1.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN peer) + IXIA traffic gen
a55c6e9a176ef4b1.expectation:
Pass:    BGP process restarts; ES route re-advertised with DF Election Extended Community preference = 32767 (default per RFC9785 §3 MUST); traffic interruption ≤ 3 seconds (es-waiting-time default).
Fail-on: ES route missing or advertises preference ≠ 32767 post-restart, or BGP session does not re-establish, or traffic interruption > 3 seconds.

a5b9329b1949ac2d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 1 interface; configure lacp-key to a non-default value (e.g., 100, different from interface number 1); commit and verify the configured key is active.
Action: Issue `no lacp-key` under agg-eth 1 interface configuration mode; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` shows no explicit lacp-key statement for agg-eth 1; LACP operational state (`show lacp`) shows administrative key equals 1 (the interface number, per documented default).
a5b9329b1949ac2d.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
a5b9329b1949ac2d.expectation:
Pass:    LACP administrative key reverts to 1 (interface number) after `no lacp-key`; running-config contains no lacp-key statement.
Fail-on: LACP key remains at non-default value (100), becomes 0, is undefined, or lacp-key statement persists in running-config.

a5c04fab56e56277.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with multi-homing (ESI on interface), enable control-word and unknown-mac-flooding, establish iBGP peering with neighbor PE using af-l2vpn evpn address family, configure RD and import-rt/export-rt for the broadcast domain.
Action: Trigger BUM traffic from IXIA into the EVPN instance; on neighbor PE, capture BGP UPDATE messages and extract Type 3 (Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag) routes advertised by DUT; decode PMSI Tunnel Attribute and L2-Attr Extended Community.
Verify: Confirm Type 3 route contains RD (key), ET ID (key), Originating Router IP (key), PMSI Tunnel Attribute with Flags=0 and Type=6 (Ingress Replication) and Tunnel Identifier=DUT PE IP, L2-Attr Extended Community with L2 MTU + control-word flag (matches DUT config) + flow-label flag, Route Targets matching export-rt, and MP_REACH_NLRI Next-Hop=DUT PE IP; verify one Type 3 per broadcast domain.
a5c04fab56e56277.expectation:
Pass:    All mandatory Type 3 route fields present and correct: PMSI Type=6 with Tunnel Identifier=DUT IP, L2-Attr extended community present with control-word flag matching DUT control-word config (enabled), each broadcast domain advertises exactly one Type 3 route, Next-Hop=DUT PE IP.
Fail-on: PMSI Tunnel Attribute missing or Type≠6; L2-Attr extended community missing, L2 MTU=0, or control-word/flow-label flags incorrect; Type 3 route not advertised when EVPN instance+multi-homing both operationally up; Type 3 withdrawn when only multi-homing link flaps but broadcast domain still active on other interfaces.

a5d94eca3697bcd5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 (DUT) with EVPN instance, IRB interface for subnet 192.168.1.0/24, default gateway MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01, advertise-mac enable; configure PE2 (neighbor PE) with EVPN instance, BGP peer to PE1 with af-l2vpn evpn enabled, matching import-rt.
Action: IXIA connected to PE2 sends frames with source MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01 (conflicts with PE1's IRB default gateway MAC); PE2 learns MAC locally and advertises BGP MAC route without Default Gateway extended community; PE2 receives PE1's MAC route with Default Gateway extended community for same MAC 00:00:5e:00:01:01.
Verify: On PE2: `show bgp evpn routes mac 00:00:5e:00:01:01` confirms PE2 has withdrawn its own MAC route; `show logging` or alarm output shows raised alarm for default gateway MAC conflict; on PE1: `show bgp evpn routes mac 00:00:5e:00:01:01` confirms route with Default Gateway ext-comm is advertised and active; on both PEs: `show bgp evpn routes detail` verifies no MAC Mobility extended community is attached to any route carrying Default Gateway extended community.
a5d94eca3697bcd5.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 withdraws its MAC route for 00:00:5e:00:01:01 upon receiving PE1's route with Default Gateway ext-comm (RFC7432bis §10.1.1 conflict resolution); alarm raised on PE2 indicating default gateway MAC conflict; no MAC Mobility ext-comm on any Default Gateway route (sending or receiving); only PE1's Default Gateway route remains in BGP EVPN table for 00:00:5e:00:01:01.
Fail-on: PE2 does not withdraw its MAC route; no alarm raised on PE2; MAC Mobility ext-comm appears on route with Default Gateway ext-comm; both MAC routes coexist causing MAC flapping or traffic to 192.168.1.1 black-holed.

a63c38b00d4977be.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco IOS-XR PE both connected to same CE (Ethernet Segment); configure iBGP session; on DUT: `evpn evpn-multi-homing service-type vlan-based`, `auto-discovery`, `export-rt 65000:100`, on interface: configure ESI Type 1 (01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99); on Cisco: configure matching ESI and EVPN instance; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbor.
Action: Bring up Ethernet Segment on both PEs; trigger Ethernet Segment route (Type 4) advertisement; exchange routes via BGP; test ESI Type values 0 (all-zero), 1 (LACP-based), 3 (MAC-based), 4 (Router-ID), 5 (AS-based); inject invalid Type 6 from test script to validate rejection.
Verify: On DUT: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4`, `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` confirm received routes with ESI Type byte 0 ∈ [0,5]; packet capture of BGP UPDATE shows Route Type 4 NLRI with 10-octet ESI field correctly encoded; confirm Type 6 route is rejected with BGP notification or silent drop.
a63c38b00d4977be.expectation:
Pass:    DUT and Cisco PE both accept Ethernet Segment routes with ESI Type 0–5; routes installed in `show bgp l2vpn evpn` table; Ethernet Segment operationally up (`show evpn ethernet-segment` shows DF election result); ESI Type 6 (invalid per RFC7432bis §7.4) is rejected or filtered.
Fail-on: Valid ESI Type 0–5 route rejected by either PE; invalid Type ≥6 route accepted and installed; ESI field truncated/corrupted in transit; DF election fails due to route encoding mismatch.

a64af137343b5927.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 interface; verify no explicit lacp-key configuration present via show running-config; default LACP administrative key equals interface number (0).
Action: Enter configuration mode for agg-eth 0, configure lacp-key with value 100, commit. Then issue no lacp-key, commit again.
Verify: show running-config after first commit displays lacp-key 100 under agg-eth 0; after second commit (no lacp-key), explicit config is removed and key reverts to default.
a64af137343b5927.expectation:
Pass:    lacp-key accepts numeric value (100), persists in running-config; no lacp-key removes explicit configuration and key reverts to interface number (0 for agg-eth 0)
Fail-on: Command syntax error on commit, configured key value absent from running-config, or no lacp-key fails to restore interface-number default

a6c243df74039be8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs multi-homing a CE; on both PEs apply `ethernet-segment` under `agg-eth 0`; establish `af-l2vpn evpn` BGP peering between PEs.
Action: Capture BGP UPDATEs on the PE-PE link containing Type 4 (Ethernet Segment) and Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes; decode each Type 4 NLRI per RFC7432bis-§7.4 and extract (a) the 10-octet ESI field, (b) the ES-Import RT extended community per RFC7432bis-§7.6; compare bytes 0–5 of the ESI against the 6-octet value of the ES-Import RT; repeat for Type 1 routes.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` display the routes; packet-capture decode confirms ES-Import RT value equals ESI[0:5] byte-for-byte in both Type 4 and Type 1 routes; cross-check against both PEs' originated routes per RFC7432bis-§8.1.1.
a6c243df74039be8.expectation:
Pass:    ES-Import RT extended community in every Type 4 and Type 1 route equals the high-order 6 octets of the corresponding ESI value per RFC7432bis-§7.6 and RFC7432bis-§8.1.1.
Fail-on: ES-Import RT value does not match ESI[0:5], missing ES-Import RT extended community, or routes not originated when ethernet-segment is configured.

a6d8c350545ad3e7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance 'evpn-esi-test' (vlan-based), enable BGP EVPN address-family to IXIA BGP emulator peer 192.0.2.100. Configure IXIA BGP session with route-reflector capability.
Action: IXIA advertises nine Ethernet Segment routes (type 4 NLRI): six with valid ESI type bytes 0x00, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05 (RD 1.1.1.1:100-105, originating router IP 192.0.2.10), then three with invalid ESI type bytes 0x06, 0x07, 0xFF (RD 1.1.1.1:106-108, same originating IP).
Verify: Run 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4' and 'show evpn ethernet-segment' to list installed ES routes; run 'show logging | include EVPN|BGP|malformed' to capture rejection messages for invalid type bytes.
a6d8c350545ad3e7.expectation:
Pass:    Exactly six ES routes (ESI type 0x00–0x05) present in 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4' output with best-path installed; three routes (type 0x06, 0x07, 0xFF) absent from RIB and syslog shows NOTIFICATION or warning 'ESI type out of range [0-5]'.
Fail-on: Any ES route with ESI type byte ≥0x06 installed in EVPN RIB, or any route with type byte 0x00–0x05 rejected/missing from RIB.

a737207b58a0a80a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP neighbor under `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family (per CLI evidence); establish peering; confirm baseline session Established without optional capabilities.
Action: Enter `af-l2vpn evpn` neighbor sub-mode; configure `capability orf-prefix-list send` and `capability route-refresh`; commit; issue `clear bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor <neighbor-ip>` to trigger capability renegotiation.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor <neighbor-ip>` displays both ORF Prefix-List (RFC 5291) and Route Refresh (RFC 2918) under "Negotiated capabilities"; session returns to Established state; no session flaps or capability-mismatch errors in logs.
a737207b58a0a80a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP peering required to negotiate capabilities)
a737207b58a0a80a.expectation:
Pass:    Negotiated capabilities section lists "ORF Prefix-List (send)" and "Route Refresh"; BGP session stable; DUT can send/receive ORF updates and route-refresh requests for L2VPN EVPN AF.
Fail-on: Capabilities missing from negotiated list; session stuck in Active/Connect; neighbor rejects capability; or `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows capability mismatch errors.

a7bce33c544411e9.action_steps:
Problem: validate that aliasing and best-path selection remain functional when the designated forwarder PE's control plane recovers from a process restart, ensuring remote PEs correctly switch to the backup PE and back.
Method: configure multi-homed CE on PE1 and PE2 with single-active ESI, establish traffic from remote PE3 via PE1 (designated forwarder), restart PE1's EVPN/BGP process, and verify traffic switches to PE2 during restart and returns after recovery, measuring failover duration.
Setup: CE multi-homed to PE1 (agg-eth 0) and PE2 (agg-eth 0); configure `ethernet-segment` with `identifier 0 type0-value` (same ESI on both) and `load-balancing-mode single-active`. Remote PE3 with EVPN instance routing to CE. IXIA generating continuous L2 traffic from PE3-side toward CE's MAC.
Action: Verify PE1 is designated forwarder via `show evpn ethernet-segment` and PE3's `show bgp l2vpn evpn` selects PE1's Ethernet A-D per EVI route as best path. Identify PE1's EVPN/BGP process via `show platform process | include evpn`. Restart that process using the documented platform reset command. Monitor IXIA loss histogram during restart and recovery.
Verify: During restart, PE3's `show bgp l2vpn evpn` updates best path to PE2's Ethernet A-D per EVI route within ≤ `es-waiting-time` (default 3 s); `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE2 shows PE2 as designated forwarder; IXIA traffic switches to PE2's link. After restart, PE1's process recovers within ≤ 30 s; `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows stable DF state (PE1 or PE2 based on election); PE3's best path matches the active DF; IXIA histogram records ≤ 3 s total outage.
a7bce33c544411e9.expectation:
Pass: Process restarts within ≤ 30 s; traffic switches to backup PE within ≤ 3 s (es-waiting-time); PE3's best-path selection correctly tracks the active designated forwarder during and after restart; IXIA measures ≤ 3 s total data-path outage; ES state recovers on both PEs.
Fail-on: Process restart > 30 s, aliasing fails to trigger, PE2 does not become DF during restart, PE3's best-path selection does not update, data-path outage > 3 s, or ES state fails to recover post-restart.

a7d5548c96b731d0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with Ethernet Segment (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99), enable DF election algorithm 2 (Highest-Preference) with non-default preference value 15000 (range 0-65535, default 32767); verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` shows DF Election Extended Community with Alg=2 Pref=15000; stage upgrade image.
Action: Run `onie-install <next-image-url>` to upgrade; DUT reboots to new image.
Verify: Parse running-config for Ethernet Segment ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 with DF preference 15000; run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` and decode DF Election Extended Community (RFC8584 §2.2) to confirm Alg=2 and Pref=15000 still encoded; run `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` to confirm DF election state matches pre-upgrade snapshot.
a7d5548c96b731d0.expectation:
Pass:    Running-config preserves ESI and DF preference 15000; BGP ES Route Type 4 re-advertised with DF Election Extended Community Alg=2 Pref=15000 (hex 0x3A98 in bitmap); show commands match pre-upgrade baseline.
Fail-on: DF preference resets to default 32767 or out-of-range value; ES Route Type 4 not re-advertised; DF Election Extended Community missing or malformed; config playback error mentioning df-election or preference.

a7d7b8411dc591ed.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 MAC-VRFs (evpn evi-100, evi-200, evi-300) each with distinct Type 1 RD (<loopback-ip>:100, :200, :300). Enable advertise-mac, configure import-rt/export-rt, establish BGP EVPN peering, inject at least one MAC per EVI to trigger NLRI advertisement.
Action: Execute tech-support collection command (e.g., `show tech-support evpn`) after BGP sessions are Established and MAC routes are advertised.
Verify: Extract and parse tech-support archive for: `show evpn database`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route` (or equivalent), running-config sections showing RD per EVI, syslog entries related to EVPN/BGP bringup.
a7d7b8411dc591ed.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains `show evpn` output listing each EVI with its configured RD in Type 1 format (IP:number), `show bgp l2vpn evpn route` displaying NLRI with correct RD prefix, running-config with `route-distinguisher <loopback>:100|200|300` under each EVI, and all 3 RDs are distinct (no duplicates).
Fail-on: Missing any of: show evpn output, BGP EVPN route table, or running-config RD statements; RD fields are identical across 2+ EVIs; RD not visible in advertised EVPN NLRI; tech-support truncated or incomplete.

a8027b5bc8f177c0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES with `identifier 0 xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx` and `load-balancing-mode all-active` on PE1 and PE2; enable P2MP MPLS LSPs for BUM traffic; configure remote PE3 importing EVPN routes but not connected to the ES; establish EVPN instance and verify Ethernet A-D per ES routes with upstream assigned ESI labels are advertised.
Action: IXIA generates continuous mixed BUM traffic (broadcast, unknown unicast, multicast) at 50% line rate to the multi-homed ES for ≥ 24 hours; monitor ESI label stability and forwarding behavior throughout the run.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms All-Active mode and stable ESI label values; `show mpls forwarding-table` on PE1/PE2 confirms peer ESI label programmed in context label space; `show mpls forwarding-table` on PE3 confirms ESI labels programmed; packet capture on ES-facing interface confirms zero BUM packets with ESI label forwarded to ES (split-horizon); `show platform process memory` sampled hourly; `show interface counters` monotonic.
a8027b5bc8f177c0.equipment: Three PEs (PE1 + PE2 multi-homed to common ES in All-Active mode, PE3 remote PE importing routes) + P2MP MPLS LSP infrastructure + IXIA continuous BUM traffic generation (≥ 24 h)
a8027b5bc8f177c0.expectation:
Pass:    ESI labels remain advertised and programmed in correct context label space on all PEs across 24 h; split-horizon verified (zero packets with ESI label forwarded onto ES); memory usage flat (< 2% growth from baseline); all BUM traffic counters monotonic with no multiplication (Rx ≤ Tx + 5% measurement margin); no counter drops/resets.
Fail-on: ESI label advertisement disappears or value changes; any packet with ESI label forwarded onto ES (split-horizon failure); memory leak (≥ 5% growth); BUM loop detected (CE Rx traffic > Tx + margin); counter drops, resets, or errors; functional regression in BUM forwarding.

a81c3d493df674a2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (e.g., `evpn EVPN-100 service-type vlan-based`), enable auto-discovery under the VPLS context. Establish BGP neighbor peering. No prior `export-rt` configuration.
Action: Navigate to `configuration / l2-services / vpls / auto-discovery` mode. Issue `export-rt 65000:100`, then `export-rt 65000:200` to configure multiple route-target values. Commit and bring up the EVPN instance to trigger route advertisement. Issue `no export-rt 65000:200` and commit to test removal.
Verify: `show running-config` displays both export-rt entries before removal, one entry after. Capture BGP updates at neighbor PE or use `show bgp l2vpn evpn` to confirm advertised routes carry extended community RTs 65000:100 and 65000:200 initially, then only 65000:100 after removal.
a81c3d493df674a2.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (to verify RT presence in received EVPN routes)
a81c3d493df674a2.expectation:
Pass:    Multiple export-rt values accepted, persist in running-config, and advertised EVPN routes carry the configured RTs in the BGP extended community attribute. The no form successfully removes the specified RT from config and subsequent advertisements.
Fail-on: Commit rejects the configuration, export-rt does not appear in running-config, or advertised routes lack the configured RT values in extended communities.

a839123fd248432e.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT configured with EVPN instance EVI 100, `control-word enable`, local interface MTU 1500, RT 65000:100. 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) configured with same EVI 100, control-word disabled, MTU 9000, same RT 65000:100. BGP EVPN peering established (`af-l2vpn evpn` on DUT neighbor config).
Action: Both PEs advertise EVPN type-2 MAC/IP routes with L2-Attr Extended Community (Type 0x06) carrying local MTU and control-word C-flag. Capture BGP UPDATEs with tcpdump/Wireshark showing Extended Community attribute.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route` on DUT confirms routes received from remote PE but NOT installed as best path. `show evpn evi 100 mac` shows zero remote MACs from that PE. `show log` or syslog contains L2 attribute mismatch notification (MTU 9000≠1500, C-flag 0≠1). IXIA sends unicast frames to remote MACs; DUT floods them (no unicast forwarding to remote PE).
a839123fd248432e.expectation:
Pass:    DUT receives L2-Attr Extended Community with MTU=9000 and C=0; detects mismatch against local MTU=1500 and control-word=enable; does NOT install remote PE as EVPN destination for EVI 100; operator notification logged (syslog/SNMP trap); IXIA traffic to remote MACs is flooded, not unicast-forwarded.
Fail-on: DUT installs remote PE routes despite MTU or control-word mismatch; remote MACs appear in `show evpn evi 100 mac`; no mismatch notification logged; IXIA unicast traffic forwarded to misconfigured remote PE.

a84335628ba20a49.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed ES on agg-eth 0 (two PEs) using `identifier 0 <type0-value>` and `load-balancing-mode all-active`; set `es-waiting-time 3` (default); IXIA generates continuous bidirectional traffic (1 Gbps) through the ES.
Action: Run for ≥ 24 hours while periodically (every 30 min) shutting down one PE's agg-eth 0 to trigger RFC7432bis §8.2 mass withdraw, then restore after 60 sec; log convergence time (traffic interruption duration) per event.
Verify: `show platform process memory` every 2 hours; `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment`; packet captures on IXIA to measure loss per event; verify monotonic increment of `show interface agg-eth 0` counters.
a84335628ba20a49.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
a84335628ba20a49.expectation:
Pass:    Convergence time per event ≤ (es-waiting-time + 200 ms route propagation) throughout 24 h; no memory growth (±2% baseline); traffic loss per event < 150 ms; all interface/EVPN counters monotonic.
Fail-on: Convergence time degrades >30% after 12 h; memory growth >5%; traffic loss >500 ms in any event; counter rollback, freeze, or ES flapping beyond triggered events.

a87a56600d1bcf7c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT PE with ethernet-tag 100, `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (both defaults per CLI doc). Establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE (`af-l2vpn evpn`, matching import-rt). Connect IXIA as CE.
Action: IXIA sends Frame-1: src=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01, dst=00:00:00:00:00:99 (unknown MAC) on Tag 100. After 5s, send Frame-2: src=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01, dst=<MAC learned from neighbor PE Type-2 route>.
Verify: `show evpn mac ethernet-tag 100` includes AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 with local flag. `show evpn route type 2` advertises route for AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01. Packet capture on neighbor PE confirms Frame-1 arrival (flooded per §13.1). Frame-2 arrives at correct destination PE (unicast forwarded).
a87a56600d1bcf7c.expectation:
Pass:    Source MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 appears in `show evpn mac ethernet-tag 100` within 5 seconds. BGP Type-2 route advertised for AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01 visible in `show evpn route type 2`. Unknown-destination Frame-1 received at neighbor PE (flooding per RFC7432bis §13.1 MUST). Known-destination Frame-2 correctly forwarded per Type-2 route.
Fail-on: Source MAC not learned after Frame-1 ingress, Type-2 route not advertised, Frame-1 dropped (neighbor PE receives no packet), or Frame-2 misrouted.

a95081552e301ed8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 with member ports x-eth 0/0/1 and x-eth 0/0/2 in LACP active mode; bring up LACP with neighbor; note LAG MAC address from `show interface agg-eth 0`.
Action: In configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 context, configure lacp-system-mac with value 00:11:22:33:44:55; `commit`. Verify LACP remains up. Issue `no lacp-system-mac`; `commit`.
Verify: After custom MAC config: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` includes lacp-system-mac line; `show lacp` displays System ID 00:11:22:33:44:55; neighbor sees same System ID. After `no` form: lacp-system-mac removed from running-config; `show lacp` shows System ID equal to LAG MAC address.
a95081552e301ed8.expectation:
Pass:    Custom LACP System MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55) reflected in running-config, DUT operational state, and neighbor's view; default LAG MAC restored after `no lacp-system-mac`.
Fail-on: Custom System MAC not used in LACP PDUs (neighbor sees different System ID), or default not restored after removal.

a9b296f7fbe6e584.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT running current image with `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based` configured, local CE interface in evpn1 with mac-aging-time 300, IXIA generating L2 traffic (5+ unique source MACs) to populate data-plane MAC table; capture MAC count and running-config. ONIE upgrade image staged.
Action: Execute `onie-install <image-url>` to upgrade to next release. After reboot to new image, verify `show version` confirms new release. Regenerate L2 traffic from IXIA (10 new unique source MACs) to CE-facing interface. Issue `show evpn mac` and verify data-plane learning.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn1` shows preserved config including mac-aging-time 300. `show evpn mac evpn evpn1` displays new data-plane learned MACs (10 entries, flags indicating data-plane learned, not control-plane or LACP). No MACs learned from LACP source addresses (00:80:c2:00:00:02).
a9b296f7fbe6e584.equipment: DUT + ONIE image server + IXIA traffic gen
a9b296f7fbe6e584.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN config intact post-upgrade; data-plane MAC learning operational within 30 seconds of traffic generation; 10 new MACs present with data-plane learned flag; mac-aging-time preserved; zero LACP-sourced MACs.
Fail-on: EVPN config lost or modified; MAC learning disabled; control-plane learning enabled on local CE interface; LACP source MACs (00:80:c2:00:00:02) appear in MAC table; MAC learning timeout > 30s.

aa1c4ac6488a6483.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT + Cisco IOS-XR or Juniper MX PE connected over MPLS; BGP EVPN session established (af-l2vpn evpn enabled); shared EVI/VPLS instance with matching import-rt. On 3rd-party PE, configure sticky MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:01 (advertises Type-2 MAC/IP route with MAC Mobility extended community: static flag=1, sequence number=0).
Action: 3rd-party PE advertises sticky MAC via BGP; DUT receives and processes route. Then send Ethernet frame with source MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:01 from DUT's local AC (via IXIA or CE device) to trigger local MAC learning of same address.
Verify: 'show l2-services evpn mac-table' shows remote sticky MAC from 3rd-party PE with static indicator; 'show logging' or syslog capture confirms operator alert ("MAC address conflict detected" or similar) when local learning detects 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:01; verify DUT drops subsequent frames with that MAC SA on local AC if drop policy active; capture BGP UPDATE to confirm MAC Mobility ext-comm (Type 0x06, static=1, seq=0) parsed correctly.
aa1c4ac6488a6483.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts Type-2 route from Cisco/Juniper with MAC Mobility ext-comm (static=1, seq=0); MAC installed as remote/sticky in FIB; when same MAC learned locally, DUT generates operator alert (syslog severity ≥ warning); DUT MAY drop frames with that MAC SA over local ACs; BGP session remains stable.
Fail-on: Type-2 route rejected or MAC Mobility ext-comm not recognized; no operator alert logged when MAC conflict occurs; DUT installs conflicting MAC without alerting; BGP session flaps; or encoding mismatch (e.g., extended community type/format not interoperable).

aa21e555fd0dda76.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that BGP control-plane commands from EVPNS-REQ#20 (allow-as-in, maximum-prefix, route-reflector-client, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, private-as, weight, group) are accepted under the `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family and enforce their intended behavior on EVPN route advertisement, acceptance, and MAC learning.
Method: Configure a two-PE BGP EVPN topology with the EVPNS-REQ#20 commands applied under `af-l2vpn evpn`, bring up a VLAN-based EVI with bound access interfaces, send IXIA traffic to trigger MAC learning and Type 2/Type 3 route advertisement, capture BGP UPDATEs, and verify via `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and packet inspection that the configured BGP knobs enforce their intended behavior.
Setup: Two-PE topology over MPLS with BGP EVPN peering established. Under BGP neighbor configuration, enter `af-l2vpn evpn` mode and configure allow-as-in, maximum-prefix (threshold 10), route-reflector-client, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, and other commands listed in EVPNS-REQ#20. CE attached single-homed to PE1 on an access interface mapped to VLAN 100.
Action: On both PEs: create VLAN-based EVPN instance with `evpn evi-1 service-type vlan-based` under `configuration l2-services`; configure `auto-discovery`; set `import-rt 65000:1` and `export-rt 65000:1` under the auto-discovery context; bind the access interface to the EVPN instance; commit configuration. From IXIA, send 1 Gbps known-unicast and unknown-unicast Ethernet frames PE1→PE2 and PE2→PE1 for ≥ 60 s to trigger MAC learning. Capture BGP UPDATEs on the PE↔PE link via tcpdump or IXIA, and capture access-side frames on ingress/egress ACs.
Verify: `show configuration routing bgp` confirms all `af-l2vpn evpn` commands from EVPNS-REQ#20 accepted (allow-as-in, maximum-prefix, route-reflector-client, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, capability, private-as, weight, group). `show evpn evi evi-1` reports EVI operational state up, access AC bound, MAC table populated from data-plane learning. `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows MAC/IP Advertisement (Type 2) and Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag (Type 3) routes exchanged between PEs. BGP UPDATE captures show route-reflector attributes (ORIGINATOR_ID, CLUSTER_LIST) if route-reflector-client configured. If injecting >10 routes, `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms maximum-prefix threshold enforced. IXIA counters show frames received at line rate (≥ 0.99 Gbps for 1 Gbps offered load).
aa21e555fd0dda76.expectation:
Pass: All BGP commands from EVPNS-REQ#20 accepted under `af-l2vpn evpn`; EVI up within ≤ 10 s of commit; EVPN routes (Type 2, Type 3) installed in BGP L2VPN EVPN table; bidirectional unicast forwarded; local MAC table reflects learned MACs; remote MAC table reflects received routes; ≤ 0 packet drops over the 60 s steady-state window. Configured BGP knobs enforce behavior: allow-as-in permits routes with own ASN in AS-PATH, maximum-prefix enforces limit when threshold exceeded, route-reflector-client adds RR attributes (ORIGINATOR_ID, CLUSTER_LIST), soft-reconfiguration preserves pre-policy routes.
Fail-on: Any command from EVPNS-REQ#20 rejected in `af-l2vpn evpn` context; EVI never reaches operational state up; MAC learning fails; frames black-holed or dropped (>0 drops); commit rejected; or BGP knobs do not enforce intended behavior (e.g., own-AS routes rejected despite allow-as-in, maximum-prefix limit ignored, RR attributes absent when route-reflector-client configured).

aa4b7842f518e6db.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure complete EVPN instance: create EVPN with service-type (`evpn <evpn-name> service-type vlan-aware-bundle` under `configuration / l2-services`), configure LACP and ethernet-segment on `agg-eth 0` interface, configure VLAN translation on attachment circuit (testing the optional component), enable auto-discovery with route targets (`import-rt <route-target>`, `export-rt <route-target>`). Commit and verify LACP bundle up, BGP EVPN routes advertised.
Action: Exercise the configuration by forwarding tagged traffic across the EVI. Collect tech-support bundle via CLI.
Verify: Extract and parse tech-support archive.
