Confirm presence of: (1) running-config sections for EVPN instance, LACP, ethernet-segment, VLAN translation, route targets; (2) show command output for LACP status, EVPN/ES operational state, BGP l2vpn evpn route table; (3) syslog entries for EVPN/LACP/ES events; (4) forwarding tables (bridge FDB, MPLS label mappings).
aa4b7842f518e6db.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support archive contains complete running-config for mandatory EVPN components (instance with service-type, LACP per ES, ethernet-segment, route targets) and optional VLAN translation; operational state for LACP/EVPN/ES; BGP l2vpn evpn routes; syslog history; and forwarding plane state.
Fail-on: Missing running-config for EVPN instance/LACP/ethernet-segment; missing LACP or ES operational status; missing BGP l2vpn evpn routes; missing syslog entries; or absent forwarding tables (FDB/MPLS).

aa5592509b5d054b.action_steps:
Problem: tech-support bundle must capture complete diagnostic evidence for vlan-bundle service including configuration, forwarding state for configured VLANs, and drop behavior for non-configured VLAN IDs.
Method: configure EVPN instance with `service-type vlan-bundle` associating specific VLANs, drive traffic on both configured and non-configured VLANs to trigger forwarding and drops, collect tech-support bundle, and verify it contains configuration snapshots, MAC tables, and drop statistics.
Setup: Two-PE topology over MPLS with BGP EVPN established; configure EVPN instance with `service-type vlan-bundle` on interface x-eth 0/0/1, associate VLANs 100 and 200 with the bundle; CE sends traffic on VLANs 100, 200 (configured), and 300 (non-configured).
Action: Drive 1000 frames per VLAN (100, 200, 300) from CE through DUT to neighbor PE; execute `tech-support` command; save bundle off-box; extract and inspect bundle for EVPN configuration, state tables, interface statistics, and drop logs.
Verify: Bundle contains `show running-config | include evpn` with `service-type vlan-bundle` configuration; `show evpn evi` output; `show evpn mac address-table` showing learned MACs for VLANs 100, 200; interface counters or FIB drop statistics showing VLAN 300 frames discarded; relevant syslog entries.
aa5592509b5d054b.expectation:
Pass: tech-support bundle includes vlan-bundle configuration snapshot, MAC address tables for configured VLANs 100 and 200, and drop counters or logs confirming non-configured VLAN 300 frames were discarded.
Fail-on: Missing EVPN configuration in running-config, missing `show evpn` output, no MAC table entries for valid VLANs, or no drop evidence for non-configured VLAN 300.

aa7c39bb0195fc79.action_steps:
Setup:  Enter `configuration / l2-services` mode and configure `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based`. Bind the EVPN instance to VLAN 100. From IXIA send L2 frames with at least 5 distinct source MAC addresses into VLAN 100 on the DUT.
Action: Exit to operational mode and execute `show evpn mac address-table`.
Verify: Output table contains all 5+ learned MAC addresses. For each entry verify EVPN instance (evpn-1), VLAN (100), MAC address, ingress interface/port, and type (dynamic) fields are present.
aa7c39bb0195fc79.expectation:
Pass:    All learned MAC addresses from IXIA traffic appear in the table with populated EVPN instance, VLAN, interface, and type fields.
Fail-on: Command returns error, empty output, or missing any of the learned MAC addresses despite successful traffic reception.

aaafebc1a9ef1d82.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface `agg-eth 0` under `configuration / interface`.
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0` mode, issue `ethernet-segment` command, observe the CLI prompt to confirm mode transition to ethernet-segment sub-mode, exit to top level, issue `commit`, then run `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`. Re-enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0`, issue `no ethernet-segment`, commit, and re-run `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`.
Verify: After `ethernet-segment`, the CLI prompt indicates ethernet-segment mode. After first commit, `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` contains the `ethernet-segment` block. After `no ethernet-segment` and second commit, the ethernet-segment block is absent.
aaafebc1a9ef1d82.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
aaafebc1a9ef1d82.expectation:
Pass:    The `ethernet-segment` command transitions to sub-mode, persists in running-config under `agg-eth 0`, and is cleanly removed by `no ethernet-segment` with no orphaned configuration.
Fail-on: Command rejected, no mode transition, ethernet-segment block missing from running-config after first commit, or ethernet-segment block still present after `no ethernet-segment` commit.

aab7a48d0c42f098.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance "evpn-bundle-1" with `evpn evpn-bundle-1 service-type vlan-bundle`, bind VLANs 10,20 to the bundle on x-eth 0/0/1; send IXIA traffic streams with VLAN-ID 10 (in bundle, 1000 frames) and VLAN-ID 30 (not in bundle, 1000 frames); confirm VLAN 30 frames are dropped via interface counters.
Action: Execute `tech-support` CLI command after traffic validation completes.
Verify: Extract and parse tech-support archive for: running-config containing "evpn evpn-bundle-1 service-type vlan-bundle" and bound VLAN list (10,20); `show interfaces x-eth 0/0/1 statistics` showing ingress VLAN filter drops ≥ 1000; EVI FIB tables for evpn-bundle-1; syslog entries for VLAN mismatch if implementation logs them.
aab7a48d0c42f098.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support archive contains: (1) evpn-bundle-1 config with service-type vlan-bundle and VLAN list 10,20 in running-config; (2) x-eth 0/0/1 interface statistics show ≥ 1000 VLAN filter drops matching the VLAN-ID 30 stream; (3) EVI FIB entries for evpn-bundle-1 present; (4) no missing show command outputs for EVPN instance or interface.
Fail-on: Missing evpn-bundle-1 section in running-config, zero or missing VLAN filter drop counters on x-eth 0/0/1 when VLAN-ID 30 traffic was sent, or missing `show l2-services evpn` / `show interfaces` outputs in archive.

aadff23fba9b8e8f.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN instance 'evpn1' (service-type vlan-based) with local interface eth1 configured as member; neighbor PE up, BGP EVPN peering active; IXIA behind neighbor PE.
Action: Under 'configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1', run 'mac-address-static 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01 interface eth1'; commit. IXIA sends L2 frame with DA=00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01 from neighbor PE side.
Verify: 'show evpn mac-table evpn1' output contains entry: MAC=00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01, Type=Static, Interface=eth1. Packet capture (tcpdump) on eth1 shows frame with DA=00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01 arrives.
aadff23fba9b8e8f.expectation:
Pass:    'show evpn mac-table evpn1' displays 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01 with Type=Static, Interface=eth1; frame with DA=00:aa:bb:cc:dd:01 received on eth1.
Fail-on: MAC entry missing, Type != Static, Interface != eth1, frame floods to all interfaces, or frame not received on eth1.

ab033e4f1d0409f6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`; establish BGP EVPN peering with a neighbor PE; attach local AC x-eth 0/0/1 and verify the neighbor PE has a corresponding AC in the same EVI. Initially set `unknow-mac-flooding enable` (default) and commit.
Action: (1) Via IXIA, send 100 frames with unknown destination MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff into x-eth 0/0/1. (2) Reconfigure to `unknow-mac-flooding disable` and commit. (3) Send another 100 frames with a different unknown destination MAC bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00.
Verify: Capture traffic on the neighbor PE's AC. In step (1), count received frames (expect 100). In step (3), count received frames (expect 0). Before each test, verify the destination MAC is not present in the EVPN MAC tables on either PE.
ab033e4f1d0409f6.expectation:
Pass:    With `unknow-mac-flooding enable`, all 100 frames flood to the neighbor PE (100/100 received, 0% loss). With `unknow-mac-flooding disable`, 0 frames reach the neighbor PE (flooding blocked).
Fail-on: Enable state does not flood unknown-MAC frames (< 100 received in step 1), or disable state still floods (> 0 received in step 3).

ab1261596f18cadc.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that port-based service type can be configured on both LAG and physical Ethernet interfaces, where all ingress frames (regardless of VLAN tags) map to a single EVPN instance.
Method: Configure port-based EVPN instances on LAG `agg-lag 1` and physical port `x-eth 0/0/1`, send mixed-VLAN traffic to both interfaces, and verify all frames are accepted and forwarded within their respective EVI.
Setup:  Two-PE MPLS topology with BGP EVPN session established between PE1 and PE2. PE1 has LAG interface `agg-lag 1` and physical Ethernet port `x-eth 0/0/1` connected to IXIA.
Action: On PE1: enter `l2-services` configuration mode; create `evpn evi-lag service-type port-based` and `evpn evi-eth service-type port-based`; bind interface `agg-lag 1` to evi-lag and `x-eth 0/0/1` to evi-eth; configure `auto-discovery` with `import-rt` and `export-rt` for each EVI; commit configuration. IXIA sends mixed traffic (untagged, VLAN 10 tagged, VLAN 20 tagged) at 100 Mbps per stream to both interfaces.
Verify: `show evpn evi` displays both EVIs with service-type port-based; `show evpn mac address-table` shows MACs learned from all VLAN tags in each EVI; `show running-config l2-services` confirms the configuration; PE2 receives all traffic regardless of VLAN tags.
ab1261596f18cadc.expectation:
Pass:    Both LAG `agg-lag 1` and physical port `x-eth 0/0/1` accept all frames (untagged, VLAN 10, VLAN 20); all traffic maps to the respective EVI; PE2 receives forwarded frames from all VLAN types per interface.
Fail-on: Interface rejects frames based on VLAN tag; only specific VLAN-tagged or only untagged frames are processed; VLAN-based filtering occurs at ingress.

ab1af11b5e66a841.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that standard BGP neighbor commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) are available and operational under af-l2vpn evpn, enabling full BGP session control and policy for EVPN route exchange.
Method: Build two-PE VLAN-based EVPN service, configure multiple BGP commands under af-l2vpn evpn, drive MAC learning traffic, and verify command acceptance, route distribution, and local/remote MAC learning functionality.

Setup: Configure PE1 and PE2 with MPLS transport and BGP. Create EVPN instance on both using `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`. Configure RD and import/export RT. Establish BGP neighbor session between PE1 and PE2. On PE1, under BGP neighbor config for PE2, enter `af-l2vpn evpn` and configure `route-reflector-client`, `maximum-prefix 500`, `inbound-soft-reconfiguration`. On PE2, under BGP neighbor config for PE1, enter `af-l2vpn evpn` and configure `allow-as-in`, `weight 100`. Attach CE1 to PE1 interface `x-eth 0/0/1` in VLAN 100, CE2 to PE2 in VLAN 100.

Action: Generate bidirectional traffic between CE1 and CE2 using 10 unique source MACs per side. Verify PE1 learns local MACs from CE1, PE2 learns local MACs from CE2. Verify each PE advertises MAC/IP routes to peer via BGP EVPN. Verify remote MAC installation on both PEs from received EVPN routes. Confirm bidirectional traffic forwarding.

Verify: `show running-config` on PE1 displays `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`, RD, RT config, and under BGP neighbor for PE2 shows `af-l2vpn evpn` section containing `route-reflector-client`, `maximum-prefix 500`, `inbound-soft-reconfiguration`. PE2 config shows `allow-as-in`, `weight 100` under `af-l2vpn evpn`. `show evpn mac address-table` on PE1 shows local MACs from CE1 and remote MACs from CE2; on PE2 shows local MACs from CE2 and remote MACs from CE1. `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows MAC/IP routes exchanged. Traffic forwards successfully.
ab1af11b5e66a841.expectation:
Pass: All required BGP commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) accepted under af-l2vpn evpn without syntax error; EVPN service-type vlan-based operational; RD and RT configured; local MAC learning functional; remote MAC learning via EVPN operational; routes exchanged; traffic forwards; configuration persists in running-config.

Fail-on: Any of the eight BGP commands rejected under af-l2vpn evpn; EVPN instance creation failed; local or remote MAC learning broken; no EVPN route exchange; traffic failure; or configuration not persisted.

ab88fc5674d69e35.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs with EVPN instance and all-active ethernet-segments (load-balancing-mode all-active, es-waiting-time 3 sec default); IXIA scale rig attached to both PEs generating 10,000 unique MACs with bidirectional traffic at 10 Gbps.
Action: IXIA moves 1,000 source MACs/sec from PE1 to PE2 attachment by changing source port; trigger BGP EVPN MAC/IP Advertisement routes (RT-2) with MAC Mobility extended community and incrementing sequence numbers per RFC7432bis §15.
Verify: Capture BGP updates for MAC Mobility extended community presence and sequence number increments; measure per-MAC move detection latency and traffic reconvergence time; count packet loss during each mobility event; verify no MAC duplication or route flapping.
ab88fc5674d69e35.expectation:
Pass:    MAC move detection ≤ 100 ms (first packet to BGP RT-2 update); reconvergence ≤ 200 ms (update to forwarding switchover); packet loss < 0.1% per mobility event; all RT-2 updates include MAC Mobility extended community with correct monotonic sequence numbers; 1,000 MACs/sec sustained without event drops.
Fail-on: Detection > 100 ms; reconvergence > 200 ms; packet loss ≥ 0.1%; missing MAC Mobility extended community in RT-2; non-monotonic or duplicate sequence numbers; MAC mobility events dropped or stalled under load; MAC duplication detection false positives.

abb93e274ded1f91.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT (PE1) and neighbor PE2 with identical ESI (identifier 0 xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx under agg-eth/ethernet-segment) to multihomed CE; set load-balancing-mode all-active on both; IXIA sends bidirectional traffic (1000 pps) through CE to remote EVPN destinations.
Action: Hard-shut the agg-eth interface on DUT or disable ethernet-segment; force PE2 to become sole forwarder via Aliasing path (RFC7432bis §8.4 DF election).
Verify: Capture EVPN route type 4 (ES route) withdrawal from DUT; measure traffic convergence time from IXIA loss counters; confirm PE2 now forwards 100% of CE↔remote traffic; check `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows PE2 as DF.
abb93e274ded1f91.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen (multihomed CE topology: both PEs connected to same CE segment)
abb93e274ded1f91.expectation:
Pass:    Traffic fails over to PE2 within es-waiting-time + 2 s (≤5 s total); ES route type 4 withdrawn from DUT; zero packet loss after convergence; PE2 `show evpn ethernet-segment` indicates DF role for the ESI.
Fail-on: Traffic outage >5 s, permanent packet loss >0.1%, Aliasing path not activated (PE2 does not assume DF), or ES route type 4 not withdrawn within 10 s.

ac316bd7957fb5f6.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology; configure `af-l2vpn evpn` + `advertise-mac enable` on DUT; attach IXIA host with MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 + IPv4 10.1.1.10 and second host with MAC 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee + IPv6 2001:db8::1.
Action: Learn both MACs on DUT access port; capture BGP UPDATE carrying Route Type 2 on DUT↔neighbor-PE eBGP session; decode NLRI octets per RFC7432bis §7.
Verify: Extract RD (8 octets, Type field per §7.9), MAC Address Length field (1 octet = 0x30 = 48 decimal), MAC Address (6 octets = 00:11:22:33:44:55), IP Address Length (1 octet = 0x20 for IPv4 or 0x80 for IPv6), IP Address (4 octets = 0x0a010101 or 16 octets = 0x20010db8…0001).
ac316bd7957fb5f6.expectation:
Pass:    Route Type 2 NLRI contains: RD (Type 0/1/2, 8 octets total), MAC Address Length = 48, MAC = 6 octets, IPv4 length = 32 + address = 4 octets OR IPv6 length = 128 + address = 16 octets.
Fail-on: RD format invalid per §7.9, MAC Address Length ≠ 48, MAC encoded as ≠ 6 octets, IP Address Length ≠ 32 (IPv4) or ≠ 128 (IPv6), or IP field size mismatch (not 4 or 16 octets).

ac7c033e1286ea30.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVI 100 with BGP EVPN peer; establish multihomed ES (ESI 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:11:22:33) to generate Type-1/Type-4 routes carrying ESI Label EC and ES Import RT EC; add MAC/IP binding to trigger Type-2 route with MAC Mobility EC; optionally configure neighbor PE to inject routes with duplicate EC instances (2+ ESI Label ECs, or 2+ MAC Mobility ECs).
Action: Trigger route exchange and processing (advertise/receive Type-1/Type-2/Type-4 routes with the three EC types); if duplicate ECs or malformed attributes are injected, allow DUT to process them; execute `tech-support` CLI command to capture full diagnostics.
Verify: Inspect tech-support bundle for: `show bgp evpn routes detail evi 100` showing ESI Label, ES Import RT, MAC Mobility ECs; `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:11:22:33` confirming ESI Label value used (first instance if duplicates present); `show log | grep "treat-as-withdraw\|attribute"` for any malformed-attribute handling; running-config with `af-l2vpn evpn`, `import-rt`, `export-rt` for EVI 100; FIB/MAC tables reflecting processed routes.
ac7c033e1286ea30.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains BGP EVPN route details with EC attributes visible (ESI Label in Type-1, ES Import RT in Type-1/Type-4, MAC Mobility in Type-2); logs confirm only first EC instance processed when duplicates received; treat-as-withdraw events logged with NLRI details if forwarding-relevant attribute errors triggered; complete running-config and forwarding-plane snapshots present.
Fail-on: Missing `show bgp evpn routes` output with EC details, missing `show evpn` state, no log evidence of duplicate-EC first-only processing or treat-as-withdraw behavior when triggered, or incomplete config/FIB snapshots.

aca6501a8a00b289.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT + neighbor PE) in the same EVPN instance, both multihomed to the same Ethernet Segment (shared ESI value, e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) using MP2MP tunnels (Ingress Replication or P2MP RSVP-TE). Enable BUM traffic forwarding and EVPN multicast.
Action: Trigger BGP EVPN route advertisements from both PEs. Capture Type 3 (IMET) and Type 4 (ES) routes with `show bgp evpn route-type 3` and `show bgp evpn route-type 4` on both PEs. Extract the ESI Label Extended Community (Type 0x06) from the advertisements.
Verify: Compare the ESI label value in the ESI Label Extended Community from both PEs for the shared Ethernet Segment. Confirm the label is identical and appears in the MP2MP tunnel context (not per-PE upstream allocation).
aca6501a8a00b289.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise the identical ESI label value (e.g., label 5000) in the ESI Label Extended Community for the shared Ethernet Segment ESI. The label is allocated from a Domain-wide Common Block, evidenced by the same value across all PEs.
Fail-on: PEs advertise different ESI label values for the same ESI when MP2MP tunnels are configured, or no ESI label is present in Type 4 ES routes, or labels differ per PE indicating upstream allocation instead of DCB.

acba30e2659de215.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco IOS-XR PE connected over MP-BGP EVPN; configure matching ESI Type 1 (LAG-based) on both PEs connected to same CE; enable RT Constraint (RFC4684) on BGP session via route-target filter on both sides.
Action: On DUT: configure `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based` with ESI; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor; verify DUT advertises Ethernet Segment route (Type 4) with ES-Import RT extended community (Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x02, 6-octet value auto-derived from ESI high-order bytes). On Cisco PE: advertise RT Constraint allowing ES-Import RT value; then withdraw constraint to block import.
Verify: Capture BGP UPDATE on wire; decode ES-Import RT Type/Sub-Type and value matches ESI derivation; use `show bgp l2vpn evpn ethernet-segment` on DUT and Cisco equivalent; confirm Cisco PE imports ES route when RT Constraint permits and filters when constrained out.
acba30e2659de215.expectation:
Pass:    ES route carries ES-Import RT Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x02; Cisco PE imports ES route only when RT Constraint match succeeds; filtered when constraint blocks; ES multi-homing active convergence under 1 sec.
Fail-on: ES-Import RT missing or wrong Type/Sub-Type in BGP UPDATE; Cisco PE ignores RT Constraint and always imports; Cisco PE rejects route due to encoding mismatch; ES route not installed when constraint permits.

acc6eaf2f92d9c48.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed CE connected to PE1 (DF) and PE2 (Non-DF) with shared ESI; PE3 as ingress PE; all PEs in same EVI with `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; verify PE2's ESI label via `show evpn ethernet-segment` and BGP Type-1 route advertisement.
Action: Inject broadcast Ethernet frame from PE3 toward the multi-homed EVI; capture MPLS packets on PE3→PE1 and PE3→PE2 links using IXIA; note PE2's advertised ESI label value.
Verify: Captured packet to PE2 (non-DF) contains MPLS label stack: outer (transport LSP), middle (VPN/EVI label), inner (ESI label matching PE2's Type-1 advertisement per RFC7432bis §8.3.1.1); PE2 does NOT forward frame to multi-homed CE (split-horizon filter active).
acc6eaf2f92d9c48.equipment: Three routers (PE1 + PE2 multi-homed pair, PE3 ingress PE) + IXIA for BUM injection and MPLS packet capture on inter-PE links
acc6eaf2f92d9c48.expectation:
Pass:    MPLS packet to non-DF PE2 includes ESI label matching value in PE2's BGP Type-1 route; PE2 drops frame on egress (no CE forwarding); packet to DF PE1 may omit ESI label (DF forwards normally).
Fail-on: ESI label missing in packet to non-DF PE2, wrong ESI label value, or PE2 forwards broadcast back to multi-homed CE (split-horizon failure causing loop).

acdfbebbd35361f1.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP AS 65000, iBGP peering to neighbor PE/RR, LAG agg-lag 1 (LACP enabled, member ports up) and Ethernet interface x-eth 0/0/1 in no-switchport mode, both interfaces admin-up.
Action: Enter `config / l2-services`; create `evpn evpn-pb-test service-type port-based`; configure auto-discovery with `import-rt 65000:200` and `export-rt 65000:200`; bind agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 to the EVPN instance; commit configuration.
Verify: `show evpn evpn-pb-test` confirms service-type=port-based, admin/oper=Up, bound interfaces agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 listed; `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-pb-test` displays service-type, auto-discovery, import-rt, export-rt; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` and `route-type 3` show routes generated for the port-based instance.
acdfbebbd35361f1.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (iBGP peer or route reflector for EVPN route exchange verification)
acdfbebbd35361f1.expectation:
Pass:    Configuration commits successfully; show evpn confirms service-type port-based, both agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 bound and operationally Up, auto-discovery enabled; running-config reflects all parameters (service-type port-based, auto-discovery on, import-rt/export-rt 65000:200); BGP table contains Type-1 (Ethernet AD per interface) and Type-3 (IMET per EVI) routes with correct RT 65000:200.
Fail-on: Configuration rejected with error; service-type not port-based in show output; either interface fails to bind or shows oper-down; auto-discovery disabled in running-config; import-rt or export-rt missing or incorrect; no Type-1 or Type-3 routes present in BGP EVPN table; or advertised routes have wrong RT values.

acff8931773ef3c1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and PE2 with identical ESI (e.g., identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88) on agg-eth interfaces to shared CE; set load-balancing-mode single-active on both; establish EVPN instance <EVI, BD> with BGP peering to remote PE3; enable MAC learning.
Action: Capture BGP Ethernet A-D per ES routes (type 1) advertised from DUT and PE2 to PE3; decode ESI Label extended community, verify "Multihoming redundancy mode" field = 1; send unicast frames from IXIA (behind PE3) destined to MAC on multihomed ES; observe forwarding plane—traffic should reach only the DF PE.
Verify: show bgp evpn routes type 1 (filter by ESI); show evpn ethernet-segment (confirms single-active and DF election); Wireshark/tcpdump BGP updates for ESI Label extended community encoding; traffic capture confirms PE3 forwards only to DF (DUT or PE2), not both; show bgp evpn routes type 1 per-EVI for L2-Attr extended community with P bit = 1 on DF's route.
acff8931773ef3c1.equipment: Three-PE multihoming topology: DUT + PE2 (both with same ESI, load-balancing-mode single-active) + remote PE3 (BGP route importer) + IXIA traffic gen
acff8931773ef3c1.expectation:
Pass:    All Ethernet A-D per ES routes (type 1) from DUT and PE2 carry ESI Label extended community with "Multihoming redundancy mode" = 1; remote PE3 deduces single-active and forwards traffic for <EVI, BD> exclusively to the DF PE (not load-balanced); DF's type 1 per-EVI route includes L2-Attr extended community with Primary bit set.
Fail-on: "Multihoming redundancy mode" field ≠ 1 in any type 1 route, or PE3 load-balances across both DUT and PE2 (all-active behavior), or L2-Attr P bit = 0 in DF's per-EVI route, or non-DF PE receives traffic for <EVI, BD>.

ad12ae0ad2d50de8.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT booted; CLI session attached via console or SSH.
Action: 1. At operational mode, type `show fib ?` to verify `evpn-neighbor` appears as a sub-token.
        2. Type `show fib evpn-n` and press TAB to test prefix completion.
        3. Execute `show fib evpn-neighbor` (no arguments) to invoke the command.
        4. Type `show fib evpn-neighbor ?` to list any documented filters or arguments.
Verify: 1. `show fib ?` includes `evpn-neighbor` with description matching "Displays fib evpn-neighbors information".
        2. TAB after `show fib evpn-n` completes to `evpn-neighbor` uniquely.
        3. Command executes without error; output is structured (column headers present) or states "no entries" if FIB is empty.
        4. `show fib evpn-neighbor ?` lists only filters/arguments documented in the CLI doc Parameters Table (or "<cr>" if none).
ad12ae0ad2d50de8.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
ad12ae0ad2d50de8.expectation:
Pass:    `show fib evpn-neighbor` is discoverable via `show fib ?`, TAB-completes from prefix `evpn-n`, executes cleanly, and `?` lists exactly the documented arguments (zero undocumented options).
Fail-on: Sub-token `evpn-neighbor` absent from `show fib ?`, TAB fails to complete `evpn-n` → `evpn-neighbor`, command returns "unknown command" or "invalid input", or `?` shows options not in the CLI doc.

ad38b8d0fa646c57.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure af-l2vpn evpn on DUT; establish BGP EVPN session to IXIA emulator; verify ESTABLISHED state and valid RT-2/RT-3 route exchange.
Action: Inject three malformed BGP UPDATEs from IXIA: (1) MP_REACH_NLRI with last NLRI truncated to 1 octet (Length field missing), (2) MP_REACH_NLRI with NLRI Length=20 but only 15 octets remaining, (3) same as (1) but MP_UNREACH_NLRI.
Verify: show bgp evpn summary after each UPDATE (neighbor state → IDLE); capture NOTIFICATION from DUT (Error Code 3 Update Message Error); syslog mentions NLRI Length validation or §7.14.1.
ad38b8d0fa646c57.equipment: DUT + IXIA with BGP negative-test capability (IxNetwork or scripted emulator for malformed UPDATE injection)
ad38b8d0fa646c57.expectation:
Pass:    DUT resets session (→ IDLE) within 5 sec per malformed UPDATE; sends NOTIFICATION Update Message Error; session re-establishes after valid OPEN from IXIA.
Fail-on: Session stays ESTABLISHED with truncated (length<2) or length-inconsistent NLRI; no NOTIFICATION; DUT crash; subsequent valid routes dropped.

ad41a0f871e277fb.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 with a non-default LACP Administrative Key value; commit and verify custom key is active via `show lacp interface agg-eth 0`.
Action: Enter configuration mode for agg-eth 0, issue `no lacp-key`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` contains no lacp-key statement; `show lacp interface agg-eth 0` displays Administrative Key equal to interface number (0).
ad41a0f871e277fb.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
ad41a0f871e277fb.expectation:
Pass:    lacp-key absent from running-config; LACP Administrative Key equals interface number (0 for agg-eth 0)
Fail-on: lacp-key persists in running-config, Administrative Key retains custom value, or LACP state inconsistent with default

ad52d5295e57896c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300` under `configuration/l2-services/evpn`. Establish multi-homing ESI LAG between DUT and neighbor PE. Connect IXIA traffic generator with two ports configured to send frames from the same source MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55) — one port behind DUT, one behind neighbor PE.
Action: Alternate traffic bursts from both IXIA ports to trigger 6 MAC moves within 180 seconds (exceeding move-count threshold of 5). Monitor BGP EVPN route advertisements and DUT syslog.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-duplication`, `show bgp evpn routes mac 00:11:22:33:44:55`, syslog for duplication alert, `show l2-services fdb` to confirm MAC frozen. Verify multi-homing ESI routes for other MACs still advertised via `show bgp evpn routes esi`.
ad52d5295e57896c.expectation:
Pass:    DUT raises syslog alert identifying duplicate MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 after 6th move; BGP MAC/IP Advertisement route for that MAC withdrawn or suppressed for 300-second freeze-time; multi-homing continues (ESI routes for other MACs still present, no flap in `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment`).
Fail-on: No alert raised after threshold breach, or DUT continues advertising/processing BGP routes for 00:11:22:33:44:55 beyond freeze-time, or multi-homing flaps/breaks for unrelated MACs.

adb96c1acfbf34d4.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 and PE2 each configure the same ESI (e.g., `identifier 0 01.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.11`) with `load-balancing-mode all-active` under `interface agg-eth` facing the same CE. DUT (remote PE) establishes BGP EVPN sessions with PE1 and PE2. Both PE1 and PE2 advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes with ESI Label extended community "Multihoming redundancy mode" = 0x00 (All-Active), and both advertise MAC/IP Advertisement routes for MAC M1 in EVI 100.
Action: DUT imports Ethernet A-D per ES routes from PE1 and PE2; verify ESI Label extended community = 0 in received routes (`show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 ... detail`). IXIA sends 1000 pps bidirectional traffic destined to M1 through DUT. Capture DUT's forwarding plane next-hop construction for M1.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI>` on DUT reports "Redundancy Mode: All-Active". `show route evpn mac <M1>` displays multiple next hops (PE1 and PE2 IPs). IXIA confirms traffic egresses toward both PE1 and PE2 (per-flow ECMP hash distribution, not single-active pinning). Label stack in forwarded packets matches labels from PE1's and PE2's MAC/IP Advertisement routes.
adb96c1acfbf34d4.equipment: DUT + 2× neighbor PE (PE1, PE2 multi-homed to same ESI) + IXIA traffic gen
adb96c1acfbf34d4.expectation:
Pass:    DUT deduces All-Active mode when all imported Ethernet A-D per ES routes have ESI Label extended community "Multihoming redundancy mode" = 0. DUT constructs next hops for M1 using all PEs that advertised reachability (PE1 and PE2). Traffic to M1 is distributed across both PE next hops per ECMP; label stack per RFC7432bis §14.1.2 bullet 3 (MAC/IP Advertisement route label stack used).
Fail-on: DUT treats ES as Single-Active despite all routes having mode = 0. M1 reachable via only one next hop, or traffic pinned to single PE. Label stack does not match MAC/IP Advertisement route labels. Traffic black-holed or `show evpn ethernet-segment` reports incorrect mode.

adcd189834050f02.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure at least one interface as administratively up (e.g., management or an x-eth interface). Ensure the interface has link.
Action: Execute `show interface detail` with no parameters to retrieve full detailed information for all interfaces.
Verify: Parse the output for each interface, confirming presence of: interface name, admin status (up/down), operational status (up/down), MAC address, MTU, and RX/TX packet/byte counters.
adcd189834050f02.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
adcd189834050f02.expectation:
Pass:    Command returns multi-line output with a block per interface, each block containing admin status, operational status, MAC, MTU, and traffic counters.
Fail-on: Command fails with an error, returns empty output, or omits any of the key operational fields (admin/operational status, MAC, MTU, counters) for interfaces known to be present.

ae020da91928ec77.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn-100` (vlan-based) on PE1 and PE2; enable BGP auto-discovery with matching RD/RT; attach interface `x-eth 0/0/1` to `evpn-100` on PE1; verify BGP EVPN peering is established.
Action: On PE1, enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-100` mode; issue `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`; commit. Wait 10 seconds for BGP propagation.
Verify: On PE1: `show evpn mac address-table evpn-100` must list 00:11:22:33:44:55 with type=static and interface=x-eth 0/0/1. `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` must show Type 2 route for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55. On PE2: `show evpn mac address-table evpn-100` must list MAC learned from PE1's RD.
ae020da91928ec77.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 MAC table shows 00:11:22:33:44:55 as static on x-eth 0/0/1; BGP advertises Type 2 route with this MAC and PE1's RD; PE2 MAC table contains the MAC learned via BGP from PE1.
Fail-on: Static MAC not in PE1 table after commit; no Type 2 BGP route with MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55; PE2 does not have the MAC in its table; or error on commit.

ae4925f3557b07ab.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with shared ESI on agg-eth 0, load-balancing-mode all-active. Configure PE1 with `service-carving highest-random-weight` and PE2 with `service-carving default`. Bring up EVI and allow Ethernet Segment Route (4) exchange.
Action: On PE1, run `tech-support` command and save the bundle off-box. Open the bundle and extract DF election state.
Verify: Bundle contains `show evpn df` output listing current DF per VLAN/EVI with algorithm in use (must show 'highest-random-weight' attempted, then fallback to 'default' triggered). Bundle contains `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` showing configured service-carving value and received DF Election Extended Community from PE2. Bundle contains `show evpn route-type 4` or equivalent showing Ethernet Segment Route advertisements with DF Alg field. Logs/syslog must record the fallback event per RFC7432bis-§8.5 when PE1 detected DF Alg mismatch.
ae4925f3557b07ab.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle includes (1) DF election state per EVI showing fallback to Default algorithm, (2) `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` listing both local service-carving config (highest-random-weight, highest-preference, lowest-preference, or default) and peer's advertised DF Alg, (3) Ethernet Segment Route (4) advertisements with DF Election Extended Community, (4) syslog entry confirming fallback to Default when algorithm mismatch detected.
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn df` output, missing DF Election Extended Community in Route-Type 4, no fallback log when algorithms differ, or bundle omits running-config showing service-carving command syntax (default | highest-random-weight | highest-preference preference | lowest-preference preference).

ae4b51b79f9d1962.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure three MAC-VRFs on DUT with Type 1 RDs (e.g., `evpn EVPN-100` RD 10.0.0.1:100, `evpn EVPN-200` RD 10.0.0.1:200, `evpn EVPN-300` RD 10.0.0.1:300); configure EVPN-100 and EVPN-200 with the same ESI (multi-homing to neighbor PE); enable `advertise-mac enable` on all three MAC-VRFs; configure BGP `af-l2vpn evpn` peering to neighbor PE.
Action: Bring up BGP EVPN session; send L2 traffic from IXIA through each MAC-VRF to trigger MAC learning and advertisement; capture BGP UPDATE messages for Type 2 (MAC/IP) and Type 3 (IMET) routes from all three MAC-VRFs.
Verify: `show bgp evpn routes` on neighbor PE; parse RD field in EVPN NLRI for each advertised route; confirm each route's RD matches the originating MAC-VRF's configured RD; verify no duplicate RD across MAC-VRFs despite shared ESI.
ae4b51b79f9d1962.expectation:
Pass:    All Type 2 and Type 3 EVPN routes from EVPN-100 carry RD 10.0.0.1:100, from EVPN-200 carry 10.0.0.1:200, from EVPN-300 carry 10.0.0.1:300; RD values remain unique across all MAC-VRFs; multi-homed MAC-VRFs (EVPN-100, EVPN-200 sharing ESI) maintain distinct RDs in their advertisements per RFC7432bis §7.9 MUST.
Fail-on: Any MAC-VRF advertises routes with incorrect RD (not matching its configured RD); duplicate RD appears in routes from different MAC-VRFs; multi-homing ESI causes RD collision or forces RD reuse; any MAC-VRF advertises routes with RD=0:0 or missing RD.

ae6bb91b3ea93c5d.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE both running, iBGP peering established (AS 65000, address-family l2vpn evpn enabled), VPLS instance "vpls-100" configured on both PEs with local attachment-circuits but auto-discovery not enabled.
Action: On DUT, enter `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-100`, issue `auto-discovery` to enter auto-discovery mode, configure `export-rt 65000:100`, configure `import-rt 65000:100`, exit and `commit`. Configure matching auto-discovery with same route-targets on neighbor PE.
Verify: `show running-config` on DUT shows auto-discovery block under vpls-100 with export-rt and import-rt both set to 65000:100, BGP control-plane shows EVPN routes tagged with RT 65000:100 exchanged between DUT and neighbor PE, VPLS status shows auto-discovered pseudowire to neighbor PE with Up state.
ae6bb91b3ea93c5d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for BGP EVPN peering and auto-discovery verification)
ae6bb91b3ea93c5d.expectation:
Pass:    auto-discovery configuration accepted, running-config persists both route-targets as configured (export-rt 65000:100, import-rt 65000:100), BGP table contains EVPN routes with RT 65000:100 from both local and remote PE, pseudowire auto-established and operationally Up
Fail-on: Commit rejected, route-targets not in running-config, no BGP EVPN routes with RT 65000:100, or pseudowire to neighbor PE not established/not Up

ae9b8805c5ef8eb2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on DUT `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment` and on 3rd-party PE multihoming interface; establish BGP EVPN session with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled; configure matching EVPN instance on both.
Action: Enable the ethernet-segment on DUT's agg-eth interface; capture Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) route advertisements in both directions using `show bgp evpn ethernet-segment` on DUT and vendor-equivalent on 3rd-party PE; decode the ES-Import RT extended community (type field + 6-octet value) from the BGP UPDATE.
Verify: On DUT's Type-4 route, confirm ES-Import RT 6-octet value = 01:11:22:33:44:55 (high-order 6 octets of configured ESI). On 3rd-party PE's Type-4 route, confirm DUT imports it and ES-Import RT matches its ESI's high-order 6 octets. Check both control plane (`show bgp evpn routes type 4`) and that neither side rejects the peer's encoding.
ae9b8805c5ef8eb2.expectation:
Pass:    DUT's Type-4 route carries ES-Import RT with 6-octet value = 01:11:22:33:44:55 (high-order 6 of ESI); 3rd-party PE imports it without errors. 3rd-party PE's Type-4 route also encodes ES-Import RT per RFC7432bis ch.7.6; DUT imports it. Both sides' `show bgp evpn ethernet-segment` lists the remote ES.
Fail-on: ES-Import RT value ≠ high-order 6 octets of ESI, extended community missing/malformed in Type-4 route, 3rd-party PE rejects DUT's route ("unsupported encoding"), DUT rejects 3rd-party route, or Ethernet Segment not established on either side.

aeb07e786472a8c5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (PE1, PE2) multi-homed to the same ES via `agg-eth 0`; on both, configure `ethernet-segment` with `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (same ESI) and `load-balancing-mode all-active`. Verify both PEs advertise ESI label in Ethernet A-D per ES routes. Configure IXIA to send L2 broadcast/multicast traffic from CE through PE1 into the EVI.
Action: PE1 (non-DF for the EVI) receives BUM from CE and replicates to PE2 via ingress replication. Capture the replicated packet on the wire between PE1→PE2 to confirm ESI label is present in the MPLS label stack. Observe PE2's forwarding decision upon receiving the labeled BUM packet.
Verify: Use `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show mpls forwarding-table` on PE2 to confirm ESI label is programmed in platform label space. Verify PE2 does NOT forward the received BUM packet back onto `agg-eth 0` (the shared ES). Confirm BUM is forwarded to local CEs on other interfaces. Capture traffic on the ES link to confirm zero BUM packets with the ESI label exit toward the CE.
aeb07e786472a8c5.expectation:
Pass:    PE2's forwarding entry for its advertised ESI label results in NOT forwarding onto the ES (RFC7432bis ch.8.3.1.1 split-horizon enforcement). BUM packet from PE1→PE2 includes the ESI label. No BUM loop observed on the shared ES.
Fail-on: PE2 forwards BUM back onto `agg-eth 0` creating a loop, OR ESI label missing in PE1→PE2 replicated BUM, OR ESI label not programmed in PE2's platform label space, OR split-horizon fails and BUM exits the ES.

aeb399235616895f.action_steps:
Setup:  Create EVPN instance `evpn EVPN1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` with RD/RTs; create `interface agg-eth 1` and set `lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55`; bind agg-eth 1 to EVPN1; commit successfully.
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1`, issue `lacp-system-mac 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE`, then `commit`.
Verify: Commit fails with error 'Cannot change EVPN parameters of already attached to EVPN interface'; `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` confirms MAC unchanged at `00:11:22:33:44:55`; unbind from EVPN1, retry command, commit succeeds.
aeb399235616895f.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
aeb399235616895f.expectation:
Pass:    Commit rejected with exact error message naming the violated constraint ('already attached to EVPN'); lacp-system-mac preserved; unbinding interface permits change; `no lacp-system-mac` restores LAG MAC default.
Fail-on: Change accepted while interface bound to EVPN, OR error message missing/ambiguous, OR running-config shows mismatched state, OR unbind-then-retry still blocked.

aebb40eb2b031571.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE share ESI on agg-eth 0, load-balancing-mode single-active. Configure service-carving highest-preference preference 100 on DUT (primary DF), service-carving lowest-preference on neighbor (backup). IXIA acts as remote PE receiving routes and generates traffic via multi-homed CE.
Action: Verify aliasing: IXIA receives Type-1 per-ES routes from both DUT and neighbor. Identify EVPN control-plane process via `show platform process | include evpn`, reset it on DUT. Capture IXIA traffic loss during failover.
Verify: Pre-reset: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms DUT=DF, neighbor=non-DF. During reset: neighbor activates as DF. IXIA traffic loss ≤1s. Post-reset: DUT process restarts ≤5s, resumes primary DF role ≤30s. `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms route re-advertisement. Only one PE forwards at any time (single-active).
aebb40eb2b031571.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA (remote PE and traffic generator)
aebb40eb2b031571.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise Type-1 per-ES routes (aliasing confirmed). During primary DF failure: backup activates ≤1s, traffic loss ≤1s, process restarts ≤5s. DF roles return to DUT=primary/neighbor=backup ≤30s. Single-active enforced: only one DF forwards traffic throughout.
Fail-on: Missing Type-1 routes from either PE, both PEs forward simultaneously (single-active violation), traffic loss >1s, no failover, process restart >5s, or DF roles incorrect after recovery.

aebb7bbc4030a024.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT with `ethernet-segment` on agg-eth interface using ESI Type 1 (01:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:00:01), ESI Type 0 on another interface (00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00); configure 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) with ESI Type 4 (04:RouterID:00:00:00:01) and ESI Type 5 (05:AS:00:00:00:02); establish MP-BGP EVPN session; configure matching EVPN instance + `auto-discovery` + `import-rt`/`export-rt` on both sides.
Action: Bring up ethernet-segments on both PEs; exchange Ethernet A-D routes (Type 1 NLRI per RFC7432bis §7.1); cycle through ESI Types 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 on test interfaces to verify all valid types exchanged; generate bidirectional L2 traffic via IXIA between CE devices.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 1` on DUT confirms A-D routes received with ESI Type field = 0x00..0x05 (first octet); `show ethernet-segment` displays remote ESI Type correctly; `show evpn instance` shows routes installed; IXIA reports bidirectional frame forwarding with zero loss; no BGP notifications in `show logging | include BGP`.
aebb7bbc4030a024.expectation:
Pass:    DUT and 3rd-party PE both accept all A-D routes with ESI Type 0–5; ESI field decoded correctly (Type displayed matches configured Type 0x00..0x05); routes installed in EVPN table; IXIA confirms bidirectional L2 forwarding.
Fail-on: A-D route rejected when ESI Type ∈ {0,1,2,3,4,5}; ESI Type field misinterpreted (wrong Type shown in `show ethernet-segment`); data plane black-hole or one-way forwarding; BGP session flaps or logs route-malformed error for valid ESI Type.

aed0d6f3244bc608.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure inbound soft reconfiguration under the EVPN address-family for a BGP neighbor on the DUT; establish peering with neighbor PE; verify neighbor sends ≥50 Type-2 MAC/IP routes; confirm routes cached via neighbor detail commands.
Action: Remove soft reconfiguration via `no inbound-soft-reconfiguration` in neighbor EVPN address-family context; commit configuration.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms `inbound-soft-reconfiguration` absent for this neighbor; `show bgp evpn neighbor <peer-ip>` indicates soft-reconfig disabled; session uptime unchanged (no reset); memory usage reduced.
aed0d6f3244bc608.expectation:
Pass:    Configuration directive removed; neighbor soft-reconfig state cleared; BGP session stable; NLRI cache memory freed
Fail-on: Directive persists in running-config, soft-reconfig flag remains set, session resets, or memory not released

aef6dbb5f003780a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE in All-Active mode on shared ES (identifier 0 xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx, load-balancing-mode all-active); enable ingress replication; verify both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes with downstream-assigned ESI labels; confirm DUT programs ESI label in platform label space with anti-loop forwarding entry (drops packets from label, does not forward to source ES).
Action: IXIA sends BUM traffic into EVI through DUT (non-DF); verify DUT includes DF's ESI label in replicated copies to DF; power-cycle DUT using PDU harness; wait for full boot and BGP EVPN re-establishment.
Verify: Show BGP EVPN routes for Ethernet A-D per ES (DUT re-advertises ESI label); show forwarding table (ESI label entry restored with anti-loop action); IXIA resumes BUM traffic—confirm DUT includes ESI label in copies to DF, packets received by DUT with its own ESI label are NOT forwarded to source ES (no loop), all-active service-carving operational.
aef6dbb5f003780a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (All-Active multi-homing) + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
aef6dbb5f003780a.expectation:
Pass:    DUT re-advertises ESI label in Ethernet A-D per ES routes within es-waiting-time (default 3 s); ESI label forwarding entry restored (drops/does-not-forward to source ES); BUM traffic forwarding resumes with correct ESI label inclusion (to DF) and exclusion (to non-ES peers); no traffic loop or black-hole.
Fail-on: ESI label missing from routes after recovery, ESI label forwarding entry incorrect (permits loop-back to source ES), BUM traffic loops or drops, BGP EVPN session fails, All-Active redundancy mode not restored, or service-carving non-operational.

af213aba07244707.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology with vlan-based service. On PE1, under `configuration / l2-services / evpn <evpn-name>`, configure `mac-address-static <mac-address> interface <interface>` (bind a static MAC to a local interface, e.g., x-eth 0/0/1). Verify the MAC appears in PE1's local table and PE2 receives the Type-2 route.
Action: Run `tech-support` on PE1. Save the bundle to external storage, extract the archive, and inspect contents.
Verify: Confirm bundle includes: (1) `show evpn mac address-table` with the configured MAC listed as type 'static' bound to the specified interface; (2) `show running-config` section with the `mac-address-static <mac-address> interface <interface>` command under the EVI; (3) BGP L2VPN EVPN routing table showing the Type-2 MAC/IP Advertisement route for the static MAC; (4) kernel forwarding tables with an entry for the static MAC pointing to the configured interface.
af213aba07244707.expectation:
Pass:    Bundle contains all four pieces of evidence: static MAC entry in address-table, configuration line, BGP Type-2 route advertisement, and forwarding plane entry.
Fail-on: Missing any evidence: static MAC not in MAC table, config command absent from running-config, no BGP Type-2 route, or FIB lacks the static MAC entry.

af36110f91b27532.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (defaults); attach CE-facing interface to Ethernet Tag 100; IXIA emulates CE; neighbor PE joined to same EVI.
Action: IXIA injects malformed L2 frames from CE: (a) source MAC = multicast address (01:00:5e:00:00:01), (b) source MAC = all-zeros (00:00:00:00:00:00), (c) undersized frame (48 bytes), (d) oversized frame (MTU + 100 bytes), (e) frame with bad FCS, (f) unknown destination MAC with valid source MAC but reserved Ethernet Tag value (0xFFFFFFFF).
Verify: `show l2-services evpn mac-table` (no invalid source MACs learned), `show interface <ce-port> statistics` (error counters: fcs-error, runt, oversized incremented per variant), `show log | match drop` (per-variant syslog entries), neighbor PE sniff (zero frames received for malformed traffic).
af36110f91b27532.expectation:
Pass:    All six malformed variants dropped at DUT ingress before source MAC lookup; error counters (fcs-error, runt, oversized, invalid-source-mac) increment by ≥1 per variant type; zero MAC table entries for multicast/zero source MACs; syslog records ≥6 drop events with frame type; neighbor PE receives zero frames for all six variants.
Fail-on: Any malformed frame accepted and source MAC learned in `show l2-services evpn mac-table`; malformed frame flooded to neighbor PE; DUT crash or hang; error counters remain zero; missing syslog drop events; valid-source-MAC + reserved-Etag frame propagated to BGP peer.

af43bb963243a02e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-count 5 freeze-time 300` and `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface <CE-if>` in EVPN. Configure multi-homed ES on agg-eth 0; configure neighbor PE with same ES but different DF algorithm.
Action: Trigger alarms: (1) bring up ES to trigger ES-misconfiguration; (2-3) move MAC 00:22:33:44:55:66 between PEs exceeding move-count to trigger temp-freeze then permanent-freeze; (4) advertise 00:11:22:33:44:55 from neighbor PE to trigger static-MAC-relearning. Collect tech-support.
Verify: Tech-support syslog contains: (a) ES-misconfiguration showing ESI, agg-eth N, peer-IP, LACP System MAC, DF algorithm; (b) temp-MAC-freeze for 00:22:33:44:55:66 (RFC7432bis ch.15.1); (c) permanent-MAC-freeze (RFC7432bis ch.15.1); (d) static-MAC-relearning for 00:11:22:33:44:55 (RFC7432bis ch.15.2). Running-config shows MAC-dup and static-MAC config. Show output for EVPN ES, MAC table, dup-detection state present.
af43bb963243a02e.expectation:
Pass:    All 4 alarms in syslog with required fields; `show running-config` includes `host mac-address-duplicate-detection` and `mac-address-static`; show output for ES, MAC table, dup-detection present.
Fail-on: Any alarm missing, alarm missing required fields (ES-misconfig: ESI/peer-IP/LACP-System-MAC/DF-algorithm; MAC-dup/static-relearn: MAC-address), or missing running-config or show output.

af511f4e58e3e2ea.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'evpn100' with 'service-type vlan-based' and normalized VLAN-ID 100. Add x-eth 0/0/1 with ingress VLAN-ID mapping (accept VLAN 50 → normalize to 100) and x-eth 0/0/2 with VLAN 100. Configure 'auto-discovery' with 'import-rt 100:100' and 'export-rt 100:100'. Connect IXIA Tx to x-eth 0/0/1, IXIA Rx to x-eth 0/0/2.
Action: IXIA sends 1000 frames tagged VLAN 50 to x-eth 0/0/1. DUT must replace incoming VLAN 50 with configured VLAN-ID 100 to match the EVPN instance normalized VLAN.
Verify: Capture all egress frames at IXIA Rx port. Run 'show evpn evpn100' and 'show l2-services evpn evpn100' to confirm VLAN mapping and forwarding counters.
af511f4e58e3e2ea.expectation:
Pass:    All 1000 captured egress frames carry VLAN tag 100 (original VLAN 50 replaced); 'show evpn' displays active VLAN normalization; frame count matches (1000 Tx = 1000 Rx); no drops.
Fail-on: Any egress frame retains original VLAN 50, or VLAN replacement does not occur, or frame loss > 0, or 'show evpn' output shows no VLAN mapping applied.

af6ec0b8d572d5a6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with two iBGP neighbors (A and B) in the same AS; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on both; configure `route-reflector-client` under neighbor A's af-l2vpn evpn mode only; create a vlan-based EVPN instance on neighbor B to originate EVPN routes.
Action: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`; run `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <A-ip> advertised-routes`; capture or inspect BGP UPDATEs sent to neighbor A to examine path attributes.
Verify: Neighbor A receives EVPN routes originated by B; BGP UPDATEs to A contain ORIGINATOR_ID (set to B's router-id) and CLUSTER_LIST (containing DUT's cluster-id) path attributes; `show running-config` shows `route-reflector-client` only under neighbor A.
af6ec0b8d572d5a6.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (iBGP route reflector topology)
af6ec0b8d572d5a6.expectation:
Pass:    Client neighbor A receives reflected routes with ORIGINATOR_ID and CLUSTER_LIST attributes per RFC 4456; non-client neighbor B does not receive routes from peer A.
Fail-on: Reflected routes lack ORIGINATOR_ID or CLUSTER_LIST attributes; non-client peer receives reflected routes; client does not receive routes it should.

af78d1a8d9aca9e0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP peering between DUT and neighbor PE with L2VPN EVPN address family enabled. On DUT under `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn`, configure `maximum-prefix 5`.
Action: Neighbor PE advertises 5 EVPN Type-2 MAC/IP routes (DUT accepts). Neighbor then advertises a 6th route. Repeat test with `warning-only` modifier configured: neighbor advertises 6+ routes. Repeat test with `restart <interval>` modifier (e.g., 2 minutes): neighbor advertises 6+ routes, then wait for the restart interval to elapse.
Verify: Default mode: `show ip bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows neighbor session in Idle state after 6th prefix; `tcpdump` or BGP debug logs capture NOTIFICATION message with code 6 sub-code 1. With `warning-only`: session remains Established in BGP summary; syslog contains maximum-prefix threshold warning. With `restart`: session tears down, then BGP summary shows session re-established after the configured interval.
af78d1a8d9aca9e0.expectation:
Pass:    Default: session transitions to Idle when 6th prefix arrives, NOTIFICATION 6/1 sent; `warning-only`: session stays Established, syslog warning logged; `restart <interval>`: session tears down then auto-recovers after interval minutes
Fail-on: Session accepts >5 prefixes without tearing down (default mode), NOTIFICATION with wrong code/sub-code, `warning-only` tears down the session, `restart` fails to bring session back up after interval

af8c39f0f712f7cf.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT + neighbor PE with advertise-mac enable configured; IXIA establishes two iBGP EVPN sessions to DUT, each injecting 10,000 identical MAC routes (RT-2) with different local-pref (session-1=200, session-2=100) so DUT selects session-1 per RFC7432bis ch.7.13.
Action: Withdraw IXIA session-1 routes; capture DUT syslog and IXIA timestamps to measure reconvergence time to session-2; restore session-1 and measure return; inject 50,000 RT-2 routes (2 paths each) from IXIA at increasing rate to measure max best-path processing throughput.
Verify: show bgp evpn route-type 2 detail confirms best-path per RFC7432bis ch.7.13 tie-breakers (local-pref → AS_PATH → origin → MED → eBGP/iBGP → IGP cost → router-ID); show bgp evpn summary for route counts; debug bgp evpn updates for convergence events.
af8c39f0f712f7cf.expectation:
Pass:    Reconvergence ≤ 5 seconds (syslog timestamp delta); sustained processing ≥ 1,000 routes/sec with correct best-path selection per RFC7432bis ch.7.13; all routes converge to correct path (session-1 preferred, session-2 on failover).
Fail-on: Convergence > 5s or processing < 1,000 routes/sec or incorrect best-path (session-2 chosen while session-1 available with higher local-pref) or routes stuck in pending state or memory/CPU exhaustion.

aff4726632246442.action_steps:
Setup:  Under configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment, configure `es-waiting-time 10` (non-default; default is 3 seconds), commit, and save the running-config.
Action: Reload the DUT (warm reboot); wait for full boot and ES feature initialization.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth <id> ethernet-segment` displays `es-waiting-time 10`; then execute `no es-waiting-time` and verify `show running-config` confirms restoration to default (line removed or implicit 3 seconds).
aff4726632246442.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
aff4726632246442.expectation:
Pass:    Non-default `es-waiting-time 10` persists byte-identical in running-config post-reload; `no es-waiting-time` removes the line and ES operates with implicit 3-second default.
Fail-on: Value reverts to 3 seconds after reload, config line is lost, `no` form fails to restore default, or ES feature requires manual re-activation of the timer.

aff6fec1f8202f58.action_steps:
Setup:  Active-active multi-homing: DUT and neighbor PE with matching ESI configured on `agg-eth 0`, both connected to CE in LAG; EVPN instance service-type vlan-based with VLANs 10, 20, 30, 40; IXIA as CE traffic generator.
Action: On DUT, enter ethernet-segment context under `configure / interface agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment`, issue `service-carving` command, commit; replicate on neighbor PE; verify DF election via `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both; send tagged traffic VLANs 10-40 from IXIA; observe forwarding via `show evpn mac` on both PEs.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms DF election per VLAN with designated forwarders; each VLAN forwarded by exactly one PE (service-carving effect visible); `show running-config` contains service-carving line; reload DUT, verify config persists and DF re-election completes; test `no service-carving`, confirm default carving method restores.
aff6fec1f8202f58.expectation:
Pass:    service-carving command accepted and committed; DF election completes per-VLAN; traffic forwarded by correct DF (no duplicate delivery to network); config persists across reload; `no service-carving` successfully reverts to default carving method, DF re-election clean, no traffic loss.
Fail-on: service-carving command rejected; DF election fails or incomplete; duplicate forwarding (both PEs deliver same VLAN traffic); traffic drops; config lost or corrupted on reload; `no service-carving` fails or disrupts service.

b00a5691187ff851.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: PE1, PE2, PE3 all advertising the same 1000 MAC/IP routes (type 2) via BGP EVPN (af-l2vpn evpn); PE1 advertises with Default Gateway extended community (0x030c), PE2 without DG community, PE3 local to DUT; IXIA generates 1 Gbps traffic to these MAC destinations.
Action: Withdraw PE1 routes (DG community) to force best path re-selection per RFC7432bis §7.13.1 ordered criteria; measure convergence time from BGP UPDATE to forwarding plane switch; repeat with PE3 local route withdrawal to measure DG vs non-DG selection latency under 1000-route scale.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 <MAC/IP>` confirms best path follows RFC order (DG-community route preferred, then local route if multiple DG); capture convergence time via timestamped syslog + IXIA packet loss counters; measure CPU/memory during path re-evaluation.
b00a5691187ff851.expectation:
Pass:    Best path selection respects RFC7432bis §7.13.1 criteria order (DG community → local preference); convergence time PE1→PE2 failover ≤ 200 ms; packet loss during convergence ≤ 0.1%; CPU ≤ 80%, memory delta ≤ 50 MB during 1000-route re-selection.
Fail-on: Wrong best path chosen (violates RFC ordering, e.g., non-DG route selected while DG route available); convergence > 200 ms; packet loss > 0.1%; CPU > 80% or memory spike > 50 MB.

b0111f9863cfe619.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance 100, enable `advertise-mac enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`. Configure BGP neighbors PE2 and PE3 with `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor`. Both PE2 and PE3 advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI route for ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, EVI-tag 100. PE2 includes EVPN Layer 2 Attributes extended community (P=0, B=1), PE3 omits Layer 2 Attributes.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN sessions. Run `show bgp evpn route-type 1 esi 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` to verify DUT receives both Type-1 routes. Run `show bgp evpn best-path` to identify selected route. Send IXIA traffic to EVI 100 MAC behind multi-homed ESI. Capture forwarding next-hop. Then modify PE3 to include Layer 2 Attributes (P=1, B=0), wait for route refresh, and re-check best-path.
Verify: Initial state: DUT best-path is PE2 route (Layer 2 Attributes present). PE3 route is not eligible per criterion 1. IXIA traffic egresses toward PE2. After PE3 update: DUT applies criterion 2 (P/B flags per RFC8214 §8.4) to choose between PE2 and PE3. Best-path may change based on P/B comparison. Traffic follows updated path.
b0111f9863cfe619.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + two neighbor PEs (PE2, PE3) multi-homed to same Ethernet Segment
b0111f9863cfe619.expectation:
Pass:    (1) With PE2 having Layer 2 Attributes and PE3 not, DUT selects PE2 as best-path (criterion 1 removes PE3 from consideration). (2) After PE3 adds Layer 2 Attributes, DUT re-evaluates both routes using criterion 2 (P/B flags), and best-path reflects correct P/B flag comparison per RFC8214. (3) Traffic consistently follows best-path.
Fail-on: (1) DUT selects PE3 route when only PE2 has Layer 2 Attributes (violates criterion order: Layer 2 Attributes filtering MUST be applied first). (2) No best-path update after PE3 adds attributes. (3) Traffic does not match best-path selection. (4) Both routes marked as best-path simultaneously.

b020de00eed7d05b.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs with multi-homed CE; configure ES (ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11) on both PEs belonging to two EVPN instances (EVI 100 with export-rt 65000:100, EVI 200 with export-rt 65000:200) using `configuration/l2-services/vpls/auto-discovery` context.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN peering (`af-l2vpn evpn` under `routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor`); capture type 4 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes on PE↔PE link using packet capture or `show bgp evpn route-type 4`; parse MP_REACH_NLRI and Extended Communities attributes.
Verify: Decode each type 4 route's Extended Communities path attribute; confirm ≥1 Route Target EC (type 0x00/0x02) present per route; union all RTs across type 4 routes for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11.
b020de00eed7d05b.expectation:
Pass:    Each type 4 route carries ≥1 RT extended community (type code 0x00/0x02); union of RTs across all type 4 routes for the ES = {65000:100, 65000:200} (complete set from both EVIs).
Fail-on: Any type 4 route with zero RT extended communities, or union missing RT 65000:100 or 65000:200 (incomplete EVI coverage).

b04186be03fa6e02.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure aggregate-ethernet 0/1/1; enter `configuration / interface / aggregate-ethernet 0/1/1 / ethernet-segment`; set ESI `00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99`; commit.
Action: Execute `load-balancing-mode single-active`; commit; capture running-config. Change to `load-balancing-mode all-active`; commit; capture running-config. Execute `no load-balancing-mode`; commit; capture running-config and verify default restored.
Verify: `show running-config interface aggregate-ethernet 0/1/1 ethernet-segment` displays only the last-configured mode (no stacking). `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` DF-election field matches the configured mode. After `no load-balancing-mode`, both outputs show `single-active` (default).
b04186be03fa6e02.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
b04186be03fa6e02.expectation:
Pass:    Each commit replaces prior mode cleanly; running-config shows only one mode at any time; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` DF-election field transitions `single-active` → `all-active` → `single-active` (after `no` form); no commit warnings.
Fail-on: Both `single-active` and `all-active` appear in running-config simultaneously; operational state (DF-election in show output) lags or contradicts running-config; `no load-balancing-mode` does not restore `single-active`; commit rejects valid mode or produces error.

b06e12e4d2117a1d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure three PEs (PE1 IP 10.0.0.1, PE2 10.0.0.2, PE3 10.0.0.3) multi-homed to ES1 via `ethernet-segment` under `interface agg-eth`. Set Highest-Preference algorithm in DF Election Extended Community. Configure PE1 Preference=200 with Don't Preempt enabled, PE2 Preference=250 (no Don't Preempt), PE3 Preference=100. Verify PE1 is current DF (Don't Preempt wins per RFC9785 §4.1 tie-breaking). IXIA sends bidirectional L2 traffic into ES1.
Action: Kill EVPN control-plane process on PE2 (highest-Preference standby) using platform debug command (e.g., `debug process kill evpn-mgr` or equivalent).
Verify: PE2 process auto-restarts. Run `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` on all PEs to confirm PE1 remains DF (Don't Preempt prevents PE2 from preempting despite higher Preference). Measure IXIA traffic loss duration.
b06e12e4d2117a1d.equipment: DUT + 2x neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen (multi-homed ES tie-breaking requires ≥3 PEs; process-kill requires lab control of standby PE)
b06e12e4d2117a1d.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 process restarts within vendor-documented bound (typically ≤5s). PE1 remains DF (Don't Preempt capability takes precedence over Preference value per RFC9785 §4.1). Traffic interruption ≤ documented HA convergence bound (typically ≤1s). ES state (DF role, route-type 4 advertisements) converges consistently on all three PEs.
Fail-on: Process does not restart, PE2 preempts PE1 as DF (violates MUST in §4.1), any PE elects wrong DF or shows inconsistent ES state, or traffic loss > documented bound.

b07163ab7ff6ae87.action_steps:
Problem: Verify that split-horizon operational state (shared ES, ESI Labels, BUM filtering) is captured in tech-support for troubleshooting.
Method: Exercise split-horizon with BUM traffic on a shared ES, run tech-support on PE1, and confirm the bundle includes ES configuration, ESI Label route-type 4 advertisements, and BUM split-horizon table entries.
Setup: Two PEs (PE1, PE2) share an ES to one CE; ESI configured on agg-eth 0 with LACP; EVI 100 active; ingress-replication tunnel established. FLOW-060 exercised through Basic Functionality and at least one Packet validation row (confirming split-horizon blocks BUM traffic from being forwarded back to the shared ES).
Action: Run `tech-support` on PE1. Save the bundle to an external location. Extract and inspect the bundle contents for split-horizon operational evidence.
Verify: Bundle contains `show evpn ethernet-segment` output with the shared ESI value, DF/NDF election result, and LACP state. Bundle contains `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output with ESI Label Extended Community (route-type 4) advertisements for the shared ES. Bundle contains BUM split-horizon table or kernel forwarding plane entries showing the shared ES is tracked for split-horizon filtering. Bundle contains `show running-config` with `advertise-mac enable` under `l2-services / evpn`, complete EVI 100 configuration, and any syslog or alarm entries related to ES or split-horizon operation.
b07163ab7ff6ae87.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
b07163ab7ff6ae87.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle includes `show evpn ethernet-segment` output with shared ESI and DF election state, `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output with ESI Label Extended Community (route-type 4) for the shared ES, BUM split-horizon table entries marking the shared ES for filtering, and `show running-config` showing `advertise-mac enable` under `l2-services / evpn`.
Fail-on: Missing ES state, missing ESI Label route-type 4 in BGP output, missing BUM split-horizon table entries for the shared ES, or running-config showing `advertise-mac disable` or missing EVPN configuration.

b0bcb329967919db.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with matching ESI: `interface agg-eth 1; ethernet-segment; identifier 0 01.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88; load-balancing-mode all-active`. Both attach agg-eth to MAC-VRF 100. Set `l2-services; evpn; es-waiting-time 3`. IXIA CE dual-homed to both PEs (LAG members), sending 1 Gbps bidirectional L2 traffic.
Action: Shut down DUT's agg-eth 1 interface (`shutdown`). IXIA measures traffic loss duration and frame count.
Verify: Neighbor PE receives Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) withdrawal from DUT. `show evpn ethernet-segment` on neighbor PE shows ES up with 1 PE. `show bgp l2vpn evpn routes` confirms route withdrawal. IXIA reports convergence time.
b0bcb329967919db.expectation:
Pass:    Traffic loss ≤ 1 second; Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) withdrawal sent by DUT and processed by neighbor PE within es-waiting-time (3 sec); traffic fully reconverged to neighbor PE with zero ongoing packet loss.
Fail-on: Traffic loss > 3 seconds, no Ethernet A-D per ES route withdrawal in BGP updates, ES state on neighbor PE not updated to single-PE, or persistent traffic loss after es-waiting-time expires.

b0cab9983cdc8713.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance for VLAN 100 with agg-eth attachment circuit. Configure ethernet-segment: identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09 (type 0 explicit), load-balancing-mode single-active (default), es-waiting-time 3 seconds (default, global EVPN setting). Establish BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE. Learn ≥10 local MAC entries (IXIA sending from CE on attachment circuit, VLAN 100) and ≥10 remote MAC entries (via BGP EVPN Type-2 routes from neighbor PE with MPLS label). Verify MACT populated: entries contain VLAN-ID=100 (key), MAC Address (key), ESI, Next Hop type (Local=attachment circuit ifIndex for local MACs, Remote=neighbor PE IP single-active for remote MACs).
Action: Power-cycle DUT via PDU harness; wait for full boot, interface up, BGP EVPN session re-establishment.
Verify: Issue show mac-address-table vlan 100, show ethernet-segment, show bgp evpn routes. Confirm: (1) all pre-cycle MACT entries restored with correct VLAN-ID, MAC Address, ESI, Next Hop types; (2) ethernet-segment config intact (identifier 0 type0-value 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09, load-balancing-mode single-active, es-waiting-time 3 seconds); (3) BGP EVPN session up; (4) send bidirectional unicast traffic to known MACs—verify local→remote forwarding (MPLS encap) and remote→local forwarding (decap to attachment circuit).
b0cab9983cdc8713.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + neighbor PE + power-cycle harness
b0cab9983cdc8713.expectation:
Pass:    MACT fully restored (all pre-cycle entries: VLAN-ID=100, MAC addresses, ESI, Next Hop types Local/Remote correct); ethernet-segment config preserved (identifier 0 type0-value 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09, load-balancing-mode single-active, es-waiting-time 3s); BGP EVPN session re-establishes; bidirectional MAC unicast forwarding operational for both Local Next Hop (attachment circuit ifIndex) and Remote Next Hop (neighbor PE IP, single-active) entries; zero packet loss after convergence (<10s from BGP up).
Fail-on: Any MACT entry missing or corrupted (VLAN-ID, MAC Address, ESI, or Next Hop type/value incorrect), ethernet-segment config lost or changed (identifier type/value, load-balancing-mode not single-active, es-waiting-time ≠3s), BGP EVPN session down, or traffic drop/blackhole (>0.1% loss after convergence) for any known unicast MAC.

b12d974786d98ea2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 100 with BD 200 on DUT and neighbor PE. Configure ES with ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 multi-homed between both PEs; verify DF election completes via `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11`. Enable `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in `configuration/l2-services/evpn` and BGP EVPN peering with `af-l2vpn evpn`.
Action: Generate unknown-destination MAC BUM traffic from IXIA on the ES interface. Capture BGP EVPN IMET (type-3) route advertisements and MPLS-encapsulated BUM packets between PEs.
Verify: Check `show evpn instance 100 mpls labels` for LT3 label allocation for BD 200. Check `show bgp evpn route-type imet` for IMET route with PMSI Tunnel attribute containing the LT3 label. Capture MPLS packets to confirm LT3 label presence when the non-DF PE forwards BUM to the DF PE. Verify `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11` shows stable DF status.
b12d974786d98ea2.expectation:
Pass:    LT3 label allocated and visible in `show evpn instance 100 mpls labels`. IMET route advertises PMSI Tunnel attribute with matching LT3 label value. Packet capture confirms MPLS label stack includes LT3 label when non-DF forwards BUM (per RFC7432bis ch.7.3 rule: add label if remote PE is not DF on input ES). DF election status unchanged. BUM traffic delivered to non-ES interfaces and DF ES interfaces only.
Fail-on: No LT3 label in `show evpn instance 100 mpls labels`. IMET route missing PMSI Tunnel attribute or label mismatch. BUM forwarding omits LT3 label in MPLS encapsulation when required. DF status flaps in `show evpn ethernet-segment`. BUM delivered to non-DF ES interfaces or dropped at valid destinations.

b139218036b5fc42.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT interface agg-eth-1 with "ethernet-segment / identifier 0 01.23.45.67.89.ab.cd.ef.01 / load-balancing-mode single-active / service-carving default". Configure EVPN instance EVI-100 with "es-waiting-time 3". Establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE (10.0.0.2). Verify "show evpn ethernet-segment" reports DF=true for EVI-100.
Action: Execute "clear bgp neighbor 10.0.0.2 evpn" to trigger BGP convergence. Simultaneously, neighbor PE injects 1000 pps unknown unicast traffic (dst MAC=00:99:99:99:99:99, not in DUT FDB) MPLS-encapsulated with P-tunnel downstream label advertised in IMET route type 3. Continue traffic for 60 seconds after BGP Established state returns.
Verify: "show bgp evpn summary" (10.0.0.2 state=Established), "show evpn ethernet-segment" (agg-eth-1 DF=true, service-carving=default), "show l2-services forwarding-database evi 100" (MAC 00:99:99:99:99:99 absent), IXIA counter on agg-eth-1: ≥ 60,000 BUM packets received, "show interface agg-eth-1 statistics" (TX packets ≥ 60,000, errors=0).
b139218036b5fc42.expectation:
Pass:    ≥ 60,000 BUM packets forwarded to agg-eth-1 (≤ 1% loss tolerated for convergence transient), no MAC lookup performed (00:99:99:99:99:99 remains absent from FDB, confirming RFC §13.2.1 default: "not required to perform destination MAC address lookup"), DF role=true throughout, BGP session recovers to Established within 30 seconds.
Fail-on: BUM packet loss > 1%, DUT adds MAC 00:99:99:99:99:99 to FDB (violates default no-lookup behavior), DF role changes to false or flaps, BGP session stuck in Active/Connect > 60 seconds, BUM traffic looped back to neighbor PE (split-horizon violation per RFC §8.3).

b1546b685af7bc3b.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that VLAN filtering in vlan-aware-bundle service correctly drops BUM frames with VLAN IDs outside the configured bundle, even when another EVPN instance is active on the same DUT.
Method: Configure a vlan-aware-bundle for VLANs 100-103, establish a second EVPN instance (vlan-based) as the neighbor feature, send BUM traffic with both in-range (VLAN 100) and out-of-range (VLAN 200) VLAN IDs from IXIA, and verify only in-range frames are forwarded to PE2 while out-of-range frames are dropped at PE1.

Setup:
1. Two-PE topology (PE1, PE2) over MPLS; BGP EVPN session established between PEs.
2. On PE1, configure a second EVPN instance using `evpn neighbor-evi service-type vlan-based` on a separate port; verify baseline operation with `show evpn evi neighbor-evi`.
3. On PE1 interface x-eth 0/0/1, configure `evpn evi-bundle service-type vlan-aware-bundle` and bind VLANs 100-103 to the bundle; commit and verify `show evpn evi evi-bundle` shows operational.

Action:
1. From IXIA connected to PE1 x-eth 0/0/1, send broadcast frames (destination MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) with VLAN 100 (in-range) at 10 Mbps and VLAN 200 (out-of-range) at 10 Mbps simultaneously.
2. On PE2 access port, capture all received frames for 60 seconds.
3. Query `show evpn mac-table evi evi-bundle` on both PE1 and PE2.

Verify:
1. PE2 receives broadcast frames tagged VLAN 100; packet capture confirms zero frames with VLAN 200.
2. `show evpn mac-table evi evi-bundle` shows MAC entries for VLAN 100 only; no entries for VLAN 200.
3. PE1 statistics (`show interface x-eth 0/0/1`) show ingress frames for both VLANs but egress/forwarded frames only for VLAN 100.
4. Neighbor EVPN instance `show evpn evi neighbor-evi` output and counters unchanged from baseline.
b1546b685af7bc3b.expectation:
Pass:    BUM frames with VLAN 100 forwarded to PE2 and MAC learning occurs; VLAN 200 frames dropped at PE1 ingress (zero frames reach PE2); neighbor EVPN instance operates without errors.
Fail-on: VLAN 200 frames appear at PE2 or in PE2 MAC table, or VLAN 100 frames are incorrectly dropped, or neighbor EVPN instance shows new errors/counter changes, or PE1 reports alarms.

b195ca822c86976c.action_steps:
Setup:  On DUT, configure EVPN instance evpn1 with `service-type vlan-based`; create Ethernet Segment with ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 on interface ge-0/0/1; enable `auto-discovery`; configure `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`; establish BGP session to neighbor PE 192.168.1.2 with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled; commit and activate.
Action: Bring up ge-0/0/1 and BGP session; wait for BGP convergence; trigger type-1 route advertisement by activating the ES.
Verify: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11`; confirm route contains RD (DUT's RD), ESI=01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, Ethernet Tag ID=0xFFFFFFFF (MAX-ET), MPLS Label=0, ESI Label Extended Community with label value; Next Hop=DUT PE IP; run `show alarms` to confirm no ESI label mismatch alarms.
