b195ca822c86976c.expectation:
Pass:    Type-1 route advertised with RD matching DUT config, ESI=01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, ET ID=0xFFFFFFFF, MPLS Label=0, ESI Label Extended Community present with valid label value (16-1048575); Next Hop=DUT PE loopback IP; zero alarms.
Fail-on: No type-1 route for configured ES; required field (RD/ESI/ET ID/ESI Label Extended Community) missing or malformed; ET ID != 0xFFFFFFFF; MPLS Label != 0; Next Hop != DUT PE IP; spurious ESI label mismatch alarm.

b1f800ac0be00445.action_steps:
Setup:  Connect Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE to same Ethernet segment with ESI Type-0: 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 (All-Active redundancy); on DUT configure `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100` under `l2-services/vpls/auto-discovery`; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` neighbor to 3rd-party PE; attach CEs via IXIA to both PEs.
Action: Bring up BGP EVPN session; DUT advertises Ethernet Auto-discovery route (Type 1) with ESI Label Extended Community; capture BGP UPDATE on link between DUT and 3rd-party PE; inject traffic from IXIA CE1 (attached to DUT) toward CE2 (attached to 3rd-party PE on same ESI).
Verify: Decode captured BGP UPDATE ESI Label Extended Community: Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01, Flags octet RED bits[1:0]=00, ESI Label 3-octet field with 20-bit non-zero label; on Cisco use `show bgp l2vpn evpn` / on Juniper `show route table bgp.evpn.0` to confirm route installed with ESI Label; verify split-horizon: IXIA CE1 traffic does NOT egress CE2 on same ESI (captured or IXIA Rx counter = 0).
b1f800ac0be00445.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Label Extended Community Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01, Flags RED=00 (All-Active), ESI Label non-zero per RFC7432bis §7.5; 3rd-party PE accepts route, visible in `show bgp l2vpn evpn`; split-horizon active (CE1→CE2 same-ESI traffic blocked); traffic to different ESI forwards correctly.
Fail-on: Type≠0x06 or Sub-Type≠0x01; Flags RED≠00; ESI Label field zero or malformed; 3rd-party PE rejects route (BGP error, route not installed); split-horizon fails (CE1 traffic loops to CE2 on same ESI, IXIA Rx counter >0); black-hole for inter-ESI traffic.

b215b035fd943ef4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVI with 'advertise-mac enable' (default, in configuration/l2-services/evpn), BGP neighbor with 'af-l2vpn evpn', RD per Section 7.9, 10-octet ESI (Section 5), Ethernet Tag ID (Section 6); IXIA emulates IPv4 and IPv6 endpoints.
Action: IXIA sends ARP/ND and L2 traffic to learn MAC+IPv4 and MAC+IPv6 bindings; collect tech-support including 'show bgp evpn route type 2', 'show evpn mac-ip', BGP UPDATE packet captures (route type 2 NLRI), running-config.
Verify: Parse tech-support for route type 2 advertisements: RD field (Section 7.9 format), MAC Address Length field = 48 bits, MAC encoding = 6 octets (IEEE 802), IP Address field = 4 octets (IPv4 routes) or 16 octets (IPv6 routes), ESI = 10 octets, Ethernet Tag ID present.
b215b035fd943ef4.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains route type 2 entries with: RD per Section 7.9, MAC Address Length = 48 bits, 6-octet MAC (IEEE 802), both 4-octet IPv4 and 16-octet IPv6 IP encodings observed, 10-octet ESI, Ethernet Tag ID; 'advertise-mac enable' in running-config.
Fail-on: No route type 2 output, MAC Address Length ≠ 48, MAC ≠ 6 octets, IPv4 IP ≠ 4 octets, IPv6 IP ≠ 16 octets, missing or malformed RD/ESI/Tag ID, 'advertise-mac disable'.

b30e1a23165b4f9f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure one EVI with advertise-mac enable (default), attach local host via IXIA, establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE, verify RT-2 (MAC/IP advertisement per RFC7432bis) routes received for remote MACs.
Action: Send bidirectional unicast L2 traffic between local and remote MAC (100 pps, 60 s), confirm forwarding via `show l2-services evpn statistics` (zero packet drops), then collect tech-support bundle.
Verify: Tech-support archive contains: `show l2-services evpn mac-table` (local + remote entries), `show l2-services evpn routes` (RT-2 routes with MAC/IP), `show l2-services evpn detail` (advertise-mac enable, control-word state), running-config (EVI, VXLAN/MPLS tunnel, BGP EVPN AF), kernel FIB entries for the EVI VXLAN/MPLS data-plane, and any MAC-learning or unicast-forwarding syslog/alarm entries.
b30e1a23165b4f9f.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support includes all diagnostic data for RFC7432bis ch.13 unicast forwarding: MAC table (≥1 local + ≥1 remote MAC), RT-2 routes with MAC addresses, advertise-mac enable state, control-word config, EVI + tunnel running-config, and kernel forwarding-plane entries.
Fail-on: Missing MAC table output, missing RT-2 routes, missing kernel FIB/forwarding entries, or missing EVI/BGP EVPN config in running-config.

b3319e3720590ac8.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs multi-home a CE on the same ES; configure `evpn` with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, `export-rt` on both PEs. Deliberately configure different ESI label values on PE1 vs PE2 for the same ES to trigger the delta alarm behaviour (per EVPNS-REQ#260 §3.1: mismatched labels → saved but unused + alarm on).
Action: Trigger Type 1 route advertisements (per-ES with ET ID = MAX-ET and per-EVI) by enabling `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbors. Confirm alarm is active. Run `tech-support` (or `show tech-support` if available) and save the bundle off-box. Open bundle and inspect.
Verify: Bundle contains: (1) `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` output showing both per-ES (ET=MAX-ET, MPLS Label=0) and per-EVI routes with RD/ESI/ET/label fields; (2) ESI label values (type LT4) visible in ESI Label Extended Community for each advertisement; (3) alarm log entry indicating mismatched ESI label configuration per EVPNS-REQ#260 (this overrides RFC7432bis §8.2.1 base behaviour which has no mismatch detection mandate); (4) BGP best-path selection state per RFC7432bis §7.13.2 showing mismatched-label routes saved but not selected; (5) running-config sections for `l2-services / evpn` with `auto-discovery` and `routing / bgp` with `af-l2vpn evpn`.
b3319e3720590ac8.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains all five evidence categories: Type 1 route advertisements (per-ES and per-EVI) with full NLRI fields (RD, ESI, ET, label, RT, Next Hop per RFC7432bis §7.1, §8.2.1.1, §8.4.1), ESI label values in extended communities, active alarm for label mismatch, route-selection state showing mismatched routes excluded, and relevant config stanzas.
Fail-on: Bundle missing Type 1 route details, ESI label values not visible, alarm state absent, or BGP selection logic for mismatched labels not captured.

b33905e3c6be7ec2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed Ethernet Segment (non-zero ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11) on DUT and neighbor PE; both advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI routes (type 1) with differing Layer 2 Attributes extended communities and P/B flags to force RFC7432bis §7.13.2 best-path criteria application.
Action: Trigger path selection by advertising competing routes, then collect tech-support bundle via `show tech-support` or equivalent after `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` confirms path selection completed.
Verify: Tech-support bundle contains `show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show evpn route type 1` (all candidates and selected path), `show bgp l2vpn evpn` with Layer 2 Attributes extended community presence/absence, P and B flag values, running-config (`configuration / l2-services / evpn`, `configuration / routing / bgp`), and syslog entries for path selection events.
b33905e3c6be7ec2.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
b33905e3c6be7ec2.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support demonstrates ordered best-path criteria: (1) routes without Layer 2 Attributes extended community removed from consideration if at least one candidate has it, (2) P and B flags evaluated for remote ES path selection per RFC8214 and RFC7432bis §8.4; selected path in `show evpn route type 1` matches the criteria order.
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn route type 1` output showing all candidate routes, missing Layer 2 Attributes extended community attribute details in BGP output, no P/B flag visibility, or selected path contradicts RFC7432bis §7.13.2 ordered criteria (e.g., route without Layer 2 Attr chosen when route with Layer 2 Attr exists).

b34c6ce18b3f4f5f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`. Enable `auto-discovery` with `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`. Configure `ethernet-segment` under `agg-eth 0` (creates non-zero ESI). Configure BGP EVPN address-family with PE loopback as next-hop. Connect LACP partner to DUT `agg-eth 0`. Configure IXIA to generate L2 traffic toward `agg-eth 0`.
Action: Commit configuration, establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE and LACP session with partner, and start IXIA L2 traffic flow (1000 pps, continuous).
Verify: On neighbor PE, issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` to capture A-D per EVI route. Issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn <prefix> detail` to inspect RD, 10-octet ESI, and MP_REACH_NLRI Next Hop fields. Verify traffic forwarding continues without drops using IXIA statistics.
b34c6ce18b3f4f5f.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA + LACP partner
b34c6ce18b3f4f5f.expectation:
Pass:    Ethernet A-D per EVI route (type 1) is present in neighbor PE BGP table with RD populated, ESI is exactly 10 octets matching `ethernet-segment` configuration, Next Hop is PE's IPv4/IPv6 loopback address (RFC7432bis §8.4.1), AND IXIA reports zero packet loss.
Fail-on: No type-1 route advertised, OR RD absent, OR ESI not 10 octets, OR Next Hop not set to PE address, OR packet loss exceeds 0.01%.

b3636ba9f5578d6e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE in same EVPN instance with multihomed agg-eth to same CE (ESI identifier type-0 or default type-4), load-balancing-mode single-active, DUT elected designated forwarder for BUM. IXIA sends unknown unicast traffic (dest MAC not in EVPN MAC table) at 1000 pps via CE to multihomed ES.
Action: Execute 'shutdown' on DUT's agg-eth interface to force DF re-election to neighbor PE. Wait default es-waiting-time (3 sec), then 'no shutdown' to trigger DF failback. Monitor MPLS encapsulation (P2MP LSP label or ingress-replication downstream label per RFC7432bis §13.2.1).
Verify: 'show evpn ethernet-segment' (DF transitions), 'show evpn instance forwarding-table' (no MAC learning for unknown dest), 'show mpls forwarding-table' (active BUM label), IXIA loss counter. Confirm BUM flooding resumes without destination MAC lookup per §13.2.1 default behavior.
b3636ba9f5578d6e.expectation:
Pass:    BUM traffic recovers within 2× es-waiting-time (6 seconds) after each DF transition; unknown unicast frames flooded via P2MP/ingress-replication without MAC table entries for dest MAC; IXIA reports ≤ 6 sec total loss across both flaps.
Fail-on: BUM flooding not restored after 10 seconds; DUT performs MAC lookups for unknown unicast (contradicts RFC7432bis §13.2.1 'not required to perform destination MAC address lookup'); DF election stalls; neighbor PE does not assume DF role during DUT shutdown.

b38abcb7b95cde1b.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT + Cisco/Juniper PE in MPLS core; EVPN instance with ingress-replication P-tunnel; multi-homed CE on LAG (agg-eth) to both PEs. Configure DUT `ethernet-segment` with `identifier 0 <esi-value>` and `load-balancing-mode single-active`; ensure DUT is elected DF for the ES. Remote PE advertises downstream label in P-tunnel attribute.
Action: From IXIA behind 3rd-party PE, send Ethernet frame with unknown destination MAC (not in either PE's MAC table) into EVPN. 3rd-party PE encapsulates as MPLS with ingress-replication label; sends to DUT.
Verify: Capture on DUT ES attachment circuit and DUT MPLS core-facing port. Check `show evpn ethernet-segment` for DF role; verify DUT receives MPLS packet with P-tunnel label, performs split-horizon check per RFC7432bis §8.3, and floods frame to local ES (LAG member ports) without MAC lookup.
b38abcb7b95cde1b.expectation:
Pass:    Unknown unicast frame arrives at DUT from remote PE's P-tunnel label, DUT floods to local ES ports where it is DF (default behavior per RFC7432bis §13.2.1 — no MAC lookup required for BUM).
Fail-on: DUT drops frame, requires MAC table entry for unknown unicast, non-DF port receives traffic (split-horizon fail), or 3rd-party PE rejects DUT's ESI/DF signaling (interop break).

b4182cbde78a4ed7.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish BGP neighbor session with af-l2vpn evpn address-family enabled. Configure maximum-prefix limit of 5 prefixes with default tear-down behavior (no warning-only or restart options).
Action: From the BGP neighbor (IXIA), advertise 6 distinct L2VPN EVPN type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) prefixes to the DUT.
Verify: Confirm BGP session state transitions to DOWN after 6th prefix is received; inspect DUT syslog for maximum-prefix threshold exceeded message; packet capture verifies BGP NOTIFICATION message code 6 sub-code 1 sent to neighbor.
b4182cbde78a4ed7.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session tears down upon receiving 6th prefix; NOTIFICATION code 6 sub-code 1 sent to neighbor; syslog event logged
Fail-on: Session remains ESTABLISHED after exceeding 5-prefix limit, or NOTIFICATION code/sub-code is incorrect, or no syslog entry generated

b425537565897fb4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with advertise-mac enable (default) on DUT and neighbor PE; establish BGP EVPN session; attach IXIA ports to access interfaces in the same EVI on both PEs; send one frame from IXIA-B (destination) to trigger MAC learning on PE2.
Action: Send unicast Ethernet frame from IXIA-A (source MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01) to IXIA-B (destination MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:02); capture BGP EVPN RT-2 advertisement on PE↔PE session and data-plane frame at IXIA-B.
Verify: `show evpn mac-table` on DUT lists destination MAC as remote with MPLS label or VXLAN VNI; `show bgp evpn route-type 2` confirms RT-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement route per RFC7432bis §7.2) received from PE2 with correct RD, Ethernet Tag, MAC address, and label; ingress L2 unicast counter increments by 1 on DUT access port; IXIA-B receives frame with unmodified source/destination MACs.
b425537565897fb4.expectation:
Pass:    RT-2 route advertised by PE2 and installed on DUT; unicast frame forwarded via EVPN tunnel (not flooded) based on RT-2 lookup; frame arrives at IXIA-B intact; ingress/egress L2 unicast counters each increment by 1.
Fail-on: RT-2 route missing, malformed (wrong MAC/label/RD), or not installed; frame flooded to all remote PEs instead of unicast-forwarded; frame dropped or corrupted (MAC rewrite, VLAN mismatch); counters show 0 or flood counter increments instead of unicast.

b43b457d8c0f25b9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance EVI 100 with MTU 1500 and control-word enabled (`configuration / l2-services / evpn; control-word enable`). Configure neighbor PE with same EVI 100 but MTU 9000 and control-word disabled. Establish BGP EVPN session (`configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor; af-l2vpn evpn`).
Action: Neighbor PE advertises EVPN type-1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) and type-2 (MAC/IP) routes carrying L2-Attr Extended Community (RFC7432bis §7.11.2) with MTU=9000 and control-word C-flag=0. Capture BGP UPDATE messages. Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn routes` and `show l2-services evpn instance 100 detail`.
Verify: DUT receives L2-Attr Extended Community with mismatched MTU (9000 vs local 1500) and control-word (disabled vs local enabled). Check that remote PE IP does NOT appear in EVPN destination list for EVI 100. Check syslog for MTU mismatch and control-word mismatch notifications referencing the remote PE and EVI.
b43b457d8c0f25b9.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (supports EVPN with L2-Attr Extended Community) + packet capture tool
b43b457d8c0f25b9.expectation:
Pass:    Remote PE is NOT added to EVPN destination list for EVI 100 (absent from `show l2-services evpn instance 100 detail` remote endpoints). Syslog contains two mismatch notifications: "MTU mismatch: local 1500, remote 9000" and "control-word mismatch: local enabled, remote disabled" for the neighbor PE.
Fail-on: Remote PE appears in EVPN destination list despite MTU or control-word mismatch, OR traffic is forwarded to remote PE, OR no operator notification logged for either mismatch.

b44e1ccda74f2de6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT ethernet-segment on agg-eth interface testing each ESI Type (0=Reserved, 1=Arbitrary, 2=LACP, 3=MAC, 4=Router-ID, 5=AS-based); configure EVPN instance with RD/RT and auto-discovery; iBGP session to neighbor PE; IXIA taps the BGP link for packet capture.
Action: Bring up the ethernet-segment interface to trigger Route Type 1 (Ethernet A-D route) advertisement; capture BGP UPDATE using tcpdump filter "tcp port 179"; decode MP_REACH_NLRI to extract Route Type 1 NLRI per RFC7432bis §7.1 structure (RD + ESI + Ethernet-Tag + MPLS-Label).
Verify: Parse the 10-octet ESI field (second field in Route Type 1, following the 8-octet RD); confirm byte[0] (ESI Type) matches the configured Type; iterate test across all six valid Types (0x00-0x05); confirm no Route Type 1 with ESI Type > 0x05 is observed.
b44e1ccda74f2de6.expectation:
Pass:    Each configured ESI Type (0-5) produces a Route Type 1 where ESI byte[0] matches the configured Type value; all six valid ESI Types generate correct Route Type 1 advertisements; no ESI Type > 0x05 observed in any capture.
Fail-on: Route Type 1 ESI byte[0] > 0x05, or ESI Type byte ≠ configured Type, or any valid ESI Type (0-5) fails to generate Route Type 1 advertisement.

b51888bba4a930d0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure one EVPN instance with `service-type vlan-based`, single VLAN binding (e.g., VLAN 100), RT import/export, `advertise-mac enable`, and at least one AC. Exercise the EVI through Basic Functionality rows (MAC learning, forwarding). Trigger VID translation if the rig uses different VIDs per PE.
Action: Collect tech-support including: `show l2-services evpn <name>`, `show l2-services evpn <name> bgp routes`, `show l2-services evpn <name> mac-table`, `show router bgp routes evpn mac`, running-config for the EVI, MPLS forwarding tables, VID translation config (if applicable).
Verify: Parse tech-support bundle for: EVI config with `service-type vlan-based`, EVPN route advertisements with Ethernet Tag ID = 0 in all Type 2/3 routes (per RFC7432bis §6.1 MUST), single MAC-VRF/bridge table, MPLS label bindings, VID translation entries (if VIDs differ per segment), syslog/alarm entries.
b51888bba4a930d0.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains running-config showing `evpn <name> service-type vlan-based`, `show bgp routes evpn` output confirming Ethernet Tag ID = 0 for all advertised Type 2/3 routes, MAC table for the single VLAN, MPLS/IP forwarding state, VID translation config (if present per RFC7432bis §6.1 MUST), and complete syslog/alarm history for the EVI.
Fail-on: Missing EVI config, EVPN route details absent, Ethernet Tag ID ≠ 0 in any advertised route, MAC table empty or incomplete, MPLS forwarding state missing, or running-config truncated.

b570379a6ec82a4d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT `agg-eth 0` with `identifier 0 <type0-value>`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `service-carving highest-random-weight`. Configure neighbor PE with same ESI but `service-carving highest-preference preference 100`. Connect both PEs to same CE via LACP.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN peering. Bring up Ethernet Segment on both PEs; each advertises Ethernet Segment Route (type 4) with DF Election Extended Community reflecting its configured service-carving. Inject BUM traffic via IXIA to multiple VLANs.
Verify: DUT show output for the ES indicates active DF algorithm is Default (not highest-random-weight). BGP route inspection confirms neighbor PE advertises different DF Alg value. Per-VLAN DF role matches RFC7432bis ch.8.5 ordinal modulo behavior, observable in traffic forwarding patterns.
b570379a6ec82a4d.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
b570379a6ec82a4d.expectation:
Pass:    DUT detects DF Alg mismatch (highest-preference from neighbor vs highest-random-weight locally configured) and falls back to Default algorithm per RFC7432bis ch.8.5. Show commands confirm fallback. Traffic forwarding uses modulo logic.
Fail-on: DUT continues using highest-random-weight, or ES fails to establish, or per-VLAN DF assignment inconsistent with Default algorithm.

b58146e573dfb8ac.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed CE on LAG to PE1 (DUT) and PE2 with `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 00.00.00.00.00.00.00.00.01; load-balancing-mode single-active` (default) and default service-carving. Wait es-waiting-time (3 s default) for DF election; verify PE1 is DF for VLAN 10 in the EVPN instance via show evpn ethernet-segment detail. Remote PE3 sends unknown-unicast frame (dest MAC not in any PE's MAC table).
Action: PE3 encapsulates frame with ingress-replication label from PE1's IMET route (P-tunnel attribute downstream label per RFC7432bis §13.2.1) and sends via MPLS. Capture MPLS packet at PE1 ingress; verify top label matches P-tunnel label for the EVPN BD.
Verify: show interface agg-eth X counters (PE1 LAG egress increments); show l2-services mac-table (dest MAC remains absent—no lookup triggered); capture frame at CE—original dest MAC preserved. On PE2 (non-DF): show interface agg-eth Y counters (no egress increment—split-horizon per §8.3).
b58146e573dfb8ac.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 (DF) floods unknown-unicast to LAG without MAC table entry; PE2 (non-DF) drops per split-horizon; CE receives exactly one copy with original dest MAC.
Fail-on: PE1 drops unknown-unicast, or PE1 requires MAC entry before forwarding (lookup dependency), or PE2 forwards to LAG (split-horizon violation), or duplicate delivery to CE.

b59d997223132c59.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure aggregate Ethernet interface agg-eth 0 on the DUT; enter `ethernet-segment` configuration mode for that interface.
Action: Issue `identifier 0 <type0-value>` specifying a 9-octet manual ESI value; commit the configuration.
Verify: Execute `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`; confirm the `identifier 0` command appears with the configured 9-octet value.
b59d997223132c59.expectation:
Pass:    ESI type 0 with the specified 9-octet value appears in running-config for interface agg-eth 0.
Fail-on: Command rejected at parse or commit, or ESI not stored in running-config (interface retains default type 4).

b5e263b671905608.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based` under configuration / l2-services, establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE, attach VLANs and bring up the instance.
Action: Issue `control-word enable` under configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-1 and commit. Send bidirectional L2 traffic across the EVPN instance while capturing encapsulated packets on the uplink. Repeat with `control-word disable` and with `no control-word` (default).
Verify: Inspect captured packets for the 4-byte Control Word field following the MPLS label stack or VXLAN header. Run `show running-config | begin evpn evpn-1` to confirm the CLI state.
b5e263b671905608.expectation:
Pass:    When `control-word enable` is configured, EVPN MAC packets include the 4-byte Control Word. When `control-word disable` or `no control-word` (default) is set, packets omit the Control Word.
Fail-on: Control Word presence does not match the configured state; `no control-word` does not restore default (disable); commit fails.

b5ec1c06714ca333.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface `agg-eth 0` with `ethernet-segment` and a 10-octet ESI under `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment`; configure a BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor` to exchange Ethernet Segment Routes.
Action: Bring up the Ethernet Segment and establish the BGP EVPN session; after the Type 4 (Ethernet Segment) route is advertised, execute tech-support collection on the DUT.
Verify: Parse tech-support bundle for: BGP EVPN route table output showing Type 4 routes with ES-Import RT extended community attribute; `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` with the configured ESI; BGP EVPN neighbor state and session details; syslog entries for EVPN/ES events (session up, route advertisement); ES-to-RT derivation tables or mappings if available.
b5ec1c06714ca333.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: (1) BGP table showing Type 4 route(s) with ES-Import RT extended community; (2) running-config with `ethernet-segment` and ESI; (3) BGP EVPN neighbor status (established or down with reason); (4) syslog entries for ES/EVPN events; (5) sufficient context to validate ES-Import RT derivation per RFC7432bis ch.7.6.
Fail-on: BGP EVPN route table missing or truncated (no Type 4 routes visible); ES-Import RT attribute not displayed in route output; `ethernet-segment` configuration absent from running-config; BGP session state missing; no EVPN/ES-related syslog entries; tech-support incomplete or failed to capture key outputs.

b608911adb31ab99.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with BGP neighbor PE that advertises EVPN routes with F-bit=1 (Flow Label capable per §18.1). Establish MAC learning for known unicast destination MAC1 at remote PE. Configure IXIA to generate known unicast frames (dest MAC1) and BUM traffic (broadcast/unknown-unicast dest MAC2).
Action: Send known unicast packets from IXIA toward learned MAC1. Send BUM packets (broadcast, unknown unicast) from IXIA. Capture MPLS-encapsulated packets on DUT→PE link via deep packet inspection. Verify BGP EVPN Extended Community F-bit exchange via show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors. Reconfigure neighbor PE to advertise F-bit=0, flush MAC table, re-learn MAC1, repeat known unicast test and capture.
Verify: Inspect captured MPLS label stack. For known unicast with F-bit=1 received: confirm VPN label (outermost service label) followed immediately by 20-bit Flow label (S-bit=0, entropy per RFC6391) before Ethernet payload. For BUM with F-bit=1: confirm VPN label followed by ESI label, no Flow label present. For known unicast with F-bit=0: confirm no Flow label in stack (VPN label directly precedes payload).
b608911adb31ab99.expectation:
Pass:    When DUT receives F-bit=1 from remote PE, known unicast packets to that PE contain MPLS Flow label (20-bit entropy, S-bit=0) immediately after VPN label, before payload. When DUT receives F-bit=0, known unicast packets contain no Flow label (VPN label→payload). BUM packets never contain Flow label regardless of F-bit (ESI label present per §18.1 rule 3). Label stack ordering: transport-label(s) → VPN-label → [Flow-label if F=1 AND known-unicast] → payload.
Fail-on: Flow label present in any BUM packet (violates MUST NOT). Flow label absent from known unicast when F-bit=1 negotiated (violates MUST send). Flow label present in known unicast when F-bit=0 negotiated (violates MUST NOT send). Flow label in wrong stack position (not immediately after VPN label). S-bit=1 on Flow label (incorrect RFC6391 encoding). DUT drops packets instead of forwarding.

b66b63a07414f8d1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVI with unknown-mac-flooding enabled; attach CE via IXIA; configure neighbor PE in same EVI; send traffic from CE with known destination MAC (learned by DUT) and unknown destination MAC (triggering flood to PE).
Action: Collect tech-support bundle after exercising source MAC lookup, destination MAC lookup, and unknown unicast flooding per RFC7432bis §13.1.
Verify: Bundle contains: show l2-services evpn mac-table (source MAC entries), show l2-services evpn forwarding-table (destination lookup state), show l2-services evpn unknown-mac-flooding (admin policy), show routing bgp evpn routes type 2 (MAC advertisements from neighbor PE), flooding counters/stats, syslog MAC learning events, running-config with unknown-mac-flooding enable.
b66b63a07414f8d1.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support includes MAC table with CE source MACs, forwarding table entries for known destinations, unknown-mac-flooding enabled in config, BGP EVPN type 2 routes from neighbor PE, and flooding counters showing packets flooded for unknown unicast.
Fail-on: Missing MAC learning tables, missing unknown-mac-flooding config/state, missing BGP EVPN type 2 routes, or missing flooding statistics for unknown destinations.

b69c5c174537e432.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with EVPN instance 'cw-test' (vlan-based), BGP EVPN peer to neighbor-PE 192.0.2.2 up, AC xe-0/0/1.100 bound, no prior control-word config.
Action: Enter configuration/l2-services/evpn cw-test, issue 'control-word enable', commit. IXIA sends Ethernet frames via xe-0/0/1.100; capture MPLS packets on DUT→neighbor-PE link. Issue 'no control-word', commit; repeat traffic and capture.
Verify: show running-config l2-services evpn cw-test (enable case: contains 'control-word enable'; no case: control-word line absent). MPLS packet decode: enable case has 4-byte CW (0x00000000) after label stack; default case has no CW (Ethernet directly after labels).
b69c5c174537e432.expectation:
Pass:    'control-word enable' commits without error; running-config shows 'control-word enable'; MPLS packets contain 4-byte control-word. 'no control-word' commits; running-config omits control-word line (default=disable); MPLS packets have no control-word field.
Fail-on: Commit failure on either command, running-config does not reflect command issued, or MPLS packet CW presence/absence contradicts configured state.

b6f4c4a9994ed30d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN VPLS with `auto-discovery` enabled and `import-rt 100:1`. Neighbor PE advertises EVPN routes with RT 100:1 and RT 200:2. Verify routes with RT 100:1 appear in `show bgp l2vpn evpn`.
Action: Issue `no import-rt 100:1` under `configuration / l2-services / vpls / auto-discovery`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services` has no `import-rt 100:1` entry; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows zero routes with import RT 100:1; no MAC entries remain from withdrawn routes.
b6f4c4a9994ed30d.expectation:
Pass:    `import-rt 100:1` removed from running-config; BGP EVPN table contains zero routes with RT 100:1; MAC table consistent (no orphaned entries)
Fail-on: Config persists after commit, any routes with RT 100:1 remain in BGP table, or MAC table shows stale entries from withdrawn RT 100:1 routes

b7621213741eb8e7.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN service 'evpn-100' (vlan-based) configured with interfaces `x-eth 0/0/1` and `x-eth 0/0/2` as members; IXIA ports connected to both interfaces.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-100`; issue `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` displays `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1` under evpn-100; `show mac-address-table` includes 00:11:22:33:44:55 with type 'Static' and egress port `x-eth 0/0/1`; IXIA sends L2 frames from `x-eth 0/0/2` destined to 00:11:22:33:44:55 — traffic received only on IXIA port connected to `x-eth 0/0/1`.
b7621213741eb8e7.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 bound to `x-eth 0/0/1` appears in running-config and MAC table with 'Static' type; L2 frames to that MAC forward exclusively to configured interface.
Fail-on: Static MAC absent from config or MAC table, MAC shows as 'Dynamic' type, or traffic to 00:11:22:33:44:55 received on wrong IXIA port or multiple ports (flooding).

b78620a5819d2585.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance. IXIA simulates two neighbor PEs: PE1 advertises MAC/IP Advertisement routes with ESI=0 (reserved), PE2 advertises MAC/IP routes with non-reserved ESI (type 0, identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88) plus corresponding Ethernet A-D per ES routes. Verify DUT installs PE1 MAC forwarding state immediately (ESI=0 rule) and PE2 MAC forwarding state only after receiving both route types (non-reserved ESI rule). Configure `configuration/l2-services/evpn/es-waiting-time 3` and `configuration/interface/agg-eth/ethernet-segment/load-balancing-mode all-active`.
Action: Trigger power-cycle via lab PDU while both EVPN sessions active and forwarding state installed. Monitor DUT boot sequence and BGP EVPN session re-establishment. IXIA continues advertising both ESI=0 and non-reserved ESI routes during and after power-cycle.
Verify: After full boot, verify `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays pre-cycle ESI config (type 0 identifier, load-balancing-mode all-active, es-waiting-time 3). Confirm `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows re-learned MAC/IP routes with correct ESI values. Validate forwarding state: ESI=0 MAC entries installed based on MAC/IP Advertisement route alone, non-reserved ESI MAC entries installed only when both MAC/IP Advertisement AND Ethernet A-D per ES routes present (check `show evpn mac` and route import timestamps).
b78620a5819d2585.equipment: DUT + IXIA (simulating 2 neighbor PEs with different ESI advertisements) + power-cycle harness
b78620a5819d2585.expectation:
Pass:    DUT recovers within es-waiting-time + 30 s. BGP EVPN sessions re-establish. All ESI=0 MAC entries re-installed from MAC/IP Advertisement routes alone. All non-reserved ESI MAC entries re-installed only after receiving both MAC/IP Advertisement AND Ethernet A-D per ES routes (not prematurely). Configuration (ethernet-segment identifier, load-balancing-mode, es-waiting-time) intact post-boot. No phantom MAC entries or ESI mismatches.
Fail-on: Lost ethernet-segment config (identifier reverts to default type 4, load-balancing-mode reverts to single-active, or es-waiting-time ≠ 3). BGP session fails to re-establish within 60 s. ESI=0 MAC entries not installed, or installed only after waiting for non-existent Ethernet A-D routes. Non-reserved ESI MAC entries installed prematurely (before both route types received). Forwarding blackhole or MAC table corruption.

b78f0b44e635844a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs in all-active multi-homing: on each PE, configure `interface agg-eth 0` with `ethernet-segment`, set `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (identical on both), configure `load-balancing-mode all-active`. Verify default `es-waiting-time` is 3 seconds under `l2-services / evpn`. Establish EVPN instance, confirm both PEs advertise ES routes. Inject continuous L2 traffic from IXIA through the multi-homed CE.
Action: Shut down `interface agg-eth 0` on one PE; capture BGP updates on the remaining PE; monitor traffic forwarding and packet loss.
Verify: Inspect BGP updates for mass-withdraw route per RFC7432bis §8.2; measure time from interface down to traffic fully converged; confirm no ongoing packet loss after convergence.
b78f0b44e635844a.expectation:
Pass:    Mass-withdraw route is sent in BGP EVPN updates; traffic converges to the remaining PE; packet loss duration ≤ 5 seconds (accounting for default `es-waiting-time` of 3 seconds).
Fail-on: No mass-withdraw route observed in BGP updates, or traffic fails to converge to remaining PE, or packet loss exceeds 5 seconds.

b7904410d919a5c5.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE with EVPN BGP peering established. On DUT, configure an agg-eth interface (e.g., agg-eth 0), enter `ethernet-segment` mode, but do NOT configure the identifier command (rely on documented default of type 4).
Action: Associate VLANs to the ethernet-segment and bring up the EVPN instance on DUT. Advertise EVPN routes to the neighbor PE.
Verify: On neighbor PE, examine received EVPN Type 1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) routes and confirm the ESI field indicates type 4. On DUT, `show running-config` for the ethernet-segment should omit the identifier line. Then explicitly configure identifier to type 4, and re-check `show running-config` to confirm it remains unchanged (idempotent).
b7904410d919a5c5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for EVPN peering and route inspection)
b7904410d919a5c5.expectation:
Pass:    Received EVPN Type 1 routes show ESI type 4; DUT running-config omits identifier line when default is used; explicit identifier type 4 configuration is idempotent (no new line added to running-config).
Fail-on: ESI type is not type 4, or identifier line appears in running-config when relying on default, or explicit type 4 configuration modifies running-config (non-idempotent).

b7e39b81cbc8cac8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as single-homing PE (L2VPN EVPN instance, access interface without `ethernet-segment`); configure 3rd-party PE with multihoming (`ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 <type0-value>`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`); establish BGP `af-l2vpn evpn` peering.
Action: Bring up BGP session; capture OPEN verifying L2VPN-EVPN capability, Layer 2 Attributes extended community, Non-Revertive Capability negotiated; 3rd-party advertises Ethernet A-D per ES (type 1) with ESI label plus MAC/IP (type 2) from multihomed segment; send traffic from DUT toward multihomed destination.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on DUT confirms type 1 routes installed with ESI visible; `show bgp neighbor` confirms all capabilities exchanged; traffic forwarding demonstrates aliasing (load-balancing across multiple PEs); packet capture confirms split-horizon enforcement (no loops back to same ESI); per SFS §2.7.9 MUST (overrides RFC7432bis ch.8.7 SHOULD).
b7e39b81cbc8cac8.expectation:
Pass:    Single-homing DUT accepts and processes all multihoming procedures from 3rd-party multihoming PE; BGP capabilities negotiated (L2VPN-EVPN, Layer 2 Attributes, Non-Revertive); type 1 Ethernet A-D per ES routes installed; aliasing functions (traffic uses multiple paths); split-horizon enforced; per SFS §2.7.9 MUST upgrade from RFC7432bis ch.8.7 SHOULD.
Fail-on: DUT rejects type 1 routes or sends NOTIFICATION; missing Layer 2 Attributes or Non-Revertive capability; aliasing inoperative (single-path forwarding only); split-horizon bypassed (traffic loops); falls back to RFC7432bis ch.8.7 SHOULD (optional support) behavior.

b7fddda4e52da955.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology over MPLS core; BGP EVPN adjacency established; PE1 has LAG agg-lag 1 and Ethernet port x-eth 0/0/1 operationally up.
Action: On PE1: `evpn test-pb service-type port-based`; configure import/export route-targets; bind agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 as service interfaces to test-pb; commit. IXIA sends 8 concurrent streams: 4 VLAN variants (untagged, VLAN 10, VLAN 20, VLAN 100) on each interface (agg-lag 1, x-eth 0/0/1) at 100 Mbps per stream with unique source MACs per stream. Capture BGP UPDATEs (tcpdump on PE-PE link) and ingress frames on both access interfaces.
Verify: `show evpn evi test-pb` lists both agg-lag 1 and x-eth 0/0/1 as attached interfaces. Decode BGP captures: all 8 source MACs advertised under single EVI (test-pb) with no VLAN-based segmentation. Remote PE receives all 8 streams at egress; `show evpn mac` on remote PE displays all 8 source MACs under test-pb.
b7fddda4e52da955.expectation:
Pass:    Port-based configuration succeeds on both LAG agg-lag 1 and Ethernet x-eth 0/0/1. All 8 streams (untagged and any VLAN tag) from both interfaces map to single EVI test-pb. BGP advertises all MACs under one EVI. Remote PE forwards all 8 streams.
Fail-on: CLI rejects LAG or Ethernet port-based attachment. Only specific VLAN-tagged streams forward while others drop. BGP advertises MACs from same port under multiple EVIs. Any of the 8 streams fails to reach remote PE.

b86467bd5d1354a2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-aware-bundle, attach ethernet-segment to agg-eth interface with ESI, enable auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt, bring up EVPN and verify route-type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) is advertised in BGP table with ESI, ET ID (VLAN-ID), MPLS label LT2, and L2-Attr extended community.
Action: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`, `show evpn <evpn-name> detail`, `show interface agg-eth <id> ethernet-segment`, confirm all route fields present (RD, ESI, ET ID, label LT2, Primary/Backup flags, next-hop = local PE IP); collect tech-support bundle via CLI or automated script.
Verify: Tech-support archive contains: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` output with all route fields visible, `show evpn` state for the EVI, running-config sections for l2-services/evpn/<evpn-name> and interface/agg-eth/<id>/ethernet-segment, BGP update logs showing route-type 1 advertisement, and kernel L2 FIB entries for the ES.
b86467bd5d1354a2.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support includes complete BGP route-type 1 output showing RD, ESI, ET ID (non-zero VLAN-ID for vlan-aware-bundle), MPLS label LT2, L2-Attr extended community with Primary/Backup flags, next-hop = local PE IP; running-config contains evpn + ethernet-segment stanzas; BGP logs present if any route churn occurred during test.
Fail-on: Missing `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` output, route dump lacks ESI/ET ID/label LT2 fields, missing ethernet-segment or evpn config sections in running-config, or absent BGP advertisement logs when route updates occurred.

b880ed43922ae4a0.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE EVPN topology (PE1, PE2, PE3) with shared EVI; verify `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` defaults under `configuration/l2-services/evpn`; configure ingress-replication; attach IXIA to PE1 and PE2 access ports.
Action: IXIA at PE2 sends frame (source MAC-B) to trigger Type 2 route; IXIA at PE1 sends (1) broadcast (dst FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF), (2) unknown-unicast (dst MAC-X), (3) known-unicast (dst MAC-B). Capture BGP updates and traffic on PE1↔PE2, PE1↔PE3 links.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` confirms Type 3 IMET per PE with PMSI Tunnel attribute (ingress-replication, label, PE IP); `show bgp l2vpn evpn detail` for attribute encoding; captures show broadcast/unknown-unicast each delivered once to PE2, PE3; known-unicast to PE2 only; `show evpn mac address-table` shows MAC-B at PE1 via PE2.
b880ed43922ae4a0.expectation:
Pass:    Type 3 IMET route from each PE with valid PMSI Tunnel attribute; BUM replicated once to each remote PE; known-unicast forwarded to destination PE only; split-horizon prevents loops.
Fail-on: Missing Type 3 IMET, absent/malformed PMSI Tunnel attribute, duplicate BUM at any PE, known-unicast replicated as BUM, or unicast not delivered.

b897502142f14fc5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn vpn-100 service-type vlan-based`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65001:100` and `export-rt 65001:100`. Establish BGP peering with neighbor 10.0.0.2, enable L2VPN EVPN address-family. Do not attach any route-policy or route-map to the neighbor's EVPN address-family (testing default behavior: no policy).
Action: Bring up EVPN BGP session. Neighbor advertises 10 MAC/IP routes with RT 65001:100 (all matching import-rt). DUT advertises 8 locally-originated MAC/IP routes. Wait for route exchange to complete.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms neighbor state Established; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` displays 10 received routes installed; query neighbor PE to confirm it received all 8 DUT-originated routes; check BGP statistics on both sides show no policy-denied routes.
b897502142f14fc5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (second router or IXIA with BGP EVPN speaker capability)
b897502142f14fc5.expectation:
Pass:    All 10 inbound routes installed (no policy filtering applied inbound); all 8 outbound routes advertised and received by neighbor (no policy filtering applied outbound); BGP policy-deny counters remain 0 on both sides.
Fail-on: Received routes < 10 due to policy filtering; neighbor received routes < 8 due to policy filtering; BGP statistics show non-zero policy-deny or policy-filtered counters when no policy is configured.

b8d654ceff7284c2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure identical ES on PE1 and PE2 (interface agg-eth / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01 / load-balancing-mode single-active). Join both to EVI 100. PE3 as remote PE with BGP to PE1/PE2, also in EVI 100. Connect CE to PE1/PE2 LAG, IXIA to PE3.
Action: PE1/PE2 advertise Type 4 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes. On PE3: show bgp evpn route-type 4 detail; decode ESI Label extended community (type 0x06, subtype 0x01) Flags octet, verify bit 0 = 1 (Single-Active redundancy mode). Send unicast traffic from IXIA (via PE3) to MAC learned behind ES.
Verify: PE3 Type 4 route ESI Label EC Flags bit 0 = 1. PE3 show evpn ethernet-segment displays ES in single-active mode. Unicast traffic from PE3 forwards only to Primary DF PE (advertising MAC/IP with L2-Attr EC P bit set), not load-balanced to both PEs.
b8d654ceff7284c2.equipment: Three routers (PE1 and PE2 multi-home ES, PE3 remote) + IXIA traffic gen
b8d654ceff7284c2.expectation:
Pass:    Type 4 route ESI Label extended community Flags bit 0 = 1 (Single-Active mode). PE3 correctly deduces single-active redundancy mode. Unicast traffic from PE3 to MAC behind ES sent only to Primary DF PE, not split to both PE1 and PE2.
Fail-on: ESI Label EC Flags bit 0 ≠ 1 when load-balancing-mode single-active configured on PE1/PE2. PE3 load-balances unicast traffic to both PEs (all-active behavior despite single-active signaling). Primary DF election or forwarding fails.

b8e2259a6f0028c5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with matching ESI (ethernet-segment / identifier 0 <9-octet-value>) on agg-eth interfaces to same CE/IXIA LAG; configure load-balancing-mode single-active; establish EVPN instance with BGP peering; verify primary and backup DF roles advertised in BGP Type-4 Ethernet A-D per ES routes (DF election extended community per RFC7432bis §8.6); start bidirectional L2/L3 traffic from IXIA at line rate.
Action: Execute 'shutdown' on the agg-eth interface of the primary DF PE; wait for backup DF to assume primary role.
Verify: Run 'show evpn ethernet-segment <esi>' and 'show bgp evpn route-type 4' on both PEs; capture traffic loss duration at IXIA; confirm new primary DF within es-waiting-time (default 3 seconds); verify no stale MAC/IP in 'show evpn mac-ip-table' on failed PE.
b8e2259a6f0028c5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
b8e2259a6f0028c5.expectation:
Pass:    Backup DF promoted to primary DF within es-waiting-time (3s default); BGP Type-4 route updated with new DF election extended community; traffic loss ≤ 3s; no stale forwarding entries.
Fail-on: No DF elected after primary failure; traffic loss > es-waiting-time + 1s; stale MAC/IP entries remain in failed PE's FIB; missing or incorrect DF election extended community in BGP routes.

b8e8e71b6f479242.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with EVPN Ingress Replication: `config/l2-services/evpn` set `advertise-mac enable`, `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; `config/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor` enable `af-l2vpn evpn`; create 100 VPLS instances with `auto-discovery import-rt` configured. Verify IMET routes via `show evpn routes imet` contain PMSI Tunnel attribute per RFC6514 ch.5 (Type=6, Flag=0, LT3 label, Tunnel Identifier=originating PE IP). IXIA scale rig attached to 10 VPLS instances, 1000 MAC addresses per instance.
Action: IXIA transmits BUM traffic (unknown unicast) at 5 Gbps aggregate across all VPLS instances for 300 seconds. Monitor ingress replication throughput, IMET route update rate (route-refresh every 60s), and BUM frame forwarding latency (first-hop to egress PE).
Verify: Capture `show evpn routes imet detail` output post-test; parse PMSI Tunnel attribute fields (Type, Flag, MPLS Label, Tunnel Identifier) for all 100 IMET routes. IXIA reports aggregate throughput, P99 latency, frame loss rate.
b8e8e71b6f479242.expectation:
Pass:    Aggregate throughput ≥ 4.75 Gbps (≥95% offered load), P99 latency ≤ 100 ms, frame loss ≤ 0.1%, 100% of IMET routes contain PMSI Tunnel attribute with Type=6, Flag=0, valid LT3 label (non-zero), Tunnel Identifier matching originating PE loopback IP.
Fail-on: Throughput < 4.75 Gbps, P99 latency > 100 ms, frame loss > 0.1%, or any IMET route missing PMSI Tunnel attribute, or PMSI attribute with Type ≠ 6, Flag ≠ 0, MPLS Label = 0, or Tunnel Identifier ≠ originating PE IP.

b9243ce9b9523b11.action_steps:
Setup:  Create agg-eth 1 (`configuration / interface / agg-eth 1`); note the default lacp-key (should equal the interface number, 1).
Action: Issue `lacp-key 100` (valid mid-range), `lacp-key 0` (lower boundary), `lacp-key 65535` (upper boundary); verify each commits. Then test invalid: `lacp-key 65536` (above range), `lacp-key -1` (below range), `lacp-key abc` (non-integer). Finally, issue `no lacp-key` and verify default restoration.
Verify: After each valid value, `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` displays `lacp-key <value>`. After `no lacp-key`, verify the LACP Administrative Key reverts to 1 (the agg-eth interface number) via `show lacp internal` or equivalent. Invalid values must produce immediate CLI error (e.g., "% Invalid input detected" or "% Value out of range") and NOT appear in running-config.
b9243ce9b9523b11.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
b9243ce9b9523b11.expectation:
Pass:    Valid keys (0, 100, 65535) accepted and persist in running-config; each invalid value (65536, -1, abc) rejected with CLI parse error naming the parameter/range; `no lacp-key` restores default (interface number 1 visible in `show lacp` output).
Fail-on: Any invalid value silently accepted or crashes the CLI session; valid value rejected; `no lacp-key` does not restore default; running-config retains invalid `lacp-key` line after error.

b994c1a11f82cca7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with `auto-discovery` enabled, `ethernet-segment` on agg-eth 0 with non-zero ESI, route targets via `import-rt` and `export-rt`. Establish BGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE. IXIA sends continuous bidirectional L2 traffic (100 Mbps) through the multi-homed segment.
Action: Maintain configuration and traffic for ≥ 24 hours. Monitor Ethernet A-D per EVI route (NLRI type 1) advertisement stability.
Verify: Capture BGP EVPN route table hourly (type 1 routes). Parse each snapshot to confirm: (1) RD field matches configured RD per RFC7432bis ch.8.4.1, (2) ESI is 10-octet non-zero value, (3) Next Hop is DUT's IPv4 or IPv6 BGP speaker address. Check platform memory statistics and agg-eth 0 interface counters.
b994c1a11f82cca7.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
b994c1a11f82cca7.expectation:
Pass:    All 24 hourly snapshots show A-D per EVI route present with stable RD, 10-octet ESI, and correct Next Hop per RFC7432bis ch.8.4.1. Memory delta < 2%. Traffic counters monotonic, packet loss ≤ 0.001%.
Fail-on: A-D route withdrawal in any snapshot, RD/ESI/Next Hop field corruption or mismatch, memory growth ≥ 2%, counter stall, or packet loss > 0.001%.

b9ce50dbd5c84819.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a vlan-based EVPN instance under `configuration / l2-services / evpn` with two local interfaces (`x-eth 0/0/1` and `x-eth 0/0/2`) in the same broadcast domain.
Action: Enter the EVPN instance configuration mode. Issue `unknow-mac-flooding disable` and commit. From IXIA connected to `x-eth 0/0/1`, send Ethernet frames to MAC address `00:11:22:33:44:99` (not in the DUT's MAC table). Monitor egress on `x-eth 0/0/2`. Issue `no unknow-mac-flooding`, commit, and repeat the traffic test.
Verify: `show running-config` displays `unknow-mac-flooding disable` before the restore command. With `disable`: frames to unknown MAC are NOT flooded to `x-eth 0/0/2`. After `no unknow-mac-flooding`: frames ARE flooded to `x-eth 0/0/2`.
b9ce50dbd5c84819.expectation:
Pass:    Running-config reflects `unknow-mac-flooding disable` when configured; unknown-MAC flooding is suppressed with `disable`, occurs with default (enable).
Fail-on: Unknown-MAC frames flood when `disable` is configured, or are suppressed when default is active; running-config does not reflect the configured state.

ba78198be7dc5e97.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVI 100, RD 65000:100, RT 65000:100, loopback 10.0.0.1/32; configure BGP peer to neighbor PE with 'af-l2vpn evpn'; enable 'advertise-mac enable' and 'unknown-mac-flooding enable' (both default per CLI doc).
Action: Activate EVI 100; add MAC entry to trigger Type 2 route; verify IMET (Route Type 3) route is advertised; capture BGP UPDATEs at neighbor PE; decode Type 3 NLRI fields: RD, Ethernet Tag ID, Originating Router IP, IP Address Length; repeat with IPv6 loopback ::1.
Verify: Packet capture shows Type 3 NLRI with RD=65000:100, Originating Router IP=10.0.0.1 (or ::1 for IPv6), IP Address Length=32 (or 128); neighbor PE 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 detail' and 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 detail' confirm same Originating Router IP across all route types from DUT.
ba78198be7dc5e97.equipment: DUT + IXIA (BGP packet capture and NLRI decode) + neighbor PE (BGP peering and route verification)
ba78198be7dc5e97.expectation:
Pass:    IMET Route Type 3 contains RD per §7 (65000:100 format), Originating Router IP=DUT loopback (10.0.0.1 or ::1), IP Address Length=32 or 128 bits, Originating Router IP identical across Type 2 and Type 3 routes from DUT, both IPv4 and IPv6 tests succeed.
Fail-on: IMET route absent, RD is 0:0 or malformed, Originating Router IP is 0.0.0.0 or :: or null, Originating Router IP inconsistent between route types, or IPv6 test fails.

ba86e20eeca15e09.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT (BGP router-id 10.0.0.2) and Cisco/Juniper PE (router-id 10.0.0.1) multihomed to same CE via LAG; configure both with ESI 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:00:01, Preference=100, DF algorithm=Highest-Preference (type 2) under ethernet-segment; enable Don't Preempt on DUT, disable on 3rd-party PE; both in EVPN instance EVI-100 (vlan-aware-bundle) with auto-discovery and matching import/export-rt.
Action: Enable agg-eth interfaces; bring up BGP EVPN session; exchange Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) routes carrying DF Election Extended Community (Preference, Don't Preempt bit, DF algorithm); trigger DF election for VLAN 100.
Verify: Capture Type-4 routes from both PEs; decode DF Election EC to confirm DUT sends (Pref=100, Don't Preempt=1, Alg=2) and 3rd-party sends (Pref=100, Don't Preempt=0, Alg=2); run 'show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF:00:00:01' on DUT (expect DF status) and equivalent on 3rd-party (expect non-DF); send BUM packet from IXIA-attached CE, capture at remote PE to verify only DUT forwards.
ba86e20eeca15e09.expectation:
Pass:    DUT elected as DF for VLAN 100 on this ES despite having higher router-id (10.0.0.2 > 10.0.0.1), because DUT advertises Don't Preempt Capability=1 which takes precedence per RFC9785 §4.1 tiebreaker; DF Election EC correctly encoded; single-active BUM forwarding by DUT confirmed.
Fail-on: 3rd-party PE becomes DF (incorrect tiebreaker: IP-based selection before Don't Preempt check), or both PEs forward BUM (split-brain), or DF Election EC absent/malformed in Type-4 route, or DF role not converged within 60 seconds of ES up.

ba9df8e514656f9a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface `agg-eth 0` and enter `ethernet-segment` sub-mode (Ethernet Segment commands are documented under `configuration / interface / agg-eth / ethernet-segment`).
Action: 1. Issue `es-waiting-time <non-default-value>` (default per requirement description: 3 seconds).
        2. Commit; inspect `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` to confirm the timer persists.
        3. Issue `no es-waiting-time`; commit and re-inspect running-config.
Verify: Running-config reflects the configured timer value after step 1; after `no es-waiting-time`, either the line disappears (defaulted) or explicitly shows 3 seconds.
ba9df8e514656f9a.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
ba9df8e514656f9a.expectation:
Pass:    Non-default `es-waiting-time` value persists in running-config; `no es-waiting-time` restores the documented default of 3 seconds (observable as absent config line or explicit `es-waiting-time 3`).
Fail-on: Command rejected, configured value not saved, or `no` form fails to restore 3-second default.

bac46c30b7d7d947.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT: EVI 100 (RD 1.1.1.1:100, advertise-mac enable, unknown-mac-flooding enable); establish BGP EVPN peers with PE1 (10.0.0.1, RID 2.2.2.2) and PE2 (10.0.0.2, RID 3.3.3.3) in AS 65000.
Action: PE1 injects IMET route (Type 3, RD 2.2.2.2:100, Tag 100, PMSI tunnel INGRESS_REPL, originator 2.2.2.2) with LOCAL_PREF=200. PE2 injects IMET route (Type 3, RD 3.3.3.3:100, Tag 100, PMSI tunnel INGRESS_REPL, originator 3.3.3.3) with LOCAL_PREF=100. Both routes same AS_PATH length.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 3 evi 100 detail`: PE1 route marked best ('>' or 'active'); `show l2-services evpn evi 100 multicast`: PE1's originator 2.2.2.2 listed as active replication endpoint.
bac46c30b7d7d947.expectation:
Pass:    PE1's IMET route selected as best path (LOCAL_PREF 200 > 100, first criterion per §7.13.1 step 5); best-path indicator present; forwarding installs PE1's tunnel for BUM traffic to EVI 100.
Fail-on: PE2 route selected or both routes marked active (tie-break failure); best-path selection takes >60s; or DUT selects based on AS_PATH or router-id before evaluating LOCAL_PREF (criteria order violated per §7.13.3 MUST).

bae72ee5a18088ef.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish BGP session between DUT and neighbor PE with L2VPN EVPN address family enabled. Configure maximum-prefix limit of 5 for EVPN routes from the neighbor under `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn`. Confirm session is Established and 0 prefixes received initially.
Action: From neighbor PE, advertise 6 distinct EVPN Type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes incrementally. Observe BGP session state after each advertisement.
Verify: After 5th route, session remains Established. After 6th route, session tears down. `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows neighbor in Idle state. `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor <neighbor-ip>` displays NOTIFICATION sent with code 6 (Cease) sub-code 1 (Maximum Number of Prefixes Reached).
bae72ee5a18088ef.expectation:
Pass:    Session accepts exactly 5 prefixes and remains Established; tears down immediately upon receiving 6th prefix; DUT sends NOTIFICATION code 6 sub-code 1.
Fail-on: Session accepts >5 prefixes without tearing down, OR tears down prematurely (<6 prefixes), OR NOTIFICATION code/sub-code is missing or incorrect.

baf29eb5c22460f0.action_steps:
Problem: validate that standard BGP neighbor policy commands (route-reflector-client, allow-as-in, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, weight) are accepted and operational under the af-l2vpn evpn address family context.
Method: build a two-PE MPLS topology; configure BGP EVPN peering with the six policy commands under af-l2vpn evpn; establish a vlan-based EVI with auto-discovery and route-targets; attach a single-homed CE; drive bidirectional traffic; verify BGP policies applied, EVPN routes exchanged, and both local and remote MAC learning operational.
Setup: PE1 and PE2 connected over MPLS transport; base BGP session configured; CE attached single-homed to PE1 on agg-eth 1 carrying VLAN 100; IXIA connected to generate traffic at CE site and remote PE2 site.
Action: On PE1, enter neighbor configuration for PE2 under `configuration routing bgp`, then under `af-l2vpn evpn` configure route-reflector-client, allow-as-in, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix 5000, private-as remove, and weight 200; configure `evpn evi-1 service-type vlan-based` under `configuration l2-services`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:1` and `export-rt 65000:1`, configure explicit router-distinguisher; bind access interface; commit. Send 1 Gbps bidirectional known-unicast and unknown-unicast traffic from IXIA for 60 s.
Verify: `show bgp neighbor <PE2-ip>` output lists all six configured policy commands (route-reflector-client, allow-as-in, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix 5000, private-as remove, weight 200) as active; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows session established; `show evpn evi evi-1` reports EVI operational; `show evpn mac address-table` displays locally learned source MACs from CE access port and remotely learned MACs from Type 2 routes; tcpdump on PE↔PE link captures Type 2 (MAC/IP) and Type 3 (IMET) route advertisements; IXIA reports ≥0.99 Gbps throughput in both directions with ≤0.01% loss.
baf29eb5c22460f0.expectation:
Pass: BGP EVPN session establishes within ≤30 s; all six policy commands (route-reflector-client, allow-as-in, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix 5000, private-as remove, weight 200) accepted during commit and visible in `show bgp neighbor` output; EVI transitions to up state within ≤10 s of commit; local MAC table populated from data-plane learning on access interface; remote MACs installed from received Type 2 routes; bidirectional traffic forwarded at ≥0.99 Gbps with ≤0.01% loss sustained over 60 s.
Fail-on: Any of the six BGP policy commands rejected during commit; BGP EVPN session fails to establish; EVI remains down; local source MACs not learned from CE; remote MACs not installed from Type 2 routes; traffic black-holed or loss exceeds 0.01%; configuration commit rejected.

bb14e686ae6e456c.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with agg-eth 5 configured in `configuration / interface / agg-eth 5` mode; default LACP Admin Key is 5 (interface number).
Action: Issue `lacp-key 200`, commit; capture `show lacp interface agg-eth 5` and `show running-config interface agg-eth 5`. Issue `no lacp-key`, commit; re-capture show commands.
Verify: After `lacp-key 200`: Admin Key = 200 in show lacp output, `lacp-key 200` in running-config. After `no lacp-key`: Admin Key = 5 (interface number), no lacp-key line in running-config.
bb14e686ae6e456c.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
bb14e686ae6e456c.expectation:
Pass:    Custom lacp-key 200 applied and visible; `no lacp-key` restores default Admin Key = 5 (interface number); running-config reflects current state.
Fail-on: Admin Key does not change to 200, default does not restore to interface number (5), or running-config inconsistent.

bb3e97da7fab04bd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN with auto-discovery under l2-services; configure ethernet-segment under agg-eth 0 with ESI type 0; establish BGP peering with neighbor PE and verify Ethernet A-D route (type 1) is advertised with ESI most significant byte = 0x00 (valid range per RFC7432bis §7.1).
Action: Initiate bidirectional IXIA traffic through agg-eth 0; kill the BGP control-plane process (platform-specific debug command) while A-D route is active.
Verify: After automatic process restart, capture BGP UPDATEs from DUT; decode Ethernet A-D route NLRI to confirm ESI field's most significant byte remains 0x00 (within mandatory range 0–5); verify traffic recovery.
bb3e97da7fab04bd.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen (process-kill harness)
bb3e97da7fab04bd.expectation:
Pass:    BGP process restarts automatically; Ethernet A-D route re-advertised with ESI type byte in valid range (0–5); traffic interruption ≤ platform HA bound (typically ≤5 sec); ESI validation enforced post-restart.
Fail-on: Process does not restart, ESI type byte outside 0–5 range, A-D route not re-advertised within HA recovery window, or traffic loss exceeds platform limit.

bb532923edca1094.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure complete EVPN instance evpn1 with service-type vlan-based, auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt 65000:100, ethernet-segment on agg-eth 1, and LACP adjacency to neighbor PE; verify `show evpn instance evpn1` operational and `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` neighbor up.
Action: Run `onie-install <staged-image-url>` to upgrade; DUT reboots to new image version.
Verify: After boot, run `show running-config | include evpn` (evpn1 present), `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` (ethernet-segment + LACP present), `show evpn instance evpn1` (operational), `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` (neighbor re-established), `show lacp neighbor` (adjacency up).
bb532923edca1094.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for BGP EVPN + LACP adjacency verification) + ONIE image server
bb532923edca1094.expectation:
Pass:    All EVPN config components in running-config (evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based, auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt 65000:100, ethernet-segment on agg-eth 1, LACP); `show evpn instance evpn1` operational; BGP EVPN neighbor up; LACP neighbor up within 5 minutes of boot.
Fail-on: Any config component missing or modified (evpn instance, ethernet-segment, LACP, import/export RTs); EVPN instance down; BGP neighbor down; or LACP not re-established after 5 minutes.

bb60b75b953fec0c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2: `interface agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 <9-octet-value> / load-balancing-mode all-active`; establish iBGP EVPN to DUT (remote PE). Verify DUT imports Ethernet A-D per ES routes from both PEs with ESI Label ext-community redundancy-mode = 0; `show evpn mac` displays MAC addresses with ECMP next-hop set (both PEs).
Action: IXIA generates continuous bidirectional L2 traffic (1 Gbps, mixed 64-1518 byte frames) to MACs on the all-active ES for ≥ 24 hours; monitor at t=0, 6h, 12h, 18h, 24h.
Verify: At each checkpoint: `show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show evpn mac`, `show route evpn ethernet-ad-per-es`, `show route evpn mac-ip`, `show platform process memory`, interface counters; confirm ECMP next-hop count unchanged, zero route withdrawals, memory delta < 1%, Tx/Rx monotonic.
bb60b75b953fec0c.equipment: DUT (remote PE) + 2 neighbor PEs (multihomed ES, all-active) + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
bb60b75b953fec0c.expectation:
Pass:    ECMP next-hop count stable (= 2) over 24 h; zero route flaps; memory growth < 1%; interface counters monotonically increasing; IXIA reports packet loss < 0.01%.
Fail-on: Next-hop count ≠ 2 at any checkpoint, ≥1 route withdrawal/re-advertisement, memory growth ≥ 1%, counter freeze/decrement, or packet loss ≥ 0.01%.

bbbd087f43d67518.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with 1000 EVPN instances (ET IDs 1–1000), each advertising one broadcast domain. On both PEs: `configuration/l2-services/evpn` set `control-word enable` and `advertise-mac enable`; configure `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbors. IXIA generates BUM traffic (100 pps per broadcast domain).
Action: Clear BGP session on PE1 (`clear bgp neighbor <PE2-IP> af-l2vpn-evpn`) to force Type 3 route re-advertisement. Capture BGP UPDATE messages on IXIA. Measure time from session clear to receipt of all 1000 Type 3 routes. Parse each captured Type 3 route for mandatory fields: RD, ET ID (1–1000), PMSI Tunnel attribute (Flag=0, Type=6 [Ingress Replication], LT3 Tunnel Identifier = Originating Router IP), control-word flag=1, L2-Attr Extended Community with L2 MTU field, RT, and Next Hop = advertising PE IP.
Verify: Use IXIA protocol analyzer to confirm all 1000 Type 3 routes received, each containing RD (key), ET ID (key), Originating Router IP (key), PMSI Tunnel Type=6, MPLS Label (LT3), Tunnel Identifier = advertising PE loopback, control-word flag=1, L2 MTU in L2-Attr Extended Community, and Next Hop = PE IP. Measure total convergence time (session clear → last Type 3 route received).
bbbd087f43d67518.expectation:
Pass:    All 1000 Type 3 routes re-advertised and received within 5 seconds of BGP session clear; each route contains all mandatory fields (RD, ET ID, Originating Router IP, PMSI Tunnel Type=6 with Flag=0, LT3 Tunnel Identifier = Originating Router IP, control-word flag=1 matching configuration, L2 MTU in L2-Attr Extended Community, RT, Next Hop = advertising PE IP); zero missing or malformed routes.
Fail-on: Convergence time >5 seconds, any Type 3 route missing mandatory fields (PMSI Tunnel attribute, control-word flag, L2-Attr Extended Community), PMSI Tunnel Type ≠ 6, PMSI Tunnel Flag ≠ 0, control-word flag ≠ 1 when configured enable, or Tunnel Identifier ≠ Originating Router IP.

bbe6f27060720097.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance evpn100 (vlan-based) with `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1`. Verify type-2 EVPN route advertising to neighbor PE via `show bgp l2vpn evpn`. Stage new image on ONIE server.
Action: Execute `onie-install <image-url>` to upgrade to next release; DUT reboots.
Verify: Run `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn100`, `show evpn mac-address evpn-name evpn100`, and `show bgp l2vpn evpn` to confirm static MAC config persists, MAC appears in local table, and type-2 route still advertised to neighbor PE.
bbe6f27060720097.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + ONIE image server
bbe6f27060720097.expectation:
Pass:    `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1` present in running-config; 00:11:22:33:44:55 listed in `show evpn mac-address` output; type-2 route for 00:11:22:33:44:55 in `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and received by neighbor PE.
Fail-on: `mac-address-static` command missing from running-config; static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 absent from `show evpn mac-address`; type-2 route for static MAC withdrawn or missing from BGP EVPN table; upgrade fails or rolls back.

bbeaa67b92dced22.action_steps:
Setup:  Enter configuration mode; create `interface agg-eth 0`; under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0`, issue `ethernet-segment`; commit the configuration. Save running-config to ensure persistence across reboot.
Action: Issue `reload` to reboot the DUT. Wait for the DUT to complete boot sequence (console or SSH access restored, all system processes running).
Verify: After boot, issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` and confirm the `ethernet-segment` line is present with identical syntax to the pre-reload configuration.
bbeaa67b92dced22.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
bbeaa67b92dced22.expectation:
Pass:    `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays the `ethernet-segment` line after reload, byte-identical to pre-reload configuration; EVPN ethernet segment mode auto-resumes without manual intervention.
Fail-on: The `ethernet-segment` line is absent from `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` post-reload, the agg-eth interface configuration is lost or partially restored, or manual re-configuration is required to restore the ethernet-segment mode.

bbfd48fff5f940f2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as BGP route reflector with two iBGP neighbors (Neighbor-A and Neighbor-B) in a VRF; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on both neighbors; establish BGP sessions; do NOT configure `route-reflector-client` on Neighbor-A initially.
Action: Enter `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <Neighbor-A-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn` mode, configure `route-reflector-client`, commit configuration, then originate or advertise an EVPN route from Neighbor-B to the DUT.
Verify: Check DUT reflection behavior via `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <Neighbor-A-ip> advertised-routes`; on Neighbor-A, examine received EVPN routes (via `show bgp l2vpn evpn` or BGP update capture) and confirm ORIGINATOR_ID attribute (set to Neighbor-B's router-ID) and CLUSTER_LIST attribute (containing DUT's cluster-ID) are present.
bbfd48fff5f940f2.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (iBGP peers to test route reflection)
bbfd48fff5f940f2.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor-A receives reflected EVPN routes with ORIGINATOR_ID matching Neighbor-B's router-ID and CLUSTER_LIST containing the DUT's cluster-ID, per RFC 4456 route reflection rules.
Fail-on: Reflected routes lack ORIGINATOR_ID or CLUSTER_LIST attributes, or routes are not reflected to Neighbor-A at all after enabling `route-reflector-client`.

bc27b0627b2bae46.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 and PE2 configure ethernet-segment identifier 0 11.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.01 and load-balancing-mode all-active on agg-eth to shared CE1. DUT (remote PE) imports Ethernet A-D per ES routes; verify show bgp evpn route-type 1 displays ESI Label extended community redundancy-mode=0 from both. IXIA sends L2 traffic to EVI MAC; verify ECMP 50/50 ±5%.
Action: On DUT, kill BGP daemon (kill -SIGKILL <bgpd-pid> or platform debug command); monitor syslog for process auto-restart and BGP session re-establishment with PE1/PE2.
Verify: show bgp evpn route-type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES from both PEs re-imported); show evpn ethernet-segment detail (All-Active mode, both PE1/PE2 active); show evpn mac (ECMP next hops to both PEs); IXIA traffic counters (ECMP 50/50 ±5%, measure traffic loss duration).
bc27b0627b2bae46.equipment: DUT (remote PE) + 2 neighbor PEs (All-Active ES to shared CE) + IXIA traffic gen
bc27b0627b2bae46.expectation:
Pass:    BGP auto-restart ≤10s; Ethernet A-D per ES routes re-imported from both PEs with redundancy-mode=0; ethernet-segment detail shows All-Active with 2 active PEs; MAC entries list 2 next hops; traffic loss ≤13s (es-waiting-time 3s default + 10s convergence); ECMP 50/50 ±5%.
Fail-on: No BGP auto-restart; routes not re-imported or missing redundancy-mode=0; ethernet-segment shows single-active or 1 PE only; MAC single next-hop; traffic loss >13s; ECMP distribution skew >±5%.

bc7183e45c863c1f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'evpn EVI-100 service-type vlan-aware-bundle' on DUT and neighbor PE; configure 'ethernet-segment' on agg-eth interface with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99; enable 'auto-discovery' with 'export-rt 100:100' and 'import-rt 100:100'; attach IXIA to DUT representing remote site traffic source.
Action: Neighbor PE advertises route type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) with RD, ESI, ET ID=100, MPLS label LT2; apply BGP policy on neighbor PE to suppress route type 4 (Ethernet A-D per ES) while keeping route type 1 active (simulates race condition per [RFC7432bis] ch.8); inject BUM traffic from IXIA destined to multihomed site; wait 15s; remove suppression to restore route type 4.
Verify: On DUT: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99' confirms route type 1 received; 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 esi <same>' confirms route type 4 absence then presence; 'show l2-fib evpn evi EVI-100' verifies remote PE next-hop NOT installed during route type 4 absence; IXIA counters confirm zero forwarding during window; convergence timer after route type 4 arrival.
bc7183e45c863c1f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT L2-FIB does NOT install next-hop toward neighbor PE when only route type 1 is received (route type 4 missing); zero BUM packets forwarded during 15s window; after route type 4 reception, L2-FIB programs next-hop and traffic forwards within 5s mass-withdrawal bound per [RFC7432bis] §8.4.
Fail-on: DUT forwards traffic using route type 1 alone before receiving route type 4 (violates MUST NOT for race condition handling in [RFC7432bis] ch.8), OR convergence >5s after route type 4 arrival, OR persistent L2-FIB mismatch.

bc91ccfbe246f25d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance EVPN-100 service-type vlan-based with RD, RT, and attach interface x-eth 0/0/1. Verify EVPN is operational.
Action: Enter configuration / l2-services / evpn EVPN-100 and configure `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`. Commit the configuration. Send test frames from IXIA with destination MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55.
Verify: Run `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN-100` to confirm static MAC entry. Query MAC address table (via show command or NETCONF) to verify MAC appears as static and bound to x-eth 0/0/1. Confirm IXIA frames to that MAC are forwarded to x-eth 0/0/1. Issue `no mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55`, commit, and verify entry removed from running-config.
bc91ccfbe246f25d.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 configured successfully, appears in running-config and MAC table as static (non-aging) on interface x-eth 0/0/1, forwards traffic correctly, and cleanly removed by no form.
Fail-on: Command rejected, MAC absent from table or running-config, MAC ages out, traffic to that MAC dropped or misdirected, or no form fails to remove the entry.

bcf22c0b27c278f6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `agg-eth 0` and enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` mode; ensure interface is not attached to any EVPN instance (per CLI Notes precondition).
Action: Issue `load-balancing-mode` with an alternate mode value; commit. Then issue `no load-balancing-mode`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0 ethernet-segment` after first commit shows the configured mode; after `no` command, running-config shows single-active or no explicit load-balancing-mode line (implicit default).
bcf22c0b27c278f6.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
bcf22c0b27c278f6.expectation:
Pass:    load-balancing-mode configuration accepted; persists in running-config; `no load-balancing-mode` restores single-active default per documented behavior.
Fail-on: Command rejected despite precondition met; `no` form doesn't restore single-active; or configured mode not reflected in running-config.

bd025abc72968b4b.action_steps:
Setup:  Create `agg-eth 0` on DUT; assign member port to the LAG; connect to neighbor PE configured for LACP; verify no prior `lacp-system-mac` override in DUT config.
Action: Enter DUT mode `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0`, issue `lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55`, commit; wait for LACP session to form with neighbor PE. On neighbor PE or via packet capture, verify LACP PDUs from DUT carry system MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55. Then issue `no lacp-system-mac` on DUT, commit, verify LACP PDUs now carry the LAG's own MAC.
Verify: DUT `show running-config` includes `lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55` under `interface agg-eth 0`; neighbor PE LACP state or captured LACP PDUs show partner/actor system MAC as 00:11:22:33:44:55; after `no` form, PDUs show LAG MAC as system MAC.
bd025abc72968b4b.expectation:
Pass:    Configured MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 persists in running-config and appears in LACP protocol messages; `no lacp-system-mac` reverts to LAG MAC address default
Fail-on: CLI rejects command, commit fails, LACP PDUs carry incorrect system MAC, or `no` form doesn't restore default LAG MAC behavior

bd03feea83d637b6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance (`configuration/l2-services/evpn/advertise-mac enable`), enable `af-l2vpn evpn` to test BGP peer, set `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100` under VPLS auto-discovery. Establish EVPN BGP session.
Action: From BGP protocol tester, send three UPDATEs with duplicate extended communities: (1) EVPN Type-2 (MAC/IP) route with TWO MAC Mobility ECs (first: seq=10, second: seq=20); (2) Type-1 (Ethernet A-D per-EVI) route with TWO ESI Label ECs (first: label=1000, second: label=2000); (3) Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) route with TWO ES Import RT ECs (first: RT=65000:1, second: RT=65000:2).
Verify: Inspect received routes with `show bgp evpn route` and route-type-specific commands; confirm DUT stores only first EC instance for each type (MAC Mobility seq=10, ESI Label 1000, ES Import RT 65000:1); verify forwarding plane (`show evpn mac`, `show evpn ethernet-segment`) uses first label/RT values, not second.
bd03feea83d637b6.equipment: DUT + BGP protocol test tool with extended community injection capability (IXIA IxNetwork BGP, Spirent TestCenter, or ExaBGP-equivalent scriptable speaker)
bd03feea83d637b6.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts all three routes, processes only the first instance of each duplicate EC type per RFC7432bis §7.14.2 MUST; MAC Mobility seq=10 installed (not 20), ESI Label 1000 active (not 2000), ES Import RT 65000:1 imported (not 65000:2); no BGP session disruption; routes remain installed; forwarding behavior matches first EC values.
Fail-on: DUT uses second or later EC instance value in RIB/FIB, or withdraws routes due to duplicate ECs (duplication is not an error condition per spec), or BGP session resets, or forwarding plane selects wrong seq/label/RT.

bdd052beb76e7282.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as remote PE receiving BGP EVPN routes; configure IXIA to advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes with ESI Label extended community (redundancy mode field = 1 for Single-Active); establish BGP EVPN session.
