Action: Inject malformed Ethernet A-D per ES route variants via IXIA: (1) invalid redundancy mode values (2, 3, 255) in ESI Label extended community, (2) truncated extended community TLV, (3) oversized redundancy mode field, (4) reserved bits set in ESI Label extended community flags, (5) missing ESI Label extended community entirely; interleave with valid redundancy mode = 1 routes.
Verify: `show bgp evpn routes`, `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment`, BGP error counters, syslog for malformed attribute notifications; confirm DUT rejects malformed variants and does NOT deduce ES mode from them; confirm valid routes (mode = 1) correctly trigger Single-Active deduction per RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1.
bdd052beb76e7282.expectation:
Pass:    All malformed variants rejected (not imported to routing table); BGP session stable; error counter increments for each malformed route; syslog entries document ESI Label extended community parse failures; valid routes (redundancy mode = 1) accepted and ES correctly deduced as Single-Active
Fail-on: Crash, BGP session reset, any malformed route accepted into routing table, incorrect ES mode deduced from malformed input, silent drop without error logging, or valid routes rejected due to fault injection

bdd63ef0df01772d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance: `evpn evpn-test service-type vlan-based` under configuration / l2-services; attach interfaces 1/1 and 1/2 to evpn-test (as EVPN member ports); configure `unknow-mac-flooding disable` under configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn-test; commit.
Action: From IXIA port connected to DUT interface 1/1, transmit Ethernet frame with dest MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (not in MAC table), source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, tagged with EVPN service VLAN. Capture on IXIA port connected to interface 1/2. Reconfigure `unknow-mac-flooding enable` (or `no unknow-mac-flooding` to restore default), commit, and retransmit the same frame.
Verify: `show mac-address-table | include AA:BB:CC` confirms dest MAC is unknown. With disable: IXIA capture on port 1/2 shows 0 frames received. With enable: IXIA capture shows ≥1 frame received (flooded to all member interfaces).
bdd63ef0df01772d.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (min 2 IXIA ports: one for frame injection, one for flooding verification)
bdd63ef0df01772d.expectation:
Pass:    `unknow-mac-flooding disable` → unknown-dest MAC frames NOT flooded (0 frames on non-ingress interfaces). `unknow-mac-flooding enable` (default) → unknown-dest MAC frames flooded to all EVPN member interfaces (≥1 frame received).
Fail-on: Flooding behavior identical for both enable/disable settings, or inverted behavior (frames flooded when disabled, not flooded when enabled), or config rejected/non-functional.

bdd6bc6f0fe93c9e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN EVI 100 on DUT and neighbor PE with RT 65000:100, associate VLAN 10. Do NOT execute `host mac-address-duplicate-detection` command (test factory defaults). Connect IXIA port A to DUT SAP vlan 10, IXIA port B to neighbor PE SAP vlan 10.
Action: IXIA port A sends ARP + ICMP traffic with MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 for 10s; stop port A, start identical traffic from IXIA port B (MAC move); repeat cycle 10 times over 5 minutes (force moves). Monitor syslog real-time. Capture BGP updates via `monitor router bgp routes evpn mac`.
Verify: Execute `show l2-services evpn mac-duplication-detection` (confirm default move-count, move-interval, freeze-time displayed), `show l2-services evpn mac 00:11:22:33:44:55` (check for frozen/duplicate flag), `show router bgp routes evpn mac 00:11:22:33:44:55` (verify route withdrawal), grep syslog for duplicate-MAC operator alert.
bdd6bc6f0fe93c9e.expectation:
Pass:    `show l2-services evpn mac-duplication-detection` displays documented default move-count (e.g., 5), move-interval (e.g., 180s), freeze-time (e.g., 300s); after N moves within M seconds, DUT marks MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 as duplicate, generates syslog operator alert ("MAC duplicate detected in EVI 100"), withdraws BGP MAC/IP Advertisement route type-2 for that MAC, freezes further processing until operator clears.
Fail-on: No default values visible in show output, or duplication undetected despite >10 moves, or BGP route remains active after threshold crossed, or syslog contains no operator alert, or default values differ from documented spec.

bdfa1d30c7ea454f.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE in same EVPN instance; configure remote PE to advertise F-bit=1 (flow-label capable) in BGP extended community; establish IMET routes and learn MAC addresses on both sides.
Action: From IXIA connected to DUT CE-facing port, send (1) known unicast frames destined to learned MAC on remote PE side, (2) BUM frames (broadcast FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF). Capture MPLS packets on core link between DUT and remote PE.
Verify: Run 'show bgp l2vpn evpn routes detail' on DUT to confirm remote PE advertised F-bit=1; decode captured MPLS packets to verify label stack structure (transport→VPN label→flow/ESI label).
bdfa1d30c7ea454f.expectation:
Pass:    Known unicast packets carry [Transport label][VPN label][Flow label] stack; BUM packets carry [Transport label][VPN label][ESI label] stack with NO flow label; DUT correctly honors F-bit=1 from remote.
Fail-on: Known unicast packets missing flow label when F-bit=1 advertised by remote; BUM packets include flow label; or F-bit negotiation fails/ignored.

be41b2d58365a6a0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN vlan-based service for VID 100; attach two physical interfaces (e.g., x-eth 0/0/1, x-eth 0/0/2) as ACs. From IXIA, send frames with identical source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 through both ports to trigger duplicate-MAC freeze.
Action: Verify frozen MAC entry exists for 00:11:22:33:44:55. Execute `clear evpn frozen mac-addresses` at operational mode prompt. Re-check the frozen MAC table.
Verify: Frozen MAC entry for 00:11:22:33:44:55 is removed from the table. Send new frames with that source MAC; confirm MAC learning resumes (entry appears in MAC table, not frozen table; forwarding works).
be41b2d58365a6a0.expectation:
Pass:    MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 is cleared from the frozen-MAC table; subsequent traffic with that MAC is learned and forwarded normally.
Fail-on: Frozen MAC persists after clear command, command returns CLI error, or the address remains blocked from normal MAC learning and forwarding.

be53398964e99098.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface agg-eth 0 on the DUT. Enter configuration mode.
Action: Navigate to `interface agg-eth 0` and issue `ethernet-segment` to enter EVPN ethernet segment configuration mode. Exit the ethernet-segment sub-mode and commit the configuration. Issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`. Then navigate back to `interface agg-eth 0`, issue `no ethernet-segment`, and commit.
Verify: After the initial commit, `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays the `ethernet-segment` keyword under interface agg-eth 0. After `no ethernet-segment` and commit, the ethernet-segment configuration is no longer present under agg-eth 0.
be53398964e99098.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
be53398964e99098.expectation:
Pass:    The `ethernet-segment` command is accepted under agg-eth interface, enters ethernet segment configuration sub-mode, persists in running-config after commit, and is successfully removed by `no ethernet-segment`.
Fail-on: Command is rejected, configuration does not persist in running-config after commit, or `no ethernet-segment` fails to remove the configuration.

be65ffdfc3690232.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE each with `agg-eth 0`, `ethernet-segment`, identical `identifier 0 <type0-value>`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`; attach both to EVI N. IXIA emulates multi-homed CE with LAG to both PEs.
Action: Commit configuration; allow ES discovery and BGP EVPN convergence; capture BGP Ethernet A-D per EVI routes (type 1) via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`.
Verify: Decode route type 1 advertisements from both PEs; confirm L2-Attr extended community present with P bit = 1 in both (§8.6 All-Active requirement: all PEs set P=1).
be65ffdfc3690232.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise route type 1 with L2-Attr extended community having P bit = 1 (all PEs in All-Active mode must set P bit).
Fail-on: Any PE's route type 1 has P bit = 0 in L2-Attr, or L2-Attr missing, or P bit = 1 only on one PE.

be660a2426be5344.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 with `ethernet-segment` on `agg-eth 0` (non-zero 10-octet ESI); create `evpn evpn-name service-type vlan-based` with `auto-discovery` and `export-rt` configured; repeat on PE2 with identical ESI.
Action: Bring up the multi-homed interface; allow BGP session to advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI route (type 1).
Verify: On PE2, inspect received BGP EVPN routes or capture UPDATE messages; decode NLRI type 1 to validate: RD field present, ESI = 10 octets, MP_REACH_NLRI Next Hop = PE1's loopback address.
be660a2426be5344.expectation:
Pass:    Ethernet A-D per EVI route contains RD, 10-octet ESI, and Next Hop = advertising PE address per RFC7432bis §8.4.1.
Fail-on: RD missing/zero, ESI length != 10 octets, or Next Hop != PE address.

be7054588a30a3d5.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with physical interfaces available for agg-eth; NETCONF client (ncclient) authenticated and YANG capabilities exchanged.
Action: Via NETCONF configure all mandatory EVPN components in sequence: (1) LACP on agg-eth interface, (2) ethernet-segment under that agg-eth, (3) evpn instance under l2-services with service-type vlan-based, (4) auto-discovery with import-rt and export-rt. Commit the NETCONF transaction.
Verify: Query via NETCONF `<get-config>` for l2-services and interface trees; execute CLI `show running-config l2-services evpn <name>` and `show running-config interface agg-eth <id>`; confirm LACP config, ethernet-segment, evpn instance, and route-targets appear identically in both views.
be7054588a30a3d5.expectation:
Pass:    All four mandatory components (LACP, ethernet-segment, evpn instance, auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt) configured via NETCONF appear with identical values in CLI `show running-config`; NETCONF `<get-config>` round-trip preserves service-type and route-target values.
Fail-on: NETCONF `<edit-config>` succeeds but CLI view missing any component (LACP, ethernet-segment, evpn, or RT), YANG schema rejects valid component config, or route-target values differ between NETCONF and CLI transports.

be92330ac3826db5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with BGP AS 65001, create EVPN instance "evpn-vlan100" using `evpn evpn-vlan100 service-type vlan-based` with Ethernet Tag 100; configure neighbor PE with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled; enable `advertise-mac`; do NOT configure manual import-rt/export-rt to allow auto-derivation per RFC7432bis §7.10.1.
Action: Inject ARP traffic on VLAN 100 to trigger local MAC learning and BGP EVPN Type-2 route advertisement; after advertisement confirmed (`show bgp evpn summary` shows routes sent), collect tech-support: `show bgp evpn route`, `show l2-services evpn evpn-vlan100`, `show running-config l2-services`, `show running-config routing bgp`, `show log | include RT|evpn`.
Verify: Parse tech-support for BGP EVPN Type-2 route; verify RT field displays as 65001:100 (Global Admin = AS 65001, Local Admin = VLAN 100 encoded in lowest 12 bits, upper 20 bits zero); confirm running-config lacks manual import-rt/export-rt under evpn-vlan100 instance.
be92330ac3826db5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for BGP EVPN session establishment and Type-2 route exchange verification)
be92330ac3826db5.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains BGP EVPN Type-2 route with RT=65001:100 (or target:65001:100), proving VLAN 100 encoded in lowest 12 bits of Local Admin field and AS 65001 in Global Admin field per RFC7432bis §7.10.1 auto-derivation rules; running-config shows no manual import-rt/export-rt commands, confirming auto-derivation was active.
Fail-on: Tech-support missing BGP EVPN route output, RT format incorrect (e.g., RT ≠ 65001:100, or VLAN not in lowest 12 bits of Local Admin), manual RT configuration present in running-config, or EVPN/BGP show commands absent from bundle.

bea7e2632ae1607e.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish BGP peering with an EVPN-capable neighbor (IXIA or PE); configure a maximum-prefix limit (e.g., 100) on the L2VPN EVPN address-family for that neighbor; advertise routes up to but not exceeding the threshold; verify session stable and limit enforced.
Action: Remove the maximum-prefix configuration from the neighbor's L2VPN EVPN address-family; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms maximum-prefix is absent for this neighbor; advertise prefixes exceeding the former limit (e.g., 150 EVPN routes); confirm BGP session remains established and all prefixes are accepted without session teardown or NOTIFICATION code 6 sub-code 1.
bea7e2632ae1607e.equipment: DUT + IXIA (acting as BGP EVPN peer)
bea7e2632ae1607e.expectation:
Pass:    maximum-prefix removed from running-config; BGP session accepts unlimited L2VPN EVPN prefixes without termination
Fail-on: maximum-prefix persists in running-config, or BGP session tears down with NOTIFICATION 6/1 when prefix count exceeds the old limit, or routes beyond the old limit are rejected

bf425580804b77e2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP router with AS on DUT and neighbor PE; configure neighbor with IP and remote-as; verify baseline BGP session Established.
Action: On DUT, under neighbor config context, enable `af-l2vpn evpn`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config router bgp` displays `af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor; `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` lists the neighbor with State=Established.
bf425580804b77e2.expectation:
Pass:    `af-l2vpn evpn` present in config; neighbor shows State=Established in L2VPN EVPN summary.
Fail-on: Command rejected, BGP session drops after commit and fails to re-establish, or neighbor not listed in `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`.

bf4612777ffe61fd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `agg-eth 0` in multi-homing mode: enter `configure`, `interface agg-eth 0`, `ethernet-segment`, configure ESI identifier, commit.
Action: Enter `interface agg-eth 0`, issue `no ethernet-segment`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` confirms no ethernet-segment stanza; BGP EVPN route table shows no Type-4 ES routes for the former ESI; interface no longer participates in multi-homing.
bf4612777ffe61fd.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
bf4612777ffe61fd.expectation:
Pass:    ethernet-segment configuration removed from running-config; no EVPN Type-4 ES route advertisements for this interface; interface exits multi-homing mode cleanly
Fail-on: ethernet-segment stanza persists in running-config; Type-4 ES routes still advertised; or interface state shows stale multi-homing parameters

bfa2280ef12b8ac0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE with `interface agg-eth X / ethernet-segment / load-balancing-mode all-active` and EVPN instance attached; neighbor PE in same ES; IXIA injects unknown-unicast frames (dest MAC absent from DUT's EVPN MAC table) at 1000 pps; verify DUT is DF and floods BUM traffic via MPLS (check P2MP LSP label or ingress-replication downstream label per RFC7432bis §13.2.1).
Action: Kill the EVPN control-plane process (platform-specific debug command, e.g., `debug process kill evpn-mgr`) while IXIA traffic runs; monitor MPLS encap and remote PE Rx counters.
Verify: Process auto-restarts; `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms DF re-election; `show mpls forwarding-table` shows P2MP/IR labels re-established; IXIA Rx counter at neighbor PE resumes incrementing (unknown-unicast frames flooded post-recovery).
bfa2280ef12b8ac0.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (same ES, remote EVPN peer) + IXIA traffic gen (process-kill harness + unknown-unicast frame injection + Rx loss measurement)
bfa2280ef12b8ac0.expectation:
Pass:    Unknown-unicast flooding recovers; BUM traffic interruption ≤ 2× `es-waiting-time` (default 6 sec, tunable via `evpn es-waiting-time`); DF role correct; MPLS label stack matches pre-failure (P2MP LSP or ingress-replication downstream label per RFC7432bis §13.2.1); IXIA Rx loss ≤ 6000 frames (6 sec × 1000 pps).
Fail-on: BUM traffic blackholed > 6 sec; DF election stuck (DUT shows non-DF but neighbor PE also non-DF); MPLS labels missing; process does not auto-restart.

bff48269975ba845.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (e.g., VPLS 100) with Ethernet Segment ESI-1 spanning two local DUT interfaces (Eth1/1, Eth1/2). Configure `unknown-mac-flooding enable` and establish BGP EVPN peer with remote PE that also connects to ESI-1. Verify Type-1 Ethernet A-D per-ES routes are advertised/received and DUT allocates unique LT4 MPLS labels for Eth1/1 and Eth1/2 (check via `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI-1>` or `show mpls forwarding-table` for per-interface labels).
Action: From remote PE or IXIA emulating remote PE MPLS encap, inject BUM traffic (unknown-unicast destination MAC) with outer label = LT3 (VPLS instance label) and inner label = LT4 corresponding to Eth1/1's ifIndex. Send 100 frames at 10 pps.
Verify: Capture egress traffic on Eth1/1 and Eth1/2. Inspect `show l2-services vpls <100> mac-table` and BUM forwarding counters. Verify DUT's BUM Split Horizon Table exists with 2 entries (one per ES interface) via `show evpn split-horizon-group` or equivalent.
bff48269975ba845.expectation:
Pass:    All 100 BUM frames egress Eth1/2 only; zero frames egress Eth1/1 (split-horizon applied per LT4 label). BUM Split Horizon Table shows 2 entries with distinct LT4 labels keyed by ifIndex of Eth1/1 and Eth1/2. Forwarding counter on Eth1/2 = 100; Eth1/1 egress counter = 0 for this flow.
Fail-on: BUM frames egress Eth1/1 (LT4 split-horizon violated), or flood to non-ES interfaces, or Split Horizon Table missing/incomplete (<2 entries), or traffic dropped entirely (counter = 0 on all interfaces).

c00fb493ba117467.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure LAG interface with ethernet-segment (identifier type 0), load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving highest-random-weight. Attach LAG to EVPN instance, establish neighbor PE peering. Start bidirectional IXIA traffic (1000 pps) through multi-homed ES.
Action: Kill EVPN control-plane process (platform debug command). Monitor process status, ES operational state, and IXIA traffic counters.
Verify: show system processes (process restarted), show ethernet-segment (ES operational, DF election complete), show evpn ethernet-segment (Type-4 routes re-advertised), IXIA traffic loss duration.
c00fb493ba117467.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + neighbor PE (multi-homing setup required for ES all-active)
c00fb493ba117467.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts within 5 s; ES returns to operational state; all-active mode preserved; Type-1/Type-4 routes reconverge; traffic interruption ≤ es-waiting-time default (3 s) + 2 s convergence margin.
Fail-on: Process does not restart, ES stuck in down state, load-balancing-mode reverts to single-active, or traffic interruption > 5 s.

c02bdcebf86e9bc8.action_steps:
Setup:  Create VPLS instance under `l2-services / vpls <vpls-name>`, enable `auto-discovery`, configure `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100` under the auto-discovery block; commit.
Action: Enter VPLS configuration mode and execute `no auto-discovery`; commit.
Verify: Confirm via `show running-config` that the entire `auto-discovery` stanza (including all `import-rt` and `export-rt` entries) is absent from the VPLS configuration. Verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn` displays no EVPN routes associated with this VPLS instance.
c02bdcebf86e9bc8.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
c02bdcebf86e9bc8.expectation:
Pass:    Complete removal of `auto-discovery` and all sub-configuration (`import-rt`, `export-rt`) from running-config; EVPN BGP sessions no longer advertise/import routes for this VPLS instance.
Fail-on: Stale `auto-discovery`, `import-rt`, or `export-rt` entries remain in running-config; EVPN routes continue to be advertised/imported; or control-plane state inconsistent with data-plane.

c0613a9c1dafa4ae.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-based` with two attachment circuits (VLAN 100 → IXIA port A, VLAN 200 → IXIA port B); learn MAC 00:11:22:33:44:01 from IXIA port A. Confirm baseline: no `unknow-mac-flooding` stanza in running-config (default is enable).
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn test-evpn` mode. 1) `unknow-mac-flooding disable`, commit; IXIA port A sends Ethernet frame dst=00:11:22:33:44:99 (unknown). 2) `unknow-mac-flooding enable`, commit; re-send dst=00:11:22:33:44:99. 3) `no unknow-mac-flooding`, commit; re-send dst=00:11:22:33:44:99.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn test-evpn` contains `unknow-mac-flooding disable` after step 1, `unknow-mac-flooding enable` after step 2, no unknow-mac-flooding line after step 3. IXIA port B counter: 0 frames Rx in step 1, 1 frame Rx in step 2, 1 frame Rx in step 3.
c0613a9c1dafa4ae.expectation:
Pass:    All CLI forms commit successfully. Disable drops unknown-MAC frames (port B Rx=0). Explicit enable and no-form (default) flood unknown-MAC frames to all EVI ACs (port B Rx=1 for each). Config state matches `show running-config`.
Fail-on: Config parse error, commit failure, unknown-MAC frames flooded when disabled, dropped when enabled/default, or incorrect running-config state.

c0c0a09c224536a0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `l2-services / evpn BD-VLAN service-type vlan-based`; configure `l2-services / evpn BD-AWARE service-type vlan-aware-bundle`; configure `l2-services / evpn BD-PORT service-type port-based`; commit and verify operational state.
Action: Issue `show evpn broadcast-domains` (unfiltered); issue `show evpn broadcast-domains name BD-VLAN` (name filter); re-issue both commands to confirm idempotence.
Verify: Unfiltered output displays all three broadcast domains (BD-VLAN, BD-AWARE, BD-PORT) with columns for domain name, service-type, and operational state; name-filtered output returns only BD-VLAN; service-type values match configuration (vlan-based, vlan-aware-bundle, port-based).
c0c0a09c224536a0.expectation:
Pass:    Command returns well-formed table; all configured domains present; service-type column shows vlan-based (BD-VLAN), vlan-aware-bundle (BD-AWARE), port-based (BD-PORT); name filter isolates the specified domain; output idempotent across repeated invocations.
Fail-on: Parser error, missing columns, configured domain absent from output, incorrect service-type value, name filter returns wrong domain, or non-idempotent output on stable configuration.

c10c70497b11d8af.action_steps:
Setup:  Jerico1-based DUT in mode `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-inst-1` with EVPN auto-discovery configured.
Action: Configure `mac-aging-time 75` (violates Jerico1 range {0, 100-600}); commit. On rejection, configure `mac-aging-time 300`; commit. Issue `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-inst-1`. Execute `no mac-aging-time`; commit; re-check running-config.
Verify: First commit fails with error message explicitly stating Jerico1 range constraint {0, 100-600}; second commit succeeds; running-config shows `mac-aging-time 300`; `no` form removes the statement and restores platform default (typically 300 seconds).
c10c70497b11d8af.equipment: DUT only (Jerico1 ASIC hardware variant; CLI session via SSH)
c10c70497b11d8af.expectation:
Pass:    Jerico1 platform rejects aging-time ∉ {0, 100-600} with explicit range error; valid values {0, 100-600} accepted and persisted in running-config; `no mac-aging-time` restores default without error.
Fail-on: Out-of-range value silently accepted, commit succeeds without validation, error message omits Jerico1-specific range, `no` form rejected, or default not restored.

c11fdd0240ee2a4a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN service using `evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-based` under configuration / l2-services. Attach an access interface (e.g., x-eth 0/0/1) to the service. Verify the service is operational.
Action: In the EVPN service configuration context, configure `mac-limit 5` and commit. Generate traffic from IXIA with 10 distinct source MAC addresses (00:00:00:00:00:01 through 00:00:00:00:00:0A) into the access port.
Verify: `show running-config` includes `mac-limit 5` under the EVPN service; `show mac-address-table` filtered for this service shows exactly 5 learned MAC addresses; MAC count remains 5 even after frames from all 10 source MACs are sent.
c11fdd0240ee2a4a.expectation:
Pass:    `mac-limit 5` accepted and persists in running-config; exactly 5 MAC addresses learned; limit enforced (6th through 10th MACs not learned).
Fail-on: Command rejected at commit; or more than 5 MACs learned; or fewer than 5 MACs learned when 10 sources send traffic; or limit not enforced (all 10 MACs learned).

c1694c24f17c1d9f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure vlan-based EVPN instance evpn4 with normalized VLAN-ID 4; bind interface x-eth 0/0/1 to evpn4 with VLAN-ID replacement (ingress VLAN 10 → normalized VLAN 4). Send tagged frames (VLAN 10) from CE; verify VLAN-ID replacement and forwarding.
Action: Execute tech-support command to collect diagnostic bundle after exercising VLAN-ID replacement functionality.
Verify: Extract tech-support archive; confirm presence of: show l2-services evpn evpn4 output, running-config l2-services section with evpn evpn4 vlan-based config and VLAN-ID normalization, FIB/MAC tables showing VLAN 4 entries (not ingress VLAN 10), interface x-eth 0/0/1 VLAN mapping (10→4).
c1694c24f17c1d9f.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: (1) show l2-services evpn evpn4 displaying service-type vlan-based and normalized VLAN-ID 4; (2) running-config with evpn evpn4 service-type vlan-based and VLAN-ID replacement config; (3) FIB/MAC tables showing VLAN 4 (not 10); (4) interface x-eth 0/0/1 VLAN-mapping evidence.
Fail-on: Tech-support missing show l2-services output, running-config l2-services section, FIB/MAC tables, or interface VLAN-mapping evidence; or tech-support shows incorrect VLAN-ID (10 in EVI tables instead of normalized 4).

c18066ffc6a9c242.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT ES on `agg-eth 0`: `identifier 0 <type0-value>`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `service-carving highest-random-weight`; configure neighbor PE with matching ES and service-carving; establish BGP EVPN session.
Action: Bring up `agg-eth 0` on DUT; capture BGP UPDATE messages sent by DUT on the PE session.
Verify: Decode Route Type 4 (Ethernet Segment route) from captured UPDATE; confirm DF Election Extended Community is present; verify DF Alg field encodes highest-random-weight per RFC8584 ch.3.
c18066ffc6a9c242.expectation:
Pass:    Route Type 4 contains DF Election Extended Community with DF Alg field = highest-random-weight (RFC8584 ch.3).
Fail-on: DF Election Extended Community absent, DF Alg field value does not match highest-random-weight, or Route Type 4 not sent.

c1a1a89f3f8cab5b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and 2 neighbor PEs in multi-homed ethernet-segment (same ESI type 0 identifier, load-balancing-mode single-active, service-carving highest-random-weight); establish BGP EVPN peering and wait es-waiting-time (3 sec) for DF election convergence.
Action: Trigger DF re-election by shutting down the primary DF's agg-eth interface; after re-election stabilizes, collect tech-support on DUT and both neighbor PEs.
Verify: Parse tech-support bundles for: show evpn ethernet-segment (DF role, primary/backup DF status per EVI), show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail (Ethernet Segment routes carrying DF Election Extended Community per RFC7432bis §8.6), running-config (ES identifier type 0, service-carving highest-random-weight), syslog (DF election events, backup DF promotion timestamps).
c1a1a89f3f8cab5b.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs (multi-homing) + IXIA traffic gen
c1a1a89f3f8cab5b.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support from each PE contains: DF role assignment (primary DF, backup DF if signaled, or non-DF), BGP type-4 routes with DF Election Extended Community indicating election algorithm and primary/backup DF per RFC7432bis §8.6, ES config section matching deployed identifier and service-carving method, syslog entries timestamping DF election and re-election events within es-waiting-time window.
Fail-on: Missing show evpn ethernet-segment output, BGP type-4 routes lack DF Election Extended Community, DF role inconsistent across PEs' tech-support, syslog missing DF failover event, incomplete running-config (ES parameters absent), or tech-support captured before election convergence (< es-waiting-time).

c1a55ebfbf4e979c.action_steps:
Setup:  Under `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1`, configure `ethernet-segment` with `esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11`; configure `interface eth 1/1` with `channel-group 1 mode active`; do NOT configure load-balancing-mode; commit.
Action: 1) `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` → confirm no `load-balancing-mode` line under ethernet-segment (default: single-active per requirement). 2) Enter `config / interface agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment`; execute `load-balancing-mode all-active`; commit. 3) `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` → confirm `load-balancing-mode all-active` appears. 4) Enter ethernet-segment context; execute `no load-balancing-mode`; commit. 5) `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` → confirm load-balancing-mode line removed.
Verify: After step 1, no load-balancing-mode line (default single-active). After step 3, explicit `load-balancing-mode all-active` line present. After step 5, load-balancing-mode line absent (default restored).
c1a55ebfbf4e979c.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
c1a55ebfbf4e979c.expectation:
Pass:    Default is single-active (no config line shown, or vendor doc states single-active is default). `load-balancing-mode all-active` successfully configures and persists in running-config. `no load-balancing-mode` removes config line and reverts to single-active default.
Fail-on: Default mode is not single-active (contradicts requirement description), or `load-balancing-mode all-active` command rejected or not saved to running-config, or `no load-balancing-mode` throws error or fails to remove line, or mode remains all-active after `no` command.

c1c5c1d8632e34ce.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 100 on DUT and neighbor PE with BGP EVPN address-family enabled; neighbor PE advertises BGP EVPN route type 2 for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 with MAC Mobility extended community (static flag=1, seq=0); verify DUT installs the MAC as sticky via 'show l2-services vpls mac-table'.
Action: Connect IXIA to DUT local AC in the same broadcast domain; transmit Ethernet frames with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 at 100 pps for 10 seconds to trigger local MAC learning on DUT.
Verify: Capture DUT syslog output for operator alert referencing MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55; run 'show l2-services vpls mac-table mac 00:11:22:33:44:55 detail' to check for sticky/conflict flag; optionally monitor IXIA Rx counters to verify frame drop behavior.
c1c5c1d8632e34ce.expectation:
Pass:    DUT generates operator alert (syslog severity ≥ WARNING, message identifying MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 and sticky/static conflict) within 5 seconds of local learning; MAC table reflects sticky status or conflict state.
Fail-on: Zero syslog alerts for the MAC conflict; sticky MAC silently overwrites to dynamic local entry; no conflict indication in MAC table; DUT control-plane crash or BGP session reset.

c2108f06b068b4ad.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance EVI 100, enable advertise-mac and unknown-mac-flooding. Establish BGP EVPN peering with PE1 (LOCAL_PREF 200, AS_PATH [65001]) and PE2 (LOCAL_PREF 100, AS_PATH [65001]) both advertising IMET route type 3 for EVI 100. Configure ES1 multi-homing on DUT with all-active mode.
Action: Verify DUT receives both IMET routes. Inject BUM traffic from IXIA into EVI 100. Modify PE1 LOCAL_PREF to 50 (lower than PE2). Re-verify path selection changes. Confirm BUM replication now uses PE2 as next-hop per RFC7432bis §7.13.3 ordered criteria (LOCAL_PREF → AS_PATH → ORIGIN → IGP cost → Router-ID).
Verify: show evpn database | match IMET confirms best path with '>' marker switched from PE1 to PE2 after LOCAL_PREF change. show bgp evpn summary confirms both peers up. Packet capture on DUT uplinks confirms BUM frames replicate to new best-path PE. show evpn ethernet-segment confirms ES1 multi-homing still all-active with no DF election regression.
c2108f06b068b4ad.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE (minimum 2 neighbor PEs required: PE1 and PE2 both capable of advertising IMET routes with manipulable BGP attributes)
c2108f06b068b4ad.expectation:
Pass:    IMET route selection changes when attributes change, following RFC7432bis §7.13.3 ordered criteria (highest LOCAL_PREF first, then shortest AS_PATH, then tie-breakers). BUM traffic replicates via current best-path PE only. Multi-homing ES1 remains all-active with correct DF state.
Fail-on: DUT selects IMET path out-of-order (e.g., prefers shorter AS_PATH despite lower LOCAL_PREF), or BUM traffic replicates to both PEs simultaneously, or multi-homing DF election fails/flaps during IMET path change.

c21288a80b895e48.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance with an Ethernet Segment and apply the service-carving command to set a non-default Default Forwarder Election method (prerequisite row establishes the happy-path configuration).
Action: Enter configuration mode, issue the no form of the service-carving command to remove it, and commit.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms service-carving has been removed; verify that Default Forwarder Election reverts to the default carving service; check operational state of any active Ethernet Segments to confirm DF election continues properly; inspect `show evpn ethernet-segment` and related outputs for stale service-carving references.
c21288a80b895e48.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
c21288a80b895e48.expectation:
Pass:    service-carving absent from running-config; Default Forwarder Election operates using the default carving service; active Ethernet Segments elect a DF without errors; no stale service-carving state in operational commands.
Fail-on: service-carving persists in running-config after commit, DF election fails or produces incorrect DF selection, or stale service-carving configuration appears in operational outputs.

c22e8fd9e8d77638.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based` with `auto-discovery` enabled (import-rt/export-rt 65000:100) in single-homed mode (no ESI configured). Establish BGP peering with neighbor PE using `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family.
Action: Activate the EVPN instance and trigger Ethernet Auto-discovery per-EVI route (type 1) advertisement. On neighbor PE, capture BGP UPDATE message containing the A-D route.
Verify: Decode Extended Communities in received A-D route. Use `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 detail` or packet capture to inspect: (a) Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01 present, (b) ESI Label field = 0x000000, (c) Flags octet RED bits = 00 (All-Active), MBZ bits = 0.
c22e8fd9e8d77638.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN peering required to exchange Ethernet A-D routes and validate Extended Community encoding)
c22e8fd9e8d77638.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Label Extended Community Type=0x06/Sub-Type=0x01 present in Ethernet A-D route; ESI Label value = 0 (per RFC7432bis §7.5: MAY be zero if no split-horizon filtering required); Flags octet correctly formatted (RED=00 for All-Active, reserved bits zero).
Fail-on: Extended Community missing from A-D route, Type/Sub-Type mismatch (not 0x06/0x01), ESI Label non-zero in single-homed scenario, Flags octet malformed (MBZ bits set or RED encoding incorrect), or route advertisement fails.

c24810a9f027664c.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: PE1 (DUT) and PE2 multi-homed to CE via agg-eth with same ESI (type 0), `load-balancing-mode all-active`, P2MP MPLS LSPs for BUM; PE3 imports EVPN routes but not connected to ESI. PE1 configured as Non-DF via `service-carving lowest-preference 100`.
Action: Trigger PE1 to advertise Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 4) with upstream assigned ESI label. Send BUM traffic from PE3 via P2MP LSP encapsulated with PE1's advertised ESI label, destined to PE2. Capture PE2's forwarding table and egress behavior on the agg-eth interface toward CE.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn routes type 4 originating-router <PE1-IP>` on PE2 and PE3 to confirm ESI label value. `show mpls forwarding-table label <ESI-label> context <PE1-IP>` on PE2 and PE3 to confirm label programmed in PE1's context label space. Packet capture on PE2's agg-eth to CE: zero packets egress with the ESI label (split-horizon enforced).
c24810a9f027664c.equipment: Three-PE topology (DUT + 2 neighbor PEs in EVPN instance, one multi-homed to same ESI as DUT, one remote) + IXIA traffic gen for BUM injection
c24810a9f027664c.expectation:
Pass:    PE1's type 4 route carries upstream assigned ESI label (non-zero); PE2 and PE3 both install label in PE1's context label space (`show mpls forwarding-table` returns valid entry); PE2 does NOT forward BUM packets received with this label onto the multi-homed Ethernet segment (0 packets out agg-eth interface).
Fail-on: Type 4 route missing ESI label field, or PE2/PE3 fail to program label, or PE2 forwards ≥1 packet with ESI label onto agg-eth (split-horizon violation per RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2).

c24b2ba9e9351b28.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT with EVPN instance (VNI 100, RD 1.1.1.1:100, RT 100:100) and 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) with matching instance; establish MP-BGP EVPN session (af-l2vpn evpn under neighbor); configure `control-word enable` on DUT EVPN context.
Action: On DUT: enable EVPN instance to trigger Type 3 route advertisement for broadcast domain; capture BGP update (`show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3` on DUT, equivalent on 3rd-party). On 3rd-party: verify Type 3 route received; on DUT verify 3rd-party's Type 3 route.
Verify: Inspect advertised Type 3 route fields on both PEs: RD matches configured value, ET ID (EVI), Originating Router IP = loopback, PMSI Tunnel Attribute Flag=0 + Type=6 (Ingress Replication) + MPLS Label (LT3), Tunnel Identifier = Originating Router IP, L2-Attr Extended Community with L2 MTU=1500 + Control Word flag=enabled, RT=100:100, Next Hop = advertising PE loopback. Confirm `import-rt 100:100` causes route installation in both peers' EVPN tables.
c24b2ba9e9351b28.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs exchange Type 3 routes with all mandatory fields present and correctly encoded: PMSI Type=6, Flag=0, Tunnel ID=Originating Router IP, Next Hop=advertising PE IP, L2-Attr Extended Community includes configured Control Word flag (enabled) and L2 MTU; routes installed in each peer's EVPN route table (one Type 3 per broadcast domain per EVI).
Fail-on: Type 3 route missing RD/ET ID/Originating Router IP, PMSI Tunnel Type ≠ 6 or Flag ≠ 0, Tunnel Identifier ≠ Originating Router IP, Next Hop ≠ advertising PE IP, Control Word flag in L2-Attr does not match DUT `control-word enable` setting, or route rejected by peer (BGP notification/route not in table).

c262a24d7c6fe733.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN vlan-based service with ethernet-segment on agg-eth interface (ESI type byte 0–5), enable auto-discovery, set import-rt/export-rt, establish BGP EVPN peering to trigger Ethernet A-D route type 1 advertisement.
Action: After `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` shows originated and received A-D routes (RD + ESI + Ethernet Tag + MPLS Label), collect tech-support output.
Verify: Parse tech-support bundle for: show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1, show evpn detail, show interface agg-eth ethernet-segment, running-config l2-services/evpn/auto-discovery stanzas, BGP update syslog for route type 1.
c262a24d7c6fe733.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains (1) decoded A-D route output showing 10-octet ESI field with type byte 0–5, (2) RD + Ethernet Tag + MPLS Label fields present, (3) running-config auto-discovery/import-rt/export-rt stanzas, (4) syslog entries for A-D route origination and reception from peer.
Fail-on: Missing show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 output, ESI type byte outside 0–5 range or not visible, missing auto-discovery config section, or missing BGP EVPN update logs.

c277379dd72e0e62.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with AS <local-asn>, establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor <peer-ip> (AS <peer-asn>) under vrf <vrf-name>, with af-l2vpn evpn address-family enabled. Configure peer to advertise EVPN routes with AS_PATH containing <local-asn>.
Action: Under configuration / routing / bgp <local-asn> / vrf <vrf-name> / neighbor <peer-ip> / af-l2vpn evpn, configure allow-as-in 1 to accept routes with local AS appearing once in AS_PATH. Issue `commit`.
Verify: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and confirm routes from <peer-ip> appear with <local-asn> in AS_PATH field. Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` to verify received-routes > 0. Remove allow-as-in, commit, and verify routes are withdrawn.
c277379dd72e0e62.expectation:
Pass:    Routes containing local AS once in AS_PATH are accepted when allow-as-in 1 is configured; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` displays routes with <local-asn> in path; removing allow-as-in withdraws them.
Fail-on: Routes with local AS in AS_PATH remain rejected despite allow-as-in config, or CLI rejects allow-as-in command, or routes persist after config removal.

c278435f10d22fcc.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 PEs (DUT, PE1, PE2) in EVPN instance EVI 100 with ingress-replication mode for BUM; ensure `unknow-mac-flooding enable` (default) on DUT to generate route type 3. Assign PE1 router-ID 1.1.1.1, PE2 router-ID 2.2.2.2.
Action: Advertise Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag route (type 3) from PE1 and PE2 for EVI 100. Sequentially manipulate BGP attributes (LOCAL_PREF, AS_PATH length, ORIGIN code, MED, IGP metric) to force best-path tie-breaking through each selection stage per RFC 7432bis §7.13.1 step 5.
Verify: On DUT, run `show bgp evpn route-type 3 evi 100` and `show bgp evpn routes detail`; confirm selected best path matches ordered criteria: LOCAL_PREF (highest) → AS_PATH (shortest) → ORIGIN (lowest) → MED (lowest if comparable) → eBGP>iBGP → IGP metric (lowest) → router-ID (lowest).
c278435f10d22fcc.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects best path strictly per RFC 4271/RFC 7432bis ordered criteria—e.g., PE1 with LOCAL_PREF 200 beats PE2 at 100 regardless of other attributes; when LOCAL_PREF equal, shorter AS_PATH wins. Each criterion overrides all subsequent ones.
Fail-on: DUT selects sub-optimal path, applies criteria out of order (e.g., chooses lower router-ID before checking LOCAL_PREF), skips a mandatory step, or oscillates between multiple paths without converging.

c2dd87c101ffcb8d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (DUT + neighbor PE) attached to same Ethernet Segment (same ESI value, e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99). On both PEs: enable EVPN instance with MP2MP MPLS LSP for BUM, configure `unknown-mac-flooding enable`, enable `af-l2vpn evpn` in BGP, and advertise auto-discovery routes (type 1).
Action: From IXIA, send broadcast traffic through the multi-homed ES to trigger BUM forwarding over the MP2MP LSP. On each PE, capture the BGP EVPN type-1 route advertisements and the MPLS label stack in outbound BUM packets.
Verify: On each PE: `show evpn ethernet-segment` (or equivalent) to display the ESI label value. `show mpls forwarding-table` to confirm label range. `show bgp evpn route-type 1` to inspect advertised ESI label. On IXIA: packet capture of BUM frames to extract MPLS label stack and ESI label.
c2dd87c101ffcb8d.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs allocate and advertise the identical ESI label value (from DCB range, typically 16–1048575 for downstream-assigned labels) in their BGP type-1 routes. BUM packets carry this ESI label in the MPLS stack. Remote PE receiving BUM traffic can parse the ESI label and identify the source ES (observable in `show evpn mac-table` or debug logs showing split-horizon drop for local ES).
Fail-on: PEs advertise different ESI label values for the same ES; ESI label is outside DCB range (indicating upstream assignment); BUM packets missing ESI label in MPLS stack; remote PE cannot identify source ES (no split-horizon filtering, duplicate delivery to local ES CEs).

c2efe61274d7cc2b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 with `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based` mapping to VLAN 100, PE2 with same EVPN instance mapping to VLAN 200. Establish BGP EVPN peering, enable auto-discovery with matching import-rt/export-rt. Connect IXIA to PE1 access port in VLAN 100, host behind PE2 in VLAN 200.
Action: Send tagged Ethernet frames (VID 100) from IXIA to MAC behind PE2. Capture frames in MPLS core (PE1→PE2 link) and at PE2 egress access port. Query EVPN routes via `show l2-services evpn evpn-100 routes` and `show router bgp routes evpn`.
Verify: Confirm Ethernet Tag ID = 0 in all advertised EVPN route types (RT-2 MAC/IP, RT-3 IMET, RT-5 IP Prefix). Confirm MPLS-encapsulated frames retain original VID 100 per RFC7432bis §6.1. Confirm PE2 translates VID 100 → 200 on egress. Confirm BGP session stable and MAC learned in both directions.
c2efe61274d7cc2b.expectation:
Pass:    All EVPN routes (RT-2, RT-3, RT-5) carry Ethernet Tag ID = 0. Frames in MPLS core tagged with ingress VID 100. PE2 performs VID translation: egress frames retagged to VID 200. BGP peering up, MAC-VRF operational, bidirectional traffic forwarded.
Fail-on: Any EVPN route with Ethernet Tag ID ≠ 0. VID modified in MPLS core (not original VID 100). PE2 egress frames not translated (remain VID 100). BGP session flaps, MAC not learned, or traffic black-holed.

c32a2f50d548e4ea.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (DUT + neighbor PE) with IXIA emulating multi-homed CE; configure Ethernet Segment on both PEs using `ethernet-segment` under agg-eth interfaces (ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99); enable BGP EVPN AF with `af-l2vpn evpn` in neighbor config; create EVI 100 with `advertise-mac enable`; IXIA advertises 5 MAC addresses; start tcpdump on DUT capturing BGP to neighbor PE (tcp port 179).
Action: Execute `shutdown` on DUT's agg-eth interface to IXIA to trigger PE-to-CE link failure; let capture run for MAC-ageout-timer duration; stop capture and parse with wireshark BGP dissector.
Verify: In wireshark, filter for BGP UPDATE with WITHDRAWN ROUTES; verify route type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) NLRI present with encoding RD (8 octets) + ESI (10 octets = 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) + Eth-Tag (4 octets) per RFC7432bis §7.1; verify route type 2 (MAC/IP) WITHDRAWs for all 5 IXIA MACs.
c32a2f50d548e4ea.expectation:
Pass:    BGP UPDATE with WITHDRAWN ROUTES contains route type 1 with NLRI = RD + ESI (01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) + Eth-Tag, total 22 octets per §7.1 encoding; route type 2 WITHDRAWs present for all 5 IXIA MAC addresses; neighbor PE receives WITHDRAWs within MAC-ageout-timer.
Fail-on: Missing route type 1 WITHDRAW, missing route type 2 WITHDRAWs (fewer than 5 MACs), NLRI encoding error (ESI ≠ 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, wrong octets count, or malformed RD/Eth-Tag), or no WITHDRAW received by neighbor PE.

c32af6b6fb6ab82a.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE multi-homed to shared CE (IXIA port) via agg-eth, same ESI identifier type 0 (00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88), load-balancing-mode single-active. IXIA simulates remote PE in same EVI, sends L2 traffic to MAC addresses behind multi-homed CE. Confirm `show bgp evpn route type 4` displays ES routes from both PEs.
Action: Kill EVPN control-plane process on DUT (e.g., `debug process kill evpn-mgr` or platform equivalent). IXIA continues traffic from remote PE to multi-homed site MACs.
Verify: `show process` confirms process restart. `show bgp evpn route type 4` confirms DUT re-advertises ES route for ESI 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88. `show ethernet-segment` shows ESI operational. IXIA measures packet loss duration.
c32af6b6fb6ab82a.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts within 30 sec; ES route (type 4) re-advertised for the ESI; aliasing path restored (remote PE forwards to DUT); packet loss ≤ 3 sec (default es-waiting-time).
Fail-on: Process does not restart, ES route missing after 60 sec, aliasing path not restored (remote PE cannot reach multi-homed site via DUT), or packet loss > 5 sec.

c335605ddd398c54.action_steps:
Setup:  On DUT, configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` on interface x-eth 0/0/1 for VLAN-IDs 1-3; configure auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt 65000:100. On neighbor PE, configure matching evpn1 instance with same RT and establish BGP EVPN session. Verify Type-3 IMET routes exchanged via show bgp evpn.
Action: From IXIA on x-eth 0/0/1, send 100 broadcast frames (dest MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) tagged VLAN 2 (in-bundle), then 100 broadcast frames tagged VLAN 10 (out-of-bundle). 64-byte frames, 10 fps.
Verify: show l2-services evpn evpn1 statistics; show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics detail (input-discard); packet capture on neighbor PE EVPN interface.
c335605ddd398c54.expectation:
Pass:    VLAN 2 BUM frames: 100 forwarded, captured on neighbor PE. VLAN 10 BUM frames: zero forwarded, x-eth 0/0/1 input-discard or evpn1 drop counter +100.
Fail-on: Any VLAN 10 frame captured on neighbor PE, or VLAN 2 frame count < 100 on neighbor PE, or drop counter unchanged for VLAN 10 traffic.

c3406710a5b75a5c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE in EVPN instance (VNI 1000, RT 65000:1000), verify `advertise-mac enable` (default), establish iBGP EVPN sessions with PE2 (router-ID 2.2.2.2, VTEP 10.0.0.2) and PE3 (router-ID 3.3.3.3, VTEP 10.0.0.3). Both PEs advertise the same EVPN Type-2 MAC route (MAC aa:bb:cc:00:00:01, IP 10.1.1.1) but PE2 with local-pref 100, PE3 with local-pref 50.
Action: On DUT, run `show bgp evpn route-type 2`, `show bgp evpn route-type 2 detail`, and `show l2-services evpn mac-table` to inspect received paths and best-path selection. Send traffic from IXIA-attached host (on DUT's local EVPN segment) destined to 10.1.1.1; capture VXLAN encap to verify next-hop.
Verify: DUT applies RFC7432bis ch.7.13 best-path selection: PE2's path (local-pref 100) is marked as best in BGP table; MAC table points to PE2's VTEP (10.0.0.2); IXIA-captured VXLAN frames are tunneled to 10.0.0.2.
c3406710a5b75a5c.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (for path diversity) + IXIA traffic gen
c3406710a5b75a5c.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects PE2's route (local-pref 100 > 50 per RFC7432bis ch.7.13); `show bgp evpn route-type 2 detail` marks PE2's path as best; VXLAN traffic forwarded to VTEP 10.0.0.2; MAC table entry points to PE2.
Fail-on: DUT selects PE3's path (local-pref 50), or no best-path selected, or traffic not forwarded to PE2's VTEP, or best-path selection ignores local-pref attribute.

c3ba42641fffa5ac.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT (configuration / l2-services / evpn) with advertise-mac enable (default); configure BGP peering with 'af-l2vpn evpn' enabled in configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor; connect IXIA as CE and send ARP or data frame to learn MAC M1 in DUT's MACT.
Action: Verify DUT advertises BGP EVPN route type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement per RFC7432 ch.7.2) for M1 with LT1 label value L1; neighbor PE imports route; neighbor PE sends MPLS-encapsulated known-unicast packet destined to M1 with label L1.
Verify: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-ip' displays M1 with LT1 label L1; capture ingress packet and confirm MPLS label stack top label is L1 (RFC7432bis ch.8.4); 'show evpn mac' confirms M1 present; packet forwarded to correct CE port.
c3ba42641fffa5ac.expectation:
Pass:    LT1 label allocated and advertised in type-2 route; ingress packet from neighbor PE contains LT1; DUT forwards to correct MACT and CE port.
Fail-on: Type-2 route advertised without LT1 label, or ingress packet missing LT1 label in MPLS stack, or forwarding to wrong port, or LT1 value shared across multiple MACTs in same EVPN instance.

c3cc01327bae5fef.action_steps:
Setup:  Under l2-services/vpls, configure auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt; under interface agg-eth 1, configure ethernet-segment (DUT derives ESI type 1 from LACP system-id and priority); establish BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE with l2vpn evpn address-family; configure IXIA as LACP partner for agg-eth 1.
Action: Activate LACP on agg-eth 1 (IXIA sends LACP PDUs; DUT forms bundle and generates ESI type 1 with MSB=0x01); capture BGP UPDATE to neighbor PE containing EVPN NLRI route type 1 (Ethernet A-D route); parse NLRI to extract 10-octet ESI field and verify ESI[0] = 1 (LACP type, within [0,5] per RFC7432bis §7.1); administratively shut member interface or withdraw LACP from IXIA; verify DUT sends BGP UPDATE withdrawal for route type 1.
Verify: show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 (confirm A-D route with RD, ESI, Ethernet Tag ID, MPLS label); show evpn ethernet-segment detail (display ESI value, confirm type 1); show lacp agg-eth 1 (confirm bundle operational state); packet capture on PE: parse EVPN NLRI route type 1, extract ESI byte 0, confirm value ∈ [0,5]; after LACP down, confirm route type 1 withdrawn from show bgp l2vpn evpn.
c3cc01327bae5fef.equipment: DUT + IXIA (LACP partner + traffic gen) + neighbor PE
c3cc01327bae5fef.expectation:
Pass:    Ethernet A-D route advertised when LACP operational with ESI[0] = 1 (in range [0,5]); A-D route NLRI contains RD (8 octets), ESI (10 octets with MSB in [0,5]), Ethernet Tag ID (4 octets), MPLS Label (3 octets) per RFC7432bis §7.1; route withdrawn when LACP transitions to down and re-advertised when LACP returns up.
Fail-on: A-D route advertised with ESI[0] outside [0,5]; A-D route missing required fields (RD, ESI, Ethernet Tag ID, or MPLS Label); route not advertised when ethernet-segment configured and LACP bundle operational; route not withdrawn when LACP goes down; LACP operational state and A-D route advertisement state diverge.

c3d4a93ad7e8979f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with an EVPN instance using `evpn` command with `service-type vlan-aware-bundle`, configure `ethernet-segment` under interface `agg-eth 0`, enable `auto-discovery` with `import-rt` and `export-rt`. Configure neighbor PE as BGP EVPN peer and capture BGP UPDATEs.
Action: Activate the EVPN instance and ethernet-segment on DUT. Monitor the order of BGP EVPN route advertisements received at neighbor PE. Send L2 traffic from IXIA (emulating CE behind ES) to remote CE behind neighbor PE.
Verify: Examine captured BGP UPDATEs for both Ethernet A-D per EVI routes (type 1, ET ID = VLAN-ID per attached BD) and Ethernet A-D per ED routes from DUT. Run `show bgp evpn` and `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs. Confirm neighbor PE does not install forwarding state from per-EVI routes until corresponding per-ED routes are received.
c3d4a93ad7e8979f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT advertises Ethernet A-D per EVI routes (one for each BD attached to the ES, with ET ID = VLAN-ID for vlan-aware-bundle or 0 for other service types) for its ES; neighbor PE defers forwarding via per-EVI routes until it receives the associated Ethernet A-D per ED routes, per RFC7432bis ch.8.4.
Fail-on: DUT fails to advertise per-EVI routes for each attached BD; or neighbor PE uses per-EVI route for traffic forwarding before receiving associated per-ED routes (race condition mishandled per RFC7432bis ch.8.4); or routes lack mandatory fields (RD, ESI, ET ID, MPLS Label, L2-Attr, RTs, Next Hop).

c3e973de489f0663.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with ethernet-segment (default identifier type 4, load-balancing-mode single-active) on agg-eth interface; populate MACT with 1000+ entries from local CE (attachment circuit ifIndex Next Hop) and remote PEs (remote IP Next Hop) across multiple VLANs; IXIA generating bidirectional L2 unicast traffic at 1 Gbps to exercise known MAC forwarding.
Action: Execute control-plane process restart (e.g., 'restart evpn-process') while MACT is actively forwarding traffic; monitor through es-waiting-time window (default 3s) and full recovery.
Verify: Use 'show l2-services evpn mac-table' to confirm all MACT entries (VLAN-ID, MAC Address, ESI, Next Hop type/value) repopulate correctly; 'show interface agg-eth ethernet-segment' confirms ESI operational; IXIA reports packet loss duration and verifies no duplicate/corrupted MAC frames post-recovery.
c3e973de489f0663.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + neighbor PE
c3e973de489f0663.expectation:
Pass:    MACT repopulates with 100% of pre-reset entries within 30s; data-path outage ≤ (es-waiting-time + 5s); all Next Hop types (local ifIndex, remote IP) restored correctly; no MAC duplication or Next Hop corruption
Fail-on: Data-path outage > 10s, MACT missing >1% of entries after 60s, ESI stuck in non-operational state, duplicate MAC entries detected, or control-plane fails to auto-restart

c448392279f43f7f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN service `evpn test-svc service-type vlan-based` under configuration / l2-services. Physical interface x-eth 0/0/1 exists and is in no-shutdown state.
Action: Enter configuration / l2-services / evpn test-svc and add interface x-eth 0/0/1 as an attachment circuit. Commit. Remove the interface using the `no` form. Commit again.
Verify: Run `show running-config` after first commit and confirm x-eth 0/0/1 appears under evpn test-svc. Run `show running-config` after removal and confirm x-eth 0/0/1 is absent from the service. Check `show l2-services evpn test-svc` (or equivalent) to verify service operational state remains clean.
c448392279f43f7f.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
c448392279f43f7f.expectation:
Pass:    Interface x-eth 0/0/1 successfully binds to the EVPN service and persists in running-config; `no interface` cleanly removes it without orphaning service state or leaving stale references.
Fail-on: Interface configuration rejected with error, interface not visible in running-config after commit, `no interface` fails to remove it, or service enters error/down state after removal.

c45a06d1f771bf5e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet segment (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on eth1 and eth2; configure EVPN instance with `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; establish BGP EVPN session (`af-l2vpn evpn`) with neighbor PE; verify Type 1 Ethernet A-D per ES routes exchanged with distinct LT4 labels for eth1 (ifIndex e.g. 100) and eth2 (ifIndex e.g. 101).
Action: From neighbor PE, send BUM traffic (unknown unicast MAC destination) encapsulated with MPLS label LT3 (Ethernet segment identifier) plus LT4 label matching eth1's ifIndex; monitor ingress on DUT and egress on both ES interfaces.
Verify: Inspect BUM Split Horizon Table via `show evpn ethernet-segment` or `show evpn split-horizon` (verify ifIndex→LT4 mappings for eth1, eth2); packet capture on eth1 and eth2; confirm BUM frame floods ONLY to eth2 (eth1 excluded per LT4 split horizon).
c45a06d1f771bf5e.expectation:
Pass:    BUM Split Horizon Table contains one entry per ES interface (eth1 ifIndex=100 LT4=label_A, eth2 ifIndex=101 LT4=label_B); BUM frame from remote PE floods to eth2 only (eth1 blocked by LT4 match); no duplicate frames on eth1; BUM flood counters increment in `show evpn statistics`.
Fail-on: BUM frame forwarded to eth1 despite LT4 match (split horizon violation), or BUM not flooded to eth2, or Split Horizon Table missing entries, or duplicate LT4 labels assigned, or table not created per EVPN instance.

c4d2b5d76f5d2619.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish baseline EVPN configuration with ethernet-segment configured under `agg-eth` interface. Confirm no explicit `es-waiting-time` is set (default 3 seconds applies).
Action: Configure `es-waiting-time` to 5 seconds under the appropriate EVPN or ethernet-segment context, commit. Query `show running-config` and verify the non-default value appears. Issue `no es-waiting-time`, commit, and confirm the configuration reverts to default (3 sec, may not appear in running-config if default).
Verify: `show running-config` displays `es-waiting-time 5` after configuration; after `no` form, verify via `show evpn ethernet-segment` detail or equivalent that the effective waiting time is 3 seconds (default) and the explicit config line is absent.
c4d2b5d76f5d2619.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
c4d2b5d76f5d2619.expectation:
Pass:    Configured value (5 sec) persists in running-config and takes effect; `no es-waiting-time` restores the documented default of 3 seconds with no explicit config line remaining.
Fail-on: Command rejected, configured value not stored, `no` form fails to restore 3-second default, or show output indicates wrong waiting time value.

c555abd106a87290.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Ethernet Segment with identical ESI on DUT and neighbor PE (`ethernet-segment` under `interface agg-eth`); configure DF election using Highest-Preference algorithm with same local policy override on both PEs; configure EVPN instance with multiple Ethernet Tags (VLANs); verify current DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment detail`; IXIA sends continuous BUM traffic across all VLANs.
Action: Perform `shutdown` on the active DF's LAG interface (`interface agg-eth`), forcing DF re-election for all Ethernet Tags to the standby PE; measure convergence window; restore interface with `no shutdown` and verify DF role reversion.
Verify: Execute `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` on both PEs to confirm DF role transition for all Ethernet Tags; capture BUM forwarding on IXIA to measure packet loss and verify zero duplicate frames; confirm original DF returns to correct role (DF or non-DF per preference) after interface recovery.
c555abd106a87290.expectation:
Pass:    DF re-election completes for all Ethernet Tags within mass-withdrawal convergence bound (RFC7432 §8); standby PE assumes DF role; BUM traffic resumes; convergence ≤50ms; zero duplicate frames; consistent DF state across all tags post-recovery.
Fail-on: Split-brain state (both PEs forward BUM for same tag) or no-DF state (neither PE forwards); convergence >100ms or above vendor spec; persistent duplicate BUM delivery; stale or inconsistent DF election state after interface restoration.

c5560c54d1b15f0c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE in all-active multi-homing to ES (agg-eth-1 with ethernet-segment identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09, load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving default) for EVI 1000 BD 100 using ingress-replication; verify DUT is DF; attach IXIA to ES interface and DUT core interface.
Action: From IXIA on core interface, inject MPLS-encapsulated unknown unicast packet with downstream label advertised in P-tunnel attribute for EVI 1000, destination MAC 00:11:22:33:44:FF (not in DUT FDB).
Verify: show l2-services forwarding-database instance 1000 (confirm 00:11:22:33:44:FF absent); IXIA on ES interface captures flooded frame with DA=00:11:22:33:44:FF; show evpn ethernet-segment (DUT is DF).
c5560c54d1b15f0c.expectation:
Pass:    Unknown unicast frame floods to ES without DUT performing MAC lookup; MAC 00:11:22:33:44:FF does NOT appear in FDB after test; IXIA on ES receives frame with original DA unchanged.
Fail-on: DUT performs MAC lookup or learns 00:11:22:33:44:FF from MPLS-encapsulated BUM; frame dropped instead of flooded; selective forwarding based on MAC table instead of default BUM flood.

c645870294879ff2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT interface agg-eth with `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09; service-carving highest-random-weight` and enable EVPN instance. Configure 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) with same ESI and DF election method; establish BGP EVPN session.
Action: Bring up multi-homed CE behind both PEs; trigger Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) route exchange carrying DF election info and DF Preference extended community per RFC7432bis ch.8.6.
Verify: Run `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` on DUT and equivalent on 3rd-party PE; capture BGP EVPN Type-4 routes; confirm DF Preference extended community present and both PEs agree on primary/backup DF roles for each EVI.
c645870294879ff2.expectation:
Pass:    Type-4 routes exchanged with DF Preference extended community per RFC7432bis ch.8.6; DUT and 3rd-party PE converge on same primary DF and backup DF for each VLAN; `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays consistent DF role (primary or backup); traffic forwards through primary DF only.
Fail-on: Type-4 route encoding mismatch, DF Preference attribute rejected/absent, split-brain (both PEs claim primary DF), DF role inconsistency between vendors, or traffic black-hole due to DF disagreement.

c64dc48bb2d40103.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with shared ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 to dual-homed CE; create EVPN instance `evpn evpn-test service-type vlan-based`, enable `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; verify PE1 is DF, PE2 is non-DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment` DF election status.
Action: From remote PE, send BUM traffic (unknown unicast MAC dest) labeled with LT1 to both PE1 and PE2; send known unicast with MACT entry; send unicast from local CE on PE1 with source MAC already in MACT.
Verify: On PE2 (non-DF): `show evpn mac-table` shows remote MACs but `show interface <ce-facing> statistics` confirms zero BUM egress to ES. On PE1 (DF): BUM forwarded to CE. `show evpn forwarding-table` on both PEs confirms local CE traffic not hairpinned (no egress on ingress CE port). Remote unicast hits MACT, local unicast does not loop.
c64dc48bb2d40103.expectation:
Pass:    Non-DF PE (PE2) receives BUM from remote but drops it to local ES (zero egress packets on CE-facing port for BUM). DF PE (PE1) forwards BUM to ES. Local CE traffic shows ingress on CE port but zero egress on same port (split-horizon). Remote known unicast forwarded per MACT to correct local CE only.
Fail-on: Non-DF forwards BUM to local ES (non-zero BUM egress), or local CE traffic loops back (egress count on ingress port >0), or remote traffic forwarded to remote CEs, or LT1-labeled traffic not dropped when MACT entry missing.

c67b162e5172a437.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT + Cisco PE + Juniper PE in EVPN instance EVI 100 (RD/RT 100:100), iBGP sessions established. Cisco PE advertises MAC/IP RT-2 for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff / 10.1.1.1 with Default Gateway extended community (type 0x03 subtype 0x0D); Juniper PE advertises identical MAC/IP RT-2 without Default Gateway community.
Action: On DUT, verify route import via 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-ip-advertisement 10.1.1.1'; confirm both routes received. Check best-path selection (Cisco's route with Default Gateway community must be selected per RFC7432bis §7.13.1 criterion #1). Verify 'show l2-services vpls 100 mac-table mac aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff' shows next-hop = Cisco PE MPLS tunnel.
Verify: IXIA sends ICMP echo to 10.1.1.1 via DUT's AC; capture egress—frame must forward to Cisco PE tunnel (not Juniper). Withdraw Cisco's route; confirm DUT converges to Juniper's route. Restore Cisco's route; confirm DUT re-selects Cisco (selection criteria order is stable and deterministic across vendors).
c67b162e5172a437.equipment: DUT + Cisco PE + Juniper PE + IXIA (three-PE topology required to test best-path selection with competing routes from different vendors)
c67b162e5172a437.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects Cisco PE's route (has Default Gateway community) over Juniper PE's route (no community), enforcing criterion #1 precedence; BGP output marks Cisco's route as best ('>' indicator); MAC table next-hop points to Cisco; ICMP forwards via Cisco tunnel; route withdrawal/restore triggers correct re-convergence per criterion order.
Fail-on: DUT selects Juniper's route when Cisco's route with Default Gateway community is available, OR selection nondeterministic, OR DUT fails to decode Default Gateway ext-community from Cisco/Juniper routes, OR data-plane forwards to wrong PE, OR tie-breaking violates RFC-mandated criterion order.

c6c9a1d0d8286a13.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based`, attach VLAN 100 AC, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`; configure neighbor PE with matching RT and VLAN 100 AC; establish MP-BGP EVPN peering (AFI/SAFI 25/70).
Action: Enable `advertise-mac enable` (default); send L2 unicast frames from IXIA to trigger MAC/IP route (type 2) and L2 broadcast to trigger IMET route (type 3) advertisements.
Verify: Issue `show bgp evpn routes` on DUT and neighbor PE; decode EVPN NLRI for type 2 and type 3 routes; inspect Ethernet Tag ID field (4 octets immediately following Route Type in NLRI).
c6c9a1d0d8286a13.expectation:
Pass:    All EVPN routes (type 2 MAC/IP Advertisement, type 3 Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag) advertised by the vlan-based service carry Ethernet Tag ID = 0x00000000 per RFC7432bis §6.1 MUST.
Fail-on: Any EVPN route from vlan-based service instance shows Ethernet Tag ID ≠ 0, or routes fail to advertise despite service operational and `advertise-mac enable`.

c6cb4fd178d3432c.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN with Ingress Replication already exercised in prior rows (prerequisite: EVI configured with advertise-mac enabled and unknown-mac-flooding enabled at configuration/l2-services/evpn; BGP EVPN peering with af-l2vpn evpn active; Type-3 IMET routes advertised).
Action: Collect tech-support bundle (e.g., 'show tech-support' or equivalent vendor command) including BGP L2VPN EVPN subsystem, L2-services configuration, kernel FIB, and syslog.
Verify: Parse tech-support output for: (1) 'show bgp evpn route-type 3' (or 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type imet') displaying PMSI Tunnel attribute with Flag=0, Tunnel Type=6 (Ingress Replication), MPLS Label=<advertised LT3 label>, Tunnel Identifier=<DUT originating router IP> per RFC6514 ch.5; (2) running-config section configuration/l2-services/evpn showing advertise-mac and unknown-mac-flooding state; (3) syslog entries for EVPN Type-3 route updates; (4) MPLS forwarding table entries for the EVI's multicast tunnel.
c6cb4fd178d3432c.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains Type-3 IMET routes with PMSI Tunnel attribute fully decoded per RFC6514 ch.5 Ingress Replication: Type=6, Flag=0, MPLS Label=<valid LT3 non-zero label>, Tunnel Identifier=<DUT router-id/loopback IP>; includes running-config, syslog, and FIB tables.
Fail-on: PMSI attribute missing from Type-3 routes; Tunnel Type != 6; Flag != 0; Tunnel Identifier != DUT originating IP; MPLS Label = 0 or absent; missing 'show bgp evpn' output; missing configuration/l2-services/evpn config; missing syslog; missing MPLS FIB entries.

c6e3b30625df404f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with EVPN instance (`evpn <evpn-name> service-type vlan-based`, `auto-discovery`, `import-rt <route-target>`, `export-rt <route-target>`), multi-homed ES with same ESI value on both PEs, BGP with `af-l2vpn evpn`, CE multi-homed via LACP.
Action: Bring up ES and BGP peering; capture BGP UPDATEs between PEs; decode Type 1 per-ES routes (ET ID=MAX-ET, MPLS Label=0) and Type 1 per-EVI routes (specific ET ID, allocated label); extract ESI Label extended community value from each PE's per-ES route; verify via `show bgp l2vpn evpn` that ESI Label is identical across all per-ES advertisements for this ES from both PEs.
Verify: Type 1 per-ES and per-EVI routes present; packet decode confirms RFC 7432bis §8.2.1 structure (RD, ESI, ET ID, MPLS Label, ESI Label EC with Single-Active flag); ESI Label value is identical on all per-ES routes from both PEs for the shared ES; RTs present per configured `export-rt`.
c6e3b30625df404f.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
c6e3b30625df404f.expectation:
Pass:    Type 1 routes (per-ES and per-EVI) advertised per RFC 7432bis §8.2.1; ESI Label extended community value is identical across all per-ES routes for the ES from both PEs; vendor enforces ESI Label consistency per SFS §3.1 (raises alarm if mismatch detected, saves mismatched route but does not use until all routes updated or withdrawn — vendor DELTA behavior stricter than RFC MUST alone).
Fail-on: ESI Label values differ across per-ES Type 1 routes for same ES; missing ESI Label extended community; packet encoding deviates from RFC 7432bis §8.2.1; routes with mismatched ESI Labels are installed and used without alarm (fails vendor DELTA enforcement).

c70b2db64fc8dd10.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure loopback0 interface with IPv4 address 10.1.1.1/32. Create EVPN instance 'EVPN100' using command `evpn EVPN100 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` in configuration/l2-services mode; do NOT configure an explicit RD.
Action: Commit configuration; system automatically generates the RD for EVPN100 per RFC7432bis ch.7.9.
Verify: Execute `show evpn instance EVPN100 detail` and `show running-config l2-services` to capture the auto-generated RD value and confirm Type 1 format.
c70b2db64fc8dd10.expectation:
Pass:    Auto-generated RD format is Type 1 (1:10.1.1.1:N) where Administrator subfield = 10.1.1.1 (loopback0 IP) and Assigned Number N = unique EVPN instance number generated by system. RD appears in operational state and running-config.
Fail-on: No RD auto-generated, or RD Type ≠ 1, or Administrator subfield ≠ 10.1.1.1, or Assigned Number = 0/missing, or RD collides with another EVPN instance RD.

c7447e8c2c7a2c50.action_steps:
Setup:  Boot DUT, establish CLI session, configure EVPN instance using `evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-based` under `configuration / l2-services`, bind at least one interface (e.g., `x-eth 0/0/1`), and commit.
Action: At operational prompt type `show ?` and locate `show interface detail` in help output; type `show int` and press TAB to verify completion path; type `show interface detail ?` to display parameter options; execute `show interface detail` (all interfaces); execute `show interface detail x-eth 0/0/1` (specific interface bound to EVPN).
Verify: Confirm `show interface detail` is present in `show ?` with non-empty description; TAB from `show int` completes without error; `show interface detail ?` lists documented parameters (interface name, type selectors); output displays admin/operational state, speed, MTU, counters, and EVPN binding details (instance name `test-evpn`, service-type `vlan-based`).
c7447e8c2c7a2c50.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
c7447e8c2c7a2c50.expectation:
Pass:    Command discoverable via `show ?` help; TAB completion from `show int` succeeds; sub-token `?` lists parameters matching CLI specification; output for EVPN-bound interface includes EVPN instance name, service-type, and L2 forwarding state.
Fail-on: Command missing from `show ?` output; TAB fails or offers undocumented tokens; sub-token help diverges from CLI specification; output for EVPN-bound interface omits EVPN instance name or service-type fields.

c7688c5c21910c25.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure one Ethernet Segment (ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11) on DUT; configure two EVPN instances (evpn-100, evpn-200) with service-type vlan-based, each with auto-discovery, matching import-rt/export-rt; configure BGP neighbor to PE with af-l2vpn evpn enabled; bring up BGP session and start tcpdump/Wireshark capture on BGP TCP port 179.
Action: Activate the ES interface to trigger Type 1 route advertisement; wait for BGP UPDATE messages containing EVPN NLRI Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes; stop capture after updates received.
Verify: Decode captured Type 1 routes for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11; confirm 2 routes received (one per EVPN instance); check each route: RD present, ESI = 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, Ethernet Tag ID = 0xFFFFFFFF (MAX-ET), MPLS Label field = 0, ESI Label Extended Community present with Single-Active flag; extract ESI Label value from Extended Community in both routes and confirm identical.
c7688c5c21910c25.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE with BGP session packet capture (tcpdump/Wireshark)
c7688c5c21910c25.expectation:
Pass:    Both Type 1 routes carry identical ESI Label values in their ESI Label Extended Community; route structure matches RFC7432bis §8.2.1 (ET ID=MAX-ET, MPLS Label=0, Next Hop=DUT PE IP); 2 routes received (one per EVPN instance).
Fail-on: ESI Label value differs between the two Type 1 routes for the same ESI; ET ID ≠ 0xFFFFFFFF; MPLS Label ≠ 0; missing ESI Label Extended Community; fewer than 2 Type 1 routes; Next Hop ≠ DUT IP.

c7b0a3eb7379424d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface agg-eth 0 with ethernet-segment and set a specific ESI type (using identifier command with explicit type); commit and verify ESI is configured.
Action: Issue `no identifier` under the ethernet-segment configuration mode and commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` shows ethernet-segment block with no identifier statement; `show ethernet-segment interface agg-eth 0` displays a system-generated type 4 ESI.
c7b0a3eb7379424d.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
c7b0a3eb7379424d.expectation:
Pass:    After `no identifier`, interface reverts to type 4 ESI; show ethernet-segment confirms type byte is 0x04 and value is system-generated.
Fail-on: ESI remains at previously configured type/value, or ESI disappears entirely, or `no identifier` command rejected.

c7bfae9493c241d0.action_steps:
Setup:  Create a route-policy that denies EVPN routes with extended community 65000:100 and permits all others. Configure BGP EVPN peering with a neighbor PE and attach the route-policy to the inbound direction for the L2VPN EVPN address-family on this neighbor.
Action: From the neighbor PE, advertise 8 EVPN Type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes: 4 tagged with extended community 65000:100 (to be denied) and 4 without the community (to be permitted). Commit configuration and wait 30 seconds for route processing.
Verify: On the DUT, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn` to list EVPN routes in the local RIB. Run `show running-config` to confirm the route-policy is attached. Count routes in the output: exactly 4 routes (those without the community) should be installed. The 4 routes with community 65000:100 must not appear.
c7bfae9493c241d0.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
c7bfae9493c241d0.expectation:
Pass:    Exactly 4 EVPN routes are installed in the local RIB (those without community 65000:100). The 4 routes with the community are blocked by the inbound route-policy before MAC/IP installation. BGP session remains established.
Fail-on: All 8 routes are installed (route-policy not applied), or 0 routes are installed (route-policy blocks all traffic), or the wrong 4 routes are installed (route-policy logic inverted), or BGP session flaps during route processing.

c80a86bd98a1dbce.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT (AS 65001) with EVPN instance and `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled under BGP neighbor 10.1.1.2 in VRF context. Establish eBGP peering. Configure remote PE to advertise EVPN Type-2 MAC/IP routes with AS_PATH manipulated to contain AS 65001 once (simulating route reflection scenario).
Action: Without allow-as-in configured, verify routes with local AS in AS_PATH are rejected per default loop-detection. Configure allow-as-in count=1 under neighbor af-l2vpn evpn context. Re-advertise route with local AS appearing once; verify acceptance. Advertise second route with local AS appearing twice in AS_PATH; verify rejection.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn routes` displays routes with single local-AS occurrence accepted, routes with double occurrence rejected. `show running-config routing bgp` confirms allow-as-in configuration persisted under neighbor af-l2vpn evpn stanza. `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor <ip>` shows received/accepted route counts match expected behavior.
c80a86bd98a1dbce.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
c80a86bd98a1dbce.expectation:
