Pass:    Default (no allow-as-in) rejects any route with local AS in AS_PATH. After configuring count=1: routes with local AS appearing ≤1 time accepted; routes with ≥2 occurrences rejected. Configuration persists in running-config.
Fail-on: Routes with local AS appearing twice accepted when count=1 configured, or single-occurrence routes rejected, or configuration not applied/saved, or feature does not operate per-neighbor per-address-family as documented.

c81540796490067f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with advertise-mac enable and unknown-mac-flooding enable; establish all-active multi-homed ES (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) between DUT (PE1) and neighbor PE2 connected to CE1; remote PE3 hosts CE2; verify BGP EVPN af-l2vpn evpn sessions up and RT-2 routes exchanged.
Action: From CE1, send traffic with 3 new source MACs (SA1, SA2, SA3) to: (a) known unicast DA (CE2's learned MAC), (b) unknown unicast DA (not in any PE MAC table). Capture forwarding at DUT: verify source MAC lookup occurs first per RFC requirement, then destination MAC lookup, then forward or flood decision.
Verify: show mac-table confirms SA1/SA2/SA3 learned locally on DUT; show evpn mac confirms RT-2 MAC/IP advertisement sent for each; known unicast traffic forwarded as single copy to PE3 (check forwarding counters, no EVPN BUM label); unknown unicast flooded to PE2 and PE3 with EVPN BUM label; multi-homing DF election prevents duplicate delivery at CE2 (IXIA captures single copy); check that control-word setting and ESI split-horizon filtering operate correctly.
c81540796490067f.equipment: DUT (PE1) + neighbor PE (PE2) in all-active multi-homed ES + remote PE3 + IXIA traffic gen at CE1 and capture at CE2
c81540796490067f.expectation:
Pass:    All 3 source MACs learned in local MAC table and advertised via RT-2; known unicast forwarded to single PE (PE3) with no flooding; unknown unicast flooded to all remote PEs (PE2, PE3) using EVPN BUM label per unknown-mac-flooding enable policy; CE2 receives exactly one copy of each frame (multi-homing DF/split-horizon working); no errors in EVPN or forwarding counters.
Fail-on: Source MAC lookup skipped (MACs not learned locally); known unicast flooded instead of unicast-forwarded; unknown unicast not flooded when unknown-mac-flooding enable set; duplicate frames delivered to CE2 (multi-homing DF broken); forwarding drops/errors in counters; RT-2 routes not generated.

c8336361034b1d64.action_steps:
Problem: Verify that tech-support diagnostic bundle captures static MAC configuration and EVPN advertisement state so field engineers can troubleshoot static MAC binding issues without live CLI access.
Method: Configure a static MAC binding on an EVPN instance with a neighbor PE peer, generate tech-support bundle, and inspect for config stanzas, MAC table entries, and BGP route advertisements.
Setup:  Configure a vlan-based EVPN instance with auto-discovery enabled. Under the EVPN instance, add `mac-address-static <mac-address> interface <interface>` binding a specific MAC to a local interface. Establish BGP EVPN peering with a neighbor PE to verify route advertisement.
Action: Execute `tech-support` command to generate diagnostic bundle. Save the bundle off-box and extract contents for inspection.
Verify: Inspect bundle for: (1) `show running-config` section containing the `mac-address-static` command under the EVPN instance; (2) `show evpn mac address-table` output displaying the static MAC entry marked as static or local; (3) `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output showing Type 2 MAC/IP Advertisement route for the static MAC; (4) any EVPN-related syslogs or alarms from the session.
c8336361034b1d64.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains all four elements: running-config with mac-address-static line, MAC address table showing the static entry, BGP L2VPN EVPN route table with Type 2 route for that MAC, and session logs.
Fail-on: Missing any of: the static MAC configuration stanza, the MAC table entry showing static binding, or the BGP EVPN Type 2 route advertisement for the configured MAC.

c83bc5866817657c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based`, EVI 100, MTU 9000, Control Word enabled, ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 on interface. Configure neighbor PE2 at 10.0.0.2 with `af-l2vpn evpn` activated under `routing bgp 65000`. Establish BGP session and enable packet capture or BGP update logging on PE2.
Action: Activate EVPN instance on DUT. Capture or display BGP UPDATE messages containing RT-3 (Inclusive Multicast, route-type 3) and RT-1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI, route-type 1) for EVI 100 on PE2 using `show bgp l2vpn evpn <prefix> detail` or equivalent with extended-community visibility.
Verify: Parse L2-Attr Extended Community (Type 0x06, Sub-type 0x04) from both route types. For RT-3: MTU field = 9000 (0x2328), C-bit (Control Word) = 1 if enabled, F-bit (Flow Label) per config, P-bit = 0, B-bit = 0. For RT-1: MTU field = 0x0000 (MBZ), C-bit = 0, F-bit = 0, P-bit and B-bit reflect DF election state (Primary/Backup). Confirm all Reserved and MBZ octets are zero.
c83bc5866817657c.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN capable) + packet capture or BGP route inspector
c83bc5866817657c.expectation:
Pass:    RT-3 L2-Attr Extended Community carries MTU=9000, C/F flags matching DUT config, P=0, B=0. RT-1 L2-Attr Extended Community carries P/B bits per DF state, MTU=0, C=0, F=0. Type/Sub-type = 0x06/0x04 in both. All MBZ fields zeroed per RFC7432bis §7.11.1 figure format.
Fail-on: RT-3 with non-zero P or B bits. RT-1 with non-zero MTU, C, or F fields. Missing L2-Attr Extended Community on either route type. Wrong Type/Sub-type values. Non-zero Reserved or MBZ octets.

c86fad8f633b8093.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure evpn under configuration/l2-services on DUT and two neighbor PEs; configure ethernet-segment under interface agg-eth 0 on all three PEs with identical ESI; set Preference to 100 on all PEs for this ES; enable Don't Preempt capability on PE2 only; assign PE1 (DUT) loopback 10.0.0.1, PE2 10.0.0.2, PE3 10.0.0.3. Verify initial DF election via show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4. Start IXIA bidirectional traffic.
Action: Identify current DF from Type-4 route; administratively disable agg-eth 0 on current DF to trigger re-election; after convergence, identify new DF; disable agg-eth 0 on new DF to trigger second re-election. Capture BGP updates for each transition.
Verify: After first DF failure, show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 and show evpn ethernet-segment confirm PE2 becomes DF (Don't Preempt capability per RFC9785 §4.1); after second failure, confirm PE1 becomes DF (lowest-IP tiebreaker per RFC9785 §4.1); IXIA reports < 50 ms traffic loss per event; no duplicate frames.
c86fad8f633b8093.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs (3-PE multihoming rig) + IXIA traffic gen
c86fad8f633b8093.expectation:
Pass:    First re-election selects PE with Don't Preempt set (PE2); second re-election selects PE with lowest IP (PE1); Type-4 routes contain DF Election Extended Community with Preference=100, correct algorithm, and DF bit; each failover converges < 50 ms; no split-brain or duplicate DFs.
Fail-on: Wrong PE elected as DF (violates Don't Preempt priority or lowest-IP fallback); either failover > 200 ms; duplicate frames; multiple PEs simultaneously claim DF role; stale or missing Type-4 route after re-election.

c8f77ff960dc9a40.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance (vlan-based service type) under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`. Configure the mac-limit parameter to restrict learning to 10 MAC addresses for the service. Attach IXIA traffic generator to the EVPN access port.
Action: From IXIA, inject L2 frames with 15 unique source MAC addresses (exceeding the configured limit of 10) at 100 pps. Let traffic run for 60 seconds to ensure all MACs have opportunity to be learned. Issue `show running-config` to confirm the mac-limit value persists. Issue the no form of the mac-limit command and re-inject the same 15-MAC traffic stream.
Verify: Query the MAC address table for the EVPN instance via `show l2-services evpn <name> mac-table` or equivalent. Count learned entries. Inspect syslog or `show l2-services evpn <name> statistics` for any mac-limit-violation events or dropped-learning counters. After removing the limit, re-count learned MACs.
c8f77ff960dc9a40.expectation:
Pass:    With mac-limit configured to 10, exactly 10 MAC addresses appear in the table (first 10 learned); MACs 11–15 do not appear; limit-violation counter increments or syslog shows limit-exceeded events for the 5 rejected MACs. After `no mac-limit`, all 15 MACs are learned.
Fail-on: More than 10 MACs learned while the limit is active, OR fewer than 10 MACs learned when fewer than 10 were offered, OR no limit-violation indication when excess MACs arrive, OR the removed limit still blocks learning.

c9522ec383bd8951.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish BGP EVPN peering between DUT and neighbor PE; configure inbound-soft-reconfiguration under configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn; apply inbound route-map that initially blocks specific route-targets; verify neighbor Established and advertising EVPN type-2/type-3 routes.
Action: Record BGP session uptime via show bgp l2vpn evpn summary; modify inbound route-map to accept previously blocked route-targets; commit and issue clear bgp l2vpn evpn <neighbor-ip> soft in to re-apply policy without session reset.
Verify: show bgp l2vpn evpn summary confirms session uptime unchanged (no reset occurred); show bgp l2vpn evpn displays newly accepted routes per updated policy; show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <neighbor-ip> confirms soft-reconfiguration enabled with stored-prefix count > 0.
c9522ec383bd8951.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (physical or IXIA BGP EVPN emulator)
c9522ec383bd8951.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session uptime unchanged after policy modification; route table reflects new inbound policy without session teardown; soft-reconfiguration stored-prefix count matches neighbor's advertised routes (NLRI caching active).
Fail-on: BGP session resets (uptime → 0), or routes not re-evaluated despite policy change, or stored-prefix count = 0 indicating caching inactive, or peer sends NOTIFICATION due to missing Route Refresh capability.

c9a106e9f24b543a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with same ESI (identifier 0 type0-value 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88), load-balancing-mode single-active, service-carving default; establish EVPN BGP session; verify both PEs advertise RT-4 for ESI; start IXIA bidirectional traffic (1Gbps) to/from multihomed CE.
Action: Run continuous traffic for ≥24 hours; periodically shut/no-shut DUT's agg-eth link to CE (every 2h cycle, 15min down) to force aliasing path forwarding via neighbor PE; monitor traffic continuity and system resources.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` operational throughout; `show platform process memory` RSS/VSZ delta <5%; IXIA packet loss <0.01% during aliasing transitions; `show evpn mac` stable MAC route count; traffic forwards via neighbor PE during local link-down phases.
c9a106e9f24b543a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (same ESI) + multihomed CE + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
c9a106e9f24b543a.expectation:
Pass:    Aliasing path forwards CE-destined traffic via neighbor PE when local link down; memory growth <5% over 24h; IXIA packet loss <0.01%; ESI and MAC routes stable (no flaps).
Fail-on: Traffic black-hole when local link down (aliasing failure); memory growth ≥10%; persistent packet loss >0.1%; ESI flapping or unexpected RT-4/MAC route withdrawals; functional regression in ES or EVPN state.

c9de17703db4069f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on PE1 (DUT) with `unknown-mac-flooding enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`. Configure PE2 and PE3 as multi-homed to remote CE-2 (identical ESI, all-active mode). Verify BGP EVPN Type-1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) and Type-3 (IMET) routes exchanged; PE2/PE3 Type-1 routes show same ESI.
Action: From IXIA simulating CE-1 (attached to PE1), send broadcast ARP packet (dest MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF, VLAN per EVPN instance). Trigger PE1 ingress replication.
Verify: IXIA monitors at PE2 and PE3 VTEP uplinks capture VXLAN-encapsulated packets. Check `show l2-services evpn statistics` on PE1 (broadcast Tx counter), PE2, PE3 (broadcast Rx counters). Verify `show l2-services evpn routes type 3` on PE1 includes both PE2 and PE3 endpoints.
c9de17703db4069f.equipment: Three PEs (DUT + 2 neighbors multi-homed to same CE) + IXIA
c9de17703db4069f.expectation:
Pass:    IXIA captures confirm both PE2 and PE3 receive exactly one copy of the broadcast. `show l2-services evpn statistics`: PE1 Tx = 2, PE2 Rx = 1, PE3 Rx = 1. RFC7432bis §16.1 MUST satisfied: broadcast sent to all remote PEs regardless of multi-homing.
Fail-on: Broadcast missing at PE2 or PE3 (violates "must be sent to all remote PEs"). Duplicate packets at any PE (Rx >1). Counter mismatch (PE1 Tx ≠ 2). Only one of the multi-homed PEs receives traffic (aliasing incorrectly applied at sender).

c9f646dee3dcab73.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 100 on DUT with ES 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 multi-homed to neighbor PE; on neighbor PE set `advertise-mac disable` in that EVPN instance to suppress MAC/IP Advertisement routes. Verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` on DUT sees Ethernet A-D per-EVI route from neighbor PE with MPLS label field populated (LT2).
Action: IXIA injects known unicast frame destined to MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF behind the ES; DUT must forward to neighbor PE. Capture MPLS-encapsulated packet on DUT→PE link.
Verify: `show evpn instance 100 ethernet-segment 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 label` confirms LT2 allocated; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 rd <DUT-RD>` shows LT2 label value in route; packet capture shows MPLS stack with LT2 (not LT1) as bottom label.
c9f646dee3dcab73.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (same ES) + IXIA traffic gen
c9f646dee3dcab73.expectation:
Pass:    LT2 label allocated per ES, advertised in Ethernet A-D per-EVI route (RFC7432bis ch.7.1 route type 1), and present in MPLS encapsulation when forwarding known unicast to PE that suppressed MAC/IP Advertisement (RFC7432bis ch.8.4). LT2 value in BGP matches label in packet.
Fail-on: No LT2 label allocated for ES, Ethernet A-D per-EVI route missing MPLS label field, packet uses wrong label (LT1 or broadcast label), or packet flooded instead of unicast-forwarded.

ca01d2f16daa6a10.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0 with a known 10-octet ESI (e.g., 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11); configure BGP EVPN session (af-l2vpn evpn) between DUT and neighbor PE; enable BGP update logging or packet capture on neighbor PE.
Action: Activate agg-eth 0; DUT advertises Ethernet Segment route (type 4) carrying ES-Import RT extended community per RFC7432bis ch.7.6.
Verify: On neighbor PE, inspect the received type-4 route via show commands or decoded packet capture; extract the ES-Import Route Target extended community value; confirm it equals the first 6 octets of the configured ESI (0x01:11:11:11:11:11 in the example).
ca01d2f16daa6a10.expectation:
Pass:    ES-Import RT extended community in the type-4 route exactly matches the high-order 6 octets of the DUT's configured ESI, per RFC7432bis ch.7.6.
Fail-on: ES-Import RT does not match the first 6 octets of the ESI, type-4 route not advertised, or ES-Import RT extended community malformed or absent.

ca450ce488416621.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT at configuration / l2-services mode; no existing EVPN instance with name "test-evpn-01".
Action: Issue `evpn test-evpn-01 service-type vlan-based` and commit; verify CLI transitions to EVPN configuration mode. Exit and repeat for service-type vlan-bundle, vlan-aware-bundle, and port-based (using unique instance names). Create one instance without service-type parameter to observe default behavior. Execute `no evpn test-evpn-01` and commit.
Verify: After each instance creation, run `show running-config l2-services` to confirm evpn stanza with correct service-type. Observe mode prompt indicating EVPN configuration context. After `no evpn`, verify instance removed from running-config.
ca450ce488416621.expectation:
Pass:    All four service-type variants (vlan-based, vlan-bundle, vlan-aware-bundle, port-based) successfully create instances; mode transition occurs; running-config reflects correct evpn syntax and parameters; default behavior documented; `no evpn` removes instance without residue.
Fail-on: Any service-type variant rejected; mode transition fails; running-config missing or malformed; `no evpn` fails or leaves orphaned configuration.

ca94abf2b6dc216a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance (advertise-mac enable under configuration/l2-services/evpn) and iBGP neighbor with af-l2vpn evpn enabled (configuration/routing/bgp/vrf/neighbor). Use test neighbor PE or IXIA BGP emulator capable of crafting EVPN routes with duplicate extended communities.
Action: Test neighbor advertises three EVPN routes: (1) Type-1 Ethernet-AD route for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 with two ESI Label ECs (first EC value=label 16001, second EC value=label 16002), (2) Type-4 ES route for same ESI with two ES-Import RT ECs (first=RT 65000:1, second=RT 65000:2), (3) Type-2 MAC/IP route for MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 with two MAC Mobility ECs (first=sequence 10, second=sequence 20). Inject all three routes via BGP.
Verify: Run show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 detail, show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail, show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 mac aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 to inspect received routes and confirm DUT installed first EC only (label 16001, RT 65000:1, seq 10). Run show bgp neighbor <ip> to confirm session Established, Update-Err counter=0.
ca94abf2b6dc216a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE with BGP route injection tool (capable of crafting non-compliant EVPN routes with duplicate ECs) or DUT + IXIA with BGP EVPN protocol stack
ca94abf2b6dc216a.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts all three routes, processes only first EC instance per RFC7432bis §7.14.2 MUST requirements (uses ESI label 16001 not 16002, ES-Import RT 65000:1 not 65000:2, MAC mobility sequence 10 not 20). BGP session remains up, no Treat-as-withdraw, forwarding plane reflects first EC values.
Fail-on: DUT uses second EC value in any route (16002, 65000:2, or seq 20), treats any route as withdrawn, increments BGP Update-Error counter >0, or drops session upon receiving duplicate ECs.

caaf7746ed7c643a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor-PE in EVPN instance 100, shared ES (ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, DF election, advertise-mac enable) on agg-eth-1 to IXIA-CE; verify `show bgp evpn route-type 1` shows A-D per ES from DUT; learn 5 MACs.
Action: Shut DUT agg-eth-1 (`config/interface/agg-eth-1/shutdown`); wait 20 s; collect tech-support (`admin tech-support evpn-failure <file>`); restore interface.
Verify: Extract from tech-support: `show bgp evpn route-type 1` (ESI routes withdrawn), `show bgp evpn route-type 2` (MAC/IP withdrawn), `show evpn ethernet-segment` (oper-down), `show interface agg-eth-1` (admin-down status), syslog (ES down event), running-config (ethernet-segment stanza).
caaf7746ed7c643a.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA (as multihomed CE)
caaf7746ed7c643a.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: BGP route tables showing Ethernet A-D per ES (type 1) and MAC/IP (type 2) withdrawals for ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11; ESI operational state down; interface agg-eth-1 admin-down; syslog with ES failure + route withdrawal events; ethernet-segment config section.
Fail-on: Missing: BGP EVPN route tables (type 1 or 2), ESI state, interface status, ES deactivation syslog, or ethernet-segment running-config.

cb009c690f3847ae.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with agg-eth 0 interfaces in multihomed Ethernet segment; configure ethernet-segment on each agg-eth 0 (Type 1 LACP-based ESI); enable auto-discovery in EVPN instance; establish BGP EVPN peering; start packet capture on BGP session.
Action: Bring up CE (IXIA) with LACP enabled, System MAC = 00:11:22:33:44:55; trigger Route Type 1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) and Route Type 4 (Ethernet Segment) advertisement from both PEs.
Verify: Decode captured BGP UPDATE messages; extract 10-octet ESI Value field from Route Type 1 and Type 4; verify octets 0–5 = 00:11:22:33:44:55; confirm both PEs send identical ESI; verify auto-discovery routes are present.
cb009c690f3847ae.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Value octets 0–5 match CE LACP System MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 exactly (RFC7432bis §5 MUST); both PEs advertise same ESI; Route Type 1 and Type 4 routes captured.
Fail-on: High-order 6 octets of ESI Value ≠ CE LACP System MAC, or ESI mismatch between PEs for same Ethernet segment, or auto-discovery routes missing from BGP capture.

cb42dc63156ea3bb.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs in EVPN topology (BD 100, RD 1:100, RT 100:100), P-multicast tree = Ingress Replication, `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on both PEs. Verify PE1 advertises IMET route (type 3) with PMSI Tunnel attribute: tunnel-type=Ingress-Replication, MPLS label L1 bound to BD 100. IXIA connected to PE1 (10Gbps sender) and PE2 (10Gbps receiver).
Action: IXIA generates 10,000 BUM flows (5000 broadcast ARP, 5000 unknown unicast MAC) at 9.5 Gbps (95% line rate) for 120 seconds. Measure sustained BUM throughput and average latency via P-tunnel. At t=60s, trigger P-tunnel re-identification by changing PE1 PMSI Tunnel attribute (update MPLS label to L2), measure convergence time until BUM traffic resumes at PE2.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route type 3 rd 1:100` on PE2 confirms PMSI Tunnel attribute present with correct tunnel-type and MPLS label (L1, then L2 post-change). `show l2-services evpn statistics` reports BUM packet counters via P-tunnel. IXIA captures BUM RX throughput, latency histogram, and packet loss during steady-state and convergence window.
cb42dc63156ea3bb.expectation:
Pass:    BUM forwarding via P-tunnel sustains ≥9.0 Gbps (≥95% of injected rate) with average latency ≤10ms and ≤0.01% packet loss during steady state (t=0–60s, t=70–120s). P-tunnel re-identification convergence (PMSI Tunnel attribute update L1→L2) completes within 5 seconds with ≤1% transient packet loss.
Fail-on: BUM throughput <8.5 Gbps, average latency >15ms, steady-state loss >0.1%, P-tunnel convergence >10s, or transient loss >5% during PMSI Tunnel attribute change.

cba5a4fdf698f9e5.action_steps:
Setup:  Create `agg-eth 5` and enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 5` mode.
Action: Issue `lacp-key 1000` and commit; verify running-config. Issue `no lacp-key` to restore default (interface number 5 per requirement). Set boundary values: `lacp-key 0`, then `lacp-key 65535`. Attempt invalid inputs: `lacp-key 65536`, `lacp-key -1`, `lacp-key xyz`.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 5` shows committed key value after each valid set. After `no lacp-key`, running-config either omits the line or shows default value 5. Invalid values produce CLI parse errors before commit.
cba5a4fdf698f9e5.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
cba5a4fdf698f9e5.expectation:
Pass:    Valid keys (0, 1000, 65535) accepted; `no lacp-key` restores default (interface number); invalid values (65536, -1, xyz) rejected with parse errors
Fail-on: Invalid value committed; boundary value rejected; `no` form fails to restore interface-number default; parse error missing

cc0d1a8c52667a35.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with identical ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on their access interfaces toward the same multi-homed CE. Configure MP2MP MPLS LSP (not P2MP) as the tunnel type. Enable `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`. Establish iBGP peering with `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor`.
Action: Bring up EVPN BGP sessions; verify both PEs advertise EVPN route type 1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery per-EVI) with ESI label. IXIA sends broadcast frame to CE → PE1. Capture MPLS encapsulation on core link PE1 → PE2, and BGP route type 1 updates from both PEs.
Verify: On PE1 and PE2, run `show evpn ethernet-segment esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 detail` to extract advertised ESI label. Run `show mpls label-range` to confirm DCB range boundaries. Decode captured route type 1 updates to confirm both PEs advertise the same ESI label value. On PE2, verify BUM packet from PE1 carries ESI label in MPLS stack and that PE2's forwarding log/counter identifies PE1's ES as the source.
cc0d1a8c52667a35.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 and PE2 both allocate and advertise the same ESI label value (e.g., label 5000) for ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99; the label falls within the DCB range shown by `show mpls label-range`; PE2 correctly identifies PE1's Ethernet Segment as the BUM source using the ESI label in the MPLS stack.
Fail-on: PE1 and PE2 allocate different ESI labels for the same ESI; allocated ESI label is outside the DCB range; route type 1 is missing ESI label field; PE2 cannot determine source ES (split-horizon fails or loop occurs); EVPN session does not establish.

cc7b4ed16b9642ba.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT loopback 0 with IPv4 address 192.0.2.1/32; authenticate NETCONF client.
Action: Via NETCONF, create EVPN instance "EVPN100" (service-type vlan-aware-bundle) without explicit RD config; query <get-config> for auto-generated RD; execute CLI `show evpn evpn-name EVPN100` to confirm RD.
Verify: Parse RD from both NETCONF response and CLI output; confirm Type 1 format (IPv4:assigned-number where IPv4 = 192.0.2.1); repeat for second instance EVPN101 to verify unique assigned-number increment.
cc7b4ed16b9642ba.expectation:
Pass:    Auto-generated RD is Type 1, Administrator subfield = 192.0.2.1 (loopback 0 IP), Assigned Number subfield is unique system-generated ID (e.g., 192.0.2.1:100 for EVPN100, 192.0.2.1:101 for EVPN101); NETCONF <config> and CLI `show evpn` display identical RD values.
Fail-on: RD is not auto-generated, RD Type ≠ 1, Administrator subfield ≠ loopback 0 IP, assigned-number collides across EVPN instances, or NETCONF/CLI RD values diverge.

ccc6eb8b2ac8b921.action_steps:
Setup:  Create VPLS instance vpls1 with two member interfaces: `x-eth 0/0/1` and `x-eth 0/0/2`. Verify VPLS is operationally up.
Action: Configure a static MAC address entry (00:11:22:33:44:55) in vpls1, binding it to interface `x-eth 0/0/1`. Commit the configuration.
Verify: Query VPLS MAC table to confirm static entry exists (MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, interface `x-eth 0/0/1`, type static). Send continuous traffic from IXIA with source on `x-eth 0/0/2` and destination MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55; verify egress only on `x-eth 0/0/1`, no flooding.
ccc6eb8b2ac8b921.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 present in VPLS MAC table, correctly bound to `x-eth 0/0/1`, marked as static type. Traffic to this MAC unicast-forwarded to `x-eth 0/0/1` only with 0% loss.
Fail-on: Static entry not in MAC table, entry type is learned/dynamic instead of static, traffic flooded to all member interfaces, or forwarded to incorrect interface.

cccdf1287efcbc5a.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with agg-eth 0 configured; ethernet-segment with ESI assigned to agg-eth 0; enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` mode.
Action: Issue `load-balancing-mode all-active` and commit. Capture `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` and `show ethernet-segment`. Issue `no load-balancing-mode` and commit; capture outputs. Issue `load-balancing-mode single-active` and commit; capture outputs.
Verify: Check show running-config under agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment for load-balancing-mode keyword and value. Check show ethernet-segment for Mode field.
cccdf1287efcbc5a.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
cccdf1287efcbc5a.expectation:
Pass:    All three commits succeed; running-config contains `load-balancing-mode all-active` after first, no load-balancing-mode line after second (defaulted), `load-balancing-mode single-active` after third; show ethernet-segment Mode displays all-active, then single-active, then single-active respectively.
Fail-on: Commit error, unknown keyword, running-config doesn't reflect committed value, show ethernet-segment Mode doesn't match configuration, or default after `no` is not single-active.

ccd1e4b2afc8aa72.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn EVPN-1 service-type vlan-based` with ≥2 member ports; DUT MAC table empty for test destination MAC 00:00:00:00:00:99. No explicit `unknow-mac-flooding` configuration.
Action: IXIA sends L2 frame (dst-MAC 00:00:00:00:00:99, unknown) to DUT port 1. Repeat after: (a) `unknow-mac-flooding disable`, (b) `no unknow-mac-flooding` (restore default), (c) `unknow-mac-flooding enable` (explicit default).
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN-1` for presence/absence of `unknow-mac-flooding` line; IXIA port-2+ rx counters (flooding when enabled, zero when disabled); confirm `no unknow-mac-flooding` removes config line and restores flooding behavior.
ccd1e4b2afc8aa72.expectation:
Pass:    Default (no config line) floods frame to all EVPN ports except ingress; `unknow-mac-flooding disable` drops frame (rx=0 on other ports); `no unknow-mac-flooding` restores enable (flooding resumes); explicit `unknow-mac-flooding enable` is idempotent (no line in running-config).
Fail-on: Default drops unknown-MAC frames, or `no unknow-mac-flooding` does not restore enable, or running-config shows `unknow-mac-flooding enable` when at default, or flooding occurs when disabled, or drops occur when enabled.

cd22f3ed8bf91f8b.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PE routers in EVPN topology with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled under BGP neighbors, `advertise-mac enable` configured; IXIA scale rig attached to both PEs generating 10,000 unique source MAC addresses (5K IPv4 hosts, 5K IPv6 hosts).
Action: IXIA sends initial packets from all 10K MACs at 1000 MACs/sec rate; measure time from first packet arrival at DUT to BGP Type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) route visible at remote PE; capture BGP UPDATE messages at remote PE.
Verify: On remote PE, run `show bgp evpn route-type 2` to confirm all 10K routes received; decode BGP UPDATE captures to verify: MAC Address Length field = 48 bits (0x30), IPv4 addresses encoded as 4 octets, IPv6 addresses as 16 octets, RD format matches Section 7 encoding; measure convergence time and per-second advertisement rate.
cd22f3ed8bf91f8b.expectation:
Pass:    All 10,000 MAC/IP Advertisement routes received within 15 seconds (≥666 routes/sec sustained); 100% of captured BGP UPDATEs have MAC Address Length = 48 bits, IPv4 = 4-octet encoding, IPv6 = 16-octet encoding, RD correctly formatted per RFC7432bis §7.
Fail-on: Convergence time exceeds 15 seconds, advertisement rate falls below 666 routes/sec, any MAC/IP route with MAC Address Length ≠ 48 bits, any IPv4 address ≠ 4 octets or IPv6 ≠ 16 octets, malformed or missing RD field in BGP NLRI.

cd34b6b24214f3f4.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT booted; create EVPN instance using `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based` under configuration/l2-services. Physical interface x-eth 0/0/1 is administratively up, not bound to any L2 service.
Action: Enter configuration/l2-services/evpn evpn-100 context. Issue `interface x-eth 0/0/1` to bind the attachment circuit. Commit. Issue `no interface x-eth 0/0/1`. Commit.
Verify: After add: `show running-config` under evpn-100 shows the interface line. After remove: interface line removed from evpn-100 configuration.
cd34b6b24214f3f4.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
cd34b6b24214f3f4.expectation:
Pass:    Add: interface binding commits cleanly, `interface x-eth 0/0/1` persists in running-config under evpn-100. Remove: no form commits cleanly, interface line disappears from config.
Fail-on: Commit rejection on add or remove; interface accepted but doesn't persist in running-config; no form fails to parse or leaves stale configuration entries.

cd4751c8c55b8351.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS/EVPN instance under `configuration / l2-services / vpls`; set `mac-aging-time` to a test value distinct from the system default; commit and save the configuration with `write memory`.
Action: Use IXIA to transmit L2 frames that populate the DUT's MAC address table; capture the timestamp when MACs appear in `show mac address-table`; wait for the configured aging duration to elapse; verify MACs have aged out; reload the DUT; after full boot, inspect running-config for the persisted `mac-aging-time` value; execute `no mac-aging-time` and re-inspect running-config.
Verify: Execute `show mac address-table` to confirm MACs are present immediately after learning, then absent after the aging interval; post-reload, `show running-config` must display the same `mac-aging-time` value; after `no mac-aging-time`, running-config must not contain an explicit mac-aging-time line (default restored).
cd4751c8c55b8351.expectation:
Pass:    MAC table entries age out after the configured time (confirmed by before/after comparison of `show mac address-table` output with timestamps); `mac-aging-time` configuration persists across reload byte-identical; `no mac-aging-time` removes the explicit config line from running-config, restoring default aging behavior.
Fail-on: MAC entries age out at an incorrect interval (default or other value, not the configured time), fail to age out, or disappear immediately; `mac-aging-time` is missing, altered, or corrupted after reload; `no mac-aging-time` fails to remove the configuration line or doesn't restore default behavior.

cdf89fe533d615d8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment under agg-eth with 10-byte ESI; configure l2-services/evpn with auto-discovery, import-rt, export-rt; iBGP EVPN peering to IXIA emulating second PE in same ES.
Action: IXIA transmits EVPN route type 4 (AD per-ES) with DF Election Extended Community containing invalid DF Alg field (0xFF, valid range 0–7 per RFC 8584), malformed length, or reserved Bitmap bits set.
Verify: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4' (malformed route absent or invalid); 'show ethernet-segment esi <value>' shows DF elected using valid peers only; 'show log' records BGP UPDATE error; 'show bgp neighbor' Recv-UpdateErr counter incremented.
cdf89fe533d615d8.expectation:
Pass:    Malformed DF Election Extended Community rejected (treat-as-withdraw or error notification per RFC 7606); DF election excludes malformed advertisement; error logged and counted; no crash.
Fail-on: Malformed route installed in RIB; DF election uses invalid DF Alg value; system crash; ES state corruption; or no error logged/counted.

ce6d71f9e650050c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two DUTs as multi-homed PEs for ES (ESI type-0 `identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88`) in `load-balancing-mode all-active`, service-carving `highest-random-weight`, attached to EVI 100. IXIA dual-homed generating bidirectional L2 traffic (1000 MAC/s, 100 Mbps).
Action: Monitor BGP EVPN route-type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) advertisements from both PEs over 24 hours; trigger DF re-election every 4 hours by toggling `service-carving highest-preference preference 100` on PE1.
Verify: Decode L2-Attr extended community (type 0x04) in all Ethernet A-D per EVI routes via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88 evi 100 detail`; confirm P bit (0x01) set in all routes from both PEs; log BGP update count and traffic counters.
ce6d71f9e650050c.equipment: Two routers + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
ce6d71f9e650050c.expectation:
Pass:    L2-Attr extended community present in 100% of Ethernet A-D per EVI routes with P bit=1 for all PEs in all-active redundancy group over 24 h; no BGP flaps; traffic loss <0.01% per DF transition; memory flat (`show platform process memory evpn`).
Fail-on: Any Ethernet A-D per EVI route with L2-Attr missing or P bit=0 in all-active mode; BGP session reset; traffic loss >0.1% during transitions; memory growth >5% over 24 h.

cec74c9ec205add0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `config / l2-services / evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based / mac-limit 1000 / commit`; generate IXIA traffic with 500 unique source MACs; verify `show evpn mac-table evpn-100 | count` = 500.
Action: Enter `config / l2-services / evpn evpn-100`, issue `no mac-limit`, commit; generate IXIA traffic with 1200 unique source MACs.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn-100` contains no `mac-limit` line; `show evpn mac-table evpn-100 | count` = 1200 (exceeds former limit); `show evpn instance evpn-100 detail` shows no mac-limit parameter; no warnings in `show logging | include mac-limit`.
cec74c9ec205add0.expectation:
Pass:    `mac-limit` removed from running-config and operational state; all 1200 MACs learned (exceeds previous 1000-MAC cap); no commit errors; data plane MAC learning unlimited.
Fail-on: `mac-limit` line persists in running-config; MAC learning stops at 1000; commit fails; `show evpn instance detail` still displays old limit; or syslog shows continued limit enforcement.

cf0a13649093b1f8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with BD 100, unknown-mac-flooding enable, verify LT3 label allocated via `show evpn instance detail`. IXIA neighbor PE establishes BGP EVPN peering (af-l2vpn evpn), exchanges IMET routes, confirms baseline BUM forwarding works.
Action: IXIA injects malformed BUM packets over MPLS tunnel: (1) invalid LT3 label value (0xFFFFF reserved range), (2) corrupted MPLS label stack (S-bit=1 on LT3 when additional labels follow), (3) wrong LT3 label not matching BD 100, (4) oversized label field exceeding 20 bits. Send each variant 10 times over 10 seconds.
Verify: `show evpn instance <name> statistics` confirms label-error counter increments (≥40 drops total). `show mpls forwarding-table` shows LT3 label for BD 100 unchanged. `show logging` contains "EVPN LT3 label validation failed" or equivalent per variant type. IXIA confirms zero malformed packets received on BD 100 interfaces.
cf0a13649093b1f8.expectation:
Pass:    All 40 malformed variants dropped at ingress; EVPN label-error counter shows ≥40 increments; syslog has 4 distinct error messages (one per variant type); legitimate BUM traffic (control test) still forwarded with correct LT3 label
Fail-on: Crash; any malformed packet forwarded into BD 100; label-error counter does not increment; missing syslog entries; LT3 label state corrupted (show command output changed); legitimate BUM traffic disrupted

cf47ef9d4dd5e3c3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance with vlan-based service-type. Apply mac-aging-time 60 under the EVPN instance configuration. Commit.
Action: Send bidirectional L2 traffic between two IXIA ports through the EVPN instance for 30 seconds to populate the MAC address table. Verify MAC entries are learned using `show mac-address-table`. Stop all traffic and wait 70 seconds.
Verify: Check `show mac-address-table` at T=0 (after learning), T=60s, and T=70s. Confirm learned MAC addresses exist immediately after traffic stops and are removed from the table after the 60-second aging period expires.
cf47ef9d4dd5e3c3.expectation:
Pass:    MAC addresses learned from IXIA traffic are present in the MAC table after learning and are aged out (removed) within 70 seconds of inactivity.
Fail-on: MAC entries persist beyond 70 seconds of inactivity, age out prematurely (before 60 seconds), or the mac-aging-time command is rejected during configuration.

cf5394e4b201e5f8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `ethernet-segment` with ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on DUT's agg-eth-1 and PE2's agg-eth-1. Enable `af-l2vpn evpn` in BGP neighbor config. Verify DF/BDF election complete via `show evpn ethernet-segment`. Learn ≥10 MACs from CE. On remote PE3, confirm `show bgp evpn route-type 4 esi 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` lists both DUT and PE2.
Action: Execute `configuration/interface/agg-eth-1/shutdown` on DUT to fail PE-CE link. Start packet capture (tcpdump filter: tcp port 179) on DUT↔PE3 link. Record link-down timestamp T0.
Verify: Decode captured BGP UPDATEs: (1) Withdrawn Routes NLRI contains route type 4 with RD, ESI=01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 (10-octet format), Eth-Tag ID per RFC7432bis §7.1. (2) Subsequent UPDATEs withdraw MAC/IP (type 2): verify NLRI has RD, ESI, Eth-Tag, MAC-len=48, MAC address, MPLS label per §7.2. (3) On PE3, `show evpn mac-table` no longer shows DUT as next-hop for any MAC learned on that ES.
cf5394e4b201e5f8.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (multi-homing peer) + 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper remote observer) + IXIA (CE emulation, ≥10 MAC sources) + packet capture tool
cf5394e4b201e5f8.expectation:
Pass:    Route type 4 withdrawal properly encoded (10-octet ESI, correct RD/Eth-Tag per RFC7432bis §7.1) and received at PE3 within 90 s (3× default BGP keepalive). All MAC/IP (type 2) routes withdrawn with correct NLRI structure (MAC-len=48, label field present per §7.2). For N≥10 MACs, withdrawal timestamps span ≥1 s (indicates randomization per §17.3 SHOULD). PE3 removes DUT from next-hop set.
Fail-on: Route type 4 NLRI malformed (ESI ≠ 10 octets, missing RD/Eth-Tag) or not withdrawn within 120 s. Any type 2 withdrawal has MAC-len ≠ 48 or missing label field. PE3 still forwards to DUT >180 s after link down.

cf9d84d76395c9f7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure maximum-prefix limit of 10 L2VPN EVPN routes under the neighbor configuration at af-l2vpn/evpn. Establish BGP EVPN peering with a neighbor PE.
Action: From the neighbor PE, advertise 11 EVPN routes (mix of type-2 MAC/IP and type-3 IMET routes) to exceed the configured threshold.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` immediately after the 11th route is sent; `show log` or syslog capture for BGP NOTIFICATION sent with code 6 sub-code 1.
cf9d84d76395c9f7.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session state = Idle or Active (session torn down); syslog contains NOTIFICATION code 6 (Cease) sub-code 1 (Maximum Number of Prefixes Reached)
Fail-on: Session state remains Established after receiving 11+ prefixes; or NOTIFICATION code/sub-code is absent, zero, or not 6/1

d00cc93eac07af0b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with `configuration / l2-services / evpn` including `advertise-mac enable`; establish BGP EVPN session via `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor / af-l2vpn evpn`; both PEs advertise F-bit=1 in BGP Extended Community (RFC7432bis §18.1); attach IXIA to each PE access port; enable wire-capture between PEs to inspect MPLS label stacks.
Action: From IXIA, send known-unicast frames (specific learned dest MAC) to trigger MPLS forwarding via Type-2 route; capture encapsulated packets on core link; repeat test after disabling F-bit on neighbor PE (F=0); separately send BUM traffic (broadcast dest MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) and capture.
Verify: Parse MPLS label stack in captures: (1) known-unicast when F-bit=1 received → stack MUST contain [transport][VPN label][flow label with S=1][payload]; (2) known-unicast when F-bit=0 received → stack MUST be [transport][VPN label S=1][payload] with NO flow label; (3) BUM packets MUST show [transport][VPN label][ESI label S=1][payload] with NO flow label. Cross-check `show bgp evpn summary` for F-bit in received Extended Communities; `show l2-services evpn mac` confirms MAC learning.
d00cc93eac07af0b.expectation:
Pass:    Known-unicast to F-bit=1 peer contains flow label (entropy label per RFC6790) between VPN label and payload; known-unicast to F-bit=0 peer has NO flow label; all BUM packets have ESI label and zero flow labels (RFC7432bis §18.1 ¶3: "Flow Label MUST NOT be used for EVPN-encapsulated BUM packets").
Fail-on: Flow label present when remote PE advertised F-bit=0; flow label absent when remote PE advertised F-bit=1 and DUT supports flow label; any BUM packet contains a flow label; BUM packet missing mandatory ESI label.

d02d0d58cc435a0c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT (vlan-based or vlan-aware-bundle), establish MP-BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE, advertise at least one EVPN route (type-2 or type-3) to populate remote neighbor in FIB. Confirm `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows neighbor in Established state.
Action: Run `show fib evpn-neighbor` unfiltered; note VTEP IP of displayed neighbor. Run `show fib evpn-neighbor | include <vtep-ip>`, then `| exclude <vtep-ip>`, then `| begin <column-header>`, then `| count`.
Verify: Unfiltered output displays table with neighbor VTEP IP(s), associated VNI(s), tunnel encapsulation type. `| include` returns only matching lines. `| exclude` removes them. `| begin` starts output from header. `| count` returns integer line count matching unfiltered.
d02d0d58cc435a0c.expectation:
Pass:    Command recognized and executes; ≥1 EVPN neighbor entry visible with VTEP IP, VNI, encap fields; all pipe filters (include/exclude/begin/count) produce correct output subsets.
Fail-on: Command syntax error, empty table despite active EVPN peering and route exchange, pipe filter fails or returns wrong result, output format corrupted.

d03a0ae3b75c968e.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that when PEs in the same Ethernet Segment advertise conflicting DF election algorithms via Route Type 4 DF Election Extended Community, the device enforces the SFS-mandated fallback to Default Algorithm (RFC7432bis-§8.5) rather than proceeding with mismatched algorithms per PE.
Method: Configure shared-ESI all-active multi-homing with PE1 (DUT) using `service-carving highest-random-weight` and PE2 using `service-carving default`, verify PE1 detects the mismatch in Route Type 4 DF Election Extended Community and falls back to Default.
Setup: PE1 (DUT) and PE2 connect to same CE via LACP LAG on agg-eth 0 with identical ESI configured via `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (e.g., 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88). On PE1: `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `service-carving highest-random-weight`. On PE2: `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `service-carving default`. Establish EVI and BGP L2VPN EVPN session between PE1 and PE2.
Action: Bring up `ethernet-segment` on both PEs. PE1 advertises Ethernet Segment Route (Type 4) with DF Election Extended Community 'DF Alg'=HRW (per RFC8584-§3); PE2 advertises Route Type 4 with 'DF Alg'=Default (per RFC7432bis-§8.5). Each PE receives the other's Route Type 4, detects 'DF Alg' mismatch, and per SFS EVPNS-REQ#120 must fall back to Default Algorithm. Verify both PEs also advertise Ethernet A-D per-ES Route (Type 1 per EVPNS-REQ#260) with ES-Import Route Target derived from ESI (per RFC7432bis-§7.6, EVPNS-REQ#250).
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE1 displays DF election algorithm as "Default" (not "highest-random-weight"), confirming fallback despite local config. `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` shows Route Type 4 advertisements from both PEs with their respective DF Election Extended Community attributes. Syslog on PE1 logs algorithm mismatch detection and fallback event. `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows DF election completed for the EVI VLANs using Default Algorithm modulo function with numerically lowest Ethernet tag (per RFC7432bis-§8.5). IXIA traffic forwarded correctly through the elected DF with no loss.
d03a0ae3b75c968e.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + LACP partner CE + IXIA traffic gen
d03a0ae3b75c968e.expectation:
Pass: PE1 detects 'DF Alg' mismatch in Route Type 4, falls back from highest-random-weight to Default Algorithm, syslog confirms fallback, DF election completes using RFC7432bis-§8.5 modulo, Route Type 1 and Route Type 4 present for both PEs, IXIA traffic forwards with 0% loss.
Fail-on: PE1 continues using highest-random-weight despite mismatch; PE2 does not fall back; DF election stalls or shows incomplete; Route Type 1 or Route Type 4 missing; no fallback logged in syslog; IXIA traffic loss > 0%; DF assignment incorrect.

d0494c5c4230c68a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT: `evpn <evpn-name> service-type vlan-based`. Establish BGP peering and enable `af-l2vpn evpn` under the neighbor. Configure import route-targets to accept EVPN routes. Verify BGP session up and neighbor advertising type 1 (Ethernet Auto-discovery), type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement), and type 3 (Inclusive Multicast) routes.
Action: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi` at operational prompt and capture output.
Verify: Confirm output contains entries with route-type values 1, 2, and 3. Verify EVI identifier field populated for each entry. Check that imported routes from neighbor appear in table.
d0494c5c4230c68a.expectation:
Pass:    Output displays at least one route of each type (1, 2, 3) with populated EVI identifier; routes correspond to neighbor advertisements.
Fail-on: Command returns syntax error, produces empty table despite neighbor advertising routes, or is missing any of the three route types.
d0494c5c4230c68a.req_id: CLI:show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi
d0494c5c4230c68a.sub_category: show bgp l2vpn evpn table evi

d08c6cce405ebdfe.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that split-horizon prevents BUM loops by dropping frames received with the local ES's ESI Label on the shared multi-homed access.
Method: Configure dual-homed ES (PE1, PE2 → CE), flood BUM from PE1 to PE2 with shared ESI Label, confirm PE2 drops on ES access; flood BUM with different ESI Label, confirm forwarding.
Setup: Configure two PEs with a shared ES (same ESI) to one CE via LACP; establish EVPN instance with default `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; allocate ESI Label for the shared ES.
Action: Inject BUM traffic at PE1 that originates from the shared ES; PE1 floods to PE2 with the shared ESI Label; observe PE2's egress on the ES access link. Inject BUM with a different ESI Label from a non-shared source; observe PE2's egress.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms ESI Label allocated; packet capture on PE2's ES access interface shows shared-ES BUM not transmitted (split-horizon enforced); different-ESI BUM transmitted.
d08c6cce405ebdfe.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
d08c6cce405ebdfe.expectation:
Pass:    Shared-ES BUM (with local ESI Label) dropped on PE2's ES access; different-ESI BUM forwards.
Fail-on: Shared-ES BUM forwarded on ES access (loop created), or different-ESI BUM incorrectly dropped.

d0eba07afc4e7501.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT (PE1) and PE2 in All-Active mode (`load-balancing-mode all-active`) on same ES; enable P2MP MPLS LSP for BUM. Both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes with upstream-assigned ESI labels. PE3 imports routes but not on ES.
Action: Inject BUM at PE2; PE2 sends over P2MP LSP with PE2's ESI label. Verify label programming baseline, then shut PE2's ES interface (`shutdown` under `interface agg-eth`). Continue BUM injection during ES reconvergence.
Verify: Before/during/after ES flap: `show mpls forwarding-table label <PE2-ESI-label>` on PE1 confirms ES-forwarding disabled; same on PE3 confirms label in PE2 context. `show evpn ethernet-segment` tracks ES state. Packet capture at PE1's ES interface shows zero frames with PE2's label.
d0eba07afc4e7501.equipment: DUT (PE1) + neighbor PE (PE2, All-Active same ES) + 3rd PE (PE3, not on ES) + IXIA for BUM injection + LACP partner
d0eba07afc4e7501.expectation:
Pass:    Throughout ES convergence, PE1 maintains PE2's upstream-assigned ESI label programmed with action preventing ES forwarding (RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2 MUST #2). PE3 maintains label in PE2's context space (MUST #3). No BUM loop-back to ES.
Fail-on: PE2's ESI label unprogrammed or loses anti-loop action during flap, or PE1 forwards BUM with PE2's label back to ES interface, or PE3's label entry missing, or label lookup fails.

d16c27b91b7c07d6.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology connected over MPLS core; PE1 (AS 65001) and PE2 (AS 65002) with base BGP configuration; EVPN instances pre-configured on both PEs.
Action: On PE1, configure BGP neighbor for PE2 and enter af-l2vpn evpn address-family context. Configure each of the eight BGP common commands from EVPNS-REQ#20: allow-as-in (with occurrence count), capability (dynamic), inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix (threshold set to 5000), private-as (removal), route-reflector-client, weight (set to 200), and group (peer-group assignment). Commit configuration. On PE2, configure symmetric af-l2vpn evpn peering. Establish BGP session and exchange EVPN routes (Type 2, Type 3).
Verify: `show running-config routing bgp` confirms all eight commands persist under neighbor af-l2vpn evpn section. `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` reports neighbor Established with EVPN capability. Validate behavioral effects: test maximum-prefix by advertising routes approaching the 5000 threshold (session warns or tears down at limit); verify route-reflector-client by checking received routes are marked for reflection; confirm weight 200 affects route preference in path selection.
d16c27b91b7c07d6.expectation:
Pass:    All eight BGP common commands (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight, group) accept configuration in af-l2vpn evpn context without syntax error; commit succeeds; BGP session reaches Established state with EVPN capability negotiated; maximum-prefix threshold enforces at 5000 routes; route-reflector-client attribute applied; weight 200 influences path selection.
Fail-on: Any command rejected in af-l2vpn evpn context, syntax error reported, commit failure, BGP session fails to establish, EVPN capability not negotiated, maximum-prefix limit not enforced, route-reflector-client attribute missing, or weight does not affect route preference.

d19ec38d9cdd393f.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN instance (EVI 100, BD 10) with `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on DUT; configure BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn` and `import-rt`; choose P-tunnel type (ingress replication or P-multicast tree).
Action: Activate BD on DUT; capture BGP UPDATE at neighbor PE using Wireshark filtering TCP port 179; trigger route type 3 generation by enabling BUM forwarding.
Verify: Decode UPDATE NLRI (route type 3, RFC7432bis §7.3); extract PMSI Tunnel attribute (RFC6514 §5); validate tunnel type field (0x06 for Ingress Replication, 0x01–0x05 for P-multicast); confirm MPLS label field non-zero; cross-check label uniqueness via `show evpn database` on DUT.
d19ec38d9cdd393f.expectation:
Pass:    Route type 3 carries PMSI Tunnel attribute; tunnel type matches configured mode; MPLS label present (24-bit, non-reserved) and unique to BD 10; if P-multicast (type 0x01–0x05), tunnel identifier field populated per RFC6514.
Fail-on: PMSI Tunnel attribute absent; MPLS label field zero, missing, or collides across BDs; P-multicast tunnel type (0x01–0x05) with empty tunnel identifier; tunnel type mismatch vs. configured P-tunnel mode.

d1a1dc8dcf3d5d7c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance via 'configuration / l2-services / evpn' with 'unknown-mac-flooding enable'. Create two Ethernet segments (ES1 on ifIndex 10, ES2 on ifIndex 20) with multi-homing. Establish BGP neighbor with 'af-l2vpn evpn' enabled and advertise Ethernet A-D Route Type 1 carrying LT3 (flood label) and LT4 (split-horizon labels: 5001 for ES1, 5002 for ES2).
Action: IXIA injects BUM MPLS packets from simulated remote PE with malformed LT4 labels: (1) corrupted label value (e.g., 0xFFFFF), (2) LT4 referencing non-existent ifIndex 99, (3) label stack missing LT4 entirely, (4) reserved S-bit violations. Send 100 packets per variant.
Verify: Run 'show l2-services evpn split-horizon statistics', 'show mpls forwarding errors', 'show logging | include EVPN'. Confirm packets dropped, error counters increment by 400 total, no ES flooding occurred.
d1a1dc8dcf3d5d7c.expectation:
Pass:    All 400 malformed packets dropped at LT4 lookup stage; 'mpls-label-malformed' counter = 400; syslog entry 'EVPN: BUM LT4 label validation failed' present; no BUM flooding to ES1/ES2 interfaces; DUT control/data plane stable
Fail-on: Any malformed packet flooded to ES interfaces; error counter < 400; missing or incorrect syslog event; DUT crash, BGP session flap, or EVPN route withdrawal

d1bfb62ee603fb34.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn test-1 service-type vlan-based` under `configuration / l2-services`, attach an interface as an attachment-circuit per documented syntax, commit; verify EVPN instance is operationally UP.
Action: At the operational prompt type `show fib ?` to locate `evpn-ac`; execute `show fib evpn-ac`; execute `show fib evpn-ac ?` to list any filter parameters.
Verify: `show fib ?` lists `evpn-ac` as a valid token; `show fib evpn-ac` displays FIB entries for the configured AC (interface, label, forwarding status); help lists documented filters or indicates none available.
d1bfb62ee603fb34.expectation:
Pass:    `show fib evpn-ac` is discoverable via help; command executes and displays FIB entries with forwarding labels for each EVPN attachment-circuit.
Fail-on: Command absent from `show fib ?` output, command not recognized on execution, or FIB entries missing for configured and operationally UP attachment-circuits.

d22ed36801128e4e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs as multi-homed to a CE via agg-eth with identical ESI (identifier 0 <9-octet-hex>), load-balancing-mode all-active; configure l2-services evpn with es-waiting-time 3 (default); establish EVPN MAC learning and continuous traffic from IXIA via CE through both PEs.
Action: Shut down the agg-eth interface on PE1 to simulate ES link failure; capture BGP updates on PE2 and remote PE; measure traffic loss duration.
Verify: Confirm PE1 sends mass-withdrawal BGP update (RFC7432bis §8.2 Ethernet A-D per-ES route withdrawal); verify PE2 and remote PEs converge within es-waiting-time + BGP propagation (≤ 5 sec total); check `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows ES down on PE1, operational on PE2; confirm `show evpn mac` on remote PE updates within convergence window; verify no stale FIB entries.
d22ed36801128e4e.equipment: Two DUTs (PE1, PE2) multi-homed to CE + neighbor PE (remote) + IXIA traffic gen with sub-second loss measurement + BGP packet capture
d22ed36801128e4e.expectation:
Pass:    Mass-withdrawal sent immediately upon agg-eth down; remote PEs converge and reroute traffic within 5 seconds (es-waiting-time 3s + 2s BGP); zero stale MAC entries in `show evpn mac` post-convergence.
Fail-on: Traffic loss > 5 seconds, or no mass-withdrawal in BGP capture, or stale MAC/FIB entries remain after es-waiting-time × 2, or PE2 does not assume full traffic load.

d25822395432e6b5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs multihomed to shared CE via `agg-eth 0` with identical ESI (`ethernet-segment` under interface). Enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbors; configure EVPN instance with RT. Verify one PE elects as DF, the other as non-DF for the ES.
Action: From CE (IXIA), send broadcast frame into the multihomed EVPN. Capture MPLS encapsulation on inter-PE link as non-DF PE forwards BUM to remote DF PE.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 1` on both PEs confirms Type-1 (per-ES A-D) route advertises ESI Label Extended Community. Packet capture on inter-PE link shows MPLS label stack with two labels (outer LT3 broadcast label, inner LT4 ESI label per RFC7432bis ch.7.1). On DF PE, verify BUM is NOT forwarded back to local `agg-eth 0` (split-horizon enforced).
d25822395432e6b5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen
d25822395432e6b5.expectation:
Pass:    Type-1 route carries ESI Label Extended Community with allocated LT4 label; BUM packet from non-DF PE includes both LT3 and LT4 labels in MPLS stack; DF PE honors LT4 and does not loop traffic back to ESI.
Fail-on: Type-1 route missing ESI Label Extended Community; BUM packet missing LT4 label (only LT3 present); DF PE forwards BUM back to local ES (split-horizon failure).

d26d6fbd46520322.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 (DUT) and PE2 with matching ES `identifier 0 01.23.45.67.89.ab.cd.ef.00`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`, EVI 100, and multi-home CE1 (IXIA port) to agg-eth interfaces on both PEs.
Action: Wait for DF election to complete (default `es-waiting-time` 3 seconds), then capture BGP EVPN Ethernet A-D per EVI routes (type 1) from both PEs using `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 evi 100` or equivalent. Parse the L2-Attr extended community in each PE's advertisement.
Verify: Extract L2-Attr extended community from type-1 routes advertised by PE1 and PE2. Confirm both routes have the P (Primary) bit set to 1 per RFC7432bis §8.6 All-Active requirement.
d26d6fbd46520322.expectation:
Pass:    Both PE1 and PE2 advertise Ethernet A-D per EVI routes with L2-Attr extended community where P bit = 1 (All-Active mode requires P bit set for all PEs in redundancy group).
Fail-on: Either PE advertises type-1 route with P bit = 0, or L2-Attr extended community is missing, or only one PE sets P bit while the other does not.

d275a5808ac951db.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on DUT agg-eth interface with ESI Type values 0 (reserved), 1 (LACP-based), 3 (MAC-based), 4 (Router-ID), 5 (AS-based), and attempt Type 6 (invalid). Peer with neighbor PE.
Action: Commit each ESI config; trigger Ethernet A-D route (type 1) advertisement by enabling auto-discovery under l2-services/vpls; capture BGP UPDATE messages on neighbor PE.
Verify: show bgp evpn route-type 1 detail; decode ESI octet 0 from captured BGP EVPN NLRI; verify Type 0–5 generate valid A-D routes with matching ESI type byte, Type 6 rejected at commit with "invalid ESI type" error.
d275a5808ac951db.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + packet capture (BGP UPDATE decode)
d275a5808ac951db.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Type 0–5 each produce A-D route with octet[0] = configured type; Type 6 commit fails with validation error.
Fail-on: Type 6 accepted and advertised, or any Type 0–5 rejected, or A-D route ESI type byte mismatches config, or no A-D route generated.

d3c68eb2960915e9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed Ethernet Segment with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on DUT and neighbor PE; DUT EVPN instance "evpn1" with service-type vlan-based, auto-discovery enabled, export-rt 65000:100 in configuration/l2-services/vpls/auto-discovery mode; verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` on both PEs shows type-1 routes with RD, ESI, ET ID=0xFFFFFFFF, MPLS Label=0, and identical ESI Label Extended Community value (e.g., label 10000) across all routes for this ES; IXIA bidirectional traffic 1 Gbps through ES.
Action: On DUT, modify export-rt to 65000:200 via `export-rt 65000:200` command, wait 2 s for BGP convergence and capture type-1 routes; revert to original `export-rt 65000:100`, wait 2 s, capture routes again.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` on DUT shows ESI Label value unchanged throughout both transitions (same value before, during, after); neighbor PE `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms no mismatched-configuration alarm for this ESI; IXIA counters show zero packet loss; `show running-config` on DUT reflects active export-rt within 1 s of each change.
d3c68eb2960915e9.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (multi-homed ES) + IXIA traffic gen
d3c68eb2960915e9.expectation:
Pass:    DUT's ESI Label value in all type-1 routes for ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 remains identical throughout export-rt changes (RFC7432bis ch.8.2.1 compliance: "This label MUST have the same value in each Ethernet A-D per ES route advertised for the ES"); neighbor PE reports no ESI label mismatch alarm; zero traffic loss; each config change applied within 1 s.
Fail-on: DUT's ESI Label value changes during or after reconfiguration (mismatch across type-1 routes for same ES); neighbor PE raises mismatched-configuration alarm that persists > 2 s; traffic loss > 0 packets; config change not applied within 1 s; type-1 route fields incorrect (ET ID ≠ 0xFFFFFFFF or MPLS Label ≠ 0).

d3da84baa47e311b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as EVPN PE (loopback 1.1.1.1) with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled on BGP neighbor; one VPLS instance with auto-discovery; IXIA establishes BGP peer in same VRF with l2vpn-evpn capability negotiated.
Action: IXIA injects 4 malformed IMET (Route Type 3) UPDATE messages: (a) zero-length RD field, (b) IPv4 Originating Router IP (4 octets) with IP Address Length=128 instead of 32, (c) reserved NLRI bits set to 1, (d) second IMET from same fabricated source PE but different Originating Router IP value (violates RFC7432bis §11.1 'must remain the same for all EVPN routes').
Verify: `show routing bgp evpn routes type 3` confirms zero malformed routes in table; `show routing bgp neighbor <IXIA-IP>` shows session ESTABLISHED and error counter incremented by 4; syslog entries for each UPDATE error; IXIA capture shows BGP NOTIFICATION or silent discard per variant.
d3da84baa47e311b.expectation:
Pass:    All 4 malformed IMET variants rejected (not installed in EVPN table); BGP session remains ESTABLISHED; per-neighbor error counter (invalid UPDATEs received) += 4; syslog records 4 distinct errors (RD malformed, IP-length mismatch, reserved-bit violation, inconsistent-originator across routes).
Fail-on: Any malformed IMET accepted into `show routing bgp evpn routes`; BGP session reset/flap; error counter unchanged; missing syslog entry for any variant; DUT crash, memory leak, or malformed route propagated to other peers.

d420cee4021b59b9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with loopback 10.1.1.1, create evpn 'evpn-100' under l2-services with RD 10.1.1.1:100 (Type 1 format), attach Ethernet Segment with ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11. Configure BGP AS 65000, enable af-l2vpn evpn to neighbor 10.2.2.2. Configure neighbor PE (loopback 10.2.2.2) with BGP peering and route-type 4 acceptance.
Action: Execute 'auto-discovery' under the EVPN instance to trigger Ethernet Segment route (type 4) generation; activate BGP EVPN address-family; send L2 frames from IXIA to DUT Ethernet Segment member interface.
Verify: On neighbor PE, run 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail' and 'show bgp l2vpn evpn esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11'; parse RD type field (must be 0x0001), RD value (10.1.1.1:100), ESI (10 octets), and Originating Router IP (must be 10.1.1.1) from received route.
d420cee4021b59b9.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor PE receives type-4 route with RD type=0x0001 (Type 1 per RFC4364), RD value=10.1.1.1:100, ESI=01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 (exact match), Originating Router IP=10.1.1.1 (DUT loopback); BGP session stable, route installed in EVPN table.
Fail-on: RD type field ≠ 0x0001 (Type 1), RD IP portion ≠ DUT loopback (10.1.1.1), ESI mismatch or zero-value, Originating Router IP ≠ 10.1.1.1, or type-4 route absent from neighbor's BGP table after 60 seconds.

d44471912879a57b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn test-cw service-type vlan-based`, attach AC interface, establish BGP EVPN peering with IXIA (IXIA emulates remote PE).
Action: In mode `configuration / l2-services / evpn test-cw`, configure `control-word enable`; commit; inject 100 MAC frames from IXIA; capture outbound EVPN packets. Then configure `no control-word` to restore default; commit; inject another 100 MAC frames; capture outbound packets. Finally configure `control-word disable` explicitly; commit and verify identical behavior.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn test-cw` reflects current control-word setting; packet captures show 4-byte control word (sequence number starts at 0) between MPLS label and Ethernet frame when enabled, no control word (Ethernet immediately after MPLS label) when disabled or after `no control-word`; `show evpn mac` confirms MAC learning in all states.
d44471912879a57b.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen (IXIA emulates remote PE for BGP EVPN peering + packet capture)
d44471912879a57b.expectation:
Pass:    When `control-word enable`: DUT inserts 4-byte control word (flags + sequence number starting at 0) between MPLS label stack and Ethernet payload in EVPN MAC packets. When `control-word disable` or `no control-word` (both restore default disabled state): no control word, Ethernet payload immediately follows MPLS label. Running-config shows only the active choice.
Fail-on: Control word present in packets when disabled, or absent when enabled; `no control-word` does not restore default disabled behavior; running-config shows both enable/disable simultaneously; EVPN MAC advertisement or forwarding broken after any config change.

d44ab0fd3251ff4f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `agg-eth 0` with `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 0 type0-value`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`, and `service-carving highest-random-weight`; establish EVPN instance with active BGP peer; inject bidirectional IXIA traffic through the ES.
Action: Power-cycle the DUT using the lab PDU harness; monitor boot sequence and BGP convergence.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0 ethernet-segment` displays ES identifier, load-balancing-mode all-active, and service-carving settings; `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows ES in Up state; `show bgp evpn summary` confirms peer Established; IXIA traffic counters show resumed forwarding.
d44ab0fd3251ff4f.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
d44ab0fd3251ff4f.expectation:
Pass:    ES configuration (identifier type 0, load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving highest-random-weight) persists; BGP EVPN session re-establishes to Established state; traffic forwarding resumes per all-active load balancing (RFC7432bis ch.14).
Fail-on: ES config absent or load-balancing-mode reverted to default single-active; service-carving lost; BGP session stuck in Active/Connect; sustained traffic blackhole or incorrect load distribution.

d450906f9eba579a.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT with EVPN instance configured (`advertise-mac enable`, import-rt/export-rt matching 3rd-party PE); CE emulated by IXIA port attached to DUT; 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) in same EVPN instance; BGP EVPN session established.
Action: IXIA sends (a) DHCP Discover from MAC1, (b) ARP Request for gateway from MAC2, (c) ARP Request for peer IP from MAC3. DUT performs local data-plane learning per IEEE 802.1D.
Verify: `show l2-services mac-table` on DUT lists MAC1/MAC2/MAC3 as learned; `show bgp evpn route-type 2` shows Type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes for all 3 MACs with local VTEP as next-hop; 3rd-party PE CLI confirms Type-2 routes received and installed; IXIA sends traffic to MAC1/MAC2/MAC3 from remote PE-attached port → DUT forwards correctly.
d450906f9eba579a.expectation:
Pass:    DUT learns all 3 MACs in data plane within 1 second of frame arrival; advertises 3 distinct EVPN Type-2 routes to 3rd-party PE; 3rd-party PE installs routes without encoding errors; remote-to-local traffic reaches correct IXIA CE port (0% loss over 1000 frames per MAC).
Fail-on: DUT fails to learn MAC from DHCP or ARP frame; Type-2 route missing or contains non-standard encoding rejected by 3rd-party PE; remote traffic black-holed or misforwarded.

d4dcc3e92da0af47.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment under agg-eth interface with unique ESI (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99); enable auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt under EVPN instance; establish BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE; verify DF election completes and ES Type-1/Type-4 routes are advertised.
Action: Power-cycle the DUT via PDU while the Ethernet Segment is active and carrying bidirectional IXIA traffic (rate: 1 Gbps); monitor BGP session and route withdrawal/re-advertisement.
Verify: After full boot, run `show evpn ethernet-segment` to confirm ESI unchanged; `show l2-services evpn auto-discovery` to confirm Type-1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) and Type-4 (ES route) re-advertised; `show bgp evpn summary` for session UP; verify DF re-election completes and no duplicate ESI in neighbor PE's `show bgp evpn route-type 4`.
d4dcc3e92da0af47.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness (PDU-controlled)
d4dcc3e92da0af47.expectation:
Pass:    ESI configuration identical post-reboot; Type-1 and Type-4 routes re-advertised within 60 seconds of BGP session UP; DF election completes (DF flag set in Type-4); IXIA traffic loss < 120 seconds (boot time); no duplicate ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 seen on neighbor PE.
Fail-on: ESI changed or zeroed after reboot; auto-discovery routes missing after 120 seconds; DF election stalled (no DF elected after 60 s); BGP session fails to re-establish; duplicate ESI Type-4 routes from two PEs with same ESI.

d4fd05a4ae939ff0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface `agg-eth 0` on the DUT; navigate to mode `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0`.
Action: Issue `ethernet-segment` (no parameters); observe the CLI prompt change. Issue `exit` to return to interface mode, then verify persistence with `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`. Re-enter `ethernet-segment` mode, issue `no ethernet-segment`, and verify removal.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays `ethernet-segment` after entry; prompt changes to indicate ethernet-segment sub-mode; after `no ethernet-segment`, the configuration line is absent from running-config.
d4fd05a4ae939ff0.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
d4fd05a4ae939ff0.expectation:
Pass:    CLI accepts `ethernet-segment` with no parameters under `agg-eth 0`, prompt reflects ethernet-segment mode entry, and `show running-config` confirms the stanza is present (or absent after `no` form).
Fail-on: Command syntax error, prompt does not change to ethernet-segment mode, `show running-config` does not reflect the entered configuration, or `no ethernet-segment` fails to remove the stanza.

d511197121605d3c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn-instance-1 service-type vlan-based` with `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable`. Attach local CE on interface xe-0/0/1 VLAN 100; establish EVPN peering with remote PE; verify MACT contains ≥10 remote MAC entries with LT1 labels.
Action: IXIA injects from remote PE side: (1) VXLAN frame with LT1 label=999999 (not in MACT), (2) Ethernet frame with corrupt FCS, (3) BUM frame with VXLAN reserved bits 0x00FF set, (4) oversized frame (9216 bytes) with LT2 label. Concurrently inject one legitimate unicast frame from local CE as control.
Verify: `show evpn statistics evpn-instance-1` for malformed-frame-drops counter; `show log messages | match "malformed|invalid|dropped"` for drop reasons; `show evpn mac-table evpn-instance-1` unchanged; verify control frame forwarded to remote PE; confirm system uptime unchanged (no crash).
d511197121605d3c.expectation:
Pass:    All 4 malformed variants dropped at ingress; `malformed-frame-drops` counter increments by exactly 4; syslog contains 4 drop events with reasons (unknown-label, bad-fcs, reserved-bits, oversized); legitimate control frame forwarded successfully; system uptime > pre-test baseline (no crash).
Fail-on: Any malformed frame forwarded to local CE or remote PE; system crash/reload; error counter unchanged or count ≠ 4; missing/incomplete syslog drop entries; legitimate traffic blocked after malformed injection.

d5545806389e2b8e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure service-type vlan-aware-bundle EVPN instance with Ethernet tags 100, 200, 300 on agg-eth 0 with ethernet-segment shared to neighbor PE. Establish BGP peering between DUT and neighbor PE with matching import-rt and export-rt.
Action: Bring up ethernet-segment on both PEs simultaneously to trigger DF election via BGP EVPN route exchange. Send broadcast traffic from CE across all three VLANs while BGP session is active.
Verify: Inspect BGP EVPN AD per-ES routes and use show evpn ethernet-segment to confirm DF election modulo function selected Ethernet tag 100 (numerically lowest). Verify only the elected DF forwards broadcast frames to CE on all VLANs; non-DF drops them.
d5545806389e2b8e.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
d5545806389e2b8e.expectation:
Pass:    DF election modulo function uses Ethernet tag 100 (numerically lowest) per RFC7432bis §8.5; only DF forwards broadcast/multicast/unknown unicast; BGP EVPN routes exchange correctly; no duplicate frames at CE; both features operate without regression.
Fail-on: DF election uses Ethernet tag 200 or 300 instead of 100; both PEs forward broadcast (DF split-brain); BGP session flaps or routes missing; CE receives duplicate frames; DF role incorrect in show output.

d5fdedd09ad460bf.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance; under `configuration / l2-services / evpn <evpn-name>`, issue `unknow-mac-flooding disable`; attach two access interfaces; commit.
Action: Under the EVPN instance, issue `no unknow-mac-flooding`; commit. IXIA sends a frame with unknown destination MAC from one attached interface.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services evpn <evpn-name>` confirms `unknow-mac-flooding` line is absent (default restored) or shows `enable`; IXIA captures the frame on the other attached interface.
d5fdedd09ad460bf.expectation:
Pass:    `unknow-mac-flooding` absent from config or explicitly shows `enable`; unknown-MAC frame flooded to all member interfaces (default behavior).
Fail-on: Config still contains `unknow-mac-flooding disable`, or unknown-MAC frame NOT received on other member interface (disabled state persists).

d617bd8ac35f540f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance (EVI 100) with MTU 1500, control-word enabled, and ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on multi-homed interface. Configure neighbor PE-A with same EVI 100 but MTU 9000 and control-word disabled. Configure neighbor PE-B with matching attributes (MTU 1500, control-word enabled) for the same ESI.
Action: Establish af-l2vpn evpn sessions between DUT and both PEs. Exchange EVPN Type-1 (Ethernet A-D per-ESI) and Type-2 (MAC/IP) routes carrying L2-Attr Extended Community (MTU + control-word flag). Inject MAC addresses from IXIA attached to multi-homed segment.
Verify: Run 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary', 'show evpn instance 100 detail', and 'show evpn ethernet-segment' to confirm PE-A routes are received but DUT does NOT install PE-A as EVPN destination (no PE-A in next-hop list for EVI 100). Verify PE-B is installed and multi-homing (ESI all-active load-balance) operates. Check syslog for L2-Attr mismatch notification referencing PE-A loopback and MTU/control-word fields.
d617bd8ac35f540f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT receives PE-A routes but excludes PE-A from EVI 100 EVPN destination set due to MTU (1500≠9000) and control-word (enabled≠disabled) mismatches per RFC7432bis §7.11.2. PE-B is installed, ESI designated-forwarder election succeeds, and IXIA traffic hashes across DUT and PE-B. Syslog shows attribute mismatch for PE-A.
Fail-on: DUT installs PE-A as EVPN destination despite attribute mismatch, or multi-homing breaks (ESI not operational, no DF election with PE-B), or no mismatch notification logged.

d6a4e965fcbe7447.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS instance (e.g., `evpn evpn-100 service-type vlan-based`); enter `auto-discovery` mode; add `export-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:200`; establish BGP EVPN session to neighbor PE (verify ESTABLISHED).
Action: Commit the VPLS configuration; attach an AC (attachment circuit) to evpn-100 and bring up the interface to trigger EVPN auto-discovery route advertisement.
Verify: On neighbor PE, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` or capture BGP UPDATEs; inspect Route-Target extended community attribute in received Type-1 A-D per-EVI routes; confirm both RT 65000:100 and RT 65000:200 are present.
d6a4e965fcbe7447.expectation:
Pass:    All VPLS auto-discovery routes (Type-1 A-D per-EVI) from DUT contain both configured export-rt values (65000:100 and 65000:200) in the BGP Route-Target extended community attribute; neighbor can see both RTs in received routes.
