Fail-on: Routes advertised with missing RT values (only one RT, or neither RT present); incorrect RT values in extended community; or `export-rt` configuration accepted but not reflected in BGP route advertisements.

d6fc69df768a1ee6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN vlan-aware-bundle "test100" with RT 65000:100; configure BGP neighbor 192.0.2.2 in af-l2vpn evpn context with route-reflector-client, allow-as-in 1, private-as remove, maximum-prefix 500 warning-only; configure second neighbor 192.0.2.3 as RR client; establish both sessions.
Action: From IXIA (neighbor 192.0.2.2), advertise 10 EVPN Type-2 MAC/IP routes with AS_PATH containing "64512 65000" (private AS 64512, then DUT's own AS 65000); capture BGP UPDATE messages on the DUT→192.0.2.3 peering.
Verify: show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors 192.0.2.2 routes (confirm 10 routes accepted); show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors 192.0.2.3 advertised-routes (confirm routes reflected with ORIGINATOR_ID and CLUSTER_LIST attributes); decode captured BGP UPDATEs and verify AS_PATH contains only "65000" (private AS stripped) and ORIGINATOR_ID/CLUSTER_LIST present.
d6fc69df768a1ee6.expectation:
Pass:    All 10 routes accepted from 192.0.2.2 despite own AS in path (allow-as-in); reflected routes to 192.0.2.3 contain ORIGINATOR_ID (192.0.2.2) and CLUSTER_LIST attributes (route-reflector-client); captured AS_PATH shows only "65000"—private AS 64512 removed (private-as remove); no session reset occurs (maximum-prefix warning-only).
Fail-on: Routes rejected due to AS-loop detection (allow-as-in not working); missing ORIGINATOR_ID or CLUSTER_LIST in reflected UPDATE messages; private AS 64512 visible in captured AS_PATH; BGP session reset before 500 prefix threshold.

d703ecce16e01121.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn EVPN100 service-type vlan-based` under `configuration / l2-services`; verify interface `x-eth 0/0/1` exists in default state (l2-transport not enabled); ensure the interface is administratively up.
Action: Enter the EVPN100 service configuration context; configure `x-eth 0/0/1` as an attachment circuit for the service; issue `commit` to persist the configuration.
Verify: Commit operation must fail with an error message stating that the interface requires l2-transport to be enabled; execute `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN100` to confirm the interface was not added; configure `l2-transport enable` on interface `x-eth 0/0/1`; re-enter EVPN100 configuration and re-add the interface; commit should now succeed; verify `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN100` lists `x-eth 0/0/1` as an attachment circuit.
d703ecce16e01121.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
d703ecce16e01121.expectation:
Pass:    Initial commit rejected with clear error identifying l2-transport prerequisite for interface x-eth 0/0/1; running-config shows no attachment circuit; after enabling l2-transport, commit succeeds and interface appears in EVPN100 attachment circuit list
Fail-on: Initial commit succeeds with l2-transport disabled, interface added to running-config without prerequisite check, error message ambiguous or missing, or retry after enabling l2-transport fails

d7117f08c57adde6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with identical ESI (01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01) on aggregated-ethernet interfaces to multihomed CE; create 'evpn EVI100 service-type vlan-aware-bundle' with VLAN IDs 500-599; enable auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt 100:100; IXIA sends 10 Gbps aggregate traffic (100 Mbps per VLAN, BUM + unicast mix).
Action: Identify current DF using 'show evpn ethernet-segment esi 01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01:01'; configure IXIA to timestamp all packet loss events; administratively disable 'ethernet-segment' on current DF's LAG interface; measure DF re-election and traffic convergence time.
Verify: 'show evpn ethernet-segment detail' confirms new DF elected; IXIA reports per-VLAN traffic loss duration and packet drop count; verify DF selection is deterministic across 3 test iterations (validates VLAN 500 lowest-tag modulo per RFC7432bis §8.5).
d7117f08c57adde6.expectation:
Pass:    DF re-election converges in ≤ 500 ms; traffic disruption ≤ 1 second per VLAN; total packet loss < 0.01%; DF selection deterministic (same PE across iterations)
Fail-on: DF convergence > 500 ms, any VLAN with loss > 1 second, packet loss ≥ 0.01%, or DF selection non-deterministic

d7141435030ed042.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT at `configuration / l2-services` prompt; no EVPN instances configured.
Action: Execute `evpn EVPN-01 service-type vlan-based`; confirm prompt changes to EVPN configuration mode. Exit to operational mode, commit the configuration. Issue `show running-config l2-services` to verify the instance is persisted. Re-enter `configuration / l2-services`, execute `no evpn EVPN-01`, commit, and verify removal with `show running-config l2-services`.
Verify: (1) Prompt indicates EVPN configuration context after `evpn` command; (2) `show running-config l2-services` displays `evpn EVPN-01 service-type vlan-based` after commit; (3) After `no evpn EVPN-01`, the instance and all sub-configuration are removed from running-config.
d7141435030ed042.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
d7141435030ed042.expectation:
Pass:    EVPN instance `EVPN-01` created with `service-type vlan-based`; EVPN config mode entered; instance persists in running-config; `no evpn EVPN-01` removes instance cleanly with no residual config.
Fail-on: Command rejected; service-type not applied; EVPN config mode not entered; instance missing from running-config post-commit; `no evpn` fails to remove or leaves orphaned sub-configuration stanzas.

d752cd81bd21b8f2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance (BD 100) with `advertise-mac enable`, `unknown-mac-flooding enable`; configure BGP with `af-l2vpn evpn` toward IXIA peer 192.0.2.2; establish iBGP session.
Action: Use IXIA BGP Update builder to inject Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag routes (NLRI type 3, RFC7432bis §7.3) with malformed PMSI Tunnel attributes (RFC6514 §5): (a) attribute completely absent (violates §11.2 MUST), (b) Tunnel Type = 0x06 (Ingress Replication) but MPLS Label field omitted (violates §11.2 third MUST), (c) Tunnel Identifier length exceeds attribute length, (d) reserved Flags bits 0x80 set, (e) Tunnel Type = 0xFF (unassigned by IANA). Send one route per variant.
Verify: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` (UpdatesRejected counter = 5), `show evpn instance bd 100 route` (0 type-3 routes installed from 192.0.2.2), `show logging | include "PMSI|path attribute"` (5 distinct error messages citing RFC6514 or attribute code 22), `show system uptime` (no reload), `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <other-peer> advertised-routes` (DUT does not re-advertise the malformed routes).
d752cd81bd21b8f2.expectation:
Pass:    All 5 variants rejected (not in EVPN RIB); BGP session to IXIA remains Established; UpdatesRejected counter increments by exactly 5; syslog contains 5 entries naming PMSI Tunnel attribute (code 22) or RFC6514 violation; DUT uptime unchanged; no re-advertisement to other peers.
Fail-on: Any malformed route appears in `show evpn instance bd 100 route`, DUT crash/reload, BGP session flap, fewer than 5 syslog errors, or DUT propagates invalid PMSI Tunnel attribute in outbound updates (check `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <peer> advertised-routes` on a third peer).

d79ee09078dbc0b5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-based` with VLANs 100-105. Attach local interfaces (`x-eth 0/0/1` to VLAN 100, `agg-eth 0` to VLAN 105). Generate L2 traffic via IXIA to learn 8+ local MACs; configure neighbor PE to advertise 5+ remote MACs into the same EVPN instance.
Action: Issue `show evpn mac address-table` (all instances). Issue `show evpn mac address-table name test-evpn` (filtered by instance). If documented, test filtering by interface or MAC address. Repeat each variant twice on stable state.
Verify: Output displays tabular format with columns for MAC address, VLAN/BD, interface (local) or next-hop IP (remote), ESI, and type (static/dynamic/remote). Local MACs learned on `x-eth 0/0/1` and `agg-eth 0` appear with correct interface names. Remote MACs from neighbor PE list PE IP as next-hop. Instance filter returns only `test-evpn` MACs. Repeated invocations yield identical output.
d79ee09078dbc0b5.expectation:
Pass:    All learned MAC addresses (local and remote) appear in output with correct VLAN, interface/next-hop, and type fields. Instance/interface/MAC filtering (if supported) returns correct subset. Idempotent on stable state.
Fail-on: Missing local or remote MACs, incorrect interface/next-hop mapping, parser error, malformed table output, or output drift between consecutive invocations on unchanged system.

d7a6da17ed632394.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multihomed ES on PE1 and PE2: on `agg-eth 0` interface, configure `ethernet-segment` with matching `identifier 0 <type0-value>` and `load-balancing-mode single-active` on both PEs. Configure PE3 as remote PE with BGP EVPN peering to PE1 and PE2 in the same EVPN instance.
Action: Commit configuration on PE1 and PE2 to advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes (route-type 4). At PE3, capture BGP UPDATE messages containing these routes. Decode ESI Label extended community from captured routes to extract the "Multihoming redundancy mode" field value.
Verify: Confirm captured Ethernet A-D per ES routes from PE1 and/or PE2 contain ESI Label extended community with redundancy mode field = 1 (per RFC7432bis §14.1.1). Verify PE3's forwarding behavior toward the multihomed ES: send traffic via IXIA from remote site through PE3; confirm PE3 forwards only to Primary DF, not load-balanced across PE1 and PE2.
d7a6da17ed632394.equipment: DUT (remote PE) + two neighbor PEs (multihomed ES) + IXIA traffic gen
d7a6da17ed632394.expectation:
Pass:    Ethernet A-D per ES routes carry ESI Label extended community with redundancy mode = 1; PE3 correctly deduces Single-Active and forwards traffic only to Primary DF for the EVI/BD
Fail-on: ESI Label extended community missing, malformed, or shows redundancy mode ≠ 1; PE3 load-balances across PE1 and PE2 (All-Active behavior); PE3 fails to import routes

d80259315ccfc9f5.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT PE1 + Cisco IOS-XR PE2; MPLS core with LDP LSPs; BGP EVPN session (AFI/SAFI 25/70) established; both in EVI 100, Ethernet Tag 0 (VLAN 10). On DUT: configure `l2-services / evpn / unknown-mac-flooding enable` + `routing / bgp / vrf <vrf> / neighbor <PE2-IP> / af-l2vpn evpn`. IXIA ports emulate CE1 (DUT access) and CE2 (PE2 access).
Action: IXIA CE1 sends Ethernet frame: SA=00:11:22:33:44:55, DA=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (unknown MAC), VLAN 10. DUT performs source MAC lookup per RFC7432bis §13.1 step 1, then destination MAC lookup per step 2; DA unknown triggers flooding to PE2 via EVPN BUM (type-3 IMET-based forwarding).
Verify: On DUT: `show l2-services evpn mac-table evi 100` contains SA 00:11:22:33:44:55 with local AC interface. MPLS wire capture: EVPN packet from DUT→PE2 with inner Ethernet DA=AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF. On PE2: `show evpn evi 100 mac` or Cisco equivalent shows flooded MAC. IXIA CE2 port receives original frame within 1 second.
d80259315ccfc9f5.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Cisco IOS-XR or Juniper MX) + IXIA traffic gen (two ports: CE1 emulation + CE2 frame verification)
d80259315ccfc9f5.expectation:
Pass:    DUT learns source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 in local table (verified in `show l2-services evpn mac-table`); unknown destination MAC AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF triggers EVPN flood to Cisco PE2 (MPLS capture confirms EVPN encapsulation per RFC7432bis); PE2 accepts and forwards to CE2 (IXIA CE2 receives frame with correct SA/DA).
Fail-on: Source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 missing from DUT MAC table after 5s, OR no EVPN-encapsulated packet sent to PE2 within 1s, OR PE2 drops/rejects DUT's EVPN BUM frame (interop failure), OR IXIA CE2 never receives frame (black-hole).

d82a2081d9b9a81d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance with ingress-replication mode, enable `advertise-mac`, configure BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn`, attach at least one local bridge-domain to trigger Type 3 route generation. Configure neighbor PE to peer EVPN AFI/SAFI and capture BGP UPDATEs.
Action: Commit DUT configuration; wait for BGP EVPN peering UP; DUT originates Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag Route (Type 3) for the EVI.
Verify: On neighbor PE, capture BGP UPDATE containing Type 3 route from DUT; decode PMSI Tunnel attribute (RFC6514 §5): inspect Flag field, Tunnel Type field, MPLS Label field, Tunnel Identifier field.
d82a2081d9b9a81d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (with BGP packet capture capability)
d82a2081d9b9a81d.expectation:
Pass:    Type 3 route contains PMSI Tunnel attribute with Flag=0x00, Tunnel Type=6 (Ingress Replication), MPLS Label=valid LT3 label (non-zero, within DUT's label range), Tunnel Identifier=DUT's originating router IP address (BGP router-id or source loopback).
Fail-on: Type 3 route missing PMSI Tunnel attribute, OR attribute present but Flag≠0, Tunnel Type≠6, MPLS Label=0 or reserved, OR Tunnel Identifier does not match DUT's originating IP.

d875058a13db1074.action_steps:
Setup:  Define a route-policy (e.g., permit/deny based on route-target) in router configuration. Configure BGP with a neighbor address and enable AF-L2VPN EVPN address-family.
Action: Attach the route-policy to the BGP neighbor EVPN address-family context, specifying inbound direction; repeat with outbound direction. Test parameter validation: (a) valid policy name (1-63 chars, alphanumeric); (b) invalid: zero-length name, 64-char name, name referencing non-existent policy, prohibited special characters.
Verify: `show running-config bgp` displays policy attachment under correct neighbor and address-family with direction keyword; valid policy names commit successfully; commit rejects invalid policy names with parse error; commit rejects reference to undefined policy with validation error.
d875058a13db1074.equipment: DUT only (CLI session for configuration and validation; neighbor need not be reachable for syntax testing)
d875058a13db1074.expectation:
Pass:    Valid policy names (1-63 chars) accepted and stored in running-config with correct direction (in/out); undefined policy reference rejected at commit; invalid syntax rejected at parse/commit with descriptive error; running-config clean after failed attempts
Fail-on: Invalid policy name silently accepted; undefined policy reference accepted (dangling reference); policy attached without direction or with wrong direction; valid policy rejected; crash or BGP neighbor configuration corrupted

d893a7558db55a0f.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE EVPN fabric with ingress-replication; configure `advertise-mac enable` and `unknow-mac-flooding enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn` on all PEs; establish BGP EVPN sessions with Type 3 IMET routes; attach IXIA to PE1 and PE3.
Action: IXIA sends unknown-destination BUM traffic from PE1 (replicated via ingress-replication to PE2/PE3) and bidirectional unicast traffic (MAC-A ↔ MAC-B). After both traffic types forward successfully for 90 seconds, execute `tech-support` on PE1 and PE2; save bundles off-box.
Verify: Bundles contain: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output with Type 3 IMET routes and Type 2 MAC/IP routes; `show evpn mac address-table` with learned unicast MACs and tunnel labels; running-config showing `advertise-mac enable` and `unknow-mac-flooding enable`; syslog entries from test window.
d893a7558db55a0f.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundles demonstrate RFC7432bis chapter 13 unicast forwarding via Type 2 routes and populated MAC tables, plus Type 3 IMET routes and BUM config validating the full FLOW-031 scenario.
Fail-on: Type 2 MAC/IP routes missing (breaks unicast forwarding per RFC7432bis chapter 13), MAC table empty, Type 3 routes absent, or advertise-mac/BUM flooding disabled in running-config.

d8afa5d25b103b4e.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT single-homed (no ethernet-segment on CE interface, DUT does not originate RT-1); 3rd-party multihomed PE (Cisco/Juniper) with LAG to shared CE: configure ethernet-segment ESI type-0 00.aa.bb.cc.dd.ee.ff.11.22, load-balancing-mode all-active; MPLS core + BGP EVPN peering + shared EVI 100.
Action: Bring up 3rd-party ethernet-segment; verify RT-1 (Ethernet A-D per ES route, per EVI route) and RT-2 MAC/IP routes advertised with ESI 00.aa.bb.cc.dd.ee.ff.11.22; wait es-waiting-time (≥3 sec default); from IXIA behind DUT, send BUM (broadcast ARP) and unicast traffic to MAC behind multihomed CE.
Verify: DUT 'show bgp evpn route-type 1' displays RT-1 routes with ESI from 3rd-party PE; 'show l2-services fib-table evi 100' shows aliased next-hops (multiple MPLS labels/paths for same segment); IXIA captures confirm BUM flooded to all PEs in all-active set, unicast load-balanced per RFC7432bis ch.8.7 aliasing.
d8afa5d25b103b4e.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts RT-1 routes with ESI from multihomed 3rd-party PE; DUT forwarding honors all-active aliasing (traffic load-balanced across multiple next-hops to same segment); zero packet loss; correct BGP attribute parsing.
Fail-on: DUT rejects RT-1 routes containing ESI, or accepts but ignores ESI field (all traffic sent to single next-hop); encoding/attribute mismatch error; one-way or bidirectional black-hole; DUT incorrectly originates RT-1 despite being single-homed.

d8b2636cdabdfd1e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface agg-eth 0; enable L2 transport mode (per prerequisite: interface must be L2-capable); verify no VPLS or other L2 services bind agg-eth 0 or sub-interfaces.
Action: Enter configuration mode for interface agg-eth 0; issue `ethernet-segment`; `commit`.
Verify: CLI enters ethernet-segment configuration sub-mode; `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays ethernet-segment configuration block; commit succeeds with no errors.
d8b2636cdabdfd1e.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
d8b2636cdabdfd1e.expectation:
Pass:    Command accepted when prerequisites satisfied; running-config shows ethernet-segment stanza under agg-eth 0; commit clean.
Fail-on: Command rejected despite prerequisites being met; ethernet-segment absent from running-config; commit fails with errors.

d8b3f6268d74f0b2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 PEs in multi-homed Ethernet Segment with same ESI. On all PEs: `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`, configure `ethernet-segment` under `agg-eth` interface, enable auto-discovery with matching import-rt/export-rt. Configure Highest-Preference algorithm baseline, then apply local policy override (e.g., force PE2 as DF via static preference) consistently on all 3 PEs.
Action: Verify DF election converges with all PEs showing consistent DF. Modify local policy on only PE3 to force different DF election outcome (inconsistent override). Send BUM traffic from IXIA to multi-homed CE; capture ES route (Type 4) advertisements and EVPN Ethernet A-D per-ES (Type 1) routes from all 3 PEs.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` on all PEs for DF role and preference values; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` for ES route DF bit and preference consistency; BUM traffic forwarding path via packet capture on all PE-CE links.
d8b3f6268d74f0b2.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs (same vendor, EVPN-capable) + IXIA traffic gen + multi-homed CE (LACP LAG to all 3 PEs)
d8b3f6268d74f0b2.expectation:
Pass:    With consistent local policy override, exactly one PE is DF across all Ethernet Tags, all PEs advertise matching preference in Type 4 ES routes, BUM traffic forwarded by elected DF only. When policy becomes inconsistent (PE3 differs), detect split-brain: multiple PEs claim DF role OR duplicate BUM forwarding OR BGP route preference mismatch visible in Type 4 routes.
Fail-on: Consistent policy override fails to elect single DF, or inconsistent policy silently converges without observable mismatch in `show evpn ethernet-segment`, BGP routes, or forwarding (no detection mechanism for MUST violation).

d920de3ac604aff5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP neighbor under configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn with the private-as feature enabled. Establish BGP session and EVPN address-family activation. Ensure AS_PATH includes private AS numbers (64512-65534 or 4200000000-4294967294) via prepend or transit path.
Action: Configure an EVPN service that triggers route advertisement to the neighbor. On the neighbor PE, capture the incoming BGP UPDATE messages or query the BGP table after the route is received.
Verify: On neighbor PE, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and inspect AS_PATH for the received routes. On DUT, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <neighbor-ip> advertised-routes`.
d920de3ac604aff5.expectation:
Pass:    Private AS numbers (64512-65534, 4200000000-4294967294) are absent from the AS_PATH on the neighbor side; public AS numbers remain unchanged.
Fail-on: Private AS numbers appear in the neighbor's received AS_PATH, or route advertisement fails, or public AS numbers are incorrectly modified.

d92847ee1fd3ec81.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT has aggregate Ethernet interface `agg-eth 0` administratively up. CLI session in `configure / interface agg-eth 0` mode; `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` confirms no `ethernet-segment` stanza.
Action: Issue `ethernet-segment`. Capture CLI prompt change. Issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`. Issue `no ethernet-segment`. Issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` again.
Verify: Prompt after `ethernet-segment` indicates ethernet-segment configuration sub-mode. First `show running-config` displays an `ethernet-segment` stanza under the interface. After `no ethernet-segment`, second `show running-config` does not display the stanza.
d92847ee1fd3ec81.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
d92847ee1fd3ec81.expectation:
Pass:    `ethernet-segment` command succeeds, prompt changes to ethernet-segment sub-mode, running-config shows the stanza before removal and omits it after `no ethernet-segment`.
Fail-on: Command rejected (error message), prompt unchanged, `ethernet-segment` stanza absent from running-config after entry, or `no ethernet-segment` fails to remove the configuration.

d9bc89c3e03bd80c.action_steps:
Problem: Split-horizon must prevent BUM loops when multiple PEs share an ES to one CE.
Method: Configure PE1 and PE2 with identical ESI to CE1, send BUM from PE1 to PE2 carrying the shared ESI Label, and verify PE2 drops it on the CE-facing AC.
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with identical ESI (e.g., `identifier 0 <type0-value>`) on their ACs to CE1; establish EVPN instance with ingress-replication between PE1 and PE2; verify ESI Label allocated via `show evpn ethernet-segment` and advertised in `show bgp l2vpn evpn`.
Action: Inject BUM frame from CE1 that arrives at PE1; PE1 encapsulates and forwards to PE2 with the shared ESI Label; monitor PE2's egress AC to CE1 using tcpdump (should drop per split-horizon). Inject BUM with a different ESI Label from a remote source to PE2; monitor PE2's egress AC (should forward).
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE2 displays the shared ESI and ESI Label; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows ESI Label Extended Community in routes from PE1; tcpdump on PE2→CE1 AC shows shared-ES BUM is absent (dropped), different-ESI BUM is present (forwarded); decode BGP UPDATEs to confirm ESI Label encoding matches RFC7432bis ch.8.3.
d9bc89c3e03bd80c.expectation:
Pass:    BUM with shared-ES ESI Label is dropped on PE2's AC to CE1 (split-horizon enforced); BUM with different ESI Label is forwarded; ESI Label encoding matches RFC7432bis ch.8.3.
Fail-on: BUM with shared-ES ESI Label appears on PE2→CE1 AC (loop created), OR BUM with different ESI Label is dropped, OR ESI Label encoding deviates from RFC7432bis ch.8.3.

d9f92ce763018ad0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT + Cisco/Juniper PE as multi-homed PEs for shared Ethernet Segment (same ESI); enable BGP EVPN (af-l2vpn evpn neighbor <peer>); configure VPLS auto-discovery with matching import-rt/export-rt; connect IXIA to emulate CE on local segment.
Action: Configure 3rd-party PE to advertise Ethernet A-D Per EVPN Instance (type 1) routes WITHOUT L2-Attr Extended Community; observe DUT DF election. Reconfigure 3rd-party PE to include L2-Attr Extended Community (DF-preference=100, DF-capabilities); trigger re-election.
Verify: show bgp evpn route-type 1 detail confirms L2-Attr Extended Community absent (phase 1) then present (phase 2); show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI> displays DF election algorithm (modulo per RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 vs. preference per RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1) and elected DF; IXIA BUM traffic forwarded only by elected DF in each phase.
d9f92ce763018ad0.expectation:
Pass:    DUT applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 modulo-based DF election when received type-1 route lacks L2-Attr; switches to RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 preference-based election when all type-1 routes include L2-Attr; DF role transitions correctly, only DF forwards BUM to local segment.
Fail-on: DUT ignores L2-Attr presence/absence, uses incorrect DF election algorithm, DF role does not match expected winner, or both/neither PE forwards (split-brain or black-hole).

da7127df7240ff60.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-aware-bundle; configure ethernet-segment under agg-eth interface; enable auto-discovery with import-rt and export-rt; verify DUT advertises Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) routes to neighbor PE containing RD, ESI, ET ID (VLAN-ID for vlan-aware-bundle), MPLS label LT2 per <EVI,ES,ET>, and L2-Attr Extended Community with Primary/Backup flags; IXIA sends continuous L2 traffic to multihomed destination.
Action: Power-cycle the DUT using PDU harness while Type 1 routes active; monitor neighbor PE's BGP EVPN RIB and traffic forwarding state during reboot and BGP session re-establishment.
Verify: show bgp evpn route-type 1 (or show evpn auto-discovery); show ethernet-segment; capture neighbor PE: MUST NOT forward traffic on Type 1 alone until associated Type 4 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes also received (RFC7432bis ch.8); after full boot DUT re-advertises Type 1 with all fields intact (RD, ESI, ET ID=VLAN-ID, label LT2, L2-Attr with Primary/Backup); traffic resumes.
da7127df7240ff60.equipment: DUT + IXIA continuous traffic + neighbor PE + power-cycle harness
da7127df7240ff60.expectation:
Pass:    After power-cycle, DUT re-advertises Type 1 routes with all mandatory fields (RD, ESI, ET ID matching VLAN-ID for vlan-aware-bundle, MPLS label LT2 per <EVI,ES,ET>, L2-Attr Extended Community with Primary/Backup flags); neighbor PE does not forward traffic until both Type 1 and Type 4 received; auto-discovery config and RTs persist; traffic fully recovers.
Fail-on: Type 1 routes missing or incomplete after reboot (missing RD, ESI, ET ID, label, or L2-Attr flags); neighbor PE forwards traffic on Type 1 alone before Type 4 received (violates RFC7432bis ch.8 MUST NOT); auto-discovery or ethernet-segment config lost; BGP session fails to re-establish; traffic does not recover.

da881f45917a8637.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on DUT interface agg-eth-1 with ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99. Create two EVPN instances (VPLS 100, VPLS 200) both bound to this ES. Configure export-rt 65000:100 for VPLS 100, export-rt 65000:200 for VPLS 200. Establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE; enable af-l2vpn evpn.
Action: Activate the ethernet-segment on agg-eth-1 (no shutdown). Trigger Ethernet A-D per ES route advertisement by bringing up the multi-homed segment. On neighbor PE: capture received EVPN Type-1 routes for this ESI.
Verify: On neighbor PE, run `show router bgp routes evpn ethernet-auto-disc esi 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 detail`. Inspect RT extended community attributes in each Type-1 route. Confirm the union of RTs across all received A-D routes for this ES includes both 65000:100 and 65000:200.
da881f45917a8637.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN peer to receive and inspect Ethernet A-D routes)
da881f45917a8637.expectation:
Pass:    Each Ethernet A-D per ES route carries ≥1 RT extended community (RFC MUST). The complete set of Type-1 routes for ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 collectively carries both export-rt 65000:100 and export-rt 65000:200 (RFC MUST: entire set of RTs for all EVPN instances).
Fail-on: Any Type-1 route for this ES has zero RT communities, OR the union of RTs across all Type-1 routes is missing 65000:100 or 65000:200, OR routes carry RTs from only one EVPN instance when the ES belongs to two.

dabc4e6115dd43da.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as single-homing PE (interface agg-eth X, no ethernet-segment) with EVPN EVI 100; configure neighbor PE with ethernet-segment identifier 0 01.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.11, load-balancing-mode all-active, EVI 100; establish MP-BGP EVPN session; IXIA port 1→DUT CE port, port 2→neighbor multihomed CE port.
Action: On DUT, execute show evpn route type 1 and show evpn route type 4 to confirm A-D route reception from neighbor's ESI; send 10,000 L2 frames (64B, 1000 fps) IXIA port 1→port 2, then reverse; capture DUT's handling of neighbor's advertised MPLS labels.
Verify: show evpn route type 1 displays neighbor's per-ES A-D route with ESI 01.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.11, RD, and label; show evpn route type 4 lists per-EVI A-D route; show evpn mac confirms remote MAC learned via neighbor's label; IXIA Tx/Rx delta ≤10 frames.
dabc4e6115dd43da.expectation:
Pass:    DUT (single-homing PE) installs and honors Ethernet A-D per-ES (type 1) and per-EVI (type 4) routes from multihoming neighbor per RFC7432bis §8.7; traffic forwarded bidirectionally without loss or duplication.
Fail-on: Type 1 or type 4 A-D routes absent from show evpn route, ESI 01.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.11 not visible, IXIA frame delta >10, duplicate frames detected, or remote MAC learning failed.

db13ae77cb55d9d1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based`, enable `auto-discovery`, set `import-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:100`. Configure ESI on an interface. Establish BGP EVPN neighbor to a PE router with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled. Start packet capture on BGP session.
Action: Bring up EVPN instance and wait for DUT to advertise RT-1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) and RT-3 (Inclusive Multicast) routes to the neighbor PE.
Verify: Inspect captured BGP UPDATE messages for Extended Community Type 0x06 Sub-type 0x04. For RT-3: decode Control Flags bits 10–11 (C, F) and L2 MTU octets 5–6; confirm P and B bits (bits 8–9) are zero. For RT-1: decode Control Flags bits 8–9 (P, B); confirm MTU field (octets 5–6) and C/F bits are zero.
db13ae77cb55d9d1.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN capable) + packet capture (Wireshark on PE or IXIA tap)
db13ae77cb55d9d1.expectation:
Pass:    RT-3 carries non-zero L2 MTU and may set C/F flags, with P=0 B=0 in Control Flags. RT-1 carries P/B flags (values per ESI config), with MTU=0 C=0 F=0. Both use Extended Community 0x06/0x04.
Fail-on: RT-3 advertises non-zero P or B bits; RT-1 advertises non-zero MTU or C or F bits; L2-Attr Extended Community missing or wrong Type/Sub-type; partitioning rule violated (per-EVI attributes in RT-1 or per-ESI attributes in RT-3).

db45f958bc7b6401.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth with identifier type 1, service-carving highest-random-weight, and load-balancing-mode all-active; configure EVPN instance with 5 VLANs; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE advertising the same ESI.
Action: Wait for DF election convergence across all 5 VLANs; collect tech-support bundle.
Verify: Inspect tech-support for `show evpn ethernet-segment detail`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` (ES route with DF Election Extended Community DF Alg=2 for HRW per RFC8584 ch.3), `show evpn ethernet-segment service-carving`, running-config service-carving section, and syslog entries showing DF election events per VLAN.
db45f958bc7b6401.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (both advertising same ESI for multi-homing)
db45f958bc7b6401.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains ES route type 4 with DF Election Extended Community (DF Alg=2 for HRW); show commands display per-VLAN DF state (local router is DF or non-DF for each of 5 VLANs); service-carving highest-random-weight present in running-config; syslog has DF election convergence messages.
Fail-on: ES route type 4 missing DF Election Extended Community; show evpn ethernet-segment output absent; no DF state per VLAN; service-carving config missing from running-config; or zero DF election log entries.

db472fa7d22c5bae.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with EVPN instance (same RD 65000:100, export-rt/import-rt 65000:100, EVI 100); verify `advertise-mac enable` on DUT (default); BGP EVPN session up; IXIA port connected to DUT EVPN access interface.
Action: IXIA sends Ethernet frame (src MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, src IP 192.0.2.10) into DUT EVPN instance; DUT learns MAC locally; capture BGP UPDATE message from DUT to neighbor PE (tcpdump on BGP session or `show bgp l2vpn evpn route type macip` on neighbor PE).
Verify: Parse captured Route Type 2 NLRI for all mandatory fields: RD=65000:100, ESI (0 if single-homed or configured ESI), Ethernet Tag ID, MAC Address=00:11:22:33:44:55, IP Address=192.0.2.10, MPLS Label 1 (LT1, L2 VPN label), MPLS Label 2 (L3 VPN label), Sequence Number, RTs match export-rt 65000:100, MP_REACH_NLRI Next Hop=DUT PE loopback IP; confirm MAC Mobility Extended Community absent (first learn, not relearning).
db472fa7d22c5bae.expectation:
Pass:    Route Type 2 NLRI contains all mandatory fields (RD, ESI, ET ID, MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, IP 192.0.2.10, two MPLS labels, sequence number, correct RTs); Next Hop=DUT PE IP address; MAC Mobility Extended Community absent on first learn.
Fail-on: Any mandatory field missing or malformed in NLRI; Next Hop ≠ DUT PE IP; MAC Mobility Extended Community present on first learn (violates "only if relearning" spec); incorrect RD/RT/MAC/IP values; missing L2 or L3 label.

db602ef567d75d6a.action_steps:
Setup:
  1. Access EVPN instance configuration mode under configuration / l2-services / evpn.
Action:
  1. Issue `host mac-address duplicate-detection` to enable duplicate MAC detection.
  2. Commit the configuration.
  3. Run `show running-config` and verify the command appears.
  4. Issue `no host mac-address duplicate-detection` to restore defaults.
  5. Commit the configuration.
  6. Run `show running-config` again.
Verify:
  1. Command `host mac-address duplicate-detection` appears in running-config after step 2.
  2. Command is absent from running-config after step 5.
  3. Both commit operations succeed without errors.
db602ef567d75d6a.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
db602ef567d75d6a.expectation:
Pass:    Command persists in running-config when configured and is removed when `no` form is applied; both forms accepted without error
Fail-on: Command rejected with syntax error; configuration not saved after commit; `no` form fails to remove command; command state in running-config does not match expected state

db8a0a3c4bf6de87.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that the DUT installs forwarding state per RFC7432bis §9.2.2 timing rules during continuous PE-to-CE failure recovery: non-reserved ESI MACs must await both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes, while reserved-ESI (0) MACs install immediately from MAC/IP Advertisement alone.
Method: DUT receives EVPN routes from two neighbor PEs (PE1/PE2 multi-homed to Site-A via non-reserved ESI; PE1 single-homed to Site-B via ESI 0); inject periodic `agg-eth 0` link failures on PE1 over 24 hours; capture BGP route arrival timing and DUT forwarding table installation via logging and hourly snapshots.

Setup: PE1 and PE2 multi-homed to Site-A CE via `agg-eth 0` with shared `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (non-reserved ESI), `load-balancing-mode all-active`. PE1 also single-homed to Site-B CE via separate interface (ESI 0 implicit — no `ethernet-segment` configured). DUT peers via BGP EVPN with PE1 and PE2. IXIA drives continuous bidirectional traffic between Site-A and Site-B routed through DUT. DUT BGP logging enabled.

Action: Run for ≥ 24 hours. Inject `agg-eth 0` link failure on PE1 every 30 minutes (48 cycles total) causing PE1 to withdraw and re-advertise non-reserved ESI MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes. Monitor DUT route reception: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` for route presence; `show log | include "BGP|EVPN"` for arrival timestamps. Snapshot DUT forwarding table hourly: `show l2-services forwarding-table`. Track DUT memory hourly: `show platform process memory`.

Verify: DUT installs Site-B (ESI 0) MAC forwarding state immediately upon receiving MAC/IP Advertisement from PE1 (no wait for Ethernet A-D per ES). DUT installs Site-A (non-reserved ESI) MAC forwarding state only after receiving both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes from PE1/PE2. Logs show non-reserved ESI MAC installation delayed until both route types present for all 48 cycles. Forwarding state remains correct through all cycles. Memory growth ≤ 5%; zero unexpected alarms; traffic converges within `es-waiting-time` (default 3 seconds) plus BGP propagation per cycle.
db8a0a3c4bf6de87.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (multi-homed pair + single-homed segment) + IXIA continuous traffic (≥ 24 h)
db8a0a3c4bf6de87.expectation:
Pass: Non-reserved ESI MACs installed only after both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes received; ESI-0 MACs installed immediately from MAC/IP Advertisement alone; 48/48 cycles converge correctly; memory growth ≤ 5% over 24 hours; zero unexpected alarms.

Fail-on: DUT installs non-reserved ESI MAC before both routes arrive, OR delays ESI-0 MAC installation awaiting Ethernet A-D per ES routes, OR any convergence failure in the 48 cycles, OR memory growth > 5%, OR any unexpected alarms during the run.

db9d1ff933c00288.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS instance 'VPLS-100' under `l2-services`, enable `auto-discovery`; add `import-rt 65000:100` and `import-rt 65000:200`; commit and verify both RTs in `show running-config l2-services vpls VPLS-100 auto-discovery`.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / vpls VPLS-100 / auto-discovery`, execute `no import-rt 65000:100`, commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls VPLS-100 auto-discovery` shows only `import-rt 65000:200` (65000:100 absent); `show l2-services vpls VPLS-100 auto-discovery detail` displays import RT list = [65000:200]; `show l2-services vpls VPLS-100 mac-table` contains no MAC entries learned from EVPN routes tagged RT 65000:100.
db9d1ff933c00288.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
db9d1ff933c00288.expectation:
Pass:    `import-rt 65000:100` absent from running-config and operational auto-discovery state; VPLS-100 imports only EVPN routes with RT 65000:200; no stale MAC/IP entries from RT 65000:100 in forwarding table.
Fail-on: `import-rt 65000:100` remains in running-config post-commit, or EVPN routes with RT 65000:100 still imported into VPLS-100, or CLI command fails with error message.

db9fd57986c99152.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure l2-services vpls vpls1 with auto-discovery enabled; add export-rt 64500:100, export-rt 64500:200, export-rt 64500:300 under auto-discovery context; establish BGP EVPN peering with IXIA (AS 64500); verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows AD-per-ES routes (type-1) with all three RTs in extended-community.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls1 / auto-discovery`; issue `no export-rt 64500:200`; commit configuration.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls1` confirms export-rt 64500:100 and 64500:300 remain, 64500:200 removed; `show bgp l2vpn evpn advertised-routes` shows AD routes carry only RT 64500:100,64500:300; IXIA BGP peer receives UPDATE with modified route.
db9fd57986c99152.expectation:
Pass:    Running-config shows export-rt 64500:100,64500:300 present and 64500:200 absent; BGP EVPN AD routes re-advertised with only RT 64500:100,64500:300 in extended-community; IXIA receives route UPDATE ≤ 5s after commit.
Fail-on: Removed export-rt 64500:200 still in running-config, OR BGP routes still carry RT 64500:200, OR other export-rt values (100,300) inadvertently removed, OR no BGP UPDATE sent to peer, OR `show evpn vpls vpls1` shows stale export-rt in local database.

dbc9b04fb25c70d2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 2 PEs with MP2MP MPLS LSPs, both attached to the same Ethernet Segment (same ESI value); verify ESI labels allocated from DCB and consistent across PEs via `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show mpls forwarding-table`; configure IXIA with BUM traffic injection profiles.
Action: Inject BUM packets over MP2MP tunnel with malformed ESI label variants: (a) ESI label with upstream-assigned indicator instead of DCB; (b) ESI label value mismatching the configured DCB-allocated label; (c) corrupted ESI label stack (reserved bits set, invalid TTL); (d) missing ESI label when MP2MP is active.
Verify: Capture ingress PE drop statistics (`show l2-services evpn statistics`, `show mpls ldp forwarding`); confirm error counters increment for each variant; check syslog for ESI label validation failures; verify no PE crash or state corruption; send valid BUM traffic with correct DCB ESI label to confirm unaffected.
dbc9b04fb25c70d2.expectation:
Pass:    All four malformed ESI label variants dropped at ingress PE; error counter increments by 4 (one per variant type); syslog entries present for each variant (upstream-assigned, mismatch, corrupted, missing); no crash/state corruption; valid BUM traffic with correct DCB ESI label forwarded and source ES correctly identified at egress PE.
Fail-on: Any malformed variant forwarded to peer PE; silent drop (counter/syslog missing); crash or hung state; valid BUM traffic disrupted; incorrect source ES identification (egress PE cannot determine originating ES from ESI label).

dc214cf1ce27cb9c.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 (DUT) and PE2 (neighbor PE) in EVPN instance; IXIA sends CE traffic behind PE2 `agg-eth 0`.
Action: Test A (ESI=0): PE2 `agg-eth 0` has no `ethernet-segment` config (ESI defaults to 0); IXIA sends frame; verify PE1 installs MAC and forwards return traffic immediately (no ES route dependency). Test B (non-reserved ESI): PE2 `agg-eth 0` with `ethernet-segment` / `identifier 0 01.23.45.67.89.AB.CD.EF.01`; verify PE1 installs MAC only after receiving both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes; confirm via PE1 EVPN route table and MAC table correlation.
Verify: PE1 MAC table, EVPN route table (MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES route types), and bidirectional traffic forwarding; confirm ESI=0 installs MAC without ES route while non-reserved ESI requires both per RFC7432bis ch.9.2.2.
dc214cf1ce27cb9c.expectation:
Pass:    ESI=0: PE1 MAC table entry present with no corresponding Ethernet A-D per ES route; traffic bidirectional. Non-reserved ESI: PE1 MAC table entry present only when both MAC/IP Advertisement and Ethernet A-D per ES routes visible in route table; traffic bidirectional.
Fail-on: ESI=0: PE1 refuses to install MAC citing missing ES route. Non-reserved ESI: PE1 installs MAC with only MAC/IP Advertisement route present (ES route absent), or refuses to install despite both routes present.

dc5d81bde2a08c65.action_steps:
Problem: validate that static MAC entries are advertised via EVPN Type 2 routes without requiring data-plane traffic to trigger learning.
Method: configure a static MAC on PE1's access interface, verify PE1 immediately advertises a Type 2 route for it, and verify PE2 installs it before any traffic is sent.
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology with a shared EVI (matching import-rt and export-rt configured); PE1 has access interface agg-eth 0 assigned to the EVPN instance.
Action: On PE1 issue `mac-address-static aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff interface agg-eth 0` under the EVPN instance mode; commit. Immediately inspect BGP EVPN routes and MAC tables on both PEs without sending any data-plane traffic.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 2` on PE1 displays Type 2 route for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff with PE1 as next-hop; `show evpn mac address-table` on PE2 shows aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff installed; `show running-config | include mac-address-static` confirms the configuration persists across both query and commit.
dc5d81bde2a08c65.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC configuration accepted; PE1 advertises EVPN Type 2 route for the MAC immediately upon commit; PE2 installs the MAC in its forwarding table without requiring data-plane learning traffic.
Fail-on: Configuration rejected, no Type 2 route advertised for the static MAC, or PE2 does not install the MAC entry.

dc90691289d089ea.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance "evpn1" (any service-type) under `configuration / l2-services` with interface `x-eth 0/0/1` as a member. Verify `show mac-address-table` shows no static entries for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1` mode. Issue `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface x-eth 0/0/1`. Commit and exit to operational mode. Issue `show mac-address-table`.
Verify: MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 appears with type="static" and egress interface `x-eth 0/0/1`. Re-enter config mode, issue `no mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55`, commit, and verify the entry is removed from `show mac-address-table`.
dc90691289d089ea.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
dc90691289d089ea.expectation:
Pass:    After configuration, MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 appears in MAC table with type=static and interface=`x-eth 0/0/1`. The `no mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55` command removes the entry.
Fail-on: Entry not created, type is not "static", interface mismatch, entry persists after `no` command, or command rejected with error.

dca8ef1c8d1130ba.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-based and bind attachment circuits to customer-facing interfaces; commit configuration and verify EVPN operational state.
Action: Execute `show fib evpn-ac` at operational CLI prompt. Execute command again after 5 seconds on unchanged system to verify output stability.
Verify: Output displays FIB entries for configured EVPN attachment circuits in tabular format. Compare outputs from both executions for identity.
dca8ef1c8d1130ba.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
dca8ef1c8d1130ba.expectation:
Pass:    `show fib evpn-ac` displays FIB entries for operational EVPN ACs with tabular structure showing AC identifiers and forwarding information; successive invocations yield identical output.
Fail-on: Command error, zero entries displayed when EVPN ACs are configured and operational, malformed or incomplete table output, or output differs between invocations.

dcace2373b73153f.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that vlan-bundle service type drops ingress frames with VLAN tags outside the configured set.
Method: Configure a bundle with VLANs 200-203, inject VLAN 100 (out-of-set) and VLAN 200 (in-set) traffic, verify drops via interface counters.
Setup: DUT with CE attachment on x-eth 0/0/1.
Action: Configure an EVPN instance with service-type vlan-bundle, binding VLANs 200-203 to attachment circuit x-eth 0/0/1. From IXIA (simulating CE), transmit 1000 frames tagged VLAN 200 and 1000 frames tagged VLAN 100 to x-eth 0/0/1. Capture interface statistics before and after traffic injection.
Verify: Check interface counters on x-eth 0/0/1. Received: 2000 frames total. Discarded: 1000 frames (all VLAN 100). VLAN 200 frames: 0 discards, accepted into EVPN instance.
dcace2373b73153f.expectation:
Pass: All VLAN 100 frames dropped at ingress (1000 discards); all VLAN 200 frames accepted (0 discards).
Fail-on: Any VLAN 100 frames accepted OR any VLAN 200 frames incorrectly dropped.

dd0c6d03372dbdd5.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that the DUT correctly selects RFC7432 §14.1.1 versus RFC7432bis §14.1.1 redundancy-mode behavior based on L2-Attr Extended Community presence in Ethernet A-D per EVI routes, and that malformed L2-Attr variants are rejected without disrupting subsequent Route Type 2 MAC/IP advertisement processing.
Method: Two-PE EVPN topology with BGP session; inject IXIA-generated Ethernet A-D per EVI (Route Type 1) with missing L2-Attr, truncated L2-Attr, and invalid L2-Attr (reserved bits set); follow each with Route Type 2 MAC/IP advertisement; verify redundancy-mode behavior selection, malformed-route rejection, error logging, MAC table population, and session stability.
Setup: Configure two-PE EVPN topology with `af-l2vpn evpn` neighbor peering; establish EVPN instance with `import-rt` and `export-rt` route-targets; enable `advertise-mac`; attach CE to PE1; IXIA positioned to inject BGP EVPN routes toward DUT.
Action: (1) Inject Ethernet A-D per EVI (Route Type 1) without L2-Attr Extended Community, followed by Route Type 2 MAC/IP advertisement; (2) inject Route Type 1 with truncated L2-Attr Extended Community (shortened length field); (3) inject Route Type 1 with L2-Attr having reserved bits set to non-zero; (4) inject valid Route Type 1 with well-formed L2-Attr, followed by Route Type 2; (5) generate MAC learning traffic from CE.
Verify: (1) DUT applies RFC7432 §14.1.1 when L2-Attr absent, RFC7432bis §14.1.1 when present; (2) malformed L2-Attr routes (truncated, reserved-bits) dropped at BGP parse stage; (3) error counters in `show bgp neighbor` increment by one per malformed route; (4) `show log` records per-variant structured error; (5) Route Type 2 MAC/IP advertisements processed correctly after both valid and invalid Route Type 1 reception; (6) MAC forwarding table populated with learned addresses; (7) BGP session remains established.
dd0c6d03372dbdd5.expectation:
Pass: RFC7432 §14.1.1 applied when L2-Attr absent; RFC7432bis §14.1.1 when present; each malformed L2-Attr variant dropped; error counter +1 per variant; Route Type 2 processed; MAC table populated; BGP session stable; syslog entry per rejection; no crash.
Fail-on: Wrong RFC behavior applied, malformed route accepted, missing error counter increment, Route Type 2 rejected due to prior malformed Route Type 1, MAC table unpopulated, BGP session down, or crash.

dd8ea2b61dc90dd5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 PEs in same Ethernet Segment: under `configuration / interface / agg-eth <id>`, enable `ethernet-segment` with identical ESI (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99); configure identical local DF-election policy override on all 3 PEs that supersedes signaled Highest/Lowest-Preference (e.g., static DF preference values); under `configuration / l2-services / evpn <name>`, set `service-type vlan-aware-bundle`, enable `auto-discovery`, configure `import-rt` and `export-rt`; establish BGP EVPN peering; verify initial DF elected per local override.
Action: Power-cycle the current DF PE (DUT) via lab PDU harness; monitor EVPN Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) route withdrawal and DF re-election on remaining 2 PEs.
Verify: Post-boot, inspect DUT interface and EVPN config to confirm local DF-election override persists and matches peer PEs byte-for-byte (RFC9785 §4.2 consistency MUST); verify DF re-elected using the override (not default Highest/Lowest-Preference); confirm BGP EVPN session re-established, Type-4 ES routes re-advertised; validate BUM traffic forwarding recovery via IXIA.
dd8ea2b61dc90dd5.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
dd8ea2b61dc90dd5.expectation:
Pass:    Local DF-election override config on DUT matches peer PEs exactly after power-cycle (RFC9785 §4.2 compliant); DF re-election uses consistent override policy; BGP EVPN sessions re-establish; Type-4 ES routes re-advertised; BUM traffic recovers within 5 sec.
Fail-on: Local override lost, reverted, or differs across PEs (violates RFC9785 §4.2 MUST); DF election uses default algorithm instead of override; policy inconsistency; split-brain (multiple DFs); BUM traffic loss > 5 sec.

dd9a113ce43a0f98.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology (EVI 100) with host H1 (MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01) initially at PE1. Configure interface agg-eth 0 with ethernet-segment and load-balancing-mode all-active if testing multihoming.
Action: Move H1 to PE2 (trigger MAC mobility). After BGP convergence (≥ es-waiting-time default 3 seconds), run `tech-support` and save bundle off-box.
Verify: Extract bundle; inspect `show evpn mac address-table` for MAC with sequence number > 0, `show bgp l2vpn evpn` for routes with MAC Mobility extended community per RFC7432bis ch.15, running-config for ethernet-segment and es-waiting-time values, and syslog for MAC move events.
dd9a113ce43a0f98.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains: MAC table entry with incremented sequence number (> 0), BGP EVPN routes with MAC Mobility extended community, syslog MAC move events, and ethernet-segment config if multihoming is deployed.
Fail-on: MAC sequence number missing or remains 0; MAC Mobility extended community absent from BGP routes; no MAC move logged; ethernet-segment configuration missing when multihoming is used.

dda41999a57ea6d4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-aware-bundle under configuration/l2-services; configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0; enable auto-discovery with export-rt 65000:100 import-rt 65000:100; attach VLAN 100 as BD; establish BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE.
Action: Commit configuration; verify BGP EVPN neighbor state = Established; observe advertised routes via neighbor PE show commands or packet capture.
Verify: Confirm neighbor PE receives Ethernet A-D per EVI route (Type 1) for ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0 and BD VLAN 100; decode route structure for RD, ESI, ET ID = 100, MPLS label LT2, L2-Attr extended community with RT 65000:100, Next Hop = DUT IP.
dda41999a57ea6d4.expectation:
Pass:    Type 1 route advertised for ESI + BD VLAN 100; ET ID field = 100 (VLAN-ID); mandatory fields RD, ESI, label LT2, RT 65000:100 present per RFC7432bis ch.8.4.
Fail-on: Type 1 route not received by neighbor PE; or ET ID ≠ 100 for vlan-aware-bundle; or missing RD/ESI/label/RT; or route advertised without ethernet-segment configured on agg-eth 0.

ddc0d160428fa818.action_steps:
Problem: Tech-support bundle must capture complete EVPN control-plane state (BGP af-l2vpn evpn parameters, RD/RT, MAC tables, kernel FIB) to enable offline diagnosis of VLAN-based EVPN failures.
Method: Establish VLAN-based EVPN with BGP neighbor using common CLI parameters (allow-as-in, capability, maximum-prefix), run tech-support after MAC learning completes, and verify bundle contains all configuration, operational state, and forwarding tables.
Setup: Two-PE topology with MPLS core and BGP EVPN session; PE1 VLAN-based EVPN instance configured with RD, `import-rt` and `export-rt` under `auto-discovery`, BGP neighbor in `af-l2vpn evpn` context with allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, route-reflector-client parameters; single-homed CE attached; local and remote MACs learned.
Action: Execute tech-support command; save bundle off-box; extract and inspect contents.
Verify: Bundle contains: (1) `show evpn evi` with RD and state, (2) `show evpn mac address-table` with local-learned and remote-learned MACs, (3) `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary`, (4) running-config `l2-services / evpn` section (service-type, RD, RT via `import-rt`/`export-rt`), (5) running-config `routing / bgp / neighbor / af-l2vpn evpn` (allow-as-in, capability, maximum-prefix), (6) syslog entries for EVPN and BGP events, (7) kernel forwarding tables (EVI FIB, MAC table FIB).
ddc0d160428fa818.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains all seven items: show evpn evi, show evpn mac address-table (local + remote), show bgp l2vpn evpn summary, EVPN running-config (service-type, RD, import-rt/export-rt), BGP af-l2vpn evpn config (allow-as-in, capability, maximum-prefix), syslog entries, and kernel FIB tables.
Fail-on: Missing any of: show evpn evi, MAC table output, BGP summary, EVPN running-config, BGP af-l2vpn evpn parameters, syslog entries, or kernel FIB.

dddb94f9eb8d2ba6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure evpn test-evpn service-type vlan-based, establish BGP neighbor with af-l2vpn evpn enabled, attach VLAN 100, send IXIA traffic to create 10 local MAC table entries.
Action: (1) Verify default behavior: show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor <peer> advertised-routes displays 10 type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes. (2) Configure no advertise-mac under evpn test-evpn, commit. (3) Re-check advertised-routes (expect 0 type-2 routes). (4) Configure advertise-mac (or remove no advertise-mac), commit, re-check advertised-routes (expect 10 type-2 routes).
Verify: Count type-2 NLRI in show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor <peer> advertised-routes output; capture BGP WITHDRAW messages (step 2) and UPDATE messages (step 4) on neighbor PE using packet capture.
dddb94f9eb8d2ba6.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (for BGP EVPN peering and route verification) + IXIA traffic gen (to populate MAC table with known source MACs)
dddb94f9eb8d2ba6.expectation:
Pass:    Default (advertise-mac enabled) advertises 10 type-2 routes; no advertise-mac reduces advertised type-2 count to 0 and triggers WITHDRAW; re-enabling restores 10 type-2 routes and triggers UPDATE.
Fail-on: Type-2 routes still present after no advertise-mac committed, or routes absent after advertise-mac re-enabled, or command rejected, or no BGP WITHDRAW/UPDATE messages observed on neighbor.

de18c04dd53501c3.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that the tech-support bundle captures complete MAC Mobility diagnostic evidence (sequence numbers, MAC Mobility extended community, duplicate-MAC state, FIB entries) when a host moves between PEs in an all-active multi-homed topology.
Method: Trigger a MAC move by migrating host H1 from PE1 to PE2 using IXIA, wait for convergence, collect tech-support bundle on PE2, and verify it contains MAC tables with sequence numbers, BGP routes with MAC Mobility extended community, duplicate-MAC output, and FIB entries.
Setup: Configure two-PE EVPN topology with EVI 100, `ethernet-segment` using `identifier 0` and `load-balancing-mode all-active` on `agg-eth 0` on both PEs. Attach host H1 to PE1 initially and generate traffic to populate MAC table.
Action: Migrate host H1's attachment from PE1 to PE2 using IXIA (move traffic source). Wait for convergence. Execute `tech-support` on PE2 and save the bundle.
Verify: Verify bundle contains: `show evpn mac address-table` with H1's MAC and incremented sequence number; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` with MAC/IP routes carrying MAC Mobility extended community (RFC7432bis ch.15); `show evpn duplicate-mac` state; `show configuration` with `ethernet-segment` and `load-balancing-mode all-active` on `agg-eth 0`; kernel FIB entries for H1's MAC.
de18c04dd53501c3.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle includes MAC table with sequence numbers, BGP routes with MAC Mobility extended community, duplicate-MAC state, ethernet-segment configuration, and FIB entries for the moved MAC.
Fail-on: Bundle missing MAC sequence numbers, MAC Mobility extended community in BGP routes, duplicate-MAC output, or FIB entries for the moved MAC.

de23d8dd80aa2c2e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn EVPN-BUNDLE service-type vlan-bundle` on x-eth 0/0/1 with VLANs 100, 200, 300 explicitly allowed in the bundle; IXIA connected to that port.
Action: Send 1000 frames each with VLAN-ID 100, 200, 300 (in-bundle) and 1000 frames each with VLAN-ID 50, 400, 999 (out-of-bundle) from IXIA at line rate.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn EVPN-BUNDLE` confirms bundle VLANs; `show interfaces x-eth 0/0/1 statistics` shows ingress drop counter incremented by exactly 3000 (out-of-bundle frames); IXIA RX counters show only 3000 frames received (in-bundle traffic).
de23d8dd80aa2c2e.expectation:
Pass:    Ingress drop counter = 3000 (frames with VLAN-ID 50, 400, 999 dropped); IXIA receives exactly 3000 frames (VLAN-ID 100, 200, 300 only); no syslog errors.
Fail-on: Any frame with VLAN-ID 50, 400, or 999 forwarded to IXIA RX; drop counter != 3000; crash; `show evpn` state shows unknown VLAN-IDs accepted.

de53669260a65113.action_steps:
Setup:  On PE1, configure two vlan-aware-bundle EVPN instances on access port `x-eth 0/0/1`: `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` for VLANs 100-103 and `evpn evpn2 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` for VLANs 200-203; under each, configure `auto-discovery`, matching route-targets (evpn1: `import-rt 64500:1` `export-rt 64500:1`, evpn2: `import-rt 64500:2` `export-rt 64500:2`); establish BGP EVPN peering PE1↔PE2 with both EVIs active.
Action: From IXIA connected to PE1 `x-eth 0/0/1`, send broadcast ARP requests (1000 frames per VLAN, unique source MACs per frame): VLAN 101 (in evpn1), VLAN 202 (in evpn2), VLAN 50 (not configured), VLAN 150 (not configured); capture PE1 interface statistics and PE2's received EVPN routes.
Verify: On PE1: `show evpn mac` displays learned MACs from VLAN 101 under evpn1, VLAN 202 under evpn2; zero MACs from VLANs 50 or 150; interface drop/discard counters increment for VLANs 50 and 150. On PE2: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` receives MAC routes only for VLANs 101 and 202; no routes advertised for VLANs 50 or 150.
de53669260a65113.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 learns and advertises MACs from in-bundle VLANs (101→evpn1, 202→evpn2); PE2 receives corresponding BGP EVPN routes; PE1 drops all frames from VLANs 50 and 150 at ingress (zero MACs learned, zero routes advertised, interface drop counters increment by 2000 frames); both EVIs operational.
Fail-on: PE1 learns MACs from VLAN 50 or 150 (indicating frames were accepted instead of dropped); or PE2 receives BGP EVPN routes for VLANs 50 or 150; or interface drop counters do not increment by 2000; or either EVI fails; or BGP session flaps.

de6fd63627f3f4b0.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE EVPN topology (DUT as receiving PE, neighbor PE as advertising PE) with IXIA scale rig generating 1 Gbps background traffic. On DUT: configure `interface agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 10.00.00.00.00.00.00.00.01` (non-reserved ESI). Configure `l2-services evpn es-waiting-time 3`. IXIA emulates 10K MAC endpoints.
Action: Test case 1 (reserved ESI=0): Neighbor PE advertises 5K MAC/IP Advertisement routes (route-type 2) with ESI = 00.00.00.00.00.00.00.00.00.00. Measure convergence time from route advertisement to DUT forwarding installation via timestamped `show evpn mac-address-table` polls. Test case 2 (non-reserved ESI): Neighbor PE advertises 5K MAC/IP Advertisement routes with ESI = 20.00.00.00.00.00.00.00.02.00, followed 2s later by corresponding Ethernet A-D per ES routes (route-type 1). Measure: (a) no forwarding before route-type 1 arrives, (b) convergence time after both routes received. Scale each test to 10K routes.
Verify: Capture `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2`, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`, and `show evpn mac-address-table detail` with sub-second timestamps. Validate ESI=0 routes install immediately upon route-type 2 receipt. Validate non-reserved ESI routes do NOT install with route-type 2 alone, DO install after route-type 1 arrival within es-waiting-time (3s default) bound.
de6fd63627f3f4b0.expectation:
Pass:    ESI=0 routes: ≥99% converged within 100ms of route-type 2 advertisement, 100% within 500ms. Non-reserved ESI routes: 0% installed before route-type 1 received (MUST compliance), ≥99% installed within 3.5s (es-waiting-time 3s + 500ms margin) after both routes present, 100% within 5s. Packet loss ≤0.01% during all convergence events.
Fail-on: Any ESI=0 route takes >1s to install. Any non-reserved ESI route installs before its route-type 1 is received (violates RFC7432bis §9.2.2 MUST). Any non-reserved ESI route takes >10s to install after both routes present. Packet loss >0.1%. Any route remains unresolved after 30s with all required routes present.

de91bcc0e832b324.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (PE1, PE2) connect to same CE (IXIA) via LACP LAG; configure identical ESI on both: `interface agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment / identifier 1`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`; EVI configured on both PEs.
Action: On PE1 configure `service-carving highest-preference preference 100`; on PE2 configure `service-carving default`. Bring ES up; verify both PEs advertise Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) and Type 4 (ES) routes with ES-Import RT. Capture Type 4 routes from both PEs: PE1 must advertise DF Election Extended Community with 'DF Alg' = highest-preference; PE2 with 'DF Alg' = default (or no DF Election Extended Community). Verify both PEs detect algorithm mismatch and fall back to Default algorithm (RFC7432bis §8.5). Reconfigure PE2 to `service-carving highest-preference preference 50`; commit; verify DF election now uses highest-preference algorithm and PE1 (preference 100 > 50) becomes DF for all VLANs.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms exactly one DF per VLAN; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows active algorithm and ES-Import RT; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` and `route-type 4` confirm Type 1 and Type 4 routes advertised; BGP capture confirms DF Election Extended Community present and correct; BUM traffic forwarded only by DF (verify via IXIA traffic capture).
de91bcc0e832b324.expectation:
Pass:    When algorithms mismatch (highest-preference vs default), both PEs fall back to Default algorithm (RFC7432bis §8.5) and elect DF correctly; when algorithms match (both highest-preference), DF election uses preference values and PE1 (100 > 50) becomes DF; Type 4 routes carry ES-Import RT and DF Election Extended Community with correct 'DF Alg' field; exactly one DF per VLAN; BUM forwarded by DF only.
Fail-on: No fallback on algorithm mismatch (split-brain or no DF elected); incorrect DF selection under preference algorithm; Type 4 routes missing DF Election Extended Community or ES-Import RT; multiple DFs or zero DFs per VLAN; non-DF forwards BUM to access.

deacf296879b1799.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with Ethernet segment containing 2+ interfaces (e.g., ifIndex 10, 11); configure same ES on neighbor PE; enable `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (default); establish BGP EVPN session with `af-l2vpn evpn`; verify DUT advertises Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) routes with distinct LT4 labels per ifIndex via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1`.
Action: From IXIA simulating remote PE, send BUM frame (unknown unicast MAC) encapsulated with MPLS label LT3 (flood label for this EVPN instance) into DUT; vary the LT4 label in subsequent frames to point to different ifIndex values within the ES.
Verify: Capture egress frames on all ES interfaces; for each ingress LT4 value, confirm BUM frame flooded to all ES interfaces except the one matching that LT4's ifIndex; run `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` and `show mpls forwarding-table label <LT4>` to correlate label → ifIndex mapping.
deacf296879b1799.expectation:
Pass:    BUM frame flooded to N−1 interfaces (all ES interfaces except the one identified by ingress LT4 label); Type 1 routes present for ES with one unique LT4 label per local interface (e.g., label 1001→ifIndex 10, label 1002→ifIndex 11); zero frames observed on the excluded interface.
Fail-on: BUM frame forwarded to the interface matching LT4 (split horizon broken); missing or duplicate LT4 labels in Type 1 routes; BUM frame not flooded to any ES interface; LT4 label does not match ifIndex in BUM Split Horizon Table.

ded34a9189fe6d63.action_steps:
Setup:  Enter configuration mode, navigate to interface agg-eth 0, then enter the ethernet-segment sub-configuration mode.
Action: Issue the identifier command to configure a non-default ESI value and commit. Then issue `no identifier` to restore the default (type 4) ESI and commit again.
Verify: Run `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` after each commit; after the first commit, confirm an explicit identifier statement appears under ethernet-segment; after the `no identifier` commit, confirm the configuration either omits the identifier line (using implicit default) or shows type 4.
ded34a9189fe6d63.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
ded34a9189fe6d63.expectation:
Pass:    Both commits succeed; the first shows explicit identifier configuration in running-config; the second shows type 4 ESI (either implicit via omission or explicit); no syntax errors occur.
Fail-on: Either command is rejected with a syntax error, commit fails, or running-config after `no identifier` shows an ESI type other than the documented default (type 4).

ded75fb9116f648b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT interface `agg-eth 1`, enter mode `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment`, ready to configure ESI.
Action: Test `identifier` command with: (a) valid type-0 ESI `identifier 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88` (9 octets); (b) restore default type-4 with `no identifier`; (c) invalid values: wrong octet count (`identifier 00.11.22.33`), non-hex characters (`identifier 00.gg.22.33.44.55.66.77.88`), extra octets (`identifier 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88.99.aa`).
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` and `show ethernet-segment` display the committed ESI; invalid values trigger parse errors before commit.
ded75fb9116f648b.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
ded75fb9116f648b.expectation:
Pass:    Valid type-0 ESI `00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88` accepted and appears in running-config; `no identifier` restores default type-4 ESI (router-id derived); each invalid value rejected with CLI error naming the issue (format, octet count, character validation).
Fail-on: Invalid ESI silently committed to running-config; `show ethernet-segment` displays malformed ESI; CLI crash or hang on invalid input; `no identifier` fails to restore type-4 default.

def1397d36b2d28f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance for BD-100, enable multi-homing with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on lag1 toward neighbor PE1, configure P-tunnel (ingress-replication or P-multicast tree via mLDP/RSVP-TE), enable `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` under `configuration / l2-services / evpn`, configure `af-l2vpn evpn` under `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor` for PE2.
Action: Bring up EVPN BGP session, confirm multi-homing AD route (type-1) and ES route (type-4) are advertised with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, then verify IMET route (type-3) for BD-100 is advertised. Send BUM (broadcast ARP, unknown-unicast) from IXIA to trigger P-tunnel forwarding. Simultaneously send known-unicast traffic to MAC addresses behind ESI to trigger multi-homing load-balancing.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 3` confirms IMET route carries PMSI Tunnel attribute with tunnel identity (P-multicast tree ID or ingress-replication endpoint) and MPLS label unique to BD-100 per RFC7432bis §11.2. `show bgp evpn route-type 1` and `type 4` confirm ESI routes intact. Packet capture on P-tunnel LSP shows BUM frames with BD-100 label. Known-unicast frames continue to hash across lag1 ESI links.
def1397d36b2d28f.expectation:
Pass:    IMET route for BD-100 contains PMSI Tunnel attribute (RFC6514 format) with P-tunnel identity (if P-multicast: tree type + identifier; if ingress-replication: tunnel type 6 + originating PE) and MPLS label allocated uniquely to BD-100; multi-homing type-1/type-4 routes for ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 remain advertised; BUM traffic encapsulated with BD-100 label over P-tunnel; known-unicast traffic load-balances across ESI links per multi-homing.
Fail-on: IMET route missing PMSI Tunnel attribute, or PMSI attribute missing tunnel identity or label field, or BD-100 label shared with another BD, or multi-homing routes (type-1/4) withdrawn after P-tunnel config, or BUM not using P-tunnel (flooding as unicast replication instead), or known-unicast no longer hashing to ESI.

defcd3bc886a1167.action_steps:
Problem: Validates that MAC Mobility prevents traffic black-holing when a host moves between PEs by ensuring MAC Mobility extended community signaling, route withdrawal, and FIB updates occur correctly per RFC7432bis chapter 15.
Method: Configure two-PE EVPN with ethernet-segment on access ports; physically move host H1 from PE1 to PE2; capture BGP updates to verify MAC Mobility EC sequence increment and route withdrawal; confirm FIB convergence.
Setup: Two PEs with EVPN VLAN-based service. Each PE has `agg-eth 0` configured with `ethernet-segment`, `load-balancing-mode single-active`, and matching ES `identifier`. Host H1 initially connected to PE1's access port, MAC address learned in EVI.
Action: Detach H1 from PE1's access interface, attach to PE2's access interface. Send frames from H1 to trigger MAC learning on PE2. Capture BGP UPDATEs on PE-PE link using `tcpdump`. Execute `show evpn mac address-table` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on both PEs before and after the move.
Verify: PE2 advertises Type 2 route with MAC Mobility extended community carrying sequence number higher than PE1's previous value. PE1 withdraws its Type 2 route for H1's MAC. FIB on both PEs updates to PE2 as egress within fast-convergence time. Sticky MAC not incorrectly applied.
defcd3bc886a1167.expectation:
Pass: MAC Mobility extended community present with sequence number incremented from PE1's value; PE1 withdraws route; FIB converges to PE2 within fast-convergence time; sticky MAC not misapplied.
Fail-on: MAC Mobility EC missing or sequence does not increment, PE1 does not withdraw route, FIB does not update or convergence exceeds bound, or sticky MAC incorrectly blocks mobility.

df127a8e16eb94af.action_steps:
Problem: Validate backup DF promotion and aliasing route propagation when the primary DF fails in single-active multi-homing (RFC7432bis ch.8.6).
Method: Configure two PEs with shared ESI in single-active mode, induce primary DF failure, and verify backup promotion within es-waiting-time via IXIA traffic continuity.
Setup: Two PEs (PE1, PE2) connected to one CE via LACP on `agg-eth 0`. Configure `ethernet-segment` with `identifier 0 <type0-value>` and `load-balancing-mode single-active` on both PEs. Accept defaults: `service-carving default`, `es-waiting-time` 3 seconds. Verify PE1 elected as primary DF. IXIA generates continuous traffic through the CE.
Action: Confirm primary DF on PE1 via `show evpn ethernet-segment`. Simulate PE1 failure by shutting down PE1's `agg-eth 0` interface or killing the EVPN control-plane process via platform debug command. Monitor PE2 during the `es-waiting-time` window.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE2 shows local PE transitioned to primary DF within ≤ 3 s of PE1 failure. Aliasing route for the shared ESI appears in PE2's BGP EVPN table. IXIA traffic loss histogram shows service interruption ≤ 1 s; forwarding fully resumes through PE2.
df127a8e16eb94af.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
df127a8e16eb94af.expectation:
Pass: PE2 promoted to primary DF within 3 s; aliasing route present in BGP EVPN table; IXIA traffic loss ≤ 1 s; forwarding resumes through PE2.
Fail-on: PE2 does not promote to DF, promotion > 3 s, aliasing route absent, traffic loss > 1 s, or forwarding stuck.

df39b03fc0ec97c3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with advertise-mac enable (default), BGP neighbor with af-l2vpn evpn enabled, attach IXIA to DUT access port.
Action: IXIA sends frames with source MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 and IPv4 10.1.1.10; DUT learns MAC/IP and originates BGP MAC/IP Advertisement route (type 2); capture BGP UPDATE at neighbor PE.
Verify: Decode EVPN NLRI in captured UPDATE: RD field (8 octets, format per RFC7432bis §7.9), MAC Address Length = 48 bits, MAC Address = 6 octets (00:11:22:33:44:55), IP Address Length = 32 bits, IP Address = 4 octets (0x0a010a0a); repeat with IPv6 2001:db8::10 → IP Address Length = 128, IP Address = 16 octets.
df39b03fc0ec97c3.expectation:
Pass:    MAC/IP Advertisement NLRI contains: RD (8 octets), MAC Len = 48, MAC = 6 octets, IPv4 Len = 32 + 4-octet IP, IPv6 Len = 128 + 16-octet IP.
Fail-on: MAC Length ≠ 48 bits, MAC ≠ 6 octets, IPv4 ≠ 4 octets, IPv6 ≠ 16 octets, or RD missing/malformed.

df892c42b35006bd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as ingress PE in EVI 100; configure IXIA + neighbor PE to simulate remote PE advertising Ethernet A-D per EVI route (RFC7432bis ch.7.1) for ES 01:00:00:00:00:01 with LT2 label 50000 but no MAC/IP route for MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 (forcing LT2 usage per RFC7432bis ch.8.4). Install MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 in DUT's local table pointing to remote ES.
Action: Send 1000 pps known unicast traffic from local CE toward aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 for 60 seconds (DUT forwards using LT2 label). Collect tech-support: `show tech-support evpn evi 100` or vendor equivalent.
Verify: Parse tech-support archive for: `show evpn evi <id> ethernet-segment` (LT2 labels allocated per ES), `show bgp evpn route-type 1` (received Ethernet A-D per EVI with LT2 label 50000), `show evpn evi <id> mac-table` (aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 → ES forwarding state), `show mpls forwarding-table label 50000` (LT2 in outgoing label stack), running-config (EVPN instance, `af-l2vpn evpn` neighbor config), syslog (LT2 allocation/signaling events).
df892c42b35006bd.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: (1) LT2 label per ES in ES state output, (2) BGP Ethernet A-D per EVI route type 1 showing remote LT2=50000, (3) MAC table entry for aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01 with ES forwarding next-hop, (4) MPLS forwarding entry using LT2 in label stack, (5) running-config with EVI 100 and `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor, (6) syslog entries for LT2 label allocation/distribution.
Fail-on: Missing any of: Ethernet A-D per EVI route output (type 1), LT2 label allocation in ES state, MPLS forwarding table entry for LT2, or EVPN instance running-config; or tech-support bundle size < 10 KB indicating incomplete collection.

dfc029152766b691.action_steps:
Setup: Two-PE topology (PE1, PE2) with MPLS core and iBGP EVPN session. PE1: VLAN-based EVPN instance EVPN-100 with RD 100:100 and route-targets; CE single-homed on x-eth 0/0/1 VLAN 100. PE2: matching instance. PE1 BGP neighbor configured in af-l2vpn evpn context with common CLI parameters: allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix 1000, private-as remove, route-reflector-client, weight 100.
Action: Generate bidirectional L2 traffic between CE and PE2 to populate MAC tables (local learning on PE1, remote advertisements from PE2). Execute tech-support command on PE1. Save bundle off-box and inspect contents.
Verify: running-config shows EVPN-100 with service-type vlan-based, RD 100:100, route-targets (REQ#30, 70) and BGP neighbor af-l2vpn evpn section with all seven common CLI parameters (REQ#20: allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight). show evpn evi displays EVPN-100 operational state. show evpn mac address-table shows local MAC(s) from CE (REQ#90) and remote MAC(s) from PE2 (REQ#100). show bgp l2vpn evpn summary confirms neighbor state. Syntax in running-config demonstrates EVPN command support for all service-types: vlan-based, vlan-bundle, vlan-aware-bundle, port-based (REQ#40, 50, 60).
dfc029152766b691.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains running-config with VLAN-based EVPN instance (including RD, route-targets), BGP neighbor af-l2vpn evpn context with all common CLI parameters (allow-as-in, capability, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, maximum-prefix, private-as, route-reflector-client, weight), and show evpn mac address-table displaying both local and remote MAC entries.
Fail-on: Any of the seven BGP common CLI parameters missing from running-config af-l2vpn evpn context, EVPN instance missing service-type vlan-based or RD, or MAC address-table showing only local or only remote entries (both must be present).
