dfecfd2f52692ed7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based` under `configuration / l2-services`; verify physical interface `x-eth 0/0/1` is up/up.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1`, configure the interface as an attachment circuit for the service, `commit`; capture config with `show running-config l2-services`; use `no interface` form to remove, `commit`, verify removal in running-config; re-add interface, `commit`, save configuration, reload DUT.
Verify: Post-reload `show running-config l2-services` shows interface under `evpn evpn1`; `show evpn` indicates interface is attached with operational status reflecting successful binding to the EVPN instance.
dfecfd2f52692ed7.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
dfecfd2f52692ed7.expectation:
Pass:    Interface config accepted under EVPN service, visible in running-config, `no` form removes cleanly with no residual entries, interface configuration byte-identical after reload
Fail-on: Command rejected (syntax/semantic error), `no` form incomplete (partial config remains), or interface not present in EVPN service config/status after reload

dffaa2f0d801b00c.action_steps:
Setup:  Navigate to `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment`.
Action: Configure a type 0, 1, or 3 identifier (ESI) using the `identifier` command with appropriate type-specific parameters; commit. Issue `no identifier`; commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` displays the configured identifier line under ethernet-segment after first commit. After `no identifier`, confirm the default type 4 identifier is restored (verify in running-config).
dffaa2f0d801b00c.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
dffaa2f0d801b00c.expectation:
Pass:    Non-default identifier appears in running-config with correct type; `no identifier` restores type 4 default
Fail-on: Command rejected, identifier not in running-config, wrong type displayed, `no` form fails, or type 4 not restored as default

e004115dac6ffe77.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT configured as single-homing PE (no ethernet-segment configured on interfaces); 2 neighbor PEs share ES identifier type 0, load-balancing-mode all-active; BGP EVPN sessions established; IXIA traffic traversing multihomed ES.
Action: Kill EVPN control-plane process on DUT (platform-specific debug: kill -9 bgpd or evpnd).
Verify: Process auto-restarts; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` displays ES routes from neighbor ES; `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows remote ES state; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` shows AD per-EVI routes; traffic resumes within es-waiting-time (default 3 sec) + convergence.
e004115dac6ffe77.equipment: DUT (single-homed PE, no ES config) + 2 neighbor PEs (shared ES, active-active) + IXIA traffic gen
e004115dac6ffe77.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts; DUT re-imports Type 4 (ES route) and Type 1 (AD per-EVI) routes from multihomed neighbors; traffic loss ≤ 10 sec (es-waiting-time 3s + BGP reconvergence); DUT respects aliasing (no traffic to failed PE if one fails).
Fail-on: Process doesn't restart; Type 4 or Type 1 routes not re-learned from multihomed ES; traffic blackhole persists > 10 sec; DUT forwards to failed PE in multihomed ES pair.

e034690af136499e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE multi-homed to the same Ethernet Segment (identical ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99); establish MP2MP MPLS LSP (LDP or RSVP-TE MP2MP) between both PEs; enable `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on DUT EVPN instance; bring up BGP EVPN session (`af-l2vpn evpn` under neighbor).
Action: Verify both PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per-ES route (type 4) for the shared ESI; capture ESI Label value from BGP EVPN route (NLRI field) on both PEs; check label allocation tables to confirm ESI Label is from Downstream Common Block (DCB) range; inject BUM (broadcast) frame from IXIA into one PE; capture encapsulated packet at egress PE.
Verify: `show bgp evpn routes type 4` on DUT and `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on 3rd-party to extract ESI Label; `show mpls forwarding-table` or vendor equivalent to confirm label range is DCB; compare ESI Label values — must be identical on DUT and 3rd-party PE; egress PE's received MPLS packet must carry the shared ESI Label, allowing source ES identification per RFC7432bis §8.3.1.3.
e034690af136499e.expectation:
Pass:    DUT and 3rd-party PE both allocate the same ESI Label value from DCB range for the shared ES (e.g., label 5000 from DCB block 5000–5999); BUM packets received at egress PE carry that ESI Label in the MPLS stack; egress PE correctly identifies source ES.
Fail-on: ESI Labels differ between the two PEs attached to the same ES, or ESI Label is allocated outside DCB range, or egress PE receives BUM packet with wrong/missing ESI Label, or egress PE cannot determine source ES from the label.

e087ede8d402e414.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 (DF) and PE2 (Non-DF) multi-homed to ES-200: under `interface agg-eth-1/1/1/1`, enable `ethernet-segment` with `identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88` and `load-balancing-mode all-active`. PE3 configured for ingress replication to PE1/PE2/PE4. `evpn es-waiting-time` at default 3 sec. IXIA: BUM generator on PE3, split-horizon monitor on ES-200.
Action: IXIA transmits 10 Gbps L2 multicast BUM traffic (200K MAC flows, IMIX 64-1518 byte frames) from PE3 for 300 sec. PE3 replicates to PE1/PE2 with ESI label, to PE4 without ESI label. Measure: sustained Gbps/pps, P50/P99 latency, CPU% on PE3.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn route type 1` confirms ESI label advertised for ES-200. `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows label programmed in platform label space. IXIA MPLS decode: ESI label present in copies to PE1/PE2, absent in copy to PE4. IXIA on ES-200: 0 packets.
e087ede8d402e414.equipment: Four PEs (PE1/PE2 multi-homed to ES-200, PE3 ingress, PE4 non-ES egress) + IXIA scale rig
e087ede8d402e414.expectation:
Pass:    PE3 sustains ≥9.8 Gbps (≥98% line rate) for 300 sec; P99 latency ≤50 µs; ESI label correctly inserted in 100% of copies to PE1/PE2, omitted in 100% to PE4; 0 split-horizon violations; packet loss ≤0.001%; CPU ≤80%.
Fail-on: Throughput <9.8 Gbps sustained, P99 latency >50 µs, any ESI label insertion error, any packet on ES-200 IXIA port (split-horizon failure), loss >0.001%, or CPU >80%.

e098be12f8d27a88.action_steps:
Problem: validate that the vlan-bundle service-type drops frames with VLAN-IDs outside the configured bundle, preventing incorrect forwarding or MAC learning.
Method: configure an EVPN instance as vlan-bundle with VLANs 200-203, inject traffic on both bundle VLAN 200 and non-bundle VLAN 300, and verify VLAN 300 is dropped at ingress.
Setup: Two-PE topology with MPLS transport and BGP EVPN peering established; CE trunk to PE1 on x-eth 0/0/1 carrying VLANs 200-203 and 300.
Action: On PE1 enter `configuration / l2-services` and issue `evpn evpn-12 service-type vlan-bundle`, bind VLANs 200-203 to evpn-12 and configure route-targets per documented syntax, commit. From IXIA send 50 Mbps unicast with unique source MACs on VLAN 200 and 50 Mbps on VLAN 300, both toward PE2.
Verify: On PE1 run `show running-config`, `show evpn mac address-table evi evpn-12`; on PE2 capture frames and check rx counters per VLAN. Confirm VLAN 200 MACs learned and forwarded to PE2, VLAN 300 MACs absent from MAC table and zero rx at PE2.
e098be12f8d27a88.expectation:
Pass:    VLAN 200 source MACs present in PE1 MAC table; VLAN 200 frames arrive at PE2. VLAN 300 generates zero MAC entries; zero VLAN 300 rx frames at PE2.
Fail-on: VLAN 300 source MACs appear in evpn-12 MAC table, or VLAN 300 frames arrive at PE2.

e09ddece8349d6f5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE in All-Active mode on shared ES (agg-eth with `load-balancing-mode all-active`); enable ingress replication for BUM; verify DUT is Non-DF and neighbor is DF; IXIA sends ARP broadcast from CE behind the ES.
Action: Capture BUM packets at ingress PE to confirm ESI label is present; kill EVPN control-plane process (evpnd) on DUT while BUM traffic flows; wait for restart.
Verify: Process restarts within `es-waiting-time` (3s default); `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms ESI label re-advertised; `show mpls forwarding-table` shows ESI label entry with split-horizon action (do not forward to ES); capture BUM packets post-recovery—ESI label still present in MPLS stack, 0 packets forwarded back to source ES.
e09ddece8349d6f5.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen (process-kill harness)
e09ddece8349d6f5.expectation:
Pass:    evpnd restarts ≤3s; ESI label re-programmed in platform label space; BUM packets from Non-DF include ESI label (visible in MPLS stack capture); DF does NOT forward labeled packets to source ES (0 count on ES CE-facing port); traffic loss ≤3s.
Fail-on: Process down >3s, ESI label missing from per-ES route or MPLS forwarding entry, BUM packets lack ESI label post-recovery, or any packet with ESI label forwarded back to source ES (split-horizon violated).

e0e285c2d6efd4ce.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that MAC/IP advertisement route processing follows RFC version selection determined by L2-Attr Extended Community presence in received Type 1 Ethernet A-D routes, ensuring both RFC7432bis §7.2 packet encoding compliance and redundancy-mode-aware forwarding.

Method: Establish multi-homed EVPN with Type 1 routes carrying or omitting L2-Attr Extended Community, trigger MAC learning via IPv4/IPv6 traffic, capture Type 2 routes, and verify packet format and redundancy behavior match the applicable RFC.

Setup:
1. Three-PE topology: PE1/PE2 dual-homed to CE-A, PE3 connected to CE-B; configure BGP `af-l2vpn evpn`, route targets via `import-rt` / `export-rt`, `advertise-mac enable`; establish iBGP sessions; clear MAC tables.

Action:
1. Configure PE1/PE2 to advertise Type 1 Ethernet A-D per EVPN Instance routes to PE3 without L2-Attr Extended Community.
2. Send IPv4 unicast frame (10.0.0.1 → 10.0.0.2) from CE-A, then IPv6 frame (2001:db8::1 → 2001:db8::2).
3. Capture BGP UPDATEs on PE1→PE3 link carrying Type 2 MAC/IP routes; decode NLRI per RFC7432bis §7.2: RD, ESI, Eth-Tag, MAC (length 48), IP (length 32 for IPv4, 128 for IPv6), MPLS label(s).
4. Verify PE3 applies RFC7432 §14.1.1 redundancy interpretation via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` and observe single-active forwarding behavior (default when L2-Attr absent).
5. Reconfigure PE1/PE2 to include L2-Attr Extended Community with redundancy mode signaling in Type 1 routes; clear PE3 MAC table; repeat steps 2-3.
6. Verify PE3 now applies RFC7432bis §14.1.1, interpreting redundancy mode from L2-Attr content; confirm forwarding behavior matches the signaled mode.

Verify:
1. All Type 2 NLRI fields match RFC7432bis §7.2 byte-for-byte for both IPv4 and IPv6 host IPs: MAC length = 48, IP Address Length = 32 (IPv4) or 128 (IPv6), MPLS label(s) present, RD/ESI well-formed.
2. PE3 MAC forwarding table populated for both address families; `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` shows learned routes with correct labels; bidirectional traffic flows for both IPv4 and IPv6.
3. Redundancy behavior changes observably when L2-Attr is added to Type 1 routes: RFC7432 §14.1.1 behavior (single-active default) transitions to RFC7432bis §14.1.1 behavior (mode per L2-Attr).
e0e285c2d6efd4ce.expectation:
Pass:    Type 2 routes encoded per RFC7432bis §7.2 for both IPv4 and IPv6; all NLRI fields correct (MAC length=48, IP Address Length=32/128, label present, RD/ESI valid); PE3 correctly selects RFC7432 §14.1.1 when L2-Attr absent from Type 1 routes and RFC7432bis §14.1.1 when L2-Attr present; MAC forwarding table installs entries for both address families; bidirectional forwarding succeeds; redundancy mode interpretation changes appropriately based on L2-Attr presence.

Fail-on: Type 2 NLRI field deviates from RFC7432bis §7.2 (wrong MAC/IP length, missing label, malformed RD/ESI, IPv6 host IP not carried); PE3 misapplies RFC version or does not change redundancy interpretation when L2-Attr is added; MAC not learned; forwarding fails; or PE3 drops routes.

e1ef17dbf76af5c5.action_steps:
Problem: when a PE in an all-active multi-homed setup fails, remote PEs must reroute traffic to the surviving PE(s) using the aliasing mechanism (RFC7432bis §8.4).
Method: configure all-active multi-homing between CE and PE1/PE2 with matching ESI, send known-unicast from remote PE3, administratively disable PE1's ethernet-segment, verify PE3 switches to PE2 via best-path selection on Ethernet A-D routes.
Setup: Configure PE1 and PE2 with `ethernet-segment` under `agg-eth 0`, set identical `identifier 0 <type0-value>` on both, configure `load-balancing-mode all-active`, establish EVPN instance, advertise a test MAC from CE; PE3 is remote PE learning the MAC via BGP.
Action: From PE3, send continuous known-unicast to the multi-homed MAC; capture `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on PE3 showing Ethernet A-D routes from both PE1 and PE2 with best-path selected; administratively shut down PE1's `agg-eth 0` or issue `no ethernet-segment`; observe BGP withdrawal and best-path recalculation.
Verify: Before failure, `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE1 and PE2 both show ESI in DF election; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on PE3 shows Ethernet A-D per EVI routes from PE1 and PE2, with one selected as best-path; after PE1 failure, PE3's best-path switches to PE2 within the `es-waiting-time` convergence window; traffic resumes on PE2 path; no black-hole beyond the BGP convergence interval; capture MAC address-table and route updates.
e1ef17dbf76af5c5.expectation:
Pass: PE3's BGP best-path for Ethernet A-D routes switches from PE1 to PE2 within convergence window (default `es-waiting-time` = 3 seconds); traffic resumes via PE2; no duplicate frames; `show evpn ethernet-segment` on PE2 reflects DF role update.
Fail-on: black-hole lasting > 3 seconds, no BGP best-path switch, traffic continues attempting PE1, duplicate frames on CE access, or Ethernet A-D routes not withdrawn.

e20aa5a72d0f846e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with VPLS instance vpls-100, auto-discovery enabled, import-rt 65000:100, export-rt 65000:100; configure BGP neighbor 10.0.0.2 with af-l2vpn evpn enabled. Configure neighbor PE to advertise Ethernet A-D per EVPN Instance route (type 1, RD 10.0.0.2:100, RT 65000:100, ESI 00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00, Ethernet-Tag-ID 100) with L2-Attr Extended Community attribute included.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN session; neighbor PE sends BGP Update containing route type 1 with L2-Attr Extended Community; DUT processes route and selects RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 behavior (all received routes include L2-Attr).
Verify: show bgp evpn route type 1 — confirm route received with L2-Attr Extended Community present in path attributes; show l2-services vpls vpls-100 — verify operational state = up and route accepted; show bgp evpn summary — verify routes-received count incremented.
e20aa5a72d0f846e.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (requires PE supporting L2-Attr Extended Community in Ethernet A-D per EVI routes; alternatively DUT + IXIA with BGP EVPN route emulation if L2-Attr Extended Community attribute configurable)
e20aa5a72d0f846e.expectation:
Pass:    Route type 1 with L2-Attr Extended Community received and parsed; DUT applies RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 route target handling; VPLS instance vpls-100 operational state = up; show bgp evpn route type 1 displays L2-Attr Extended Community in received route.
Fail-on: Route rejected despite L2-Attr Extended Community present; RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 (legacy) behavior applied when L2-Attr present in all routes; L2-Attr Extended Community not parsed or missing from show output; VPLS operational state != up; routes-received counter frozen.

e220d60e19ee29ba.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT IRB interface for subnet 10.1.1.0/24 with default gateway MAC aa:bb:cc:00:00:01 and advertise via BGP EVPN with Default Gateway ext-community. Configure neighbor PE to advertise conflicting MAC route (aa:bb:cc:00:00:01 without Default Gateway ext-community) to trigger conflict detection.
Action: Initiate BGP route exchange. Wait for DUT to detect MAC conflict and raise alarm per RFC7432bis §7.13.1 best-path rules. Collect tech-support via `show tech-support` CLI command.
Verify: Extract from tech-support archive: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` (MAC routes showing Default Gateway ext-community field), `show evpn mac-table` (gateway MAC aa:bb:cc:00:00:01 for subnet), `show alarm` or `show log` (MAC conflict alarm for subnet 10.1.1.0/24), running-config IRB section (gateway MAC config), `show forwarding-table vrf` (FIB state for EVI).
e220d60e19ee29ba.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains: BGP EVPN route-type 2 output with Default Gateway ext-community visible on DUT route, alarm log entry indicating MAC address conflict detected on IRB subnet 10.1.1.0/24 MAC aa:bb:cc:00:00:01, IRB interface running-config showing gateway MAC assignment, and FIB/forwarding tables for the EVI.
Fail-on: Missing any of: BGP route details with ext-community attributes, MAC conflict alarm/syslog entry referencing the IRB subnet and conflicting MAC, IRB interface configuration, or forwarding-table output for the EVI.

e30f73a1de1ddd86.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with EVPN service-type vlan-based, different VIDs per PE (PE1 VLAN 100, PE2 VLAN 200), auto-discovery enabled, matching import-rt/export-rt; IXIA scale rig generates bi-directional tagged traffic at line rate (10 Gbps, 64-byte frames).
Action: Inject IXIA traffic requiring VID translation on disposition PE; measure throughput, latency (min/avg/max/99th percentile), and frame loss over 300-second test window.
Verify: Check `show l2-services evpn statistics` for throughput counters, IXIA latency histogram, verify all BGP EVPN routes show Ethernet Tag ID = 0 via `show bgp evpn routes`.
e30f73a1de1ddd86.expectation:
Pass:    Throughput ≥ 9.8 Gbps (line rate - 2% overhead), avg latency ≤ 100 µs, 99th percentile latency ≤ 500 µs, frame loss ≤ 0.01%, all EVPN routes Ethernet Tag ID = 0
Fail-on: Throughput < 9.8 Gbps, avg latency > 100 µs, 99th percentile > 500 µs, frame loss > 0.01%, or any EVPN route Ethernet Tag ID ≠ 0

e31230e7fceaf5da.action_steps:
Problem: validate that BUM frames arriving on a vlan-aware-bundle access port with a VLAN-ID outside the configured bundle are dropped at ingress and never learned or advertised.
Method: configure a vlan-aware-bundle EVI for VLANs 10-12 on PE1, inject BUM traffic on both an in-bundle VLAN (10) and an out-of-bundle VLAN (99) from IXIA, and verify via MAC table and PE2 egress capture that the out-of-bundle frame is silently dropped.
Setup: Two-PE topology over MPLS with BGP EVPN session established. On PE1, configure `evpn evpn100 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` and bind VLANs 10-12 to the bundle; bind access port x-eth 0/0/1 to evpn100; commit.
Action: From IXIA connected to PE1 x-eth 0/0/1, send broadcast frames: (1) VLAN 10 tagged with source MAC aa:aa:aa:aa:aa:01, (2) VLAN 99 tagged with source MAC bb:bb:bb:bb:bb:01. Each frame 100 packets at 10 Mbps.
Verify: On PE1, `show evpn mac address-table` confirms MAC aa:aa:aa:aa:aa:01 learned in VLAN 10; MAC bb:bb:bb:bb:bb:01 does NOT appear. On PE2, `show evpn mac address-table` shows the VLAN 10 MAC remotely learned; VLAN 99 MAC absent. Capture on PE2 access port: VLAN 10 broadcast arrives; VLAN 99 does not.
e31230e7fceaf5da.expectation:
Pass:    BUM frames with VLAN-ID in the configured bundle (10-12) are forwarded, learned, and advertised; frames with VLAN-ID outside the bundle (99) are dropped at PE1 ingress with zero MAC table entries and zero EVPN advertisements.
Fail-on: MAC bb:bb:bb:bb:bb:01 (VLAN 99) appears in PE1 or PE2 MAC tables, or PE2 receives VLAN 99 broadcast traffic, or EVPN routes are advertised for the out-of-bundle VLAN.

e32b9dfedd34b6e1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 on PE1 and PE2 with identical identifier 0 <type0-value> and load-balancing-mode all-active. Attach both to CE via LACP bundle. Establish BGP EVPN peering between PE1, PE2, and remote PE3. Advertise host MAC routes from CE.
Action: From PE3, send bidirectional traffic to CE MAC addresses. After traffic stabilizes, trigger BGP session reset on PE1 (session shutdown or clear bgp). Continue traffic generation throughout the convergence event.
Verify: Pre-event: show bgp l2vpn evpn on PE3 confirms Type-1 routes from both PE1 and PE2. Post-event: verify PE3 forwarding switches to PE2 as next-hop for CE MACs. Measure packet loss and recovery time.
e32b9dfedd34b6e1.equipment: Two DUT PEs (ES members) + remote PE + IXIA traffic gen + LACP partner
e32b9dfedd34b6e1.expectation:
Pass:    PE3 maintains CE reachability via PE2 during PE1 BGP event (RFC7432bis §8.4 Aliasing). Type-1 routes present from both PEs pre-event. Packet loss ≤ BGP convergence time (typically < 2 sec).
Fail-on: PE3 loses all CE reachability when PE1 fails (aliasing not functioning), or Type-1 routes never advertised from ES members, or traffic never recovers via PE2.

e3448c11134a2a3a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance (evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based), Ethernet Segment with ESI and All-Active redundancy, auto-discovery enabled with import-rt/export-rt. Configure neighbor PE as BGP peer with af-l2vpn evpn enabled. Enable BGP UPDATE capture on peering session.
Action: Activate EVPN instance to trigger Ethernet Auto-discovery (route-type 1) advertisement. Capture BGP UPDATE messages containing Auto-discovery routes. Decode ESI Label Extended Community from wire format.
Verify: show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 detail on neighbor PE confirms receipt. Decode captured Extended Community: Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01, Flags octet (bits 6-7 RED=00 for All-Active), Reserved fields=0, ESI Label in 3-octet field (high-order 20 bits contain label value). Confirm ESI Label value is non-zero and matches DUT allocation.
e3448c11134a2a3a.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Label Extended Community present in Ethernet Auto-discovery routes with RFC7432bis §7.5 encoding: Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01, total 8 octets, Flags.RED=00 (All-Active), Reserved=0, ESI Label field = 3 octets (high-order 20 bits), non-zero label value.
Fail-on: Extended Community absent, Type≠0x06 or Sub-Type≠0x01, encoding≠8 octets, ESI Label not in 3-octet field or high-order 20 bits unused, RED bits≠00 for All-Active mode, Reserved fields non-zero, or ESI Label=0 when split-horizon required.

e391135574bfa04f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (service-type vlan-based), enable auto-discovery with import-rt and export-rt matching neighbor PE config, establish BGP af-l2vpn evpn session to PE. Attach IXIA port to access interface; send frames to learn 3 local MACs on DUT.
Action: Capture `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on DUT and neighbor PE showing Type-2 MAC/IP Advertisement routes. Apply `no advertise-mac` under EVPN instance config and commit. Wait 60s for BGP convergence. Capture same show commands.
Verify: Compare before/after outputs: neighbor PE should withdraw all Type-2 routes learned from DUT after `no advertise-mac`.
e391135574bfa04f.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor PE's BGP table contains DUT's 3 MAC routes before toggle; 0 routes (or only PE's own) after `no advertise-mac` + convergence.
Fail-on: MAC routes remain on neighbor PE after `no advertise-mac`, partial withdrawal (e.g., only 1 of 3 withdrawn), or DUT's own `show` still lists routes with Next Hop = self

e3c93aa2e92642ac.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs with EVPN instance, each with agg-eth multi-homed to the same CE using identical ESI (ethernet-segment / identifier 1 / load-balancing-mode all-active). IXIA emulates Host-A behind PE1 and Host-B behind PE2, traffic flowing between them.
Action: During active traffic, trigger BGP convergence on PE1 (clear bgp neighbor or flap BGP session). While BGP is reconverging, move Host-A's MAC from PE1 to PE2 (IXIA re-announces ARP from PE2's segment). Simultaneously monitor BGP EVPN type-2 route updates and MAC table updates on both PEs.
Verify: show evpn mac-table and show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2 <mac> on both PEs; capture BGP updates to confirm MAC/IP route seq-number increment per RFC7432bis ch.15; measure traffic loss duration (should not exceed es-waiting-time + BGP convergence).
e3c93aa2e92642ac.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen (with MAC mobility simulation)
e3c93aa2e92642ac.expectation:
Pass:    MAC/IP route seq-number increments by ≥1 when Host-A MAC appears at PE2; both PEs converge to PE2 as the MAC owner; traffic resumes within (es-waiting-time [3s default] + BGP reconvergence [<5s]); no duplicate MAC alerts or loops.
Fail-on: MAC seq-number stays unchanged; MAC remains at PE1 or shows duplicate at both PEs; traffic black-holes >10s; BGP session fails to reconverge; MAC Mobility withdrawal not sent by PE1.

e3d1cf9170ce9bd6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an EVPN instance (`evpn evpn-test service-type vlan-based`) with VPLS auto-discovery enabled. Under the auto-discovery context, configure `export-rt 100:1` and `export-rt 200:2`. Establish BGP L2VPN EVPN peering with a neighbor PE.
Action: Commit configuration; bring up the EVPN service by attaching a VLAN/EVI. Observe BGP EVPN route advertisements to the peer.
Verify: On DUT: `show running-config l2-services` confirms `export-rt` entries; `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows advertised routes. On peer PE or via BGP packet capture: verify UPDATE messages for EVPN NLRI contain route-target extended communities 100:1 and 200:2.
e3d1cf9170ce9bd6.expectation:
Pass:    All EVPN routes advertised by the DUT carry both configured export route-targets (100:1 and 200:2) in the BGP extended community attribute, verifiable on the peer PE or in captured BGP UPDATEs.
Fail-on: Route-targets absent from BGP EVPN advertisements, incorrect RT values attached, or no EVPN routes advertised to the peer.

e3dffd0e9de2a97f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure BGP session between DUT and neighbor PE with L2VPN EVPN address family. Set maximum-prefix limit (e.g., 10 prefixes) for EVPN routes from this neighbor.
Action: Advertise L2VPN EVPN Type 2 routes from the neighbor incrementally until the count exceeds the configured maximum-prefix limit. Monitor session state via `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` and capture NOTIFICATION messages using tcpdump on port 179.
Verify: Session transitions to Idle state when the (limit + 1)th prefix is received, and the DUT sends NOTIFICATION code 6 (Cease) sub-code 1 (Maximum Number of Prefixes Reached) to the neighbor.
e3dffd0e9de2a97f.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session tears down exactly when prefix count exceeds maximum-prefix limit, with NOTIFICATION code 6 sub-code 1 sent to neighbor.
Fail-on: Session remains Established after exceeding limit, or tears down prematurely, or sends wrong/no NOTIFICATION code.

e4066828e593f048.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP AS 65001 (private AS) or with upstream routes from private AS; configure eBGP neighbor under af-l2vpn evpn; advertise EVPN MAC/IP routes.
Action: Enable private-as configuration on the neighbor; commit; send BGP UPDATE with EVPN routes whose AS_PATH includes private AS numbers (64512..65534 or 4200000000..4294967294); capture outbound BGP UPDATE messages via tcpdump or neighbor PE packet capture.
Verify: On neighbor PE, issue `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and confirm received routes show AS_PATH with all private AS numbers stripped; public AS numbers remain in correct order; packet capture of UPDATE messages confirms AS_PATH attribute excludes the documented private AS ranges.
e4066828e593f048.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + packet capture (tcpdump or IXIA port mirror)
e4066828e593f048.expectation:
Pass:    All private AS numbers (64512-65534, 4200000000-4294967294) removed from AS_PATH in outbound L2VPN EVPN updates to this neighbor; public AS numbers preserved and ordering intact; neighbor receives and installs routes successfully.
Fail-on: Private AS numbers appear in AS_PATH on neighbor PE, or public AS numbers incorrectly removed, or BGP session fails/flaps after enabling the feature.

e4075318161b55a5.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that BUM frames with VLAN IDs not belonging to the configured vlan-aware-bundle are dropped at ingress, enforcing strict VLAN admission control per the bundle configuration.
Method: Configure vlan-aware-bundle EVI for VLANs 100-103 on two PEs, inject broadcast frames on both valid VLANs (100, 102) and invalid VLANs (99, 200) via IXIA, and verify via interface counters and remote IXIA statistics that invalid-VLAN frames are dropped while valid-VLAN frames are forwarded.
Setup:  Two-PE topology with MPLS transport and BGP EVPN session established. On both PE1 and PE2: `configure`, `l2-services`, `evpn evi-bundle service-type vlan-aware-bundle`, bind VLANs 100-103 to access interface `x-eth 0/0/1`, `commit`. IXIA ports connected to PE1 and PE2 access interfaces.
Action: From IXIA connected to PE1 `x-eth 0/0/1`, send 1000 broadcast frames each (destination MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) on VLAN 100, VLAN 102, VLAN 99, and VLAN 200 at 10 Mbps rate. Run for 10 seconds while monitoring interface counters and remote IXIA receive statistics.
Verify: On PE1: `show interface x-eth 0/0/1` and check ingress error/drop counters (should reflect invalid-VLAN frames). On PE2-side IXIA: verify receive statistics show ~1000 frames on VLAN 100 and ~1000 on VLAN 102, but exactly zero frames on VLAN 99 and zero on VLAN 200. Run `show evpn evi evi-bundle` on both PEs to confirm only VLANs 100-103 are listed as active in the bundle.
e4075318161b55a5.expectation:
Pass:    IXIA at PE2 receives broadcast frames only on VLANs 100 and 102 (the configured bundle range); zero frames received on VLAN 99 or VLAN 200. PE1 ingress interface counters increment for dropped frames (VLANs 99, 200). Valid-VLAN forwarding (100, 102) is unaffected.
Fail-on: IXIA at PE2 receives any frames on VLAN 99 or VLAN 200, or valid-VLAN frames (100, 102) are incorrectly dropped at PE1 ingress.

e42e713896da5a53.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT PE1 with EVPN EVI 100 (evpn evi-100 service-type vlan-based), L2 MTU 9100, control-word enabled, multi-homed ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11. Establish iBGP EVPN session with neighbor PE2 (af-l2vpn evpn), configure auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt 65000:100.
Action: Activate EVI 100 to trigger Route Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) and Type 3 (Inclusive Multicast) advertisements. Capture BGP UPDATEs: tcpdump -i <pe1-pe2-link> port 179 -w /tmp/l2attr.pcap. Decode with tshark -r /tmp/l2attr.pcap -Y 'bgp.evpn.nlri' -V, filter for L2-Attr Extended Community (Type 0x06, Sub-type 0x04).
Verify: Type 3 NLRI L2-Attr EC: L2 MTU field = 0x2384 (9100 bytes), Control Flags C bit (control-word) = 1, F bit (flow-label) per config, P/B bits = 0. Type 1 NLRI L2-Attr EC: MTU field = 0x0000 (MBZ per §7.11.1), C/F bits = 0, P/B bits reflect ESI role (primary/backup).
e42e713896da5a53.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (iBGP EVPN session; packet capture and tshark/Wireshark L2-Attr EC validation)
e42e713896da5a53.expectation:
Pass:    Type 3 L2-Attr EC carries MTU 9100 + C/F bits, P/B = 0. Type 1 L2-Attr EC carries P/B bits, MTU/C/F = 0. Partitioning per RFC7432bis §7.11.1.
Fail-on: Type 1 has MTU ≠ 0 or C/F ≠ 0. Type 3 has P/B ≠ 0. L2-Attr EC (0x06/0x04) missing on either route type.

e431bff39592adc8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (e.g., `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`) with `auto-discovery` enabled; establish BGP session with neighbor PE in EVPN address-family.
Action: Under `configuration / l2-services / vpls / auto-discovery`, issue `export-rt 100:200`, then `commit`. Trigger route advertisement (add a MAC entry or bring up an attachment circuit).
Verify: Run `show running-config | begin l2-services` to confirm `export-rt 100:200` appears in the auto-discovery block. On the neighbor PE, run `show bgp l2vpn evpn routes` and inspect extended communities for RT:100:200 in received routes.
e431bff39592adc8.expectation:
Pass:    `export-rt 100:200` persists in running-config after commit; neighbor PE's BGP table shows EVPN routes (type 1, 2, or 3) with extended community target 100:200.
Fail-on: Configured route-target does not appear in running-config after commit, or neighbor PE receives EVPN routes without the RT:100:200 extended community, or receives routes with a different RT value.

e4ba87c6ad8d6dad.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT and neighbor PE both connected to IXIA ports emulating a multihomed CE. Configure `agg-eth 0` on DUT with basic ethernet-segment (identifier, export-rt) but no explicit `load-balancing-mode` (defaults to single-active). Bring up EVPN BGP peering between DUT and neighbor PE.
Action: Enter `configure / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` and issue `load-balancing-mode all-active`. Commit. Send bidirectional L2 traffic from IXIA through the multihomed link. Verify both PEs forward traffic. Then issue `no load-balancing-mode`, commit, and verify single-active behavior (only one PE forwards; other blocks).
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0 ethernet-segment` displays `load-balancing-mode all-active` (or absent after `no`). `show evpn ethernet-segment` reflects the configured mode. IXIA Rx counters confirm traffic distribution: all-active → both PEs increment Tx; single-active → only one PE increments Tx.
e4ba87c6ad8d6dad.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA (emulating multihomed CE with LAG or active-active attachment)
e4ba87c6ad8d6dad.expectation:
Pass:    `load-balancing-mode all-active` accepted, persists in running-config, IXIA receives traffic from both PEs (per-flow hash distribution). `no load-balancing-mode` restores default single-active; only one PE forwards (other remains designated-forwarder standby).
Fail-on: Commit error on `load-balancing-mode` command. Config not persisted across `show running-config`. All-active configured but only one PE forwards (behavior stuck in single-active). `no` form fails to restore single-active default.

e4cc67d3ba8b80dd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (configuration/l2-services/evpn) with RD, RT, control-word (default|enable|disable), advertise-mac (default enable), unknown-mac-flooding (default enable); establish BGP EVPN peering using af-l2vpn evpn with neighbor PE; confirm Type-3 route advertised per broadcast domain.
Action: After EVPN instance active and Type-3 routes exchanged, collect tech-support including: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3 detail` (PMSI + L2-Attr), `show evpn instance`, running-config (configuration/l2-services/evpn, configuration/routing/bgp), BGP session and EVPN-instance syslog entries.
Verify: Parse tech-support for Type-3 route mandatory fields: RD (key), ET-ID (key), Originating-Router-IP (key), PMSI-Tunnel-Attribute {Flag=0, Type=6 (Ingress-Replication), MPLS-Label, Tunnel-ID=Originating-Router-IP}, L2-Attr-Extended-Community {L2-MTU, Flow-Label-flag, Control-Word-flag (must match configured control-word setting)}, RTs, Next-Hop=advertising-PE-IP.
e4cc67d3ba8b80dd.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains complete Type-3 route: PMSI attr with Flag=0 Type=6 MPLS-Label Tunnel-ID=Originating-Router-IP, L2-Attr with L2-MTU Flow-Label-flag Control-Word-flag consistent with config (default|enable|disable), Next-Hop=PE-IP, all key fields (RD, ET-ID, Originating-Router-IP) present.
Fail-on: Type-3 route absent, PMSI attribute Type≠6 or Flag≠0 or Tunnel-ID≠Originating-Router-IP, L2-Attr Control-Word-flag inconsistent with configured control-word value, or Next-Hop≠advertising-PE-IP.

e4f302e6f4639c5d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE with shared 9-octet ESI (interface agg-eth / ethernet-segment / identifier 0 xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx / load-balancing-mode all-active); remote PE imports type-4 Ethernet A-D per ES routes from both PEs (ESI Label extended community redundancy mode = 0x00); IXIA sends bidirectional traffic from remote PE toward MAC addresses behind the ES.
Action: Shutdown DUT's LAG interface (interface agg-eth X / shutdown), wait for BGP route withdrawal and remote PE convergence, then re-enable (no shutdown).
Verify: On remote PE: show evpn ethernet-segment <esi> (confirms All-Active mode deduced from surviving PE's type-4 route), show bgp evpn routes type 4 <esi> (redundancy mode = 0x00 in ESI Label extended community), show l2-services mac-table <evi> (MACs retain reachability via neighbor PE during outage, rebuild multiple next-hops from type-2 routes after recovery); IXIA reports traffic loss duration.
e4f302e6f4639c5d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (shared All-Active ES) + remote PE (route reflector or direct BGP peer) + IXIA traffic gen
e4f302e6f4639c5d.expectation:
Pass:    Remote PE deduces All-Active mode throughout (redundancy mode = 0x00 in ESI Label extended community from surviving PE); MACs remain reachable via neighbor PE during DUT outage; after DUT recovery, remote PE constructs multiple next-hops from type-2 MAC/IP Advertisement routes; traffic loss ≤ es-waiting-time default (3 seconds per RFC7432bis §8 mass-withdrawal).
Fail-on: Traffic blackhole > 3 seconds, remote PE incorrectly interprets redundancy mode or switches to Single-Active, stale next-hops to failed DUT persist beyond 3-second convergence window, or MAC forwarding fails during single-PE operation.

e52e972c0e8c6ee4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT in EVPN topology with EVI 100; establish BGP EVPN peer to IXIA; IXIA advertises valid Type 3 (Inclusive Multicast) route for EVI 100 with PMSI Tunnel Attribute (Ingress Replication, Originating Router IP = 192.0.2.1). Prepare IXIA fault-injection script to send malformed Type 3 variants: (a) invalid PMSI Tunnel Type = 0xFF, (b) truncated PMSI Tunnel Attribute (length mismatch), (c) reserved Flags bits set in route NLRI, (d) corrupted Originating Router IP encoding.
Action: Inject each malformed Type 3 variant from IXIA while valid Type 3 remains advertised; send 10-second intervals between variants.
Verify: CLI `show router bgp routes evpn incl-mcast` confirms only valid Type 3 route in RIB (Originating Router 192.0.2.1 selected); `show router bgp neighbor <IXIA-peer> statistics` shows Update Error counter increments for each malformed variant; `show router bgp error-log` or syslog captures BGP NOTIFICATION (Update Message Error, Malformed Attribute) for PMSI or NLRI errors; confirm no crash via `show system uptime`.
e52e972c0e8c6ee4.expectation:
Pass:    Only valid Type 3 route (192.0.2.1) present in `show router bgp routes evpn incl-mcast`; malformed variants rejected at BGP UPDATE validation; "Update Message Error" counter >= 4 in neighbor statistics (one per variant); syslog entries present for each NOTIFICATION sent (subcode 3 = Malformed Attribute or subcode 10 = Invalid NLRI); DUT uptime unchanged (no crash/restart).
Fail-on: Malformed Type 3 route accepted into RIB; best-path selection chooses malformed route over valid route; no BGP error counter increment; missing syslog NOTIFICATION event; DUT crash/reboot; multicast forwarding state corrupted (e.g., `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment` shows stale ESI).

e55b325c5126e012.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology with EVPN instance active; PE1 configured with `agg-eth 0`, `ethernet-segment`, `identifier 1` (non-reserved type 1 ESI); PE2 is the receiving DUT not locally attached to the same ESI; IXIA connected to both PEs.
Action: Trigger MAC learning on PE1 by sending IXIA frame to PE1's `agg-eth 0`; control BGP route advertisement from PE1 such that the MAC/IP Advertisement route (type 2) with the type 1 ESI arrives at PE2 first, followed by the corresponding Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) after a 10+ second delay; send test traffic from PE2-side IXIA toward the learned MAC address during the delay window and after both routes are received.
Verify: On PE2 execute `show evpn route type 2`, `show evpn route type 1`, `show l2-services mac-table` to observe route arrival timing and MAC table installation; verify traffic is not correctly forwarded to PE1 during delay, but is forwarded after both routes present.
e55b325c5126e012.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 does not install forwarding state for the MAC address until BOTH the MAC/IP Advertisement route (type 2) AND the associated Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) are received per RFC7432bis §9.2.2; traffic to the MAC is correctly forwarded only after both routes are present.
Fail-on: PE2 installs forwarding state and correctly forwards traffic to the MAC address after receiving only the MAC/IP Advertisement route (type 2), before receiving the associated Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1).

e57f1987b804d364.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT and 2 neighbor PEs with ingress-replication mode (per RFC7432bis §11); verify `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` (defaults) active in `configuration / l2-services / evpn`; confirm IMET route type 3 exchange from all 3 PEs (`show router bgp routes evpn imet`).
Action: From IXIA connected to DUT's access port, inject broadcast frame with DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF (e.g., ARP request in VLAN 100); simultaneously capture VXLAN-encapsulated packets on DUT→PE1 and DUT→PE2 core links.
Verify: On DUT, execute `show l2-services evpn instance <name> statistics` and confirm flooded-frame-tx counter increments by 1; packet captures must show exactly one VXLAN packet sent to PE1's VTEP IP and one to PE2's VTEP IP (inner DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF); on PE1 and PE2, flooded-frame-rx counter increments by 1 each.
e57f1987b804d364.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs + IXIA (traffic gen + capture) — plural PEs required to validate 'all remote PEs' clause
e57f1987b804d364.expectation:
Pass:    DUT replicates the broadcast frame to all 2 remote PE VTEPs (RFC7432bis §16.1 MUST: 'sent to all remote PEs'); flooded-frame-tx == 1; each neighbor PE's flooded-frame-rx == 1; captures show one packet per VTEP with no duplication.
Fail-on: Broadcast packet missing from one or more remote PE captures (violates 'all remote PEs' MUST), duplicate VXLAN packets to same VTEP, or zero replication despite IMET routes present.

e5bab6ffccf026b6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT PE1 and neighbor PE2 with identical ESI (type 0: 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88) on agg-eth interfaces facing CE; set load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving highest-random-weight, es-waiting-time 5; create EVPN instance with 20 VLANs (100–119); establish BGP EVPN peering; advertise 100 MAC/IP routes per VLAN.
Action: Generate 1 Gbps bidirectional L2 traffic from IXIA through CE (LACP to both PEs); execute 'shutdown' on DUT PE1's agg-eth interface; DUT PE1 triggers Fast Convergence via Ethernet A-D per ES route with withdraw flag per RFC7432bis §8.2.
Verify: 'show evpn ethernet-segment' on DUT shows ESI down; 'show bgp evpn routes type 4' on PE2 confirms Ethernet A-D withdraw received; IXIA measures traffic loss duration; 'show evpn mac-table' on PE2 shows zero MAC entries via PE1; 'show evpn service-carving' confirms all 20 VLANs now active on PE2 only.
e5bab6ffccf026b6.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (EVPN multi-homing capable, same ESI config) + IXIA traffic gen + CE device (LACP active-active to both PEs)
e5bab6ffccf026b6.expectation:
Pass:    DUT sends Ethernet A-D per ES withdraw within es-waiting-time (5s); PE2 receives withdraw and flushes PE1 MAC routes; traffic loss ≤ 1s; service-carving reassigns all 20 VLANs to PE2; zero stale MAC routes after 10s; 'show evpn ethernet-segment' reflects ESI operational state down.
Fail-on: No Ethernet A-D per ES withdraw sent; traffic blackhole > 1s; stale MAC routes pointing to PE1 persist beyond 10s; service-carving fails (VLANs inactive or split across both PEs post-failure); convergence time > es-waiting-time + 2s (7s total).

e5cf58783722877d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE both connected to same IXIA port via LACP. On each, create agg-eth with `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 01.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88; service-carving highest-preference preference 100` (DUT) and `preference 50` (neighbor PE). Configure `es-waiting-time 3` globally. Establish BGP EVPN to common RR; create EVI with ES attached.
Action: Start 1Gbps IXIA traffic. Execute `clear bgp evpn neighbor <RR-IP>` on DUT to reset BGP session. Capture Type-4 ES route updates for 30s. Poll `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI>` every 1s on both PEs.
Verify: Parse captured Type-4 routes for DF Preference extended community (RFC7432bis §8.6): DUT advertises pref=100, neighbor PE pref=50. Post-reconvergence (es-waiting-time + BGP convergence), `show evpn ethernet-segment` on DUT displays `DF: Yes` and neighbor PE `DF: No`. IXIA reports <100ms loss.
e5cf58783722877d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA (as LACP-bonded CE to both PEs) + BGP route reflector
e5cf58783722877d.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs' Type-4 routes contain DF Preference extended community (DUT=100, neighbor=50); DUT elected as DF; DF role stable (no flap) during BGP session reset; IXIA traffic loss <100ms; backup PE (neighbor) also advertises its preference.
Fail-on: Missing DF Preference community in Type-4 routes from either PE, DF role flap during convergence, traffic loss >100ms, or neighbor PE does not advertise Type-4 route (backup not signaled).

e601776f076d89d6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS or EVPN service instance (e.g., `evpn evpn-name service-type vlan-based` in configuration / l2-services). Configure mac-limit to a specific threshold (e.g., 5 MAC addresses) within the service configuration mode. Attach at least one physical interface to the service.
Action: Generate Layer-2 traffic from more unique source MAC addresses than the configured limit (e.g., 8 distinct source MACs) into the attached interface. Allow the DUT to process the traffic and learn MAC addresses.
Verify: Inspect the service's learned MAC address table to count entries. Execute `show running-config` to confirm the mac-limit value is present in the service configuration. Review syslog output for MAC limit threshold notifications.
e601776f076d89d6.expectation:
Pass:    The service learns exactly the configured limit (5 MAC addresses); the 3 additional source MACs beyond the limit are not learned or are aged out; syslog contains an event indicating the MAC learning limit was reached.
Fail-on: More than 5 MAC addresses appear in the service's MAC table, or learning stops prematurely (< 5 entries), or the mac-limit configuration is rejected during `commit`, or system instability (crash, interface flap) occurs under limit stress.

e60de61fd7a8a7a7.action_steps:
Setup:  Create VPLS instance vpls100 (configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls100); configure route-distinguisher 65000:100; enable auto-discovery; establish iBGP session to neighbor PE.
Action: Enter configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls100 / auto-discovery. Issue `export-rt 65000:100`, commit. Capture BGP UPDATE on neighbor. Add `export-rt 65000:200`, commit. Remove first RT: `no export-rt 65000:100`, commit.
Verify: After each commit, execute `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls100 auto-discovery`; verify export-rt lines present. Execute `show bgp l2vpn vpls vpls100` to confirm advertised routes. Capture BGP UPDATEs on neighbor PE shows RT extended community values 65000:100 and 65000:200, then only 65000:200 after removal.
e60de61fd7a8a7a7.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (iBGP session for extended community capture)
e60de61fd7a8a7a7.expectation:
Pass:    `export-rt 65000:100` and `export-rt 65000:200` both accepted by commit; running-config shows both lines under auto-discovery; `show bgp l2vpn vpls vpls100` lists routes with both RTs; BGP capture confirms extended community attribute carries both 65000:100 and 65000:200; `no export-rt 65000:100` removes only that line; subsequent BGP UPDATE advertises routes with only 65000:200.
Fail-on: Commit fails for valid route-target syntax (ASN:nn format); export-rt missing from running-config after commit; BGP does not advertise routes with configured RT values; wrong RT removed by `no` command; BGP continues advertising removed RT; cannot configure multiple export-rt values.

e68beecffec3a692.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE multi-homed to same CE via agg-eth; configure ethernet-segment with identical ESI (type 0), load-balancing-mode all-active on both PEs; establish P2MP MPLS LSP for BUM; BGP EVPN session up.
Action: DUT advertises Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 1) with upstream-assigned ESI label; inject unknown-unicast BUM frame from CE; observe BUM replication over P2MP LSP with ESI label; verify 3rd-party PE receives MPLS packet with DUT's ESI label.
Verify: On DUT: `show evpn ethernet-segment` (ESI label present), `show bgp evpn route-type 1` (A-D per ES route with ESI label). On 3rd-party PE: confirm ESI label programmed in MPLS table for DUT's context, capture BUM packet egress interfaces.
e68beecffec3a692.expectation:
Pass:    3rd-party PE programs DUT's upstream-assigned ESI label in DUT's context label space; BUM packet received with that label does NOT egress the agg-eth interface to CE (split-horizon enforced per §8.3.1.2).
Fail-on: ESI label not in 3rd-party MPLS table, OR BUM packet with ESI label forwarded onto the shared Ethernet segment (causes duplicate delivery or loop).

e69e49fa845c886a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance evpn-1 with bridge domain BD-100; configure agg-eth 1 with ethernet-segment identifier type 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09, load-balancing-mode all-active. Establish iBGP EVPN session with IXIA acting as remote PE at 10.0.0.2.
Action: IXIA sends BGP UPDATE messages with MAC/IP Advertisement routes (RT-2) containing ESI field violations: (a) ESI type = 0x99 (undefined type per RFC7432bis), (b) ESI field truncated to 6 octets (should be 10), (c) ESI type 0 with only 5-octet value instead of required 9, (d) UPDATE with ESI path attribute length field = 15 octets (should be 10). Each UPDATE includes otherwise valid MAC 00:00:01:01:01:01, IP 192.0.2.10, RD 10.0.0.2:100, RT 65000:100, label 10000.
Verify: `show evpn mac-address bd BD-100` confirms no MAC entries from IXIA. `show bgp evpn summary` shows neighbor 10.0.0.2 with UPDATE error counter ≥ 4. `show logging | include ESI|UPDATE|malformed` shows 4 distinct error events. `show bgp evpn` confirms BGP session still established (no tear-down).
e69e49fa845c886a.expectation:
Pass:    Zero MAC entries installed from 4 malformed UPDATEs; BGP UPDATE error counter shows 4+ errors; 4 syslog entries documenting ESI field violations; BGP session remains up.
Fail-on: DUT crash/reload; MAC 00:00:01:01:01:01 present in evpn mac-address table; BGP session torn down; UPDATE error counter = 0; no syslog entries.

e6c287be57869af3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 2 PE routers with identical ESI on agg-eth interfaces (ethernet-segment; identifier 0 <same-9-octet-hex>), service-carving highest-random-weight, load-balancing-mode single-active. Establish MP-BGP EVPN sessions between both PEs and a route reflector. Attach IXIA as CE to both PEs' LAG interfaces.
Action: Enable EVPN instance, bring up agg-eth on both PEs, wait for DF election to complete (≤ es-waiting-time 3 sec). Capture BGP Type 4 (Ethernet Segment) routes exchanged between PEs.
Verify: On each PE run 'show evpn ethernet-segment' and 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4' to confirm primary DF elected on one PE, backup DF role on the other, and that BGP Type 4 routes carry DF Election Extended Community (0x06) with both primary and backup PE identifiers.
e6c287be57869af3.equipment: Two routers + IXIA (both PEs with ES multi-homing config, IXIA as dual-homed CE; route reflector for BGP EVPN exchange)
e6c287be57869af3.expectation:
Pass:    BGP Type 4 ES routes advertised by both PEs contain DF Election Extended Community encoding primary DF (PE with winning service-carving value) and backup DF (other PE). 'show evpn ethernet-segment' on each PE displays local DF role and peer backup DF IP. DF election converges within 3 seconds (es-waiting-time default).
Fail-on: Type 4 routes lack DF Election Extended Community, only primary DF present in signaling (no backup), DF role unknown after es-waiting-time expires, or 'show' commands report 'DF election incomplete'.

e6db18b674dc78f2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance with BD 100, `unknown-mac-flooding enable` in `configuration/l2-services/evpn`, P2MP MPLS LSP (ID 1000) as PMSI Tunnel for BUM traffic via Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag route. Configure IXIA to encapsulate Ethernet frames in MPLS with P2MP LSP label stack targeting BD 100.
Action: Inject four malformed variants: (1) PMSI Tunnel attribute TLV with length field 0xFF (exceeds RFC7432bis §12.2 max), (2) P2MP LSP label with MPLS reserved bits 4–7 set, (3) MPLS label stack with TTL=0, (4) valid P2MP label but inner Ethernet frame carrying unicast MAC with corrupted EtherType 0xFFFF.
Verify: `show mpls counters` for rx-label-errors, `show l2-services evpn statistics bd 100` for unknown-unicast-drops, syslog for "PMSI tunnel attribute invalid" or "MPLS label error", `show l2-services mac-table bd 100` to confirm zero MAC learning from malformed packets.
e6db18b674dc78f2.expectation:
Pass:    All four malformed variants dropped before MAC learning; `show mpls counters` rx-label-errors ≥ 4; syslog contains ≥ 1 PMSI/MPLS error entry; `show l2-services mac-table bd 100` count unchanged from baseline (no pollution from bad packets).
Fail-on: System crash/reload, MAC table in BD 100 shows entries learned from malformed source MACs, MPLS error counter remains 0, syslog silent, or IXIA capture on remote PE port shows forwarded malformed packet (violates RFC7432bis §12.2 treatment requirement).

e6fd58a02a8781dc.action_steps:
Setup:  On DUT and neighbor PE, configure agg-eth 0 (to same CE) with ethernet-segment, identical ESI (identifier 0 <type0-value>), load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving highest-random-weight; verify both PEs advertise ES Route with DF Election Extended Community; establish IXIA traffic to CE.
Action: On DUT, shutdown then no shutdown agg-eth 0 while traffic flows.
Verify: show ethernet-segment displays DF role re-converged within es-waiting-time (default 3 seconds); IXIA traffic resumes with packet loss bounded per RFC7432bis ch.8.5; no stale FIB entries.
e6fd58a02a8781dc.expectation:
Pass:    DF re-election completes within es-waiting-time (3s default); traffic loss ≤ convergence bound (RFC7432bis ch.8.5); forwarding state updated with new DF assignment.
Fail-on: DF role stuck in transition after es-waiting-time expires; packet loss exceeds convergence bound; FIB retains stale pre-flap next-hop.

e74761bbde4687b7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with shared ES to CE1 using `identifier 0 <same-type0-value>` and `load-balancing-mode all-active`; configure DUT (PE3) as remote PE; CE1 advertises MAC M1.
Action: From PE3, capture received Ethernet A-D per ES routes from PE1 and PE2 (verify ESI Label extended community redundancy mode field = 0); capture MAC/IP Advertisement routes for M1 from both PEs (note MPLS labels L1, L2); send unicast traffic from PE3 destined to M1.
Verify: At PE3, decode BGP EVPN Type-1 routes and confirm ESI Label ext-community byte offset 7 = 0x00 (All-Active); check forwarding table shows M1 with ECMP next-hops to PE1 and PE2; MPLS encapsulation uses label L1 for PE1 path, L2 for PE2 path; traffic from PE3 load-balances across both PEs (measure per-flow distribution).
e74761bbde4687b7.equipment: DUT (remote PE) + two neighbor PEs (multihomed to same ES, All-Active) + IXIA for CE emulation and traffic generation
e74761bbde4687b7.expectation:
Pass:    PE3 deduces All-Active mode (ESI Label ext-community = 0 on all Type-1 routes); M1's next-hop set includes both PE1 and PE2 with distinct MPLS labels from MAC/IP routes; traffic exhibits ECMP (both paths carry flows).
Fail-on: PE3 selects only one PE (Single-Active behavior); M1 reachable via single next-hop only; traffic forwarded to one PE despite multiple advertisements; incorrect MPLS label stack (not matching MAC/IP route labels).

e773b56b6754c3aa.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS instance `vpls-test-1` with `auto-discovery` enabled; add `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface ethernet 1/1` under VPLS context; commit.
Action: Execute `no mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55` in `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-test-1` mode; commit; wait 5 seconds for FDB convergence.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-test-1` contains no `mac-address-static` line for 00:11:22:33:44:55; `show vpls mac-table vpls-test-1` shows no static entry for that MAC; `show l2fib` (kernel FDB) has no residual static binding.
e773b56b6754c3aa.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
e773b56b6754c3aa.expectation:
Pass:    MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 absent from running-config, VPLS MAC table, and kernel L2FIB; VPLS instance operational; traffic to that MAC now triggers dynamic learning instead of static forwarding.
Fail-on: `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55` still in running-config after commit; MAC entry still marked 'static' in `show vpls mac-table`; kernel L2FIB retains stale static entry; or VPLS instance transitions to down state.

e789cc4ed9fd3a29.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs sharing ES on `agg-eth 0` with LACP and ESI configured; ingress-replication tunnel established between PEs. IXIA positioned to inject crafted BGP UPDATEs (for ESI Label Extended Community signaling) and BUM traffic.
Action: Inject EVPN Type-2 routes with malformed ESI Label Extended Community: (1) invalid Type byte, (2) Length > 8 octets, (3) ESI Label = 0, (4) truncated field. Send BUM from CE through PE1; verify PE2 consults BUM Split Horizon Table and blocks forwarding to shared ES per RFC7432bis §8.3 despite malformed signaling.
Verify: Malformed UPDATEs rejected; error counters visible in `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbor` output increment by 4. PE2's BUM Split Horizon Table remains consistent (no malformed entries); `show l2-services evpn` shows valid state. Split-horizon enforcement active per §8.3: `tcpdump agg-eth 0` on PE2 shows zero BUM packets forwarded to CE; `show log` contains four error entries.
e789cc4ed9fd3a29.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
e789cc4ed9fd3a29.expectation:
Pass:    All malformed ESI Label UPDATEs rejected; error counter +4; BUM Split Horizon Table stable; split-horizon active per §8.3; four syslog entries; no crash.
Fail-on: Malformed ESI Label accepted into split-horizon table, BUM loopback to CE on shared ES, PE crash, or missing syslog entries.

e7ae7b756b00f958.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure an ethernet-segment with ESI type 0 on `agg-eth 0`; attach the interface to an EVPN instance.
Action: Navigate to ethernet-segment configuration mode; enter `service-carving` command; commit configuration. Then issue `no service-carving`; commit again.
Verify: After first commit, `show running-config` contains `service-carving` within the ethernet-segment configuration block. After `no service-carving`, the line is removed from running-config.
e7ae7b756b00f958.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
e7ae7b756b00f958.expectation:
Pass:    CLI accepts `service-carving` command; running-config includes it post-commit; `no service-carving` removes it and restores implicit default DF election behavior
Fail-on: Syntax error, config line not stored in running-config, `no` form ineffective, or command appears in wrong configuration context

e7ae8c2465ceeae5.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT running current image with `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle` on x-eth 0/0/1 for VLAN-IDs 1-3; IXIA sends BUM (broadcast) frame tagged VLAN 2 (baseline: forwarded) and VLAN 10 (baseline: dropped, not in bundle); upgrade image staged on ONIE server.
Action: Execute `onie-install <upgrade-image-url>`. Post-reboot, run `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn1` to confirm service-type vlan-aware-bundle with VLAN-IDs 1-3 intact. IXIA re-transmits BUM frames VLAN 2 and VLAN 10.
Verify: `show l2-services evpn evpn1` displays service-type vlan-aware-bundle, VLAN-IDs 1-3; `show interface x-eth 0/0/1 statistics` or IXIA Rx counters confirm VLAN 2 BUM forwarded (Rx count increments), VLAN 10 input dropped (no egress, Rx = 0 on remote IXIA port).
e7ae8c2465ceeae5.equipment: DUT + IXIA traffic gen + ONIE image server
e7ae8c2465ceeae5.expectation:
Pass:    Post-upgrade VLAN 10 BUM frame dropped (0 packets received on IXIA remote port), VLAN 2 BUM forwarded (Rx > 0); evpn evpn1 config matches pre-upgrade snapshot (service-type vlan-aware-bundle, VLAN-IDs 1-3).
Fail-on: VLAN 10 BUM frame forwarded (Rx > 0) post-upgrade, or evpn1 missing/VLAN-IDs changed, or VLAN 1-3 frames dropped, or upgrade fails to complete/boot new image.

e7f4e816c454a0a3.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure L2VPN EVPN with BGP neighbor 192.0.2.1 in VRF EVPN-VRF; under `routing bgp 65001 vrf EVPN-VRF neighbor 192.0.2.1 af-l2vpn evpn` apply: route-reflector-client, weight 200, maximum-prefix 5000 warning-only, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, allow-as-in 2. Save running-config; stage upgrade image on ONIE server.
Action: Execute `onie-install <image-url>` and reboot; wait for system ready and BGP session re-establishment.
Verify: Check `show running-config routing bgp 65001 vrf EVPN-VRF neighbor 192.0.2.1 af-l2vpn evpn` contains all 5 commands; verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn vrf EVPN-VRF neighbor 192.0.2.1` displays weight 200 and route-reflector-client status; confirm `show bgp l2vpn evpn vrf EVPN-VRF neighbor 192.0.2.1 received-routes` returns stored routes (soft-reconfig functional).
e7f4e816c454a0a3.equipment: DUT + ONIE image server + neighbor PE
e7f4e816c454a0a3.expectation:
Pass:    All 5 BGP common CLI commands (route-reflector-client, weight 200, maximum-prefix 5000 warning-only, inbound-soft-reconfiguration, allow-as-in 2) present in running-config; `show bgp l2vpn evpn vrf` confirms weight and RR-client active; BGP session UP; EVPN routes exchanged.
Fail-on: Any command missing or reverted to default; BGP session fails to re-establish; `show bgp` outputs do not reflect configured values; upgrade aborts or rolls back.

e820fed56d52818e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 in EVPN instance with shared ES (agg-eth 0 LACP mode) to CE1. Verify `show evpn ethernet-segment` reports ESI Label allocated for the shared ES. Establish ingress-replication tunnel PE1↔PE2.
Action: On PE1, inject BUM frame (broadcast dest-MAC ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff) from a non-shared port (x-eth 0/0/1). PE1 replicates to PE2 with the shared-ES ESI Label. Inject second BUM from a different ES or non-multihomed port.
Verify: On PE2, capture egress on agg-eth 0 (shared ES LAG) via tcpdump. Confirm BUM carrying the shared-ES ESI Label is NOT forwarded on agg-eth 0 (split-horizon per RFC7432bis §8.3). BUM from different ES forwards normally. Check `show bgp l2vpn evpn` for ESI Label signaling.
e820fed56d52818e.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + LACP partner
e820fed56d52818e.expectation:
Pass:    BUM with shared-ES ESI Label is dropped on PE2's agg-eth 0 egress (split-horizon enforced). BUM from different ES forwards to CE1. No loop observed.
Fail-on: BUM with shared-ES ESI Label egresses on PE2's agg-eth 0, creating a loop to CE1. Or non-shared BUM is incorrectly dropped.

e843a9214db588fd.action_steps:
Setup:
  1. DUT in `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn` mode with an EVPN-capable neighbor.
Action:
  1. Type `private-as ?` and review help text for AS_PATH private-AS handling description.
  2. Type `priv` and TAB to confirm completion to `private-as`.
  3. After `private-as `, type `?` to list available strip/replace modes or parameters.
Verify:
  1. Help describes stripping or replacing private AS numbers (64512..65534, 4200000000..4294967294) from outbound L2VPN EVPN updates.
  2. TAB completes uniquely (or lists a small candidate set including `private-as`).
  3. Next-token help shows modes/parameters for AS_PATH manipulation on outbound updates to this neighbor.
e843a9214db588fd.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
e843a9214db588fd.expectation:
Pass:    `private-as` is present at the BGP neighbor af-l2vpn evpn configuration prompt; help explicitly references AS_PATH private-AS manipulation (2-byte range 64512..65534, 4-byte range 4200000000..4294967294) for outbound updates; parameters discoverable via `?`.
Fail-on: Command not listed in `?` output, TAB fails to complete, help omits private-AS ranges or AS_PATH context, or next-token `?` shows zero parameters for strip/replace configuration.

e8640fd606b03280.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure base BGP with a neighbor address; establish BGP session with a PE neighbor that supports EVPN.
Action: Enter the neighbor configuration mode; issue `af-l2vpn evpn` to enable L2VPN EVPN address-family; commit.
Verify: `show running-config` displays `af-l2vpn evpn` under the neighbor stanza; `show bgp neighbors <neighbor-ip>` confirms L2VPN EVPN (AFI 25, SAFI 70) in both advertised and received capabilities; session state = Established.
e8640fd606b03280.expectation:
Pass:    `af-l2vpn evpn` present in running-config; BGP neighbor shows L2VPN EVPN AFI/SAFI successfully negotiated; session remains Established
Fail-on: Command rejected; L2VPN EVPN AFI/SAFI not advertised or not received (negotiation failure); BGP session goes down or transitions to non-Established state

e8c1df1d0894a940.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (PE1, PE2) share ESI `identifier 0` on `agg-eth 0` to CE with LACP. Configure `load-balancing-mode single-active`, `service-carving highest-preference preference 100` on PE1 and `preference 50` on PE2. Remote PE3 as traffic endpoint.
Action: Bring ESI up; verify PE1 elected DF. Capture Type 1 per-EVI A-D routes for primary/backup signaling per RFC7432bis §8.6; confirm PE3 learns aliasing path via both PEs. Disable PE1 `agg-eth 0`; inject IXIA bidirectional traffic CE↔PE3 during failover.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms PE1 DF pre-failover, PE2 post-failover; `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` shows `preference` values (100, 50). Type 1 routes carry primary/backup signaling. Aliasing: PE3 reaches CE via both paths pre-failure. IXIA traffic: loss ≤1s, no dual-forwarding.
e8c1df1d0894a940.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + remote PE + IXIA
e8c1df1d0894a940.expectation:
Pass:    PE1 elected DF via higher `preference`; Type 1 routes signal primary/backup per RFC7432bis §8.6; PE2 becomes DF ≤1s after PE1 down; aliasing allows PE3 to reach CE via both PEs; IXIA traffic continues seamlessly.
Fail-on: Wrong DF elected, missing primary/backup signaling in Type 1 routes, failover >1s, aliasing broken (PE3 can't reach CE via backup path), traffic black-hole, or simultaneous forwarding.

e8d9f348568049df.action_steps:
Setup:  Create EVPN service `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`; confirm interface x-eth 0/0/1 exists and is unused.
Action: Under `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1`, enter `interface x-eth 0/0/1` and commit. Then enter `no interface x-eth 0/0/1` and commit.
Verify: After add: `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn1` includes interface x-eth 0/0/1. After removal: interface absent from config.
e8d9f348568049df.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
e8d9f348568049df.expectation:
Pass:    Both `interface x-eth 0/0/1` and its `no` form complete without error; interface appears in running-config when added, removed when negated.
Fail-on: Syntax error on either command, interface missing from config post-add, or interface persists post-removal.

e8eb162442dec245.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on DUT agg-eth 0 with identifier 0 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09 and load-balancing-mode single-active (default). Configure matching ES identifier on neighbor PE's CE-facing interface. Attach CE via LAG to both PEs; configure EVPN instance with same EVI on both PEs.
Action: Administratively disable the active PE's LAG member link to force failover. Send bidirectional L2 traffic from IXIA through the fabric to the CE.
Verify: Issue show evpn ethernet-segment and show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 on both PEs. Capture forwarding path via tcpdump or IXIA flow statistics. Confirm traffic reaches CE via backup PE.
e8eb162442dec245.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs advertise Type 1 ES routes per RFC7432bis §8.4. Traffic converges to backup PE within es-waiting-time (default 3 seconds). Zero packet loss beyond the convergence window.
Fail-on: Type 1 ES routes absent from DUT or neighbor PE, or traffic blackhole exceeds es-waiting-time, or aliasing path not established (CE unreachable).

e943b5db06a7dae1.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT ↔ MPLS core ↔ Cisco XR or Juniper MX PE; configure BGP peer with 'af-l2vpn evpn' on both sides; create EVPN instance (EVI 100); attach multihomed CE via LAG to both PEs; configure matching ESI on LAG (test ESI types 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 across iterations or multiple EVIs).
Action: On DUT: 'advertise-mac enable'; inject L2 frames from CE to trigger MAC learning; start packet capture on BGP peering session (tcpdump port 179); for each ESI type (0=arbitrary, 1=LACP, 2=bridge-MAC, 3=MAC-based, 4=router-ID, 5=AS-based): send traffic, capture BGP UPDATE containing MAC/IP Advertisement route (type 2).
Verify: Wireshark: filter 'bgp.evpn.nlri.rt == 2', decode 10-octet ESI field, confirm first byte ∈ {0,1,2,3,4,5}; on DUT CLI: 'show bgp evpn route-type 2' → verify routes from 3rd-party PE installed; on Cisco/Juniper: equivalent show command; verify MAC table entries on both PEs; run bidirectional L2 ping from CE through both PEs.
e943b5db06a7dae1.expectation:
Pass:    All captured MAC/IP Advertisement routes (type 2) have ESI field byte[0] in range [0,5]; no BGP NOTIFICATION messages; both PEs accept peer's routes ('show bgp evpn route-type 2' displays ≥1 imported MAC/IP route); MAC addresses learned in both FIBs; L2 ping succeeds bidirectionally (0% loss, RTT < 10ms).
Fail-on: ESI field byte[0] > 5 detected in wire capture; BGP NOTIFICATION code 3 subcode 11 (Malformed EVPN NLRI); route rejected by peer (missing from 'show bgp evpn'); ESI type mismatch between advertisement and local config; MAC learning failure; L2 forwarding black-hole or one-way only; or BGP session reset during route exchange.

e99b9c951171ffc5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES1 on DUT (PE1) and neighbor PE2 with matching ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11. Enable EVPN instance (vlan-based) with `auto-discovery` on both PEs. Configure BGP `af-l2vpn evpn` peering between PE1 and PE2. Attach IXIA CE to both PEs forming All-Active multihomed segment.
Action: PE1 advertises Ethernet A-D per-ES route (type 1) with ESI Label Extended Community (Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01, Flags=0x00 for RED=00 All-Active, ESI label=1000). PE2 receives route via BGP. IXIA sends BUM Ethernet frame via PE1; PE1 forwards to PE2 with ESI label 1000 imposed.
Verify: On PE2, `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11` displays ESI Label Extended Community with label=1000, Flags bits [5:6]=00. `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows local ESI label value. Capture PE2 behavior: BUM frame received from PE1 with ESI label=1000 is NOT forwarded back to ES1 (split-horizon enforced).
e99b9c951171ffc5.expectation:
Pass:    ESI Label Extended Community present in BGP A-D route with correct encoding (Type=0x06, Sub-Type=0x01, Flags=0x00, label=1000); split-horizon active (PE2 drops BUM destined for ES1 when ESI label matches local label); both BGP session stable and EVPN multihoming operational.
Fail-on: ESI Label Extended Community missing or malformed (wrong Type/Sub-Type/Flags); split-horizon failure (PE2 forwards BUM back to ES1 causing L2 loop); BGP session flaps during ESI Label advertisement; neighbor PE incorrectly processes RED=00 as non-All-Active mode.

e9a5304dc2019f9a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with ethernet-segment on agg-eth 0; configure identifier 0 <type0-value> and load-balancing-mode all-active; configure service-carving highest-preference to establish DUT as designated forwarder for BUM traffic; configure IXIA as remote PE to originate MPLS-encapsulated unknown-unicast traffic.
Action: From IXIA, inject malformed MPLS packets: (a) reserved MPLS label values 4-15 in label stack, (b) P2MP LSP label with TTL=0, (c) corrupted downstream-label in ingress-replication mode, (d) Ethernet frame exceeding MTU inside MPLS payload, (e) reserved EXP bits set in MPLS header, (f) malformed ESI encoding in received EVPN route update.
Verify: Capture ingress with tcpdump; confirm all variants dropped before BUM flooding to agg-eth 0; check MPLS and EVPN error counters; verify syslog entries for each malformed variant type; confirm no traffic egresses to ethernet-segment; verify DUT stability (no crash, no stale forwarding state).
e9a5304dc2019f9a.expectation:
Pass:    All malformed variants dropped at MPLS ingress processing before RFC7432bis ch.13.2.1 default BUM-flooding (no-MAC-lookup) behavior applies; MPLS/EVPN error counters increment for each variant; syslog records malformed-packet event for each type; no BUM forwarding to agg-eth 0; DUT remains stable with no crash or memory leak.
Fail-on: Malformed packet forwarded to ethernet-segment; DUT crash, hang, or memory corruption; missing MPLS/EVPN error counters; missing syslog entry; corrupt FDB entry created from invalid frame; BUM flooding attempted despite invalid MPLS encapsulation.

e9a7cf345fb36cef.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVI 100 on DUT and neighbor PE with ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 (all-active multi-homing). Enable `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on both. Attach IXIA to remote PE; learn MAC_A (aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01) from DUT's ESI.
Action: IXIA sends 1000 unicast frames to MAC_A (known, in BGP Type 2 route from DUT). IXIA sends 1000 unicast frames to MAC_B (bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:02, unknown). Capture `show evpn mac-table evi 100`, `show evpn routes type 2 evi 100`, `show interface ethernet 1/1 statistics` (DUT's ES interface).
Verify: Known unicast (MAC_A) forwarded per DF election or load-balance to DUT's Ethernet 1/1 (ES member); zero loss. Unknown unicast (MAC_B) flooded to all PE members in EVI 100 per BUM handling. Type 2 route for MAC_A present with ESI label and multi-homing flags.
e9a7cf345fb36cef.expectation:
Pass:    1000/1000 frames to MAC_A received on correct ES member (DUT or neighbor PE per load-balance); Type 2 route shows MAC_A with ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 and MPLS label. Unknown unicast (MAC_B) flooded to ≥2 PEs (DUT + neighbor). Multi-homing active (both PEs in `show evpn ethernet-segment`).
Fail-on: Known unicast loss >0 packets; MAC_A not in `show evpn mac-table` or Type 2 route missing ESI; unknown unicast dropped when unknown-mac-flooding=enable; only one PE receives traffic (multi-homing broken); control-word mismatch errors in `show evpn statistics`.

ea1fc015e8149877.action_steps:
Setup:  Via NETCONF <edit-config>, configure EVPN instance 'evpn1' service-type vlan-aware-bundle on x-eth 0/0/1 with VLAN-IDs 10-15; configure import-rt/export-rt 65000:100 under auto-discovery; bind interface to EVPN. IXIA emulates EVPN peer or sends raw tagged frames.
Action: Send BUM frames (broadcast dst MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF) from IXIA with VLAN tags: in-bundle (10, 12, 15) and out-of-bundle (5, 20, 100). Capture NETCONF <get> response for /l2-services/evpn[evpn-name='evpn1'] to confirm VLAN range.
Verify: show evpn instance evpn1 detail (CLI), show bridge vlan statistics, show interface x-eth 0/0/1 counters | include drop. Capture egress on DUT EVPN tunnel or local AC.
ea1fc015e8149877.equipment: DUT + NETCONF client (ncclient) + IXIA traffic gen
ea1fc015e8149877.expectation:
Pass:    NETCONF config applied (VLAN range 10-15 in <get> response); BUM frames with VLAN-ID 10, 12, 15 forwarded into EVPN (MAC learned, tunnel encap sent); frames with VLAN-ID 5, 20, 100 dropped (interface drop counters increment, no tunnel encap, no MAC learning for out-of-range VLANs).
Fail-on: Any BUM frame with VLAN-ID outside 10-15 is forwarded or MAC-learned; any frame with VLAN-ID 10-15 is dropped; NETCONF <get> does not reflect configured VLAN range; schema error on valid vlan-aware-bundle config.

ea204e80626be6c7.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs connected via iBGP with EVPN AFI/SAFI enabled. On PE1 (DUT): configure `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-based` under `l2-services`, configure `ethernet-segment` under `interface agg-eth 0` (multi-homed to CE), set RD and RT for evpn-1.
Action: Bring up interface agg-eth 0 on PE1 to trigger type-1 route advertisement. Capture BGP UPDATE on the PE1-PE2 link with `tcpdump -i <link-if> -s0 -w /tmp/evpn-ad.pcap tcp port 179`. Decode PCAP with Wireshark or `tcpdump -vvv -r /tmp/evpn-ad.pcap`.
Verify: Locate MP_REACH_NLRI for AFI/SAFI 25/70, Route Type 1. Extract NLRI fields: RD (8 octets), ESI (10 octets), Ethernet Tag ID, MPLS label. Extract Next Hop from MP_REACH_NLRI. Compare Next Hop to PE1's BGP router-id via `show running-config router bgp` or `show bgp evpn summary`.
ea204e80626be6c7.expectation:
Pass:    RD field present (8 octets, non-zero value per §8.4.1). ESI field exactly 10 octets matching PE1's configured ESI. MP_REACH_NLRI Next Hop matches PE1's loopback or BGP router-id (4 octets for IPv4 or 16 octets for IPv6).
Fail-on: RD missing, wrong length, or all-zeros. ESI not exactly 10 octets or doesn't match configured value. Next Hop is 0.0.0.0/:: or doesn't match PE1's address.

ea51ad651057a9de.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Cisco/Juniper PE connected via MPLS; configure BGP EVPN peering with `af-l2vpn evpn`, matching import-rt/export-rt on both sides; ensure DUT `advertise-mac enable`; attach IXIA traffic generator to both PEs.
Action: Learn one MAC from IXIA behind DUT (ARP/GARP to trigger Type 2 route); learn one MAC from IXIA behind 3rd-party PE; capture BGP UPDATE messages on both sides using Wireshark/tcpdump; send bidirectional L2 unicast frames between the two MACs.
Verify: Parse captured Type 2 routes from DUT and confirm presence of all mandatory fields: RD, ESI, Ethernet Tag ID, MAC Address, IP Address, MPLS Label1 (LT1), L3 Label, Static flag, Sequence Number, RTs, Next Hop = DUT loopback IP; confirm 3rd-party PE installs DUT's Type 2 route (show bgp l2vpn evpn); confirm DUT installs 3rd-party Type 2 route; measure bidirectional traffic success rate.
ea51ad651057a9de.expectation:
Pass:    All 12 mandatory fields present in DUT's Type 2 route (RD, ESI, ET ID, MAC, IP, LT1, L3 Label, Static flag, Seq#, RTs, Next Hop=DUT IP); 3rd-party PE accepts and installs DUT's route; DUT accepts and installs 3rd-party route; bidirectional traffic success ≥ 99.9%.
Fail-on: Any mandatory field missing or malformed in DUT's Type 2 route; route rejected by 3rd-party PE (BGP notification or route not in RIB); DUT rejects 3rd-party route; traffic black-hole or success rate < 99.9%.

eadf9f321d4f58c4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE with one EVPN instance (vlan-based service-type) and two local CE attachments; establish MP-BGP with neighbor PE; configure multi-homed ESI on one interface and force DUT to non-DF state; enable advertise-mac and unknown-mac-flooding (defaults); populate MACT with one known MAC, leave one unknown.
Action: Send six frame types via IXIA: (1) unicast from local CE1 → verify not looped to CE1 itself; (2) frame from neighbor PE (remote CE) → verify forwarded only to local CE ports; (3) BUM from neighbor PE → verify NOT forwarded to the non-DF ES; (4) known unicast MAC → verify MACT lookup and forward; (5) BUM frame → verify BUM database flooding; (6) MPLS frame with LT1 label for unknown MAC → verify drop.
