Verify: `show l2-services evpn <name> mac-table`, `show l2-services evpn <name> ethernet-segment` (confirm non-DF), `show l2-services evpn <name> statistics` for drops; IXIA packet captures confirm frame disposition per forwarding rules.
eadf9f321d4f58c4.expectation:
Pass:    (1) Local CE1 frame not returned to CE1 (zero loopback); (2) remote CE frame reaches both local CEs, zero copies to remote PEs; (3) BUM to non-DF ES dropped (drop counter +1); (4) known MAC forwarded per MACT; (5) BUM flooded per BUM database; (6) LT1 with unknown MAC dropped (MACT miss counter +1).
Fail-on: Local CE hairpin observed; remote CE frame forwarded to remote PE; non-DF PE forwards BUM to its non-DF ES; known MAC not found in MACT or forwarded incorrectly; BUM not flooded; LT1 unknown MAC forwarded instead of dropped.

eaf1f9ec72238c7a.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVI-100 on Exaware DUT (configuration/l2-services/evpn: RD 1.1.1.1:100, RT 100:100, unknown-mac-flooding enable, advertise-mac enable). Configure matching EVPN instance on Cisco IOS-XR PE with ingress replication. Verify BGP EVPN af-l2vpn evpn neighbor up; confirm IMET route-type 3 exchanged with P-tunnel attribute (PMSI tunnel-type 6, ingress-replication label L1).
Action: From Cisco PE, send three MPLS-encapsulated frames to DUT's advertised label L1: (1) unknown unicast DA=00:11:22:33:44:55 SA=AA:00:00:00:00:01, (2) broadcast DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF SA=AA:00:00:00:00:02, (3) multicast DA=01:00:5E:01:01:01 SA=AA:00:00:00:00:03. IXIA captures on DUT's two local attachment circuits (AC1, AC2).
Verify: show l2-services evpn route-type 3 (IMET from Cisco present, label L1 noted). show mpls forwarding-table label L1 (confirm entry, packet-count = 3). show l2-services evpn mac-table evi 100 (DA 00:11:22:33:44:55 absent). Confirm IXIA captures all three frames on both AC1 and AC2 (flooded).
eaf1f9ec72238c7a.expectation:
Pass:    All three frames flooded to both ACs; unknown-unicast MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55) treated per RFC7432bis §12.1 (flooded, not dropped); show mpls forwarding-table label L1 packet-count ≥ 3; broadcast and multicast also flooded.
Fail-on: Ingress replication label not installed; packet-count = 0; unknown unicast dropped or forwarded to single AC only; broadcast/multicast not flooded; IMET route rejected due to encoding mismatch.

eb00f5761644dab7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure interface `agg-eth 0` with `l2-transport` to designate it as an L2 interface; verify no sub-interfaces on `agg-eth 0` are bound to VPLS or other non-EVPN L2 applications.
Action: Enter `configure` mode, navigate to `interface agg-eth 0`, issue `ethernet-segment`, observe the CLI mode change, then issue `commit`.
Verify: Observe CLI prompt changes to indicate ethernet-segment configuration mode; issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` to confirm the `ethernet-segment` stanza appears.
eb00f5761644dab7.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
eb00f5761644dab7.expectation:
Pass:    CLI transitions to ethernet-segment mode (prompt indicates sub-mode) and `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` includes the `ethernet-segment` stanza after commit
Fail-on: Command rejected despite `l2-transport` being configured, no mode transition occurs, or `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` lacks the `ethernet-segment` stanza post-commit

eb7dbe8a6571682a.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that the DUT correctly selects RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 behavior when at least one received RT1 Ethernet A-D per EVPN Instance route lacks L2-Attr Extended Community, preventing incorrect RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 logic from being applied.
Method: Configure two neighbor PEs in a multihomed EVPN topology; PE1 sends RT1 without L2-Attr, PE2 sends RT1 with L2-Attr. Drive MAC learning traffic to generate RT2 routes while DUT processes mixed RT1 formats, and verify DUT applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 logic while maintaining RT2 forwarding.
Setup: Two-PE EVPN multihoming with shared ESI. DUT peers with both PEs via `af-l2vpn evpn`. Configure `import-rt` and `export-rt` for EVPN route exchange. PE1 configured to omit L2-Attr Extended Community in RT1 advertisements; PE2 configured to include L2-Attr Extended Community. Clean MAC learning table.
Action: Bring up BGP EVPN neighbors. Verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` receives RT1 from both PEs. Enable MAC advertisement via `advertise-mac enable`. Send bidirectional unicast traffic between CE-A (MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:01) and CE-B (MAC aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:02) to populate MAC learning and trigger RT2 advertisement. Capture RT1 and RT2 BGP UPDATEs. Inspect extended communities in received routes to confirm L2-Attr presence/absence.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` shows RT1 from PE1 without L2-Attr and RT1 from PE2 with L2-Attr. DUT applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 redundancy mode behavior (not RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1) because at least one RT1 lacks L2-Attr. `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` displays learned MAC/IP routes. MAC forwarding table contains both CE MACs. Bidirectional traffic forwarded successfully.
eb7dbe8a6571682a.expectation:
Pass:    DUT applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 when any RT1 lacks L2-Attr Extended Community. RT2 MAC/IP routes advertised and learned correctly. MAC forwarding table populated per EVPNS-REQ#350. Traffic forwarded bidirectionally with 0% loss. No BGP errors.
Fail-on: DUT applies RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 despite missing L2-Attr in PE1's RT1, or RT2 advertisement/learning fails, or MAC forwarding drops traffic (>0% loss), or BGP session errors, or MAC table empty.

eb7fbf34ba957955.action_steps:
Setup:  Multi-homed Ethernet Segment configured on DUT and neighbor PE; neighbor PE is current DF (Type-4 ES route advertised, DF Election Extended Community with DF bit clear); continuous bidirectional L2 traffic via IXIA through neighbor PE.
Action: Bring up DUT with higher DF Preference (or lower IP in default algorithm) but with Don't Preempt capability enabled; DUT advertises Type-4 ES route with DF Election Extended Community carrying Don't Preempt (DP) bit set (bit position per RFC9785 §4.3); capture BGP EVPN updates on both PEs.
Verify: `show bgp evpn ethernet-segment <esi>` confirms neighbor PE still DF, DUT is non-DF; `show bgp evpn routes type 4` shows DUT's Type-4 route has DF Election EC with DP=1; wireshark capture of BGP update decodes EC flags byte correctly; IXIA traffic counters show zero disruption.
eb7fbf34ba957955.expectation:
Pass:    Neighbor PE remains DF for all Ethernet Tags in the ES despite DUT having higher election priority; DUT Type-4 route carries DF Election Extended Community with Don't Preempt bit=1 (per RFC9785 §4.3 encoding); IXIA Rx counters match Tx (no frames lost during DUT return).
Fail-on: DUT preempts neighbor PE as DF; DF Election Extended Community missing or DP bit=0; traffic drop >0.1% during convergence; DF role flapping in syslog.

eb9ba32a4ccc1c30.action_steps:
Problem: validate that an EVPN instance correctly forwards both known-unicast and BUM traffic using ingress replication, while advertising Type 3 IMET routes with proper PMSI Tunnel attributes and enforcing split-horizon.
Method: build a three-PE EVPN topology with ingress replication, exchange IMET routes, send known-unicast, unknown-unicast, and broadcast frames from PE1, and verify selective unicast forwarding vs. BUM replication to all remote PEs.
Setup: Three PEs (PE1, PE2, PE3) in one EVI; BGP EVPN peering established; configure `advertise-mac enable` and `unknown-mac-flooding enable` on all PEs; attach IXIA ports to access interfaces (e.g., `x-eth 0/0/1` on each PE); learn one MAC address from PE2's access port.
Action: From PE1's IXIA access port, send (1) a known-unicast frame destined to the learned MAC on PE2, (2) an unknown-unicast frame to an unlearned MAC, and (3) a broadcast frame (DA FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF). Capture BGP Type 3 IMET routes on PE control plane and data-plane packets on PE1→PE2 and PE1→PE3 core links.
Verify: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3` and confirm each PE advertises one Type 3 IMET route with PMSI Tunnel attribute encoding ingress replication tunnel type and label per RFC7432bis §8.3.1.1. Confirm known-unicast frame reaches only PE2 (no copy to PE3). Confirm unknown-unicast and broadcast frames replicate to both PE2 and PE3 (one copy each, no duplication). Verify no frames loop back to PE1's access port (split-horizon enforcement per EVPNS-REQ#360).
eb9ba32a4ccc1c30.expectation:
Pass: Type 3 IMET routes present from all three PEs with valid PMSI Tunnel attributes; known-unicast forwarded exclusively to PE2; unknown-unicast and broadcast each replicated exactly once to PE2 and PE3; zero frames forwarded back to PE1's access port.
Fail-on: Missing or malformed IMET route; missing PMSI Tunnel attribute; known-unicast replicated to multiple PEs or dropped; BUM traffic missing at any remote PE, duplicated, or looped back to source PE.

ebdd12df68310fa5.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (PE1, PE2) sharing ES; PE1: `service-carving highest-preference preference 40000`, PE2: `service-carving highest-preference` (no explicit preference to verify default 32767); same ESI, load-balancing-mode all-active.
Action: Bring up ES; capture BGP EVPN Type-4 (ES route) from both PEs; parse DF Election Extended Community (Type 0x06 0x06) octets 6-7 for preference field; verify PE1 advertises 40000, PE2 advertises 32767; confirm PE1 becomes DF (highest-preference wins).
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows PE1 as DF; `show bgp evpn route-type 4` displays preference values; BUM from IXIA flows only via PE1; PE2 blocks access-side BUM; reconfigure PE2 `preference 50000` → DF shifts to PE2 within es-waiting-time.
ebdd12df68310fa5.expectation:
Pass:    CLI accepts preference 0, 32767, 65535; default is 32767 (verify in BGP attribute when omitted); DF election follows preference per algorithm (highest/lowest); DF Election Extended Community octets 6-7 = configured preference in network byte order.
Fail-on: Preference outside 0-65535 accepted; default ≠ 32767; BGP attribute missing or wrong; DF election ignores preference (both PEs forward BUM, or wrong PE elected).

ec0be94d1ab7b649.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 EVPN instances on DUT under `configuration / l2-services`: `evpn evpn-A service-type vlan-based`, `evpn evpn-B service-type vlan-based`, `evpn evpn-C service-type vlan-based`; assign distinct Type 1 RDs (e.g., 10.0.0.1:100, 10.0.0.1:200, 10.0.0.1:300) via route-distinguisher command or auto-generation; establish iBGP EVPN AF peering (`af-l2vpn evpn`) with neighbor PE.
Action: Trigger EVPN route advertisement from each MAC-VRF (inject L2 frames to learn MACs, or configure static MACs to generate type 2 routes; IMET type 3 routes auto-advertised); capture BGP UPDATE messages with `tcpdump -i <pe-link> -s 0 -w evpn.pcap tcp port 179`; use Wireshark EVPN dissector or manual decode of MP_REACH_NLRI attribute.
Verify: Parse each EVPN route's NLRI per RFC7432bis §7: for type 2 (MAC/IP) and type 3 (IMET), extract RD field (8 octets immediately after route-type and length fields); map each route to its originating MAC-VRF via RT or manual correlation; confirm evpn-A routes carry 10.0.0.1:100, evpn-B carry 10.0.0.1:200, evpn-C carry 10.0.0.1:300; verify RD format: Type 1 (first 2 octets = 0x0001, next 4 octets = 10.0.0.1, last 2 octets = unique number).
ec0be94d1ab7b649.expectation:
Pass:    Each MAC-VRF's EVPN routes (type 2, type 3) carry the MAC-VRF's assigned RD in the NLRI; all 3 RDs are distinct (10.0.0.1:100, :200, :300); RD field is 8 octets, Type 1 format, non-zero, and unique per MAC-VRF.
Fail-on: Same RD value appears in routes from two different MAC-VRFs; RD in NLRI ≠ configured RD for that MAC-VRF; RD is 0:0 or malformed; RD type field ≠ 0x0001 when Type 1 expected.

ec2b8c2bd6ffa2b5.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `interface agg-eth 0` and enter `ethernet-segment` sub-mode.
Action: Test ESI configuration: (a) Configure Type 0 ESI with valid 9-octet hex value 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 in format xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx.xx. (b) Attempt truncated 6-octet value 00.11.22.33.44.55. (c) Attempt non-hex characters GG.HH.II.JJ.KK.LL.MM.NN.OO. (d) Issue `no identifier` to restore Type 4 default. Commit after each attempt.
Verify: After (a), `show running-config interface agg-eth 0 ethernet-segment` displays the configured 9-octet ESI; attempts (b) and (c) rejected at CLI parse time with error message before commit; after (d), explicit ESI config removed from running-config.
ec2b8c2bd6ffa2b5.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
ec2b8c2bd6ffa2b5.expectation:
Pass:    Valid 9-octet hex ESI committed and persisted in running-config; truncated and non-hex values rejected with parse error; `no identifier` restores Type 4 default
Fail-on: Invalid ESI value silently committed to running-config, valid value rejected, system crash/hang on malformed input, or `no identifier` fails to remove explicit config

ec497151c411754b.action_steps:
Setup:  After establishing EVPN BD 100 with BGP neighbor via `af-l2vpn evpn`, unknown-mac-flooding enabled (default), and forwarding BUM traffic that triggered IMET route advertisement with PMSI Tunnel attribute per §11.2.
Action: Execute DUT tech-support collection command.
Verify: Extract from tech-support archive: (1) `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type imet detail` output showing PMSI Tunnel attribute with P-tunnel identity and BD-bound MPLS label, (2) `show mpls forwarding-table` with label→BD 100 binding, (3) `show l2-services evpn` displaying P-tunnel type/state, (4) running-config capturing RT/RD and unknown-mac-flooding setting, (5) syslog entries for IMET route installation.
ec497151c411754b.expectation:
Pass:    All five diagnostic outputs present; IMET route contains PMSI Tunnel attribute with non-zero Tunnel Type, Tunnel ID, and MPLS label; label in PMSI Tunnel attribute matches label in `show mpls forwarding-table` bound to BD 100; P-tunnel state is active.
Fail-on: Any show command output missing from tech-support, PMSI Tunnel attribute fields absent or zeroed in IMET route, label mismatch between PMSI attribute and MPLS FIB, or P-tunnel state not captured.

ec9bab88170b61dc.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'EVI-100' with service-type vlan-aware-bundle, RD 65000:100, import-rt/export-rt 65000:100, auto-discovery enabled. Configure agg-eth 1 with ethernet-segment ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99. Establish iBGP EVPN session to neighbor PE (AS 65000); verify Type-1 A-D per EVI route (RD 65000:100, ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, Next Hop = DUT loopback) is advertised.
Action: Power-cycle DUT via lab PDU. Monitor boot sequence, BGP session re-establishment, and EVPN route re-advertisement.
Verify: On neighbor PE, capture BGP EVPN received routes; confirm Type-1 A-D per EVI route present with RD=65000:100, ESI=00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 (10 octets), Next Hop=DUT loopback. On DUT, run 'show bgp evpn route type 1 rd 65000:100' and 'show l2-services evpn EVI-100'.
ec9bab88170b61dc.expectation:
Pass:    Type-1 A-D per EVI route re-advertised post-reboot with identical RD (65000:100), ESI (00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, 10 octets), and Next Hop (DUT loopback IPv4/IPv6). BGP EVPN session re-establishes and route appears within 120 seconds of boot completion.
Fail-on: Type-1 route missing, RD mismatch, ESI length ≠ 10 octets, ESI value mismatch, Next Hop ≠ DUT loopback, or BGP session fails to recover.

ed5c9ba72c5436f3.action_steps:
Setup:  Under `configuration / l2-services / evpn EVPN100 service-type vlan-based`, configure `host mac-address duplicate-detection` and commit; verify command appears in `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN100`.
Action: Execute `copy running-config startup-config`; reload DUT via `reload` command; wait for boot completion and CLI access.
Verify: Post-reload `show running-config l2-services evpn EVPN100` displays `host mac-address duplicate-detection` in identical position within the evpn context.
ed5c9ba72c5436f3.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
ed5c9ba72c5436f3.expectation:
Pass:    `host mac-address duplicate-detection` persists in running-config post-reload under evpn EVPN100 context; no manual re-configuration required; default state (disabled, per `no` form) correctly applies when command absent.
Fail-on: Command absent or relocated post-reload; config partially lost; command requires manual re-entry; `no` form applied but command still present after reload.

ed7b0aa50a568b5e.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure operational EVPN instance (e.g., `evpn EVPN1 service-type vlan-based` with RD, RT, AC interface, BGP EVPN peering to neighbor PE). No explicit `control-word` command configured.
Action: Send L2 frames from IXIA into EVPN AC interface. Capture encapsulated packets on DUT-to-PE link; verify no Control Word field (4 bytes after MPLS label stack, before Ethernet header). Configure `control-word enable` under EVPN instance, commit. Send frames, capture packets, verify Control Word is present. Configure `control-word disable`, commit, verify Control Word absent. Issue `no control-word`, verify equivalent to disable.
Verify: `show running-config` under EVPN instance section reflects command state (line appears only when non-default). Packet captures (tcpdump on inter-PE link, MPLS filter) show Control Word presence/absence per configuration.
ed7b0aa50a568b5e.expectation:
Pass:    Default (no command) = no Control Word in packets. `control-word enable` adds Control Word (4-byte field: flags + sequence number). `control-word disable` and `no control-word` both remove it. Configuration idempotent and persists.
Fail-on: Control Word presence/absence mismatches configuration, or EVPN session flaps when command is applied.

eda359460c7b7303.action_steps:
Setup:  On PE1, configure MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 as static/sticky in EVPN instance (advertises EVPN Type-2 route with MAC Mobility extended community: static flag=1, seq=0); PE2 is neighbor PE in same EVI receiving the sticky MAC route; capture BGP update on PE2 to confirm EC fields.
Action: Trigger sticky MAC conflict by one of: (a) from CE attached to PE2, IXIA sends frames with MAC SA 00:11:22:33:44:55, causing PE2 local learning; or (b) at PE1, learn same MAC from second ES (ES2), simulating multihoming conflict.
Verify: Run `show l2-services evpn mac-table` to confirm MAC entry still marked sticky (static=1, seq=0, not overwritten); check syslog on detecting PE for operator alert message naming MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, sticky violation, and source; if PE drops frames, verify `show interfaces <ac-interface> statistics` increments SA-discard counter.
eda359460c7b7303.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
eda359460c7b7303.expectation:
Pass:    Syslog alert generated (e.g., "Sticky MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 conflict detected from [source]"); MAC table entry retains sticky attributes (static=1, seq=0); SA-discard counter increments if drop enabled.
Fail-on: No operator alert logged; MAC entry overwritten (seq > 0 or static flag cleared); frames forwarded without conflict detection; or syslog message missing MAC/source details.

ee84c0895854aa0b.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (PE1, PE2) multi-homed to CE via LACP LAG, shared ESI type 1. EVI 100 configured on both PEs. QoS marking policy applied to `agg-eth 0` on both PEs as neighbor feature; baseline QoS counters captured.
Action: On PE1: `interface agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment / identifier 1 / load-balancing-mode all-active / service-carving highest-preference preference 30000 / commit`. On PE2: `interface agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment / identifier 1 / load-balancing-mode all-active / service-carving default / commit`. Bring up both ESs; capture BGP EVPN advertisements with tcpdump. Verify Type 4 routes carry DF Election Extended Community with mismatched 'DF Alg' values (Highest Preference on PE1, Default on PE2). Drive bidirectional L2 traffic through LAG for 60 seconds.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment` on both PEs reports DF election algorithm converged to Default (fallback per EVPNS-REQ#120 MUST due to mismatch detected). `show evpn route type ethernet-segment` shows Type 4 routes present with DF Election Extended Community. `show evpn route type ethernet-auto-discovery` shows Type 1 routes per RFC7432bis-§7.6. `show evpn route-target` confirms ES-Import RT derived per EVPNS-REQ#250. Traffic forwarded bidirectionally without drops; QoS counters increment per policy; no errors in `show interface agg-eth 0 statistics`.
ee84c0895854aa0b.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
ee84c0895854aa0b.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs detect DF Alg mismatch (highest-preference vs. default) in received Type 4 routes and fall back to Default algorithm per [RFC7432bis-§8.5] as required by EVPNS-REQ#120; DF election converges; Type 1 and Type 4 routes exchanged; ES-Import RT present; all-active forwarding operational; QoS policy unaffected.
Fail-on: Either PE fails to fall back to Default algorithm when mismatch detected (continues using highest-preference), or DF election does not converge within 10 seconds, or Type 1 or Type 4 routes missing from BGP table, or ES-Import RT not derived, or traffic loss >0.1%, or QoS counters frozen or policy bypassed.

eec0a8e397fd4f0f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 2-PE EVPN topology with multi-homed CE: PE1 (DUT, ingress/DF) and PE2 (neighbor, non-DF egress) share ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 to CE. Enable `unknown-mac-flooding enable`, `advertise-mac enable` on DUT. Configure `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbor PE2. Verify PE2's Ethernet A-D per-ES route (type 1) advertises ESI label: `show bgp l2vpn evpn routes type 1 esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11`.
Action: IXIA (emulating CE) sends broadcast frame (dst MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF, VLAN matching EVI) into PE1. PE1 performs Ingress Replication, sending BUM copy to PE2.
Verify: IXIA captures MPLS-encapsulated packet on PE1→PE2 link. Decode MPLS label stack: confirm ESI label (matching PE2's type-1 route label field) is present. Cross-reference label value with `show bgp l2vpn evpn routes type 1` output for PE2's advertised ESI label.
eec0a8e397fd4f0f.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (non-DF for shared ESI) + IXIA traffic gen and MPLS packet capture
eec0a8e397fd4f0f.expectation:
Pass:    BUM packet from PE1 to non-DF PE2 includes ESI label in MPLS stack, with label value matching PE2's advertised ESI label from Ethernet A-D per-ES route (RFC7432bis §8.3.1.1).
Fail-on: ESI label absent in MPLS label stack of BUM packet to non-DF egress PE, or label value mismatch (not equal to PE2's advertised ESI label from type-1 route).

eec49cea5e67b90b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based` under `configuration / l2-services`. Configure `agg-eth 0` with `ethernet-segment` enabled. Boot with no explicit `es-waiting-time` configured under the ethernet-segment context.
Action: Enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0 / ethernet-segment` mode. Issue `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` to confirm no `es-waiting-time` line (default 3 seconds is implicit). Configure `es-waiting-time 3`, commit, verify the line does NOT appear in running-config (idempotent with default). Configure `es-waiting-time 5`, commit, verify the line appears. Issue `no es-waiting-time`, commit, verify the line disappears (default restored).
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0 ethernet-segment` after each configuration change confirms: (1) unconfigured → no line present (default 3s implicit), (2) explicit 3s → no line present (idempotent), (3) explicit 5s → `es-waiting-time 5` present, (4) `no` form → no line present (default restored).
eec49cea5e67b90b.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
eec49cea5e67b90b.expectation:
Pass:    Default value is 3 seconds (implicit, not shown in running-config). Explicit configuration of `es-waiting-time 3` does not add a line to running-config (idempotent). Non-default values (e.g., 5 seconds) persist and display correctly. `no es-waiting-time` removes the line and restores default 3-second behavior.
Fail-on: Default differs from 3 seconds, or explicit config of `es-waiting-time 3` appears in running-config (breaking idempotency), or `no` form does not restore default, or non-default value is not retained across commit.

eee29ddd41cd7b48.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure neighbor PE with ethernet-segment (identifier 0 AA.BB.CC.DD.EE.FF.00.00.01, load-balancing-mode all-active) on agg-eth interface. Configure DUT as single-homing PE (no ethernet-segment on attachment). Establish BGP EVPN session; verify DUT processes A-D per-ES (route-type 4) and per-EVI (route-type 1) routes with ESI label from neighbor. Start IXIA BUM traffic from DUT single-homed site.
Action: Power-cycle the DUT via lab PDU/smart-PSU harness while BGP EVPN session is up and multihoming A-D routes are active in RIB.
Verify: Run `show running-config interface agg-eth`, `show bgp evpn summary`, `show evpn ethernet-segment remote`, `show evpn route-type 1`, `show evpn route-type 4`. Confirm config intact, BGP re-establishes within es-waiting-time (default 3s) + margin, remote ES AA.BB.CC.DD.EE.FF.00.00.01 present, ESI label in A-D routes processed, BUM traffic honors split-horizon (no loop to multihoming PE per RFC7432bis 8.7).
eee29ddd41cd7b48.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
eee29ddd41cd7b48.expectation:
Pass:    DUT recovers; BGP EVPN session re-establishes ≤10s; remote ES AA.BB.CC.DD.EE.FF.00.00.01 in `show evpn ethernet-segment remote`; A-D per-ES/per-EVI routes with ESI label present in `show evpn route-type 1/4`; BUM traffic correctly filtered using ESI label (no reflection to neighbor PE).
Fail-on: Configuration lost on ethernet-segment or evpn context, BGP fails to re-establish, remote ES not recognized, A-D routes missing or ESI label not processed, or BUM traffic loops back to multihoming PE neighbor.

ef970ff6180f7b93.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`, enable `auto-discovery`, and set `import-rt 65000:100` to import ES routes. Configure `ethernet-segment` on interface agg-eth 0. Establish BGP L2VPN EVPN peering with neighbor PE that advertises Type 4 routes with matching export-rt.
Action: Execute `show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment` at the operational prompt. Repeat the command after 30 seconds with no configuration or topology changes.
Verify: Inspect output for Type 4 route entries containing ESI, RD, and originating PE next-hop fields. Cross-reference displayed route count with `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` Type 4 received counter.
ef970ff6180f7b93.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE with BGP L2VPN EVPN session
ef970ff6180f7b93.expectation:
Pass:    Command returns ≥1 Type 4 route with populated ESI, RD, and next-hop fields matching neighbor's advertised routes; output identical on successive invocations.
Fail-on: Zero routes displayed when neighbor advertises Type 4 routes with correct RT, missing or malformed ESI/RD/next-hop fields, route count mismatch with BGP summary, or output inconsistency on stable topology.
ef970ff6180f7b93.req_id: CLI:show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment
ef970ff6180f7b93.sub_category: show bgp l2vpn evpn table ethernet-segment

f02e8128b43e4cbe.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two-PE EVPN topology with `af-l2vpn evpn` under BGP neighbors and matching `import-rt`/`export-rt` on EVPN instance. Enable `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5` on both PEs to verify M and N configurability (per RFC7432bis §15.1 MUST #2). Connect syslog collector.
Action: Use IXIA to send frames with MAC `00:11:22:33:44:55` from PE1 access port (e.g., `x-eth 0/0/1`), wait 10 seconds, move IXIA connection to PE2 access port (`x-eth 0/0/1` on PE2), repeat 6 times within 180 seconds to exceed move-count threshold. Start `tcpdump -i <bgp-interface> -w /tmp/bgp.pcap` on both PEs before first move.
Verify: After 6th move, confirm: (1) syslog alert raised on detecting PE mentioning MAC duplication; (2) `show evpn mac address-table` displays `00:11:22:33:44:55` as frozen/duplicate; (3) pcap analysis shows PE stops sending BGP MAC/IP Advertisement UPDATEs (AFI=25 SAFI=70 NLRI with that MAC) after detection; (4) `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows no new route updates for that MAC.
f02e8128b43e4cbe.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
f02e8128b43e4cbe.expectation:
Pass:    Detection triggers on 6th move within 180 seconds; operator alert raised via syslog; PE stops sending/updating/processing BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes for duplicate MAC per RFC7432bis §15.1 MUST #1; MAC frozen until manual intervention; move-interval and move-count parameters accepted.
Fail-on: No detection after exceeding threshold (6 moves in 180s), no operator alert, or PE continues advertising the MAC in BGP UPDATEs post-detection. OR move-interval/move-count configuration rejected.

f056d7c6d64d1406.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ES-1 (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) multi-homed between DUT and neighbor PE; configure EVPN instance EVI-100 on both with `advertise-mac enable`; verify DUT allocates LT2 label for ES-1 in `show evpn ethernet-segment` and advertises it in BGP route type 1 (Ethernet A-D per EVI) via `show bgp evpn route type 1`; attach IXIA CE behind ES-1, remote IXIA site behind PE-2.
Action: From remote site, send 1000 pps known unicast to CE MAC; ensure PE-2 does not advertise MAC/IP route (type 2) with LT1 for destination, forcing ingress PE to use LT2 per RFC7432bis §8.4; run packet capture on DUT→neighbor PE link.
Verify: Inspect `show bgp evpn route type 1 evi 100 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99` for LT2 label value; confirm MPLS packet capture shows LT2 label in stack; check traffic counters on both DUT and neighbor PE ES-1 sub-interfaces (load-balancing evidence); verify `show evpn multi-homing` DF state unchanged.
f056d7c6d64d1406.expectation:
Pass:    LT2 label present in Ethernet A-D per-EVI route (RFC7432bis §7.1), MPLS packets carry LT2 label when LT1 unavailable (RFC7432bis §8.4), traffic load-balances across DUT and neighbor PE (both ES-1 interfaces show Rx counters >0), multi-homing DF election remains stable.
Fail-on: LT2 label absent from type-1 route, packets missing LT2 label or carry incorrect label, traffic reaches only one PE (counters = 0 on the other), DF election flaps, or ES-1 goes down on either PE.

f06ce543f1bf4648.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT VPLS instance: `configuration / l2-services / vpls / auto-discovery`, `import-rt 100:1`, `export-rt 100:1`. Configure BGP neighbor: `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf / neighbor <PE-IP>`, enable `af-l2vpn evpn`. Configure neighbor PE to advertise Ethernet A-D Per EVPN Instance (route type 1, RD 1.1.1.1:1, ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, Ethernet Tag 0) with ES-Import extended community but without L2-Attr Extended Community.
Action: Neighbor PE sends BGP UPDATE with route type 1; DUT processes the route and checks for L2-Attr Extended Community presence.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 1` confirms route received; `show bgp evpn route-type 1 detail` shows ES-Import extended community present, L2-Attr absent; verify DUT installs route, applies RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 logic (backward-compatible mode), no errors in `show log | grep evpn`.
f06ce543f1bf4648.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (must support selective extended community advertisement)
f06ce543f1bf4648.expectation:
Pass:    Route type 1 without L2-Attr Extended Community is accepted, installed in BGP table, and processed per RFC7432 ch.14.1.1; no error/warning logs.
Fail-on: DUT rejects route due to missing L2-Attr Extended Community, applies RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1 when L2-Attr is absent, or logs error about incompatible extended communities.

f0767ed4fb1cd3fb.action_steps:
Setup: Configure PE1 and PE2 with shared ESI on agg-eth 0 (`ethernet-segment`, `identifier 1` for LACP-based, `load-balancing-mode all-active`). Configure mismatched DF algorithms: PE1 `service-carving highest-random-weight`, PE2 `service-carving highest-preference preference 100`. Configure EVI 100 on both PEs. IXIA generates bidirectional L2 traffic.
Action: Bring up BGP EVPN peering. Verify Route Type 4 (Ethernet Segment Route) advertised with DF Election Extended Community showing mismatched algorithms per RFC8584 §3/§4. Verify Route Type 1 (Ethernet A-D per ES) with ES-Import RT. Observe automatic fallback to Default algorithm per RFC7432bis §8.5. Inject IXIA traffic to verify DF forwarding.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 4` confirms DF Election Extended Community present. `show bgp evpn route-type 1` confirms ES-Import RT. `show evpn ethernet-segment` shows both PEs using Default algorithm (modulo-based per RFC7432bis §8.5), DF elected per VLAN. `show evpn` confirms all-active mode operational. IXIA traffic forwards without loss, load-balanced across both PEs.
f0767ed4fb1cd3fb.expectation:
Pass: Both PEs advertise Route Type 4 with DF Election Extended Community per RFC8584, detect algorithm mismatch, fall back to Default algorithm per RFC7432bis §8.5 modulo function, Route Type 1 with ES-Import RT advertised correctly, DF elected per VLAN, all-active forwarding works with load-balancing.
Fail-on: Missing DF Election Extended Community in Route Type 4, no fallback to Default (RFC7432bis §8.5) when mismatch detected, DF election fails, traffic black-holes, or missing all-active load-balancing.

f0a85dbb98dde57f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two neighbor PEs multi-homed to the same Ethernet Segment (same ESI value) with EVPN enabled, MP2MP tunnel for BUM traffic, and af-l2vpn evpn peering. Enable unknown-mac-flooding on both PEs.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN peering, advertise Ethernet Auto-Discovery routes with ESI labels, send BUM traffic from IXIA to trigger PMSI Tunnel Attribute advertisement, capture BGP updates with `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and decode PMSI Tunnel Attribute and ESI label TLVs.
Verify: Parse ESI label values from both PEs' Ethernet A-D per-ES routes; confirm labels are identical and from DCB range (not per-PE upstream allocation); inspect PMSI Tunnel Attribute in Inclusive Multicast routes for DCB label usage; verify BUM traffic delivered without duplication.
f0a85dbb98dde57f.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs attached to the same ES use the identical ESI label value allocated from the Domain-wide Common Block; PMSI Tunnel Attribute carries DCB labels instead of upstream labels; egress PE label state count ≤ number of Ethernet Segments (not number of ingress PEs); BUM traffic forwarded correctly.
Fail-on: PEs on same ES advertise different ESI label values; ESI labels allocated per-PE (upstream) rather than from DCB; PMSI Tunnel Attribute contains per-ingress-PE upstream labels; label count on egress PE scales with number of ingress PEs; BUM traffic duplicated or black-holed.

f0aef3d7a62c0ac9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two EVPN instances (EVI 100 with export-rt 65000:100, EVI 200 with export-rt 65000:200) on DUT. Configure ethernet-segment ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 on agg-eth 1 and attach to both EVIs. Establish af-l2vpn evpn peering with neighbor PE.
Action: Enable the ES interface; trigger Ethernet A-D per ES route advertisement. On neighbor PE, capture received BGP EVPN Type-1 routes via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 detail`.
Verify: Confirm Type-1 route(s) exist for the ESI. Parse extended community attributes to extract all RT values. Verify each Type-1 route carries at least one RT extended community (RFC4360 Type 0x00/0x02). Verify the union of RTs across all Type-1 routes for this ES equals {65000:100, 65000:200}.
f0aef3d7a62c0ac9.expectation:
Pass:    Each Type-1 route contains RT extended community TLV; union of RTs = {65000:100, 65000:200} matching all associated EVPN instances
Fail-on: Any Type-1 route missing RT extended community attribute, or RT set incomplete (e.g., only 65000:100 present when ES belongs to EVI 100 and 200)

f0c513ad821a31a1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure 3 PEs (PE1, PE2, PE3) in same Ethernet Segment (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) under `interface agg-eth / ethernet-segment`; all PEs initially use HRW DF algorithm; PE1 configured with Don't Preempt capability (`df-election dont-preempt`); vlan-aware-bundle EVPN instance with `auto-discovery`, matching `import-rt`/`export-rt` (e.g., 65000:100) in `l2-services / vpls` mode; IXIA multi-homed to all 3 PEs via LACP.
Action: Shut down PE1's ethernet-segment interface (`shutdown` under `interface agg-eth / ethernet-segment`); wait for DF re-election on PE2/PE3 (verify via `show evpn ethernet-segment esi <esi>`); bring PE1 back with `df-election preference` configured (algorithm mismatch: HRW vs. Preference); send bidirectional L2 traffic from IXIA through the ES; capture `show l2-services evpn routes type 4` to confirm DF Election Extended Community with mismatched algorithm.
Verify: `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` on all 3 PEs for DF algorithm field and DF role; confirm all PEs reverted to modulo (default per RFC7432) despite PE1's Don't Preempt capability; verify DF stable (no flapping); check traffic counters for loss; inspect syslog for "DF algorithm mismatch, falling back to default" event.
f0c513ad821a31a1.equipment: DUT + 2 neighbor PEs + IXIA as multi-homed CE (LACP) — need 3-PE redundancy group to test Don't Preempt override by algorithm mismatch fallback; IXIA validates traffic during DF transition
f0c513ad821a31a1.expectation:
Pass:    All 3 PEs fall back to modulo DF algorithm within 10 seconds of PE1 rejoining with mismatched algorithm (HRW→Preference); PE1's Don't Preempt capability correctly overridden by algorithm mismatch per RFC9785 §4.3 MUST ("all the PEs... MUST fall back to the default DF algorithm"); DF role stable after fallback, no flapping; traffic loss < 1% during transition; syslog shows algorithm mismatch detection on all PEs.
Fail-on: Any PE continues using HRW or Preference algorithm after PE1 rejoins; PE1 preempts DF using wrong (non-modulo) algorithm; traffic blackholed (> 5% loss) or BUM frames duplicated; DF flaps > 1 time; no fallback to modulo observed within 30 seconds; ES state inconsistent across PEs (different DF per `show evpn ethernet-segment`).

f16951b29175f7cb.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with BGP AS 64512 (private 16-bit range); establish eBGP neighbor under L2VPN EVPN address-family; configure vlan-based EVPN service with `evpn <evpn-name>` and `auto-discovery`.
Action: Verify no `private-as` configuration exists under the BGP neighbor; advertise EVPN auto-discovery routes; capture outbound BGP UPDATE messages or inspect routes received on neighbor.
Verify: `show running-config` confirms absence of `private-as` directive; packet capture or neighbor's BGP table shows AS_PATH containing AS 64512 verbatim (not stripped).
f16951b29175f7cb.expectation:
Pass:    Private AS (64512..65534 or 4200000000..4294967294) appears unmodified in AS_PATH of L2VPN EVPN routes (default: disabled).
Fail-on: Private AS is stripped/replaced despite no configuration, or explicit config of default value changes running-config (non-idempotent).

f17d2bd1f654620d.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT as EVPN PE with iBGP to neighbor PE. Neighbor PE advertises MAC/IP Advertisement (RT-2) for MAC1 with ESI=0 (all zeros) and MAC2 with non-reserved ESI (e.g., type-0, 01.02.03.04.05.06.07.08.09); also advertises Ethernet A-D per ES (RT-1) for MAC2's ESI.
Action: Baseline: `show evpn mac-table` confirms MAC1 and MAC2 installed. Control-plane reset: `restart bgp`. After re-establishment, neighbor PE sends RT-2 for MAC1; for MAC2 sends RT-2 first (no RT-1), waits 5s, then sends RT-1.
Verify: `show evpn mac-table`: MAC1 installed within `es-waiting-time` (3s) from RT-2 alone; MAC2 absent during 5s RT-2-only window, then appears within 3s of RT-1. `show bgp evpn routes` confirms routes. IXIA reports convergence.
f17d2bd1f654620d.expectation:
Pass:    MAC1 (ESI=0) installs from RT-2 alone ≤3s post-reset; MAC2 (non-reserved ESI) installs only after both RT-2+RT-1 received (≤3s after RT-1); BGP auto-recovers; data loss ≤ documented convergence
Fail-on: MAC1 requires RT-1 to install, or MAC2 installs before RT-1, or stale state >5s, or no auto-recovery, or permanent forwarding loss >10s

f1be69f92d5aa595.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure ethernet-segment on DUT agg-eth interface with ESI = 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 (Type-01 ESI). Configure EVPN instance, enable af-l2vpn evpn on BGP neighbor to PE2.
Action: Activate agg-eth interface; establish BGP EVPN session. DUT advertises EVPN Type 4 (Ethernet Segment) route for the configured ESI.
Verify: On PE2, capture BGP update for Type 4 route (route-type 4, ESI 01:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99). Extract ES-Import Route Target extended community from the route. On DUT, run `show bgp evpn ethernet-segment` and `show bgp evpn routes type 4` to confirm route advertisement.
f1be69f92d5aa595.expectation:
Pass:    ES-Import RT extended community in Type 4 route = 01:11:22:33:44:55 (exact match to high-order 6 octets of configured ESI). Type 4 route successfully received by PE2.
Fail-on: ES-Import RT value differs from ESI[0:5] (first 6 octets), ES-Import RT extended community missing from Type 4 route, or Type 4 route not advertised by DUT.

f1cae7069024a9d8.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with one BD, enable advertise-mac and unknown-mac-flooding (defaults), establish BGP af-l2vpn evpn session to neighbor PE, configure one ES interface with DF election and one non-ES interface.
Action: Verify LT3 label allocation for the BD via `show evpn instance <id> labels`; capture BGP IMET route (type 3) advertisement and confirm PMSI Tunnel attribute carries the LT3 label; inject BUM frames from IXIA toward the ES interface where remote PE is not DF.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3` displays IMET route with PMSI Tunnel attribute containing the allocated LT3 label; `show evpn ethernet-segment` confirms DF status; IXIA capture on remote PE shows MPLS-encapsulated BUM frames with the LT3 label; counters in `show evpn instance <id> statistics` increment for BUM forwarding.
f1cae7069024a9d8.expectation:
Pass:    LT3 label is allocated per BD and visible in instance label table; IMET route (type 3) advertised with PMSI Tunnel attribute field = LT3 label value; BUM frames forwarded via MPLS carry the LT3 label when remote PE is non-DF on input ES (per RFC7432bis §7.3).
Fail-on: No LT3 label allocated, IMET route missing or PMSI Tunnel attribute absent/zero, BUM frames forwarded without label or with wrong label, or DF logic inverted (label applied when remote PE is DF).

f1f64039d5ec04f0.action_steps:
Problem: Validate that tech-support diagnostic output captures complete DF election state, ESI configuration, and aliasing/backup-path BGP advertisements in a single-active multi-homing scenario for post-incident troubleshooting.
Method: Configure two PEs with matching ESI in single-active mode connected to a LACP partner, trigger DF election, collect tech-support bundle, and verify it contains DF state per service, service-carving parameters, ESI details, and EVPN AD routes for aliasing and backup signaling.

Setup: Configure PE1 and PE2 with matching ESI `identifier 1` on `agg-eth 0` interfaces to one CE. Set `load-balancing-mode single-active` and `service-carving highest-random-weight` under the ethernet-segment. Establish EVPN instance, bring up LACP, and wait for DF election to complete (default `es-waiting-time` 3 seconds).

Action: Execute tech-support commands on both PEs: `show evpn ethernet-segment`, `show evpn ethernet-segment detail`, `show evpn df`, `show evpn route`, `show running-config interface agg-eth 0`, `show bgp evpn summary`, and `show log | include DF`. Save the bundle off-box and inspect.

Verify: Bundle must contain: (1) ESI identifier type and value for `agg-eth 0`, (2) DF election outcome showing which PE is primary/backup per service (from `show evpn df`), (3) `service-carving` algorithm and any preference values, (4) `load-balancing-mode single-active` in running-config, (5) EVPN AD per-ES routes (type 1) for aliasing and AD per-EVI routes (type 1) for backup-path signaling from `show evpn route`, (6) any DF-related syslog entries showing election events.
f1f64039d5ec04f0.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
f1f64039d5ec04f0.expectation:
Pass:    Bundle contains ESI parameters (type, value), DF state (primary vs backup) per service from `show evpn df`, service-carving algorithm, single-active mode config, EVPN type-1 AD per-ES and AD per-EVI routes, and DF election log entries.
Fail-on: Missing DF election state, missing ESI identifier or mode, missing type-1 EVPN routes for aliasing/backup signaling, or incomplete ethernet-segment stanza in running-config.

f2b5d477b20798f2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two neighbor PEs with same ESI (type 0 or auto) on agg-eth interfaces; set `load-balancing-mode all-active` under `interface agg-eth ethernet-segment` on both. Configure DUT as remote PE with BGP EVPN peering to both neighbors; place all in same EVI.
Action: Neighbor PEs advertise Ethernet A-D per ES routes (BGP EVPN type 1) with ESI Label extended community field "Multihoming redundancy mode" = 0. Generate MAC learning on the shared ES; neighbor PEs advertise MAC/IP routes (type 2). From IXIA behind DUT, send L2 unicast frames destined to the learned MAC.
Verify: On DUT: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` confirms A-D per ES routes received with redundancy mode = 0; `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays the ES as All-Active; `show evpn mac-table` shows the MAC with multiple next-hops (both neighbor PE IP addresses); IXIA Tx/Rx counters confirm traffic load-balanced across both neighbor PE paths (≥40% each, allowing for hash distribution).
f2b5d477b20798f2.equipment: DUT (remote PE) + two neighbor PEs (shared ES, all-active) + IXIA
f2b5d477b20798f2.expectation:
Pass:    DUT deduces All-Active mode when all imported A-D per ES routes have redundancy mode = 0; constructs ≥2 next-hops for the MAC; traffic hashes across neighbor PEs per RFC7432bis §14.1.2; label stack uses MAC/IP Advertisement route label.
Fail-on: DUT interprets ES as Single-Active despite redundancy mode = 0; MAC resolved to single next-hop; >90% traffic sent to one PE only; or DUT uses wrong label (e.g., A-D route label instead of MAC/IP route label).

f2c5e2015c374ddf.action_steps:
Setup:  Exaware DUT and Juniper MX PE configured as multi-homed PEs for Ethernet Segment ESI 01:23:45:67:89:ab:cd:ef:01:23. DUT: `interface agg-eth 1/1/1` → `ethernet-segment` → assign ESI and DF election mode; `evpn evpn-vlan-bundle service-type vlan-aware-bundle` for VLANs 100-110 with `auto-discovery`, `import-rt 65000:100`, `export-rt 65000:100`. Juniper: equivalent config. Configure identical local policy on both PEs to override default DF algorithm (e.g., static DF preference per VLAN: DUT=DF for VLANs 100-105, Juniper=DF for VLANs 106-110).
Action: Establish BGP EVPN session; advertise Ethernet A-D per-ES routes (Type 4) and per-EVI routes (Type 1) with DF Election extended community per RFC9785. Inject BUM traffic (broadcast frames) on VLANs 100, 105, 106, 110 from CE side.
Verify: DUT: `show evpn ethernet-segment` → verify DF status matches policy (DF for 100-105, non-DF for 106-110). Juniper: `show evpn instance` → verify reciprocal DF assignment. IXIA: capture uplink traffic to confirm only elected DF forwards BUM per VLAN (no duplication).
f2c5e2015c374ddf.equipment: DUT + 3rd-party PE (Juniper MX) + IXIA traffic gen
f2c5e2015c374ddf.expectation:
Pass:    Both PEs converge on identical DF election per VLAN per consistent local policy override (RFC9785 §4.2 compliance); DF Election extended community exchanged and honored; BUM traffic on VLANs 100-105 forwarded only by DUT, VLANs 106-110 only by Juniper; zero duplication or loss.
Fail-on: DF election mismatch (Juniper ignores DUT's local policy signaling or vice versa); BUM traffic duplicated (both PEs forward same VLAN) or black-holed (neither forwards); Juniper rejects/malforms RFC9785 DF Election extended community.

f300efdabfa2b12b.action_steps:
Setup:  VPLS instance "vpls-100" configured with VPN-ID 100, local AC (interface GigabitEthernet 0/1) and remote peer 192.0.2.1 operational, MAC table empty.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-100`, execute `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface GigabitEthernet 0/1`, commit. Send Ethernet frame (SA=00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee, DA=00:11:22:33:44:55) from IXIA via remote peer pseudowire.
Verify: `show l2-services vpls vpls-100 mac-table` displays 00:11:22:33:44:55 with flags "static" bound to GigabitEthernet 0/1; IXIA capture confirms frame egresses only on GigabitEthernet 0/1 (not flooded to other ACs); `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-100` persists the mac-address-static line.
f300efdabfa2b12b.expectation:
Pass:    00:11:22:33:44:55 appears in MAC table with type "static" on GigabitEthernet 0/1; test frame forwarded exclusively to that interface (no flood); static binding survives `reload` and overrides any dynamic learning attempts for the same MAC.
Fail-on: Commit rejected with parse error; MAC appears as "learned" instead of "static"; test frame flooded to multiple VPLS interfaces or peers; dynamic MAC learning for 00:11:22:33:44:55 overwrites the static entry; configuration absent from running-config post-commit.

f3624ac9b65642b4.action_steps:
Setup:  Physical topology with x-eth 0/0/1, x-eth 0/0/2 cabled; DUT in factory config.
Action: Configure LACP and create agg-eth 0 interface; under `configuration interface agg-eth 0` issue `ethernet-segment`; under `configuration l2-services` issue `evpn evpn-1 service-type vlan-aware-bundle`; configure auto-discovery with import-rt and export-rt route targets; commit.
Verify: `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` shows ethernet-segment; `show running-config l2-services` shows evpn instance evpn-1; `show lacp` shows operational aggregation.
f3624ac9b65642b4.equipment: DUT only (read-back via show running-config and show lacp)
f3624ac9b65642b4.expectation:
Pass:    All four mandatory components configured and present in running-config: (1) LACP operational on agg-eth 0, (2) ethernet-segment under agg-eth 0, (3) evpn instance evpn-1 with service-type, (4) auto-discovery with route targets; commit succeeds.
Fail-on: Config commit fails, any mandatory component missing from running-config, evpn instance not instantiated, or LACP bundle not operational.

f3705e85a315e402.action_steps:
Setup:  Two PEs (DUT + neighbor) with multi-homed ES on `agg-eth 0` (same ESI). DUT syslog collector configured. Enable MAC duplication detection: `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-count 3 move-interval 10 freeze-time 60`. Configure static MAC: `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface agg-eth 0`. IXIA connected to CE behind multi-homed ES.
Action: (1) Neighbor PE: change DF algorithm while keeping ESI → trigger ES misconfiguration alarm. (2) IXIA: move MAC-A >3 times within 10s → temporary freeze alarm. (3) Continue moving MAC-A through freeze-time → permanent freeze alarm. (4) IXIA: send frames from 00:11:22:33:44:55 via different port → static MAC relearn alarm. After each trigger: `show alarms` and verify syslog collector.
Verify: `show alarms` displays all 4 alarms with complete fields: ESI value, `agg-eth 0`, peer IP address, LACP System MAC, DF algorithm (for ES misconfiguration); MAC addresses and peer IPs (for duplication and static relearn). Syslog collector receives structured entries (timestamp, severity, facility, feature, all alarm-specific fields). Resolve each condition; confirm alarm clears within ≤30s via `show alarms` polling.
f3705e85a315e402.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + syslog collector
f3705e85a315e402.expectation:
Pass:    All 4 alarms (ES misconfiguration with ESI/IP/LACP-MAC/DF-algorithm; MAC temporary freeze; MAC permanent freeze; static MAC relearn with MAC/IP) appear in `show alarms` and syslog with correct severity and complete structured fields. Each alarm clears within ≤30s of resolution (DF corrected, MAC stops moving, static traffic stopped).
Fail-on: Any alarm missing from `show alarms` or syslog, wrong severity, incomplete fields (missing ESI/agg-eth/IP/LACP-MAC/DF-algorithm for ES misconfiguration; missing MAC/IP for duplication/relearn), or alarm persists >30s after condition resolved.

f37caede9500bedf.action_steps:
Setup:  Enter configuration mode. Create and configure interface `agg-eth 0`; enter `ethernet-segment` sub-mode under that interface and configure an ESI (per documented identifier type). Commit the configuration.
Action: At the operational CLI prompt, execute `show evpn ethernet-segments`.
Verify: Parse the command output; confirm it lists the configured ES with its ESI value, bound interface (`agg-eth 0`), operational status, and ES type/attributes.
f37caede9500bedf.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
f37caede9500bedf.expectation:
Pass:    Output displays the configured Ethernet Segment entry with correct ESI, interface binding `agg-eth 0`, status field, and type information.
Fail-on: Command returns empty or error when ES configured; ESI missing or incorrect; interface not shown; status or type fields absent.

f3ee0f9b2390b1b3.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP configured, at least one neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family enabled; neighbor session reaches Established state.
Action: Issue `show bgp neighbors brief` to display neighbor summary table. Issue `show bgp neighbors detail` (or specify a neighbor IP) to display full session data. Repeat both commands on stable state to confirm idempotence.
Verify: `brief` output displays neighbor IP, remote AS, session state, uptime, EVPN prefix counts in tabular format. `detail` output includes BGP timers, capabilities negotiated (including EVPN capability), message statistics, address-families active. Repeated invocations produce identical output when system state unchanged.
f3ee0f9b2390b1b3.expectation:
Pass:    Both `brief` and `detail` commands execute without error; `brief` table includes neighbor IP / AS / state / EVPN prefix counters; `detail` includes capabilities (L2VPN/EVPN), timers, message counts; output idempotent on stable state.
Fail-on: Command returns parser error, mandatory fields missing (neighbor IP, state, AS, prefix counts for brief; capabilities, timers for detail), output format deviation, or non-idempotent output on unchanged system.

f3febe9e2bdb3df1.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with 100 EVIs (vlan-aware-bundle service-type), assign 10 ethernet-segments with unique non-zero ESIs (10-octet) across agg-eth interfaces. Enable auto-discovery per EVI (l2-services/evpn/auto-discovery). Peer DUT with IXIA simulating neighbor PE via BGP EVPN L2VPN address-family.
Action: Trigger A-D per EVI route advertisement at scale: (1) enable auto-discovery on all 100 EVIs, (2) flap 5 ethernet-segments concurrently (shut/no shut on agg-eth). Measure time from interface-up to BGP UPDATE transmission, total routes advertised, DUT CPU/memory during convergence.
Verify: IXIA captures BGP UPDATEs; decode route-type 1 NLRI to confirm RD format (unique per EVI or per PE per §7), ESI field = 10 octets, Next Hop = DUT loopback IPv4/v6. On DUT: `show bgp evpn route-type 1 detail` to validate RD/ESI/Next-Hop correctness, `show evpn ethernet-segment` for ESI binding, `show system resources` for CPU/memory.
f3febe9e2bdb3df1.expectation:
Pass:    1000 A-D per EVI routes (100 EVIs × 10 ESIs) advertised within 3 seconds of config commit or interface recovery. All routes have: RD set per configured policy (not 0:0), ESI length exactly 10 octets, Next Hop = DUT's loopback IPv4 or IPv6 address. No routes with ESI=0 (per §8.4.1, ESI=0 means A-D route not needed). CPU <75%, memory delta <400 MB, no dropped BGP UPDATEs.
Fail-on: Advertisement latency >5 seconds; any route with malformed RD (0:0 or missing), ESI length ≠10 octets, Next Hop ≠ DUT IP address; presence of A-D routes for ESI=0; CPU sustained >85%; memory spike >600 MB; missing routes (count ≠ EVIs × non-zero ESIs).

f4025e07f67cc1f7.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00, route-target 65000:100 import/export. Enable BGP neighbor X.X.X.X with `af-l2vpn evpn` address-family. Configure neighbor PE (scriptable BGP speaker) to inject malformed EVPN routes with duplicate extended communities.
Action: Neighbor PE sends three BGP UPDATEs: (1) Type-1 Ethernet A-D route with duplicate ESI Label ECs (first=1000, second=2000); (2) Type-1 route with duplicate ES Import RT ECs (first=65000:200, second=65000:300); (3) Type-2 MAC/IP route (MAC 00:00:5e:00:53:01) with duplicate MAC Mobility ECs (first seq=5, second seq=10). Capture BGP UPDATEs at DUT ingress.
Verify: Run `show bgp evpn route` to confirm all three routes are installed (not withdrawn). Run `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show evpn mac` to inspect EC values in operational state. Verify DUT uses only FIRST instance of each duplicate EC type: ESI Label=1000 (not 2000), ES Import RT=65000:200 (not 300), MAC Mobility seq=5 (not 10).
f4025e07f67cc1f7.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (scriptable BGP speaker e.g., ExaBGP for crafting duplicate ECs) + IXIA traffic gen
f4025e07f67cc1f7.expectation:
Pass:    All three routes accepted and installed in DUT EVPN RIB. Operational tables show only first EC instance per RFC7432bis §7.14.2: ESI Label 1000, ES Import RT 65000:200, MAC Mobility seq 5. BGP session stable, no treat-as-withdraw.
Fail-on: Any route withdrawn; DUT processes wrong EC instance (2nd/3rd instead of 1st); multiple EC instances visible in show outputs; BGP session reset; or forwarding uses incorrect label/seq values.

f42e75c716798929.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with a shared Ethernet Segment (ES1) to CE1 using LACP on `agg-eth 0` interfaces on both PEs. Establish BGP EVPN peering between PE1 and PE2 with ingress-replication. Verify ESI Label allocated and advertised in Type-1 Auto-Discovery routes (ESI Label extended community per RFC7432bis §7.5) using `show bgp l2vpn evpn`.
Action: From IXIA behind PE1, inject BUM traffic (broadcast or unknown-unicast) into the EVPN VLAN. PE1 encapsulates with the ESI Label for ES1 and forwards to PE2. Monitor PE2's egress on `agg-eth 0` (shared ES interface to CE1). Then inject BUM originating from a different, non-shared ES and observe forwarding.
Verify: Capture on PE2's `agg-eth 0`: zero BUM packets egress when carrying the shared-ES1 ESI Label (split-horizon per RFC7432bis ch.8.3). BUM from non-shared ES forwards normally. Confirm ES1 operational via `show evpn ethernet-segment`; verify MAC learning via `show evpn mac address-table`.
f42e75c716798929.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
f42e75c716798929.expectation:
Pass:    BUM traffic carrying shared-ES1 ESI Label is dropped at PE2's egress interface `agg-eth 0` (no loop to CE1). BUM from non-shared ES forwards to all local interfaces. ESI Label extended community present in Type-1 AD routes.
Fail-on: BUM from shared ES1 egresses PE2's `agg-eth 0` creating a loop, or non-shared-ES BUM incorrectly dropped, or ESI Label missing from BGP advertisements.

f449d5616b3c46ff.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT and neighbor PE each with EVPN instance 100, agg-eth 1 toward shared LACP CE. Set `ethernet-segment / identifier 0 00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88 / load-balancing-mode single-active` (default) on both. Establish EVPN BGP peering. IXIA sends bidirectional L2 traffic (VLAN 100, MACs 00:00:00:00:00:01 local CE, 00:00:00:00:00:02 remote CE). Verify MACT entries: local MAC → attachment circuit agg-eth 1 ifIndex, remote MAC → EVPN label (LT1) from BGP Type-2 route.
Action: During active traffic, execute `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1 / ethernet-segment / load-balancing-mode all-active` on DUT. Poll `show l2-services evpn mac-table` every 1s for 10s. Capture `show interface agg-eth 1 ethernet-segment` for ESI state, `show bgp evpn route-type mac-ip vlan-id 100` for Type-2 route updates. IXIA measures packet loss and forwarding interruption.
Verify: MACT entries persist: VLAN-ID=100 (key), MAC=00:00:00:00:00:01 (key, local next-hop agg-eth 1), MAC=00:00:00:00:00:02 (key, remote next-hop EVPN label), ESI=00.11.22.33.44.55.66.77.88. BGP Type-2 routes re-advertised with all-active A-D per-EVI. IXIA captures confirm both PEs forward ingress traffic (all-active multihoming operational).
f449d5616b3c46ff.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner (CE dual-homed to both PEs via agg-eth)
f449d5616b3c46ff.expectation:
Pass:    All MACT entries survive ESI mode change with VLAN-ID, MAC Address, ESI, and Next-Hop fields intact. Local MAC next-hop remains attachment circuit ifIndex, remote MAC next-hop remains EVPN label. ESI transition completes within 2× es-waiting-time (≤ 6s; default es-waiting-time=3s per CLI doc). Packet loss ≤ 150 packets. Post-transition: IXIA port stats show traffic load-balanced across DUT and neighbor PE (all-active verified).
Fail-on: Any MACT entry deleted, or VLAN-ID / MAC Address / ESI / Next-Hop field corrupted/missing. ESI load-balancing-mode stuck at single-active after >10s. Traffic blackhole >5s post-BGP convergence. Only one PE forwards traffic (all-active not operational; captured via IXIA Rx port counters). BGP Type-2 routes missing all-active ESI import RT or A-D per-EVI route absent.

f457dcadb77ab2a9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure neighbor PE advertising MAC/IP routes for two remote CEs: CE1 behind ESI=0 (single-homed), CE2 behind non-reserved ESI. Configure DUT with local access interface agg-eth 0 using `identifier 1`. IXIA simulating traffic from DUT-attached hosts to both remote CEs at 1 Gbps for ≥ 1 min.
Action: On DUT, kill EVPN control-plane process (`show platform process | include evpn` → platform kill command). This simulates PE-to-CE adjacency loss from control-plane perspective. Capture BGP UPDATE sequence during recovery via `tcpdump -i <bgp-interface> 'port 179' -w /tmp/bgp.pcap`. Poll `show evpn mac` every 1 s with timestamps. Track route arrival sequence: (1) CE1 MAC/IP, (2) CE2 MAC/IP, (3) CE2 Ethernet A-D per ES.
Verify: Correlate BGP captures with mac-table state. CE1 MAC (ESI=0) installs forwarding immediately after receiving MAC/IP route (step 1), independent of Ethernet A-D per ES routes. CE2 MAC (non-reserved ESI) installs forwarding only after receiving BOTH MAC/IP (step 2) AND Ethernet A-D per ES (step 3). Process restarts ≤ 5 s, reconvergence ≤ 30 s, IXIA interruption ≤ 1 s. `show alarms` clears post-recovery.
f457dcadb77ab2a9.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen (process-kill harness + BGP packet capture)
f457dcadb77ab2a9.expectation:
Pass:    ESI=0 MAC forwarding installs from MAC/IP route alone, before Ethernet A-D per ES arrival. Non-reserved ESI MAC forwarding installs only after receiving both route types. Process restart ≤ 5 s, reconvergence ≤ 30 s, data interruption ≤ 1 s, no persistent alarms.
Fail-on: ESI=0 forwarding waits for Ethernet A-D per ES, or non-reserved ESI forwarding installs prematurely (before Ethernet A-D per ES), or restart failure, or recovery > 30 s, or interruption > 1 s, or alarms persist.

f46a7edd95a02016.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT with multi-homing: assign non-zero ESI (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) to access interface, enable `advertise-mac enable` (default), configure BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn`, set `export-rt` and `import-rt`. Connect IXIA to multi-homed interface, neighbor PE as BGP peer.
Action: From IXIA, send ARP request + IP traffic to trigger MAC+IP learning on DUT multi-homed interface. On neighbor PE, capture BGP UPDATE for Type 2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) route using `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type macip detail` or packet capture.
Verify: Parse received Type 2 route on neighbor PE and confirm presence of all mandatory fields: RD (DUT's RD), ESI (matches configured 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99), Ethernet Tag ID, MAC Address (IXIA's learned MAC), IP Address (IXIA's IP), MPLS Label LT1 (L2 label, non-zero), MPLS Label L3 (if IP present), Sequence Number, RTs (matching DUT's export-rt), Next Hop = DUT's PE loopback IP. Verify ESI is non-zero (not 00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00) since interface is multi-homed.
f46a7edd95a02016.expectation:
Pass:    Type 2 route contains all 11 mandatory fields (RD, ESI, ET ID, MAC, IP, LT1, L3 label, Seq#, RTs, Next Hop = DUT PE IP). ESI matches configured multi-homing value (non-zero). Labels LT1 allocated per EVPN instance (same for all MACs in one VPN, unique across VPNs).
Fail-on: Any mandatory field missing from Type 2 route; ESI = 00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00:00 despite multi-homed interface; Next Hop ≠ DUT's PE IP; LT1 label not allocated or incorrectly shared across different EVPN instances; MAC Mobility Extended Community present when first learn (should only appear on relearning).

f4ac654b4d5ec3d8.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with agg-eth 0 configured; physical members x-eth 0/0/1, x-eth 0/0/2 assigned; peer PE with LACP-enabled LAG connected to both members. Default LACP Administrative Key is the agg-eth interface number (0).
Action: Enter configuration / interface / agg-eth 0; configure lacp-key with value 100; `commit`. Wait for LACP negotiation with peer to complete. Verify, then issue `no lacp-key`; `commit` again.
Verify: `show running-config` includes lacp-key 100 under agg-eth 0; LACP operational status shows Administrative Key = 100 and Active/Aggregated state. After `no lacp-key`, running-config omits the lacp-key line; LACP status shows Key = 0.
f4ac654b4d5ec3d8.expectation:
Pass:    lacp-key 100 accepted and persists; LACP status displays Key = 100; aggregation succeeds with peer; `no lacp-key` restores default (interface number 0)
Fail-on: Configuration error; lacp-key absent or incorrect in running-config or LACP status; LACP negotiation fails; `no` form does not restore default

f54a451c184a1857.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as PE with EVPN instance, ES identifier type 4 (default), load-balancing-mode single-active, and ingress replication for BUM traffic. Establish BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE. Start unknown unicast traffic from neighbor PE via MPLS with downstream label in P-tunnel attribute. Verify BUM frames flooded to ES without MAC lookup.
Action: Power-cycle DUT via PDU harness while neighbor PE continuously sends unknown unicast MPLS packets. Monitor ES waiting time (default 3 seconds) and BGP session recovery.
Verify: show evpn ethernet-segment, show l2-services evpn, show bgp evpn summary. Capture forwarded BUM frames on ES interface; confirm flooding resumes without MAC lookup.
f54a451c184a1857.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE + IXIA traffic gen + power-cycle harness
f54a451c184a1857.expectation:
Pass:    ES configuration intact (identifier type 4, single-active mode, es-waiting-time 3 seconds), BGP EVPN session re-establishes, unknown unicast MPLS packets flooded to ES without destination MAC lookup (default behavior per RFC7432bis §13.2.1)
Fail-on: ES configuration lost, BGP session fails to re-establish, BUM traffic blocked/dropped, or DUT incorrectly performs MAC lookup on unknown unicast (non-default behavior)

f587e7f99912ec8f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as single-homing PE (no ethernet-segment, simple interface to CE1 in EVI-100). Configure two neighbor PEs (PE2, PE3) as multihoming pair: `ethernet-segment; identifier 0 <9-octet-hex>; load-balancing-mode all-active` serving CE2 in same EVI-100. Establish BGP EVPN peering between DUT and PE2/PE3.
Action: Verify DUT receives Ethernet A-D routes (type 1 per-ES, per-EVI) from PE2/PE3 via `show bgp evpn route type 1`. Send bidirectional L2 unicast traffic CE1 ↔ CE2 (1000 pps, 5 min). Inject MAC move event (move MAC-A from CE1 to CE2) and observe forwarding.
Verify: `show bgp evpn route type 1 | include <ESI>` confirms DUT installed A-D routes with ESI label. `show evpn mac-table evi 100` shows MAC-A learned via multihoming next-hop. IXIA captures confirm traffic reaches CE2 via either PE2 or PE3 (aliasing), and return traffic arrives at DUT without duplicate frames (split-horizon honored). `show evpn next-hop` lists PE2 and PE3 as aliased next-hops for the ESI.
f587e7f99912ec8f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT installs Ethernet A-D routes from multihoming PEs (ESI label present in route). Traffic forwarded to CE2 using either PE2 or PE3 (aliasing active). Return traffic reaches CE1 with zero duplicate frames (split-horizon working). MAC move completes in <1s without loops. All procedures in RFC7432bis §8.7 items 1–3 (A-D route processing, aliasing, split-horizon) demonstrated.
Fail-on: DUT logs "unsupported route type" or discards A-D routes. Traffic black-holes or loops continuously (>5 duplicate frames). DUT forwards return traffic back to PE2/PE3 (split-horizon violation). MAC move triggers L2 loop or >10s convergence.

f597168649c21d47.action_steps:
Setup:  Create interface agg-eth 1, no lacp-system-mac configured; capture the LAG's own MAC address from `show interface agg-eth 1 | include Hardware`.
Action: Verify default with `show lacp 1 internal`; configure `lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55`; check `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` and `show lacp 1 internal` for the custom MAC; issue `no lacp-system-mac`; verify `show lacp 1 internal` reverts to the LAG MAC and running-config omits the line.
Verify: `show lacp 1 internal | include System` displays the effective LACP system MAC; `show running-config interface agg-eth 1` shows presence/absence of lacp-system-mac line; configure the original LAG MAC explicitly and confirm running-config remains unchanged (idempotent).
f597168649c21d47.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
f597168649c21d47.expectation:
Pass:    Default (no config): LACP System MAC = LAG hardware MAC. Custom config: LACP System MAC = 00:11:22:33:44:55 and line present in running-config. After `no lacp-system-mac`: System MAC reverts to LAG MAC, line absent from running-config. Explicit config of default value is idempotent (no change to running-config).
Fail-on: Default System MAC ≠ LAG MAC; custom MAC not reflected in `show lacp` output or running-config; `no` form does not restore LAG MAC; explicit default config alters running-config (non-idempotent).

f5a1c3b2fe81e7d6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (`evpn EVPN1 service-type vlan-based`) with `auto-discovery`, `export-rt 65000:100`, `import-rt 65000:100`; configure Ethernet Segment ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11 on LAG interface; establish BGP neighbor with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled; run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` and record ESI Label value from ESI Label Extended Community; verify all Type 1 routes for this ESI carry identical ESI Label (RFC7432bis 8.2.1 MUST requirement).
Action: Execute `onie-install http://image-server/evpn-next.bin`; system reboots to new image.
Verify: Run `show running-config` confirms auto-discovery config persisted; run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11` shows Type 1 routes re-advertised with RD, ESI key, ET ID=MAX-ET, MPLS Label=0, ESI Label Extended Community; confirm ESI Label value matches pre-upgrade and is uniform across all Type 1 routes for the ES; from neighbor PE inject Type 1 route with same ESI but different ESI Label; verify DUT raises ESI label mismatch alarm and does not use conflicting route until resolved.
f5a1c3b2fe81e7d6.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN peering + Type 1 route injection for mismatch testing) + ONIE image server
f5a1c3b2fe81e7d6.expectation:
Pass:    Post-upgrade Type 1 routes advertised with ESI Label value identical to pre-upgrade and uniform across all routes for the same ES (per RFC7432bis 8.2.1); ESI label mismatch alarm mechanism operational (alarm raised on label conflict, cleared on resolution); auto-discovery config and BGP EVPN peering restored.
Fail-on: Type 1 routes not advertised post-upgrade, ESI Label value differs across Type 1 routes for same ES or changed from pre-upgrade value, ESI label mismatch alarm not triggered on label conflict, auto-discovery config lost/corrupted, or BGP EVPN session fails to re-establish.

f5a31150a45c24d8.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT booted; no existing EVPN configuration (confirm `show running-config l2-services` is empty or has no evpn stanzas); CLI session established.
Action: `configure` → `l2-services`. Create one EVPN instance per service-type to verify all four mutex options:
  1. `evpn EVPN-VB service-type vlan-based` → confirm prompt changes to `(config-l2-services-evpn)#` → `exit`
  2. `evpn EVPN-VBU service-type vlan-bundle` → confirm prompt → `exit`
  3. `evpn EVPN-VAB service-type vlan-aware-bundle` → confirm prompt → `exit`
  4. `evpn EVPN-PB service-type port-based` → confirm prompt → `exit`
Commit config. Test `no evpn EVPN-VB` to verify deletion, then re-add and commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services` contains all four evpn stanzas with correct service-type assignments; `show evpn` or `show evpn instance` lists all active instances.
f5a31150a45c24d8.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
f5a31150a45c24d8.expectation:
Pass:    All four service-type values (vlan-based, vlan-bundle, vlan-aware-bundle, port-based) parse and commit successfully; prompt transitions to (config-l2-services-evpn)# on each evpn command; running-config reflects exact service-type per instance; `no evpn` cleanly removes instance; re-add succeeds.
Fail-on: Syntax error on any service-type, commit rejection, prompt does not enter EVPN config mode, service-type value stored incorrectly or defaults unexpectedly, `no evpn` fails to remove instance, or show commands return empty/incorrect data.

f5c05453fd1d429f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance 100 (`configuration / l2-services / evpn instance 100`, route-target 100:100, `advertise-mac enable` default); establish BGP sessions to PE2 (10.0.0.2, local-pref 200 outbound) and PE3 (10.0.0.3, local-pref 100 outbound); both PEs advertise EVPN route-type 2 for MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11, with MPLS labels 100200 (PE2) and 100300 (PE3).
Action: PE2 sends BGP UPDATE with EVPN route-type 2, local-pref 200, label 100200; PE3 sends same route-type 2, local-pref 100, label 100300; DUT receives both and applies RFC7432bis ch.7.13 best-path selection (local-pref comparison at tie-break step 4).
Verify: `show bgp evpn route-type 2 00:11:22:33:44:55` confirms '>' best-path marker for PE2's route; `show evpn mac 00:11:22:33:44:55` shows MPLS label 100200 installed in forwarding; IXIA sends L2 traffic to MAC, capture confirms MPLS encapsulation uses label 100200.
f5c05453fd1d429f.equipment: DUT + two neighbor PEs (PE2 10.0.0.2, PE3 10.0.0.3, both advertise EVPN) + IXIA traffic gen
f5c05453fd1d429f.expectation:
Pass:    DUT selects PE2's route (local-pref 200 > 100) per RFC7432bis ch.7.13 best-path algorithm; `show bgp evpn` displays '>' marker only for PE2 (10.0.0.2), 'not best' for PE3; forwarding table installs single label 100200; IXIA traffic to MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 encapsulated with label 100200, reaches destination.
Fail-on: DUT selects PE3's route (local-pref 100, violates RFC7432bis ch.7.13); both labels 100200 and 100300 active in forwarding without ECMP configured; no '>' marker shown (best-path algorithm not applied); traffic encapsulated with label 100300 or black-holes.

f60977fcbb62fafd.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'cw-test' service-type vlan-based EVI 100; establish BGP EVPN peering with neighbor PE at 10.0.0.2; bind AC interface (e.g., GigabitEthernet0/1.100) to EVPN. Connect IXIA to AC port. Verify default state: `show evpn cw-test detail` reports control-word disabled or absent.
Action: Navigate to mode `configuration / l2-services / evpn cw-test`, enter `control-word enable`, commit config. Send 100 Ethernet frames from IXIA (VLAN 100, unique MAC SA) to trigger MAC learning. Capture MPLS packets on DUT's core interface during EVPN route exchange. Reconfigure `control-word disable` and repeat capture.
Verify: Parse captured MPLS packets: locate EVPN service label (bottom-of-stack bit=1), confirm when enabled next 4 bytes are PW control word (first nibble=0, RFC 4385 format) before Ethernet payload; when disabled, Ethernet frame immediately follows service label. Run `show evpn cw-test detail` after each config and verify control-word field reflects enabled or disabled state.
f60977fcbb62fafd.expectation:
Pass:    control-word enable: 100% of MPLS packets contain 4-byte control word (first nibble = 0x0, per RFC 4385) between service label and Ethernet frame; show command reports 'control-word: enabled'. control-word disable: control word absent, Ethernet follows service label directly; show reports 'disabled'.
Fail-on: Control word presence/absence does not match configured state; packet loss or corruption; MAC learning failures; EVPN route withdrawal/flap after control-word config change; show command output does not match running config.

f61c0265cea98d82.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance 'evpn <name> service-type vlan-aware-bundle' (l2-services mode); configure ethernet-segment on agg-eth interface; configure auto-discovery with import-rt/export-rt under vpls; establish BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE; verify type-1 Ethernet A-D per EVI routes advertised for each BD (VLAN) via 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1' showing RD, ESI, ET ID (VLAN-ID), MPLS label LT2, L2-Attr with Primary/Backup flags; IXIA sends continuous bidirectional traffic.
Action: Kill BGP/EVPN control-plane process (use platform debug command or 'kill -9 <bgpd-pid>') to test RFC7432bis ch.8.4: per-EVI routes MUST NOT be used for forwarding until associated per-ES routes (type-4) are received.
Verify: Process auto-restarts; BGP session re-establishes; 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1' confirms per-EVI routes re-advertised for all BDs with matching RD/ESI/ET/label; 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4' confirms per-ES routes present; neighbor PE receives type-4 before using type-1 for forwarding; IXIA measures traffic loss duration.
f61c0265cea98d82.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN peer) + IXIA traffic gen (process-kill harness)
f61c0265cea98d82.expectation:
Pass:    Process restarts ≤ 10s; type-1 routes re-advertised for all BDs with correct RD, ESI, ET ID (VLAN-ID), label LT2 (per <EVI, ES, ET>), L2-Attr Extended Community (Primary/Backup flags); neighbor PE receives type-4 routes before using type-1 per RFC7432bis MUST; traffic loss ≤ 3s.
Fail-on: Restart > 10s; type-1 routes missing/incomplete (not all BDs) or incorrect fields (ESI, ET ID, label, flags); neighbor PE uses type-1 before type-4 received (RFC violation); traffic loss > 3s or no recovery.

f66e54957f650961.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 loopback 192.0.2.1/32; configure EVPN instance with RD 192.0.2.1:100 (Type 1); configure Ethernet Segment with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on LAG interface; enable iBGP session to PE2 with `af-l2vpn evpn` enabled.
Action: Bring up the Ethernet Segment interface; capture BGP UPDATE on PE2 using tcpdump filtering on TCP port 179; wait for route type 4 (Ethernet Segment Route) advertisement.
Verify: Decode captured UPDATE with Wireshark EVPN dissector or manual hex parse: RD type field (offset +0) = 0x01, RD value = 0xC0000201:0064 (192.0.2.1:100); ESI field (offset +8) = 0x00112233445566778899; Originating Router IP Address field = 0xC0000201 (192.0.2.1), IP Address Length = 32.
f66e54957f650961.expectation:
