Pass:    RD type = 0x01 (Type 1 per RFC4364 §4.2), RD value encodes PE1 loopback 192.0.2.1 in first 4 octets; ESI = configured 10-octet value 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99; Originating Router IP = 192.0.2.1 (PE1 loopback).
Fail-on: RD type = 0x00 (Type 0 ASN-based) or 0x02 (Type 2); ESI field truncated, zero-filled, or mismatched; Originating Router IP = 0.0.0.0, physical interface IP, or different from RD IP.

f69b4391475b6654.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS instance with `mac-limit 10`; attach x-eth 0/0/1 to the instance; commit.
Action: Send Ethernet frames from IXIA on x-eth 0/0/1 with 15 unique source MAC addresses (00:00:00:00:00:01, 00:00:00:00:00:02, ..., 00:00:00:00:00:0F) at a rate allowing address learning.
Verify: `show mac-address-table` for the VPLS instance; `show l2-services` counters; syslog for MAC limit messages.
f69b4391475b6654.expectation:
Pass:    MAC table for the instance shows exactly 10 learned addresses (first 10 source MACs); MACs 11-15 are absent; logs confirm MAC learning limit reached.
Fail-on: More than 10 MACs in the table; OR fewer than 10 learned when sending valid unique MACs within the limit; OR limit not enforced.

f6bfcec9cba3c94d.action_steps:
Setup:  DUT with BGP neighbor configured under `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn`; neighbor PE sends 10+ EVPN Type-2 routes with RT 64512:100; initial inbound policy accepts all; BGP session established.
Action: Issue `inbound-soft-reconfiguration` under the neighbor af-l2vpn evpn context; commit; verify session remains up; modify inbound policy to filter routes with RT 64512:100 via route-map; issue `clear bgp <neighbor-ip> soft in` or equivalent soft-refresh command.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` confirms session uptime unchanged (no reset); `show bgp l2vpn evpn` shows filtered routes removed from RIB per new policy; `show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <neighbor-ip>` indicates soft-reconfiguration enabled and displays cached route count.
f6bfcec9cba3c94d.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (supports EVPN) + IXIA traffic gen
f6bfcec9cba3c94d.expectation:
Pass:    Inbound policy re-applied without BGP session reset (session uptime counter unchanged); route table updated to reflect new filter; neighbor status shows soft-reconfiguration active with non-zero cached route count.
Fail-on: BGP session resets (uptime counter zeroed); policy change ignored (all routes still in RIB); soft-reconfiguration status shows disabled; or commit fails with memory allocation error.

f6cd83b9a357a0ea.action_steps:
Setup:  Two-PE topology in single EVPN instance; PE1 configured with 1,000 Ethernet Segments (ES), each mapped to unique sub-interface (ET ≠ 0); enable `advertise-mac enable` (default) and `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbors. PE1 advertises Ethernet A-D per EVI (Type 1) routes with LT2 label per ES (RFC7432bis §7.1). IXIA scale rig connected to PE2 generates 100K known-unicast flows at line-rate (64-byte frames).
Action: IXIA transmits traffic toward MACs behind PE1 ESs for which no MAC/IP (Type 2) routes exist; PE2 forwards using LT2 labels per RFC7432bis §8.4. Run for 60 seconds; measure throughput, latency (avg/P99), packet loss.
Verify: PE2 `show evpn route type 1` displays 1,000 Type 1 routes with unique LT2 labels; packet capture confirms MPLS stack contains LT2; PE1 `show evpn label` shows LT2-to-ES/ET mappings; IXIA reports metrics; PE1 interface counters confirm packets delivered to correct sub-interfaces per LT2→ES/ET mapping.
f6cd83b9a357a0ea.expectation:
Pass:    Throughput ≥ 95% line-rate (10G/40G/100G per IXIA spec), avg latency ≤ 1 ms, P99 latency ≤ 5 ms, 0% packet loss; all 1,000 LT2 labels allocated and advertised; MPLS encapsulation includes correct LT2; PE1 maps LT2 to correct ES/ET sub-interface 100% of packets.
Fail-on: Throughput < 95% line-rate, avg latency > 1 ms, P99 > 5 ms, any packet loss, LT2 label missing or incorrect in Type 1 routes or MPLS stack, PE1 delivers packets to wrong ES or sub-interface.

f703827e76c66ff2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS vpls-100: `configure; l2-services; vpls vpls-100; auto-discovery; import-rt 65000:100; export-rt 65000:200; commit; end`. Verify both RTs in `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-100 auto-discovery`.
Action: Remove auto-discovery content: `configure; l2-services; vpls vpls-100; no auto-discovery; commit; end`.
Verify: Check `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-100` for absence of import-rt and export-rt lines. Check `show l2-services vpls vpls-100` confirms instance still exists.
f703827e76c66ff2.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
f703827e76c66ff2.expectation:
Pass:    `show running-config` contains vpls-100 but zero occurrences of 'import-rt 65000:100' or 'export-rt 65000:200' under auto-discovery; vpls-100 appears in `show l2-services vpls` output.
Fail-on: Running-config still contains 'import-rt 65000:100' or 'export-rt 65000:200', or vpls-100 deleted entirely, or commit fails with error.

f71f37853edfdea2.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two EVPN instances: `configure / l2-services / evpn EVPN-100 service-type vlan-based` and `evpn EVPN-200 service-type vlan-bundle`, then commit.
Action: Execute `show evpn global` at the operational prompt.
Verify: Output lists both EVPN instances (EVPN-100, EVPN-200) with their respective service types and displays global EVPN operational state.
f71f37853edfdea2.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
f71f37853edfdea2.expectation:
Pass:    Output displays both configured EVPN instance names and their service-types; command executes successfully.
Fail-on: Command fails, missing instance names from output, incorrect service-type mapping, or displays zero instances when two are configured.

f76d6e145e3cc6c4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance (EVI 100) on DUT with 2+ remote PEs advertising IMET routes (type 3) with differing BGP attributes (PE1 local-pref 200, PE2 local-pref 100). Establish BGP sessions and verify route reception.
Action: Trigger `show tech-support` command to collect diagnostic bundle after IMET best path selection stabilizes.
Verify: Unpack tech-support archive and confirm presence of: `show bgp evpn type 3`, `show bgp evpn type 3 detail`, `show evpn instance`, running-config (`configuration / l2-services / evpn`), syslog, and multicast forwarding tables.
f76d6e145e3cc6c4.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle includes: (1) `show bgp evpn type 3` output showing ≥2 IMET routes for EVI 100 with best-path selection marker ('>'), (2) `show bgp evpn type 3 detail` containing BGP decision reason per RFC7432bis §7.13.1 step 5 order, (3) running-config with `advertise-mac enable`, `control-word`, `unknown-mac-flooding enable`, (4) forwarding state consistent with selected IMET.
Fail-on: Tech-support missing `show bgp evpn type 3`, no best-path marker, missing decision criteria, incomplete EVPN config, or forwarding entries inconsistent with BGP selection.

f76dfa8d7027a037.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 and PE2 configured in all-active ES with matching ESI (LACP-derived via `identifier 1`); EVI operational on both; IXIA emulates LACP CE with LAG to both PEs.
Action: Configure PE1: `interface agg-eth 0; ethernet-segment; identifier 1; load-balancing-mode all-active; service-carving highest-random-weight`. Configure PE2: `interface agg-eth 0; ethernet-segment; identifier 1; load-balancing-mode all-active; service-carving highest-preference preference 50000`. Capture BGP UPDATEs (AFI/SAFI 25/70) on PE1↔PE2 link; bring ES operational; allow DF election to converge.
Verify: Decode Type 4 routes: confirm DF Election EC (Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x06 per RFC8584) present with 'DF Alg' = 0x01 (HRW) from PE1, 'DF Alg' = 0x03 (Highest Pref) + preference field = 50000 from PE2; ES-Import RT present; ESI LACP-derived per RFC7432bis §7.4. Decode Type 1 routes from both PEs. Confirm `show evpn ethernet-segment` reports DF elected via Default Algorithm (fallback due to DF Alg mismatch per EVPNS-REQ#120). Cross-check all NLRI and EC encoding byte-for-byte against RFC7432bis §7.4, RFC8584 §3, §4.
f76dfa8d7027a037.expectation:
Pass:    Type 4 routes carry DF Election EC with correct 'DF Alg' = 0x01 (PE1) and 0x03 (PE2); mismatch detected; fallback to Default Algorithm (RFC7432bis §8.5 modulo) confirmed via DF-per-VLAN election pattern; Type 1 routes present from both PEs; ES-Import RT present; all encoded fields match RFC7432bis §7.4 and RFC8584 §3, §4 byte-for-byte.
Fail-on: DF Election EC missing from Type 4 (RFC7432bis-only behavior, violates SFS multi-algorithm requirement), OR 'DF Alg' values incorrect (device doesn't support HRW or Highest Pref), OR no fallback to Default on mismatch (violates SFS fallback mandate), OR Type 1 or ES-Import RT missing, OR any encoded field deviates from RFC specifications.

f78da5e65ef660d6.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure all-active multi-homed ES on DUT agg-eth interface (identifier 0 <9-octet-hex>, load-balancing-mode all-active, es-waiting-time 3s default) and neighbor PE; attach EVI 100 to ES; establish bidirectional traffic via IXIA through both PEs.
Action: Trigger ES link down on neighbor PE (simulate PE failure), wait for Mass Withdraw (RFC7432bis §8.2 Ethernet A-D per ES route type 4 withdrawal) to complete, then run `tech-support` on DUT.
Verify: Extract and inspect tech-support archive for: `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` (ES state, DF election), `show bgp evpn routes` (route type 4 withdrawal from neighbor PE), `show evpn mac`, `show evpn arp`, `show running-config | section ethernet-segment` (identifier/load-balancing-mode/es-waiting-time), syslog entries (ES down, Mass Withdraw trigger), FIB tables post-convergence.
f78da5e65ef660d6.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains ES identifier (type 0, 9 octets), load-balancing-mode all-active, es-waiting-time 3s, DF state transition, Ethernet A-D per ES route (type 4) withdrawal from failed PE with timestamp, MAC/ARP route updates showing convergence to DUT-only forwarding, syslog showing Mass Withdraw event within es-waiting-time window, and FIB snapshot reflecting traffic redirection to DUT.
Fail-on: Missing `show evpn ethernet-segment` output, absent BGP route type 4 withdrawal history, no Mass Withdraw or ES-down syslog entries, es-waiting-time value not captured, or FIB tables missing post-convergence state.

f7afc6b094550271.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed Ethernet Segment on DUT with ESI 01:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11:11; establish BGP EVPN session with IXIA emulating peer PE; verify `advertise-mac enable` (default) is active.
Action: IXIA injects malformed EVPN Type-1 (Ethernet Auto-Discovery) and Type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes: corrupted ESI field length (13 bytes instead of 10), reserved ESI type 0xFF, all-zero ESI when DF election enabled, ESI Label field with invalid MPLS label value 0xFFFFF.
Verify: DUT rejects malformed routes (check `show bgp evpn routes rejected`, `show bgp evpn summary` peer state remains Established); no MAC entries created from corrupted ESI routes (`show l2-services evpn mac-table`); syslog shows ESI validation errors; EVPN process stable (`show system processes | grep evpn`).
f7afc6b094550271.expectation:
Pass:    All malformed ESI variants rejected; `show bgp evpn routes rejected` lists ≥4 entries with reason "ESI format invalid" or "ESI label out-of-range"; syslog contains "Split Horizon ESI validation failed" entries; legitimate traffic on valid ES continues with split-horizon enforced (no loopback to originating ES).
Fail-on: Crash/restart of evpn/bgp process; malformed ESI route accepted into RIB/FIB; split-horizon bypassed (BUM traffic forwarded back to source ES); BGP session flaps; missing error log; stale MAC entries from corrupted routes.

f826a35f77404eb4.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure a VPLS instance under `configuration / l2-services / vpls <name>`, enter `auto-discovery` mode, add `import-rt <route-target>` and `export-rt <route-target>`, commit.
Action: Verify auto-discovery is present with `show running-config l2-services`. Issue `no auto-discovery` under the VPLS config, commit.
Verify: Run `show running-config l2-services` and confirm the entire auto-discovery block (including import-rt and export-rt sub-commands) is removed; no orphaned statements remain.
f826a35f77404eb4.expectation:
Pass:    `no auto-discovery` removes the auto-discovery container and all nested sub-commands (import-rt, export-rt) atomically; VPLS instance remains but with no auto-discovery config.
Fail-on: Orphaned import-rt or export-rt statements persist, auto-discovery container remains, or CLI rejects the `no auto-discovery` command.

f82d7e16b87ca53f.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT EVPN instance 100 with BD 100, RD/RT, ESI on access port; enable unknown-mac-flooding (default); establish BGP EVPN session (af-l2vpn evpn) with neighbor PE; verify IMET routes exchanged via `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 3`.
Action: Send broadcast Ethernet frame from IXIA on DUT access port; capture BGP IMET route (route-type 3) for BD 100 on BGP session; capture MPLS-encapsulated BUM packet on PE↔PE link with Wireshark; retrieve LT3 label allocation with `show l2-services evpn instance 100 label`.
Verify: IMET route contains PMSI Tunnel attribute with non-zero LT3 label value; MPLS label stack in captured BUM packet matches LT3 label advertised in IMET PMSI Tunnel attribute; label is unique per-BD (RFC7432bis §7.3 compliance: label identifies DUT's non-ES interfaces + ES interfaces where DUT is DF).
f82d7e16b87ca53f.expectation:
Pass:    IMET route advertises PMSI Tunnel attribute with LT3 label; BUM packet forwarded via MPLS carries matching LT3 label in label stack; `show l2-services evpn instance 100 label` output matches captured IMET and data-plane label.
Fail-on: IMET route missing PMSI Tunnel attribute or LT3 label field = 0; MPLS label in captured BUM packet ≠ LT3 label from IMET; BUM packet dropped or forwarded without MPLS encapsulation on MPLS-enabled PE-PE link; label allocation not unique per-BD.

f8986a6a43bda237.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT1 and DUT2 as All-Active PEs for ES1 (agg-eth interface, `load-balancing-mode all-active`, `identifier 0 01.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.01`). Configure P2MP MPLS LSPs for BUM transport. Verify `show evpn ethernet-segment detail` displays upstream assigned ESI label in Ethernet A-D per ES routes from both PEs. Start IXIA BUM traffic (broadcast, 100 Mbps) from CE behind ES1.
Action: While BUM traffic is active, reset EVPN control-plane process on DUT1 (`platform restart evpn-manager` or equivalent).
Verify: Check `show mpls forwarding-table context-label` on DUT2: upstream assigned ESI label from DUT1 re-appears within es-waiting-time (default 3 s). Capture packets on DUT2's agg-eth to ES1: confirm zero BUM packets received with DUT1's upstream ESI label are forwarded back onto ES1 (RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2 split-horizon). Check IXIA Rx for duplicates/loops.
f8986a6a43bda237.equipment: Two PEs (All-Active multi-homing) + IXIA traffic gen
f8986a6a43bda237.expectation:
Pass:    DUT1's upstream assigned ESI label re-programmed on DUT2 in context label space within 3 s; forwarding entry enforces NOT forward to ES1 (split-horizon intact per RFC7432bis §8.3.1.2); BUM traffic loss ≤ 3 s; IXIA shows no duplicate frames.
Fail-on: Upstream ESI label missing from DUT2's MPLS table post-recovery, BUM packets with ESI label forwarded to originating ES1 (loop), traffic loss > 5 s, or EVPN control-plane does not recover.

f8b6098941107327.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT with EVPN instance evpn1 of service-type vlan-aware-bundle for VLANs 10-20. Attach access interfaces x-eth 0/0/1 and x-eth 0/0/2 to evpn1. Connect IXIA ports to both interfaces.
Action: From IXIA on x-eth 0/0/1, send broadcast frames (destination MAC FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF, 100 pps each) with VLAN-IDs 5 (outside bundle), 10 (inside), 15 (inside), 20 (inside), and 25 (outside bundle). Run for 60 seconds.
Verify: On IXIA port attached to x-eth 0/0/2, confirm that only frames with VLAN-IDs 10, 15, 20 are received; VLAN-IDs 5 and 25 show zero RX frames. Check `show evpn evi evpn1` to confirm no MAC entries exist for VLAN-IDs 5 or 25.
f8b6098941107327.expectation:
Pass:    BUM frames with VLAN-IDs 5 and 25 (outside the configured bundle 10-20) are dropped at ingress; IXIA RX count is zero for these VLANs on x-eth 0/0/2, and `show evpn evi evpn1` shows no MAC learning for VLANs 5 or 25. Frames with VLAN-IDs 10, 15, 20 are flooded normally.
Fail-on: Any BUM frame with VLAN-ID outside the bundle (5 or 25) is received on x-eth 0/0/2, or a MAC entry is learned for these VLANs. OR valid VLAN-IDs (10, 15, 20) are incorrectly dropped.

f926c6451d1c0523.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance using `evpn EVPN-TEST service-type vlan-based` with at least one interface attached as AC, commit configuration, and verify `show fib evpn-ac` displays forwarding entries.
Action: Run `show fib evpn-ac` unfiltered. Run `show fib evpn-ac | include EVPN-TEST`. Run `show fib evpn-ac | exclude EVPN-TEST`. Run `show fib evpn-ac | begin <column-header>` where header is from the actual output. Run `show fib evpn-ac | count`.
Verify: Unfiltered output shows AC entries with instance name, attached interface identifier, and forwarding state. `| include EVPN-TEST` returns only lines containing EVPN-TEST. `| exclude EVPN-TEST` returns output without those lines. `| begin` starts from specified header. `| count` returns line count or unsupported message.
f926c6451d1c0523.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
f926c6451d1c0523.expectation:
Pass:    Command displays EVPN-AC FIB entries showing instance, interface, and forwarding state; all pipe filters (include, exclude, begin, count) operate per CLI pipe semantics.
Fail-on: Command fails, returns empty output when EVPN-AC configured, missing entries for configured AC, or any pipe filter returns incorrect subset/breaks structure.

f9548f26772ef36b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 with identical `ethernet-segment` on `agg-eth 0`: `identifier 0 <type0-value>` (same 9-octet value), `load-balancing-mode single-active`. Attach CE to both PEs. Configure PE3 with EVPN to the same EVI.
Action: From PE3 (via IXIA), send known-unicast to MAC behind the multi-homed CE. Identify the DF via `show evpn ethernet-segment`, then shutdown the DF's `agg-eth 0` or BGP session. Capture BGP UPDATEs on PE3.
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` on PE3 lists Ethernet A-D per-EVI routes from both PE1 and PE2 (aliasing). After DF failure, traffic switches to non-DF PE; measure convergence time and packet loss via IXIA counters.
f9548f26772ef36b.expectation:
Pass:    PE3 installs A-D per-EVI routes from both PEs before failover (RFC7432bis ch.8.4 aliasing). On DF failure, traffic converges to non-DF PE within ≤ 1 s with < 100 ms black-hole.
Fail-on: PE3 installs only one A-D route (no aliasing path), or convergence > 1 s, or black-hole > 100 ms.

f95f4d91b641c55d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure agg-eth 0 interface. Enter configuration mode `configuration / interface / agg-eth 0`.
Action: Apply lacp-system-mac with MAC address 00:11:22:33:44:55 and commit. Then apply no lacp-system-mac and commit.
Verify: After first commit, `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` contains lacp-system-mac and `show lacp interface agg-eth 0` displays system MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55. After no form, `show lacp` confirms system MAC reverts to the LAG's default MAC address.
f95f4d91b641c55d.expectation:
Pass:    Configured MAC (00:11:22:33:44:55) appears in running-config and operational state. No form restores the LAG MAC address as the system MAC.
Fail-on: Configured MAC not applied to operational state, or no form fails to restore default, or configuration persists after no command.

f9c9281623f9b3a9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 (DUT) and PE2 in EVPN instance with matching RT; enable MAC duplication detection on PE1: `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300` (M=180s, N=5 moves). Attach IXIA ports emulating CE-A (behind PE1) and CE-B (behind PE2), both using MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55.
Action: Send ARP/traffic from MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 alternating between CE-A and CE-B to trigger 6 MAC mobility events within 180 seconds (exceeding N=5 threshold); capture BGP updates and check `show l2-services evpn mac-duplication` and `show log` on PE1.
Verify: After 6th move, PE1 logs MAC duplication alert (syslog event); PE1 stops sending BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes for 00:11:22:33:44:55 (verify `show bgp evpn route` shows no new sequence increments at PE2); MAC enters frozen state for 300s freeze-time; verify M, N, freeze-time are configurable by changing move-count to 3 and observing detection at 4th move.
f9c9281623f9b3a9.expectation:
Pass:    PE detects duplication after N=5 MAC moves within M=180 seconds, generates operator alert (syslog/SNMP trap), stops processing/advertising BGP routes for duplicate MAC during freeze-time=300s, and allows operator to modify M/N/freeze-time parameters via CLI.
Fail-on: No duplication alert after 6th move within threshold window, BGP MAC/IP Advertisement routes continue with incrementing sequence numbers beyond N moves, MAC not frozen after detection, or inability to configure/modify move-interval/move-count/freeze-time parameters.

fa609512a2c26301.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance evpn1 (service-type vlan-based), attach Ethernet1/1, configure export RT, establish BGP EVPN session with neighbor PE.
Action: Navigate to `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn1`, execute `mac-address-static 00:11:22:33:44:55 interface Ethernet1/1`, commit.
Verify: On DUT: `show evpn mac-table evpn1` (check MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55, Type=static, Interface=Ethernet1/1); `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 2` (check type-2 route with MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55). On neighbor PE: `show bgp l2vpn evpn` (verify route received).
fa609512a2c26301.equipment: DUT + neighbor PE (BGP EVPN peer to verify route advertisement)
fa609512a2c26301.expectation:
Pass:    Static MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 appears in DUT evpn1 MAC table (Type=static, Interface=Ethernet1/1); DUT advertises EVPN type-2 route containing MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 with correct RD and export RT; neighbor PE receives the route.
Fail-on: mac-address-static CLI rejected; MAC absent from table or Type≠static; no type-2 route advertised; neighbor PE does not receive route; route has wrong MAC/RD/RT.

faaeaf21d905ebf1.action_steps:
Setup:  Create EVPN instance (`evpn evpn100` under `configuration / l2-services`), configure BGP EVPN peering with a neighbor PE, attach at least one VLAN or port to the EVPN service.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn evpn100`, issue `control-word enable`, commit. Send MAC traffic through the EVPN, capture outbound MPLS packets on the DUT's core-facing interface (tcpdump or port-mirror). Reconfigure with `control-word disable`, commit, send traffic, capture again. Finally issue `no control-word` and verify default behavior.
Verify: Decode MPLS-encapsulated EVPN packets: when `enable` is active, each packet must contain a 4-byte Control Word between the MPLS label stack and the Ethernet payload. When `disable` or `no control-word` is active, payload starts immediately after the label stack. Check `show running-config l2-services evpn evpn100` to confirm only one setting at a time.
faaeaf21d905ebf1.expectation:
Pass:    Control Word presence in EVPN packets matches configured state (present when `enable`, absent when `disable` or default). Configuration changes take effect on commit without reload. Only one control-word setting in running-config.
Fail-on: Control Word presence contradicts the configured state, or both `enable` and `disable` appear simultaneously in running-config, or packet format unchanged after commit.

faaf1b0b7bda4f9b.action_steps:
Setup:  Create agg-eth 0 with member interface x-eth 0/0/1; enter configuration mode for interface agg-eth 0.
Action: Configure lacp-system-mac 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee; commit. Test invalid inputs: lacp-system-mac notahexstring, lacp-system-mac ZZ:ZZ:ZZ:ZZ:ZZ:ZZ, lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44 (5 octets), lacp-system-mac 00:11:22:33:44:55:66 (7 octets). Issue no lacp-system-mac; commit.
Verify: show running-config interface agg-eth 0 displays lacp-system-mac 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee; each invalid format rejected at CLI parse time with error message; after no form, explicit lacp-system-mac removed from running-config (LACP system-id defaults to LAG MAC address per requirement).
faaf1b0b7bda4f9b.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
faaf1b0b7bda4f9b.expectation:
Pass:    Valid MAC 00:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee committed and visible in running-config; all invalid formats rejected with parse error before commit; no form clears config, restoring default LAG MAC
Fail-on: Invalid MAC value stored in running-config, valid MAC rejected, or no form fails to remove configuration

fb11492cc1ab23ca.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `test-evpn` with service-type vlan-based; attach two local ports x-eth 0/0/1 (ingress) and x-eth 0/0/2 (egress peer); flush MAC table.
Action: Enter `configuration / l2-services / evpn test-evpn`, issue `unknow-mac-flooding disable`, commit. Send Ethernet frame from IXIA via x-eth 0/0/1 to unknown destination MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55. Issue `no unknow-mac-flooding`, commit. Resend same frame.
Verify: `show mac-address-table evpn test-evpn` confirms 00:11:22:33:44:55 not learned. `show interface x-eth 0/0/2 counters` egress packet count after first send (disabled state) vs. second send (default enable state). `show running-config evpn test-evpn` reflects `unknow-mac-flooding disable` when configured, omits line when default.
fb11492cc1ab23ca.expectation:
Pass:    When `unknow-mac-flooding disable` is active, unknown MAC frame generates zero egress packets on x-eth 0/0/2. After `no unknow-mac-flooding` restores default (enable), same frame increments x-eth 0/0/2 egress counter (flooded). Config output matches active setting.
Fail-on: Unknown MAC frame floods to x-eth 0/0/2 when disabled, OR frame is not flooded (zero egress delta) when enabled/default, OR `no` form does not restore flooding behavior, OR config does not reflect command state.

fb71938ba1519262.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN instance with 2+ local interfaces on multi-homed Ethernet Segment (e.g., eth1/1, eth1/2 on ES1); remote PE sends Type-1 Ethernet A-D per-ES route for ES1 with LT4 label; configure unknown-mac-flooding enable.
Action: Send unknown-unicast BUM frame from remote PE toward ES1, then collect tech-support bundle (or execute: show evpn split-horizon, show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1, show mpls forwarding-table, show evpn instance ethernet-segment, show running-config l2-services evpn).
Verify: Tech-support contains: (1) BUM Split Horizon Table entries (ifIndex → LT4 label per ES interface), (2) received Type-1 A-D per-ES routes with LT4 labels, (3) MPLS forwarding table with LT3/LT4 labels, (4) ES interface membership list, (5) unknown-mac-flooding enabled in running-config, (6) syslog for BUM flooding events.
fb71938ba1519262.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support includes: split-horizon table (ifIndex → LT4 mappings for each ES interface), Type-1 routes from remote PE with LT4 labels, MPLS forwarding entries for LT3/LT4, ES-to-interface mappings, unknown-mac-flooding enabled in config.
Fail-on: Missing split-horizon table, missing Type-1 route details with LT4 labels, missing MPLS forwarding entries, missing ES interface list, or unknown-mac-flooding config not captured.

fb750f01da845624.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance on DUT (vlan-based service-type), establish BGP peering with neighbor PE under af-l2vpn evpn, attach local interface x-eth 0/0/1 to the EVPN instance.
Action: Start IXIA traffic to/from x-eth 0/0/1 to learn source MAC. Issue `no advertise-mac` in EVPN instance context, wait for BGP convergence. Re-enable with `advertise-mac`.
Verify: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn` and capture BGP UPDATEs at neighbor PE; confirm MAC route advertised by default, withdrawn under `no advertise-mac`, re-advertised under `advertise-mac`.
fb750f01da845624.expectation:
Pass:    Default state (or `advertise-mac` configured): learned MAC appears in BGP EVPN routes advertised to peer. `no advertise-mac`: MAC route withdrawn or suppressed from BGP.
Fail-on: MAC route advertised to peer when `no advertise-mac` is active, or MAC route not advertised when `advertise-mac` is enabled and MAC is learned.

fbe5f035de7a294f.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE topology: PE1 and PE2 multi-homed to CE1 via agg-eth with ESI-1 `identifier 0 01.11.11.11.11.11.11.11.11`, `load-balancing-mode all-active`, P2MP MPLS LSPs configured for BUM; PE3 single-homed to CE2; all PEs in same EVI.
Action: On PE1, verify Ethernet A-D per ES route advertises upstream-assigned ESI label (BGP capture or `show evpn routes type 1`). From PE3, send P2MP-encapsulated BUM toward ESI-1 with PE1's upstream label. Monitor CE1 interfaces on PE1 and PE2 with packet capture.
Verify: `show mpls forwarding-table` on PE2 and PE3 confirms PE1's ESI label installed in PE1's context label space. Packet capture on PE2→CE1 link shows NO egress of BUM received with PE1's ESI label (split-horizon drop). PE3 LFIB also has the label but forwards normally (not connected to ESI-1).
fbe5f035de7a294f.equipment: Three routers (PE1, PE2, PE3) + IXIA traffic gen + multi-homed CE device; requires P2MP MPLS LSP support and packet capture on ES links
fbe5f035de7a294f.expectation:
Pass:    PE2 and PE3 program PE1's upstream ESI label in PE1's context space; PE2 does NOT forward P2MP BUM with PE1's ESI label onto CE1; PE3 processes the label without ES constraint.
Fail-on: PE1's ESI label missing from PE2/PE3 LFIB, wrong label context (not tied to PE1), or PE2 forwards to CE1 (split-horizon violation per §8.3.1.2 MUST).

fc1aacd498514f73.action_steps:
Setup:  Create VPLS instance "vpls-test" at configuration/l2-services/vpls; navigate to auto-discovery sub-mode; configure "import-rt 65001:100" followed by "import-rt 65001:200" (verify list accumulation).
Action: Execute "show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-test" and confirm both import-rt lines present under auto-discovery; execute "no import-rt 65001:100"; verify candidate config shows only "import-rt 65001:200"; re-add "import-rt 65001:100"; commit and save; reload DUT.
Verify: Post-reload, "show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-test" output contains both "import-rt 65001:100" and "import-rt 65001:200" lines under auto-discovery sub-section; "show l2-services vpls vpls-test detail" reports auto-discovery operational status.
fc1aacd498514f73.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
fc1aacd498514f73.expectation:
Pass:    Post-reload config displays exactly two lines: "import-rt 65001:100" and "import-rt 65001:200" under auto-discovery with ASN:NN format preserved; "no import-rt 65001:100" removes only that entry (65001:200 persists); show output indicates auto-discovery Status: Up.
Fail-on: Fewer than two import-rt lines post-reload, OR any RT syntax corruption (e.g., "100:65001", "65001 100"), OR "no import-rt 65001:100" deletes both entries or wrong entry, OR auto-discovery Status: Down/Error, OR parser accepts invalid RT format (missing colon, letters in numeric field).

fc25f90d2e287c6b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure DUT as single-homing PE with interface in EVI 100 (no ESI or ESI type 0 only). Configure neighbor PE as multihoming PE with `ethernet-segment` on `agg-eth 0`, `identifier 1` (LACP-based), `load-balancing-mode all-active`, participating in EVI 100. LACP partner attached to neighbor PE. IXIA injects MAC learning traffic toward multihoming site.
Action: Establish BGP EVPN session between DUT and neighbor PE. Verify DUT receives Ethernet A-D per-EVI (route type 1) and per-ES (route type 4) from neighbor. Send continuous traffic from IXIA through DUT to MACs behind multihoming ES. Trigger link failure on one link in neighbor's multihoming bundle.
Verify: On DUT: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` and `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4` confirm route acceptance. `show evpn mac` lists MACs behind multihoming ES with correct next-hop. Monitor IXIA traffic forwarding and loss during failover event.
fc25f90d2e287c6b.equipment: DUT + IXIA + neighbor PE + LACP partner
fc25f90d2e287c6b.expectation:
Pass:    DUT accepts and processes Ethernet A-D route types 1 and 4 from multihoming neighbor per RFC7432bis §8.7. Traffic forwards successfully to multihoming site before and after link failure. Forwarding converges within `es-waiting-time` default (3 seconds) after failover.
Fail-on: DUT rejects or ignores Ethernet A-D routes (absent from `show bgp l2vpn evpn` output). Traffic blackholed or looped. Convergence exceeds 3 seconds or forwarding does not recover.

fc9fae0ae4730c64.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure VPLS vpls-ad-01 under configuration / l2-services; enter auto-discovery mode and add import-rt 65000:100, export-rt 65000:200; commit and verify stanza appears in `show running-config l2-services`.
Action: Execute `no auto-discovery` under configuration / l2-services / vpls vpls-ad-01; commit.
Verify: `show running-config l2-services vpls vpls-ad-01` shows zero auto-discovery configuration (no auto-discovery line, no import-rt, no export-rt); VPLS instance vpls-ad-01 itself remains configured.
fc9fae0ae4730c64.expectation:
Pass:    `no auto-discovery` atomically removes the entire auto-discovery stanza including import-rt 65000:100 and export-rt 65000:200; running-config for vpls-ad-01 contains zero auto-discovery remnants while VPLS instance persists.
Fail-on: import-rt or export-rt entries survive `no auto-discovery`; auto-discovery stanza partially remains; or VPLS instance is deleted when only auto-discovery sub-config should be removed.

fca6eaac6794dec9.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure Exaware DUT: ethernet-segment ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 on agg-eth-1 ('configuration/interface/agg-eth/ethernet-segment'); three VPLS instances (VPLS-100, VPLS-200, VPLS-300) via 'configuration/l2-services/vpls' with 'auto-discovery/export-rt' = 65000:100, 65000:200, 65000:300; bind agg-eth-1 to all. Configure Cisco/Juniper PE: BGP EVPN peer ('af-l2vpn evpn'), import-rt 65000:100, 65000:200 (omit 65000:300 to test RT filtering). IXIA at DUT and PE sites.
Action: Bring up agg-eth-1; trigger Type 1 route advertisement. Capture on 3rd-party PE: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 detail'. Send IXIA traffic tagged for VPLS-100, VPLS-200, VPLS-300.
Verify: Each Type 1 route carries ≥1 RT (extended community type 0x0002); union of RTs = {65000:100, 65000:200, 65000:300}. 3rd-party PE imports VPLS-100/200 (RT match), drops VPLS-300. IXIA: VPLS-100/200 forward, VPLS-300 zero.
fca6eaac6794dec9.expectation:
Pass:    Each Type 1 route (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) has ≥1 RT extended community (RFC4360 0x0002); full RT set {65000:100, 65000:200, 65000:300} present across routes. 3rd-party PE imports VPLS-100/200, rejects VPLS-300. IXIA shows VPLS-100/200 bidirectional, VPLS-300 dropped.
Fail-on: Type 1 route missing RT; incomplete RT set (e.g., missing 65000:300); 3rd-party PE BGP error; wrong RT filtering (VPLS-300 forwards or VPLS-100/200 drops).

fd2570d76f700b49.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with service-type vlan-aware-bundle, bind agg-eth 0 with ethernet-segment, enable auto-discovery with export-rt and import-rt. Establish iBGP session with neighbor PE; on neighbor PE capture baseline Type 1 Ethernet A-D per EVI routes showing RD, ESI, ET ID (VLAN-ID for vlan-aware-bundle), MPLS label LT2, RTs, next-hop per RFC7432bis ch.8.4.
Action: Stage new image via ONIE; run onie-install; wait for reboot and BGP session re-establishment.
Verify: Post-reboot issue `show running-config l2-services` and `show running-config interface agg-eth 0` to confirm evpn and ethernet-segment config preserved; on neighbor PE capture BGP NLRI and confirm Type 1 routes re-advertised with identical RD, ESI, ET ID, label LT2, RTs, next-hop fields as pre-upgrade baseline.
fd2570d76f700b49.equipment: DUT + ONIE image server + neighbor PE (for BGP route validation)
fd2570d76f700b49.expectation:
Pass:    Post-upgrade Type 1 routes re-advertised with all mandatory fields (RD, ESI, ET ID, MPLS label LT2, RTs, next-hop) matching pre-upgrade baseline; neighbor PE receives routes; evpn, auto-discovery, and ethernet-segment config intact in running-config.
Fail-on: Type 1 routes missing post-upgrade, route fields corrupted or incomplete (wrong/missing ESI, ET ID, label, RTs, or next-hop), evpn or ethernet-segment config lost or requires manual re-entry, onie-install fails or rolls back, BGP session fails to re-establish within 5 minutes.

fd903796875f7a55.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure two PEs (PE1=DUT, PE2) in EVPN instance EVI-100, route-target 100:100, ingress replication enabled. On PE1: `configuration / l2-services / evpn` → `unknown-mac-flooding enable`, `advertise-mac enable`. Configure BGP neighbor on PE2: `configuration / routing / bgp / vrf EVI-100 / neighbor <PE1-IP>` → `af-l2vpn evpn`. Verify PE1 advertises BGP route type 3 to PE2; capture PMSI tunnel attribute label (e.g., L1=100001). Attach two CEs to PE1 (CE1 on port 1/1/1, CE2 on port 1/1/2, both in VLAN 100).
Action: From IXIA port emulating PE2, send three MPLS-encapsulated packets to PE1 with label L1: (1) inner Ethernet DA=FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF (broadcast), (2) DA=01:00:5E:00:00:01 (multicast), (3) DA=00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE (unknown unicast MAC). Capture egress traffic on PE1 ports 1/1/1 and 1/1/2.
Verify: Run `show l2-services evpn mac-table vrf EVI-100` on PE1 to confirm 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE is absent. Run `show l2-services evpn routes type 3 vrf EVI-100` to verify PMSI tunnel attribute label L1. Inspect IXIA captures on both CE ports; confirm all three packets appear on both CE1 and CE2 (flooded).
fd903796875f7a55.expectation:
Pass:    All three packets (broadcast, multicast, unknown unicast) are flooded by PE1 to both CE ports. Unknown unicast MAC 00:AA:BB:CC:DD:EE is treated identically to broadcast/multicast per RFC7432bis §12.1 MUST (flooded, not unicast forwarded). MPLS label L1 correctly demuxes ingress replication traffic.
Fail-on: PE1 drops unknown unicast packet, OR forwards it to single CE port instead of flooding, OR does not flood broadcast/multicast packets, OR does not recognize label L1 as ingress replication label, OR treats unknown unicast MAC differently from broadcast/multicast (violates RFC7432bis §12.1 MUST).

fdb8c25ae3fe027d.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure PE1 and PE2 in EVPN instance; on PE2 configure `interface agg-eth / ethernet-segment` with default type-4 `identifier`, `load-balancing-mode single-active` (default); verify PE2 is DF for the BD. Confirm PE2 advertises ingress-replication downstream label in P-tunnel attribute.
Action: From PE1, send MPLS-encapsulated unknown-unicast frame (destination MAC 00:00:00:00:99:99 not in PE2's FDB) carrying PE2's advertised ingress-replication label and passing split-horizon checks (non-local ESI label). Send 100 frames.
Verify: On PE2, run `show l2-services fdb` to confirm 00:00:00:00:99:99 is absent; capture on ES interface agg-eth to confirm all 100 unknown-unicast frames egressed without MAC lookup delay; run `show evpn instance detail` to verify BUM replication active.
fdb8c25ae3fe027d.expectation:
Pass:    All 100 unknown-unicast frames flooded to ES interface immediately (per §13.2.1 default: no destination MAC lookup required for BUM)
Fail-on: Frames dropped or delayed pending MAC learning, or fewer than 100 frames egress on ES interface

fe04408aca00ed84.action_steps:
Setup:  PE1 (loopback 1.1.1.1) and PE2 (loopback 2.2.2.2) with BGP EVPN peering; configure ES on PE1 with ESI type 0 value 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99, RD 1.1.1.1:100 (Type 1 format).
Action: On PE1: `evpn evpn1 service-type vlan-based`, assign RD 1.1.1.1:100, attach ES; enable `af-l2vpn evpn` on BGP neighbor to PE2. Capture BGP UPDATE on PE1→PE2 link (tcpdump port 179). On PE2: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 detail`.
Verify: Decode captured Route Type 4: RD field is Type 1 (0x00 0x01 | 4-octet-IP | 2-octet-admin); ESI field is 10 octets matching 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99; Originating Router IP field = 1.1.1.1; IP Address Length = 32 bits.
fe04408aca00ed84.expectation:
Pass:    RD type octet = 0x00 0x01 (Type 1), value = 1.1.1.1:100; ESI = 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99 (10 octets); Originating Router IP = 1.1.1.1 (PE1 loopback); IP Address Length = 32.
Fail-on: RD type ≠ 0x00 0x01 (not Type 1), ESI length ≠ 10 octets or value mismatch, Originating Router IP ≠ 1.1.1.1, or Route Type 4 not advertised to PE2.

fe0d426b2389b7f5.action_steps:
Setup:  Three-PE EVPN topology (PE1=DUT, PE2=neighbor PE, PE3=IXIA BGP speaker); EVPN instance 100 with matching route-targets; PE2 advertises route type 2 (MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 / IP 192.0.2.10) with Default Gateway extended community (Type 0x0D); IXIA injects same MAC/IP route without Default Gateway EC.
Action: Inject both routes to PE1; capture BGP EVPN UPDATEs on PE1; verify PE1 selects PE2's path (§7.13.1 criterion 1: Default Gateway EC presence); then configure PE1 to advertise same MAC/IP locally (enable Default Gateway on IRB interface); capture new UPDATEs and verify best-path switches to local route (criterion 2: local preferred).
Verify: Decode BGP UPDATEs in Wireshark: confirm Default Gateway EC (Type 0x0D) present in PE2's route, absent in IXIA's route; on PE1 run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type mac-ip-advertisement` to confirm best-path marker on PE2 initially, then on local route after IRB config; check `show evpn mac-table instance 100` for active next-hop change.
fe0d426b2389b7f5.expectation:
Pass:    PE1's BGP RIB marks PE2's route as best-path (has Default Gateway EC) over IXIA's (no EC)—criterion 1 applied; after local MAC/IP advertisement, best-path switches to local route—criterion 2 applied; BGP captures confirm EC presence/absence; MAC table shows corresponding next-hop changes; criteria applied in RFC7432bis §7.13.1 order.
Fail-on: PE1 selects IXIA's route when PE2's route with Default Gateway EC is available; PE1 retains PE2 as best-path after local route appears with Default Gateway EC; criteria applied out of order (e.g., local preference checked before EC presence); Default Gateway EC missing or malformed in PE2 or PE1 UPDATEs; no best-path change observed in RIB or MAC table.

fe29c55495fe2fbf.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVI 100 with `import-rt 65000:100; export-rt 65000:100` in `configuration l2-services evpn evi 100`. Configure BGP neighbor 192.0.2.1 with `af-l2vpn evpn` in `configuration routing bgp vrf default neighbor 192.0.2.1`. Neighbor PE advertises 2 Ethernet A-D Per EVPN Instance routes (type-1) for EVI 100: one WITH L2-Attr Extended Community (MTU=1500), one WITHOUT L2-Attr.
Action: Verify DUT receives both A-D routes and processes RFC selection logic; trigger tech-support collection via platform command (e.g., `admin tech-support`, `show tech-support evpn`, or CLI equivalent).
Verify: Extract tech-support archive; confirm inclusion of: `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 1` (both A-D routes with ext-community presence/absence visible), `show evpn evi 100 detail` (RFC behavior applied), `show running-config l2-services evpn evi 100`, `show running-config routing bgp vrf default neighbor 192.0.2.1`, syslog filtered for EVPN A-D route events.
fe29c55495fe2fbf.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains all 5 named outputs; BGP EVPN table shows route 1 with L2-Attr Extended Community fields (e.g., MTU, control-flags per RFC7432bis ch.14.1.1), route 2 without L2-Attr; evidence DUT applied RFC7432 ch.14.1.1 due to ≥1 route missing L2-Attr; complete config snapshot.
Fail-on: Missing any of the 5 show/config outputs; L2-Attr Extended Community details not visible or not parseable; no RFC selection evidence; incomplete running-config; missing syslog for A-D route processing.

fe51914b0c1fad50.action_steps:
Setup:  EVPN EVI 100 running with all four alarm types triggered from prior test rows: (1) multi-homed ES misconfiguration on agg-eth 0 due to DF algorithm mismatch with neighbor PE, (2) MAC 00:00:00:22:22:22 frozen temporarily by host mac-address-duplicate-detection (move-count exceeded within move-interval), (3) MAC frozen permanently (if supported), (4) static MAC 00:00:00:11:11:11 relearned from remote PE. Syslog collector configured and capturing alarm messages.
Action: Run tech-support command on DUT. Transfer bundle to external storage, extract, and inspect for alarm evidence: show alarms output, syslog entries, show running-config sections for host mac-address-duplicate-detection and mac-address-static, and MAC address table.
Verify: Bundle contains show alarms listing all four alarm types with complete parameters: "Multi-homed ES misconfiguration for ESI <esi-value>(agg-eth 0): from <ip-address>, LACP System MAC <MAC-address>, DF Algorithm <algorithm>"; "Duplicated MAC address 00:00:00:22:22:22 is frozen temporarily"; "Duplicated MAC address <mac-address> is frozen permanently" (if supported); "Relearning of static MAC address 00:00:00:11:11:11 from <ip-address>". Syslog entries match show alarms. Bundle includes show running-config with host mac-address-duplicate-detection and mac-address-static configuration. MAC address table shows frozen and static entries.
fe51914b0c1fad50.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support bundle contains show alarms and syslog entries for all four alarm types with complete parameters (ESI value, peer IP, LACP System MAC, DF algorithm for ES misconfiguration; MAC addresses and freeze-time for duplication alarms; MAC address and source IP for static MAC relearning). Bundle includes show running-config sections for host mac-address-duplicate-detection and mac-address-static. MAC address table shows frozen and static entries matching alarm conditions.
Fail-on: Missing alarm type in show alarms or syslog, incomplete alarm parameters (ESI/IP/MAC/algorithm/freeze-time missing), alarm text format does not match documented messages, bundle missing show running-config sections for host mac-address-duplicate-detection or mac-address-static, or MAC table missing frozen or static entries.

fe610fc751ba71b0.action_steps:
Setup:  Establish BGP L2VPN EVPN peering between DUT and neighbor PE; neighbor advertises 8 type-2 MAC/IP routes; verify `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows neighbor in Established state with 8 prefixes received.
Action: On DUT under `configuration / routing / bgp / <asn> / vrf / <vrf> / neighbor / <neighbor-ip> / af-l2vpn / evpn`, configure `maximum-prefix 10`; commit. From neighbor PE, advertise 3 additional routes (total 11, exceeding the 10-prefix limit).
Verify: `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` (neighbor state no longer Established, transitioned to Idle or Active); `show logging` or syslog capture (prefix-limit-exceeded message with neighbor IP); packet capture via `tcpdump port 179` (NOTIFICATION message code 6 sub-code 1 from DUT to neighbor).
fe610fc751ba71b0.expectation:
Pass:    BGP session tears down when the 11th prefix is received; DUT sends BGP NOTIFICATION code 6 sub-code 1 (Maximum Number of Prefixes Reached); `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` shows neighbor in Idle or Active state (not Established); syslog contains prefix-limit event with neighbor IP and prefix count.
Fail-on: Session remains Established beyond the 10-prefix limit; routes 11+ are accepted into the EVPN table; no NOTIFICATION sent, or wrong code/sub-code; no syslog message; session auto-recovers without `restart` configured.

fea1863f3a82f5a0.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure `host mac-address-duplicate-detection enable move-interval 180 move-count 5 freeze-time 300` in the target EVI. Establish EVPN instance with two attachment circuits (IXIA ports or neighbor PE). Trigger MAC duplication by generating ≥5 MAC moves for the same MAC (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55) within 180 seconds via IXIA or learning events from different sources.
Action: After duplicate detection triggers and the MAC enters frozen state, collect tech-support. The bundle must include: `show l2-services evpn mac-duplication`, `show l2-services evpn mac-table` (showing frozen MAC), `show logging | include duplicate`, `show bgp evpn routes mac 00:11:22:33:44:55` (confirming route withdrawal), and running-config section `configuration l2-services evpn host mac-address-duplicate-detection`.
Verify: Parse tech-support for: duplicate MAC 00:11:22:33:44:55 in frozen state with move-count ≥5, operator alert (syslog EVPN_MAC_DUPLICATE or equivalent) with MAC and timestamp, configured move-interval=180 move-count=5 freeze-time=300, and no active BGP MAC/IP Advertisement route for that MAC.
fea1863f3a82f5a0.expectation:
Pass:    Tech-support contains all five artifacts: frozen MAC entry with move counter, operator alert identifying the duplicate MAC and EVI, M=180 N=5 freeze=300 in running-config, MAC route withdrawn from BGP (or show output confirms no route), and syslog timestamp correlating with freeze event.
Fail-on: Duplicate MAC still shows active (not frozen), no operator alert in syslog, BGP still advertising the MAC route, or M/N/freeze-time values missing or incorrect in config snapshot.

feb48ec5d2b00a5b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance with 3 ethernet-segments on agg-eth interfaces: ES1 (identifier 0 type0-value 01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01.01, load-balancing-mode single-active, service-carving default), ES2 (identifier 0 type0-value 02.02.02.02.02.02.02.02.02, load-balancing-mode all-active, service-carving highest-random-weight), ES3 (identifier 1, load-balancing-mode single-active, service-carving highest-preference). IXIA emulates local CEs on attachment circuits and remote PEs sending EVPN-labeled frames; seed MACT with 10k known MAC entries across 100 VLANs.
Action: IXIA sends continuous bidirectional known-unicast MAC traffic (2 Gbps aggregate) for 24 hours, exercising both attachment-circuit next-hop lookups (local CE traffic) and EVPN label LT1 next-hop lookups (remote PE traffic) across all VLANs, ESI types, and load-balancing modes.
Verify: Hourly snapshots of `show l2-services evpn mac-table count`, `show l2-services evpn ethernet-segment`, `show platform process memory | grep evpn-mact`. Log MACT entry count, ESI operational states (single-active DF election stable, all-active forwarding stable), EVPN process RSS/VSZ, IXIA frame loss.
feb48ec5d2b00a5b.expectation:
Pass:    MACT entry count stable at 10k ±3% over 24 h (MAC learning/aging balanced); EVPN process memory VSZ growth < 5%; all 3 ESIs remain operationally up with consistent DF election (ES1, ES3) and all-active forwarding (ES2); IXIA frame loss < 0.01%; service-carving stable (VLAN distribution per configured mode).
Fail-on: MACT unbounded growth (> 15k entries) or depletion (< 5k); EVPN process memory leak (VSZ growth > 10% or RSS leak); any ESI state flap; frame loss > 0.1%; service-carving drift (VLAN reassignment without topology change); DF election thrashing on single-active ESIs.

fed5c7e9563ed82c.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure aggregate-ethernet interface agg-eth 1.1, enter `configuration / interface / agg-eth 1.1 / ethernet-segment` mode, set esi type 0 to allow manual value entry.
Action: Issue `identifier type 0 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88` (9-octet manual value for type 0 ESI), commit; verify with `show evpn ethernet-segment` and `show running-config interface agg-eth 1.1`; issue `no identifier`, commit; re-check operational state and running-config.
Verify: After first commit, `show evpn ethernet-segment` displays ESI type 0 with value 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88; after `no identifier` commit, ESI reverts to type 4 (system-generated default) in both running-config and operational output.
fed5c7e9563ed82c.equipment: DUT only (CLI session via console / SSH)
fed5c7e9563ed82c.expectation:
Pass:    Type 0 ESI with the exact 9-octet value 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88 appears in `show evpn ethernet-segment` and running-config; `no identifier` successfully restores type 4 default ESI; both transitions commit without error.
Fail-on: Type 0 ESI value rejected, stored incorrectly, or truncated; `no identifier` does not restore type 4 default; operational state does not match running-config; commit fails with precondition error when prerequisites are met.

ffb5ee36fcfbe93b.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure multi-homed Ethernet Segment (ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99) on DUT `interface agg-eth 1/1/1` with `ethernet-segment`, DF election algorithm preference-based, and Don't Preempt capability enabled. Configure matching ESI on 3rd-party PE (Cisco/Juniper) with same DF algorithm. Both PEs in same EVPN instance with `import-rt 65000:100` / `export-rt 65000:100` under `l2-services / evpn`. Start with DUT interface shutdown; 3rd-party PE becomes active DF.
Action: Execute `no shutdown` on DUT `agg-eth 1/1/1`. Capture BGP UPDATE containing EVPN Type-4 (Ethernet Segment) route from DUT using tcpdump or 3rd-party PE BGP debug. Inject bi-directional L2 traffic (1000 pps, 64-byte frames) from IXIA through CE.
Verify: Decode captured Type-4 route: DF Election Extended Community (Type 0x06, Sub-Type 0x06) present with Flags octet bit 0 (Don't Preempt) set to 1. Run `show evpn ethernet-segment <ESI> detail` on DUT and vendor-equivalent on 3rd-party PE; confirm 3rd-party PE shows DF status and DUT shows Non-DF. Monitor IXIA Rx/Tx counters for 60 seconds; traffic continues via 3rd-party PE with zero loss.
ffb5ee36fcfbe93b.expectation:
Pass:    DUT Type-4 route includes DF Election Extended Community with Don't Preempt flag (bit 0) = 1; 3rd-party PE retains DF role for all Ethernet Tags; IXIA frame loss = 0 during and after DUT interface-up event.
Fail-on: (a) DF Election Extended Community missing or Don't Preempt flag = 0, (b) DUT preempts 3rd-party PE and becomes DF, (c) IXIA reports >0.01% frame loss, or (d) when DUT later advertises mismatched DF algorithm (e.g., HRW vs. preference), both PEs fail to fall back to default modulo-based election per RFC7432 §8.5.

ffdb857e81630291.action_steps:
Setup:  Configure EVPN instance `evpn100 service-type vlan-aware-bundle`. Configure Ethernet Segment with ESI 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00 on interface Eth1; enable multi-homing but omit explicit DF preference/weight parameter. Configure BGP with `af-l2vpn evpn` to neighbor PE at 10.0.0.2.
Action: Activate ES interface and establish BGP EVPN session. DUT generates Ethernet Segment Route (Type 4) for the ES and advertises it to the neighbor PE.
Verify: Run `show bgp l2vpn evpn route-type 4 esi 00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:00` on DUT. Capture BGP UPDATE on IXIA/neighbor PE, decode DF Election Extended Community (Type 0x06 Sub-Type 0x02), extract DF preference field value.
ffdb857e81630291.expectation:
Pass:    Type 4 route advertised with DF preference field = 32767 (default per spec § 3.5); ES-Import RT present; no BGP errors.
Fail-on: DF preference ≠ 32767, or value outside range 0–65535, or Type 4 route missing, or DF Election Extended Community absent.
